title: Trilinos: Enabling Scientific Computing Across Diverse Hardware Architectures at Scale

authors: Edidiong Christopher James, Ivano Bongiovanni, Mashhuda Glencross, Lewys Brace

title: Unified Multimodal Representation Learning With Prototype Calibration for Cross-Subject Few-Shot Emotion Recognition
authors: Haiqi Liu, C. L. Philip Chen, Tong Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Multimodal cross-subject emotion recognition has gained increasing attention due to the complementary nature of physiological and behavioral signals, such as electroencephalography (EEG) and eye movements (EM). However, modality heterogeneity and subject variability remain major obstacles, especially in few-shot scenarios. Existing approaches often combine increasingly complex architectures with unsupervised domain adaptation to address these issues, but they typically require large amounts of unlabeled target data and struggle to generalize when samples are scarce. To overcome these limitations, this article proposes a unified multimodal representation learning (UMRL) framework with prototype calibration under the few-shot paradigm. Specifically, the UMRL strategy is designed from an optimization perspective rather than architectural complexity to learn a compact and discriminative joint representation. It encourages compact representations by jointly minimizing cross-modal inconsistency and penalizing modality dominance, ensuring that complementary cues are effectively integrated in a unified embedding space. Nevertheless, emotional prototypes constructed from a few labeled samples of novel subjects are often unstable and sensitive to subject-dependent variability. To further alleviate this issue, this article introduces an emotional prototype calibration mechanism that aligns novel emotional prototypes with semantic priors learned from seen subjects to suppress noise and improve cross-subject generalization. Extensive experiments on multiple multimodal emotion recognition benchmarks with EEG and EM demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves state-of-the-art performance and delivers consistent robustness under few-shot conditions.

title: CRIXP: Cultural Knowledge Representation for Inherent eXPlainability
authors: Abhisek Sharma, Sarika Jain
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Cultural awareness plays a vital role in making AI systems beneficial for a globalized audience. This cultural awareness is lacking in modern decision-support systems and in the current state of the work, where explainability is the paramount requirement now from all AI systems. To address this, the article introduces CRIXP, a framework with decisive cultural knowledge and the capability to explain the reasoning behind its suggestions. CRIXP comprises: 1) a cultural ontology (CRO); 2) a cultural knowledge graph (CKG); 3) a general-purpose adaptable algorithm providing recommendations and explanations; and 4) a CKG enrichment module. The cultural traits of all the locales are consolidated and represented as an ontology and a knowledge graph. A cultural shift suggestion system has been demonstrated for recommendations and explanations. The response generated is compared with ChatGPT. CRIXP is found to outperform ChatGPT by providing more relevant recommendations with inherent explainability.

title: Segment-wise Joint Pose and Parametric Geometry Estimation for Multi-Part Quasi-Conical and Cylindrical Objects
authors: Hao Wu, Lijuan Li, Aolei Yang, Zhile Yang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Accurate modeling of multi-part objects with diverse quasi-conical and cylindrical geometries requires a unified understanding of both their semantic composition and underlying geometric structure. To address this challenge, a joint Pose and Parametric Geometry Estimation (PPGE) framework is proposed, which integrates learned representations with explicit geometric priors for simultaneous segment-wise segmentation and parametric geometry prediction from raw point clouds. The proposed hybrid architecture employs an augmented PointNet++ backbone to extract multi-scale spatial features and a squeeze-and-channel-attention module to enhance inter-channel discriminability and context awareness. Three slot-based task-specific prediction heads are designed to separately infer segment membership, symmetry axis orientation, and point-wise surface normals, providing complementary semantic and geometric cues. These multi-branch outputs are further coupled by a geometrically-constrained probability-axis-normal fusion module, which enforces alignment between semantic segmentation, axis direction, and normal consistency, yielding coherent segment reconstructions even under partial or noisy observations. To refine parametric accuracy, a mode-climb parameter estimation module iteratively adjusts axis direction, height and radius parameters toward physically valid local optima. Experimental evaluations on multiple datasets of quasi-conical and cylindrical assemblies, along with physical prototype validation, demonstrate that PPGE achieves superior robustness and accuracy compared to baseline and ablated variants, particularly under challenging conditions of segment overlap, noise, and occlusion.

title: FedMeld: A Model-Dispersal Federated Learning Framework for Space-Ground Integrated Networks_supp1-3647858.pdf
authors: Xianhao Chen
publish: N/A
abstract: To bridge the digital divide, space-ground integrated networks (SGINs) are expected to deliver artificial intelligence (AI) services to every corner of the world. One key mission of SGINs is to support federated learning (FL) at a global scale. However, existing space-ground integrated FL frameworks involve ground stations or costly inter-satellite links, entailing excessive training latency and communication costs. To overcome these limitations, we propose an infrastructure-free federated learning framework based on a model dispersal (FedMeld) strategy, which exploits periodic movement patterns and store-carry-forward capabilities of satellites to enable parameter mixing across large-scale geographical regions. We theoretically show that FedMeld leads to global model convergence and quantify the effects of round interval and mixing ratio between adjacent areas on its learning performance. Based on the theoretical results, we formulate a joint optimization problem to design the staleness control and mixing ratio (SC-MR) for minimizing the training loss. By decomposing the problem into sequential SC and MR subproblems without compromising the optimality, we derive the round interval solution in a closed form and the mixing ratio in a semi-closed form to achieve the optimal latency-accuracy tradeoff. Experiments using various datasets demonstrate that FedMeld achieves superior model accuracy while significantly reducing communication costs as compared with traditional FL schemes for SGINs.

title: Argus: 3D Radar Point Cloud Registration Using Complementary Cameras for Infrastructure-Assisted Autonomous Driving
authors: Kaikai Deng, Dong Zhao, Shuyue Wang, Wenxin Zheng, Zihan Zhang, Huadong Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: mmWave radar is an emerging sensing modality that expects to significantly improve the safety and reliability of autonomous vehicles, especially in adverse conditions. A key technology to achieve this vision is to register point clouds between infrastructure and vehicle to improve the sensing ability of the vehicle. In this work, we design Argus, a novel lightweight system that achieves decimeter-level and real-time registration. Argus consists of two components: (i) a point cloud registration network component exploits the complementary advantages of cameras and radars to extract semantics of salient objects (e.g., traffic signs), followed by a keypoint extraction strategy and an early stopping strategy to quickly obtain keypoints and transformation directions for achieving accurate registration; (ii) a multi-vehicle-infrastructure adaptive scheduling component combines two key modules, request scheduler and adaptive trigger, to ensure real-time registration in multi-vehicle scenes. We implement and evaluate Argus with two multi-view image and radar point cloud datasets collected in the campus and indoor scenes. Experimental results show that Argus extends the vehicle's sensing range by 89% with an average end-to-end latency of 25.22 ms, and delivers a 3X improvement in accuracy over state-of-the-art baselines; meanwhile, it requires only an average bandwidth of 0.45 Mbps for each vehicle.

title: Two-Stage Variable Universe Fuzzy Model-Free Sliding Mode Control of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor Based on Double-Power-Combination Reaching Law
authors: Xinhong Zou, Kai Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion
abstract: Aiming at the problem that the parameter variation and external load disturbance of permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) lead to the decrease of system control performance in actual operation, a two-stage variable universe fuzzy (TSVUF) model-free sliding mode control method of PMSM based on double-power-combination reaching law (DPCRL) is proposed in this paper. Firstly, a new ultra-local model of the motor speed loop is established according to the mathematical model of the PMSM under parameter perturbation. Secondly, a model-free double-power-combination reaching law sliding mode controller (MFDPSMC) for the speed loop is designed, and a TSVUF extended sliding mode disturbance observer (ESMDO) is designed to observe the unknown part of the ultra-local model, and the observed value is used to compensate the system. The TSVUF control method is used to dynamically adjust the reaching law gain of the ESMDO, which improves the observation performance of the observer. Finally, the experimental results show that the proposed control method effectively improves the response speed and anti-interference ability of the system.

title: Explainable Detection of False Promotions for Digital Collectibles: A Visual Language Attention Analysis Framework
authors: Yitong Yang, Fang Lin, Jianjun He
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Digital collectible (DC) platforms have become a major channel for promoting non-fungible tokens and related assets, but they are also increasingly flooded with exaggerated or misleading claims about scarcity, investment return, and creator credibility. Existing detection methods either rely on text-only classifiers that ignore visual design and on-chain context, or adopt multimodal large language models (MLLMs) whose explanations are shallow and weakly grounded in evidence. In this paper, we propose an explainable detection framework for false advertising in digital collectibles, built on top of LLaVA-Next and enhanced with a tri-modal attention architecture. We formulate claim-level detection as a consistency assessment task between textual claims, advertisement images, and blockchain metadata, and introduce a Cross-layer Logical Attention Alignment (CLAA) module that jointly refines image-patch and metadata attention conditioned on each claim. A tri-modal evidence encoder integrates visual patches, tokenized claims, and structured attributes into a unified representation, while an explanation calibration loss encourages attention to concentrate on human-interpretable evidence regions and fields. Extensive experiments on two DC advertising benchmarks show that our model consistently outperforms text-only, image-only, multimodal fusion, and MLLM baselines. On the MFND dataset, our method improves F1 from 0.795 (fine-tuned LLaVA-Next) to 0.842 and AUC from 0.884 to 0.916, while on CrossPlatform-Ads-Analysis it raises F1 from 0.729 to 0.792 and AUC from 0.828 to 0.881. In terms of explanation quality, our framework boosts Evidence IoU from 0.32 to 0.49 and the reasoning consistency score from 0.59 to 0.73. Under cross-domain transfer, the relative performance drop is reduced from 8.3% for LLaVA-Next to 6.0% for our model. Qualitative case studies further demonstrate that the proposed framework produces stable, fine-grained, and regulator-friendly explanations, making it a practical tool for auditing and monitoring false advertising in digital collectible ecosystems.

title: How Accurate Should Prediction Be for Connected Autonomous Vehicles at Blind Intersections?
authors: Lu Tao, Yousuke Watanabe, Shen Ying, Yuhuan Lu, Zhengshu Zhou, Hiroaki Takada
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Vehicle trajectory prediction (VTP) is a prominent research topic in autonomous vehicles (AVs). However, existing studies concentrate on improving prediction accuracy, overlooking how VTP accuracy influences the effectiveness of downstream tasks such as collision avoidance. In this paper, we investigate the relationship between VTP accuracy and key performance indicators (KPIs) of cooperative collision avoidance systems (CCAS) for connected AVs operating at blind intersections. To this end, we propose a generalized error model (GEM) that enables the generation of customizable VTP errors. GEM is integrated into an error injection framework to produce diverse, error-controlled trajectories in real time. Our experiments quantitatively characterize the relationship among VTP accuracy, warning thresholds and strategies, and CCAS KPIs. The results reveal a common pattern: as VTP error increases, CCAS recall improves while precision declines, demonstrating a negative correlation that is difficult to balance. This underscores the need for effective trade-offs between recall and precision in CCAS design. With a general collision warning strategy, CCAS can preemptively report over 90% of collisions, substantially enhancing traffic safety, though at the expense of a reduced precision. To mitigate this, we devise a density-based warning strategy (DCWS) that improves precision across the entire VTP accuracy space while maintaining recall at acceptable levels. Notably, DCWS gradually decouples CCAS KPIs from VTP accuracy performance, highlighting the crucial role of warning strategies. The quantitative results presented in this paper can help practitioners assess the impact of their VTP models on CCAS and select appropriate warning thresholds.

title: ConSSAC: Conservative Sigmoidal Soft Actor-Critic for Offline Dialogue Policy Optimization
authors: Fatemeh Shamsezat, Saeed Shiry Ghidary, Ali Mohades
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Optimizing dialogue policies in multi-domain task-oriented systems is challenging because of the limited opportunities for real user interaction. Pipeline-based architectures offer efficiency and interpretability; however, their dependence on user simulators limits scalability and generalization to real-world conditions. Offline reinforcement learning has emerged as a promising alternative to reduce simulator reliance. However, offline settings amplify inherent difficulties, including a distribution shift and Q-value overestimation when training on imperfect data. We propose Conservative Sigmoidal Soft Actor–Critic (ConSSAC), an offline reinforcement learning method for discrete dialogue action spaces. ConSSAC extends Sigmoidal Soft Actor–Critic (SSAC) by integrating a conservative Q-learning objective for actions that are weakly supported or unobserved in the dataset, while retaining the sigmoidal policy architecture for calibrated multi-label action selection. This design enables the reliable exploitation of indistribution behaviors. In addition, we introduce RewardWOZ-21, a turn-level reward–annotated extension of MultiWOZ 2.1, which enables scalable and fine-grained offline training. Experiments on ConvLab-3 indicate that ConSSAC achieves higher performance than SSAC, PPO-based models, and recent LLM-based policies under the evaluated settings. It achieves a 4.1% absolute improvement in F1 by reducing false positives and false negatives in dialogue-act selection, while preserving computational efficiency comparable to that of standard actor–critic architectures. These results suggest that combining conservative offline reinforcement learning with sigmoidal policy parameterization is associated with improved robustness and reliability in multi-domain task-oriented dialogue systems.

title: Lift-Augmentation Aware Backstepping Control for an Underactuated Bird-Scale Flapping Wing Vehicle_supp1-3678452.mp4
authors: Nak-seung Hyun
publish: N/A
abstract: In this paper, we aim to design a nonlinear controller to achieve altitude tracking and pitch stabilization for a class of underactuated nonlinear systems, namely, bird-scale flapping-wing vehicles (BFWVs) with a single actuator. The major challenge lies in the underactuated nonlinear system dynamics and the dependency of lift generation on forward translational velocity. In this paper, a lift-augmentation-aware reduced-order model for a class of BFWV incapable of hovering is introduced with an emphasis on the sagittal plane (body <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$YZ$</tex-math></inline-formula> plane) dynamics. A feasible fixed point for altitude control of the underactuated system is studied, and a Lyapunov-based switching controller is developed to force the states to stabilize at the desired altitude. We demonstrate its effectiveness in both numerical simulations and closed-loop indoor experiments and address the challenges of controlling the underactuated BFWV.

title: G-MAPP: GPU-accelerated Multi-Agent Planning and Perception for Reactive Motion Generation
authors: Tanmay Bishnoi, Riddhiman Laha, Tobias Löw, Jose Alex Chandy, Luis F.C. Figueredo, Sami Haddadin
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Reactive motion generation in unstructured environments remains an open challenge in robotics. Due to the computational complexity of collision-free motion generation, existing methods either generate global trajectories for static scenarios, or employ models that make conservative assumptions about the environment. This paper identifies the primary bottleneck as the runtime performance demand of planning on high-fidelity environments, and the temporal integration between the perception and planning modules. Therefore, we propose a framework that does not compromise on runtime performance and world representations for perception and planning by accelerating world modeling and vector-field based planning using the GPU. This allows us to achieve faster parallel state exploration for quasiglobal trajectory planning, and tighter coupling of the perceptionaction loop in real-time for dynamic cluttered environments with off-the-shelf depth sensors. We quantitatively evaluate the computation-time and success rate differences for the CPU and GPU versions of our planner, and perform qualitative evaluations of our coupled framework using real-world experiments on a 7- DoF Franka Emika robot. Experimental results demonstrate that our GPU-based framework achieves up to a 5x speedup over the CPU version and successfully avoids collisions across both trivial and challenging physical world scenarios. The implementation is available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/chart-research/g-mapp</uri>

title: A Low-Latency MTPA Control Strategy Based on Dual Virtual Signal Injection for IPMSM
authors: Dongjin Wang, Zhonggang Yin, Yanping Zhang, Yixuan Gao, Qi Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Since the existence of low-pass filters (LPF) in the conventional virtual signal injection control, the system suffers from delays and inaccurate maximum-torque-per-ampere (MTPA) trajectories. To tackle the issues of dynamic response lag and MTPA trajectory deviation, this paper proposes a current vector angle determination strategy based on dual virtual signal injection (DVSI). The DVSI method superimposes two sine and cosine waves of identical frequency onto the measured current angle signal. Using the Taylor expansion and superposition principle, the proposed method cancels high-frequency components in the torque derivative to realize direct extraction of the torque derivative signal, thereby avoiding delays and errors introduced by LPF. The experiments conducted on a 3.0 kW interior permanent-magnet synchronous machine (IPMSM) drive platform validated the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: ARM-Powered Chaotic PWM: A Simplified Solution for EMI Reduction in High-Gain DC–DC Converters
authors: M Nandhini, Sudhakar Natarajan
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In this paper a simplified Chaotic Pulse Width Modulation (CPWM) method is proposes for minimizing conducted electromagnetic interference (EMI) in high-gain DC-DC converters, address the shortcomings of traditional PWM techniques. In contrast to conventional methods, CPWM uses deterministic chaos to randomize switching pulses, which efficiently distributes spectrum energy and reduces high-frequency harmonic peaks without changing the output voltage and convertor performance. A randomized carrier frequency modulation with a fixed duty ratio (RCFMFD) technique is used for achieving spectral spreading with minimum hardware complexity. The proposed high-gain converter is modelled and simulated in MATLAB/Simulink under periodic and chaotic PWM settings. A hardware prototype is developed with a DC input of 12 V and it was stepped up to a 200 V DC at a 200 kHz switching frequency with a 35% duty cycle. An ARM based processor generates both conventional and CPWM switching pulses, interfaced to the MOSFET gate through a driver. A power spectral density (PSD) comparison reveals that CPWM achieves a maximum conducted EMI suppression of about 23.08 dBμV (equivalent to 22.6 μV) compared to conventional PWM. The results verify that CPWM offers a compact, cost-effective, and high-performance EMI suppression solution suitable for EMI-sensitive applications such as electric vehicles, aerospace, and medical electronics.

title: Information Experience: The Strategy and Tactics of Design Thinking: Craig Baehr: [Book Review]
authors: James Berryhill
publish: IEEE Transactions on Professional Communication
abstract: Presents reviews for the following list of books, Information Experience: The Strategy and Tactics of Design Thinking.

title: Revolutionizing Healthcare with Quantum Computing: Bridging Innovation and Practical Challenges
authors: Bhawna Tunwal, Mamtha Prajapati, Sudesh Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: This Healthcare is a critical sector were integrating emerging technologies like Quantum Computing (QC) could transform medical research, diagnosis, and treatment. Despite its achievements, significant obstacles still exist in healthcare, including disease detection, ineffective drug discovery procedures, and an excessive amount of data that is too big for conventional computing systems to analyze. The processing and analysis of medical data could be entirely transformed by applying QC in healthcare, resulting in more precise diagnoses and enhanced efficiency. This paper examines key milestones at the intersection of quantum computing and the healthcare sector, highlighting advancements in drug development, processing data, and medical diagnostics that have led to improved patient outcomes. This paper examines challenges within the field, specifically focusing on data privacy and security, scaling, hardware constraints, and ethical considerations. This paper examines the potential for quality control in light of emerging innovations within the healthcare sector.

title: Interpretable CESM Channel Weighting: Policy-Guided Fusion of Low-Energy and Recombined Images for Microcalcification Detection
authors: Sugitha N, Ragul S, Nithaesh S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The detection of microcalcifications is a critical biomarker for early-stage breast cancer, yet differentiating benign from malignant clusters remains a significant diagnostic challenge. Contrast-Enhanced Spectral Mammography provides two key images: a Low-Energy image for structure and a Recombined image for contrast uptake. To leverage this data, we introduce a novel, interpretable deep learning framework, the Policy-Guided Fusion Network. This dual-encoder model uses a lightweight “policy module” to learn a dynamic attention map, which adaptively fuses the LE and S streams, making its reasoning transparent. The PGFN achieves a mean Dice Score of 0.815 and an AUC of 0.92, a statistically significant improvement over standard methods. The model's interpretable policy maps align with clinical findings, demonstrating its potential to enhance diagnostic confidence and reduce unnecessary biopsies.

title: Hybrid ML Models for Anticipating Glucose Fluctuations
authors: Harshita Sharma, Aakanksha Rajpal, Deepika Bishnoi
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Type 1 Diabetes (T1D) is a chronic medical condition that necessitates ongoing monitoring and control of blood glucose (BG) levels to prevent hazardous variations that can cause serious medical complications. This paper introduces a state-of-the-art machine learning (ML) methodology for precisely predicting future glucose trends, based on data gathered from the HUPA-UCM Diabetes dataset, which includes continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) readings, insulin doses, food consumption, and physical activity data. The research employs a systematic procedure with data preprocessing, feature engineering, and the creation of five hybrid machine learning models: an LSTM-XGBoost hybrid model, a GRU-XGBoost hybrid model, a Bi-LSTM-XGBoost hybrid model, a CNN-LSTM model, and a Transformer-GRU model. These models were tested against prominent measures such as Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), and the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}$</tex>-squared (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>) score. Experimental findings showed that the LSTM-XGBoost hybrid model had the lowest error rates across all models compared. This study showcases the high potential of hybrid machine learning models, especially the LSTM-XGBoost architecture, in facilitating real-time prediction of blood glucose, allowing proactive insulin control, and opening the door to more personalized diabetes treatment.

title: A LNA Optimization for Higher Gain and Low Noise Figure Using PSO Algorithm for IoT Applications
authors: Rahul Agarwal, Manish Kumar, Aasheesh Shukla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: With the rising demand for high-speed devices operating in ultra-wideband spectrum and 5G Networks, there is a need for low-power battery-operated devices. LNA, being the first block of the receiver chain, must be optimized. This research paper outlines that implementing the particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm improves LNA design. The recommended LNA design consists of two cascaded phases, and the initial stage of the circuit employs a current-reuse topology. Two transistors are cascaded in the subsequent phase to enhance the entire gain of the circuit. Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) balances the aspect ratio, all passive variables with voltage gain and NF, and basic parameters of interest at the target frequency. The proposed LNA circuit is simulated in a standard 45 nm CMOS process for a bandwidth of 1 GHz–20 GHz. Forward gain (S21) of 16.8 dB over the entire band, input return loss (S11) of -19.2 dB, and (NF)min of 1.7 dB were observed with a power consumption of 3.6mW. A process corner simulation analyzed the adaptability of the LNA, and the findings showed an approximate ten per cent deviation from the theoretical value. The chip footprint is about 3.52 mm2, with a core area of 645 µm by layout.

title: Presentation Attack Detection Using Fingerprint Image by Efficient Ensemble Stacking Method (EESM)
authors: Dharmendra Kaushik, Alok Kumar Singh Kushwaha, Vinay Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: The security of biometric systems can be compromised when characteristics of persons are presented in a deceptive manner. To maintain the confidentiality and integrity of sensitive data, fake fingerprint detection systems are employed. Individuals attempting to conceal their identity may use altered or damaged fingerprints, which are used in both legal system settings and huge level of authentication programs. To face this problem a lot of work is done in the field of fingerprint recognition system. By using ensemble learning algorithm, Random Forest, Extreme Gradient Boosting and CatBoost methods are ensembled as base models along with Random Forest as a meta model for Efficient Stacking Ensemble Method (EESM).The results show 65.53%, 91.05% and 94.11% accuracy for CustomFing SOCOFing and Clarkson2015Fing datasets respectively.

title: The Influence of Media Reporting on Public Perception of Justice: A Case Study on Sub-Judice Criminal Cases and the Need for Media Accountability
authors: Sornalakshmi R. R, Petikam Sailaja, Vishwanathan S, Nithish J. B, Prakalya M, Ajas Khan N
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Public opinion is greatly influenced by the media, especially when it comes to well-known criminal cases that are still pending—also known as sub judice situations. This study looks about the media coverage based on the impact of public views, with a particular emphasis on how biassed or early coverage might sway public opinion and jeopardise the fairness of court processes. The study illustrates how sensationalism, speculative narratives, and media trial impacted public perception and put excessive pressure on court decisions by examining certain case studies. The study assesses the harmony between the right to a fair trial and freedom of expression, as well as the moral limits and legal restrictions placed on media organisations. The study promotes more robust accountability systems, including more precise rules, prompt judicial actions, and institutional supervision, by leaning on jurisprudential theories, media ethics, and legal structures from different jurisdictions. The study emphasises how urgently competent journalism that protects the public's right to information while honouring the sanctity of current trials is needed. The study concludes by urging a reevaluation of media practices in order to guarantee with justice, legal system integration that carried out that are perceived to carry out free from the excessive impact of public opinion influenced by careless reporting.

title: Results and Analysis of Deep Neural Network Based Video Compression Algorithms
authors: Richa Verma, Umesh Pathak, Anand Kumar, Anand Ojha, Sadashiba Pati, Adarsh, Nitesh Kumar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: As Internet-scale video traffic grew almost exponentially during the previous few decades; significant improvements in video compression technologies were made. A lot of promise has been found for enhancing the effectiveness of the aforementioned functional blocks using artificial intelligence-driven approaches, both individually and jointly (AI). Next, we go over three in detail. Today's real-time multimedia applications demand acceptable bitrates and decent video quality. H. 264 coding offered cheap bitrate costs while maintaining premium video quality in comparison to previous coding methods, but it was constrained in its capacity to create higher qualities. High-Efficiency Video Coding (HEVC), which offers superior video quality at a more efficient bitrate, later improved H.264. Due to this advancement, using improved strategies for CTU partitioning, such as quad-tree, requires more expensive processing. This study suggests a method for CTU partitioning called CtuNet that substantially mimics its capabilities using deep learning techniques.

title: A ResNet Transformer Hybrid Approach for Enhanced Detection of Retinal Diseases
authors: Gouri Sharma, Vijay Shankar Sharma, Atul Srivastava, Sandeep Kumar Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid deep learning model that combines ResNet50 and Transformer encoder layers for multiclass classification of retinal diseases from fundus images. Using a publicly available dataset, we demonstrate that our architecture outperforms standard CNN baselines, achieving a test accuracy of 94.20%. The robustness of the model to class imbalance is enhanced by a boundary-aware focal loss. The proposed model shows consistent performance across all four categories of retinal diseases, with particularly high precision and recall for diabetic retinopathy and cataract. Notably, it achievesa glaucoma recall of 91.91%, addressing a common weakness in many existing models. Validation and testing metrics indicate strong generalization and minimal overfitting, supported by stable learning curves and class-wise evaluation. The model also demonstrates resilience to challenging cases near decision boundaries, improving diagnostic reliability in real-world scenarios. The proposed approach demonstrates that hybrid architectures can significantly enhance automated retinal disease screening by achieving high overall accuracy and balanced performance across multiple disease classes. This makes it a promising tool for sup- porting ophthalmologists in the early detection and classification of retinal disorders in real-world screening environments.

title: TinyEdge Based IoT-enabled Real Time Turbidity Monitoring System Using Image Processing Techniques
authors: Prashant Pandey, Rajan Mishra, R. K. Chauhan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This paper presents a novel TinyEdge-based IoT-enabled system for real-time turbidity monitoring using image processing techniques. The system is developed using an ESP32 microcontroller integrated with a 2MP camera module. Proposed system used standard turbid solutions ranging from 0 to 400 NTU for model development. A robust methodology involving controlled image acquisition, grayscale intensity analysis, and regression modeling is adopted to establish the relationship between image features and turbidity levels. The best grayscale intensity values from multiple image samples using a nearest-neighbor approach to improve accuracy. The processed data is used to estimate turbidity both locally on the edge device and remotely through the ThingSpeak IoT platform. Experimental results demonstrate high accuracy in turbidity prediction, with less than 10% error when validated against four standard test solutions. The proposed system offers a lowcost, scalable, and real-time solution for water quality monitoring in remote location.

title: IRIS 1 GW Superconducting Line: Phase Transition Analysis and Identification of Preliminary Requirements for the Protection System
authors: S. Maffezzoli Felis, M. Statera, L. Rossi, C. Santini
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: The transition from the superconducting to the normal state is a critical phenomenon with significant implications for the performance and reliability of superconducting systems. In this study, we employ COMSOL Multiphysics simulations to analyse the physical thresholds governing this transition for the IRIS Green Superconducting Line—a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$130\ \mathrm{m}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1\ \rm{GW}$</tex-math></inline-formula> magnesium diboride-based power transmission line operating at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$25\ \rm{kV}$</tex-math></inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$20\ \mathrm{K}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, with a direct current (DC) capacity of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$40 \ \rm{kA}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. The model integrates thermal, electrical, and magnetic interactions to provide an understanding of the progression of the transition. These simulations offer a predictive framework to optimise the diagnostic system design and operation, ensuring enhanced performance and safety. This paper will cover the simulation methodology, key findings, and their implications for the development of the protection system.

title: Over-the-Air Computation on Network Edge for Collaborative Estimation
authors: Vincent Huynh, Weiwei Wang, Zhi Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: Parameter estimation is an important task in distributed sensing systems, where data from multiple devices contributes to real-time decision-making. Over-the-air computation (AirComp) provides a new approach for spectrally efficient edge computing in collaborative parameter estimation. In this work, we propose a novel AirComp estimation framework based on the natural aggregation of pre-processed signals from participating agents in wireless multiple access channels. We focus on maximum likelihood estimation of source signal magnitude and time delay, proposing two approaches to complete this task. Our first approach involves a noisy approximation of the joint log-likelihood function (LLF) of agent observations. Our second approach employs noise-whitening to address the effect of pulse shaping. We present four channel compensation schemes to address destructive inter-agent channel interference and derive the corresponding Cramér-Rao lower bound (CRLB) in each scheme. We apply a practical partial phase pre-compensation that achieves nearly the same performance as complete phase compensation, thereby simplifying the conditions of the proposed AirComp estimation in practical deployment.

title: Reliability-Based Design and Control of Crystallization Systems Under Parametric and Process Uncertainties
authors: Yash G. Barhate, Zoltan K. Nagy
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: Population balance model-based approaches are widely employed in crystallization process design to meet the industry-specific critical quality attributes (CQAs). However, their reliability is often limited by parameter uncertainties arising from noisy experimental data, which if, unaccounted for, can lead to suboptimal or failed designs. This study introduces a novel reliability-based design optimization (RBDO) framework for open-loop design of crystallization systems under uncertainty. The framework formulates CQA constraints as probabilistic constraints with user-defined reliability thresholds, enabling quantified, risk-informed process design. To solve the resulting RBDO problems efficiently, a surrogate modeling-based, nested simulation-optimization workflow is developed. The proposed methodology is applied to both batch and continuous pharmaceutical crystallization systems, demonstrating its ability to generate robust design trajectories with significantly higher probabilities of meeting CQAs under both parametric and operational uncertainties compared to the deterministic designs. The computational efficiency and accuracy of the approach are further validated through benchmarking against back-mapping-based solutions of the underlying chance constrained formulations.

title: 6G SDVN: UAV-Assisted VANETs for AI-Enabled Controller Selection and Heterogeneous Multi-Band Connectivity
authors: Muhammad Rafid, Abid Afridi, Sara Sualiheen, Amjad Iqbal, Ihsan Ali, Adnan Akhunzada
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Road sparsity is a major cause of routing instability, leading to intermittent connectivity and increased packet loss. To enhance stability in 6G software-defined vehicular networks (SDVNs), we integrate unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) into a dynamic vehicular environment. We propose a novel ground-to-air communication protocol that integrates Dedicated Short Range Communication (DSRC), millimeter-wave (mmWave), and terahertz (THz) bands to address path stability, load balancing among controllers, and technology switching to accommodate diverse vehicular demands. Roadside Units (RSUs) and UAVs act as local controllers (LCs), with RSUs communicating via LTE and UAVs via DSRC. The optimal controller (OC) is selected using supervised learning algorithms, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Random Forest (RF), to ensure balanced load distribution and to manage stable routing across hybrid wireless technologies. The proposed scheme evaluates multiple UAV roles, including LC, relay, and destination, under different communication interfaces. Simulation results in a highway scenario demonstrate that the hybrid approach outperforms the reference DSRC, mmWave, and THz schemes in terms of packet delivery ratio, end-to-end delay, and routing overhead. Specifically, SVM-based OC selection achieved a maximum packet delivery ratio of 97.94%, with an end-to-end delay as low as 0.089 ms and minimal routing overhead, validating its effectiveness in mitigating road sparsity, improving connectivity, and enhancing network performance.

title: Scalable and Distributed Systems for High performance computing
authors: Deepti Singh, Kanika Singhal, Kirti Kushwah, Harshita, Madhav Bansal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Modern High-Performance Computing (HPC) relies heavily on scalable and distributed systems, which use parallelism across cloud, grid, and cluster infrastructures to efficiently execute computationally demanding tasks. In the context of HPC workloads, this paper provides a qualitative and quantitative performance analysis of the well-known distributed computing frameworks, including Dask, Ray, Apache Spark, and MPI. Using metrics like execution time, scalability, CPU utilization, memory overhead, and fault tolerance, we compare these frameworks on a range of workloads, such as big data analytics, AI-driven tasks, and scientific simulations. The study provides insights into their practical suitability for various HPC environments by examining architectural features, scheduling granularity, and resource efficiency in addition to raw performance. Scalable distributed systems are positioned as crucial enablers for fulfilling the increasing computational demands in research and industry, as the analysis highlights important trade-offs and new trends in hybrid computing.

title: Navigating the Competence Paradox: Student Engagement with AI, Ethics, and Educational Equity in Higher Education
authors: Ronel F. Ramos, Angelo C. Arguson, Ace C. Lagman, Alfredo L. Calimbo
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: This study examines the Competence Paradox in AIassisted learning. The students need digital and ethical competencies to understand how to use these AI tools, but they again use those same AI tools to develop such competencies. Using a sequential dominant mixed-methods approach, the research investigated how IT students engage with generative AI platforms such as ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini, and Claude. Quantitative findings from 200 participants uncover strong correlations between AI literacy and awareness of ethics, instructional support, and perceptions of AI's alignment with SDG4: Quality Education. Qualitative insights emphasize recurring tensions: confidence built through informal learning, uncertainty about ethical boundaries, inconsistent guidance provided by educators, and dependence on AI that may undermine deeper learning. The results confirm hypotheses that, though students are digitally capable, their responsible and equitable use of AI is wholly dependent on structured support and clear ethical frameworks. In addressing the Competence Paradox, this study contributes to the discourse on sustainable innovation in education and provides actionable implications for curriculum design and policy development.

title: ScribeLight for Enhancing Student Engagement and Comprehension of Video-Based Learning Using a Conversational AI System
authors: Sinansha Thayyil, Irfan Abdul Hameed
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: The proliferation of online video has resulted in the creation of vast educational libraries such as YouTube. Although a wide range of information is available, locating specific content within these videos remains a significant challenge for learners. The effort required to manually navigate long videos highlights the need for an efficient and practical tool to support educational use. To address this issue, we present ScribeLight, a web-based application that transforms video learning into an interactive process by enabling users to query video content in natural language and receive text-based answers. To evaluate the system's effectiveness, we conducted a within-subject study with 24 participants, comparing it with traditional manual viewing for information retrieval tasks. The mean task completion time with the manual method was 1180.3s, which was reduced to 581.8s using ScribeLight. This reduction was statistically significant (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{p}< 0.001$</tex>) and represented a very large effect size (Cohen's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{d}_{\mathrm{z}}=5.44$</tex>). The system also achieved a mean System Usability Scale (SUS) score of 81.2, corresponding to an excellent rating from participants.

title: Blockchain's Role in Redefining the Fintech Ecosystem: Current Trends and Future Prospects
authors: Karan Kumar, Manu Sharma, Smitha Kothari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: With its 2008 introduction with Bitcoin, blockchain technology has quickly grown beyond cryptocurrencies to become a game-changing force in financial technology (Fintech). Financial institutions and tech entrepreneurs are showing a great deal of interest in blockchain since it uses decentralized, distributed ledger systems to improve financial transactions in terms of operational efficiency, transparency, data security, and efficiency. This paper studies blockchain's increasing role within Fintech, bringing out its current usage and future potential. Smart contracts, crowdfunding, Know Your Customer (KYC) procedures, and safe cross-border payments are important Fintech blockchain uses. Because blockchain technology is decentralized, it eliminates the need for middlemen, increasing transaction efficiency and reducing expenses. Additionally, blockchain facilitates real-time audits and improves security through cryptographic protocols, both of which are essential for sustaining digital banking and lowering the danger of fraud. Depending on the use case, the study also looks into the various types of blockchain networks, each of which has unique benefits. As technology continues to grow, its impact on Fintech and banking sector is also set to rise and increase, bringing out innovation and contributing to sustainability in financial and banking services. The future of blockchain technology in the financial sector is robust, except a few challenges it poses such as privacy, data protection and environmental impact which require important focus. The study concludes with the fact that blockchain will remain an anchor pillar in designing the future of digital finance, shifting economies towards better efficiency and bringing transparency and sustainability.

title: AI Document Search Chatbot Using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
authors: Shaik Rafi, L. V. Siva Rama Krishna Boddu, Gali Lakshmi Pujitha, Sai Charitha Yella, Geethika Gangireddy, P Y Nishanth Bhuvaneswar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: This work describes the design and the implementation of an AI document search chatbot using a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture that combines generative modelling with information retrieval to provide an accurate and verifiable responses. The system supports PDF, DOCX, CSV, and TXT uploads, which are automatically processed, segmented, and indexed in a vector database for semantic search. It is implemented in Python using LangChain for orchestration, Facebook AI Similarity Search (FAISS) for vector retrieval, and multiple large-language-model Application Programming Interface (API), including OpenAI, Google Generative AI, and Hugging Face. For each query, embeddings are computed and the most relevant document segments are retrieved using cosine similarity before being passed to the selected model to ensure responses are grounded in user-supplied content. Owing to its modular design, the architecture is scalable, maintainable, and easily extensible for enterprise and academic knowledge-management applications.

title: Intelligent Soil Nutrient Management Using Ensemble Machine Learning for Site-Specific Fertilizer Recommendation
authors: Lourdeena J S, Graceline Jasmine S, Febin Daya J L
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Soil nutrient balance provide healthy crops and an efficient foundation for the agricultural wealth, where the fertility of the soil should be well maintained. This research evaluated the soil samples for various districts of Tamil Nadu using Machine learning (ML) classifiers to identify deficiency patterns in soil macronutrients like Nitrogen, Potassium, Phosphorous and soil micronutrient like Zinc, Iron, Manganese, Boron and copper. Ensemble Voting Classifier produced the best classification accuracy of 99% compared to the individual classifiers. The performance of the ML models were further evaluated using Grid Search cross-validation and stratified K- fold cross-validation and to prevent Class Imbalance and Overfitting. The SND observed can be treated with site-specific fertilizer recommendations based on the soil to improve soil health and productivity of crops. The scalability of this research framework allows for data-driven decision-making to improve the effective management of fertilizers and the promotion and implementation of Sustainable Agricultural Practices across a range of Agro-climatic Zones in India.

title: WELLUS: An Intelligent Dietary Management System Using OCR, Adaptive Monitoring, and Explainable AI
authors: Dhairya Singh, Pawan Kumar Goel, Anamika Verma, Lakshya Saxena, Naman Jain
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The WELLUS system introduces an integrated mobile health application that merges Optical Character Recognition (OCR), personalized AI-based guidance, a rule-based adaptive module, and Explainable AI (XAI) for dietary management. Unlike traditional food tracking apps that depend on human entry, WELLUS simplifies process by scanning food labels of packaged foods and quickly extracting nutritional information such as calories, proteins, macronutrient and sodium level. The fetched data is analysed in context of user’s health goals—weight control, general nutrition, or muscle gain and adjusts recommendations using rule-based mechanism that responds to recurring user patterns like high sodium intake or eating at late-night. To improve user trust, the system uses XAI methods providing clear numerical explanations of suggestions, to daily goals with a reasoning behind it. The system is implemented with open-source OCR tools (Tesseract/ML Kit), lightweight convex databases for user logs, and Firebase Authentication for secure access. Initial expectations show improved accuracy in tracking nutrition, reduced manual efforts, and higher user engagement compared with existing mHealth apps. By combining automation, personalization, and transparency, WELLUS demonstrates a scalable framework for offering a clear and accessible nutrition advice.

title: Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)

title: Applying the Use of an Energy Storage Device with Solar Energy Systems to Improve the Performance of the Electrical Energy System in Iraq
authors: Hayder J. Mohammed, Bishro H. Rasool
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: The whole world faces the challenge of overcoming the obstacles of the energy crisis. Declining stocks of non-renewable energy resources such as coal, natural gas, fossil fuels, etc., may exacerbate this concern. Thus, it is quite clear that there is a need to develop alternative and renewable energy sources. In the modern world, the issue of rational use of electrical resources concerns both consumers and electricity suppliers. Forming an algorithm to optimize the use of electrical energy storage allows solving the problem of autonomous system load management. The energy sector in Iraq is suffering from deterioration due to continuous power outages and a processing rate of less than 12 hours per day for consumers, with growing demand during peak hours, especially in times of hot summer and cold winter. Thus, there is an urgent need to propose faster solutions and add renewable energy, and also store it as an additional source to fill the energy deficit, as Iraq is characterized by the highest intensity of solar radiation throughout the year for various regions of the country, which reflects the efficiency of the work of solar energy stations in Iraq. The purpose of this paper is to increase the efficiency of the energy system of Iraq using energy storage devices and photovoltaic power systems to fill the shortfall in hours of electrical power supply as a result of the lack of production and the ever-increasing demand for electricity. To achieve this goal, it is necessary to develop and analyze a mathematical model of the object and operating modes of a photovoltaic system under different climate conditions throughout the year.

title: Design and Implementation of a Coordinated Protection Scheme for a Modern $220 / 500 \text{kV}$ Power Plant
authors: Khan Zarak Ali, Alexander G. Kalimov, Irfan Hamza, Khadim Muhammad Nadeem, Zaib Shah, Chanda Atish
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: The paper is concerned with the merging of 220 k V and 500 k V systems in the current power plants introduces complex paths of fault current that have complicated the traditional protection systems. Uncoordinated plans are dangerous in that they may cause delayed clearance of faults that may lead to cascading outages and damage of equipment. To deal with this, it was created a synergized protection plan based on ETAP modeling. The methodology involved the use of differential protection as the main protection mechanism on generators, transformers and busbars with overcurrent protection being well-calibrated to allow backup protection. The scheme was able to perform well in selective fault isolation by simulating different fault conditions. The basic protection concept uses primary differentiation relaxing on the important apparatus such as generators, block transformers, and busbars to be sure of clearance of internal faults instantaneously. The coordinated overcurrent relays always gave secure backup without unjustified running, and the internal faults in its zones always cleared immediately. This proven method has a great effect on increasing system reliability, reducing outage zone, and creating an overall system resistance to electrical failures, which provides consistent functionality of complex multi-voltage power systems.

title: Analysis of FinFET Based 10T SRAM Cell for IoT Based Devices
authors: Priya Chaudhary, V. K. Tomar
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: This study tackles SRAM model power usage restrictions by introducing FinFET technology into a 10 transistor SRAM cell structure. This cell obtains reduced leakage together with better write-stability and lower powerutilization from FinFET technology advantages. Simulations done using cadence Virtuoso tools show that using FinFET in SRAM cells makes them work better and more reliably. The technology represents a good option for developing memoryready next-generation low-power devices with compact designs. The results demonstrate that the FinFET-based 10T SRAM cell represents a practical solution as next-generation memory technology because it works effectively with high-density and low-power systems. This research expands memory architecture development by identifying the effective integration of FinFET devices for improved SRAM functionality.

title: Multimodal Underwater Object Detection and Segmentation Using MOUD (RGB + LiDAR) Point-Cloud Dataset
authors: D.Revathy, R.Swathika, N.Radha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Underwater image enhancement is critical for improving the performance of marine applications such as archeological exploration, autonomous navigation, surveillance, and underwater object detection. However, underwater images often suffer from severe degradation caused by wavelength-dependent light absorption, scattering, and artificial illumination, resulting in reduced contrast, color distortions, hazy appearance, and loss of structural details. While traditional enhancement methods such as histogram equalization and physical model–based correction—address basic distortions, modern deep learning techniques, including CNN- and GAN-based models, provide superior reconstruction capabilities through deep feature extraction. This paper aims to develop a hybrid enhancement framework that integrates classical underwater correction methods with advanced deep learning techniques (ADLT) to improve raw underwater RGB image quality. A specialized underwater-aware loss function will be designed to handle color imbalance, haze removal, and structural consistency. To ensure compatibility with modern multimodal systems, the enhanced RGB images will be aligned with the MOUD dataset, a multimodal RGB + LiDAR point-cloud benchmark used for underwater object detection and segmentation. Enhancing RGB images before multimodal fusion is expected to significantly improve the accuracy of object recognition, depth estimation, and segmentation tasks in challenging underwater environments. By combining conventional preprocessing, deep learning–based enhancement, and multimodal alignment with MOUD, the proposed approach is expected to deliver visually superior and analytically reliable underwater images that enhance the performance of real-time underwater robotics and sensing applications. The proposed ADLT method achieves 73% mAP, 67% mIoU, 31 PSNR, and 36 ms inference time, demonstrating superior detection, segmentation, enhancement quality, and real-time efficiency.

title: FIF-YOLO: Feature-Interactive Detection Framework for Traffic Signs in Complex Road Environments
authors: Hang Chen, Chunlin Liu, Minzhang Mei, Yu Liu, Shengfang Qiao, Haifeng Zhang, Yewen Huang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: Accurate Traffic Sign Detection and Recognition (TSDR) serves as the cornerstone for safe decision-making in autonomous driving perception systems. However, in real unstructured road scenarios, traffic signs are frequently challenged by severe scale variation and partial occlusion. Existing one-stage detectors based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are constrained by the inductive bias of local receptive fields, making it difficult to reconstruct the global semantic topology of occluded targets in the feature space, which leads to missed detection of long-range small targets and false detection of heavily occluded targets. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel detection architecture named FIFYOLO (Occlusion-aware Intra-scale Feature Interaction YOLO). Built upon the YOLOv8 baseline, this architecture innovatively integrates an Attention-guided Intra-scale Feature Interaction (AIFI) module and a SEmantic Attention-enhanced Detection Head (SEAM-Head). First, drawing on the design paradigm of Real-Time DETR, the AIFI module is embedded into the highlevel semantic feature extraction stage. Leveraging the MultiHead Self-Attention mechanism, the AIFI module can capture global long-range dependencies in the S5 feature layer, effectively suppress background noise and enhance semantic consistency, thus resolving the problem of detailed feature loss during downsampling in the traditional SPPF module. Second, to tackle feature deficiency caused by occlusion, we design a decoupled detection head integrated with SEAM. This module introduces an exponential attention activation function, which can adaptively amplify the feature response weights of occluded regions based on the contextual information of the visible parts of targets, achieving implicit feature completion. Experiments conducted on our self-constructed large-scale occlusion dataset demonstrate that FIF-YOLO achieves an mAP50 of 80.19%, representing an 8.06% improvement over the baseline model. Meanwhile, while maintaining real-time inference speed, it significantly improves the recall rate for extremely small targets and heavily occluded targets.

title: PSO and ACO-Optimized Cascade PI Control of DC-DC Buck-Boost Converter for High-Performance DC Machine Speed Regulation in Mecha-Electrical Wind Pumping Systems
authors: Abdelkader Elmeddah, Djalloul Achour
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: The reliable operation of Mecha-Electrical Wind Pumping Systems (MEWPS) requires accurate speed regulation of the DC machine. The quality of DC machine speed regulation depends directly on the controlling of bidirectional dc-dc converter. Cascade PI control strategy is one of the good control methods, but the difficulty with this type of control lies in tuning the PI parameters. To address this limitation, this work explores the designing of cascade PI controller for MEWPS and tuning its parameters using Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) algorithms. A comparative study is conducted to evaluate parameter convergence, cost function behavior, transient response, and standard performance indices including IAE, ISE, ITAE, overshoot, steady-state error, and settling time. The robustness of both methods is further analyzed under load torque variations. Simulation results demonstrated strength and weaknesses of each algorithm and which was better for controlling the speed of the DC machine.

title: Proceedings
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 7th International Communication Engineering and Cloud Computing Conference (CECCC)

title: Emphasizing the Importance of Storage in Solar PV System: A Case Study
authors: Subhash Chandra, Jai Prakash Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: Large-scale solar photovoltaic (PV) plants are being deployed worldwide. Unlike conventional systems, PV plants face challenges due to non-dispatchable power output, making energy storage technologies essential for enhancing reliability and continuous power delivery. This study models a 5.2 kW DC-AC standalone system powering a 5 kVA load, using PSIM simulations to compare system behavior with and without storage under varying solar irradiance levels (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.9,0.6$</tex>, and 0.3 sun). At 0.9 sun, 3.97 kW is delivered without the need for storage; performance ratio (PR) and capacity utilization factor (CUF) are 78% and 18%, respectively. At 0.6 sun, 3.68 kW is delivered, but PR and CUF drop to 72% and 16%, necessitating partial storage of 460 Ah. Under 0.3 sun, only 2.19 kW reaches the load, with PR at 55% and CUF at 10%, requiring 1183 Ah of storage. Notably, voltage and current total harmonic distortion (THD) increase when storage is absent. The study suggests a total storage capacity of 5228 Ah to support full-load operation for eight hours. Incorporating storage significantly enhances system performance, reliability, and operational stability under fluctuating solar conditions.

title: Predictive Modeling For Crop and Fertilizer Selection Based On Soil Characteristics
authors: Likhitha M, Revathi V, Girisha G S
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Inaccurate crop selection and fertilizer recommendation are one of the most important challenges faced in precision farming because bad decisions affect yield and efficient resource utilization. Herein, this work presents a machine learning–driven intelligent crop and fertilizer recommendation system using integrated data analytics into a real-time web-based deployment framework. The presented recommendation system makes use of several soil and environmental parameters, such as N, P, K, temperature, humidity, pH, and rainfall, to decide the best crop for the land and suggest appropriate fertilizers. After preprocessing the features, scaling them using Standard Scaling, and performing stratified train-test splits to maintain class balance, a Random Forest classifier produced an excellent test accuracy of 99.55%, with superb predictive reliability compared to traditional rule-based and manual decision-making approaches. In order to ensure practical usability of the trained model, it was serialized together with a scaler and a label encoder and then integrated into a simple Flask-based web application capable of providing real-time predictions through a user-friendly interface. Experimental results confirm that the proposed system provides an appropriate crop recommendation, effective analysis of nutrient deficiencies in the soil, and actionable fertilizer suggestion with low latency. This study showcases how integrating robust machine learning models with scalable web deployment can result in an efficient and accessible decision-support tool for sustainable and data-driven agricultural practices.

title: Experimental and Numerical Evaluation of the Thermal Performance of Parallel Tube Type Solar Collector (PLTSC) Under the Influence of MWCNT-Based Hybrid Nanofluid
authors: Kuwar Mausam
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: The thermal behavior of a parallel tube-type solar collector (PLTSC) by water and a hybrid nanofluid (Cu-MWCNTs) is investigated using experiments and numerical analysis in this study. XRD was used to characterize the nanoparticles. The experimental setting varied flow rates (0.3, 0.6, 1.2 LPM), inclination angle <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(25^{\circ}, 35^{\circ}, 45^{\circ})$</tex>, volume concentration (0.0% and 0.5%), and intensity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(500 \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m} 2)$</tex> for PLTSC thermal performance. Fluents <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5. 0}$</tex> created a 3D model of a flat plate collector. The SST turbulence model incorporated unpredictable temperature, velocity, and pressure fluctuations. The experimental results demonstrated an 80.07% increase in instantaneous efficiency at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 5 {\%}}$</tex> volume, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 2}$</tex> litres per minute, a 45° inclination angle, and an intensity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$500 \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex>. The hybrid nanofluid exhibited a maximum fluctuation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 5 {\%}}$</tex> between the output and intake temperature differentials (T), as estimated theoretically and empirically. The numerical instantaneous efficiency and heat gain exhibit a variance of 2.8% and 2.9% from the experimental values, respectively. Flat plate collectors using nanofluids cannot be observed for temperature and flow pattern under a set of operational parameters without a numerical simulation study.

title: Design of GNRFET-Based Quaternary Flip-Flops and Counters with Comparative Analysis against Ternary Multi-Valued Sequential Logic
authors: T. Kishore, M. Kiranmayee, T. Deepa, R. Deepthi Crestose Rebekah, O. Roopa Devi, P. Raga Chandrika
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: By encoding several logic levels on a single signal, multi-value logic (MVL) lowers interconnect complexity and increases information density. Compared to binary and ternary systems, quaternary logic (4 states) requires less wiring and storage because it can represent two binary bits per node. The tunable bandgap, robust electrostatic control, and high carrier mobility of graphene nanoribbon FETs (GNRFETs) make them ideal for MVL. By altering the ribbon width and device configuration, their threshold voltage can be engineered, allowing multi-threshold inverters and gates to function dependably at various voltage levels. This study suggests quaternary sequential circuits based on GNRFETs, including mod-4/mod-8 counters and a quaternary D flip-flop. Prior GNRFET ternary flip-flops/counters and binary reference circuits are compared to the quaternary designs, which are constructed using multi-threshold inverters and MVL transmission gates. A nanoscale GNRFET device model is used to simulate each design in HSPICE. Average power, propagation delay, and power-delay product (PDP) are examples of metrics. We also compare how many interconnect lines and flip-flops are needed for a given modulus. According to the results, quaternary counters support quaternary GNRFET sequential logic for low-power, high-density nanoelectronic systems by delivering normalized PDP per bit that is either better or comparable while requiring fewer flip-flops and wiring.

title: Real Time Deepfake Detection in Live Video Streams
authors: Sourabh S Hegde, Vivek Tutika, Lenish Pramiee, V N Manju
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: With the emerging threat of realistic deepfakes, distinguishing between real and fake has become a formidable challenge. Although there have been attempts made in solving this persisting issue, not many have been able to fill the existing gaps of previous models. Using a hybrid architecture integrating MobileNetV2 for efficient feature extraction coupled with a Tiny Vision Transformer (TinyViT) for enhanced temporal-spatial representation, the system accurately distinguishes between authentic (real) and manipulated or synthetic facial inputs with minimal latency. Experimental evaluations on benchmark datasets and custom-real time live feeds demonstrate high accuracy (98.3%) and low false-positive rates, making the model suitable for deployment in virtual conferencing, streaming, and other content moderation environments. With a wide range of applications, from online job interviews to online school examinations, this model can be targeted to catching impersonators using deepfake face-swapping techniques by identification via detection.

title: BAG-HRF: A Bayesian Attack Graph-Based Honeypoint Recommendation Framework
authors: Chenguang Wang, Rui Wang, Xin Deng, Xiang Yu, Mohan Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: Traditional static defense mechanisms are increasingly proving inadequate against modern cyber threats. While BAGs introduced probabilistic analysis, they struggle to optimize defenses across multiple high-risk paths. This paper proposes the BAG-HRF. A primary innovation of this framework is the integration of a total network risk (TNR) metric, which balances attack path probabilities with core asset values. The framework uses a hybrid CVSS/EPSS model for accurate exploit probability calculation and a greedy marginal risk reduction algorithm to find optimal honeypoint placements under a budget. Validation within a representative airport network environment demonstrates that BAG-HRF prioritizes strategic pivot points-such as the Airport Operational System-distinct from human experts' focus on immediate targets. Furthermore, operational deployment confirmed the framework's efficacy in successfully capturing and tracing active attacker sources.

title: Artificial Intelligence for Transgender Community: En route to a Mental Health Support System
authors: Jessy K Jayanth, Asha Sundaram, B. Lavaraju
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: A person's emotional, mental, and the well-being of society are all represented in their mental condition. It affects a person's feelings, ideas, and actions. At each phase of living, from birth and teenage to maturity, mental health is crucial. There is a significant difference in mental wellness disorders between the transgender group in comparison with general community. The use of broad language models (BLM)-powered artificial intelligence (AI) chatbots as a mental health support system (MHSS) is growing within the transgender population. The ability of these AI chatbots to effectively and securely offer specialized assistance for transgender community hasn't been thoroughly investigated, though. This study suggests using a variety of machine learning (ML) methods to assess the mental health status of an intended community. Initially, unsupervised learning methods were used on the target group's replies to the survey. By calculating the Mean Opinion Assessment, the tags derived from clustering were verified. Classification algorithms have been developed using these cluster tags to predict a person's mental health. Because of a lack of support in the real world, transgender people depended on these AI chatbots for MHSS. Interestingly, whereas BLMs provide timely assistance, they usually fail to understand the subtleties of Transgender-based difficulties. While modifying BLMs to better meet the requirements of transgender individuals may be a positive step, it is not a cure-all. The underlying problem is rooted in inequality in society. As a result, future study focuses on comprehensive approaches that address and mitigate the cultural prejudices that the transgender population faces, rather than just technological improvements.

title: Advanced MSOGI Virtual Impedance Control for Power-Sharing Improvement in Standalone Microgrids
authors: Camelia Ait Hammouda, Rafik Bradai, Rachid Boukenoui, Abdelhammid Kherbachi, Ahmed Bendib
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: The droop control strategy is necessary for the gridforming operation of Microgrid (MG) to control the power-sharing among parallel-connected Distributed Generations (DGs). However, the main drawback of this control strategy is the poor sharing of the reactive power due to the line impedance mismatch. To address this limitation, a Virtual Impedance (VI) scheme is introduced to reduce the impact of impedance mismatches and consequently enhances the accuracy of reactive power sharing. Nevertheless, the sharing of harmonics under nonlinear load operation is the major problem of the existing virtual impedance-based control strategies. This paper proposes an advanced virtual control scheme for improving the reactive power and harmonics sharing among three-phase Voltage Source Inverters (VSIs) forming a standalone MG. The proposed control scheme is designed based on a Multiple Second-Order Generalized Integrator (MSOGI) method, which is introduced to provide accurate estimation of the current harmonics in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha \beta$</tex> frame. Therefore, an improvement of the active power, reactive power, and harmonics sharing is expected. To validate the effectiveness of the developed primary control structure using VI achieves reliable power-sharing performance, a testbed consisting of three DGs-based MG is simulated in MATLAB/Simulink. The results prove the effectiveness of the proposed control scheme for ensuring accurate active and reactive power sharing under linear and nonlinear loads operation.

title: Characterization of PMU Delay Time: Standard Approaches and On-Line Metric Validation
authors: Guglielmo Frigo, Zhiwang Feng, Pedro Barbalho, Yljon Seferi, Graeme Burt
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: In modern power systems, monitoring and control applications are subject to stringent requirements in terms of responsiveness and accuracy. The reference technology in this sense is represented by the Phasor Measurement Units (PMUs) which produce time-stamped measurements of synchrophasor, frequency, and rate-of-change-of-frequency. The delay introduced by different PMU acquisition and processing stages can be characterized in laboratory controlled conditions as per the relevant standards. This information, however, is hard to be integrated into online routines without prior knowledge of the system operating condition. To address this issue, this paper presents a PMU prototype which implements an online trust-worthiness metric, which can be used to preliminarily assess the measurement accuracy and track the settling time due to external disturbances. The results confirm the robustness of the proposed approach and its potential applicability to an emerging use case involving the online estimation of power system inertia level.

title: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
