title: Smart Manufacturing with Oracle Cloud: AI, Compliance and Global ERP Integration
authors: Rajeev Vinodkumar Rungta, Rashmi Bharathan, Manoj Srivastava
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: It is an empirical study about the opportunities of integrating machine learning (ML) into the Oracle Cloud-based smart manufacturing systems and its implications to the functioning efficiency, compliance with regulations, and interoperability of the global ERP. The research utilized a mixed-methodology because it analyzed the data of 4 multinational production firms that adopted the Oracle manufacturing cloud, Oracle integration cloud, and OCI machine learning services. The ML models that consist of predictive maintenance, demand forecasting and quality anomaly detection were trained on six months of historical production data to be tested within 6 months of operation. The results suggest that the time that had been saved was by a considerable margin (21%), forecast accuracy was better (17%), inventory optimization (26%), and accuracy was better in compliance reporting (98%). The distributed cloud system and embedded AI capabilities at Oracle served to offer the simple data integration of the ERP systems without compromising the ISO 9001 and GDPR-compliant data integration. The paper concludes with the conclusion that the AI-ML system of the Oracle Cloud can be employed to offer scalable real-time analytics, operations automation, and global compliance synchronization in Industry 4.0 systems. This analysis can be extended to future research to support reinforcement learning based adaptive manufacturing and cross industry benchmarking.

title: SAM: A Lightweight Static Attention Module for Kidney Stone Detection in CT Images
authors: Shukai Ding, Nirattaya Khamsemanan, Cholwich Nattee
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: Kidney stone disease is a common and painful condition that requires early and accurate diagnosis. Computed tomography (CT) is the preferred imaging tool, but the large image size and abundant background tissue make automatic detection challenging. Existing deep learning methods often rely on manual region-of-interest (ROI) cropping or complex multistep pipelines, which are labor-intensive and difficult to generalize. In this work, we propose the Static Attention Module (SAM), a plug-and-play component that learns dataset-level spatial priors at the input stage. SAM emphasizes kidney regions and suppresses irrelevant background without requiring expert annotations or incurring measurable computational cost. The experiments on the Yildirim CT dataset demonstrate that SAM consistently improves the accuracy of the four CNN backbones, with ResNet50 + SAM achieving 99.42%, thereby surpassing previously state-of-the-art methods. Further visualizations demonstrate that SAM directs networks toward clinically meaningful regions. These results indicate that SAM is an efficient and practical method for lightweight yet interpretable detection of kidney stones.

title: Artificial intelligence in plant disease detection: A systematic and comprehensive survey
authors: Ubaid Mubashir Wani, Sumit Kumar, Surbhi Vijh, Kartik Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Plant leaf disease detection has emerged as a critical application of artificial intelligence in precision agriculture, driven by the need for scalable, accurate, and real-time diagnostic systems. This study presents a comprehensive synthesis of recent deep learning approaches, encompassing CNNs, Vision Transformers (ViTs), attention mechanisms, ensemble learning, and self-supervised frameworks. Benchmarking across diverse datasets—including PlantVillage and hyperspectral sources—reveals classification accuracies ranging from 94.3 to 99.4 percent, with ViT and hybrid CNN-ViT models consistently outperforming traditional architectures. Innovations such as GAN-based augmentation, leaf vein segmentation via U-Net, and preprocessing techniques like CLAHE significantly enhance model robustness and generalization. Furthermore, deployment-oriented strategies including YOLOv5 for real-time detection, EfficientNet for transfer learning, and federated learning for privacy-preserving collaboration demonstrate practical viability. The integration of graph neural networks and multi-modal fusion further advances spatial reasoning and spectral sensitivity. This meta-analysis underscores the importance of reproducible benchmarking, adaptive feature refinement, and lightweight architectures for field deployment, offering a rigorous foundation for future research in AI-driven plant pathology.

title: Scoring-Based Cross-Disciplinary Admission System with Knowledge Graph Planning
authors: Yingying Huang, Feng Wang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: This paper presents a novel scoring-based admission framework for cross-disciplinary programs in the domain of higher education institutions, addressing the growing demand for interdisciplinary talents. Our system enables students to select micro-credentials upon completing foundational coursework, guided by personal interests and career aspirations. Admission decisions are determined through a scoring mechanism that quantitatively evaluates each student's choice using multi-dimensional criteria, including academic performance, favorability level and market trend. Post-admission, the system generates personalized knowledge graphs that visualize optimal curriculum pathways and future specialization trajectories. The core innovations of the system are the scoring mechanism, admission algorithms and the knowledge graph planning, ensuring transparent and merit-based allocation of limited seats. The system delivers the following benefits. For students, it enables informed selection of suitable cross-disciplinary specializations while providing structured academic road maps. For higher education institutions, it optimizes talent matching through datadriven admission decisions. The results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieved a 95.5% program fulfillment rate, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$75.0{\%}$</tex> securing first-choice placements.

title: An Energy-Aware Hybrid Adaptive Preprocessing Framework for Attention-Guided Histopathological Cancer Detection
authors: Kanishk Agrawal, Javed Wasim, Raghav Mehra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The demand for histopathological cancer diagnosis to be reproducible and high-quality spurs the need for preprocessing frameworks that increase image interpretability and optimize computational cost. However, current algorithmic approaches are mainly limited by noise sensitivity, staining variability, and energy load. To that end, we present the Energy Aware Hybrid Adaptive Pre-processing Framework for cancer detection in histopathological images (HAPA-D) by using hybrid adaptive contrast, attention guided denoising, and staining normalization, and optimizing for energy efficiency while maintaining quality detection of cancer. As an early step, the input images for HAPA-D methods were collected from the CHAVI dataset. The input histopathological images are pre-processed as per the proposed HAPA-D pipeline to improve interpretability and computational costs through incorporating multiple preprocessing and optimization module into a framework. Adaptive Contrast Enhancement (ACE) enhances local tissue contrast, with Attention-Guided Denoising (AGD) selectively removing noise and retaining significant diagnostic regions. A Stain Normalization Module (SNM) addresses inter-slide discoloration by making sure datasets are as similar a color as possible. Energy-Aware Feature Optimization (EAFO) helps improve energy efficiency by removing redundant features and retaining clinically necessary diagnostic information. The features optimized using EAFO are then classified using the Efficient Focal Attention-Based Extreme Learning Classifier (EFA-ELC), which offers multi-class cancer diagnosis with accuracy and reliability. All techniques are implemented in a Python framework and are compared with the many existing methods based on their accuracy (99.4%) and energy consumption (45J). In conclusion, EFA-ELC provides accurate, reliable, and computationally sustainable multi-class cancer detection from histopathological images.

title: ACA-Net: A Modified YOLOv11 Network for Accurate Wildlife Detection
authors: Shaode Yu, Xuemin Ren, Qiurui Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: Wildlife detection is challenging due to various sizes, distance variability, and appearance similarity. To enhance the wildlife detection performance, the YOLOv11 network is updated with an ADown module in the trunk and neck network for efficient sampling of feature maps, a CBAM attention module at the feature fusion endpoint to focus on salient regions, and an ASFFHead module in the detection head for adaptive multi-scale fusion. Consequently, ACA-net, a modified YOLOv11 network, is formed. Extensive experiments on the NTLNP database show that the ACA-net achieves increase on the mAP@0.5(2.6% ↑) and Recall <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(4.4 {\%} \uparrow)$</tex> metrics compared to the baseline architecture. Network upgrading with efficient modules could further improve the baseline performance and benefit wildlife detection.

title: Mitigating Artificial Intelligence Hallucinations in Education: A Comparative Study of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and Large Language Models
authors: Pei-Hua Chen, Yueh-Min Huang, Ting-Ting Wu, Hsin-Yu Lee
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into educational technologies promises to revolutionize personalized learning. However, their propensity for “hallucination,” the generation of factually incorrect or nonsensical information, poses a significant risk to knowledge integrity and student trust. To address this critical challenge, this paper investigates Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), an architectural approach that mitigates hallucinations by grounding the model's responses in factual data. Before generating an answer, the RAG framework retrieves relevant information from a verified knowledge base, ensuring the output is contextually accurate. We conducted a comparative study between a standard LLM and a RAG-powered system whose knowledge base was populated with specific curriculum materials. The generated answers to domain-specific questions were assessed through two distinct lenses: a quantitative evaluation of factual accuracy by subject matter experts and a qualitative analysis of student perceptions gathered through semi-structured interviews. Our preliminary findings indicate that the RAG model demonstrates a marked improvement in factual accuracy and a significant reduction in hallucinatory content. Furthermore, students perceived the RAG-generated responses as more trustworthy and useful for their learning. This research provides empirical evidence for the necessity of adopting RAG in educational AI, offering a pathway toward developing more reliable and effective digital learning tools.

title: Optimizing VGG-16 for Early Lung Cancer Detection: A Deep Learning Approach with Enhanced Interpretability
authors: Aadrika Awasthi, Shiv Kumar Verma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Lung cancer continues to be a major cause of death from cancer globally, and late presentation remains a cause for low survival. Screening is hence vital, but established methods of diagnosis, including biopsy and human-driven radiological interpretation, are slow, expensive, and vulnerable to human fault. Recent developments in deep learning and artificial intelligence hold promise for accurate and efficient computerized analysis of medical images.Here, a transfer learning technique from the VGG-16 convolutional neural network (CNN) is utilized for the classification of images from CT scans of the lungs as benign or malignant. Preprocessing was done for the dataset via resizing, normalization, and augmentation for enhanced generalizability. Pre-trained VGG-16 was fine-tuned for the target set of data with adjustment of the fully connected layers for binary classification. Training was carried out for optimization via the Adam optimizer along with regularization methods for minimizing overfitting.Model performance was measured via Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score and ROC-AUC with a marked improvement being realized after optimization. Pre- and post-optimization confusion matrices are readily observable to indicate the reduction of misclassifications, particularly false negatives, critical in detecting cancers. Findings indicate the prospect of VGG-16 transfer learning in the classification of medical images and as a supplement in diagnosis decision-making. Limits in data quantity and generalizability are highlighted, with future studies to focus on larger and diversified data and Clinical Verification.

title: Apulian Geo-Spatial Data Clustering and Analysis
authors: Antonio Bellanova, Cristina Cici, Michele Gargano, Michele Gramegna, Gaetano Grasso, Giuseppe Pirlo, Gianfranco Semeraro
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The analysis of spatial data has acquired a fundamental importance in contemporary decision-making in various fields: from governance to administration to emergency management. This paper highlights the efforts to analyse the geo-spatial environmental data of Apulia, a south-eastern Italian region, in collaboration with the technical working group of InnovaPuglia S.P.A., which provided the public regional geospatial data and the necessary computing power to manage the mass of data. The work presented is the last step in a long journey that has led the work to be based on the use of UMAP to reduce the dimensionality of the data and on the use of BIRCH to clustering the enormous amount of data. Indeed, the amount of data available was enormous considering the detail obtained with a grid of 100×100 metre cells. The results reveal distinct spatial clusters within the region, each characterised by unique environmental conditions, which were recognised as crucial by the experts' analysis. In addition, the work provides a cluster analysis through the evaluation of metrics such as Silhoutte Score, CHI and DBI.

title: Detecting Student Depression Using Non-Clinical Measures with Explainable Predictive Modeling
authors: Akshat Kotnala, Nidhi Arora, Shilpa Srivastava
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Depression among students is a serious global mental health concern, affecting academic performance, emotional wellbeing, and long-term development. While traditional diagnostic tools like self-reported questionnaires and clinical interviews are useful, they often suffer from subjectivity, recall bias, and limited scalability. This study introduces a data driven, interpretable machine learning approach to predict student depression using both academic and nonacademic factors, without relying on clinical indicators. The dataset comprises student information on demographics, academic workload, lifestyle habits, social interactions, financial stress, and emotional state. Following thorough preprocessing including handling missing values, encoding variables, correlation-based feature reduction, and SMOTE to address class imbalance, ten supervised machine learning models were trained and assessed. Among them, a SMOTE enhanced XGBoost model achieved the highest test ROC AUC score of 0.95. To maintain transparency, SHAP (Shapley Additive Explanations) was employed to interpret the model’s predictions, highlighting key risk factors such as academic pressure, poor sleep quality, financial difficulties, and low social support. These findings can help guide early interventions and build trust with stakeholders. Future work may involve incorporating longitudinal and multimodal data, deploying real time solutions in educational settings, and addressing ethical considerations around privacy, fairness, and consent in AI based mental health systems.

title: Plant Disease Detection Using CNN
authors: Sonu Agrawal, Abhishek Danu, Vinay Prasad Tamta, Shivam Bahuguna, Kanchan Naithani, Shrikant Tiwari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Crop disease is one of the major challenges smallholder farmers face regarding diseases in crops, which have devastatingly affected crop yield as well as income generation. However, the widespread utilization of smartphones and the advancement of computer vision present major opportunities for improving agricultural practice. This paper explores whether convolutional neural networks have the potential to identify plant diseases using image analysis. We present a new approach based on using a pre-trained ResNet34 adapted model for leaf image classification and symptom identification from several diseases. This model is presented in a web application format such that one can input images of leaves and predict their likelihood of being healthy or afflicted by one of the seven common diseases. A training dataset of 8,685 images of captured leaves in a controlled environment is used. The validation results were impressively high with a classification accuracy of 97.2% and an F1 score of 96.5%. The result reflects the practicality and cost-effectiveness of AI-driven disease detection systems for smallholder farmers to monitor and manage crop diseases efficiently.

title: 2025 ICECIT Review Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)

title: Bias Detection and Mitigation in Datasets and Language Models
authors: Nikita Bhedasgaonkar, Kanan Shah, Shraddha Dolas, Ayush Chhetry, Dev Gupta, Anjali Deshpande
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Many Natural Language Processing (NLP) activities, including text generation and classification, have been greatly simplified by the development of language models. These models have shortcomings despite their increasing usefulness and popularity, with bias becoming among the most urgent issues. This study uses advanced techniques including Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Fairlearn, and AIF360 to investigate the existence of bias within AI systems, namely language models. It looks at transformer-based models like BERT, DistilBERT, RoBERTa, and XLM-RoBERTa as well as datasets. The study uses fairness criteria such as Equalized Odds Difference (EOD) and Demographic Parity Difference (DPD) to assess the performance of Fairlearn and AIF360 across various machine learning methods. Results show discernible biases, particularly genderrelated ones, and suggest a way to reduce these biases in encoderdriven language models. The study emphasizes how crucial it is to recognize and deal with bias in various NLP model designs in order to advance justice and moral AI development.

title: Hybrid GAN–Diffusion and Lesion-Aware Generative Models for Crop Disease Classification in Precision Agriculture
authors: P. M. G. Jegathambal, J Madhavan, K M Monica, B Gayathri, Z. Justin, Deepa Simon
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Crop diseases are an ongoing threat to global food production, and advancements made in automated diagnosis are limited due to the public image datasets being sparse and disorganized. Applications of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to training set augmentation mostly neglect important lesion characteristics or produce images that focus on idealized laboratory conditions, and therefore, miss capturing conditions in the field. To address this gap, we created a hybrid framework that combines conditional GANs, lesion-aware synthesis, diffusion-based refinement, and Cycle GAN-driven domain adaptation. This framework was validated on Plant Village and the New Plant Disease dataset for tomato late blight and grape Esca. We evaluated image quality using SSIM, FID, and LPIPS metrics. Classification performance was evaluated using CNN and lightweight models, Mobile Net and Efficient Net. The pipeline showed a 7.2% increase in recall and 6.6% increase in F1-score compared to standard augmentation, specifically targeting the under sampled disease class, where augmentation impact was most significant. All figures are reproducible given the five independent iterations per dataset. These were executed on an NVIDIA RTX 3090 (24 GB) GPU, with PyTorch 2.1. The additional computational burden that diffusion refinement entails and the dependence on the quality of segmentation in lesion-aware synthesis do not overshadow the framework’s enhancement in reliability and robustness, nor the fully operational pathway toward edge-ready solutions in precision agriculture.

title: Compression and Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models
authors: Josephine Prem Kumar, R Abhiram, V Madhan Kumar, Vishnu Ramchandra Naik
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) such as GPT, LLaMA, and BERT deliver state-of-the-art performance but require high memory and computation, limiting use on resource-constrained devices. This paper presents a unified compression and fine-tuning pipeline integrating ultra-low-bit quantization (3-bit/4-bit) using BitsAndBytes, Activation-Wise Quantization (AWQ), and Quantized Low-Rank Adaptation (QLoRA). A 14B-parameter LLM is compressed to 8–10 GB, fine-tuned on downstream tasks, and evaluated on GSM8K, OpenBookQA, and SQuAD. The system achieves up to 75% model size reduction, 2× faster inference, and 2% accuracy drop. Stability limits in 3-bit quantization and fairness effects on StereoSet are also analyzed. The results show that hybrid quantization–fine-tuning enables efficient, high-performance LLM deployment on edge devices with minimal accuracy loss.

title: UPDet: Learning-based darting-out pedestrian detection by ultrasonic sensors
authors: Yingying Wang, Chenyu Jin, Hu Cheng
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Ultrasonic sensors are among the critical components in vehicular applications, primarily due to their cost-effectiveness, which is also the main constraint limiting the performance of vehicle-embedded ultrasonic sensors, particularly in detecting dynamic objects. This paper introduces UPDet, a benchmark system for detecting and localizing pedestrians darting out in front of vehicles using only low-cost ultrasonic sensors. The system embeds a novel prototype system for simultaneous signal collection of raw ultrasonic echoes and automatic labeling sensor data, including RGB images and 3D LiDAR point clouds. During the offline signal processing phase, the 3D positions of pedestrians, detected from the fusion of RGB and LiDAR data, are used to annotate the echo data captured by the ultrasonic sensors. Various models that are constructed by 2D and 1D layers with specially designed attention mechanisms are trained and evaluated. More than 13 hours of synchronized sensor data in urban environments are collected. The trained model achieved an 82.28% detection accuracy on the split test set. The high detection performance of the UPDet system, when embedded in the NVIDIA Xavier platform, further demonstrates the substantial potential of ultrasonic-based sensory perception for enhancing automotive safety.

title: Overhead-Aware Adaptive RIS Subarray Grouping for Uplink Random Access in Cellular Networks
authors: Yizhu Wang, Zhou Zhang, Saman Atapattu, Marco Di Renzo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: This paper investigates uplink reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-assisted massive random access (RA) in multiuser cellular networks with heterogeneous user distances. The goal is to enhance media access control (MAC)-layer throughput under practical signaling and channel constraints. Conventional RIS configurations using full-array probing incur excessive overhead, especially with large numbers of reflective elements (REs). To address this, we propose an overhead-aware, multi-phase uplink RA framework that integrates adaptive RIS subarray grouping with scalable multiuser scheduling for dynamic user activity. The access optimization is formulated as a sequential decision process, from which an optimal threshold-based policy with closed-form structure is derived. Building on this, an opportunistic RA algorithm is developed to jointly optimize user scheduling, subarray configuration, and access timing with low online complexity that scales with RIS resolution, subchannel count, and user density. Numerical simulations, supported by analytical results, demonstrate significant throughput gains over existing schemes—including full-array, fixed-grouping, and both orthogonal and non-orthogonal multiple access methods—across varying coherence times, RIS sizes, and user distributions. The robustness of the proposed strategy is further verified under imperfect CSI conditions, confirming its suitability for large-scale, dynamic cellular deployments. The proposed framework is further shown to remain effective under practical impairments such as spatial correlation and finite-resolution RIS phase control.

title: Mem-ADPE: A Footprint-based Memory Prediction Method for Anomaly Detection and Prefetching in Consumer Electronics
authors: Jinting Ren, Hengyi Ren, Runyu Zhang, Chaoshu Yang, Thippa Reddy Gadekallu, Mohammad Shabaz, Zhaoyang Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: The rise of smart technologies has subjected consumer electronic devices to dual pressures: the need for optimized performance and robust security. Traditional memory access models often fail to accurately capture complex spatio-temporal memory locality, which simultaneously limits the efficiency of data prefetching and the reliability of security baselines. This paper proposes Mem-ADPE (A Footprint-based Memory Prediction Method for Anomaly Detection and Prefetching in Consumer Electronics), a unified framework designed to enhance both the security and efficiency of consumer electronic systems. Mem-ADPE innovatively represents memory access history as a 2D “footprint” image, utilizing a lightweight Micro-UNet deep convolutional network to achieve high-fidelity prediction of future cache line accesses. By establishing this single prediction result as the shared baseline for normal behavior, Mem-ADPE can effectively perform Anomaly Detection (by flagging significant structural deviations from the predicted footprint) and Data Prefetching (by accurately capturing complex spatial locality). This “one-image, dual-purpose” approach provides proactive security defense and optimized performance management for next-generation intelligent consumer electronic systems.

title: Gain Assessment of Environmental-Informed Subarray Processing: The ALMA 2017 Example
authors: Alexandre L’Her, Angélique Drémeau, Florent Le Courtois, Gaultier Real, Xavier Cristol, Yann Stéphan
publish: IEEE Journal of Oceanic Engineering
abstract: Underwater acoustic propagation can be affected by ocean fluctuations. In particular, internal waves can reduce the spatial coherence of the acoustic signal on SONAR arrays, causing performance degradation. Subarray processing is a popular method, used to mitigate the performance loss caused by environmental fluctuations. The problem lies in the correct parameterization of the length of the subarrays, itself linked to the acoustic coherence length, with the latter influenced by the fluctuations of the environment. In this work, we propose the use of a linear model, derived from canonical correlation analysis (CCA), to configure the length of the subarrays according to statistics of acoustic and environmental measurements, in the context of direction-of-arrival estimation. To compensate for the loss of angular resolution inherent to the combination of subarrays using conventional beamforming, we propose to consider a sparse regularization, solved using SoBaP, a Bayesian algorithm. We show on at-sea data from the 2017 ALMA campaign that regularization compensates for some of the resolution loss due to the use of the subarrays. In addition, detection performance is increased when using the regularized subarray scheme, particularly when acoustic coherence is low. Finally, using the CCA-informed scheme shows better detection performance than the noninformed schemes for low coherence and low signal-to-noise ratio, demonstrating the benefits of informing array-processing algorithms with environmental statistics.

title: Experimental Analysis of a Self-Coherent M-QAM Receiver by Means of Recurrent Optical Spectrum Slicing and Direct Detection
authors: Kostas Sozos, Francesco Da Ros, Metodi Yankov, Stavros Deligiannidis, George Sarantoglou, Charis Mesaritakis, Adonis Bogris
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: High order modulation formats constitute the most prominent way for increasing spectral efficiency in transmission systems. Coherent transceivers that support such higher order formats require heavy digital signal processing (DSP), which increases the power consumption of coherent pluggables, well above the intensity modulation and direct detection (IM/DD) counterparts. Self-coherent or phase retrieval methods have emerged as potential solutions, trying to combine the merits of coherent technology with the simplicity of direct detection. In this work, we experimentally demonstrate the reception of quadrature amplitude modulation (QAM) modulation formats based on direct detection aided by the recurrent optical spectrum slicing (ROSS) photonic accelerator, utilizing minimal DSP and low modulator driving voltages. We experimentally demonstrate 32 Gbaud QAM-4/16 for 25 km, 50 km and 75 km in the C-band aided by a linear digital equalization and the use of programmable photonics as recurrent optical spectrum slicers. We showcase successful detection with driving swings below Vπ/3 in contrast to the full swing required by conventional coherent transceivers. We further improve the system performance utilizing geometric constellation shaping. Finally, we explore the potential power consumption improvement for the next-generation 1.6T pluggables, showcasing over 40% reduction with respect to the most lightweight state of the art coherent solutions reported in literature.

title: Proofreading and Editing in Student and Research Publication Contexts: International Perspectives: Nigel Harwood: [Book Review]
authors: Cooper Smitherman
publish: IEEE Transactions on Professional Communication
abstract: Presents reviews for the following list of books, Proofreading and Editing in Student and Research Publication Contexts: International Perspectives.

title: Quantum-Enhanced Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces with Hierarchical AI Orchestration for Secure 6G Networks
authors: Ali Ali, Demos Serghiou, Anton Tishchenko, Pei Xiao, Mohsen Khalily
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The forthcoming 6G wireless systems must simultaneously achieve extreme capacity, ultra-low latency, and quantum-grade security. While classical physical-layer security techniques provide probabilistic protection, free-space quantum key distribution (QKD) suffers degradation under mobile and turbulent conditions. This paper introduces a Quantum-Enhanced Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (QE-RIS) framework that synergistically integrates quantum photonics with classical electromagnetic control via hierarchical AI orchestration. Our design embeds quantum-capable devices into RIS unit cells operating at 12.5GHz (RF) and 850nm (optical) wavelengths and employs a dual-loop control mechanism, a fast loop at 5 ms and a mid loop at 50 ms, to dynamically select classical, quantum, or hybrid modes based on real-time signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and quantum bit error rate (QBER). Monte Carlo simulations with 20 independent channel realisations demonstrate SNR gains up to 29.3 dB for RIS with N = 512 elements and 4-bit phase quantisation, QBER reductions ranging from 20% to 35% compared to free-space QKD, control latencies below 10 ms on edge-class hardware, and secure key rates exceeding 1 Mbit s−1 under moderate turbulence. In this paper, we detail implementation assumptions, control information elements (IEs), and key performance indicators (KPIs) aligned with emerging standardisation efforts. The proposed framework offers a rigorous and reproducible foundation for advancing quantum-enhanced wireless communications.

title: Reconfigurable Varifocal Lens Antenna Based on Plasma Technology
authors: Fatemeh Abbasian, Mohammad-Ali Shameli, Tavakol Pakizeh, Antonio-Daniele Capobianco, Mirko Magarotto
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The growing demand for higher capacity in wireless communication networks has created a critical need for smart devices with dynamic focusing and beam steering capabilities in the 5G mid-band spectrum. Conventional focusing techniques and metasurface-based flat lenses often suffer from static operation and mechanical complexity, restricting their practical use in adaptive platforms. In this work, we propose a reconfigurable reflective lens based on plasma-filled glass tubes designed to operate at 5 GHz. Through full-wave numerical simulations, we demonstrate that selective modulation of plasma density permits continuous focal length tuning from 1.5λ to 3.5λ. Furthermore, by increasing the number of plasma tubes, we achieve dynamic lateral shifts of the focal spot exceeding 2λ. Compared with prior reconfigurable lens approaches in the microwave and millimeter-wave range, the proposed design offers a broader tuning range and introduces lateral reconfigurability, which has not been previously reported. These results highlight the potential of plasma-based flat lenses as a compact, fast, and energy-efficient solution for integration into next-generation wireless communication systems.

title: BANet: Bidirectional Feature Aggregation and Adaptive Multi-Scene Perception-Based Lane Detection for Autonomous Driving
authors: Yunzuo Zhang, Zhiwei Tu, Weiqi Lian, Yubo Hu, Shibo Sun, Yaoge Xiao, Yu Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Lane detection is a hot topic in the field of autonomous driving, providing essential assistance to vehicles. Due to the lack of effective integration among hierarchical features and insufficient adaptability to variations of lanes under diverse background conditions, accurate lane detection remains challenging. To address the aforementioned issues, we propose BANet, an efficient lane detection network based on bidirectional feature aggregation and adaptive multi-scene perception, aiming to improve the lane detection accuracy under different backgrounds. Firstly, we propose a Bidirectional Feature Aggregation Module (BFAM) that, via the proposed composition built from PG2f, effectively integrates complementary information across scales, improving anchor localization. Secondly, we propose an Adaptive Multi-scene Perception Module (AMPM) that learns scene-aware spatial information to enhance lane-relevant cues, addressing the no-visual-clue problem. Finally, we propose an Edge Refinement Attention (ERA), which models spatial and channel information in parallel to refine lane representations. The experimental results on the CULane, CurveLanes, and TuSimple datasets show that the proposed network outperforms existing methods and exhibits excellent performance in the most challenging scenes.

title: A Cubic High-Gain DC-DC Converter for Improved Maximum Power Extraction in Partially Shaded PV Systems
authors: Vikash Kumar Raushan, Sankar Peddapati, S.V.K. Naresh
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a high-gain cubic DC–DC converter designed for three-phase two-level grid-connected photovoltaic (PV) systems, offering enhanced performance under partial shading conditions. The converter achieves a cubic voltage gain using a single active switch and fewer passive components, with lower input current ripple and reduced voltage stress on semiconductor devices. A comprehensive steady-state and power loss analysis is presented to derive the converter’s high gain and efficiency. Here, a grid-current-based maximum power injection to the grid (MPIG) strategy is used instead of a traditional PV-side maximum power point tracking (MPPT), eliminating the need for PV-side voltage and current sensors. Further, the particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm is applied to track the global peak under partial shading conditions. The effectiveness of the converter and control strategy is demonstrated through experiments on a three-phase PV-grid prototype, tested with different array configurations and shading patterns. Experimental results show the proposed converter’s improved peak tracking, stable grid operation, and better performance under partial shading than the conventional boost converter, validating the proposed converter’s suitability for practical PV-grid integration.

title: 5G-Enabled Cyber-Physical Infrastructure for Intelligent Automotive Battery Management
authors: Eduardo Hidalgo-Fort, Pedro Blanco-Carmona, Manuel Martín-Romero, Antonio Torralba, Fernando Muñoz-Chavero, Ramón González-Carvajal
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: This paper presents a prototype of an architecture of a cyber-physical system (CPS) for extending the lifetime of electric vehicle batteries. The proposed solution features a secure, AUTOSAR-compliant architecture with wireless connection designed to facilitate advanced optimization techniques for battery usage. Central to this architecture is the integration of a cloud-based complex Digital Twin - built upon high-precision behavioural models - and the deployment on the vehicle of corresponding simplified models for edge computing. This enables real-time estimation of battery status and optimal usage parameters locally, without relying on continuous cloud connectivity. To address performance degradation over time, the system incorporates a methodology for remote, wireless retraining of edge models using the cloud Digital Twin via 5G communication. The proposed solution has been fully implemented and validated in laboratory using a real battery yielding State-of-Charge (SoC) predictions with an error margin below 3.5%. Additionally, behavioural models were also developed for State-of-Health (SoH), Remaining Useful Life (RUL), State-of-Security (SoS) and State-of-Power (SoP). These models also provide, in a second iteration analysis, values for the maximum current to demand from the battery and the optimal operating temperature. Collectively, the proposed CPS architecture enables advanced battery management with a positive impact in the battery lifetime.

title: Enhancing Electric Vehicles Charging Service Through Mixed Charging: A Two-Stage Distributionally Robust Optimization Model
authors: Weiwen Zhou, Yiyang Peng, Zhuowei Wang, Anthony Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: In response to the need for sustainable transportation, this study addresses the challenges of providing the charging service to fluctuating and geographically dispersed electric vehicles (EVs) charging demands. This work proposed a mixed EV charging system that incorporates mobile charging services to complement fixed charging stations. The overall coverage rate of charging services is enhanced under this charging system. To cope with the complexities of estimating the EV charging demands due to evolving adoption of EVs and uncertain external factors (e.g.,driver’s car use behaviors, fluctuations in electricity rates), a two-stage distributionally robust optimization (DRO) model is developed to determine the placement and capacity of fixed charging stations, the location of mobile depots, and the number of mobile trucks to deploy. Acase study on HongKong’s North District is conducted to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed model by solving it using a decomposition algorithm, through which the computational complexity is significantly reduced by iteratively solving the first andsecond stage problems. This study demonstrates that the mixed charging strategy significantly improves coverage, even with limited data, and highlights how investment levels affect performance. The mixed system improved coverage by 20% compared to fixed only infrastructure under the budget of 90million HKD. Additionally, the case study shows an increase of average coverage rate as budget level increases, which offers insights into the investment levels required to achieve a specific coverage rate. The contributions include a novel mixed charging strategy and a two-stage DRO model, providing a robust and scalable framework to support EV charging infrastructure planning under uncertainty.

title: Perceptual Decoupling with a New Dataset and the HiFi-Net Blind Image Quality Assessment Model
authors: Nanlin Xu, Ming Ronnier Luo
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Traditional Image Quality Assessment (IQA) often treats perceptual quality as a monolithic concept, overlooking the independent contributions of its color and spatial dimensions. To address this gap, this research first introduces a large-scale subjective study to investigate the relationship between total image quality (IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</sub>) and its constituent color (IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</sub>) and spatial (IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</sub>) components. The results provide strong empirical evidence that IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</sub> can be effectively decoupled and accurately represented by a weighted linear combination of IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</sub> and IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</sub> (Pearson Correlation Coefficient of 0.96), while IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</sub> and IQ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</sub> themselves are largely independent (correlation of 0.092). Theoretically, this perceptual decoupling aligns with the functional specialization of the Human Visual System (HVS), which processes chromatic and structural attributes through segregated neural pathways. Moreover, we propose a novel Blind IQA (BIQA) model, the Hierarchical Fusion Network (HiFi-Net). This model leverages a pre-trained Vision Transformer (ViT) backbone to extract rich, multi-level features and introduces an innovative layer-independent perceptual attention mechanism (comprising intra-layer and inter-layer attention). This mechanism adaptively captures layer-specific quality-aware features and aggregates them across depths, extracting critical information from complex distortions for final score regression. Extensive experiments on nine public benchmark datasets demonstrate that HiFi-Net achieves state-of-the-art performance on both synthetic and authentic distortions, validating its effectiveness and strong generalization capability.

title: Observer-based Fault Estimation and Compensation Control for Nonlinear Multi-delays Systems With Multi-Source Faults
authors: Ming-Yang Qiao, Xiao-Heng Chang, Ju H. Park
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper addresses the fault estimation and compensation control problem for nonlinear stochastic multi-delays systems with multi-source faults. The T–S fuzzy model is used to describe the nonlinearity of the system. By assuming that each stochastic delay is independent and follows the Bernoulli distribution to describe the stochastic multi-delays possibly caused by sensor/actuator faults, the targeted fault observers with historical information are introduced to estimate the sensor/actuator faults respectively, so that the designed fault-tolerant compensation control (FTCC) method can ensure the stochastic stability and H∞ control performance for the closed-loop FTCC system. The sufficient design conditions for the fuzzy observer and the fault-tolerant compensation controller are obtained through the application of Lyapunov-Krasovskii stability theory and techniques. Finally, the effectiveness of the designed algorithm is verified through a common automation control system.

title: A Single-Layer Printed Cross-Dipole Antenna With Small Footprint and Ultra-Wide Axial-Ratio Beamwidth
authors: Zhi Yang, Mei Li, Yi Tan, Ming-Chun Tang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A single-layer circularly polarized (CP) printed cross-dipole antenna featuring a compact size and an ultrawide 3-dB axial ratio (AR) beamwidth is presented. To address the severe input resistance reduction in the fundamental series resonant mode induced by ground-plane image currents, the parallel resonant mode of the low-profile printed dipole is utilized. For miniaturization, shorting pins are loaded at the dipole's equivalent electric wall, transforming the parallel resonance into a half-mode operation, and substantially reducing the resonant frequency. Subsequently, near-field resonant parasitic (NFRP) technique is employed to effectively reduce the associated high impedance, facilitating source matching. Owing to the inherent high polarization purity of the printed dipole, CP radiation is readily generated by implementing a fan-shaped cross-dipole radiator with a dimension perturbation on one arm. Benefitting from its specific symmetrical structure, this single-layer antenna achieves an exceptionally wide AR beamwidth. The final design attains a compact footprint of 0.0419λ0<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>×0.042λ0 while providing 3-dB AR beamwidths of 219° and 192° in the xz- and yz-planes, respectively.

title: Mode Sensitive Topological Interface State Dual-Mode Absorber in Hyperbolic Metamaterials Based One-Dimensional Photonic Crystals With Graphene and Gold Layers
authors: Gulzar Ali J, Sanjay Kumar Mohanty, Chittaranjan Nayak
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nanotechnology
abstract: Dual resonance absorption modes realized by a graphene in one-dimensional photonic crystal heterostructure of all-hyperbolic metamaterial (HMM)-based unit cells terminated with a gold- dielectric bilayer is examined. Resonance frequencies exhibit a distinct angular dependence: they shift upward under transverse electric (TE) polarization and downward under transverse magnetic (TM) polarization as the incidence angle increases from normal incidence up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$89^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula>. This angular response appears almost linear in both TE and TM modes, as visualized through contour plots. The effect is primarily demonstrated in the terahertz (THz) regime using Indium Phosphide (InP) and Tellurium (Te) as the constituent materials of the HMM, which exhibit metallic behavior within this frequency range. The same phenomenon is further verified separately with Indium Antimonide (InSb)/Te and Gallium Arsenide (GaAs)/Te combinations, confirming the robustness of the observed response. These findings emphasize the tunable and polarization-sensitive nature of resonances in hypercrystal systems, underscoring their potential for advanced THz photonic applications.

title: Biomedical Fragmented Attention Spectral Transformer Network for Infrared spectra analysis
authors: Durgesh Ameta, Hardik Sharma, Praful Hambarde, Laxmidhar Behera
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Rapid and non-invasive molecular characterization of biological samples using Fourier Transform Infrared (FTIR) spectroscopy is increasingly important in clinical diagnostics, tissue analysis, and microbiology. However, existing approaches rely on conventional chemometric and machine learning pipelines that require extensive preprocessing and lack real-time capability. In this work, we propose BioFASTNet (Biomedical Fragmented Attention Spectral Transformer Network), an end-to-end deep learning framework for real-time spectral analysis. The model integrates a Multi-resolution Convolutional Feature Extractor (MCFE) to capture multi-scale spectral features and a chemistry-informed Fragment-wise Attention Module (FAM) to model localized, functional group–specific regions. This design enables effective learning of both global spectral patterns and localized vibrational signatures. BioFASTNet is tailored for imbalanced multilabel classification, addressing challenges arising from overlapping molecular attributes and skewed data distributions. Experimental results demonstrate superior performance on a functional group prediction dataset, achieving 97.81% accuracy and 91.97% F1-score, outperforming state-of-the-art (SOTA) models such as CIRSNet and AggMapNet. On an additional odor prediction dataset, the model achieves a SOTA F1-score of 36.68%. Furthermore, interpretability analyses using fragment-wise attention and Grad-CAM++ reveal that the model focuses on chemically meaningful spectral regions. These results highlight BioFASTNet as an efficient, accurate, and interpretable solution for real-time FTIR-based biomedical analysis. Source code and pre-trained models are available at: https://github.com/durgesgameta/biofastnet

title: I5CPS 2026 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)

title: Energy and Migration Cost Minimization in Virtual Network Embedding for Disaggregated Cloud Data Centers
authors: Jiahe Xu, Chao Guo, Xinyu Wang, Moshe Zukerman
publish: 2025 7th International Communication Engineering and Cloud Computing Conference (CECCC)
abstract: Cloud data centers are evolving toward a disaggregated architecture to achieve higher efficiency in handling unprecedented large-scale workloads, particularly acceleratorassisted workloads such as AI training and inference. The core idea of this architecture is to decouple resources-including CPUs, memory, NICs, and accelerators-that were traditionally integrated within standalone servers, and reorganize them into shared resource pools. In this work, we study the virtual network embedding problem, which has been extensively explored in traditional server-based data centers, within the context of disaggregated data centers. Specifically, we address the consolidation of ongoing virtual network requests by migrating them to fewer nodes and links, thereby improving overall resource efficiency. We explicitly account for migration costs in the optimization process and aim to minimize them. Finally, we present Pareto fronts that characterize the trade-offs between power consumption and migration cost.

title: Copyrigh Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)

title: A Reputation-Aware Price Adjustment Mechanism for Load Balancing in Cloud Computing
authors: Lilia Chourou Gherir, Ahmed Elleuch, Mohamed Jemni
publish: 2025 7th International Communication Engineering and Cloud Computing Conference (CECCC)
abstract: Dynamic price adjustment plays a central role in regulating supply and demand in cloud computing environments. We introduce a pricing mechanism based on a trial-and-error model, supervised by an auctioneer, and initially relying on the assumption of honest agents. However, in practice, some agents adopt opportunistic behaviors that may distort price formation and compromise the efficiency of resource allocation. To increase the resilience of the system against such manipulations, we integrate a reputation mechanism designed to penalize malicious actors. Experimental results show that this approach significantly improves the robustness of the pricing process, by limiting imbalances and protecting honest agents from both malicious suppliers and customers. This coupling between pricing and reputation thus promotes a fairer and more efficient allocation of resources.

title: CVSM: Contrastive Vocal Similarity Modeling
authors: Christos Garoufis, Athanasia Zlatintsi, Petros Maragos
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: The availability of large, unlabeled datasets across various domains has contributed to the development of a plethora of methods that learn representations for multiple target (downstream) tasks through self-supervised pre-training. In this work, we introduce CVSM (Contrastive Vocal Similarity Modeling), a contrastive self-supervised procedure for music signal representation learning in the audio domain that can be utilized for musical and vocal similarity modeling. Our method operates under a contrastive framework, maximizing the similarity between vocal excerpts and musical mixtures containing the same vocals; we devise both a label-informed protocol, leveraging artist identity information to sample the contrastive pairs, and a label-agnostic scheme, involving artificial mixture creation from randomly sampled vocal and accompaniment excerpts, which are paired with vocals from the same audio segment. We evaluate our proposed method in measuring vocal similarity both objectively, through linear probing on a suite of appropriate downstream tasks, and subjectively, via conducting a user study consisting of pairwise comparisons between different models in a recommendation-by-query setting. Our results indicate that the representations learned through CVSM are effective in musical and vocal similarity modeling, outperforming numerous baselines across both isolated vocals and complete musical mixtures. Moreover, while the availability of artist identity labels during pre-training leads to overall more consistent performance both in the evaluated downstream tasks and the user study, a label-agnostic CVSM variant incorporating hybrid pre-training with real and artificial mixtures achieves comparable performance to the label-informed one in artist identification and perceived vocal similarity.

title: Improving Fast Project Delivery using Decision Support Vector Machine with ACO approaches in SAP
authors: Sravan Katragadda
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: One of the most critical issues concerning enterprise resource planning systems like SAP is fast project delivery, where the correct risk forecasting, optimal scheduling, and resource allocation support the timely execution. The earlier methods are generally not effective in selecting features, and in prediction and judgment making are unscalable, so pathetic. This research proposes a hybrid model combining the Decision Support Vector Machine (DSVM) with Ant Colony Optimization (ACO)-based Feature Selection (ACO-FS) to improve the quality of project delivery. The workflow starts with gathering project data from SAP modules, including resource consumption, budget variances, vendor lead times, and risk logs. The resulting data undergoes Z-Score Normalizationto achieve a standard scale across all attributes. Afterward, ACO-FS is used to trim down the most valuable and non-redundant features, effectively downsizing the dimensions without losing information important to project delivery outcomes. The refined feature set is then categorized using DSVM, which classifies the project’s risk categories. Correspondingly, the ACO algorithm is conducive to optimal resource allocation and task scheduling to reduce bottlenecks. The usefulness of the suggested framework is evaluated based on performance measures such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and computational efficiency. Generally, the integration of DSVM and ACO-FS provides a smart and reasonable decision support system capable of achieving significant improvements in the speed of project execution and asset use, and in business agility in dynamic business structures.

title: Permissioned Blockchain-Enabled Decentralized Streaming for Adaptive QoS in 6G Networks
authors: J. Chandra Priya, G. Nanthakumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: With the emergence of 6G networks, there is a need for a highly reliable and secure infrastructure to support the demand for fast and seamless communication services. In this paper, we propose a customized blockchain architecture as a solution, taking advantage of its enhanced security features, traceability, and pseudo-transparency. Our proposed architecture aims to improve Quality of Service (QoS) in 6G networks by leveraging a permissioned blockchain. It ensures security and scalability to meet the future demands of high-speed and low-latency communication. The salient feature of Decentralized Adaptive Quality Streaming (DAQs)is the use of a permissioned blockchain, which provides inherent accessibility by adding an additional layer to multimedia streaming, thereby preventing unauthorized tampering. The chaincodes enforce service level agreements (SLAs) between content providers, network operators, and end-users. These self-executing contracts automatically enforce predefined QoS parameters, ensuring that the agreed-upon service standards are met. This improves the integrity and authenticity of transmitted content, enhancing the overall user experience. In our experiments, we observed that this architecture achieved an impressive 88% improvement in overall response time compared to the standard architecture. The architecture provides a novel and robust solution for improving QoS in 6G networks through its customized blockchain infrastructure.

title: A Novel Nanobiosensor Based on Carbon Nanotube Tunnel Field-Effect Transistor for Low-Power and Label-Free Detection: Design and Quantum Simulation Analysis
authors: Khalil Tamersit, Hocine Bourouba, Abdellah Kouzou
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: In this paper, a novel nanoscale biosensor based on a coaxial-gate carbon nanotube tunneling field-effect transistor (CG-CNTTFET) is proposed and analyzed using quantum simulations. The simulation framework relies on the selfconsistent solution of the Poisson equation coupled with the nonequilibrium Green's function (NEGF) formalism. The nanobiosensor features an open lateral biosensing region located on the drain side. The performance of the proposed ballistic biosensor is evaluated through its transfer characteristics, band diagrams, sensitivity, and subthreshold swing. The results reveal that the biosensor exhibits high sensitivity within a dielectricmodulated TFET configuration featuring open lateral biosensing regions. Moreover, the biomolecule-induced electrostatic variations are shown to produce enhanced sensitivity when the device operates in a steep subthreshold swing regime. Optimizing the CNTTFET subthreshold characteristics can therefore pave the way for the development of ultra-high-sensitivity biosensors with unprecedented detection performance.

title: A Machine Learning Approach for Web Service Autoscaling: EMD-LSTM and Attention Mechanism in Cloud Computing
authors: Lendy Pradhana Hartono, Royyana Muslim Ijtihadie, Tohari Ahmad
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Traditional reactive autoscalers respond only after resource exhaustion occurs, causing SLA violations and oscillating scaling behaviour. This paper proposes a proactive autoscaling system that combines Empirical Mode Decomposition (EMD), LSTM networks, and attention mechanisms to predict web service traffic and provision resources. The approach decomposes non-stationary RPS patterns into 7 Intrinsic Mode Functions, predicts each component using independent LSTM models, and weights predictions using multi-head attention to emphasise informative signals. Evaluation on real e-commerce traffic demonstrates that EMD-LSTM achieves an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.991, compared with 0.223 for the baseline LSTM. Production deployment in Kubernetes environments shows the system processed 7.67× more requests while consuming only 72.7% fewer resources, achieving a 28x improvement in resource efficiency. The work shows that combining accurate signal decomposition, temporal learning, and attention-based weighting simultaneously achieves both SLA compliance and cost efficiency, while reducing the lost request ratio from 87.17% to 1.63%.

title: KPGS: Towards Real-World Complex Dynamic Scene Rendering With Keyframe-Driven Predictable Gaussian Splatting
authors: Yiqian Chang, Haoran Xu, Jianing Li, Xuan Wang, Yonghong Tian, Peixi Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Rendering complex dynamic scenes offers the advantage of observing and understanding the real world. However, existing Dynamic Scene Rendering (DSR) methods remain challenged by suboptimal reconstruction fidelity. These limitations stem from relying on a single, unified deformation model, which struggles to capture complex motions involving multiple sub-motions and abrupt geometric transitions. While temporal decomposition methods could alleviate such shortcomings, they introduce the additional challenge of ignoring motion correlations and increasing storage requirements. To address these issues, we introduce Keyframe-driven Predictable Gaussian Splatting (KPGS)—an efficient framework for high-fidelity complex dynamic scene rendering. Firstly, we present a patch- wise HSV clustering for extracting keyframes. Secondly, a prediction network based on the Transformer is utilized to calculate the deformable Gaussians at discrete keyframe times via voxelization. Thirdly, we propose an inter-frame deformation network and a mutual supervision between adjacent segments to maintain the temporal continuity. Extensive experiments on our newly built dataset (MotionGS), as well as public benchmarks HyperNeRF and Neu3D, demonstrate that KPGS could achieve a higher average view synthesis performance than SOTA approaches, while maintaining a balance between storage cost and performance. More details of the demo and dataset are available at KPGS Supplementary.

title: PSO Optimized Wireless Solar Charger for Electric Vehicles
authors: Snigdha Chaturvedi, Aditya Kumar, Ankush Yadav, Aditya Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The shift toward electric vehicles (EVs) is essential for reducing carbon emissions and addressing climate change. Nonetheless, the broad adoption of EVs encounters obstacles, especially in creating an effective and sustainable charging network. This paper introduces an innovative solution that combines renewable energy with wireless technology to transform EV charging. Our strategy focuses on integrating solar-powered wireless charging technology, representing a significant shift from traditional charging methods. Solar panels capture sunlight and convert it into electrical energy, which is then stored in batteries for use when needed. This renewable energy source promotes sustainability and lessens reliance on non-renewable power grids. The key innovation is the wireless power transmission system, which employs magnetic induction between transmitter and receiver coils. This approach removes the necessity for physical connectors or cables, providing a smooth and convenient experience. Our system utilizes wireless technology to improve access to charging stations and minimize the wear and tear that comes with traditional plug-in methods. Furthermore, our solution tackles significant issues within the EV charging infrastructure. By harnessing solar energy, we greatly lessen the environmental impact linked to conventional charging techniques, thereby supporting a cleaner and more sustainable transportation ecosystem. Additionally, our system's reliability is bolstered as it functions independently of the grid, ensuring continuous charging services even in remote or off-grid areas. We are confident that our approach has great potential to accelerate the adoption of EVs and promote a more environmentally aware future.

title: Dynamic Quarantine Slicing for DDoS Mitigation in Healthcare SDN Environments
authors: Marshal S, Jaspher W. Kathrine, Shajin Prince, Samson Arun Raj A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Software-Defined Networking is increasingly integrated into healthcare networks to ensure effective network traffic control. SDN is highly vulnerable to Distributed Denial-of-Service attacks that exploit OpenFlow, critical medical workings such as tele-surgery and patient monitoring. The existing systems such as reactive filtering and static VLAN slicing, produce high latency up to 100ms and may not be effective against low-rate stealthy attacks that mimic the legitimate medical network traffic. This paper proposes a framework Dynamic Quarantine Slicing, a framework for realtime DDoS mitigation in healthcare networks. It combines a hybrid GRU-LSTM deep learning model with Ryu controllerbased firewall and switching mechanisms in adaptive order to apply policies of flow isolation. Results done on the Mininet healthcare testbed shows a 96% classification accuracy, it outperforms existing system's logistic regression 85 %.DQS can quarantine malicious flows within a latency of 5ms, ensuring rapid recovery within 60 seconds to ensure continuity in critical medical communications.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Skin Disease Classification Using Adaptive Segmentation and Dual-Stage EfficientNet
authors: Susmita Panda, Debashish Kar, Sambandh Bhusan Dhal, Krishna Chaitanya Gadepally, Stavros Kalafatis
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Skin lesion classification from dermoscopic images remains challenging due to severe class imbalance and confounding artifacts. We present a comprehensive deep learning pipeline that synergistically combines morphological segmentation, partial oversampling, and two-phase transfer learning to address these challenges. Our approach employs adaptive threshold-based preprocessing to isolate lesions from hair and background artifacts, strategic partial oversampling to balance minority classes without overfitting, and a dual-stage EfficientNet-B3 training strategy-first freezing the backbone for stability, then finetuning with focal loss for enhanced minority class detection. Evaluated on HAM10000 (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0, 0 1 5}$</tex> images, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7}$</tex> classes) using patientlevel data splitting, our method achieves 82.3% accuracy, 65.0% balanced accuracy, and 66.9% macro F1-score. A comprehensive ablation study reveals that two-phase training contributes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{+} \mathbf{3. 6} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> accuracy individually, while the complete pipeline improves balanced accuracy by 13.5 percentage points over baseline. Notably, melanoma F1-score improves from 0.509 to 0.586, demonstrating significant enhancement in detecting this critical malignancy. Our approach offers a practical, reproducible solution for automated dermoscopic screening with strong performance on minority classes.

title: Speech Emotion Recognition: A Human-Centric Framework with Enhanced Data Augmentation and Lightweight CNN
authors: Shreyansh Shakya, Aloukik Das, Preetam Suman, Sasmita Padhy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Speech Emotion Recognition (SER) has significant potential to enhance human-computer interaction, particularly in real-time applications. However, deploying SER models on resource-constrained edge devices necessitates efficient and compact solutions. This paper proposes a lightweight Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) framework enhanced with targeted audio augmentation techniques and optimized feature representation. The preprocessing pipeline incorporates controlled pitch shifting, time stretching, and Gaussian noise injection to improve data diversity. Thirteen Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs), along with their first and second-order derivatives, are extracted and subsequently reduced using Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to form 32 by 32 feature maps. Trained on the Ryerson Audio-Visual Database of Emotional Speech and Song (RAVDESS) dataset, the proposed model achieves 72 percent of classification accuracy, demonstrating a favorable trade-off between performance and computational efficiency. Comparative evaluations against hybrid and attention-based architectures highlight the model's suitability for edge deployment. Future work includes integrating lightweight attention mechanisms and extending validation across more diverse emotion datasets.

title: IoT Based Flood Monitoring and Control System
authors: Thakur Ankit Krishna Kumar, Abhishek Kumar, Ankit Singh, Gaurav Pal, Manish Pal, Mahima Tyagi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This paper presents an IoT-based flood monitoring and control system designed to provide real-time flood detection, early warning systems, and management of flood-prone areas. This system is used by a network of sensors deployed to location strategically to measure parameters such as water levels, rainfall, and soil moisture. These sensors are connected to the center of the IoT platform, which continuously monitors the data and processes it for a potential flood risk. When disapproved thresholds are met, the system triggers alerts to the local authorities and the public via SMS or mobile applications. In addition, the system can be activated to automate flood control measures, such as opening floodgates or diverting water to prevent further damage. The architecture is designed for scalability, reliability, and cost-effectiveness, making it suitable for both urban and rural areas flood management applications. The system operates with minimal human intervention and does not rely on complex AI algorithms, focusing instead on straightforward data collection, threshold-based alerting, and simple control mechanisms. This makes it highly accessible and easy to implement, providing a practical solution for flood risk mitigation

title: Capacitive Energy Harvesting from the Electric Field of Overhead Power Lines
authors: Jussi Keskikuru, Tuomas Rauta, Joel Haverinen, Qudrat Ullah, Pekka Ruuskanen
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)
abstract: This article introduces a capacitive energy harvester that harvests energy from the electric field of an overhead power line through a low-potential electrode. In this method, the electric field between the power line and the ground generates a displacement current, which is converted into usable DC energy after rectification and storage stages. Experiments were conducted at line-to-ground voltages of $20 \mathrm{kV}, 33 \mathrm{kV}$, and 63.5 kV, which showed that the charging speed of the output capacitor directly depends on the line voltage. The results confirm the trend that as the line voltage increases, the electric field strength and energy harvesting ability also improve. This research demonstrates that capacitive energy harvesting from the electric field of power lines is a reliable and cost-effective method that can effectively provide power to monitoring devices, wireless sensors, and IoT devices in smart grids applications.

title: Smart Grids in the Era of Digital Transformation: A Bibliometric Analysis
authors: Nikita Bhati, Ruchi Jain Garg
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Technology has brought with it countless dynamics of attaining the power to change in accordance with the ever-changing environment in this era, and no organization in the world is immune to this force. Digital transformation along with smart grid technology constitute a revolution to create efficient, resilient, and sustainable energy systems that power every society. This article applies bibliometric analysis to investigate the evolution of literature on smart grid and digital transformation from the information published in the major databases. The research analyses existing researches using performance measures, co-authorship networks, and keyword networks. The findings allow the reader to follow the overlap between digital innovation and grid modernization in key aspects such as the Internet of Things (IoT), artificial intelligence, and integration of renewable energy sources. Furthermore, the paper contributes to the deepening of our knowledge on the increased pace of smart grids research development in the framework of the digital transformation and provides the systemic description of the knowledge development. The paper will introduce a new approach to the role of digital technologies to the sustainable energy system by uniting two previously distinct areas, which has been demonstrated by bibliometric mapping.

title: GCET 2025 Commentary
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)

title: Static Hand Gesture Recognition using Score Fusion of CNN based RGB-D Net
authors: Dayananda Kumar N.C, K.V Suresh, Dinesh R
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Hand Gesture Recognition (HGR) involves detection of various hand postures and signs to convey information in non-verbal communication. It is widely used in human computer interaction for machine interface and sign language recognition to aid people with verbal and speech impairments. HGR systems with multimodal data fusion have proved to be more efficient as compared to unimodal systems for real time applications. In this work, RGB and depth multimodal data based score fusion using dilated convolutional neural networks is proposed for static hand gesture recognition. Experiments are conducted on OUHANDS dataset and achieved the accuracy of 88.91% in complex real time scenarios.

title: Exploring Gen Z's Adoption of AI-Based Learning Tools: Evidence from Can Tho, Vietnam
authors: Thai Anh Vu Nguyen
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: The study looks at how students in Vietnam decide whether to use AI tools in their learning. Data were collected from 470 university students in Can Tho through a structured survey. The model was based on well-known technology adoption theories, focusing on five possible influences: effort expectancy, performance expectancy, perceived usefulness, social influence, and perceived trust. Analysis with SmartPLS 3.0 shows that usefulness, ease of use, and expected performance are the main drivers of willingness to adopt AI. In contrast, trust and social influence were not found to play an important role. These results suggest that students pay closer attention to whether AI tools save time, improve their study outcomes, and are easy to handle. The findings provide practical insights for universities and educators seeking to promote the adoption of AI in higher education in a sustainable way.

title: Enhancing the Hail Resistance of Photovoltaic Modules Through Glass Thickness Optimization and Nanocoatings
authors: Yuanbiao Deng, Vadim Davydov, Aleksandr Bobyl
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: Hailimpact is one of the main problems that reduces the performance and lifetime of photovoltaic (PV) modules, especially in cold or severe climates. This study examines how hail causes damage and how the damage can be reduced by using thicker glass and protective nanocoatings. Our field study in Saint Petersburg and review of existing research show that small cracks in the front glass can lower power output by up to 20%, while further damage from dust, water, and ice can reduce it by more than 50%. Tests also show that PV modules with 4 mm glass are much stronger and can keep normal operation after hail impact. In addition, hydrophobic coatings help prevent snow accumulation and make the panel easier to clean, though they lose their effect after long outdoor use. The results show that combining stronger glass with improved surface coatings can make PV modules more reliable and longer lasting in hail-prone and cold regions.

title: Data-Driven Learning in Physical Education: The Role of Arm Muscle Strength and Digital Feedback in Handball Jump Shot Performance
authors: Amran, Wawan Sundawan Suherman, Ali Satia Graha, Asmuddin, Ade Gusti Ningsih
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: This study investigates how data-driven learning and digital feedback can enhance the understanding and improvement of sport-specific skills in physical education, focusing on the relationship between arm muscle strength and jump-shot performance in handball. A quantitative correlational approach was employed with 32 male university athletes from Halu Oleo University. Arm muscle strength was assessed using the 30 -second push-up test, and jump-shot performance was measured through a 60 -second accuracy test, with digital video recordings analyzed using motion-analysis software to provide real-time feedback. The results revealed a strong, significant correlation between arm muscle strength and jump-shot success (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=0.67>r$</tex>-table=0.349, p<0.05), indicating that 45% of performance variance is explained by upper-body strength.
