This study suggests a hybrid diagnostic framework that integrates deep learning (DL) techniques with large scale speech models (LLM) to improve image analysis and patient commitment. Folding networks (CNNs) are used for accurate characteristic extraction and classification of brain tumors from MRI scans. To complement this, personalized chatbots for medical support were developed using trans-based LLMS and advanced natural language processing technology (NLP). Chatbots provide patient specific knowledge, interpret clinical queries, and recommend specialized healthcare service providers nearby. By fusing LLM-controlled language understanding with visual DL based visual analyses, the system supports accurate tumor localization, intelligent patient interaction, and efficient clinical decision making. This approach highlights the potential for transformation combining visual and verbal models to improve diagnostic accuracy, streamline workflows, and provide accessible and personalized medical support in brain tumor diagnosis.

title: Synthesis of Energy Efficient and Performance Oriented Feed Forward Artificial Neural Network on Kintex-7 Low Voltage
authors: Pankaj Singh, Sandeep Budhani, Shilpi Bisht
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: In this paper we have proposed the synthesis of an energy efficient feed forward artificial neural network (FFANN) on Kintex-7 low voltage FPGA. The implementation of FFANN on FPGA is achieved by using a number of configuration such as varying capacitance, voltages and I/O standards. LVCMOS 15 and LVCMOS 18 are used as I/O standards. The maximum power efficiency is achieved with LVCMOS 15 I/O standard when voltage is varied from 0.970 to 0.870 and capacitance is varied from 10000 pF to 1 pF. By using varying voltage and capacitance, a reduction of 99.84% in I/O power, 15.55% in static power and 83.35% in total power consumption is observed. The reduction in I/O, static and total power consumption doesn't affect the performance of the FFANN. For the implementation of FFANN on FPGA we trained it using TensorFlow and then implemented the learned weights on FPGA. We have used 16-bit fixed point representation for data and Sigmoid is used as activation function. The model is tested on MNIST dataset. The implementation of FFANN requires a fraction of the available resources on the FPGA. The FFANN operates at a frequency of 216.5 MHz with a total negative slack of 0.004 ns which provides promising performance.

title: Smart Solutions for Sustainable Cotton Farming: Integrating Sentinel Data and Machine Learning for Enhanced Water Stress Detection
authors: Neetish Kumar Chandrakar, Aakanksha Sharaff
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Cotton is an important part of the Indian cultures and history, it is the backbone of Indian textiles industry and symbol of craftsmanship. Cotton needs water but can't overdo it. Currently farmers face several irrigation difficulties in waterstress areas. Water stress is not only a problem that affects the lives of farmers but also affects the well-being of the whole community and economy. The big data analytics of satellites provides a separate platform to identify the absence of water in cotton cultivation. The analysis of satellite imagery helps farmers monitor the water stress and drought conditions, making informed irrigation decisions and eliminating unnecessary wastage of water. In this paper, the comparative study of different Machine Learning algorithms: Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest, Multi-layer Perceptron classifier (MLPClassifier), XGBoost, Logistic Regression, and k - nearest neighbor (kNN) will be evaluated to predict water stress in the agricultural fields using Sentinel-1 (S1) and Sentinel-2 (S2) satellite data. This research paper provides the practical information on the monitoring of crop health, which encourages the development of sustainable farming methods, through the comparison, based on the accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. The findings demonstrate the role of village-level data of water stress in enabling farmers to emphasize on water scarce regions, enhancing effective water use and sustainable food production. The research will assist farmers to make superior irrigation choices and encourage sustainable farming.

title: Robust Tunnel Point Cloud Matching: A Coarse-to-Fine Framework Integrating PCA and GICP with DBSCAN Noise Reduction
authors: Jianxin Wu, Ruiyang Zhang, Haoliang Yang, Lin Yang, Junwei Nie, Diyang Chen, Qiang Li
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: This paper proposes a coarse-to-fine matching framework integrating physical priors with probabilistic optimization: First, a physically parameter-driven DBSCAN clustering denoising algorithm is constructed. Based on clearance requirements in tunnel electromechanical installation specifications, the clustering radius is adaptively determined to effectively strip structured noise closely adhering to the wall surface. Second, Principal Component Analysis (PCA) combined with gravity vector constraints and density skewness detection resolves the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8 0}$</tex>-degree ambiguity in the principal axis direction, enabling fully automated initial pose correction. Finally, the Generalized Iterative Nearest Point (GICP) algorithm is introduced. By constructing a probabilistic model of the local covariance matrix, it transforms the matching problem into a plane-to-plane maximum likelihood estimation problem. This model effectively suppresses axial slippage through a mathematical mechanism utilizing high-weight constraints on normals and low-weight tolerances for tangent planes. Field validation in a large-diameter submarine shield tunnel in Shandong demonstrated that this method significantly reduced the matching root mean square error (RMSE) from the standard ICP of 0.0185 meters to 0.0109 m, while increasing the overlap rate to 84.2%. Comparisons with the Normal Distribution Transformation (NDT) algorithm demonstrate that the proposed method maintains sub-centimeter accuracy while exhibiting superior environmental adaptability, providing robust technical support for the digital operation and maintenance of tunnels throughout their entire lifecycle.

title: Efficient ANPR-Based Parking Management Using YOLOv8
authors: Anirudh Kumar, Raghav Garg, Kanderp Narayan Mishra, Amit Bhola, Anubhava Srivastava, Himanshu Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The sudden surge in automobile ownership has resulted in parking issues, such as space management inefficiencies, excessive waiting time, and safety issues. This article introduces an Automated Parking System (APS) incorporating YOLOv8 for real-time detection of vehicles and EasyOCR for recognition of license plates. The system detects and extracts vehicle license plate information automatically, allocates parking spaces, and allows entry and exit tracking. A web-based system facilitates pre-booking, automatic space availability updates in real-time, and electronic payments, which increases user convenience. Experimental outcomes indicate enhanced efficiency, accuracy, and lower processing time than in conventional systems. The system helps achieve smart urban parking solutions by maximizing space utilization and security.

title: Design of a Hybrid Hydrogen Storage Microgrid via a New Weighted Mean of Vectors and Beluga Whale Optimization Technique
authors: Alahari Hanumat Prasad, Md Ankushavali, Anandakumar Haldorai, Pradeep Kumar Chilakala Sri Krishna Balaji, Mukesh Kumar, Deekshant Varshney
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: This research paper presents a new hybrid metaheuristic technique combining the use of Weighted Mean of Vectors (WMV) and Beluga Whale Optimization (BWO) to optimally design a hydrogen storage multi-user hybrid microgrid (HMG). The hybrid microgrid is based on the use of photovoltaic (PV) panels, wind turbines (WT), fuel cell (FC) system, electrolyze, hydrogen tank, and battery bank. The aim is to improve the system to cut total net present cost (NPC) down to the least possible amount and at the same time to increase reliability and renewables penetration under different operation constraints. The simulation results proved that the hybrid WMV-BWO algorithm was better than each single WMV, BWO, and other state-of-the-art algorithms. The upper limits and performance of the hybrid WMV-BWO algorithm over a wide range of loads and weather conditions were confirmed by conducting a sensitivity and robustness analysis.

title: Bringers of Order: Wearable Technologies and the Manufacturing of Everyday Life: James N. Gilmore: [Book Review]
authors: Maggie Hart
publish: IEEE Transactions on Professional Communication
abstract: Presents reviews for the following list of books, Bringers of Order: Wearable Technologies and the Manufacturing of Everyday Life.

title: Integration of a 60-GHz-Band Wide-Coverage Beamforming Array Antenna by Subarray Switching for Three Orthogonal Beams
authors: Satoshi Yoshida, Suguru Kameda, Noriharu Suematsu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In this study, a 60-GHz-band beamforming array antenna structure with a wide coverage beamscanning capability is proposed. Conventional 60-GHz-band beamforming array antennas have a narrow beam-scanning capability (narrow coverage) because they consist of only one or two array antennas. Another paper proposed four orthogonal beam switching; however, there was no beamforming capability, resulting in limited coverage. The challenge of the 60-GHz-band beamforming antenna is to expand the coverage as large as possible. Based on this background, we propose and design a structure of a wide-coverage 60-GHz-band beamforming array antenna with the assistance of subarray switching using three orthogonal subarrays. Two 2×4 dipole subarrays and one 2×4 orthogonal-fed patch subarray were packaged into a one-packaged structure to realize orthogonal three-direction beams. A 2-D beamforming is enabled within each subarray. In addition, seamless wide coverage is achieved by switching the three subarrays. The objective of this paper is to realize a wide-coverage 60-GHz-band beamforming array antenna using the designed antenna structure. The integration and manufacturing of the designed antenna structure are significant issues. It is difficult to manufacture a 60-GHz-band large-scale array antenna using conventional packaging techniques. An improvement in the manufacturing yield rate is required. To overcome this issue, a copper ball integration technique with excavation was utilized, and the antenna was successfully realized. This technique is essential for packaging a total array antenna in one package. The measurement result of the total coverage area with over 10 dBi gain at 60 GHz was 7.64 sr, while the ideal one was 6.28 sr. This measurement result has the widest coverage of all previous papers. We successfully confirmed and validated the expansion of the coverage using the proposed antenna structure.

title: Shadowless Projection Mapping for Tabletop Workspaces with Synthetic Aperture Projector
authors: Takahiro Okamoto, Masaki Takeuchi, Masataka Sawayama, Daisuke Iwai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Projection mapping (PM) enables augmented reality (AR) experiences without requiring users to wear head-mounted displays and supports multi-user interaction. It is regarded as a promising technology for a variety of applications in which users interact with content superimposed onto augmented objects in tabletop workspaces, including remote collaboration, healthcare, industrial design, urban planning, artwork creation, and office work. However, conventional PM systems often suffer from projection shadows when users occlude the light path. Prior approaches employing multiple distributed projectors can compensate for occlusion, but suffer from latency due to computational processing, degrading the user experience. In this research, we introduce a synthetic-aperture PM system that uses a significantly larger number of projectors, arranged densely in the environment, to achieve delay-free, shadowless projection for tabletop workspaces without requiring computational compensation. To address spatial resolution degradation caused by subpixel misalignment among overlaid projections, we develop and validate an offline blur compensation method whose computation time remains independent of the number of projectors. Furthermore, we demonstrate that our shadowless PM plays a critical role in achieving a fundamental goal of PM: altering material properties without evoking projection-like impression. Specifically, we define this perceptual impression as “sense of projection (SoP)” and establish a PM design framework to minimize the SoP based on user studies.

title: Ψ-xLSTM: Automated Behavioral Verilog-A Generation from Distilled Physics-Informed xLSTM Networks for High-Frequency Device Modeling
authors: Sorin Liviu Jurj
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) have emerged as a powerful tool for device modeling, yet they suffer from a fundamental spectral bias, often failing to capture high-frequency transient dynamics essential for modern circuit simulation. While recent implementations of Extended Long Short-Term Memory (xLSTM) networks have successfully mitigated this bias by leveraging exponential gating and matrix memories, they introduce prohibitive computational complexity O(N2) that renders them unsuitable for standard compact modeling and hardware deployment. In this work, we introduce Ψ-xLSTM, a framework that extends the Ψ-HDL methodology to recurrent architectures, enabling the automatic generation of efficient behavioral Verilog-A models that retain the high-frequency fidelity of xLSTMs. We propose a novel Recurrent Relation-Aware Distillation (RRAD) strategy that preserves temporal dynamics and targets the specific architectural innovations of xLSTM. First, we demonstrate that the xLSTM exponential gating mechanisms can be clustered to reveal discrete, physically interpretable time constants (relaxation times), replacing continuous gate values with sparse, physics-based parameters. Second, we apply structural compression to the xLSTM matrix memory (mLSTM), proving that the governing physics of device dynamics resides in a low-rank subspace, allowing the dense matrix to be collapsed into a few dominant eigen-modes. We validate Ψ-xLSTM on high-frequency transient memristor dynamics, where the framework achieves the spectral accuracy of full xLSTM-PINNs while reducing parameter count by up to 84%. Extended evaluation across additional spectral-bias baselines, multiple device classes, small-signal and large-signal active-device benchmarks, and publicly available experimental datasets further demonstrates the robustness and broader applicability of the approach. SPICE circuit simulation validates the complete model generation pipeline with a mean absolute error of 0.40 mA, confirming quantitative accuracy. The resulting models are automatically exported as compact behavioral Verilog-A code that captures high-frequency harmonics missed by standard PINNs, while simulating 7.6× faster in Python, with substantial additional gains expected in compiled SPICE due to elimination of matrix-memory overhead, supported by complexity analysis and behavioral simulation validation.

title: Service Deployment and Task Offloading Algorithm for UAV-Parking-Vehicles Assisted Mobile Edge Computing
authors: Biao Xiao, Zheng Yao, Yan Yan, Baoxian Zhang, Cheng Li
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: In this paper, we study a UAV-Parking-Vehicles assisted Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) network for providing enhanced edge computing services, where a UAV serves to relay ground users’ tasks to parking vehicles having idle computing resources in the vicinity for processing.We formulate the problem of average task delay minimization in this case as a long-term discrete mixed integer programming problem. We transform this problem into two subproblems: Service deployment optimization problem at large time scale and task offloading optimization problem at small time scale. For the former, we define a system utility for measuring the effect of a service deployment profile at parking vehicles, formulate the system utility minimization problem for optimizing the service deployment at vehicles, and propose a Computing resource allocation and Genetic Algorithm based Service deployment Algorithm (CGSA) for obtaining optimized service deployment profiles at parking vehicles on a per cycle basis. For the latter, we propose a Bandwidth allocation, Task offloading, and Transmission scheduling Algorithm (BTTA) for determining optimized task offloading profiles for all users on a per time slot basis. Extensive simulation results show the high performance of our proposed algorithms compared with baseline algorithms.

title: A Novel Integrated Underwater Communication and Positioning Algorithm Based on OFDM-DPPM
authors: Yichen Xue, Wanlong Zhao, Chenguang He, Yufei Cheng
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Autonomous Underwater Vehicles (AUVs) are indispensable in ocean exploration. To address their concurrent communication and positioning requirements during underwater operations, Integrated Underwater Communication and Positioning (IUCP) technology has emerged. This letter proposes a novel IUCP algorithm combining Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing and Differential Pulse Position Modulation (OFDM-DPPM), including waveform design and model construction. The OFDM-DPPM scheme demonstrates superior interference resilience and enhanced throughput compared to conventional modulation schemes. Simulations emulating underwater multipath channels validated the advanced performance of OFDM-DPPM in acoustic communication through comparative analysis. Multiple real-test experiments further confirmed its excellent communication performance and high-precision ranging capabilities.

title: Switching-Cost-Aware Deep Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Port Selection in Fluid Antenna Systems
authors: Jiaxin Liu, Kai Liang, Liqiang Zhao, Gan Zheng, Huixian Gu, Kai-Kit Wong, Chan-Byoung Chae
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Fluid antenna systems (FAS) have emerged as a promising enabling technology for sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks. By dynamically switching ports according to channel characteristics, FAS can exploit spatial diversity and significantly enhance wireless performance. However, most existing studies focus on maximizing instantaneous throughput while neglecting the long-term cost associated with frequent port switching. This work aims to jointly optimize long-term throughput and port-switching cost in FAS, considering spatio-temporally correlated channels and minimum-rate requirements for each user. To address this challenge, we formulate the dynamic port selection problem as a Markov decision process (MDP) and employ a dueling DQN framework to obtain a near-optimal policy. A Transformer encoder is incorporated to capture spatial-temporal dependencies among ports, and a candidate pre-selection mechanism is introduced to reduce training complexity. Simulation results show that the proposed method achieves up to 96% of the per-slot exhaustive-search optimum while significantly reducing decision latency, demonstrating high efficiency and scalability for large-scale FAS deployments.

title: MC-ORAM: A Concurrent ORAM Scheme for Multi-User Shared Storage
authors: Chuang Li, Duo Hu, Gang Liu, Yanhua Wen, Jianhao Wei, Zhuo Tang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computers
abstract: The expansion of cloud-based shared storage increases data privacy concerns. While data encryption technologies can safeguard data content, they cannot prevent the leakage of data access patterns. By re-encrypting data and changing storage location after each access, Oblivious Random Access Machine (ORAM) can effectively avoid information leakage from memory access patterns. While ORAM was initially designed for single-user applications, most existing multi-user ORAM solutions have drawbacks, such as dependence on a trusted proxy, high client storage overhead, and low throughput. To address the issues of multi-user ORAM systems, this paper explores the design of proxyless ORAM solutions in shared storage scenarios and proposes MC-ORAM, a new multi-user oblivious data storage framework. It ensures data consistency through client collaboration in a proxyless architecture, achieves higher throughput by differentiating request processing based on privacy protection requirements, and uses recursion for optimization. We implemented MC-ORAM and analyzed its performance using a variety of indicators, as well as conducting comparative evaluations against alternative schemes. The results show that, on average, MC-ORAM reduces response latency by 18.1% and improves throughput by 23.8% compared to TaoStore.

title: Approximate Optimal Enclosing Control for UAVs with Performance Guarantees: A Prescribed Time Learning Solution
authors: Wanning Wang, Xingling Shao, Jun Liu, Zhengrong Xiang, Junzhi Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper presents a prescribed time learning-based near optimal enclosing controller to ensure that unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) encircle around the specified target with prescribed performance constraints and minimum cost efforts. First, a basic enclosing controller is established to achieve the enclosing error stabilization and stable circumnavigation around a given target. Second, a new prescribed time behavior envelope that eliminates the availability on initial error is proposed. To render the satisfaction of performance constraints and optimal enclosing actions, a transformed enclosing error is obtained by enforcing state conversion on original error. Then, aiming at stabilizing the enclosing error to a prescribed accuracy within a prescribed time, a prescribed time learning-based near optimal enclosing controller under a critic-only adaptive dynamic programming (ADP) is explored, approximating the solution of a novel Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman (HJB) equation via pursuing the minimum cost associated with transformed errors. Especially, a novel prescribed time learning rule driven by weight errors is elaborated by revisiting real-time and historical information, such that the convergence of weights is only determined by a user-defined time constant. The prominent merit is that the optimal enclosing with performance guarantees can be achieved by a prescribed time ADP. Lyapunov stability demonstrates that involved error variables are ultimately limited and resultant controller satisfies optimality. Finally, simulations verify the values and superiority of the proposed methodology.

title: Rural Spatial Evolution Characteristics in the Suburbs of Guangzhou Based on ArcGIS and Dual Quantitative Models
authors: Hua Huang, Chen Zhang, Xiaolan Zhuo
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: To reveal the dynamic evolution law of spatial resources in rural areas of urban outer suburbs, this study takes 20 towns and streets in the outer suburbs of Guangzhou as the research object, selects four time sections from 2005 to 2020, and based on the 30m-resolution China Land Use/Cover Dataset (CNLUCC). It integrates ArcGIS spatial analysis and Python automated processing technology to construct a system of “remote sensing interpretation - technical modeling - spatial visualization”, and collaboratively applies the land use dynamic degree and transfer matrix models to analyze the evolution characteristics of primary and secondary spatial types and the law of land use type transfer. The results show that from 2005 to 2020, the regional space presented three-stage characteristics: “urban space expanded rapidly first and then stabilized, agricultural space continued to shrink but at a slowing rate, and ecological space remained overall stable but sensitive areas decreased”. During the core urbanization expansion period from 2005 to 2010, the dynamic degree of urban space reached 13.99 %, and after 2010, it shifted to the coordinated development of “development protection - sustainability”. The standardized technical framework constructed in this study provides scientific support for the optimization of territorial space planning and urban-rural integrated development in rural areas of outer suburbs.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)

title: Generative Adversarial Networks for Facies Prediction in Subsurface Modeling
authors: Ajeet Ram Pathak
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)
abstract: Facies classification represents a fundamental component of subsurface modeling, directly influencing reservoir characterization accuracy and seismic interpretation reliability. Traditional seismic inversion techniques frequently encounter challenges related to wave propagation non-linearity and substantial uncertainties from limited subsurface observations. This study presents a data-driven methodology leveraging Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Networks with Gradient Penalty to generate geologically realistic facies distributions from the SEAM Phase I Elastic Earth Model. The workflow encompasses acoustic impedance derivation from velocity and density models, binary lithology proxy generation through percentilebased thresholding, stability-enhanced WGAN-GP architecture design incorporating instance noise and exponential moving average mechanisms, quantitative evaluation using spectral distance metrics, and systematic model selection through convergence diagnostics. The architecture achieves a spectrum distance of 2.61 while maintaining class balance with generated facies proportions of 0.47 matching training distributions. Comparative analysis with conventional geostatistical methods reveals substantial improvements in training stability, sample diversity, and predictive reliability. Visual inspection of generated $1024 \times 1024$ mosaics validates spatial continuity and geological plausibility across multiple scales. These results establish the viability of deep generative models for scalable subsurface facies modeling in high-performance computing environments, with direct applicability to uncertainty quantification workflows requiring multiple equiprobable realizations.

title: An Al-Driven System For DDoS Detection And Forecasting
authors: Raja. J, Anugu Madhav Reddy, Gadhari Sanjay Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Distributed denial of service (DDoS) attacks, which can overload servers and networks and prevent authorized users from accessing essential services, are one of the most dangerous threats to modern digital infrastructures. These attacks cost companies money and damage their reputation in addition to causing downtime. To address this growing problem, the proposed study offers an artificial intelligence (AI)-powered framework for detecting and forecasting DDoS attacks using state-of-the-art machine learning techniques. The UNSW-NB15 dataset, which represents contemporary attack behaviors and provides a more accurate benchmark than earlier datasets, is used in the study. Two ensemble-based algorithms, Random Forest and XGBoost, were employed to classify and predict DDoS traffic. Random Forest’s accuracy of 89% was marginally lower than XGBoost’s 90%. The experimental results demonstrate that ensemble learning techniques are effective in distinguishing malicious DDoS traffic from legitimate network activity, achieving high accuracy and robust generalization across attack patterns. Crucially, the framework incorporates predictive analytics to enable the forecasting of attack likelihood, going beyond detection. Organizations are able to foresee threats and fortify defenses prior to disruptions thanks to this proactive approach. The results demonstrate how AI-driven models can improve cybersecurity resilience and provide a promising path for further study in predictive defense tactics.

title: Advanced Encryption Algorithm for Medical Image Security Using Chaotic Systems and DNA Computing
authors: Jayanti Rout, Ashutosh Soni, Swatisipra Das, Mrutyunjaya Sathua, Surendra Kumar Nanda
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: The medical image is important in digital healthcare systems. They are used to represent different types of scans and diagnostic results. Their illicit modifications can lead to improper medical treatment and harm life. As a result, they require proper security. Various existing encryption algorithms are prone to different cryptanalysis techniques. This necessitates the requirement for an efficient cryptosystem to secure images with minimal compromise in efficiency. This paper presents a novel multi-layer cryptosystem specifically designed for medical imaging applications. It combines chaotic systems, fractal geometry, DNA computing, and reversible matrix operations. This approach was tested on medical CT scans. It achieved an high Number of Pixels Change Rate (NPCR) of 99.605, an entropy of 7.999 bits for the encrypted image, and various other ideal metrics. It also achieves a perfect reconstruction without error. The entire encryption and decryption required 0.8s and 0.7s, respectively. Various other assessments demonstrate the robustness of the approach against various cyberattacks. The domain-agnostic design of the approach makes it flexible to deploy in diverse application scenarios. The perfect reconstruction with robust architectural design makes it ideal for a modern healthcare environment.

title: An application of Neural Network for Human Performance Modelling
authors: Keattikorn Samarnggoon, Mohammad S. Hasan, Hongnian Yu, Russell Campion
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)
abstract: Human Performance Modelling (HPM) is needed in many applications e.g. predicting driver performance for semi-autonomous vehicles, eSports gamers, adaptive machines in manufacturing to improve safety, productivity or a response from a machine. However, it is often challenging to establish a reliable HPM. This paper proposes and validates Neural Network (NN) models for human performance using a simulated Inverted Pendulum Driven Capsule Model (IPDCM) platform as a case study. The joystick data is utilised to build a classification and a regression model to predict the IPDCM’s movement direction and position, respectively. Then the number of neurons in the single hidden layer is optimised for both models using 10-time-10-fold cross-validation. Finally, the optimised classification and regression NN models are applied to a blind test dataset which exhibits accuracies of 92.2% and 77.01%, respectively.

title: Deep Audio–Visual Feature Extraction for Multimodal Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: Mamta Goyal, Vivek Srivastava
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: This work shows a deep audio-visual feature extraction framework for multimodal speech emotion recognition. Such an approach aims for robust emotion classification by jointly employing complementary information from speech and facial expression variables. On the audio side, handcrafted descriptors such as MFCC-based prosodic features are combined with CNN–BiLSTM embeddings obtained from log-Mel spectrograms in order to capture both low-level and high-level temporal patterns. The visual branch extracts facecentered frames, applies a fine-tuned residual network to obtain deep spatial features, and models expression dynamics with recurrent or transformer-based encoders and self-attention. Individual modality embeddings are projected into a shared latent space and jointly fused by a gated cross-modal attention mechanism that identifies the more reliable modality for each utterance dynamically. Experiments on benchmark multimodal emotion corpora have revealed that the proposed hybrid audio representation and gated fusion considerably outperform unimodal baselines and very simple concatenation with considerable improvement in accuracy, UAR, and macro-F1 score. This shows that a principled design of features with adaptive multimodal fusion plays a key role for the accurate and robust speech emotion recognition system.

title: A Hybrid LSTM Forecasting Models with Dual Signal Decomposition: A Comparative Study for Energy Consumption
authors: Fatima Zohra Oulad Laid, Khaled Ferkous, Charaf Abdelkarim Mosbah, Soundous Sekayar, Abderahmane Bellaour
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Accurate short-term electricity load forecasting is vital for grid reliability and renewable integration. This study evaluates Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) models enhanced with four decomposition methods—WD, WPD, CEEMDAN, and VMD—tested in three settings: raw LSTM, single-stage hybrids, and dualstage hybrids. Using five years of hourly consumption data from a Turkish Mediterranean city, results show that hybrid models significantly outperform the raw LSTM. CEEMDAN-based configurations achieve the highest accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2} > 97\%$</tex>, nRMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 3 \%$</tex>). Overall, dual-decomposition hybrids offer robust, precise forecasts for energy management. While findings are promising, validation on broader datasets is needed, and future work will address scalability and computational efficiency

title: Existing statistical measures and techniques using Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning in diagnosis and prognosis of lung cancer using multi-modal medical images: A systematic review
authors: Sivabarathi M, Kathirvelan J
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Lung cancer creates a tremendous source of cancer deaths around the world, calling attention to the requirements of accurate, automated interpretation of lung tumours and predictive approaches to enable effective treatment. "Artificial intelligence and machine learning" were playing a crucial role in localising and classifying lung cancer using "multi-modal medical imaging modalities, including X-ray, Computed Tomography (CT), Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) and Positron Emission Tomography (PET)." This systematic review synthesises current research on statistical and computational methods for examining lung cancer, using deep learning models with fusion strategies. This analysis highlights the strengths, limitations, and research gaps in the literature, indicating that hybrid attention-based approaches may surpass traditional techniques. The findings of multi-modal image combinations such as "x-rays and CT scans by modified AlexNet (MAN) with support vector machine (SVM) classifier" achieved accuracy of 97.27%, "CT and PET scans by MAN-SVM classifier" attained accuracy of 98.10%, "X-rays and CT scans with gold standard data (Histopathological data) by Inception-ResNet" has 95.35% of accuracy, also "CT and PET scans with clinical and genomic data by bi-directional encoder representation from image transformer (BEiT) based vision transformer model" has 94.0±0.7% of accuracy, which suggest that while AI-driven models hold significant promise for lung cancer diagnosis, further progress in dataset standardisation and cross-model fusion is essential for successful professional execution.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)

title: Experimental Energy and Latency Analysis in Multi-Environment MQTT IoT Deployments
authors: Izdihar Wanda Syahputra, Tohari Ahmad
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) enables continuous environmental monitoring using low power devices; however, the impact of environmental variability and communication behavior on node level energy consumption is still not well understood in real world deployments. This study evaluates a three node ESP32 based monitoring system operating for 14 days in outdoor, semi-indoor, and indoor environments for mung bean cultivation. Each node measures temperature, soil moisture, light intensity, voltage, current, and latency, and transmits data to both local and cloud MQTT brokers. The results reveal clear differences across environments, reflected in sensing frequency, current usage, and total energy consumption, while correlation analysis shows that individual environmental parameters exhibit weak relationships with overall energy use. Unlike most existing MQTT energy studies that rely on simulations or single environment indoor experiments, this work presents a measurement driven analysis based on real voltage, current, and reconstructed active time data collected from a multi environment agricultural deployment. Furthermore, the study evaluates application level MQTT delivery latency using a dual broker architecture, which differs from pure network round trip time (RTT) analysis.

title: Efficient Human Facial Emotion Recognition by SNN inspired CNN Features Based model on Video data
authors: Anu Roopa Devi S, Ruban Nersisson
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Human emotion identification from video data is a challenging but crucial endeavour. It can be used in smart systems, mental health monitoring, and human-computer interaction. The proposed methodology examines the performance effects of various feature extraction techniques and classification algorithms. A stand-alone AlexNet model and a hybrid strategy that combines data from InceptionNet and AlexNet are the two feature extraction techniques being contrasted. Through the use of threshold encoding, spikes are produced from these features and fed into Support Vector Machines (SVM) and k-Nearest Neighbours (kNN), two conventional machine learning classifiers. Using the first 10 actors from the RAVDESS dataset, our experiments demonstrate that the hybrid AlexNet + InceptionNet feature extraction outperforms standalone AlexNet, achieving 95% accuracy with SVM and 90.83% with KNN, compared to AlexNet’s 76.66% (SVM) and 64.16% (KNN) accuracy. This study demonstrates the possibility of integrating SNN-inspired spike encoding with potent pre-trained deep learning models for reliable emotion recognition.

title: Conference Committee: CECCC 2025
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 7th International Communication Engineering and Cloud Computing Conference (CECCC)

title: A Self-Healing 6G Network Model Using AI-Driven Predictive Analytics
authors: Brijesh Kumar Sharma, Jofia Jose Prakash, Abhishek Dwivedi, Vikas Sharma, Shivanshi Singh, Vineeta Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The 6G era heralds communication networks requiring strongly autonomous, resilient, and self-sustaining architectures capable of providing continuity of service under dynamic and complex operating conditions. This paper presents a Self-Healing 6G Network Model driven by AI-based predictive analytics to anticipate, analyze and self-heal faults in the network before they can impact service quality. The model incorporates deep learning-driven anomaly detection, reinforcement-learning-based fault prediction, and automated reparation policies with the support of Software-defined Networking (SDN) and edge intelligence. By leveraging real-time telemetry, spatio-temporal traffic patterns and multi-domain context-sensitive data, the model achieves early fault prognostication, intelligent root-cause analysis, and autonomous reconfiguration of network resources. Experimental software simulations yield improved accuracy of fault detection, reduced downtime and enhanced Quality of Service (QoS) when compared with traditional reactive self-healing mechanisms using Python MATLAB. The suggestions point towards the potential of AI self-healing as a fundamental foundation block for the ultra-reliable, zero-touch 6G communication networks of the future.

title: Transformer with Self Attention Mechanism for Electric Motor Fault Detection
authors: Ashish Kumar Sinha, Prince, Prashant Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: Reliable fault detection in electric motors is essential in ensuring operational safety, minimizing downtime, and lowering maintenance costs in modern industrial systems. Long-range temporal relationships and intricate nonlinear patterns found in motor state monitoring data are frequently difficult for conventional signal processing methods and conventional deep learning models to capture. The present manuscript suggests a transformer-based fault detection model with a self-attention mechanism for intelligent electric motor fault diagnosis to address these issues. The proposed methodology enables efficient feature learning without relying on handcrafted features or recurrent neural networks. This is done by directly modeling temporal correlations within multi-sensor motor signals using the Transformer architecture. Under different operating settings, the self-attention mechanism improves sensitivity to subtle fault characteristics by adaptively highlighting signal segments that are relevant to the defect. To learn discriminative fault representations, raw or minimally preprocessed vibration and/or stator current inputs are segmented and embedded before being processed by stacked attention layers. Benchmark motor fault datasets under various operating situations are used to validate the efficacy of the suggested approach. The Transformer-based model consistently outperforms traditional convolutional and recurrent neural network approaches in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, according to experimental results. Furthermore, the model has enhanced generalization performance and great resistance to noise, suggesting its appropriateness for practical industrial applications. These findings demonstrate that attention-based transformer designs provide a potent and scalable solution for sophisticated electric motor status monitoring and problem detection.

title: Hybrid Energy Model for Underground Mining: Integrating Diesel Generator, Fuel Cells, and Batteries
authors: Venkatesh G, K.R.M. Vijaya Chandrakala, Sampath Kumar S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: This paper operates on the detailing of hybrid isolated power system design, model building, simulative study and performance evaluation of hybrid isolated power system for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50 \text{HP}(37.3 ~\text{kW})$</tex> induction motor (IM). The IM should run continuously with the complete energy requirement of 149.2 kWh for 4 hours utilizing hybrid isolated power system as a backup system. The system consists of a 15 kW proton exchange membrane (PEM) fuel cell, 100 kWh lithium-ion battery, and a diesel generator with four different topologies. The model was simulated and modeled using MATLAB/Simulink ® on editor platform where parameters of every component were tuned to achieve the stable operation, best transient response with the fast dynamic behavior. The PEM fuel cell was fed with DC-DC boost converter and inverter. The lithium-ion battery component relates to the bidirectional DC-DC converter to Battery Management System (BMS), while diesel generator has an automatic voltage regulator (AVR). A control strategy whereby the control unit can provide source transition, load sharing and real-time co-ordination of the elements. The primary performance index of the work is voltage regulation in dynamic situations, Total harmonic distortion < 3% and maintaining the system efficiency above 90 % with green incentive certified system with lower carbon emission footprints

title: Integrating AI and IoT for Sustainable Operational Performance in the FMCG Industry
authors: A.Jagadhambal, R Priyadharshini, Pavithra R, Nandhini M, Aisurya M M, Dinesh Kumar K
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: The manufacturers of fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) have been working under pressure to maintain the high quality of products, lower their prices, and at the same time comply with the world sustainability requirements. Current operational decision-making systems including many rule-based or manually optimized ones, do not deal with the complexity and real-time variability of current production and logistics systems. A new AI-IoT-based Sustainable Operations Optimization Model, AISO-FMCG, that combines resource sensing with the help of IoT, AI-based demand prediction, sustainability-aware optimization of supply chains, and a digital-twin simulation engine. The proposed model has a 57.1% downtime reduction, 91.6% forecast accuracy, 33.3% inventory cost reduction, 23.1% energy savings, 28% carbon reduction, and 35.2% packaging waste reduction, which is a significant improvement over FMCG operations based on multi-modal FMCG data and synthetic operational data. Scalability and robustness of the model are proven by the simulation experiments with MATLAB. The findings indicate that AISO- FMCG is a replicable data-driven framework that can provide sustainable, resilient, and cost-effective FMCG manufacturing.

title: Examination of Power System Stability in High-Penetration Renewable Scenarios
authors: Bishro.H. Rasool, Asra. H. Al-Denenawe, Nikolay V. Korovkin
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: The proliferation of inverter-based resources, such as wind and solar photovoltaics, is driving a fundamental transformation of the global energy landscape. This is a necessary step toward decarbonization, but it also presents significant challenges to power system stability. This paper discusses some of the stability-related problems that are being experienced in systems with high renewable penetration. We review the decline in system inertia and its direct implications for frequency stability, the impact on voltage regulation, and the emergence of sub-synchronous oscillations. Time-domain simulations on a modified IEEE 9-bus test system are conducted to compare the dynamic performance under a conventional and a high-renewable penetration scenario. Results quantitatively show a 65% increase in Rate-of-Change-of-Frequency (RoCoF) and a 0.28 pu deeper frequency nadir following a generation loss contingency. The paper concludes by discussing mitigations, including grid-forming inverters, synthetic inertia, and advanced energy storage systems, that are integral to ensuring the secure and reliable operation of future power systems.

title: All-in-One Initialization of MEMS-INS for Tilt-Compensated GNSS RTK Receivers
authors: Jiahao Ai, Qijin Chen, Xiaoji Niu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: As an enhanced solution of global navigation satellite system (GNSS) real-time kinematics (RTK), RTK with tilt compensation function (tilt RTK) eliminates the need for manual pole leveling during surveying and expands the capability of high-precision RTK to some constrained environment. Through integrating a low-cost microelectromechanical system (MEMS) inertial measurement unit (IMU), tilt RTK derives tilt compensation from inertial navigation system (INS) attitude outputs. However, the navigation states of the INS, including position, velocity, attitude and preferably inertial sensor errors, must be initialized before being used to provide a navigation solution. The existing INS initialization solutions are often implemented step by step, typically requiring both static and dynamic conditions to complete the initialization of all parameters. Due to the lack of specific design for tilt RTK application, these initialization methods prove time-consuming and labor-intensive when applied to tilt RTK scenarios. A rapid and accurate initialization method for estimating all navigation states and inertial sensor errors remains vacancy for low-cost MEMS IMU in tilt RTK equipment. To address this issue, we propose an all-in-one initialization method for low-cost MEMS IMU in tilt RTK receiver, through swinging the surveying pole with its tip contact on the ground at a fixed point. The Corresponding pole motion scheme for such comprehensive initialization is designed. Through simulation and field test, we validate the function, efficiency, and accuracy of the proposed algorithm. The field test demonstrates that an 80-second swinging initialization achieves an attitude alignment accuracy (root mean square error, RMSE) of approximately 0.2 degrees, with velocity initialization accuracy of less than 1 cm/s for horizontal direction and 0.3 cm/s for vertical direction. Furthermore, the calibration precision (standard deviation, STD) is around 25 deg/h for gyroscope bias, and 600 mGal for accelerometer bias.

title: Automated PI Parameter Optimization for LLRF Systems: A Comparative Study of LLM-guided parameter tuning and Bayesian Optimization Approaches
authors: Lijuan Yang, Yilin Miao, Shihui Wei, Zhenglong Zhu, Zhen Gao, Zhaojie Chen, Qizhen Hou, Jiayi Peng, Jinying Ma, Chengye Xu, Xinghao Ding, Guirong Huang, Liepeng Sun, Rihua Zeng, Zhijun Wang, Feng Qiu, Yuan He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: This paper presents a comparative study of LLM-guided parameter tuning and Bayesian Optimization (BO) for automated PI parameter adjustment in Low Level Radio Frequency (LLRF) systems. Both methods demonstrate effective performance across continuous wave and pulsed operation modes, addressing the challenges of traditional manual tuning in superconducting accelerator facilities. Multi-cavity validation experiments were conducted on both operation modes. For continuous wave 2-parameter optimization, both methods converge to optimal regions within 15 iterations. For pulsed 6-parameter optimization involving coupled amplitude-phase control loops, both methods achieve satisfactory results in around 20 iterations, meeting stability requirements for beam operation. Comparative analysis reveals distinct application advantages: BO is better suited for large-scale batch optimization scenarios, particularly excelling in multi-cavity parallel commissioning and high-dimensional parameter space exploration due to its systematic probabilistic framework. The LLM-guided approach is more appropriate for online optimization scenarios with strict operational constraints, where its conservative optimization strategy and traceable reasoning process make it ideal for parameter fine-tuning during operations. The study provides practical method selection guidelines for intelligent operation of large-scale accelerator facilities.

title: Deep-Saliency Foveated Ray Tracing for Real-time VR Rendering
authors: Yang Gao, Wencan Li, Shiyu Liang, Weizichuan Feng, Qing Xia, Shuai Li, Aimin Hao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Immersive VR applications demand high resolutions and refresh rates, posing significant challenges for real-time rendering. Foveated rendering mitigates this cost by exploiting properties of the Human Visual System (HVS), but conventional approaches often rely on oversimplified heuristic models that neglect high-level attentional cues, resulting in artifacts in peripheral regions. To this end, we present a neural saliency-driven foveated ray tracing framework that overcomes these limitations. Our method introduces a motion-aware foveation model to capture temporal dynamics and employs a lightweight convolutional neural network to predict saliency maps that reflect complex attentional patterns derived from eye-gaze data. The combination of these guides adaptive path tracing and filtering, enabling perceptually optimized rendering with minimal artifacts. Experimental results show that our approach improves perceptual quality over prior methods while sustaining real-time performance.

title: Dual Body Sensation: Effects of Visio-Tactile Presentation Methods in Parallel Agent Control
authors: Masatoshi Serizawa, Peerawat Pannattee, Yosuke Fukuchi, Vibol Yem, Yasushi Ikei, Nobuyuki Nishiuchi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Controlling multiple agents in parallel is an important challenge in human–robot interaction, with growing applications in remote collaboration, telepresence, and multi-agent coordination. However, empirical work on distributed embodiment remains limited, particularly in scenarios where independently controlled avatars operate in distinct environments. To address this gap, we propose the concept of Dual Body Sensation (DBS): a novel theoretical construct of distributed embodiment, describing a state in which a single operator simultaneously experiences embodiment for two independent robots in distinct environments during their control. We conducted a within-subject experiment to investigate the cognitive feasibility of DBS using a 2×3 factorial design that varied visual presentation (independent view, superimposed view) and tactile presentation (no tactile, bilateral tactile, unified tactile). The results indicated that DBS emerged across all conditions and was significantly enhanced under the unified tactile condition. The combination of the superimposed view and unified tactile conditions also increased the sense of body ownership and the sense of agency, while the superimposed view condition reduced cybersickness and cognitive load. These findings provide empirical support for DBS and inform the design of future multi-agent interaction systems that support flexible distribution of bodily control and awareness.

title: TriCloud: Drug-Target-Disease Ternary Network for Drug Repositioning Research Based on Point Cloud Modeling
authors: Xiwei Tang, Wanjun Ma, Anzheng Gao, Mengyun Yang, Weijun Liang, Wenjun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: In recent years, the “drug-target-disease” association has become increasingly complex and data-scarce. Existing methods are limited by information loss caused by explicit graph construction when modeling triplet relationships, making it difficult to effectively capture geometric structures and long-range dependencies, thereby affecting prediction performance. To address this, this paper proposes a new method based on point cloud modeling—TriCloud. This method represents each triad as a spatial point cloud, encoding semantic and topological relationships through geometric coordinates, and performs feature learning directly on an unordered point set, avoiding reliance on predefined graph structures. Based on the PointNet architecture, it introduces a multi-view feature extraction and fusion mechanism to enhance the modeling capability of global structures and complex spatial patterns. Experimental results show that TriCloud significantly outperforms existing methods on multiple benchmark datasets, achieving an AUC of 0.9995 and an AUPR of 0.9996, with all metrics ranking first. External validation demonstrates its excellent generalization ability. Feature analysis reveals that the geometric-semantic joint features of positive samples play a dominant role in classification. This study provides an efficient and reliable computational framework for drug repurposing, contributing to the development of precision medicine.

title: A Novel Indicator for Improving the Fault-Tolerant Bipancyclicity of k -Ary n -Cube Networks with Exponential Faulty Edges
authors: Hongbin Zhuang, Jou-Ming Chang, Xiaohua Jia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Networking
abstract: The k-ary n-cube Qkn is a widely employed interconnection network for data center networks (DCNs) due to its favorable properties, including node/edge symmetry, recursive structure, regularity, and ease of deployment. Among the numerous structures studied in Qkn, the cycle is fundamental for addressing various graph-related problems in DCNs. This has motivated extensive research on fault-tolerant cycle embedding within Qkn. However, existing methods for cycle embedding often exhibit limited fault tolerance since they fail to account for the dimension-oriented nature of edge faults in Qkn. In this paper, we overcome this limitation by using the partitioned edge fault (PEF) model, a recently developed model that explicitly considers dimension-specific fault distributions. Under the PEF model, we propose a new indicator called partition-edge fault-tolerant bipancyclicity, which accurately reflects the practical fault characteristics of Qkn. For Qkn with even k ≥ 4, we determine the exact value of this indicator and prove its optimality under the PEF model. Moreover, for Qkn with odd k ≥ 3, we develop three fault-tolerant embedding algorithms that establish a lower bound for this indicator. Furthermore, we conduct numerical evaluations and simulation experiments to analyze the bipancyclicity of Qkn under varying scales of edge faults.

title: From Spectral Detection to Economic Decision: A Hyperspectral AI Framework for Business-Oriented Varroa Mite Control
authors: Jihan Lee, Aiden Lee, Taewoo Park, Sangho Sun, Abhijit Debnath
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The Varroa destructor mite is a leading cause of colony losses, yet most hyperspectral studies end at pixel-level detection and do not indicate when treatment is economically justified. The leakage-controlled testing with spatially blocked and Leave-One-Tile-Out (LOTO) protocols demonstrates strong within-scene robustness under conservative testing, without explicitly stating that cross-scene generalization is the case in view of the current annotation scope. The spectral-to-economic decision framework used here is validated with respect to internal consistency as well as operational feasibility, with a transition to true leave-one-scene-out evaluation postponed for future work upon obtaining additional annotations. Instead of proposing a new spectral backbone, the present work presents a decision-oriented hyperspectral framework based on strong infestation risk estimation while explicitly economically optimizing to ensure interpretability and reliability under limited annotation. Pixel-level probabilities produced by a support vector machine and a random forest are aggregated into a scene-level Hyperspectral Infestation Risk Index, which is then mapped to expected monetary loss. The operating rule is chosen by maximizing net benefit, with a closed-form threshold τ ⋆ ≈ c/(λpy) based on treatment cost c, loss cap λ, honey price p, and expected yield y. Numerical validation shows that, on the BUT-HS1 dataset, the models achieve a macro area under the precision–recall curve of 1.00 and a macro area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 1.00 under standard splits. Under a spatially conservative leave-one-tile-out protocol with buffered regions and hard negative mining, the weighted accuracy is 0.896 with only localized failures at tile boundaries, confirming genuine spectral separability. Decision-curve analysis indicates that, at 2023 United States price and cost levels, τ ⋆ = 0 (treat all) maximizes value; when per-hive treatment cost rises to 8 dollars, τ ⋆ ≈ 0.09 defers about 16–17% of treatments while retaining about 98% of avoidable loss, reduces profit-at-risk by $18–$26 per scene, and saves$2–$8 per colony per application.

title: Virtual Synchronous Compensator with Virtual Braking Resistor for Stable DC-link Voltage Control
authors: A. Camboni, J. Roldán-Pérez, F. Mandrile, M. Prodanovic, R. Bojoi
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: The Virtual Synchronous Machine (VSM) algorithm represents a well-established control method for interfacing renewable energy sources with the grid. However, VSM suffers from transient stability issues, particularly when operating as a Virtual Synchronous Generator (VSG). Furthermore, maintaining control stability can be more challenging when the DC-link voltage control is implemented. Indeed, the DC-link voltage is controlled by regulating the power exchanged with the grid. However, the injected power can be limited by the applied current saturation strategy, potentially leading to DC-link overvoltage. Therefore, this article proposes implementing a Virtual Synchronous Compensator (VSC) algorithm to regulate the DC-link voltage. The VSC structure ensures the control remains stable even under current-saturation conditions. Furthermore, the Virtual Braking Resistor (VBR) concept is introduced to keep DC-link operation within the permitted voltage range without requiring communication between the DC/DC and DC/AC converters. The proposed control solution has been experimentally validated using a 30 kVA converter setup and a grid emulator, meeting both AC and DC test requirements.

title: A 71–86-GHz 1024-QAM Direct-Carrier Phase-Modulating Transmitter With Digital-to-Phase Converter and Constant-Envelope Phasor Combiner
authors: Jia Zhou, Jieqiong Du, Chao-Jen Tien, Christopher Chen, Jhih-Wei Chen, Lachlan Cuskelly, Francisco Cardenas Beltran, Adrian Tang, Sai-Wang Tam, Mau-Chung Frank Chang
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits
abstract: This article presents a 71–86-GHz 1024-QAM direct-carrier phase-modulating transmitter (DCPM-TX) that synthesizes high-order QAM symbols using phase modulation only. The proposed approach employs dual 9-bit digital-to-phase converters (DTPCs) and a constant-envelope phasor combiner. Each DTPC uses coarse/fine-segmented digitally controlled switched-capacitor transmission lines (SCTLs) (true-time-delay operation) plus a sign mixer, providing a 360° phase range and sub-1° rms phase error while preserving wide carrier and modulation bandwidth. To avoid linear power amplifier (PA) back-off, the modulated phasors are driven into saturation and combined to realize high output power over 71–86 GHz. Using direct-digital-synthesis control with on-chip SRAM, the transmitter also generates a 77–81-GHz FMCW chirp in 5 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s with 84 kHz (0.0021%) rms frequency modulation (FM) error. The prototype DCPM-TX achieves 32 and 25.6 Gbps using 1024-QAM at 73 and 83 GHz, respectively, with peak P<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">out</sub> over 16 dBm while consuming 340-mW DC power and 0.675-mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> core silicon area on 28-nm bulk CMOS.

title: Hyperloop Communications: Intra-tube Channel Capacity Enhancement via Multi-layered Pod Structures
authors: J. Kim, K. J. Han, H. Kim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Hyperloop, a futuristic transportation system, requires robust intra-tube wireless communications for safety and passenger connectivity. Specifically, wireless communications enable seamless real-time monitoring and control of pods, while also aiming at providing passengers with high-capacity and reliable Internet access. This paper evaluates per-pod downlink channel capacity using an electromagnetic (EM) propagation analysis methodology. Conventional metallic pods, widely proposed in Hyperloop designs, fail to meet onboard Internet service demands due to intra-tube interference. Therefore, we introduce two novel pod structures: a double-layered design with an EM absorbing outer layer covering a metallic inner shell, and a triple-layered design with dual EM absorbing outer layers. Evaluations demonstrated these configurations successfully suppress interference, achieving sufficient capacity to support 4K ultra high definition (UHD) video streaming for every in-cabin passenger. Furthermore, we optimized channel capacity by adjusting EM absorbing layer thickness and selecting the optimal EM propagation mode. These findings highlight the importance of the pod structural design in meeting the high-capacity requirements for Hyperloop wireless communications.

title: A Two-Stage Modulation Strategy for Active Power Sharing Control of Open-End Winding PMSM With Isolated DC Source
authors: Guozheng Zhang, Yufei Liu, Xin Gu, Tingna Shi, Changliang Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: The open-end winding permanent magnet synchronous motor (OW-PMSM) with isolated DC source offers a flexible DC source configuration. Thus, the active control of the charging and discharging of both inverters is required to accommodate the state of charge of different DC sources. A novel two-stage modulation strategy of “power sharing” and “reference voltage synthesis” is proposed in this paper to realize the active power control of the OW-PMSM with isolated DC source. First, two new clamping modulation modes are presented by making full use of the redundant switching states of the dual-inverter. Then three power modulation methods are established by the combination of different clamping modes, which allows the bidirectional energy flow between the two DC sources. Meanwhile, the basic voltage vector sequence to synthesize the reference voltage remains the same under any arbitrary power modulation methods. Consequently, the output current waveform quality of the dual-inverter is not affected. Finally, the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed strategy are validated by the experimental prototype of a dual-inverter fed 4.3 kW OW-PMSM.

title: Separating the Road from the Driver: A Clustering Approach to Unbiased Fuel Efficiency Analysis
authors: João G. S. Botelho, Rodrigo Balani, Janaine D. Rodrigues, Eduardo A. P. Santos, E. P. José
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Fuel efficiency comparisons across different driver types are often misleading because they fail to account for substantial variations in driving context, such as urban versus highway operation. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a context-aware framework that applies clustering techniques to connected vehicle data to group trips into homogeneous driving contexts. Using the k-means algorithm on a large-scale dataset of vehicles operating across Europe, we show that trip-level features such as speed, distance, duration, and the proportion of time spent within specific velocity intervals (e.g., stationary time) are sufficient to reliably identify distinct driving contexts. This work makes three core contributions. First, we introduce a privacy-preserving method for driving context identification that relies exclusively on low-frequency, non-sensitive telematics data, eliminating the need for GPS trajectories. Second, we establish an empirical framework to disentangle and normalize environment-induced biases, enabling fair fuel efficiency comparisons by restricting performance evaluation to drivers operating under comparable conditions. Third, we validate the proposed approach using real-world data, demonstrating that the identified contexts exhibit statistically distinct fuel efficiency distributions, thereby highlighting the necessity of context-based separation for unbiased performance ranking. Beyond supporting fair fuel efficiency assessment and CO2 reduction strategies, the proposed framework is shown to be applicable to fleet behavior analysis, enhanced recommendation systems, and product development tasks, such as electric vehicle battery sizing.

title: Deploy-Safe Day-Ahead Multi-Step PV Power Forecasting Using Meteo-Only Inputs
authors: G. Tulum, V. Esen, A.S. Sarkın, T. Dindar, E. Alp
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This study investigates day-ahead multi-step photovoltaic (PV) power forecasting with 15-minute resolution in a realistic deployment-safe framework based solely on meteorological inputs. Using real-world field data from December 2020–January 2025 for a PV plant with a capacity of approximately 110 kWe–129 kWp located in Konya, Turkey, a 96-step generation profile is predicted sequentially for each day. Unlike previous studies, delayed plant measurements are completely excluded, and only meteorological variables and deterministic time features are used. The proposed end-to-end workflow includes day-level quality control, input-output alignment analysis, deploy-safe feature engineering, and two-stage feature selection. Deep learning (BiLSTM, LSTM, Transformer), classical machine learning (Boosting, ElasticNet, ExtraTrees, MLP), and NBEATSx-based models are compared under a common evaluation protocol. Experimental results show that the many-to-many BiLSTM architecture provides the best performance (RMSE = 9.487 kW, R² = 0.92) and that boosting-based classical methods offer a robust alternative with similar accuracy. The residual correction module added to NBEATSx improved performance by up to 4%, particularly in absolute error metrics. Log-error analyses reveal heavy-tailed behavior driven by operational noise across all models. The findings confirm the superiority of deep trailing models in safe deployment scenarios operating with only meteorological inputs, while also highlighting the practical applicability of classical methods.

title: Overview of Vehicle Localization: The Shift from Egocentric to Cooperative Positioning
authors: Ivo Silva, Hélder Silva, Fabricio Botelho, Jorge Cabral, Cristiano Pendão
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Accurate and reliable positioning is essential for a wide range of vehicular applications, including navigation, traffic management, Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), and autonomous driving. Traditionally, vehicle localization has relied on egocentric approaches where each vehicle estimates its own position using onboard sensors. Since the introduction of Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS), these methods have greatly advanced with multiple satellite constellations and correction techniques such as Real-Time Kinematic (RTK) and Precise Point Positioning (PPP). Yet, GNSS-based positioning remains challenged by signal obstructions in urban canyons, tunnels, and dense foliage. To overcome these limitations, sensor fusion combines data from inertial sensors, cameras, LiDAR, and radar to enhance accuracy and robustness, especially in GNSS-degraded conditions. Recently, the rise of vehicular communications has enabled a new paradigm: cooperative positioning. By exchanging data among vehicles and infrastructure, this approach mitigates individual sensor errors, improves accuracy, and supports shared situational awareness. This paper presents a comprehensive overview of vehicle localization, covering the transition from egocentric to cooperative positioning. We review key technologies and sensors, analyze cooperative architectures, and classify recent research, providing insights into current trends and future directions in vehicle localization.

title: Prescribed-Time Bipartite Synchronization of Fuzzy Signed Networks via Fuzzy Reinforcement Learning
authors: Tao Dong, Yukang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: This paper investigates the prescribed-time bipartite synchronization (PTBS) problem of fuzzy signed networks (FSNs). To solve this problem, a bipartite synchronization error is constructed and a novel performance value function integrated with the prescribed time and desired accuracy is proposed, which translates the challenge of achieving bipartite synchronization within a prescribed-time into an prescribed-time optimal regulation problem. Subsequently, a fuzzy controller is introduced to address the prescribed-time bipartite synchronization issue. The controller is realized through a fuzzy actor-critic framework, where both actor system and critic system are fuzzy logic system. Additionally, the convergence analysis shows that the bipartite synchronization error can indeed converge to a predefined residual set within the specified time threshold, thereby effectively solving the synchronization problem. Finally, the competitive-cooperative fuzzy recurrent neural network is applied to verify the obtained results. The simulation results show that the proposed algorithm not only enables the network to achieve PTPS, but also exhibits better performance than existing methods.

title: TF-ProFM: Prototype-Based Time–Frequency Foundation Model for Generalizable Industrial Bearing Fault Diagnosis
authors: Songyan Pi, Bojian Chen, Xinmin Zhang, Zhihuan Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: The development of generalizable foundation models for intelligent bearing fault diagnosis is critical for modern industrial systems, yet it is severely hindered by the challenge of handling multisource, heterogeneous data. Existing methods often struggle to adapt to varying operating conditions and diverse signal characteristics. To address this issue, we propose a novel prototype-based time–frequency foundation model (TF-ProFM) that leverages self-supervised pretraining and few-shot fine-tuning. TF-ProFM features a time–frequency embedding module that uses random patching and local fast Fourier transform to process diverse signals into unified spectral representations. For pretraining, it employs a prototype-based contrastive learning objective, which explicitly encourages the model to learn a feature space with a clear cluster structure that aligns with downstream fault classification tasks. Extensive experiments on nine public datasets validate the superiority of our approach. In rigorous cross-dataset evaluations, TF-ProFM achieves the state-of-the-art fault diagnosis performance, outperforming existing methods even when fine-tuned with 1% of the labeled data from the target domain.

title: A Reconfigurable Simultaneous Transmitting and Reflecting Array with Independent and Continuous Controls of Phases and Transmission-Reflection Ratio
authors: Kaixu Wang, Zhitao Du, Peifeng Wang, Zeyu Wu, Xue Ren, Qinyu Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper proposes a novel simultaneous transmitting and reflecting (STAR) array capable of independently manipulating the transmitted phase, reflected phase, and the transmission-reflection (T-R) power ratio. A reconfigurable metasurface (RMTS) employing a novel controlling scheme is proposed in this paper, which can realize a full range tuning on the transmitted/reflective phases synchronously or a hybrid continuous-discrete control in asynchronous mode. Meanwhile, the T-R ratio of the RMTS can be continuously and arbitrarily tuned from total transmission to total reflection without affecting the phases. The core of the RMTS is a hybrid coupler loaded with two reflective phase shifters with independently controllable phases. Based on this RMTS, a 9×9 STAR array with a center operating frequency of 5.8 GHz is constructed to validate the design. Both simulated and measured results demonstrate that the proposed STAR array achieves ± 45° beam scanning independently on both sides with high aperture efficiency while maintaining arbitrary T-R ratio tuning capability. This design enhances the STAR array’s control capabilities over both phase and power division, making it more favorable in future wireless communication systems.

title: Spatio-Temporal Delay Aware Causality: A Self-Interpretable Framework for Soft Sensing
authors: Xueqiong Tian, Han Liu, Runyuan Guo, Lingyun Wei, Ding Liu, Youmin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: With the increasing complexity of industrial systems and process data, deep learning has achieved superior performance in soft sensing but remains constrained by limited interpretability. Most existing interpretability techniques are correlation based, capturing statistical dependencies but providing little insight into underlying mechanisms. Causal modeling, by contrast, offers stronger interpretability by revealing directional and temporal influences, thereby improving both reliability and understanding. Although some recent methods consider time delays, their treatment of lags remains coarse and limited, and cannot adequately capture heterogeneous cross variable delay patterns in industrial time series. To address these limitations, we propose spatio-temporal causal learning with delay annotation (STCLD), which introduces a spatio-temporal delay attention (STDA) module to explicitly learn delay annotated spatio temporal causal graphs for soft sensing. STDA minimizes a maximum mean discrepancy objective to discover causal relations with edge specific delays, while attention path strength and multidimensional dynamic complexity are used to infer causal directions in a model-based way. The learned causal graph and delay information then guide a delay aware prediction module to build a self-interpretable soft sensor. Experiments on two real-world industrial datasets show that STCLD consistently outperforms strong baselines in both predictive accuracy and causal interpretability, providing a robust and general framework for interpretable soft sensor modeling in complex process industries.

title: Background-Noise-Driven Detection of Diffusion-Generated Images
authors: Jiaming Li, Weihua Ou, Deyu Meng, Yong Xu
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Diffusion-based image generation has proliferated, making robust detection of synthetic imagery critical. We propose a background-noise-driven detector motivated by the observation that real camera images preserve physical noise traces, whereas model-generated images tend to exhibit algorithm-induced residual statistics. Starting from sRGB, we apply a deterministic inverse ISP to obtain an approximate Bayer RAW representation, train a lightweight RAW-domain denoiser, and extract a noise residual by subtracting the denoised reconstruction from the inverse-ISP signal. A detector trained only on ADM residuals generalizes in a zero-shot manner to a wide range of unseen diffusion generators. By emphasizing noise-domain cues rather than semantic content, the proposed method offers a simple and practical approach to diffusion-generated image detection.

title: SPR-PCF Sensor with Gold-Coated Flat Surfaces for Tunable RI Detection
authors: Srishti Singh, Dharmendra Kumar, Vijay Shanker Chaudhary, Santosh Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
