abstract: This study details the design and numerical analysis of a highly sensitive photonic crystal fiber (PCF) sensor. This sensor utilizes surface plasmon resonance (SPR) to detect refractive indices between 1.34 and 1.39. The sensor structure consists of a solid-core PCF featuring a hexagonal arrangement of air holes with varying geometries (circular and elliptical), along with a flat gold-coated surface to improve the interaction between guided light and surface plasmons. Finite element method (FEM) simulations are employed to evaluate the sensor’s performance. The sensor demonstrates strong confinement loss peaks that shift with changes in analyte RI, enabling precise detection. With a peak wavelength sensitivity of $6000 \mathrm{~nm} / \mathrm{RIU}$ and an average of $2400 \mathrm{~nm} / \mathrm{RIU}$, the proposed sensor is capable of detecting refractive index changes as small as $1.66 \times 10^{-5}$.

title: Emotion-Aware Learning: A Real-Time Facial Expression Approach
authors: Suraj Singh, Shailendra K. Mishra, Sneha Soni
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The subject of detecting emotions is expanding quickly and is crucial to enhancing human-computer interaction. Recent studie demonstrate significant advancements in facial emotion recognition using computer vision as well as deep learning, particularly with datasets such a FER-2013. Studies do, but also highlight the difficulties that existing approaches still have when integrating various data kinds and preserving accuracy under practical circumstances. Researcher are currently concentrating on multimodal systems that employ text, audio, even brain signals like: EEG in addition to facial expressions to better comprehend emotions in a more comprehensive and context-aware manner in order to get around all these limitations. Based on these findings, a hybrid deep learning system designed specifically for an online learning platforms is presented in this work. The architecture employs Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCNs) to describe temporal emotional dynamics, a Convolutional Block focus Module (CBAM) for adaptive focus on prominent face regions, and ResNet50 for spatial feature extraction. In certain scenarios, efficient preprocessing methods enhance system generalization. With accuracies of 91.86% on RAF-DB, 91.71% on FER2013, 95.85% on CK+, and 97.08% on KDEF, experimental evaluations show cutting-edge performance. Additionally, the system runs in real time, with a GPU speed of 28 frames per second (fps) and a Jetson Nano speed of 15 fps.The review and proposed framework together show that while computer vision and deep learning are useful techniques for identifying emotions, future developments will be made in multimodal, context-aware, and application-specific systems that go beyond controlled environments and achieve dependable real- world deployment.

title: Data-Driven Multimodal Fault Diagnosis and Forecasting for Electric Vehicles with Hybrid Temporal Deep Learning Models
authors: Umesh K. Patil, L. S. Patil, Prashant S. Gurupadagol, Swapnil Gadgune
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) are the future of vehicle engineering because they produce zero emissions and offer better environmental and green energy benefits compared to traditional fuel-powered vehicles. In previous work, charging/discharging faults were used as a predictive fault identification method to enhance operational reliability, safety, and efficiency. To solve this problem, a novel Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT) and Temporal Convolutional Network-Long Short-Term Memory (TCN-LSTM) approach for Battery cell imbalance detection and Motor and inverter faults Input data contain source battery voltage, current, and state of charge (SOC) and motor outcome. Adaptive Noise Cancellation (ANC) preprocessing is applied to reduce noisy multimodal EV data, ensuring reliable distributions for correct fault estimate and prediction. Empirical Wavelet Transform (EWT) is employed for feature extraction, enhancing detection accuracy and computational efficiency. The classification approach should help improve the accuracy of diagnosis and become a controlling implement to promote the effectiveness of conservation and operation of modern EVs. The proposed hybrid classification properly forecasts battery degradation, SOC irregularities, thermal anomalies, motor/inverter faults, and sensor variations under variable driving situations. MATLAB simulations enhance experiment performance, resulting in MAE 0.542, MAPE (%) 54.237, RMSE 0.736 for improving the reliability of EV. This method enables real-time, data-driven decision-making for protective maintenance, enhanced safety, and improved battery lifecycle management

title: A BiLSTM-Based Framework for Toxic Comment Classification
authors: Shubhangi Tirpude, Aayush Tirmanwar, Shravani Bhuwan, Ayush Sachan, Pragati Borkar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Toxic language, including hate speech and harassment, is becoming more prevalent as online discussions expand on social media and forums. Intelligent, automated solutions are required because it is impractical to moderate this content by hand. In order to identify harmful comments, this study presents a deep learning method that combines Natural Language Processing(NLP) and a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory Network (BiLSTM) model. In order for the model to learn from all kinds of harmful comments, we first clean and prepare the text data before addressing the problem of imbalanced categories using a number of strategies. Our BiLSTM model classifies comments into six groups, ranging from hate and threats to general toxicity. With a ROC-AUC score of 0.95 and excellent performance, the system is prepared for online real-time use.

title: An Innovative Energy Management Techniques for Hybrid EVs
authors: Gundi Ice, Sundeep Siddula
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Innovative energy management systems in hybrid electric vehicles (HEVs) have advanced as a result of the move towards sustainable transportation options. This abstract examines the effects of combining ultracapacitors, batteries, and fuel cells in these systems, emphasizing how they may improve vehicle performance and have positive environmental effects. Fuel cells offer high energy efficiency and extended range by converting hydrogen into electricity, while batteries provide substantial energy storage and power for continuous driving and regenerative braking. Ultracapacitors, with their rapid charge and discharge capabilities, handle peak power demands and boost vehicle responsiveness. The synergy of these technologies within a unified energy management system optimizes power distribution, extends vehicle range, reduces emissions, this strategy not only enhances fuel efficiency but also helps cut down on greenhouse gas emissions and reduces reliance on fossil fuels. but also fosters the development of more adaptable and resilient transportation systems. The research highlights the many benefits of these hybrid setups, such as improved performance, more energy efficiency, and more operational flexibility, and it represents a major advancement in environmentally friendly automobile technology.

title: Anomaly Detection in Sensor Data using LSTM Networks for Predictive Maintenance
authors: Atul Srivastava, Geetanjali Singh, Vijay Shankar Sharma, Kamlesh Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Credit card fraud, a pervasive and costly issue, has become increasingly sophisticated as technology advances. Detecting fraudulent transactions promptly is crucial to safeguarding both consumers and financial institutions. This paper explores the challenges and potential solutions associated with credit card fraud detection. The increasing prevalence of fraudulent transactions poses a significant threat to the financial security of individuals and businesses. The increasing prevalence of fraudulent transactions poses a significant threat to the financial security of individuals and businesses. Developing robust and efficient fraud detection systems is essential to mitigate the financial losses associated with fraudulent activity. To detect fraud in real time, this study attempts to create a machine learning model. The detection of fraudulent transactions is made more accurate and efficient with the use of deep learning and anomaly detection. The goal is to provide a valuable tool for financial institutions to protect their customers from financial losses due to fraudulent activity. Our proposed machine learning model achieved a high accuracy rate in distinguishing between legitimate and fraudulent transactions, outperforming traditional rule-based methods. The implementation of real-time fraud detection capabilities significantly reduced the financial losses incurred by the financial institution.

title: Optimized V2G Strategies for Enhancing Grid Stability in Renewable Integrated Energy Systems
authors: Purnima Sharma, Rishabh Dev Shukla, Sunny Vig
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This paper presents a simulation-based study on the integration of electric vehicles (EVs) into a renewable energy grid using Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technology. The study is aimed at enhancing grid stability and reducing the necessity for costly energy storage facilities based on stored energy in EV batteries. A 24-hour energy model is created using MATLAB with the incorporation of solar and wind power generation, dynamic load demand, and bidirectional EV energy interaction. ML(ML) approaches are also investigated to optimize V2G operations through data-driven models trained on historic and real-time data. The models are used to assist charge/discharge decisions according to dynamic grid conditions. The research illustrates how ML-capable V2G scheduling has the potential to minimize peak load, reduce operating expenditures, and pave the way for a cleaner and more resilient power system. The findings illustrate the capability of smart V2G control systems to pave the way for the installation of intelligent grid infrastructures and cleaner energy use practices.

title: From Data to Action: Machine Learning Applications in Predicting and Preventing Waterborne Diseases
authors: Sonam Singh, Saroj Singh, Alok Kumar Gupta, Ayushi Patel, Divya Mishra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Machine learning has grown in significance for data Analysis, categorization, and prediction due to the rapid increase in the large quantity of data on the water environment. Compared to conventional models used for water-related disorders, data-driven models based on machine learning are more effective at solving more complicated nonlinear situations. Within the field of water borne diseases research, numerous systems have been constructed, monitored, simulated, evaluated, and optimized using models and conclusions obtained from machine learning. Waterborne infections remain the leading cause of sickness and death among people worldwide. Waterborne infections are similar to food-borne infections in that they can be caused by Bacteria that are frequently connected to food-borne transmission. Waterborne diseases can occasionally trigger breakouts associated with meals or events where food was first thought to be a component. Although the fundamentals of sanitation and water treatment are widely understood, but many people lack access these because of inadequate funding, lack of inspiring leadership, and lack of good planning. The issues include understanding links to the environment, identifying endemic and pandemic waterborne diseases, chemical contaminants, and emerging pathogens resistant to conventional water filtration methods. New mathematical methods and satellite imagery are providing fresh insights into the study of water diseases. Since diagnosing waterborne diseases is costly, it is easier and more accurate to forecast them with machine learning algorithms. Machine learning may also offer solutions for managing the security of watershed ecosystems, reducing water pollution, and improving water quality. In this research, we investigate the situations in which various water habitats, including drinking water, groundwater, surface water, & seawater, have been subjected to the evaluation of waterborne diseases using machine learning algorithms.

title: Slot-Loaded Multiband Circular Antenna for Satellite and Wireless Applications
authors: Vaishnavi Loganathan, Selvaraj Damodaran, Janani Krishnan Srinivasan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: A compact, high-gain multiband circular slotted patch radiator is proposed for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{C}, \mathrm{X}$</tex>, and Ku frequency regimes. The design employs a slot-loading progression - circular, hexagonal, and nonagonal - to achieve resonances at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.3,7.2,8.5$</tex>, and 13.8 GHz. Operational metrics are assessed through S11, bandwidth, gain, and VSWR. A detailed parametric analysis is conducted on key structural parameters including patch cut dimensions, substrate thickness, and feed width to optimize performance. The antenna demonstrates return loss below −10 dB and gain magnitudes of 10.3 dBi, confirming its suitability for advanced wireless and satellite communication systems. Future work will explore further optimizations and the potential for integration with advanced communication systems.

title: Generative AI and Its Implications for the Authenticity and Legal Use of Video Evidence
authors: RWMR Dharani Rajasinghe
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The advancement of generative artificial intelligence via deepfake technology presents significant challenges for the trustworthiness of video evidence and the requirements for legal acceptance in investigative and judicial processes. This document examines the legal implications of AI video alteration techniques by investigating how AI undermines confidence in evidence and assessing the inadequate capabilities of current detection methods. The research concludes, based on thorough technological and legal analysis, that AI-modified media necessitates urgent technological development, enhancements in legal frameworks, and ethical progress to mitigate potential risks. Studies indicate that current forensic tools are ineffective in identifying sophisticated deepfake material, necessitating the creation of more robust verification techniques, as well as a reevaluation of standard evidence assessments. The paper concludes with a recommendation for the incorporation of interdisciplinary approaches to ensure the integrity of video evidence through technological advancements aligned with legal reforms and global regulatory practices in the age of AI.

title: Deepshield34: Frame Level Deepfake Detection Using CNN with ResNet34 Backbone
authors: Tejas V. Kalal, Kamini A. Shirsath, Shital S. Bhandare, Nikhil Singh Jodha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: As deepfakes become more common on digital platforms, reliable detection is vital. For this study, we propose a hybrid ResNet-34-based deepfake detection model with a preprocessing pipeline that involves frame extraction, face detection, and resizing followed by frame-level classification. The model was tested on FaceForensics++ and Celeb-DF datasets and achieved the highest accuracy of 87.5%, demonstrating solid performance between real and fake frame discrimination. Frame-level predictions were also aggregated for the final video-level classification to further strengthen reliability. The entire system was also deployed as a web application, where the users could upload video files and obtain interpretations from clear visual output

title: An Overview of Artificial Intelligence in Neuro-Oncology: Early Tumor Detection
authors: Anupam Gupta, Preety Swami
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The incorporation of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) methodologies has several novel opportunities such as earlier tumor detection, better surgical planning, and personalized care. The combination of new methods in the advanced imaging pipeline (pre-processing, segmentation, classification, and decision support) shows that convolutional, hybrid, and ensemble models have the ability to achieve more than 90 % accuracy on public MRI datasets. In some cases, AI models achieve close to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0 0 \%}$</tex> accuracy on small datasets through transfer learning and image fusion-based features. According to the review, AI application in neuro-oncological imaging delays diagnosis and thereby increases specialist availability and enables cost-effective triage, especially in underdeveloped areas. For effective AI benefits safe swallow, we emphasize certain prerequisites for clinical implementation, which include: privacy-preserving multi-center data sharing, an explainable AI to reveal solutions for the black-box opacity, strict external validation on heterogeneous populations and different scanners, and workforce/infrastructure preparedness (e.g., federated deployment, optimized protocol, and user training). The review finishes with recommendations and research gaps designed to address the challenges experienced with the implementation studies to provide fair, dependable, and wide-reaching care in neuro-oncology.

title: A Hybrid AI Approach for Precision Agriculture: Combining Time-Series Analysis with Large Language Models
authors: Kiran Patil, Harish Vijay V, Manasha Arunachalam, Ippatapu Venkata Srichandra, Keerthika T
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Selection of crop is important decision in agriculture which impacts profitability and sustainability. Farmers and land owners often face this challenges in choosing best crops in variable factors like climate conditions, fluctuating market prices, and other financial constraints. This project presents the design of hybrid approach(LLM and ML) which helps in Multi-Criteria Decision Support System (MCDSS) in crop selection by integrating multiple data sources based on trends. Traditional decision making approaches are experience-based, which makes them inefficient in handling complex, dynamic data patterns. To address these limitations and constraints, this system uses predictive models to estimate profit projections for viable crops under given conditions and also making use of Large Language Model (LLM) to synthesize complex outputs into natural language recommendations and supporting realtime, context-aware query resolution through a chatbot. This application aims to help in decision accuracy, improve the return on investment (ROI) in agriculture sector.

title: Temporal Sentiment Analysis and Explainability in Yelp Reviews: Mitigating Fake Review Impact
authors: Nithish Kumar S, Lalithamani N
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Generally online review platforms such as Yelp, Google, Facebook, Amazon, etc., provide a solid foundation for truly understanding the customer sentiments of any product. But these reviews can be influenced by the presence of fake reviews and the lack of transparent sentiment analysis. In this study, we have used both traditional and modern NLP (Natural Language Processing) techniques such as Logistic Regression, BiLSTM (Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory), and DistilRoBERTa for sentiment prediction using YelpZip and YelpNYC datasets. In addition to that, we have used DistilRoBERTa model for FRD (Fake Review Detection), CPD (Change Point Detection) technique to visualize sudden changes in the temporal trend of the sentiment of a specific restaurant and finally, we have used model explainability techniques such as LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations) and SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations). LIME was used for predicted sentiments and for fake review detection tasks, both LIME and SHAP were used. Our analysis revealed that fake reviews in the Yelp datasets have a subtle but noticeable impact on the temporal trends of customer sentiments of a restaurant. For sentiment prediction, Logistic Regression yielded 80.97% accuracy on the YelpZip dataset and 10.34% on YelpNYC, demonstrating its low generalizability. The BiLSTM model performed better, with accuracy of 83.08% on YelpZip and 83.39% on YelpNYC. Notably, the DistilRoBERTa model outperformed both, with accuracy of 86.91% and 89.21% on YelpZip and YelpNYC respectively, signifying their excellent cross-dataset generalization capability. In FRD, DistilRoBERTa also showed strong performance, with an accuracy of 86.79% on YelpZip and 89.84% on YelpNYC.

title: Monad-Stream: Decentralized Pay-Par-Use Streaming Platform
authors: Akhilesh Chaudhary, Ayush, Ali Rehman Ansari, Arham Jain, Divyansh Narula, Nikhil Ranjan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Digital streaming has changed the way individuals access media but it is centralized in a way that supports corporates against the creators and the consumers. Monetization of content creators is less and later, consumers are decreasing to either costly subscription believes or flooded with excessive advertising. In this paper, a proposal is presented to use Monad-Stream, a streaming solution that is going to be constructed on top of the Monad blockchain and IPFS. Monad-Stream is a microtransaction payment system based on a pay-per-use model that users simply pay the creators the content they read with no intermediaries. Fairness, and transparency and scalability is achieved through smart contracts that help in intelligently controlled distribution of payments with the help of smart contracts and decentralized storage through the help of IPFS. In a study of this architecture, it is possible that it can reward creators in real time proportionally and allow users to consume content at low costs and under their terms.

title: Neuro-Cognitive Entropy in the Age of Hyperconnectivity: AI, Immersive Tech, and the Digital Mind
authors: Shirin Abdallah Alimour, Khawlah M. Al-Tkhayneh, Samira Brahmia
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)
abstract: This paper introduces Neuro-Cognitive Entropy (NCE), a novel theoretical and computational framework for quantifying how artificial intelligence (AI) systems and immersive technologies reorganize and destabilize human cognition. NCE conceptualizes the mind as an entropy-prone adaptive network, continuously shaped by algorithmic feedback, synthetic perception, and sustained digital immersion. A mixed-method study involving 120 participants demonstrated that high-immersion exposure led to a $2.6 \times$ increase in NCE, a 32% reduction in alpha-band EEG coherence, and a 14% decline in vagal tone, indicating measurable disruption of attentional and emotional regulation. Two diagnostic constructs—the Digital Self Fragmentation Index (DSFI) and the Algorithmic Dependency Ratio (ADR)—jointly accounted for 72% of the variance in cognitive entropy ($\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}=\mathbf{0. 7 2}, \mathbf{p}\lt \mathbf{0. 0 0 1}$) The findings identify emerging digital psychopathologies, including AI Identity Diffusion Disorder (AIDD), Virtual Reality Dependence Disorder (VRDD), and Synthetic Reality Detachment Disorder (SRDD), as manifestations of entropy—driven cognitive disorganization. The study positions AI and immersive technologies as neurocognitive co-processors, underscoring the need for entropy-aware interface design and cognitive sustainability frameworks to preserve mental coherence and identity stability in an era of pervasive human-AI symbiosis.

title: I5CPS 2026 Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)

title: Smart CVD-Guard: A Performance-Optimized Hybrid Deep Learning Model with IoT Framework for Early Prediction of Cardiovascular Disease and Real-Time Patient Alerting
authors: Shajidha RS, Sathish Kumar V R, Suganya M, Suguna C, Saranya V, Praveena B
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) remains one of the leading causes of mortality worldwide, as this demands accurate early detection strategies for the support of timely medical intervention. Existing deep learning models for CVD prediction suffer from performance degradation due to data imbalance, noise, weak generalization across diverse patient profiles, limited spatial-temporal features extraction in traditional models, such as CNN or LSTM alone, and a lack of real-time monitoring and alerting mechanisms. To overcome these challenges, this paper presents Smart-CVD-Guard, a novel hybrid and IoT-integrated framework that combines robust preprocessing, deep sequential learning, and real-time health alerting. This system first applies K-means clustering to eliminate outliers and reduce noise, followed by synthetic minority oversampling techniques (SMOTE) to balance data. The dataset is proposed by a hybrid CNN-BiLSTM-BiGRU model, where CNN learns important patterns from the data, BiLSTM understands how those patterns change over time in both past and future directions, and BiGRU makes the process faster, enabling smartwatches to keep track of a person’s heart signals, heartbeat, and blood pressure in real time. If it notices anything unusual, it immediately sends an alert to the person and their doctors, and they take severe action before a serious heart problem occurs. This system makes heart care smarter, faster, and more personal by combining deep learning with wearable technology.

title: Institutionalizing Social Networks for Collaborative Learning in Accounting Education: Developing Countries Context
authors: Paschal Kpimekuu Boruzie, Emmanuel Awuni Kolog, Jarkko Suhonen
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: Despite the importance of social network platforms (SNPs) in collaborative learning, their integration into academic curricula faces various challenges, including accreditation requirements, faculty resistance, and infrastructural issues. Using institutional theory, this study examines how coercive, normative, and mimetic pressures affect SNP integration to enhance collaborative accounting learning. A structured questionnaire, grounded in a conceptual framework, was completed by 116 accounting students and faculty members. The data collected include demographic information and responses to 39 statements rated on a Likert scale related to the five structural constructs in the framework. The data were analyzed with partial least squares structural equation modeling (PLS-SEM) via SmartPLS 3, which provides a rigorous assessment of both measurement and structural models. The findings show that coercive and mimetic pressures significantly influence SNP integration, whereas normative pressures are weak due to deep-rooted pedagogical traditions. On this basis, we recommend policy reforms, faculty development, and mobile-centric infrastructure to facilitate SNP integration. These findings contribute to the ICMET discourse by offering practical strategies to transform accounting education into a more collaborative, inclusive, and future-oriented discipline.

title: Redesigning Networks for the Future: Challenges That Define the Next Decade
authors: Pushpendra Pal Singh, Rakesh Kumar Dixit, Deepa Gupta, Mukul Gupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Some of the key networking issues queried in the context of this research include issues of bandwidth limitation, latency, security vulnerabilities, and scalability, especially in areas like the healthcare sector, financial sector, and the telecommunication sector. The study follows a quantitative design and structures the research through Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) to inquire about the direct or indirect impact of such challenges on the performance of the system and user experience. In the study, the researcher uncovers the effects of connectivity problems on real-time-based applications such as telemedicine and online gaming, together with the increasing issues of cybersecurity. The study involves the data obtained by surveys of 150 participants and network performance indicators. Results indicate the importance of bandwidth in enhancing system performance and how high latency impacts user experience negatively. Also, the research points to a connection between security flaws and augmented cyber-attacks. The findings provide information on how to optimize networks, such as new technologies that facilitate network standards, including 5G, Wi-Fi 6, and edge computing. This study also underscores the ethical responsibility of ensuring data privacy and user consent in all future networking research and applications.

title: Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)

title: Nature Inspired Based Optimal LB Techniques in Cloud Computing Service
authors: A.Ajil, Anooja Ali, A Ajina, Nithin Ramakrishnan, Shilpa V, Sowmya Somanath
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Nowadays, a wide range of scientific and engineering applications need computational intelligence approaches to achieve data processing, decision-making, and goals of optimization. In the contemporary digital era, there are several applications that demand a sizable quantity of computer processing power. The widespread distributed computing architecture known as "cloud computing" has huge computational and storage capacity and is used to address a variety of problems in the academic, scientific, and information technology communities. In cloud computing, load balancing is one of the main research questions. By evenly dividing the workload across machines linked through the network, an intelligent scheduler helps to lessen the burden on any particular machine. The two main problems with cloud computing are efficient scheduling and load balancing. A well-known task scheduler has to be flexible enough to adjust to both the workload-based job scheduling strategy and the dynamic distributed environment. The bio-inspired algorithms, like Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), Butterfly Optimization, Crow Search Inspired Algorithm, and Harris Hawk Optimization, is used to balance workload and used to optimize resource scheduling from the perspective of cloud computing systems. Also, this study focuses on designing a powerful intelligent scheduler with focuses on creating a strong intelligent scheduler using well-known bio-inspired algorithms, to forecast the load based on some performance metrics, like Response time, Turnaround time, Implementation time, and cost of Communication. The suggested algorithm and other algorithms are used in Python, and the effectiveness is assessed using some performance metrics. The BOA algorithm performs better performance than other algorithms with 17.26%, 25.39% and 19.57% lower execution time, 32.39%, 12.39% and 29.78% lower communication cost with comparing existing technologies like Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), Crow Search Inspired Algorithm (CSIA), and Harris Hawk Optimization Algorithm (HHOA), respectively.

title: PrediscanNetV2: Early Risk Classification of Coronary Artery Disease via Gated Cross-Attention on Retinal Fundus Images
authors: Nithesh K G, Karthik K, Vishwa R, Kaarthikeyan A V, Karthick Krishna M, Sasi A, RamKumar T J
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Preventive medicine presently faces challenges in identifying the risk of Coronary Artery Disease (CAD) prior to the occurrence of symptoms. We employ PrediscanNetV2, a method that simply uses basic demographic data and non-invasive fundus photography to estimate Carotid Intima-Media Thickness (CIMT), which is an important sign of atherosclerosis. Most current approaches just integrate tabular and visual information. Our method is different because it uses a Gated Cross-Attention mechanism on top of a Siamese EfficientNet-B3 backbone. This architecture makes it possible to change the weights of visual features based on the patient’s age and gender, which is a good way to simulate a clinical setting. We examined the model with the China-Fundus-CIMT dataset (n=2,903) using a strict 5-fold cross-validation procedure. The results showed that the average accuracy was 84.52% and the Area Under the Curve (AUC) was 0.9104. PrediscanNetV2 demonstrates its efficacy as an automated, scalable instrument for assessing cardiovascular risk by statistically surpassing conventional performance benchmarks.

title: Research on Model Construction and Performance Optimization of City Brain and Community Micro Brain Collaborative Governance
authors: Wei Sun, Xuedong Xu, Jie Li
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: Addressing the challenge of diminishing “decisionexecution” efficiency in grassroots governance by urban brains, this study proposes a collaborative governance model and performance optimization method for “City Brain-Community micro-Brain”. By integrating temporal Petri nets and multi-agent system theory, a formalized model of hierarchical collaboration is established. An improved Contract Net Protocol (ICNP) that integrates dynamic trustworthiness and load balancing is designed to achieve precise task allocation and emergency collaboration. Simulation results show that this method reduces the average response time by approximately 65.2%, increases throughput by 41.8%, and achieves a task allocation accuracy rate of 94.7%. The research provides a modelable, optimizable, and verifiable collaborative governance solution to overcome the “peripheral dilemma”in smart city governance.

title: Hybrid Plasmonic Metamaterial Absorber for Methane Detection
authors: Sakthi Abirami Balakrishnan, Avinaash R, S K Nithesh, Devisowjanya P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: A hybrid gold metamaterial absorber is designed for highly sensitive methane detection, in terahertz (THz) range as a method for addressing some limitations of conventional absorption optical sensors, such as tunable diode lasers and non-dispersive sensors, which may lack selectivity, portability, and ability for multi-gas detection. The multilayer unit cell with a gold ground layer, a polyimide dielectric spacer and methane sensitive hexagonal gold nano rings utilized localized surface plasmon resonances enhanced by graphene’s tunable Fermi level to obtain near-perfect absorption of 99.9%. Simulations using CST Studio Suite show significant shifts in the resonance peaks (1.01 THz with absorption at 112.45 ppm) with absorption efficiencies of ¿ 98% because the refractive index changes in presence of methane. The layer unit cell will have increased selectivity and portable development for their usage, and multi-gas sensing capabilities making it a viable method for applications in environmental monitoring and safety monitoring in industrial processes. The ability to monitor multiple gases even at low concentrations for climate change mitigation is an important factor. There are still foreseeable challenges to the development of hybrid metamaterial absorbers associated with nanoscale patterning, which should be solvable, and removing energy losses in nanoscale systems. Overall, this proposed design will represent an important step in the development of scalable selective methods for gas sensing using THz absorption methods.

title: Enhancing Honey Quality Assurance Using Hyperspectral Imaging and ResNet-50 with Attention Mechanism
authors: V. Lakshmi Priya, Senthil Selvi A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Adulteration of honey is an emerging issue in the world that has been a challenge to the food authenticity and consumer confidence. Traditional chemical and spectroscopic techniques are either expensive or time-intensive and thus there is a need to have powerful automated methods. This paper offers a deep learning architecture combining ResNet-50 as a feature extractor with an attention network to improve the identification of adulterant honey. The data used is that of hyperspectral honey samples with pure honey and adulterated honey in different concentrations (0, 5, 10, 25, and 50). The process starts with the preprocessing stage that includes the normalization of the spectrum, noise reduction, and segmentation to maintain the uniformity between different sources of honey. ResNet-50 generates features, which capture discriminative spectral-spatial representations, and an attention module emphasizes the adulteration-sensitive areas and ignore redundant features. The smooth features are fed through a global average pooling layer and dense layers which are fully connected and finally a Softmax classifier to get the final predication is used. There are two classification considerations, binary classification to differentiate pure and adulterated honey and multi-class classification to estimate adulteration levels. It has been demonstrated in experimental results that the proposed model has a 98.7% binary classification accuracy and a 96.2% multi-class prediction accuracy, which outperforms the traditional machine learning and baseline deep learning methods. These results affirm that attention in combination with ResNet-50 is much better in detecting and quantifying adulteration, which can provide a scalable, non-destructive, and consistent answer to honey quality inspection in the real world.

title: Hemodynamics Spatio-Temporal Modeling with Graph Recurrent Neural Networks
authors: Niraimathi K R, Dhanush R, Amutha S, Surendiran B
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Hemodynamic and cardiovascular systems modelling hemodynamics and cardiovascular systems modelling is a challenging problem because blood flow is complex in geometry (spatial) and in pulsation (temporal). The conventional computational fluid dynamics (CFD) models are accurate but in most of the cases they are computationally heavy and cannot be executed in real-time in clinical practice. This paper suggests the use of Graph Recurrent Neural Networks (GRNNs) to estimate the dynamics of pressure and flow in the cardiovascular system. With the vascular network presented as a graph, i.e., the vessels are the edges and the bifurcations are the nodes, we use Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs) to learn the geometric constraints and local connectivity of the circulatory system. The spatial characteristics are combined with Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) including Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) units that can model the time-varying waveforms of the cardiac cycle. Our findings prove that the GRNN architecture is highly effective in comparison with the traditional sequence models because it considers the non-Euclidean topology of blood vessels. The model is effective in the propagation of waves, reflection and attenuation of waves in different arterial structures. The method is an alternative to patient-specific hemodynamic monitoring that offers high fidelity and computationally-efficient methods that hold potential to conduct real-time surgical planning and the timely diagnosis of cardiovascular diseases including stenosis or aneurysms.

title: Performance Evaluation of IM-NOMA Over Fading Channels Using Detection Algorithms
authors: Balireddi Pavan Sai, Anuradha Sundru, Uday Babu Peeka, Venkat Mudavath
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) has emerged as an important multiple access scheme for fifth-generation (5G) and sixth-generation (6G) networks by enabling multiple users to operate over the same time-frequency resources. Despite its spectral efficiency benefits, NOMA performance is constrained by inter-user interference and imperfect successive interference cancellation (SIC). To mitigate these limitations, index modulation-assisted NOMA (IM-NOMA) incorporates additional information through the selective activation of subcarriers or antennas, thereby enhancing reliability and detection performance without increasing bandwidth. This work provides a comparative bit error rate (BER) assessment of conventional NOMA and IM-NOMA using MATLAB-based simulations. The evaluation is conducted over four representative channel models: additive white Gaussian noise (AWGN), Rayleigh, Rician, and Nakagami-m fading using four detection algorithms: greedy, log-likelihood ratio (LLR), message passing algorithm (MPA), and maximum likelihood (ML). Simulation results show that IM-NOMA consistently outperforms NOMA across all channel conditions, achieving improved robustness and lower BER, particularly when combined with MPA and LLR detectors. These results indicate that IM-NOMA is a strong candidate for supporting reliable communication in future wireless systems requiring high robustness.

title: Analysis of the 5G NR Waveform with RF Deteriorations
authors: M. Divya, N. Mahendran, M. Dharani Devi
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: The rapid expansion of Internet of Things applications is reshaping the requirements of fifth generation and emerging wireless communication systems. One of the central challenges in this evolution is identifying a waveform that can deliver high spectral efficiency, strong reliability, and improved data rates. This work examines the behaviour of leading 5G New Radio waveform candidates in accordance with the 3GPP TS 38.104 specifications. The study places particular emphasis on major radio frequency impairments, including nonlinearities introduced by high power amplifiers, phase noise, and mismatches in the in phase/quadrature paths. Through system level simulations, performance metrics such as Error Vector Magnitude and Adjacent Channel Power Ratio are analysed to quantify their influence on waveform integrity. The findings provide useful guidance for RF engineers, aiding in waveform selection and optimization for diverse IoT oriented deployment scenarios.

title: Lifeline: A Traffic Jam, Road Safety, and Driver Assistant Smartphone Application With Driving License Application and Fitness Checking Features
authors: Mahfuzulhoq Chowdhury, Rahma Simin Ali, Mohammad Akbar Bin Shah, Rehenuma Ilman, Mohammad Kamal Uddin, Asmaul Hosna Sadika
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: In addition to saving lives, traffic jam monitoring, road safety, and vehicle driver assistance systems are crucial for extending working hours and reducing a nation’s economic losses. No automated assisted system with features for predicting traffic jams, suggesting roads free of traffic jams, checking driver and vehicle fitness, promoting road safety, paying traffic fines, registering vehicles and checking driver licenses, and reporting traffic accidents was developed in the previous works for vehicle owners and general users. This paper offers a Smartphone application for road safety and traffic jams with features like traffic jam detection, jam-free road recommendations, vehicle and driver fitness detection, traffic fines, vehicle tax payment, application for a driver’s license and vehicle registration, traffic incident reporting, driving school search, application for a route permit, and question-answering capabilities. According to the app adequacy and features competency results, over 55 percent of reviewers provided highly supportive feedbacks regarding the proposed mobile application.

title: Highly-efficient Blue TADF Organic LEDs with Optimized ETLs for Photodynamic Skin Cancer Detection
authors: Rajesh Kumar Tiwari, Sanjay Kumar Sharma, Rajesh Mishra, Mangey Ram Nagar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Thermally Activated Delayed Fluorescence (TADF) Organic Light Emitting Diodes (OLEDs) offer a promising pathway for energy-efficient optoelectronic devices with biomedical sensing potential. In this study, we present a simulation-based investigation of highly-efficient blue TADF OLEDs employing a range of Electron Transport Layer (ETL) materials, aiming to optimize device performance for non-invasive photodynamic skin cancer detection applications. Through device modeling, we analyze the impact of various ETLs on charge transport, exciton confinement, emission spectra, and power efficiency. With a high exciton recombination rate of 3.72 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">23</sup> cm⁻<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>•s⁻¹ across the emissive layer, the device using the optimized ETL outperforms the BPhen-based device by more than 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>. Furthermore, we simulate the interaction of emitted blue light from the optimized OLED structure with skin tissue models mimicking cancerous and non-cancerous regions. The changes in optical response are mainly affected by the subject tissue absorption criteria and scattering behavior. These variation in characteristics provide insight into the feasibility of OLED-based photodynamic cancer detection. This work mainly provides a simulation-driven foundation for futuristic development of wearable or portable diagnostic tools using blue TADF OLEDs and emphasis the role of ETL optimization in advancing OLED-based biosensors.

title: A Model for Studying the Device for Protecting Arresters from Impulse Currents
authors: Alexander S. Chistyakov, Alexander S. Yul, Vladimir Ya. Frolov
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: This study focuses on the development of a modeling framework for analyzing the design of surge arresters intended for protection against impulse-current phenomena. The engineering of new protective devices is inherently complex and typically requires substantial time and financial resources, which underscores the growing relevance of numerical simulation techniques. In this context, two complementary modeling approaches are introduced for the analysis of a hybrid protection system composed of a multichamber surge arrester and a dedicated protective electrode. The first model characterizes the pre-breakdown phase and provides the initial conditions required for subsequent electricarc simulations, as it accounts for the mechanisms governing arc initiation and early propagation. The second model describes the arc discharge under the assumption of local thermodynamic equilibrium and enables computation of the arc behavior once breakdown has occurred. When combined, these models offer a comprehensive description of the operating processes within the proposed protective device.

title: Unjammable and Resilient Communications for UAV Swarms: A Hybrid FSO/mmWave Approach
authors: Ali Eslami
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) swarms require resilient, secure, and high-bandwidth communication to operate effectively in contested or GPS-denied environments. Traditional radio frequency (RF) links are susceptible to jamming and interception, while Free-Space Optical (FSO) communication, though secure, is vulnerable to atmospheric conditions and alignment challenges. This paper presents a novel hybrid communication architecture that combines FSO and $\mathbf{6 0 ~ G H z}$ millimeter-wave (mmWave) RF technologies to create a robust and unjammable network for UAV swarms. Our approach introduces several innovations, including a dual-mode optical system (heliograph and active laser) for 24-hour operation, MEMS-based beam steering for improved LPI/LPD, a cross-layer protocol stack that performs link-aware routing across both optical and RF channels, and a cooperative localization framework for GPS-denied navigation. We detail the system’s architecture, the methodology for its design and prototyping, and present preliminary results from an FPGA-based FSO testbed that validates the feasibility of high-speed Pulse Position Modulation (PPM). The proposed system provides a pathway toward achieving secure, high-capacity, and continuous communication for next-generation autonomous systems.

title: Intelligent Wearable Biosensors for Integrated Stress, Substance-Use, and Chronic Health Monitoring
authors: Bharath M B, Mala B A, Sinchana K, Sanjana A, Umabharathi, Veeresh M Patri
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Wearable health monitoring has emerged as a vital tool in preventive care, enabling continuous assessment of physiological and biochemical markers outside clinical settings. Traditional single-modality approaches, such as ECG/PPG or biochemical sensing, provide valuable insights but suffer from limitations including reduced accuracy, high latency, or inability to capture complex interactions between body signals. These shortcomings restrict timely detection of health risks and affect the reliability of remote monitoring. To overcome these challenges, a multi-modal framework is introduced that integrates ECG/PPG signals with biochemical sensor data, thereby leveraging complementary information from different physiological domains. The system design ensures low-latency analysis while enhancing robustness against noise and signal fluctuations. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the multimodal approach achieves an accuracy of 92.5%, precision of 89.7%, and recall of 91.3% with an average latency of 1.8 seconds, significantly outperforming single-modality systems. These findings highlight the potential of multimodal monitoring for reliable, real-time health assessment in everyday environments.

title: Technology-Enhanced Active Learning in Engineering Education: Promoting Equity and Engagement Through Digital Innovation
authors: Mohammad Al Bataineh
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: Traditional lecture-based engineering education faces challenges engaging students in mathematically intensive courses such as signals and systems. This paper presents a case study on redesigning engineering education through technologyenhanced problem-solving methodologies in undergraduate Signals and Systems courses at the United Arab Emirates University. The study introduces a pedagogical framework integrating iPad annotations, interactive worksheets, recorded lectures, and computational tools with Matlab and GeoGebra. The intervention was implemented across three intervention semesters with comparative analysis against three baseline semesters, involving 147 students. Results demonstrate significant improvements in student performance, with average course grades increasing from 2.72 to 3.33 (representing a 22.4% improvement on a 4.0 scale). Statistical analysis revealed effect sizes ranging from 0.67 to 0.89, indicating large practical significance across all performance measures. The methodology particularly benefited students with diverse learning styles, with international students showing the largest performance gains (28.7% improvement). This research provides a replicable framework for transforming engineering courses and contributes evidence supporting active learning strategies in STEM education.

title: Unveiling Knowledge Insights from Large-Scale Knowledge Graphs for the Research Community
authors: Suruchi Sabherwal, Mou Dutta, Adwitiya Sinha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: With vast growth in the amount of data that keeps on generating over the internet, it becomes a challenge to find useful insights from such massive networked structures. The concept of knowledge graphs has become public due to this development. A knowledge graph presents a common outline for extracting knowledge, which is based on analysis, extraction of entities, and relationships. This paper provides a characterization of knowledge graphs by presenting two case studies, both of which comprise two datasets from academia. The first one is a single-author publication dataset, and the second is an organisational dataset. Our study includes the construction of knowledge graphs, along with an exploratory analysis of the dataset. In addition, the paper showcases the applications of academic knowledge graphs, which include recommendation applications, finding collaborators for research in the domain of interest, and revealing people who have worked on the same publication.

title: Comparative Investigation of Energy Management Systems in Electric Two-Wheelers: A Current Industrial Practice
authors: Indresh Yadav, Himanshi Sharma, Sulabh Sachan
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: An important component of India's growth towards sustainable urban and rural maneuverability are Electric two-wheelers or (E2Ws). Along with rapid industrial growth, Electric two wheelers show significant variety in voltage class, motor topology, and battery management solutions. This paper provides a verified comparative study of six major Indian manufacturers which are Ather, TVS, Bajaj, Revolt, Hero, and Ultraviolette based on verified specifications. Results show voltage ranges from 48 V to 400 V, with clear distinctions between hub-motor and mid-drive PMSM systems. The study distinguishes gaps in algorithmic clarity within Battery Management Systems (BMS), slightest adoption of widebandgap (WBG) semiconductors, and lack of standardized exchanging protocols. Upcoming research should give priority to thermal management, SiC-based inverter for the cost reduction, V2G integration for minimize the grid load, and AIassisted predictive diagnostics.

title: Adaptive Under-Voltage Load Shedding for Distribution Systems with High DG Penetration
authors: Leila Lalia Mizat, Mohamed Lahdeb, Youcef Oubbati, Hichem Nemouchi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Distribution power systems experience voltage instability, which results in extensive blackouts due to extreme system overload. Current load shedding systems, which use fixed voltage thresholds, fail to provide an adequate response to rapidly changing system conditions because they lack the necessary flexibility. The research develops an adaptive under-voltage load shedding (UVLS) system for distributed generation (DG) distribution networks to handle their voltage stability needs. The research develops an adaptive UVLS system for distribution networks that contain high levels of DG. The system aims to improve voltage stability while protecting the network from catastrophic failures during severe system disturbances. The Sugeno fuzzy logic controller determines the amount of load shedding by optimizing its parameters by the evolutionary algorithm. The Voltage Stability Index (VSI) helps identify essential buses for accurate load shedding operations. The researchers tested their method on the IEEE 33-bus distribution network, which included two DGs. The proposed adaptive UVLS system outperforms standard UVLS methods by providing better voltage management, quicker system restoration, and improved network stability, according to simulation results.

title: Design and Performance Analysis of Programmable Network Edge Solution for IoT Security
authors: Meera George, Bhagath Krishna P S, Divya Devaraj, Sibi C Joseph, Aneesh Kumar K B
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Recent years have witnessed the rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) devices and the massive data they generate. Many of these devices lack built-in security or run insecure firmware, transmitting data over the Internet in an unsecured manner, exposing devices and networks to attacks. Since communication between IoT environments and the cloud is handled by edge devices, securing the network at the edge is crucial. Deploying custom physical nodes as an edge requires specialized hardware, which is both expensive and difficult to maintain. Transitioning to Commercial Off-the-Shelf (COTS) hardware offers cost effectiveness, but with a tradeoff in performance and system efficiency. These limitations led to a shift towards software edge solutions, which enable zero-touch maintenance and deployment on COTS hardware. Traditional software-based solutions face challenges in achieving low latency due to the overhead introduced by the network stack of the underlying Operating System. This paper presents a programmable software-based network edge solution for IoT security, capable of achieving inline hardware-level performance without compromising efficiency. The solution, deployable in pairs, secures traffic through a tunnel between its instances and uses a control channel for the orchestration. The edge platform achieved low latency using the Berkeley Extensible Software Switch (BESS) framework, whose modular pipeline enables customizable packet processing and supports kernel-bypass technologies such as the Data Plane Development Kit (DPDK) and eXpress Data Path (XDP). Programmability helped to reduce the overhead of security functions by selectively applying them to traffic flows based on criticality. Performance evaluation shows that the throughput can be further improved by using specific network drivers and multi-core scaling. Our experiments confirm that the solution can achieve improved security with an overall 90-92% inline hardware-level performance.

title: Simulation of the Excitation System of Synchronous Generators of Marine Electric Power Systems
authors: Nikita Yu. Filimonov, Ilyas R. Laypanov, Polina V. Timoshkova, Dmitrii S. Gulnev, Alexey S. Sabanov
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: Electrical generators (EG) are electromechanical energy converters. Generators and synchronous compensators must be equipped with control and measuring instruments, control devices, and Automatic Voltage Regulators (AVR).

title: How Information Asymmetry Affects Higher Education Teachers' Adoption of Generative AI: An Extended TAM Approach
authors: Meng-Chen Lin, Cheng-Feng Wu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: This study investigates how information asymmetry affects higher education teachers' adoption of generative AI through an extended Technology Acceptance Model approach. Generative AI creates information asymmetry where teachers lack visibility into AI decision-making processes. We integrated TAM with the Diffusion of Innovation theory, and positioning transparency as a key mechanism for reducing information asymmetry. Results support all seven hypotheses and demonstrate that transparency influences all TAM and DOI constructs. The results also highlight educators' pedagogical orientation in technology adoption decisions. This research contributes by extending TAM with transparency as an antecedent variable, addressing AI-specific information asymmetry concerns, and providing insight for facilitating informed generative AI adoption in educational contexts.

title: Performance Evaluation and Comparative Analysis of NPC, H-Bridge, and Flying Capacitor of Five Level Converters
authors: Assala Guergouri, Farid Merahi, Abdessalam Badoud
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Multilevel converters have gained significant attention in power electronics due to their ability to improve power quality, efficiency, and voltage handling in high-power applications. They provide a number of benefits, including as enhanced voltage waveform quality, decreased electromagnetic interference (EMI), and decreased total harmonic distortion (THD). These features make them well-suited for applications such as renewable energy systems (e.g., solar and wind), electric vehicle drives, and industrial motor control. Three popular multilevel converter topologies—Neutral Point Clamped (NPC), H-Bridge, and Flying Capacitor (FC)—are compared in this work. Electrical performance measures including control complexity and total harmonic distortion (THD) are taken into account. Each topology's benefits and drawbacks are examined, offering insights into how well-suited they are for different applications. The results highlight the trade-offs between performance and complexity, ultimately identifying the most promising topology for specific use cases.

title: Evaluation and Comparison of MPPT Methods in Shaded PV Systems Using HHO and GA
authors: Y. Izgheche, T. Bahi, A. Lakhdara
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This study investigates the application of a genetic algorithm to identify the global maximum power point in photovoltaic systems operating under partial shading conditions. When solar irradiance is unevenly distributed across the photovoltaic array, the resulting power-voltage characteristic exhibits multiple local maxima, among which only one represents the true global maximum power point. To guarantee optimum system efficiency, it is essential to reach this global point. This work highlights the effectiveness of the genetic algorithm in detecting the genetic algorithm for tracking the global maximum power point, even in the presence of complex shadows. The proposed approach is evaluated in different non-uniform shading scenarios, and in each case the global maximum power pointis correctly identified, whatever its position on the irradiation profile.

title: P-CMBC-QPF: A Predictive Quality Based Mobility Aware Clustering Protocol for Large Scale Software Defined Sensor Networks
authors: P. Pavithra, P. B. Pankajavalli, M. Sridhar, A. Muniyappan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Software Defined Sensor Networks (SDSNs) allows centralized control in mobile wireless sensor networks (WSNs) to ensure stable clustering. In large scale and high mobility environments, distributed clustering algorithms like Mobility Based Clustering (MBC), Weighted Clustering and Routing Algorithm (WCRA), Improved Mobility and Energy Conscious Algorithm (IMP-MECA) often re-cluster and suffer from high overhead due to the lack of global topology awareness. The Centralized Mobility Based Clustering (CMBC) protocol improves stability using a centralized control based on connection time, however, the protocol performance degrades with extreme mobility. To overcome this issue, a Predictive Quality Based Mobility Aware Clustering Protocol (P-CMBC-QPF) is proposed in this paper. It integrates CMBC approach with Quality Predictive Fitness (QPF) measure for cluster head selection. QPF is calculated by combining predictive mobility trends, residual energy and estimated link duration. Pre selected backup cluster heads and adaptive Time Division Multiple Access (TDMA) based rescheduling are used to minimize the packet loss and network disruption. Simulation results show that at 1500 mobile nodes P-CMBC-QPF shows 2.5% improvement in success rate when compared to CMBC, 15.8% reduction in control overhead and 8.4% reduction in energy consumption. The protocol outperforms traditional approaches at node speeds of more than 20m/s, thus proving its suitability for large-scale, dynamic SDSNs. Predictive CH selection mechanisms offer robustness against mobility induced instability and guaranteed data delivery, prolonged network lifetime, and scalable operation for industrial and environmental monitoring applications.

title: Experimental Evaluation of Predictive Torque Control for B6 and B4 Inverter-Fed Induction Motors
authors: Aissa Kheldoun, Soufian Khettab, Oussama Merabet, Hamza Belmadani
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This paper presents an experimental analysis of the control of three-phase induction motors using advanced torque control strategies. The focus is placed on Model Predictive Torque Control (MPTC) and Virtual Model Predictive Torque Control (VMPTC) methods applied to IM driven by both six-switch inverter (B6) and four switch inverter (B4) topologies. The paper begins with a comprehensive modeling of the induction motor followed by the theoretical background and practical MPTC. Special emphasis is placed on the challenges of applying these techniques to B4 due to its reduced number of switching vectors. To overcome these challenges, a modified Model Torque Predictive Control strategy is developed. The test results of a real test rig of induction motor being used to drive a DG generator as a load demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed control schemes in reducing torque ripple, improving dynamic response, and enhancing overall system performance. The findings confirm the viability of four-switch inverter-based motor drives for applications requiring reduced hardware or fault tolerant torque control without compromising performance.

title: A Practical Fog-Based Deep Vision System for Wildlife Intrusion Monitoring
authors: Mishu Sachan, Sangeetha V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Farmers in forest regions often struggle with wild animals entering their fields. This problem continues to disrupt agricultural activity and cause financial loss. Traditional monitoring systems tend to be slow or unreliable, particularly in places where Internet connectivity is often fluctuating. To address these limitations, this study explores fog-based intrusion detection setup that combines local IoT sensors with a lightweight deep-learning model. Instead of sending every frame to a remote server, the system handles most of the computation on a device located near to the farmland. During preliminary trials, YOLOv5s was compared with the two-stage Faster R-CNN model to understand their suitability for real-time use on a fog node. YOLOv5 delivered better results, making it a more practical choice for deployment. Using these findings, an architecture is shaped in which the fog device performs the detection tasks, while the cloud stores event logs and provides access to past intrusion records. Although some components of the full system are still being implemented, the benchmarking study and design analysis done so far indicates that a fog-based setup can improve responsiveness despite weak network conditions.

title: Explainable Machine Learning for Multiclass Stress Prediction Using Questionnaire Data_supp1-3678412.pdf
authors: Jeevan Medikonda
publish: N/A

title: Topology-Aware Stable Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Framework for Air-Ground-WANET Inspection
authors: Qingwei Tang, Wei Sun, Zhi Liu, Nikos D. Hatziargyriou, Yang Xiao, Xiaohui Yuan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Large-scale substations face challenges such as complex inspections, high labor costs, and limited communication, requiring intelligent and efficient solutions. An air-ground system of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) and inspection robots (IRs) offers strong mobility and wide coverage. Wireless Ad Hoc Networks (WANETs), with their scalability, self-organization, and reliability, enable secure, stable, and decentralized communication, making them ideal for supporting substation inspections. The integrated Air-Ground-WANET system features a high-dimensional state space, strong coupling, and challenging multi-objective trade-offs. Dynamic topologies and constraints further complicate optimization. Traditional methods lack real-time adaptability, while conventional reinforcement learning (RL) often suffers from oscillation and instability, limiting practical use. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a multi-agent RL framework with Lyapunov stability guarantees, employing a structurally constrained monotonic neural network controller to ensure convergence and stability during training. A topology-aware graph attention Critic network is designed to effectively capture spatial dependencies and node coordination, improving policy evaluation accuracy. Additionally, a policy optimization mechanism incorporating topology-aware gradient regularization and neighborhood consistency constraints is introduced to enhance coordination and training stability in dynamic communication environments. Extensive simulations demonstrate its effectiveness and superior performance in complex scenarios.

title: Performance Evaluation of Meta Quest 3 and Meta Quest Pro in Static and Dynamic Conditions for Shoulder Rehabilitation in Virtual Reality
authors: Alessia Fabrizio, Erica Rocchi, Arianna Carnevale, Anna Ferrarotti, Rocco Papalia, Emiliano Schena, Umile Giuseppe Longo, Marco Carli
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Virtual Reality is gaining increasing relevance as a system to monitor joint kinematics during rehabilitation and as a tool to improve patient adherence to rehabilitation protocols. This study aims to evaluate the performance of Meta Quest 3 and Meta Quest Pro by comparing their tracking accuracy against the Qualisys™ stereophotogrammetric reference system in static and dynamic conditions. In static conditions, translational and rotational accuracy were assessed using two custom-made setups: one assessing displacements along three axes (from 0 to 505 mm) and another covering rotations from 0° to 180°. Dynamic performance was evaluated through three clinically relevant upper-limb movements (flexion, external rotation, and abduction) executed by NAO humanoid robot at three different durations (S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sub> = 1s, S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> = 2s, S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub> = 4s) to simulate varying patient conditions. Results showed a maximum translational mean absolute error of 4.7 ± 2.8 mm at 505 mm along the X-axis and a maximum rotational error of 2.8 ± 1.2° at 90° around the Y-axis for Meta Quest 3. In dynamic conditions, the highest mean absolute percentage error was 17.0 ± 2.9% for Meta Quest 3 during abduction at S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>. Overall, the Meta Quest 3 and Meta Quest Pro, featuring inside-out tracking technology, demonstrate promising accuracy, supporting their potential use as reliable tools for Virtual Reality-based motor rehabilitation.

title: Hardware-Agnostic Quantum Kernel Feature Mapping for Anomaly Detection in Critical Infrastructure: A Cross-Testbed Validation on NISQ Processors
authors: Shujaatali Badami
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Modern Industrial Control Systems (ICS) face sophisticated cyber-physical attacks that exploit nonlinear correlations between process variables, rendering traditional linear classifiers ineffective. This paper presents a hardware-agnostic Quantum Support Vector Machine (QSVM) framework employing an 8-qubit ZZFeatureMap kernel for anomaly detection in critical water treatment and thermal power infrastructure. Performance benchmarks were established via noise-free statevector simulation to determine the theoretical quantum kernel performance ceiling. Physical realizabilitywas separately validated via circuit execution on IBM’s 156-qubit ibm_fez processor, with completed hardware jobs enabling quantitative analysis of the simulation-to-hardware performance gap. Through rigorous cross-testbed validation on the SWaT (Secure Water Treatment) and HAI (Hardware-in-the-Loop Augmented ICS) datasets, our simulated approach achieves an AUC-ROC of 0.9912 ± 0.004 on SWaT and 0.8309 ± 0.050 on HAI, demonstrating consistent quantum kernel improvement of +10.8% AUC over classical RBF-kernel SVMs on the more challenging HAI testbed. Statistical robustness is established through 5-seed cross-validation with stratified sampling. Hardware execution on ibm_fez confirms physical realizability with circuit depth 76 and 28 CNOT gates, while revealing an expected fidelity degradation of approximately 17–20% compared to ideal simulation due to gate errors and decoherence. The quantum kernel’s ZiZj entanglement structure geometrically captures pairwise feature correlations, providing inductive bias well-suited for coupled sensor dynamics. Unlike deep learning approaches that require massive attack datasets unavailable in critical infrastructure domains, our kernel method generalizes effectively from limited training samples. All experimental artifacts are publicly available at https://github.com/Ali-Badami/Quantum-IDS. IBM Quantum job identifiers: d5l9htjh36vs73bgsi3g (SWaT), d5l9huk8d8hc73cfb0pg (HAI).

title: HyPSI: A Hybrid Protocol Semantic Inference Framework for Industrial Control Systems
authors: Yuxiang Lei, Xuejun Zong, Kan He, Lian Lian, Yifei Sun, Plamen Vasilev, Bowei Ning
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The convergence of Operational Technology (OT) and Information Technology (IT) exposes legacy Industrial Control Systems (ICS) devices to distinct protocol-level attacks due to the lack of semantic filtering mechanisms in modern edge gateways. However, effective security monitoring is hindered by the prevalence of proprietary, undocumented protocol implementations that create a semantic gap. Existing solutions struggle to generalize across vendor-specific implementations due to severe distribution shifts in protocol encoding and the prohibitive cost of manual annotation. To bridge this gap, this paper proposes HyPSI (Hybrid Protocol Semantic Inference), a framework that reformulates semantic inference not merely as a classification task, but as a domain adaptation problem. Operating on a knowledge-guided weakly supervised paradigm, HyPSI utilizes a Knowledge-Guided Semantic Mapper to transform constant-cost expert heuristics into high-confidence pseudo-labels, effectively addressing the initialization challenge with-out large-scale labeled data. Subsequently, to overcome the barrier of protocol heterogeneity, we introduce a Domain-Adaptive Contrastive Learning mechanism. This approach explicitly aligns the representation spaces of diverse protocols, extracting domain-invariant structural features that persist across proprietary variants. Experimental results on a high-fidelity Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) testbed demonstrate that HyPSI achieves an average inference accuracy of 88.4%. Crucially, the framework proves its practical utility in downstream security tasks: HyPSI-guided fuzzing significantly improves firmware code coverage compared to blind approaches, validating its effectiveness in automating vulnerability discovery for legacy industrial assets.
