title: Navigating Crisis: How Government Support Shapes Operational Resilience
authors: Ning Ning You, Yitian Lou, XiaoPing Zhao, Pufan You, Pedro Chumpitaz Flores, Kaixun Hua
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: The unexpected crises create significant disruptions in business operations, prompting many companies to re-evaluate their operational strategies and explore new methods to enhance their resilience. While government support may offer greater flexibility and adaptability, its effectiveness in improving operational resilience during such disruptions remains understudied. This study aims to address this knowledge gap by examining the operational resilience of government-supported organizations before and after disasters in 2018 and 2023. It investigates the impact of government support on operational resilience through R&D inputs during these periods and explores the moderating role of dynamic capabilities. The findings indicate that government support has fostered operational resilience amid crises. In 2018, the positive effect of government support on operational resilience was more pronounced in organizations with higher levels of dynamic capabilities. By 2023, however, dynamic capabilities no longer significantly influenced the relationship between government support and operational resilience. Instead, R&D inputs mediated, strengthening the relationship between government support and enhanced operational resilience. These insights contribute to our understanding of improving operational resilience and provide valuable guidance for organizations seeking to leverage government support to bolster their resilience during disruptive times.

title: A High-Precision Wearable System for Gait Analysis Integrating High-Spatial-Resolution Pressure Insoles with Embedded Walking-Pattern-Informed Algorithms
authors: Yidan Wu, Ruoyu Sun, Shuaishuai Li, Kang Li, Yong Nie, Huizhi Wang, Tingrui Pan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Contemporary wearable devices for gait analysis still encounter precision limitations that preclude their qualification as medical practicing tools. This study presents a fidelity-tracking wearable system (FTW) for gait analysis with novel walking-pattern-informed algorithms leveraging pressure insoles integrated with inertial measurement unit (IMU). Customized pressure insoles achieved high spatial resolution (5.2 points/cm²) based on an interleaved network multiplexing electrode (INME) sensor design. Controlled experiments involving nine healthy subjects demonstrated that this design significantly improves the estimation accuracy of both pressure-related and temporal gait parameters. Walking-pattern-informed algorithms were developed to further enhance the accuracy of parameter estimation. Specifically, pressure and area thresholds were respectively used for detecting toe-off and strike moments, optimizing the accuracy of temporal parameter calculations. A gradually zeroing the pressure in the metatarsal region (GZPM) strategy was employed to mitigate bending noise at peak thrust, enhancing the accuracy of pressure parameter calculations. Additionally, IMU-determined toe-off timing was advanced by 25% to better align with typical gait characteristics and optimize step width calculations. A classification algorithm was developed based on four fundamental gait conditions to calculate clinically defined foot progression angles. FTW enables the simultaneous and precise output of 13 gait-related parameters. Compared to previous studies, errors in step time and stance phase were reduced by 49.4% and 36.2%, respectively. The root mean squared (RMS) error of the measured plantar pressure center trajectory length was 0.48 ± 0.48 cm, and the accuracy of the spatial parameters was consistent with previous studies. This high-precision, multi-parameter wearable gait monitoring system holds significant promise for home-based long-term monitoring of chronic musculoskeletal and neurological disorders.

title: A High-Precision Positioning and Force Sensing Robotic System for Multi-Instrument Manipulation During Robot-Assisted Endovascular Intervention
authors: Wenke Duan, Wenjing Du, Zihao Li, Toluwanimi O. Akinyemi, Olatunji M. Omisore, Qiqi Pan, Xingyu Chen, Hongbo Wang, Lei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Robotic-assisted endovascular interventions have shown great potential to protect surgeons from prolonged X-ray exposure and improve the precision and outcomes of interventions. However, existing interventional robots remain limited in treating complex coronary lesions due to the lack of multi-instrument manipulation and high-precision force sensing and feedback. In this paper, a novel multi-instrument robotic system with integrated force sensing for complex endovascular interventions was developed and validated. The manipulation mechanism was designed to mimic surgeon’s fingers motions, achieving independently decoupled linear and rotational control to replicate natural manual operation. Additionally, a high-precision force sensing mechanism which is suitable for the multi-instrument delivery mechanism based on the lever amplification principle was proposed. This method can significantly reduce the length of the force transmission path and the impact of friction and inertial force, thereby enhancing the detection sensitivity of subtle vessel-tool interaction force during robot-assisted endovascular intervention. Furthermore, to address the issue of uncontrollable clamping force leading to instruments slippage and damage, a multi-roller adaptive clamping sensing mechanism based on series elastic actuation was designed and analyzed. Finally, several evaluation experiments were performed to validate that the precision and performance of the developed robotic system.

title: Three Fundamental Decisions in the Evaluation of Autonomous Naval Mine Hunting Operations
authors: Timothy R. Hammond
publish: IEEE Journal of Oceanic Engineering
abstract: This article examines three key decisions in evaluating autonomous mine hunting operations using the Mine Shape Bayes Net framework. These decisions concern the classification of mine-like contacts (MILCOs) in side-looking sonar imagery. The first asks which mine classes to consider. The second asks whether to calibrate assessments of MILCO classification performance with mine garden trials or target simulation techniques. The third asks about the optimal division of responsibility for classification between human operators and Artificial Intelligence (AI). Three options for this division are considered: human supervised, shared responsibility, and fusion (this last giving humans the smallest role). Simulation results show that fusion consistently yields the lowest mean sum squared error in estimating mine counts by shape, particularly under conditions of uncertain underwater navigation. When underwater navigation is perfect, human-centered approaches improve. It is not that AI is inherently superior, but rather that it is more robust to navigation uncertainty underwater, giving it an edge in current operational contexts.

title: Pre-Op Diagnostics of Power Switch in Three-Phase Full-Bridge Inverters
authors: Chung-Chia Wu, Le-Ren Chang-Chien, Hsuan Liang, Chun-Kai Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Wide-bandgap (WBG) semiconductors, such as silicon carbide (SiC) and gallium nitride (GaN), have demonstrated clear advantages in power electronics by enabling higher switching frequencies and improved efficiency under high system voltages. However, these benefits come with increased switching stress on power switches, leading to a higher risk of failure. To enhance system reliability, a timely health assessment of power switches before operation becomes critical. This paper presents a Pre-Operational (Pre-Op) Health Assessment Circuit designed for SiC-based power switches in a three-phase full-bridge inverter. Unlike conventional monitoring methods, the proposed approach performs diagnostics before the device is energized, allowing early detection of degradation. The design is theoretically based on the power switch aging characteristics, which serves as a foundation for pre-failure diagnostics. Because the diagnostic circuit is embedded inside the inverter system, the effects of printed circuit board (PCB) parasitics on monitoring accuracy are thoroughly investigated, addressing practical implementation challenges. Simulation and experimental results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed circuit in detecting the aging phenomena. The findings demonstrate that the Pre-Op Diagnostics provides an effective early-warning capability, enhancing the reliability of WBG-based inverter systems.

title: Brain Tumor Classification Using Deep Convolutional Neural Networks with Explainable AI Integration
authors: A S Angelin Jerusha, Julia Punitha Malar Dhas, Deva Priya Isravel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: This paper describes a multimodal deep machine learning method of brain tumor detection and classification using the combination of Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) and Computed Tomography (CT) data. This framework combines dual-input convolutional neural networks (CNNs) based on VGG16 and the tailored CNN frameworks in extracting different spatial and textural information of the MRI and CT modalities. To multimodally learn a paired dataset was created by matching the MRI and CT tumor-non-tumor categories. The fusion model obtained values of training and validation accuracy of 93.4 % and 94.7 percent respectively, which was a great enhancement as compared to unimodal networks. Also, to classify the tumor subtypes, a multiclass CNN classifier was utilized with the yield of 80.4 % accuracy on glioma, meningioma, pituitary and non tumor. Predictions of the model were interpreted using Grad-CAM visualization to highlight areas with tumors. Based on the experimental findings, the combination of MRI and CT modalities increases the reliability of the diagnosis and the ability of the model to portray the generalization. The two-branch hybrid method that has been proposed gives a powerful, interpretative and efficient computer-aided tool of diagnosing the computer brain tumors.

title: Multi-Asset Cryptocurrency Price Forecasting Using Attention-Enhanced LSTM
authors: N. Madhuri, P. M. M. Subrahmanya Sarma, R. Tamilkodi, S. Mohan Sai, K. J. S. S. Bharan, G. H. Veerabhadram
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The cryptocurrency market's high volatility and interconnected asset dynamics create significant challenges for accurate high-frequency forecasting, crucial for algorithmic trading and portfolio management. Traditional models often fail to address non-linear price movements and inter-asset relationships, limiting their utility for hourly predictions. This study proposes an Attention-Enhanced Long Short-Term Memory framework to predict hourly prices and volatility for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}$</tex> major cryptocurrencies. The model incorporates technical indicators alongside cross-asset volatility features. A two-stage feature selection process, combining mutual information and Random Forest importance, enhances predictive accuracy. Utilizing multi-task learning, the framework simultaneously predicts price and volatility, improving robustness. The model outperforms traditional and machine learning baselines across varied market conditions, providing a scalable solution for traders and portfolio managers in the dynamic cryptocurrency ecosystem as of July 2025.

title: Enhancing Image Forgery Detection with Cognitive Neural Frameworks
authors: Divya Beebireddy, Rakshitha Madarapu, Sanjana Kasireddy, Sai Sree Mandadi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: With the widespread availability of advanced image editing tools, detecting digital image forgeries has become a growing challenge in critical domains such as media, security, and forensics. This paper presents a cognitive deep learning framework that integrates Error Level Analysis (ELA) with transfer-learning-based convolutional architectures for identifying tampered images. Unlike conventional CNN or transformer-based methods, the proposed system emphasises interpretability, efficiency, and real-time usability through its cognitive ELA preprocessing and GUI integration. The system evaluates four models—custom CNN, VGG16, VGG19, and ResNet50—on the CASIA 2.0 dataset using key performance metrics. Experimental outcomes reveal that VGG16 and ResNet50 achieve superior accuracy in forgery detection. The cognitive preprocessing approach enhances detection sensitivity to compression-based inconsistencies, providing more transparent and explainable outputs compared to standard CNN-based detectors. Additionally, a PyQt5-based application is developed to facilitate real-time analysis for non-expert users. The approach demonstrates an effective and practical solution for enhancing the trustworthiness of digital imagery.

title: Robotics and Automation: Transforming Industries and Workforce Dynamics
authors: Satyam Prakash Srivastava, Rupa Khanna Malhotra, Himani Upreti, Riya Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Robotics and automation in different arenas are a major aspect of Industrial Revolution 4.0 and are motivating industries to bring transformational dynamism. The paper investigates the applications of robotics and automation in a number of sectors such as hospitality, logistics, production, medical healthcare, agriculture, automobile, and service maintenance. It reflects the role of Artificial Intelligence (AI), humanoids, and machine learning in leveraging advanced productivity with a sense of security and precision. With the integration of such technologies in different sectors, efficiency is enhanced but it also poses challenges and risks of job displacement, cybersecurity risk, and ethical concerns in medical healthcare. Through the Technology-Organisation-Environment framework, the paper explores and reviews systematically the literature with industry reports to comprehend the drivers and obstacles of adoption. Certain cases have been covered to connect with real-world examples. Findings recommend that the economic factors are prospering through robotics and bringing gaps between high-skilled and low-skilled manpower, rejuvenating skills that workers need to upscale. The study concludes ethical adoption of robotics with balanced approach that covers technological intervention with overall socio-economic development.

title: Judicial Evolution and Matrimonial Rights: An Analytical Examination of Kerala High Court's Stance on LGBTQI+ Community Rights (2015-2025)
authors: Jessy k Jayanth, Asha Sundaram, B. Lavaraju
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: This empirical work investigated the Kerala High Court's jurisprudence on LGBTQI+ matrimonial and related rights between 2015 and 2025. This is an important period when the legal recognition of LGBTQI+ rights will be evolving as evidenced through Kerala High Court judgments between 2015 and 2025. The focus on matrimonial rights, importantly, is both a symbolic and substantive indicator of equality in constitutional law. Using a mixed-methods approach combining legal doctrinal analysis and advanced machine-learning (ML) procedures (topic modelling, supervised classification, sentiment analysis and network analysis), this paper will map judicial sentiment, thematic trends and their transitions; and construct narrative-based trends. The methods and open reproducible pipeline I described here can be used to quantify the Courts view of rulings, the clusters unique to the predecessor cases, and to demonstrate known points in jurisprudence. The paper provides potential codes to compile, assessment metrics, and template tables and figures that may be used to illustrate the results.

title: Shift-Aware Stacking Ensemble with Conformal Prediction for Brain Stroke Detection
authors: Shravya Batchala, Tanupriya Kamble, Vivek Katkam, Basetty Mallikarjuna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Machine learning models for clinical risk prediction often lose accuracy when used in new environments because of domain shift, where the training (source) and testing (target) data differ. This paper introduces a shift-aware framework for brain stroke detection that adapts a model trained on a large cardiovascular disease dataset to a stroke-specific population. The proposed multistage approach handles both covariate and label shifts through careful data preprocessing, feature alignment, and domain shift measurement using a domain classifier. The study reduces covariate shift through importance weighting, and trains stacking ensemble of Gradient Boosting models such as LightGBM, XGBoost, CatBoost, and Random Forest, on the weighted data in order to increase predictive accuracy. The ensemble's results are then measured with Isotonic Regression and filtered using conformal prediction to provide reliable, distribution free uncertainty estimates. On the target stroke dataset, the method achieves a ROC AUC of 0.972 and a PR AUC of 0.976, introducing effective knowledge transfer over domains. By combining calibration and conformal prediction, this framework gives trustworthy confidence measures, making it suitable for clinical use where reliability is essential.

title: Impact of Channel Fading on BER Performance in Downlink NOMA Systems
authors: Nimisha Srivastava, Jaspreet Kaur, Neelam Srivastava
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA) plays a major role in meeting the increasing demand for high-speed, low-latency, and highly dependable communication since it may increase spectrum efficiency, user fairness, and system capacity with 5G and beyond. In contrast to Orthogonal Numerous Access (OMA) systems, NOMA enables several users to share the same time-frequency resources through code domain multiplexing or power by utilizing strategies like successive interference cancellation (SIC) and superposition coding. NOMA’s significance in influencing the direction of wireless communication is further cemented by its ongoing research into improved wireless systems, Internet of Things applications, and huge connectivity. This proposed work uses MATLAB simulations with superposition coding and SIC to assess NOMA’s BER performance under AWGN, Rayleigh, and Rician fading across varying SNRs for near and far users. The results reveal significant degradation, especially in Rayleigh fading, and a clear BER gap between users, highlighting the need for improved interference management and power control in realworld NOMA deployments.

title: A Fusion-Driven Deep Learning Framework for Robust and Scalable Face Recognition in Biometric Systems
authors: Princy Tyagi, Amit Kumar Bindal, Deepak Srivastava
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Facial recognition is essential in biometric authentication systems, requiring great precision and resilience across various operational situations. This study presents an efficient deep learning pipeline that integrates sophisticated preprocessing, data augmentation, and model evaluation to improve recognition performance. The Selfies ID Images Dataset, comprising casual and ID-style images from 29 individuals, was utilized to train and test various architectures: Artificial Neural Networks (ANN), single-layer and four-layer Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), as well as transfer learning models VGG16 and ResNet50. Preprocessing encompassed face detection using Multi-task Cascaded Convolutional Networks (MTCNN), followed by cropping, resizing, Gaussian blurring, grayscale conversion, normalization, and one-hot encoding. Data augmentation introduced variations in posture, scale, and illumination to enhance generalization. The results indicate that VGG16 attained the best validation accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(99.91 \%)$</tex>, closely followed by CNN architectures, whilst ANN exhibited marginally lower accuracy, and ResNet50 underperformed due to overparameterization relative to the dataset size. The research illustrates that efficient preprocessing and suitable architectural selection substantially enhance recognition accuracy and generalization. The suggested system provides a scalable and dependable solution for biometric applications, including identity verification, secure access control, and surveillance, where precision and resilience are essential.

title: DEEPOcuDx: A Multi-Model Deep Learning Framework for Interpretable Ocular Disease and Symptom Diagnosis
authors: Indhumathy L, Malathi G
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The early detection of underlying eye disease is important in order to avoid irreversible vision impairment and to hopefully improve the treatment timelines. We developed a solution to address this issue, and we call it the Deep Ocular Diagnosis System (DODS). This automated diagnosis system for retinal disease is based on a multi model deep learning framework to analyze fundus images. We trained DODS on the ODIR-5K dataset, which is advantageous for multi-label classification due to its extensive clinical presentation diversity. DODS consists of five state-of-the-art neural network architectures: ResNet50, DenseNet121, EfficientNetB0, Swin Transformer, and ViT-B/16, each trained with focal loss to address class imbalance. The overall diagnosis was made via consensus-based majority voting. To enhance interpretability, we introduced a symptom mapping layer mapped upon observable clinical features associated with disease e.g., optic disc pallor, tortuosity of vessels, etc. We provided transparency to the systemic decision-making process through Grad-CAM visualizations that provided evidence of the most important parts of the image. The performance of the system may not summarize the complexities of multi label retinal diagnosis but demonstrates the potential of deep learning with clinical interpretability for scalable and interpreted ophthalmic diagnosis.

title: Federated CNN-LSTM Framework for Sleep Disorder from Wearable Sensor Data
authors: Nandini Sharma, Rashmi Bhardwaj, Ankur Sisodia, Swati Vishnoi, Simran Chugwani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Sleep disorders affect the millions of the people worldwide and are effectively monitored using the wearable devices. The paper proposes federated deep learning based framework that associate Convolutional Neural Networks and Long Short-Term Memory networks (CNN-LSTM) in order to predict the sleep disorders from smartwatch sensor data that preserve user privacy. Experiments are conducted on the Sleep-EDF dataset partitioned across the simulated clients. The federated CNN-LSTM model is achieved an accuracy of 96% that outperforms both of the centralized deep learning and Random Forest baselines. These results are demonstrate that the federated learning are deliver high predictive performance without compromising the data confidentiality and offering a promising solution for the real-world wearable health monitoring.

title: Robotic Process Automation Tool to Enhance Efficiency in Public Services
authors: Shivam Kumar, Prakhar Kumar, Abhay Kumar, Vanshika, Vishavjeet Singh, Mannat Thakur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Problems such as work duplication, manual data entry, and the time-consuming nature of the processes may be encountered in the public service organizations contributing to productivity problems and even a decrease in the level of citizen satisfaction. Robotic Process Automation is a new technology that has turned operational efficiencies to reality in the recent years by offering a streamlined automation of rules-based and repetitive processes that enable organizations to use the resources more efficiently to deliver services. The paper addresses the design and implementation of an RPA-based framework to automate public service processes specifically concerning the use of UiPath as the automation technology. The work presented in this study explains why UiPath workflows are beneficial due to the automation of operations that include document verification, management of queries of citizens and data entry. The findings of the research indicate that there is a remarkable change in the time of completing the task, human workforce accuracy and productivity of handling tasks within the framework of the public service. The research paper addresses scalability of RPA solutions in public administration, the issues that have been associated with the system in terms of security and compliance, and opportunities and prospects of integrating it with artificial intelligence to create smart automation. As per the results of the study RPA uses in the public sector will optimize the public service and provide opportunities of operational efficiency and reduce human error or effort.

title: Scalable Parallel Transmission in LoRa Networks: A Regulatory-Aware Analysis
authors: Soumadeep De, Santosh Joshi, Himanshu Upadhyay, Renny Fernandez
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Low-Power Wide-Area Network technologies such as LoRa offer long-range connectivity at extremely low data rates, making the transmission of large payloads, such as images, prohibitively slow when using a single transceiver. Beyond compression and protocol-level optimizations, a natural approach to increasing throughput is to partition the payload and transmit it concurrently over multiple LoRa radios operating on distinct frequencies. This paper investigates the scalability of such hardware-level parallelism under FCC dwell-time regulations in the US902–928 MHz ISM band. We develop a regulatory-aware analytical model that captures the interaction between packet time-on-air, frequency hopping, and the FCC 20 s channel-occupancy constraint, and use it to characterize how transmission latency scale with the number of parallel radios. To validate the model, we implement a multi-radio LoRa system using synchronized transceivers. The experimental results closely match the analytical predictions, with less than 2% deviation, and further reveal how energy consumption scales with the degree of parallelism. These findings highlight how FCC dwell-time limits fundamentally shape the scalability of parallel LoRa transmission and provide practical design guidance for regulation-compliant, high-throughput LoRa systems.

title: Adaptive Online Learning for Real-Time Spam Filtering in the Music Industry
authors: Madhu Bala Myneni, Rohit Dandamudi, Thayyaba Khatoon Mohammed, Kirammai C
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Spam detection has evolved significantly with deep learning models such as LSTM networks, resulting in considerable advances in recognizing spam in online environments. However, present solutions are limited to offline training on static datasets and have not been adapted for continuous use in live systems. This study presents an adaptive online learning framework for real-time spam filtering that continuously updates its model with streaming data, addressing the challenge of evolving spam patterns. The proposed framework uses the Keras framework and is deployed on the MusicBrainz platform for the dynamic detection of existing and newly added spam types. Experimental assessments on realworld and synthetic datasets show a significant increase in detection accuracy and adaptability compared to typical batchtrained models. To classify editor accounts in real-time, we created and implemented a REST API that utilizes an LSTM neural network and is continuously enhanced by incorporating fresh feedback. The algorithm continuously improves its accuracy and resilience as it gets knowledge from real-time data and adjusts to new spam trends. This method's practical impact and scalability are demonstrated by its ability to remove 50,000100,000 spam accounts from the MusicBrainz platform and proactively handle future spam threats. In order to increase anti-spam resistance and broaden adaptability, future research will extend this online learning framework to other real-time applications within MetaBrainz, investigating strategies such as online transfer learning.

title: Artificial Neural Network-Controlled Wind Energy System Based on MATLAB/SIMULINK
authors: Indrajeet Kumar, Saket Bihari, Anand Singh, Ashish Kumar Rai, Sandeep Kumar Wasnik
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: By using sustainable energy sources, India, a growing country, could boost energy availability. In addition to improving many people's standard from life, this shift helps lessen the effects of global warming. Due to the high costs associated with traditional electrical systems, numerous locales in India continue to lack access to power. As a result, regions with strong economic and industrial development as well as well-established grid infrastructure tend to have stable electricity. Because renewable energy sources are decentralized, they may offer more affordable options for electricity distribution along with transmission. To address these demands, a variety of renewable energy sources are readily available like wind and solar power. Within Wind Energy Conversion Systems (WECS), controlling torque changes and successfully monitoring optimal power are major problems. Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) is lessened with the usage for inverters close to the wind turbine generator's control network connection. The study uses a dynamic model made in MATLAB Simulink to assess how well a wind turbine generator powered by an ANN controller performs.

title: Automated Ayurvedic Healthcare Chatbot Using NLP and Machine Learning
authors: Shristi Pradhan, Ishan Raizada, Vishal Kumar, Akash Kumar, Rajveer Singh, Dipti Das, Shaswati Patra
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: With the advanced in Natural Language processing machine can interpret human language and generate meaningful responses. Ayurveda is an ancient Indian medical system that provides holistic healthcare solutions which inaccessible to many. This paper presents an AI-powered chatbot using NLP and Machine Learning to provide Ayurvedic healthcare guidance. The chatbot analyses user queries, identifies the intents and suggests the user remedies for common ailments. In addition to that the system it provides an Ayurvedic texts and practitioner to ensure reliable recommendations. By integrating machine learning, the chatbot continuously improves based on user interactions, refines it's accuracy and effectiveness. This research shows us how AI can be used to make traditional healthcare wisdom more accessible, efficient, and user friendly in the digital era.

title: Hardware Implementation of DC-DC Converters for SPV-Battery System Using DSP-28379D Texas Microcontroller
authors: Kanishq Sahu, Rohit Kumar Lodhi, Siddharth Singh Parihar, Deepti Jain, Jitendra K Tandekar, Monika Jain
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: This paper presents the modelling, design, and implementation of Buck, Boost, and Buck-Boost DC-DC converters controlled using the Texas Instruments DSP28379D microcontroller. These converters are essential in modern power electronics applications such as electric vehicles, renewable energy systems, and embedded power supplies, where efficient voltage regulation is critical. The DSP28379D, with its high-speed processing and PWM generation capabilities, enables precise control of switching operations, leading to improved converter efficiency and dynamic response. The paper discusses the closed-loop control strategy implemented using feedback from output voltage, the generation of control pulses using PWM modules, and simulation results obtained through MATLAB/Simulink. Experimental validation on hardware setup confirmed the performance of DSP-based control in various load and input conditions. This research demonstrates the effectiveness of digital control in power electronics and lays the foundation for advanced real-time control techniques.

title: Performance Analysis of High Voltage Distribution System: A Case Study of MMMUT Gorakhpur
authors: Akash Deep Chaudhary, Prabhakar Tiwari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This paper deals with benefits obtained by restructuring the existing distribution system, with reference to the Madan Mohan Malaviya University of Technology (MMMUT) Campus, Gorakhpur. A case study of its distribution network has been analysed. Replacement of Low Voltage Distribution (LVDS) using overhead three-phase, four wire, 440 V, bare stranded conductors with High Voltage Distribution (HVDS) using underground three-phase, three-core, 11 KV armoured cables and installing separate distribution transformers for respective load, nearer to consumer end eliminates several problems. These include voltage fluctuations, high distribution losses, voltage drop, damage to distribution transformers (DTR), reduced system reliability, voltage instability, phenomenon of power cut, shortened lifespan of electrical equipment and components, occurrence of faults, considerable energy dissipation in overhead conductors, and risks to human safety. Around 22% of electricity generated in India got dissipated during distribution, according to data accomplished from Indian Ministry of Power in May 2018. The major cause for energy dissipation is due to high line losses, commonly referred as distribution losses. These losses are a consequence of dissipation of energy in overhead conductors and power transformers used for transformation. Existing system uses longer length of three phase, 440 V overhead low voltage lines which are responsible for maintaining low voltage profile at trail end. Therefore, to promote energy conservation, prevent overloading of distribution transformers, maintain better voltage profile, minimize distribution losses and overcome above stated problems, restructuring of Low Voltage Distribution System (LVDS) can give contribution towards economic development of the country. Field investigation method has been used for performance analysis.

title: A Grid-Friendly Universal Short-Circuit Ratio Online Estimation Strategy for Grid Connected Inverters
authors: Rui Zhao, Xiaofeng Yang, Ange Wei, Menghan Lv
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: The accurate and fast short-circuit ratio (SCR) online estimation (SOE) technology is critical to the stable operation of the grid-connected inverters (GCI), particularly in a wide range of grid strength. However, the existing SOE methods cannot balance the indicators of grid-friendliness, accuracy, and rapidity. Furthermore, most strategies are designed exclusively for either grid following (GFL) or grid forming (GFM) control, which cannot be directly applied to other control structures. To solve the above-mentioned questions, a grid-friendly universal short-circuit ratio online estimation (FUSOE) strategy is proposed in this paper. FUSOE strategy has grid friendliness, whose estimation function is derived from the steady-state variables of the system. Therefore, FUSOE has no additional disturbance to the grid, making the continuous SCR online estimation possible. Moreover, the inner current loop reference serves as a middle bridge, breaking through the application limitations of the traditional method between GFL and GFM modes while ensuring estimation rapidity. Therefore, the proposed FUSOE strategy can adapt to multi control structures without second design. The proposed method is verified in simulations and experiments under grid strength fluctuations and different controls. The simulation and experimental results verify the correctness of the theoretical analysis and the advantage of FUSOE strategy in accuracy, rapidity, and universality.

title: A Novel Fourier-Based Thresholding Method for Efficient Drone Detection in Images
authors: Oleh Polishchuk, Dmytro Haponov, Kateryna Babii, Oleksandr Pliekhov
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Drone detection in images is a critical task for security and surveillance applications. Traditional thresholding methods are commonly used for contrast-based object detection but often suffer from high false positives, low precision, or computational inefficiency. In this paper, we propose a novel Fourier-based adaptive thresholding algorithm that utilizes Fourier transform to approximate the image histogram, enabling robust identification of multi-modal background distributions for precise threshold selection. The method introduces several key innovations: (1) low-pass filtering of the histogram via FFT to suppress noise while preserving dominant intensity modes, (2) multi-peak detection for handling complex outdoor backgrounds with sky, clouds, and terrain, (3) explicit overexposure attenuation to mitigate bright sky effects, and (4) computational efficiency through histogram-based processing independent of image resolution. We evaluate the proposed method against 12 existing thresholding methods on 116 benchmark images, demonstrating superior performance with precision of 81.4% (11.66% higher than the nearest competitor), recall of 94.0% (11.37% improvement), and coverage of 61.4% (48.31% improvement). The method achieves processing times of 0.817 ms at 4K resolution (3840Ö2160), delivering 27.4Ö speedup over the nearest quality competitor while maintaining exceptional detection accuracy, making it ideal for real-time drone surveillance in resource-constrained embedded systems.

title: Prompt Recovery from Source Code: A Comparative Study of Large Language Models
authors: Avishek Mukherjee
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a rigorous empirical study on prompt recovery, the task of inferring the original natural language instruction from a given source code snippet. We evaluate four state-of-the-art Large Language Models (LLMs): OpenAI’s GPT model, Google’s Gemini, Meta’s Llama3, and DeepSeek’s DeepSeek-R1 on their ability to recover prompts from Python code. The analysis also investigates the impact of code degradation (via variable renaming and comment removal), which serves as a proxy for poorly documented legacy systems. Additionally a preliminary study of generative sampling temperature effects was conducted using OpenAI’s GPT model. We evaluate performance using a comprehensive suite of metrics, including BLEU, ROUGE, BERTScore, and Levenshtein Distance. Our findings reveal that while code cleaning degrades lexical similarity scores (e.g., average BLEU scores for OpenAI are 20% lower), it consistently maintains semantic similarity (e.g., average BERT-F1 remains above 0.90). This suggests that in the absence of lexical cues, models are forced to rely on deeper algorithmic reasoning and structural understanding of source code. Comparative analysis shows cloud-hosted models exhibit superior semantic fidelity, while DeepSeek-R1 demonstrates competitive character-level accuracy. These results provide critical insights into the code comprehension capabilities of modern LLMs and have significant implications for their application in reverse engineering, legacy system documentation, and the development of more robust evaluation benchmarks for code intelligence.

title: Bandwidth-Tunable Mode-Locked Fiber Laser Enabled by Hetero-Core Offset-Spliced GIMF Saturable Absorber
authors: Xuanyu Chen, Ya Liu, Shiping Zhou, Xinyu Wen, Mengmeng Wang, Guoqing Hu, Ying Zhang
publish: IEEE Photonics Journal
abstract: We demonstrate a passively mode-locked erbium-doped fiber laser utilizing a hetero-core offset-spliced gradient-index multimode fiber (GIMF) structure. The all-fiber saturable absorber is fabricated by offset-splicing two GIMF segments with mismatched core diameters of 62.5 μm and 50 μm. By leveraging the enhanced excitation of higher-order modes, the strict fiber length restrictions typically required for the self-imaging effect are significantly relaxed, simplifying the fabrication process. Taking into account both the insertion loss and fusion firmness, the optimal offset of 7 μm is adopted for splicing. The saturable absorber exhibits a modulation depth of 10.0% and a saturation intensity of ∼0.25 MW/cm². The laser achieved stable fundamental mode-locking at a center wavelength of 1560 nm, delivering pulses with a width of 0.50 ps at a repetition rate of 23.95 MHz. Furthermore, owing to the mode asymmetry induced by the offset structure, the device also functions as a polarization-dependent multimode interference filter. By adjusting the intracavity polarization at a fixed pump power, a continuously tunable bandwidth ranging from 1.4 nm to 6.0 nm is realized, while maintaining a stable center wavelength. This fabrication method shows reduced complexity as well as excellent reproducibility. The proposed all-fiber mode-locked laser features a high damage threshold, low manufacturing cost, and excellent long-term stability, demonstrating significant application potential in ultrafast photonics and industrial processing.

title: Multiplexing Orbital Angular Momentum Modes Generator by Long-Period Fiber Grating Arrays
authors: Yuehui Ma, Kaiming Zhou, Lukman Kamarudin, Chen Jiang, Lilong Dai, Siyu Chen, Zhiqiang Wang, Chengbo Mou, Yunqi Liu
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Orbital angular momentum (OAM) modes, characterized by helical phase fronts, enable powerful spatial manipulation of light for applications in optical communications, quantum information, and imaging. However, compact and flexible devices for generation of multiplexed OAM modes remain challenging. Here we propose and demonstrate a compact, reconfigurable long-period fiber grating (LPFG) array for multi-channel, multi-order OAM modes in a few-mode fiber. Femtosecond inscription enables micrometer-scale, highly localized refractive-index modulations (RIMs) that can be positioned azimuthally within the fiber core. Coupled-mode analysis reveals that the coupling coefficients for the mode conversion from <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$LP_{01}$</tex-math></inline-formula> to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$LP_{21},LP_{31}$</tex-math></inline-formula> modes are strongly controlled by the RIM transverse position and modulation depth of the RIMs. By employing distinct grating periods, phase mismatch effectively suppresses inter-grating coupling and minimizes crosstalk, enabling independent channels. Experimentally, rotating-fiber inscription produces LPFG arrays comprising up to five parallel gratings with resonance channels spanning 1300–1700nm, achieving coupling efficiencies exceeding 95% and a minimum insertion loss of 0.60dB. With phase control, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$OAM_{\pm 2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$OAM_{\pm 3}$</tex-math></inline-formula> modes are synthesized, including a five-channel third-order OAM configuration. The LPFG-array platform provides a scalable, low-loss solution for multiplexed OAM sources in mode-division multiplexing and integrated photonics.

title: Decentralized Optimization for MEC Enabled Heterogeneous Networks: A Deep Reinforcement Learning and Convex Optimization Approach
authors: Xun Tong, Shubin Zhang, Yongpeng Shi, Kaikai Chi
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Wireless-powered mobile edge computing (WPMEC) has gained significant attention for enabling energy-sustainable and low-latency Internet of Things (IoT) applications in domains such as smart cities and industrial automation. Heterogeneous computing resources, which encompass devices with diverse computational capabilities, are essential to support adaptive task offloading and resource-efficient service provisioning. However, integrating wireless power transfer (WPT) with mobile edge computing (MEC) in dynamic environments remains challenging, particularly in the efficient coordination of heterogeneous resources. This paper investigates a distributed optimization problem in a wireless-powered heterogeneous MEC network, where base station association, binary offloading decisions, and distributed resource allocation are closely intertwined. We formulate the problem as a mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) problem and propose a novel decentralized solution framework that decomposes it into two subproblems: deep reinforcement learning is employed to optimize base station selection and task offloading strategies, while convex optimization techniques, including the Lagrangian method, are used to allocate bandwidth and computational resources at the base stations. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly enhances the energy efficiency and computational performance of wireless edge computing compared to existing benchmarks, confirming its practical value for resource-constrained MEC systems.

title: AeroSCR: Large-Scale Scene Coordinate Regression for Aerial Visual Localization
authors: Wenhao Lin, Cong Guo, Pengtao Zhang, Yu Wu, Kan Ren, Qian Chen
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Scene Coordinate Regression (SCR) is a learning-based visual localization method that estimates camera pose by directly regressing 3D scene coordinates corresponding to given 2D pixel locations. While effective in small-scale settings, existing methods struggle with illumination and viewpoint variations in large-scale environments. To address this, we present AeroSCR, a novel SCR framework for UAV localization. First, we design a structure-aware dual-stream encoder with an Adaptive Structure-Aware Fusion (ASAF) module, jointly learning semantic and structural representations to enhance robustness to appearance variations. Second, we introduce a region partitioning strategy that decomposes the global regression problem into multiple local subtasks, significantly improving localization performance in large and ambiguous environments. Finally, we construct UAV-SceneLoc2025, a large-scale UAV dataset covering diverse altitudes, viewpoints, lighting, and weather conditions, providing a comprehensive benchmark for SCR evaluation under challenging real-world scenarios. Extensive experiments demonstrate that AeroSCR achieves state-of-the-art performance over existing SCR methods.

title: Multi-Band THz Sources Based on Effective-Media Suspended-Strip All-Silicon Technology for D-Band and up to 0.5 THz
authors: Amr Samir, Ahmed M. Hegazy, Nick Dechev, Mohamed Basha
publish: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology
abstract: The cost and performance of electronic device integration in the terahertz (THz) regime remain key challenges toward the commercialization of communication and sensing systems operating beyond 100 GHz, especially during the research and prototyping phases. Such systems are critical for future high-throughput communication networks (e.g., 6G sub-THz systems), as well as for applications requiring high radiation power, including material spectroscopy, space exploration, and nondestructive medical imaging. In this work, we combine the concepts of effective-medium (EM) waveguides and suspended waveguides to introduce an all-silicon multiband hybrid integration platform that supports low-loss dielectric passive components with high-quality metallic transitions, enabling off-chip active integration using low-parasitic self-aligning embedded chip packaging and ultra-short wire bonding. The proposed approach addresses current limitations in THz integration and packaging technologies by enabling broadband operation across mmWave and THz frequencies on a single silicon platform. Using deep reactive-ion-etched effective media and double-sided photolithography, extremely low in-plane substrate modal leakage is achieved, allowing multiband THz components to be integrated on the same wafer. The technology is experimentally validated through the implementation of low-power THz sources in the D-band and Y-band using hybrid integration of commercially available planar GaAs Schottky diodes in cascaded frequency multiplier configurations. The demonstrated systems achieve conversion efficiencies of 5% in the D-band and 0.063% at 0.5 THz, validating the feasibility of the proposed hybrid integration platform for multiband THz source development and proving it to be a strong candidate for low-cost, rapid prototyping of multiband THz systems.

title: Bridging the Semantic Gap: Synergistic Feature Fusion and Multi-Scale Adaptation for Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Shaoqiang Wang, Guiling Shi, Chunxin Cheng, Xiaofeng Xu, Weixian Li, Xiangyu Gao, Yuchen Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Medical image segmentation is pivotal for clinical diagnosis but faces a systemic bottleneck in handling complex scenarios due to the inherent trade-offs in standard architectures. Existing methods often struggle with the “perception-representation dilemma” at the input stage and a “fusion-adaptation misalignment” during feature aggregation. To dismantle these interconnected challenges, we propose a novel Synergistic Fusion and Refinement Network (SFR-Net). Specifically, we design a Local-Regional Feature Perception (LRFP) module to couple fine-grained details with global context from the outset. To bridge the semantic gap, we introduce a Channel Refinement and Enhancement Module (CREM) as an intelligent gatekeeper in skip connections, alongside a Feature Mixing Module (FMM) to dynamically adapt to multi-scale targets at the bottleneck. Extensive experiments on four diverse datasets (CVC-ClinicDB, ISIC 2017, TN3K, and MICCAI Tooth) demonstrate that SFR-Net effectively overcomes these systemic limitations, achieving state-of-the-art performance in terms of accuracy and robustness.

title: Detection with Nuisance Parameters and Imperfect CSI in RIS Aided ISAC Systems
authors: Haoyang Che, Yang Liu, Qingqing Wu, Jie Xu, Qingjiang Shi, Wen Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: This paper investigates detection orientated integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system aided by hybrid reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS). Target detection is conducted through communication signal echoes under the practical condition of unknown attenuation coefficient and sensing noise covariance, which makes our study more challenging than existing pertinent works. Firstly, we develop a closedform based generalized likelihood ratio test (GLRT) detector, which first effectively extrapolates unknown parameters through maximum likelihood estimation and then conducts hypothesis testing. Besides, we derive the asymptotic detection probability of the proposed GLRT detector in an analytic form, which is highly accurate for moderate sample size. Based on the above analysis, we propose robust beamforming design to maximize the worst-case detection probability while ensuring ergodic communication rate in awareness of channel state information (CSI) uncertainties. We provide a semidefinite programming (SDP) formulation to solve the robust beamforming problem. Additionally, by converting the variational and ergodic forms in robust formulation into explicit approximations, we further develop an efficient second order cone programming (SOCP) based solution, which is highly reliable when the CSI uncertainty becomes low. Numerical results validate the efficacy of the proposed GLRT detector, the correctness of the detection performance analysis, and the benefit of robust beamforming against CSI uncertainty.

title: Pursuit-Evasion Game for UAV Anti-Jamming Communications: An Opponent Modeling Based Reinforcement Learning Approach
authors: Ziyan Yin, Zhe Wang, Jun Li, Long Shi, Yiyang Ni, Shi Jin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are widely deployed as aerial base stations to provide flexible communication coverage for ground users (GUs), yet the air-ground communications remain highly vulnerable to the jamming attacks. Unlike conventional fixed-policy jammers, the intelligent jammers dynamically adapt their jamming strategies based on the observed UAV communication policies, creating significant anti-jamming challenges particularly under asymmetric information. In this paper, we formulate the strategic interactions between a UAV-mounted server and a jammer as a partially observable pursuit-evasion game, where the UAV aims to maximize the GUs' uplink rates through dynamic evasion while the jammer strategically pursues to maximize the jamming effect. The information asymmetry is explicitly modeled by considering both the jammer's hidden location from the UAV and the jammer's inability to observe the UAV's remaining energy state. To optimize the UAV's anti-jamming policy under these challenges, we propose a novel opponent-modeling based reinforcement learning algorithm, named neural fictitious self-play with dueling double deep recurrent Q network (NFSP-D3RN). This algorithm optimizes the UAV's anti-jamming policy through reinforcement learning, while maintaining robustness against non-stationarity induced by the jammer's adaptive behavior through implicit opponent modeling. Extensive simulations demonstrate that our proposed algorithm achieves superior anti-jamming performance compared with the benchmarks under unknown jammer locations, with results approaching the upper bound of perfect location knowledge.

title: Emotion Analysis in Human-robot Communication with ELMO Robot using Deep Learning
authors: Kristina Machova, Marian Mach, Peter Demeter
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The paper focuses on the field of emotion analysis in Human–Robot Interaction (HRI). In communication with other people, people use the perception of their emotions to make communication better, smoother, and leading to better understanding. Therefore, the recognition of emotions by a robot is important in HRI. In the case where a robot communicates with a human, understanding his/her emotional state is crucial for successful communication. There are many various definitions of individual emotions, and each one of them can be conveyed in different ways. Understanding the emotions expressed in a human’s speech in terms of HRI is nowadays a widely researched problem. This task can be tackled with deep learning approaches via automatic learning from textual data collected from the application environment, where the future HRI will take place. We have utilized several deep learning architectures to learn an emotion recognition model for the robot ELMO. The best model was chosen to enable the ELMO to recognize emotions and choose an appropriate response in particular situation. The robot’s response is in the form of a visualization (changing the robot’s facial expression), a textual response on the display and a voice response, all adapted to a specific situation within communication with humans. The paper presents test results from emotion detection models and analyzes HRI experiments.

title: CFAR Detection of high-accelerating targets: a study of the probability of false alarm
authors: Alessio Balleri, Fabiola Colone, Pierfrancesco Lombardo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Radar Systems
abstract: Fast accelerating targets feature a non-negligible time-varying Doppler shift which induces a drop in Signal to Noise Ratio (SNR) at the output of a classical range-Doppler radar detector. The optimal detector against fast accelerating targets retrieves the maximum available SNR by a three-dimensional search to match the target echo to the target range, Doppler and acceleration. This operation, however, modifies the statistical properties (probability density function and the correlation) of background white Gaussian noise at the output of the filter and, ultimately, impacts the receiver probability of false alarm and how this can be robustly controlled. In this paper, we study the statistical properties of white Gaussian background noise at the output of the optimal processor and propose a modified one-parametric Largest Exponential Value (LEV) distribution to fit the receiver noise before the detection threshold. Two Constant False Alarm Rate (CFAR) schemes are proposed and developed based on the modified LEV distribution and their performance is characterised analytically and compared with simulated data and experimental data collected at C-band. Results show the distribution parameter of the modified LEV converges to a fixed value as the number of filters in the acceleration domain increases. This yields detection performance that is independent of the relevant detection parameters. CFAR results demonstrate the probability of false alarm can be robustly controlled with the proposed solution on simulated and real experimental data.

title: Federated Few-Shot Prototypical Learning for Personalized WiFi-based Human Activity Recognition
authors: Harun Ur Rashid, Seong Ho Jeong
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Wi-Fi channel state information (CSI) has emerged as a promising alternative to wearable sensors for passive human activity recognition (HAR). However, its sensitivity to environmental layouts and device heterogeneity leads to highly non-IID data across users. Also, combined with the limited number of labeled CSI samples available on each device, existing federated learning (FL) approaches often suffer from representation drift and poor convergence. To address these challenges, this paper introduces a federated few-shot learning framework that incorporates prototypical learning to promote consistent and discriminative CSI embeddings across clients. Each client extracts magnitude–phase statistical features, conducts episodic N-way K-shot training, and updates its local encoder using a prototypical loss. Unlike FedAvg, the server employs an Adam-based aggregation strategy that integrates prototype-induced parameter updates, providing more stable global alignment under heterogeneous data distributions. Experiments on a multi-configuration CSI dataset demonstrate that the proposed federated prototypical learning method significantly improves recognition accuracy, training stability, and cross-environment generalization compared with state-of-the-art FL baselines. These results highlight the potential of few-shot prototypical learning as a scalable and robust solution for CSI-based human activity recognition.

title: Hierarchical Control for Occlusion-Free Visual Servoing of Robotic Manipulators
authors: Yujie Wang, Tianxiao Ye, Xiangru Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: Visual servoing leverages image features as feedback signals for robotic control, but existing methods generally lack rigorous formal guarantees for occlusion-free operation. This article presents a hierarchical optimization-based visual servoing framework for robotic manipulators that ensures strict occlusion-free operation in continuous time. The framework integrates a high-level model predictive control (MPC) with a low-level control barrier function (CBF)-based controller. Occlusion-avoidance constraints are reformulated into differentiable convex constraints via a vertex-based image representation and a duality-based optimization approach, enabling seamless incorporation into the MPC. The CBFs are constructed from the relaxed minimum distance between polytopes, with distance derivatives computed using the Karush--Kuhn--Tucker (KKT) conditions of the distance problem. The resulting framework simultaneously achieves target feature-point regulation and continuous-time enforcement of occlusion-avoidance constraints. Hardware experiments on a Franka Research 3 manipulator validate the real-time implementability and strict occlusion avoidance of the proposed approach.

title: Unbalanced and balanced competition strategy-assisted dual-swarm optimizer for constrained multi-objective optimization_supp1-3678307.pdf
authors: Yubo Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Constrained multi-objective optimization problems often exhibit complex and fragmented feasible regions, which makes global constrained Pareto front (CPF) search challenging. To address this, we present an unbalanced and balanced competitive strategy-assisted dual-swarm optimizer (UBCSO). UBCSO introduces a balanced swarm that uses random pairing and even grouping to accelerate convergence toward feasible regions, and an unbalanced, cluster-based swarm divides each cluster into three fixed groups for intra-cluster learning, promoting diverse exploration. Within clusters, we propose an unbalanced competition strategy to achieve more precise particle selection during learning, and a cluster-based environmental selection enables effective information exchange across clusters and swarms, enhancing CPF search. Additionally, the proposed cluster integration strategy enables efficient use of computing resources by reducing exploration in non-promising regions. Finally, across 37 instances, UBCSO attained the best overall ranking compared to ten popular methods (IGD+ 1.7838, HV 1.984) and achieved the best IGD+ metric in 25 out of 37 problems.

title: Allocation of Resources for Downlink Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access Systems Through Artificial Intelligence
authors: Seema Patidar, P. Ashok Dahat
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA) emerges as a highly promising multiple access technique for 5G and future-generation wireless networks, offering improved spectral efficiency by enabling simultaneous transmission to multiusers on same frequency band through power-domain multiplexing. A central challenge in NOMA implementation is optimal power allocation, which significantly impacts the system's sum rate, bit error rate, and SNR. This paper investigates the effectiveness of an AI-based approach using Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for power allocation, and compares it with the conventional NOMA method, African Buffalo Optimization Convolution neural network Successive Interference cancellation (ABO-CNN-SIC) that exhaustively evaluates all feasible power level combinations to determine the global optimum. The study considers 2-user, 3user downlink NOMA scenarios with discrete power levels under total power and noise constraints. The performance metrics include achievable sum rate, BERs, and optimality of power allocation. Results indicate that LSTM performance is higher as compare to the conventional NOMA system. The findings position LSTM as a scalable and intelligent solution for NOMA systems, especially in latency-sensitive and computationally constrained environments such as IoT and 6G.

title: Evaluation of Drain Pocket Hetero Dielectric Double Gate TFET for Improving Low Power and High-Speed performance of transistor for various Analog, RF, and Digital Application
authors: Ankit Kumar Singh, Sandeep Kumar, Arathy Rajeev, Pranshu Upadhyay
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: In this work we present the influence of a drain pocket (DP) and heterogeneous gate dielectric (HD) upon the performance of a double-gate tunnel field-effect transistor (DGTFET). With ideal area and concentration, the presented DP near the drain region is effective in mitigating band-to-band tunneling (BTBT) at the channel-DP junction, which causes ambipolar conduction to reduce to $3.0705 \times 10^{-15}$ A/μm almost four orders of magnitude lower than traditional DGTFETs. In addition, the use of Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> as an HD at the side of source, improves charge accumulation, resulting in a more abrupt source-channel junction. This leads to a considerable improvement in I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf> to the range of $\sim 10^{-4}$ A/μm with a lowI<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf>, thereby having an I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf> ratio of about $10^{13}$. In addition, RF figures of merit analysis indicate significant improvements in transconductance (g) and cutoff frequency (f). Each simulation was run using the 2D Atlas device simulator, showing that the new structure effectively overcomes TFET limitations. These results set it up for low-power analog, radio-frequency (RF), and digital applications.

title: Emotion Detection for CrossSpecies Using VGG 16 Model with Ada-Sig-Max Optimizer
authors: Vikramathithan A C, T G Manjunath, Lakshya H E, Rakshitha R
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Emotion recognition has emerged as a vital domain in Artificial Intelligence, enabling machines to interpret emotional states from visual cues and support human-machine interaction. While most existing research has focused only on human facial expressions, there remains limited work on recognizing emotions in animals, particularly dogs, which play a significant role in human life. In order to bridge this gap, we provide a deep learning-based architecture that incorporates canine emotional states into emotion recognition beyond humans, thereby broadening the scope of affective computing. Our model leverages knowledge transfers with VGG16 as the backbone, fine-tuned with additional dense layers, dropout regularization, and optimized with the Adadelta adaptive optimizer with convergence monitoring and checkpointing to ensure stability and prevent overfitting. The framework is designed for multi-class classification, enabling robust recognition of both human and dog emotions across diverse categories. The primary goal of this system is to enhance applications in healthcare, animal welfare, security, and human-animal interaction by providing a reliable tool for cross-species emotion understanding. Experimental results demonstrate promising accuracy, strong ROC performance, and well-structured classification metrics, validating the efficiency of the proposed model. In overall, this work presents a novel and scalable solution for joint human-dog emotion recognition, paving the way for future advancements in intelligent emotion-aware systems.

title: Advancing Responsible, Generative, and Explainable AI: Challenges, Opportunities, and Future Directions
authors: Mahfooz Alam, Deepak, Kaleemur Rehman, Zubair Ashraf, Bishwajeet Pandey, Laura Aldasheva
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: As AI continues to evolve rapidly, many new and productive models have emerged in the industry. But lack of transparency and accountability even after huge developments in the field of Artificial Intelligence (AI). The reason for the lack is the focus on Responsible, Generative, and Explainable AI(RGX-AI). In this paper, we propose an integration framework for GAI (Generative AIs), XAI (Explainable AIs), and RAI (Responsible AIs). Furthermore, this paper highlights some of the challenges faced in RGX-AI, such as bias, interpretability, and governance, and suggests enhancements through technological innovations, interdisciplinary collaboration, and global policy frameworks. This paper also proposes a roadmap for advancing RGX-AI, with a strong emphasis on developing scalable solutions that are both ethical and transparent, thereby gaining societal trust and delivering benefits.

title: Innovations in Cardiovascular Monitoring: Utilizing Hybrid Temporal-Causal Modeling for Advanced Warning Systems
authors: Duwara Kishore M, Gowtham G, Arun R, Vanitha M
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The primary cause of death worldwide at present is mostly due to cardiovascular diseases, affecting individuals of all ages. The primary objective of this research is to present a novel hybrid deep learning model that merges the Mobile Net and GoogleNet architectures to improve cardiovascular prediction accuracy and with high confidence so that the image prediction for new data gives a high confidence class label. The model has lightweight MobileNet and efficient feature extraction abilities in conjunction with a structured Google deep learning approach. This method gives the confidence to the patients to check for any abnormalities by simply uploading their ECG images to this application. This study utilized the Adam optimizer to enhance the model performance and achieve optimal results with the feature fusion approach. While many deep learning models are available, our hybrid approach has demonstrated superior outcomes compared to others, achieving an accuracy rate of 98 percent. The proposed approach has classified patient conditions into four categories abnormal, normal, myocardial infarction, and a history of myocardial infarction.

title: IoT Based PV Powered Battery Swapping Station for LCV
authors: Hassan A, Annobeaut A V, Umesh Prabhu H, Ramesh Babu M
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Wide scale adoption of electric light commercial vehicles (LCVs) is constrained by limitations like insufficient sustainable energy, lengthy charging times, and mains-based dependence on the electrical grid. This work proposes an Internet of Things (IoT)-based, photovoltaic (PV) powered battery swapping station designed for autonomous, trustworthy, and efficient energy management. Real-time monitoring of electrochemical and environmental variables is used to optimize battery performance, extend cycle life, and allow predictive maintenance. A hierarchical Battery Management System (BMS) is used for adaptive state-of-charge estimation, dynamic cell equalization, and thermal management, hence assuring reliability of operation. Maximum power point tracking is used by a PV array to maintain constant energy harvesting despite changing irradiance, and IoT telemetry provides for fault prediction, intelligent scheduling of energy, and edge-to-cloud integration. The novelty of this work is the combination of renewable energy harvesting, sophisticated IoT analytics, and multi-layered intelligence of a Battery Management System (BMS), hence offering a sustainable, self-optimal, and resilient solution for urban battery-powered commercial vehicles.

title: Deep Learning-Based Early Detection of Parkinson’s Disease via Neuromelanin Color Analysis in MRI Images
authors: Rudra Pratap Singh, E. Rajesh, Krishna Kant Agrawal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The neurodegenerative disorder known as Parkinson's disease (PD) is incapacitating millions of individuals globally, leading to gradual loss of motor function, cognitive impairment, and substantial declines in quality of life. As one of the most prevalent neurological disorders, PD poses an escalating threat to healthcare systems worldwide, especially with the rise in the aging population. Delayed diagnosis restricts treatment options, ruling out timely measures that would be able to retard disease progression. The current investigation investigates the capability of of deep learning (DL) and machine learning (ML) models in detecting early neuromelanin depletion as a biomarker for Parkinson's disease. Three models were used for classification: Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). Our dataset contains 1000 Parkinson's or healthy annotated images, obtained from publicly available repositories. All images were preprocessed applying color space conversions (RGB to LAB), normalization, and data augmentation. The best classification performance was obtained by the CNN model with an accuracy of 91.7% and a ROC-AUC value of 94.1%. These results confirm the potential of neuromelanin color analysis as a trustworthy tool for the diagnosis of early PD, when merged with ML techniques.

title: Performance Analysis of Various Cryptography Algorithms in Amazon Cloud
authors: Preeti Lalwani, Sushila Sonare
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Cloud computing has transformed data storage and processing by providing scalable, cost-effective, and on-demand resources. Despite its benefits, ensuring the security of data in cloud environments remains a significant challenge. Crypto-graphic algorithms are essential in maintaining data confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity. This study conducts a comprehensive performance analysis of widely-used cryptographic algorithms-AES, RSA, ECC, Blowfish, and SHA-256-on Amazon Web Services (AWS) EC2 instances. The evaluation focuses on encryption/decryption time, CPU usage, memory consumption, and scalability. Findings reveal that symmetric algorithms such as AES and Blowfish deliver superior performance for large-scale data encryption, while ECC outperforms RSA in public-key operations. These insights support the selection of appropriate cryptographic techniques tailored to specific cloud application needs.

title: Data-Driven Intelligent Time-Series Prediction of Shield Machine Trajectories
authors: Xiangyu Li, Xuanyu Liu
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: To address the low accuracy of existing shield machine trajectory prediction models, this paper proposes a data-driven, multi-step time series prediction method based on the Informer model. After collecting tunneling data and selecting key inputs via Pearson correlation analysis, the model utilizes a ProbSparse self-attention mechanism to learn trajectory patterns. Trained and tested on real datasets, the model accurately predicts future trajectories. An interactive information extraction network is incorporated to enhance prediction accuracy and output multiple potential trajectories, reflecting real distribution and improving operational safety. Results confirm the model's high accuracy and applicability.

title: Smart Traffic Signal System Using Edge AI for Emergency Vehicle Detection
authors: Isha Lanjewar, Apeksha Sakhare, Chetan Dhule
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: The congestion and delays are experienced in the response time of emergency vehicles in the metropolitan areas due to urbanization and the high rate of vehicular traffic. The traditional traffic signal control systems that are based on fixed timing and processing at a central location cannot adjust to dynamic traffic and emergency situations effectively. In this paper, a review is provided along with an intellectual Edge-AI-based framework of intelligent traffic signal control including multi-level priority management of emergency vehicles. The literature survey covers the recent advances in Edge Artificial Intelligence-based traffic systems that encompass the emergency vehicles detection and classification using deep learning and computer vision models, including YOLOv8. Several system architectures based on low-cost edge computer systems such as Raspberry Pi and NVIDIA Jetson Nano are focused on to illustrate how local processing can be used to facilitate timely classification of vehicles, assign priority, and adaptive signal timing. The Edge-deployed system has much lower latency rates and higher scalability rates than other traditional cloud-based solutions, which is why they are the solution needed to manage traffic in real-time in smart cities. The paper presents the main design issues, challenges, and future research directions of the implementation of the Edge-AI-driven traffic signal control systems to enhance the efficiency of the emergency vehicles response and the stability of the overall traffic flow.

title: A Senior Citizen Information Management System Integrating Arduino-Based RFID Automated Attendance Tracking at Brgy. Tolosa, Cabadbaran City
authors: Agnes T. Hempesao, Francine Kaye P. Bayang, Shania G. Bugahod, Samantha Danielle Galapate
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The health center in Barangay Tolosa, Cabadbaran City, encounters several inefficiencies on a day-to-day basis. It has errors which cause it to delay the services that are being given by the barangay health workers. Certain tasks, such as managing health records, medication dispensing, and processing government aid, become disorganized. In line with this, our team created a system that integrates Arduino technology and RFID features. This set-up will automate the attendance of patients and thus effectively handle the health records, distribution of medicine, and distribution of government assistance to the said patients in their respective health centers. They used tools and software such as PHP, MySQL via XAMPP, Bootstrap, Python, and Arduino with RFID modules. All these components put together provide an easy-to-use real-time update and maintain a record. At the end, the system would be able to serve senior citizens faster, more accurately, and with better response times.

title: Student-Directed Flipped Classroom for Experimental and Sustainability Education: A Case Study in Hong Kong
authors: Dongkun Han, Martin Yun-Yee Leung
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: This work presents the design and evaluation of a student-directed flipped classroom integrated with an Internet-ofThings (IoT)-enabled “Smart Garden” to advance experiential and sustainability-focused engineering education. The platform combines renewable-energy and recycling projects with real-time sensing, actuation, and cloud dashboards, enabling authentic experimentation both in-person and remotely.
