Two cohorts implemented 24 device sets and six micro-modules covering solar, wind, and automation topics. Survey results indicate 79-92% positive feedback across learning experience, micromodules, device development, and application usability, while controlled comparisons show consistent performance gains in laboratory assessments. The approach aligns with evidencebased practices in flipped and active learning, demonstrating how IoT integration can scale hands-on engagement, strengthen sustainability competencies, and enhance resilience for hybrid delivery.

title: Multimodal EV Battery Health Prediction Using Time-Series Sensor Fusion and Graph Neural Networks
authors: Velmurugan T, Yuvaraj D, Abisheckkumaran S, Elangovan D, Karthick Kumar P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Electric car batteries must be kept safe, dependable, and simple to service by monitoring their State of Health (SoH) and Remaining Useful Life (RUL). However, because lithium-ion batteries’ performance varies with working conditions, temperature stressors, and natural aging, accurately predicting their condition remains challenging. Many contemporary deep-learning techniques capture degradation trends rather effectively, despite the fact that they primarily concentrate on time-based patterns and ignore the relationships between individual charge-discharge cycles.This research presents a hybrid model that combines Transformer-style attention, multimodal sensor data, and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to overcome this restriction.The system is intended to learn both long-term temporal behaviour and cycle-to-cycle relationships using actual battery-aging statistics. According to our findings, adding graph-based attention yields predictions that are more reliable and accurate than traditional sequence-only models.

title: Secure Edge : A Survey on Security Risks and Challenges in Edge Computing
authors: Thejaswini S, R Aparna
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: In an epoch of cyberspace, the importunity for edge computing is growing in business and organization sectors owing to the necessitate for real time data processing, escalating adoption of latest technologies namely IoT, Blockchain, Artificial Intelligence, Digital Twin and manifold perks namely diminished bandwidth, and latency costs etc., Breaches are becoming common and increasing exponentially because edge devices are widely distributed, oftentimes placed in unsecured physical locations and have limited computing power. New threat vectors are springing as the technologies unfold with inadequate security capabilities in edge computing which significantly elevates the security risks and challenges. A breach in either physical or digital layer can expose an organization to serious risks, including data loss, financial harm, reputational damage, and compliance violations. An accomplished security strategy includes not only strong digital protection such as encryption, firewalls, and access control but also robust physical security protocols to safeguard against unauthorized access, theft and tampering of devices. Therefore, the study mainly focuses on rendering the advanced edge security components, security risks, challenges and need for security to handle the physical and digital security in edge computing. These insights not only help the researchers and developers to design appropriate protection mechanisms, strengthen device level security, prevent system wide breaches but also helps to build secure, trustworthy and resilient edge-based applications.

title: A Multi-Protocol, Explainable AI-Based Framework for Real-Time IoT Forensics
authors: Manojkumar M, Sangeetha V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) deployments in smart homes, healthcare, and industrial environments has introduced significant challenges for cybercrime investigation and digital forensics. IoT networks generate large volumes of heterogeneous and often encrypted traffic over multiple communication protocols, such as Wi-Fi and Zigbee, limiting the effectiveness of traditional post-incident forensic techniques. Due to constrained device storage, a lack of centralized logging, and short-lived network interactions, critical forensic evidence may be lost before it can be preserved. This paper proposes a real-time, AI-enhanced IoT forensic framework designed to improve forensic readiness through continuous edge-based traffic analysis. The proposed framework integrates multi-protocol packet capture, machine learning-based anomaly detection, and Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) to enable transparent and interpretable forensic decision-making. The system is evaluated using multiple benchmark datasets, including CIC IoT-IDAD 2024, Edge-IIoTset, N-BaIoT, UNSW HomeNet, and ZigBee traffic traces. Several machine learning and deep learning models, such as XGBoost, LightGBM, LSTM, and GRU, are trained and compared using standard evaluation metrics. Experimental results indicate that tree-based models achieve high detection accuracy with low inference latency, making them suitable for real-time deployment on lightweight edge devices. The integration of SHAP and LIME provides feature-level explanations for detection outcomes, enhancing the reliability and interpretability of AI-based forensic evidence.

title: A Transparent Approach to Browser Tracking Analysis for Privacy Protection
authors: G. Sivakarthi, S. Srigana, S.S. Sushanthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Privacy on the internet in the modern world is more exposed than ever, as users unwittingly disclose data via sites and embedded trackers. NoTrace is a project that provides a browser extension privacy analyzer, seeking to enhance user awareness and control of their online exposure. The system scans websites in real-time to detect hidden trackers, checks browser permissions, and offers a privacy exposure score. It also offers simple-to-use, actionable recommendations for data leakage reduction. Created with today’s web technologies, including HTML (HyperText Markup Language), CSS(Cascading Style Sheets), and JavaScript, NoTrace boasts an approachable user interface and elegant visualizations for discussing complex privacy matters. Unlike most traditional privacy solutions, NoTrace emphasizes transparency, education, and empowerment of the users. NoTrace will make online tracking transparent so that users can make active attempts to safeguard their private information. By its courteous and didactic structure, the tool promotes respectful use of the internet and renders its contribution to larger goals of moral use of technology and data protection.

title: Optimized 5-Stage RISC-V Pipeline with Lightweight Instruction Cache for Embedded Systems
authors: Prathiyangaran K K, Shankar Harikrishnan, Rajesh Kannan Megalingam
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: The technological advancement in recent years has lead to significantly increase the demand for embedded systems which are having the attributes of being both high-performance and energy-efficient. This requirement has lead to continuous changes and innovation in processor architectures, especially in the field of open-source platforms like RISC-V. This work is used to present a five-stage pipelined RISC-V processor which has been integrated with a lightweight instruction cache which is used to enhance performance the performance of the processor under constrained power conditions. Data hazards which arise are mitigated by employing forwarding paths, while optimized branch control logic is used to reduce the stalls which occurs in pipieline. The design proposed in this study take the help of a 4-entry direct-mapped instruction cache with the intention of improving instruction fetch efficiency there by achieving higher execution speed with minimal additional power overhead. Based on established architectural principles and validated through FPGA-based implementation, the processor proposed in this study serves both as an effective educational platform and also acts as a practical low-power solution for future embedded and also consumer electronic applications.

title: Adaptive Malware Defense: A Multi-Agent System for Malware Detection and Adaptive Response
authors: Rahul Katara, Mary Mekala A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Malware remains a persistent threat to governments, corporations, and individuals, with conventional signature-based detection methods proving inadequate against polymorphic, fileless, and adversarially designed threats. This paper presents Adaptive Malware Defense (AMD), a multi-agent system integrating static analysis, dynamic sandboxing, threat intelligence enrichment, explainable machine learning, and Large Language Model (LLM)-based reasoning for comprehensive malware detection. The system achieves 94.5% accuracy using Logistic Regression with SHAP(SHapley Additive exPlanations)based interpretability, with advanced models reaching 97.2% accuracy. External threat intelligence from VirusTotal, AbuseIPDB, and the National Vulnerability Database enriches detection context. The complete analysis pipeline executes in an average of 9.07 seconds, demonstrating practical feasibility for Security Operations Center workflows. Adversarial robustness testing reveals resilience under obfuscation, with transformer-based models maintaining 92.5% accuracy. A Streamlit-based prototype validates operational utility through interactive dashboards, automated reporting, and analyst-friendly interfaces. By unifying detection mechanisms with explainable AI and LLM-based contextual reasoning, AMD bridges the gap between detection and actionable defense.

title: Improving Power Quality and Minimizing Harmonic Distortion in Grid-Connected Power Electronics with Power Filters
authors: Rudramurthy V.C., Gururaja H.S., Padmanabha J., Sarika Ghanshyam Jadhav, Somarouthu V.G.V.A. Prasad, T.C. Manjunath, Pavithra G.
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: Improving power quality and minimizing harmonic distortion in grid-connected power electronics with power filters for renewable energy applications using IoT Concepts is presented along with the simulation results. The electrical powered systems is defined as a framework of electric part that is inter-connected to give supply, exchanging, store & utilizing the electric power. The EP n/w would be called upon as a network which gives energy to a broadened zone. An electrical lattice control network could be categorized in to the hybridized generator which will supply the powers, transmit it to the network which will convey the electrical powers from generating station up to the distributing station, which will nourish this abilities for close use @ the homes, industries & agri uses. The work done & presented here has got lot of applications in the power electronics fields.

title: Deep Learning-Powered Automated Detection and Classification of Retina Diseases Using Multi-Modal Fundus and OCT Imaging Data
authors: Asha Joseph, K. Rajakumari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Scientists now focus on automatically spotting, categorizing eye problems. More people experience sight loss from diseases like diabetic retinopathy, macular edema, age-related macular degeneration. This makes quick, accurate diagnosis very important. We built a system using advanced computer techniques. It combines eye photos with detailed scans to help doctors make more precise diagnoses. The process uses separate computer systems to pull out details from different kinds of information. Then, it combines those details, analyzes them with a series of connections, finally delivers a result. We trained the system, testing its performance, using datasets anyone can access. These include DIARETDB1, a collection of eye images, with OCTID, a set of eye scans. We prepared the data thoroughly, creating many variations to help it work well with new information. Tests used a common method for measuring error, alongside a popular technique for refining the process. The testing numbers demonstrate this system works better than those focusing on only one type of data. It correctly identified items 95.2% of the time, scored 0.95 using the F1 method, with an area under the curve of 0.97. Looking at results by type, the system works well for all illnesses. It especially does a good job finding healthy eyes, or those with age-related macular degeneration. This research shows how combining different types of data with advanced computer analysis could help doctors find eye diseases quickly, with good accuracy.

title: Secure Offline Service Record System Using Laravel and AES for Municipal HR Management
authors: Rexon L. Timbal, Prince Kenny D. Narisma, Aicy Mae D. Andonga, Kent Melvin B. Abenion
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: This paper introduces a secure, offline Service Record Information System specifically designed to support municipal Human Resource Management Offices (HRMOs). Built using the Laravel PHP framework and integrated with AES-256 encryption, the system enables secure employee record management through CRUD operations, PDF generation, and role-based access controls—all without relying on an internet connection. By hosting the application locally via XAMPP, it ensures consistent access and data protection, particularly in LGUs where connectivity is limited. The system was evaluated using the ISO/IEC 25010 software quality model, with results showing high user satisfaction—achieving mean scores of 4.56 in functionality, 4.51 in usability, and 4.49 in security. These findings demonstrate the system’s potential to streamline HR processes while safeguarding sensitive government records in offline environments.

title: Self-Adaptive Thresholding for Semi-Supervised Multi-Modal Entity Alignment
authors: Jun Li, Lingxuan Zhu, Sunjie Huang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: Multi-modal entity alignment (MMEA) aims to identify equivalent entities across different multi-modal knowledge graphs (MMKGs), using alignment seeds as supervisory signals during training. To mitigate the scarcity of manually labeled alignment seeds, existing semi-supervised EA methods expand training data through iterative training strategies but fail to account for the model's evolving learning state. Additionally, the substantial noise in pseudo-labels severely hinders alignment performance. To overcome these limitations, we propose SATEA, a novel semi-supervised MMEA method that mitigates performance degradation from imbalanced pseudo-label filtering by dynamically updating the threshold during training. Additionally, a hard-negative-mixing noise weighting strategy is introduced to further mitigate the impact of noisy labels. Experimental results show that SATEA achieves state-of-the-art performance on two datasets, confirming its effectiveness and superiority in practical applications.

title: MediSync: An AI-Enabled Framework for Secure And Lightweight Healthcare Data Sharing using RBAC and OTP-Based Consent
authors: Abdul Rehman, Abhinav Singh Negi, Shaily Saxena, Kanika Singla, Sudeep Varshney
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: In India, medical records remain scattered across hospitals, clinics, and laboratories, creating major challenges for continuity of care, timely diagnosis, and patient empowerment. Patients often repeat tests, face delays in treatment, and struggle to access their own health history. To address these issues, we propose MediSync, a secure and patient-centered digital health platform. MediSync uses a unique patient ID with a one-time password (OTP)–based consent system that allows patients to decide who can view or add to their records. Doctors and labs receive time-limited access only with explicit patient approval, and every action is logged for full transparency. The platform also integrates AI-based tools, including a lab report summarizer that translates complex medical terms into simple language, and a chatbot that helps patients navigate their health data. Designed to be lightweight and modular, MediSync can be deployed in both urban hospitals and rural clinics and remains aligned with national initiatives like the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission. Initial testing shows that MediSync reduces delays, strengthens privacy, and increases patient trust, making it a practical step toward secure and interoperable healthcare in India.

title: AgriCredit-360: Blockchain-Enabled Intelligent Loan Management Scheme for Sustainable Agriculture
authors: Aparna Kumari, Prem Joshi, Lakshya Mehta, Maahi Goratela
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Farmers often struggle to access timely and fair credit due to inefficiencies, limited transparency, and inadequate risk assessment in traditional loan systems. This paper presents AgriCredit-360, a blockchain-enabled intelligent loan management scheme designed to improve financial inclusion and promote sustainable agriculture. Leveraging Ethereum's decentralized architecture alongside the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS), the proposed framework ensures secure, transparent, and tamperproof loan records while enabling cost-efficient off-chain storage of large agricultural datasets such as satellite imagery, IoT sensor readings, climate trends, and yield forecasts. AI-driven analytics assess farmers' creditworthiness using multi-source data, integrating sustainability metrics to incentivize eco-friendly farming practices. Ethereum-based smart contracts automate loan approval, disbursement, repayment tracking, subsidy allocation, and insurance claim settlement, significantly reducing operational delays, administrative costs, and fraud risks. The hybrid blockchain-IPFS design enhances scalability, supports multi-stakeholder interoperability, and preserves data integrity through cryptographic hashing. By combining blockchain security, decentralized storage, AI-powered decision-making, and sustainability-oriented credit policies, AgriCredit-360 delivers an efficient, transparent, and farmer-centric solution.

title: AI-Guided Schema Evolution and Real-Time Data Quality Enhancement in Multi-Source Pipelines
authors: Shreekant Malviya, Abhishek Jaiswal, Vivek Koli
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Data from different processing systems, such as industry and business applications, often contain missing values and inconsistent data formats. These issues are common in multi-source environments. This research proposes a hybrid AI framework that combines three sub-components to address these challenges effectively. A genetic algorithm (GA) optimizes rules for handling varied inputs, fuzzy logic manages errors using predictive patterns, and deep learning (DL) detects structural patterns during data processing. The proposed method improves pipeline consistency and processing accuracy. Test-bed results show significant gains, achieving 96.8% accuracy, 94.3% completeness, and 92.1% adaptability, while latency is reduced from 16 ms to 12 ms. These improvements are driven by dynamic structure pipelines, missing data updates, and adaptive rule tuning. Compared to existing models, this framework adapts to diverse data patterns and process delays, making it suitable for large and dynamic data engineering platforms such as digital systems and IoT devices.

title: ShuddhVayu: An Embedded Air-Purification System for Roadside and Industrial Emissions
authors: Sumit Tiwari, Pranjal Kumar, Parth Arora, Sanya Goyal, Miss Swarnima, Sarabjeet Kaur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Air pollution caused by vehicular traffic and industrial operations continues to pose significant environmental and health challenges, particularly in densely populated urban and industrial zones. In response to this growing concern, this study proposes a dual-purpose pollution control system capable of capturing airborne pollutants directly from moving vehicles and stationary industrial outlets. The system utilizes motion-sensitive infrared and gas sensors to detect emission sources in real-time, activating a smart filtration unit only when pollutants are present. This energy-efficient approach minimizes power consumption while maximizing pollutant capture. The collected particulate matter, primarily carbon-based, is then processed and repurposed into usable ink—contributing to a circular economy model that transforms waste into value-added material. We built and tested a working prototype in controlled conditions, and the results were quite encouraging. It effectively reduced pollutants, showed quick sensor responses, and produced a good amount of usable ink. The modular setup makes it flexible enough to be placed along roads or fitted onto factory chimneys without changing the existing systems. By combining real-time tracking, air filtration, and material recovery, this system offers a practical and scalable way for both cities and industries to reduce their environmental impact. Its strength lies in its flexibility, efficiency, and sustainable design. This marks a smart step forward in pollution control and emission management

title: VMamba-SLAM: Real-Time Dynamic Scene Adaptation Via Vision Mamba and Selective Feature Preservation for Enhanced Robotic Localization
authors: Jin Sun, Xue Shen, Haowei Huang, Ruicheng Li, Qin Wang, Haitao Zhao, Tomoaki Ohtsuki, Guan Gui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Dynamic Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) remains a challenging problem in robotics, particularly due to the interference of moving objects with feature-based localization and mapping. In this paper, we introduce a novel dynamic object-aware SLAM system built upon ORB-SLAM3, which integrates a computationally efficient Vision Mamba (VMamba) model with an advanced dynamic object filtering strategy. The proposed VMamba model, comprising only 8.8 million parameters, achieves high-precision object detection with minimal computational overhead, thereby enabling real-time operation on embedded platforms. A key contribution of this work is the development of an improved dynamic object filtering strategy that selectively retains static features within dynamic object bounding boxes. By leveraging Harris corners, optical flow, and epipolar geometry, our approach effectively eliminates dynamic outliers while preserving essential static scene information—a significant advancement over conventional methods that indiscriminately remove features. Experimental evaluations on the TUM Dynamic and BONN datasets demonstrate that our method attains superior Absolute Trajectory Error (ATE) performance compared to state-of-the-art dynamic SLAM approaches, including ORB-SLAM3, DynaSLAM, and YOLO-SLAM. Furthermore, real-time deployment on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX 16 GB platform incurs only a 6.45% reduction in Frames Per Second (FPS) relative to YOLO-SLAM, while achieving a 28.68% improvement in average ATE. These results underscore the computational efficiency of VMamba and the robust real-time capabilities of our SLAM system. Our study validates that integrating a lightweight, high-precision object detection model with an effective dynamic object filtering strategy can significantly enhance the robustness, accuracy, and real-time performance of dynamic SLAM, even on resource-constrained platforms. Future work will focus on further optimizing VMamba and extending the dynamic filtering techniques to better accommodate increasingly complex dynamic environments, thereby broadening its applicability in real-world robotic systems.

title: A Multilayer Heterogeneous Knowledge Distillation-Based Multi-Step Prediction Method for Heavy Medium Density and its Application in Coal Preparation Plants
authors: Feng Jin, Jingpei Zhang, Jun Zhao, Wei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Accurate prediction of the parameters is crucial to improve the yield of clean coal and the efficiency of the medium circulation in the heavy medium density (HMD) separation process, while the complexity and variability of the production processes along with the high dynamic operational characteristics lead to insufficient robustness and high computational costs of the existing prediction methods. In this study, a multi-step prediction method based on multilayer heterogeneous knowledge distillation (MHKD) is proposed. To achieve the structured semantic representation of different working conditions, a noise suppression model based on empirical wavelet transform is developed, enabling adaptive partitioning of the granularities for different working conditions through monotonicity and curvature characteristics. Then, an elastic standardization (ES) method based on granular polynomial elastic function is designed to align non-equal-length time series granules into equal-length ones, in order to retain the key characteristics of the data. Furthermore, to balance the computational efficiency and the accuracy, the student models with an external exit branch layer and a convolutional alignment layer are designed, so as to transfer the multi-level knowledge from the heterogeneous teacher model to the student one. Experiments by using actual operational data from two coal preparation plants in China are carried out, which illustrate that the proposed method outperforms the existing ones in terms of prediction accuracy and computational efficiency. Additionally, an industrial application software system based on the proposed method has also been successfully deployed and implemented in the management center of the enterprise.

title: Non-urgent Messages Do Not Jump into My Headset Suddenly! Adaptive Notification Design in Mixed Reality
authors: Jingyao Zheng, Xian Wang, Sven Mayer, Lik-Hang Lee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Mixed reality (MR) notification systems currently display all messages in fixed central locations regardless of urgency, leading to unnecessary interruptions and cognitive overload. Drawing from previous MR/Virtual Reality (VR) notification design work and calm technology principles, we developed an adaptive notification system that adjusts spatial placement based on urgency levels: non-urgent notifications appear as peripheral icons accessible via head movement, moderately urgent messages anchor to the user's hand, and very urgent notifications transition progressively from peripheral to central view. Through a within-subjects study (N = 18), we evaluated our adaptive system against the default centralised approach. Results demonstrate that the adaptive Get your job done now! Boss Boss Get your job done now! Call me when you have time Friend system significantly reduces mental workload (p = 0.041), temporal workload (p = 0.008), and frustration (p = 0.004) while maintaining comparable notification awareness. Logistic regression analysis reveals that users prefer the adaptive system even with classification errors, provided the combined misclassification rate (disruptiveness + omission errors) remains below a determinable threshold. Our findings establish the first empirical evidence that urgency-based spatial notification distribution effectively addresses core MR usability challenges, offering practical design guidelines for immersive notification systems that balance user attention management with information accessibility.

title: SceneHub4D: A Dataset and Evaluation Framework for 6-DoF 4D VR Scenes
authors: Jaehong Kim, Tao Jin, Mallesham Dasari, Srinivasan Seshan, Anthony Rowe
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Volumetric video and 6-DoF scene capture are becoming central to immersive applications such as telepresence and mixed reality content delivery. However, existing volumetric datasets are often short in duration, restricted to studio-captured human subjects, and provide only limited geometric representations. Consequently, evaluating real-world immersive applications in full-scene contexts often necessitates custom capture and 3D reconstruction setups, creating high practical barriers and ultimately hindering reproducibility. To this end, we present SceneHub4D, a new dataset and evaluation framework. Our dataset captures long, dynamic sequences across diverse real-world indoor environments with synchronized multi-view RGB-D streams, calibrated camera poses, and high-resolution background geometry reconstructed via photogrammetry and LiDAR. We provide multiple 3D representations, including point clouds, textured meshes, and Gaussian splats, along with a software toolkit for format conversion, rendering, and metric evaluation. To support structured comparison and perceptual analysis, we provide supplementary metrics including Geometry Complexity Score and Volumetric Temporal Information, and evaluate rendering performance across desktop GPUs and VR headsets. By lowering the practical barriers to capture, reconstruction, and evaluation, SceneHub4D enables researchers to study immersive 3D streaming and rendering systems without requiring custom hardware setups or complex data collection pipelines. We expect it will serve as a useful foundation for advancing volumetric media research.

title: Experimental Realization of the Second and Third Stages of a Solid-State Transformer
authors: Nassim Zemirline, Nadir Kabeche, Samir Moulahoum, Sofiane Khechafi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Solid-State Transformers (SSTs) are emerging as key components for future smart grids, gradually replacing conventional line-frequency transformers with more flexible and efficient power electronic solutions. Their modular structure, high controllability, and ability to integrate distributed generation make them attractive for modern power distribution systems. Among different SST topologies, the three-stage configuration has gained significant attention, as it provides galvanic isolation and independent control of both DC and AC ports. At the core of this architecture, the Dual Active Bridge (DAB) converter is considered one of the most suitable DC-DC converters due to its bidirectional power flow, soft-switching capability, and relatively simple control. These features make it a prominent candidate for SST applications. This paper presents the experimental realization of the second and third stages of a three-stage SST topology. A scaled-down prototype was developed and tested under different operating conditions to validate reference voltage tracking, load adaptability, robustness against input disturbances, and stability during integrated operation. Results confirm that the adopted control strategies ensure accurate voltage regulation, seamless power transfer, and reliable operation.

title: Experimental Implementation of FS-Predictive Current Control with MPPT-OTC for Performance Optimization of PMSG-Based Wind Energy Systems
authors: Hicham Sayhi, Amor Bourek, Abdelkarim Ammar, Ali Teta, Derradji Bakria, Ahmed Chennana
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This study proposes a predictive current control strategy for standalone wind energy conversion systems (WECSs) based on permanent magnet synchronous generators (PMSGs). The approach integrates finite-set predictive current control (FS-PCC) with an optimal-torque-control (OTC) MPPT algorithm to achieve accurate stator current regulation, reduced ripple, and improved dynamic response under variable wind conditions. Experimental tests on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 k W$</tex> setup using the dSPACE 1104 platform validate the controller's fast transient behavior, stable standalone operation, and enhanced energy extraction efficiency, demonstrating robust performance in stochastic wind environments.

title: Cost Emission Optimization in Hybrid Power System Incorporating Solar Irradiance Variations
authors: Abid Mokhtar, Mekhalfia Ramadhan, Abid Wail
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Rising electricity demand and continued reliance on thermal generation have increased both the cost of power production and greenhouse-gas emissions. Integrating renewable energy sources (RES) is therefore essential, but RES introduce variability and uncertainty that complicate the operator's task of allocating generation economically and reliably. This paper addresses the stochastic optimal power flow (SOPF) problem with solar photovoltaic generation integrated into the network. The stochastic behavior of solar irradiance is modeled using log-normal probability density functions, respectively. To solve the resulting SOPF, we propose a novel metaheuristic Electric Eel Foraging Optimization (EEFO). The algorithm is tested on standard systems, including the IEEE 30bus case, and its performance is evaluated against results reported in the literature. Numerical experiments show that RME delivers competitive solutions under uncertainty, improving cost-related and operational metrics while maintaining network security.

title: Comprehensive Lung Disease Classification and Severity Assessment using AC-CXR: A Hybrid Convolution–Transformer Framework
authors: Sadasivuni Sai Rahul, Mohammad Farukh Hashmi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Chest radiography remains a frontline imaging modality for rapid, low-cost screening and monitoring of pulmonary disease worldwide. This paper presents AC-CXR, a unified deep learning framework that couples DenseNet201 convolutional feature extraction with Transformer-based global attention and a small set of learnable atlas tokens that en code coarse anatomical priors. AC-CXR performs two clinically complementary tasks: (1) multi-class disease classification among Normal, Pneumonia, Tuberculosis, and COVID-19, and (2) continuous severity estimation aligned to radiological scoring systems (RALE and Brixia) via a regression head. We curate and harmonize a multi-source corpus of 21,652 chest X-rays drawn from six public datasets, apply a robust preprocessing pipeline (CLAHE, intensity normalization, geometric and photometric augmentation), and mitigate imbalance through class-weighted loss and stratified sampling. On held-out data AC-CXR achieves 93.5classification, and demonstrates clinically meaningful severity correlations (RALE Spearman = 0.620, Pearson r = 0.636). Ablation studies confirm the value of the DenseNet backbone for local texture cues, Transformer modules for long-range contextualization, and atlas tokens for zonal severity estimation. Wefurther provide model interpretability artifacts — normalized confusion matrix, per-class precision–recall curves, lesion/opacity overlays, and severity-balanced example panels — to facilitate clinical review and trust. Finally, we discuss limitations and outline future directions including UNet-based segmentation and prospective validation

title: Adaptive Irrigation and Crop Monitoring for Micro-Farms Using AI-Based Analytics
authors: Sinthuja PM, R Aroul Canessane
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The application of automation technologies to micro-farm environments takes advantage of a set of sensors, Internet of Things (IoT) devices, and machine learning algorithms to track vital agricultural parameters such as soil moisture, ambient temperature, light intensity, and nutrient content. The system enables autonomous decision-making by continuously collecting and analyzing real-time data, including irrigation control, power management, and fertilizer balancing. This ensures that crops are cultivated in optimal environmental conditions, increasing productivity and minimizing manual labor. In addition, the automated micro-farming system provides predictive data related to crop yield and resource consumption, supporting informed managerial decision-making. Sustainable agriculture is achieved through optimized use of water and nutrients, reduced waste, and minimized environmental impact. Furthermore, the system supports remote monitoring and control through a dedicated user interface, allowing operators to assess farm conditions and take necessary actions from any location. The proposed framework offers a feasible solution to improving efficiency, sustainability, and productivity in contemporary micro-farming by integrating automation with data-driven decision-making.

title: Learning and Debugging Assistant for Python with Retrieval Augmented Generation Capabilities
authors: Kathiroli R, Logarathan S V, Akash S, Santhiya K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Large Language Models(LLMs) often lack domain-specific grounding and may produce generic, hallucinated, or context-insensitive responses, despite their remarkable capability in assisting programming tasks. Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems aim to address these limitations by incorporating external knowledge sources; however, many existing implementations remain passive responders with limited interactivity and pedagogical depth. This work presents the Rubber Duck Assistant, a RAG-powered python learning and debugging system that combines interactive dialog, metacognitive learning, semantic retrieval via ChromaDB, and real-time code execution. Inspired by the Rubber Duck Debugging approach, the assistant guides students to articulate and reflect on their code through precise explanations, bug identification, and correction strategies. Key contributions include a hybrid keyword-based LLM query classifier, format-aware chunk embedding, and a dedicated Debug Sandbox for safe code exploration. The Evaluation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the assistant, achieving 92% accuracy and a 91% F1 score in Python query classification. By fostering deeper learning and independent debugging skills, this study highlights how interactive and grounded artificial intelligence tutors can enhance programming education.

title: Design of a Buck—Boost Converter with Parasitic Elements Using a Sliding Mode Controller
authors: Mathew John Kamara, Mustafa Aktaş, Barış Çavuş
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Buck—boost DC-DC converters are widely used in photovoltaic and electric vehicle power stages because they can step voltage up or down. Conventional control strategies often assume ideal switching components, yet real converters exhibit parasitic resistances that degrade dynamic performance and reduce efficiency. This paper derives a continuous conduction mode state space model of an inverting buck—boost converter that incorporates inductor series resistance and capacitor ESR. An integral sliding mode controller is then developed to regulate the output voltage under large input voltage and load disturbances. The sliding surface combines the integral of the output voltage error and the instantaneous inductor current. A constant rate reaching law ensures finite time convergence, and a discrete time implementation is obtained via Euler integration. Comprehensive simulations compare the proposed sliding mode controller with a conventional proportional—integral controller. As a result of the study, the sliding mode controller achieved a lower overshoot and reached the steady state more rapidly. Under load steps, the sliding mode controller recovers three times faster than the proportional—integral controller. These results demonstrate that modelling parasitic elements and using an integral sliding mode controller significantly improves transient response and robustness.

title: LIRF: A Lightweight Random Forest with Sliding Window Model for Node Anomaly Detection in Edge Based WSN
authors: Bussa V V H Chandrasekhar, Mohammad Sirajuddin, G Ramakrishna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are extensively employed in diverse applications because of their potential to provide seamless communication with sensing, collecting and transmitting real time data. Due to these strengths, WSNs are used in scenarios where physical network setup or continuous human monitoring is impractical. Despite their strengths, WSNs are susceptible to various security attacks due to open communication medium. Ensuring secure communication in WSN is one of the prominent and challenging issues. In this work, we address the issue of anomalous node detection by applying artificial intelligence. An anomalous node sends erratic data that leads to inaccurate analysis and decision making. The presence of anomalous node not only degrades network performance but also compromises data integrity. Many existing anomaly detection techniques are centralized and use batch processing models, which can lead to high latency, scalability issues, and excessive energy consumption. In this work, we propose LiRF, a lightweight anomaly detection framework based on artificial intelligence. It is deployed at the edge nodes in the WSN. LiRF incorporates a random forest classifier and sliding window mechanism to detect real time node anomalies. These properties support LiRF to operate efficiently with low computational overhead and achieve high detection accuracy. We assess the efficiency of the proposed framework by comparing it with existing machine learning based techniques. Our results justify that the proposed framework exhibits higher efficiency in detecting anomalous nodes than existing approaches.

title: Overcoming Challenge of Yeild Capacity: Machine Learning Approach Towards Precision Agriculture
authors: Vaishnavi J Deshmukh, Chanchal Kshirsagar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Agriculture is the backbone of the Indian economy, providing a living for more than half of the country's people. Weather, climate, and other environmental factors have become important threats to agriculture's long-term viability. Crop yields vary annually due to climate, operational, and economic reasons. Predicting agricultural yields improves field operations and management. Crop production predictions can contribute to national food security efforts. This is a global step towards achieving the Asian Sustainable Development Goal of Zero Hunger. This study identifies and predicts key elements influencing staple crop productivity in India. The current study focuses on a systematic review that extracts and synthesizes the features used for CYP, as well as a number of approaches created to examine agricultural yield prediction with artificial intelligence techniques. After experimenting with several strategies, Random Forest was found to be the best fit for this particular scenario providing a detailed analysis in terms of accuracy using the techniques.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Pipeline with CNN-Based Feature Embeddings and Dimensionality Reduction for Plant Disease Detection
authors: Sri Harsha Vardhan Chikkala, Triyek Reddy Tumma, Puppala Naga Sravanthi, Lohith Venkata Simhachalam Paila
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Plant leaf diseases, particularly potato diseases, are a major cause of concern for agricultural productivity and food security. Traditional methods of disease identification are time-consuming, subjective, and susceptible to human error. In recent times, attempts have been made to mitigate these drawbacks using deep learning techniques in particular CNNs, VGG networks, and hybrid models with ResNet and EfficientNet for real-time, autonomous disease detection. These models have reported very high accuracy, usually over 97%, in detecting diseases such as early blight, late blight. Methods like image preprocessing, augmentation, and transfer learning have greatly improved classification accuracy. Some of them also incorporate decision support systems for agricultural recommendations. Such advancements point to the promise of artificial intelligence for contemporary agriculture, which allows early detection, less crop loss, and environmentally friendly farming.

title: Architecting a Seamless Entry Protocol: An Automated License Plate Recognition Framework for University Campus Access
authors: Joseph A. Vistal, Cyril B. Amparo, Lezly C. Dagonio, Joanagrace M. Mejorada
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Campus access management at Caraga State University Cabadbaran Campus has traditionally relied on manual logbooks, a process prone to inefficiency, data loss, and security risks. Guards are tasked with interviewing vehicle drivers, recording license plate numbers by hand, and maintaining fragile logbooks that often result in errors, incomplete records, and difficulties in generating timely reports. To address these challenges, this study developed a "Seamless Entry Protocol Architecture for an Automated License Plate Recognition Framework for CSUCC Access". The system uses Optical Character Recognition (OCR) combined with TensorFlow to detect and recognize license plates automatically. The deep learning model based on Inception ResNetV2 architecture has been trained to detect and classify alphanumeric characters from vehicle license plates images for automated verification of registered and unregistered vehicles. To test the accuracy of the system, the images were taken using webcams and smartphone camera at different distance and angle. The result shows that webcam is more accurate in extracting plate number from the images compared to smartphone especially in controlled light and moderate distance. Although the recognition accuracy is low when the vehicle is far away and is still, it still shows an improvement when compared to manual methods. The result also shows that the automated system not only improves the accuracy and efficiency of monitoring vehicles, but also secures the campus by providing accurate records, real time verification and automated reporting. The system reduces human effort and improves the operation of the guard, and provides a basis for further development of intelligent campus access management systems.

title: Integrated Machine Learning for Real-Time Intelligent Collision Avoidance
authors: Naveen Rathee, Rajeev Kumar Chauhan, Jaishanker Prasad Keshari, Priyanka, Jain Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: During foggy seasons, accidents are a major problem and can result in serious injury or even death. To solve this problem, we are developing an intelligent vehicle system that predicts accidents in foggy conditions using machine learning. This system warns drivers in time and helps to avoid potential dangers. By analyzing factors such as vehicle speed, reaction time, line of sight, traffic flow and communication, we train the system to accurately detect and respond to threats. Our goal is to reduce accidents in foggy conditions and improve overall road safety. Traditional methods of detecting accidents on foggy roads are ineffective and increase the risks for drivers and passengers. To address this challenge, we propose a hybrid model that combines vehicle cameras and advanced machine learning techniques. By analyzing camera images and taking into account historical data about weather, traffic and other factors, our model accurately predicts future road conditions. Real experiments have proven the effectiveness of our approach. By adopting this hybrid model, we can significantly reduce accidents caused by poor visibility in foggy conditions, making our roads safer for smart cities

title: A Machine Learning Approach for Apple Leaf Disease Classification using GLCM Features
authors: Sarvachan Verma, Mona Kumari, Ajitesh Kumar, Rupa Rani, Jaishree Jain
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Classic Approaches of diagnosis are time consuming; A large number of farmers are unable to detect apple leaf diseases in their initial phase. This causes farmers to loss best opportunity for efficient protection and cure. Opportune and accurate Recognition of these diseases’ leftovers a notable challenge, with the principal target being the development of a strong and efficient image-based diagnostic tactic. In this study, apple leaf images were first polished using a Gaussian filter to minimize noise, followed to by segmentation to detached diseased regions. Texture features were then recovered by Gray Level Co-occurrence Matrix (GLCM) technique, which supplied as input for classifier using Decision Tree and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) algorithms. The outcomes Showed that the Decision Tree classifier excelled than KNN, reaching the supreme accuracy and proving its compatibility for three-class apple leaf disease classification.

title: An Automated Spatial Computing Method for Urban Public Transport Accessibility Based on Multi-Source Big Data Fusion and Supply—Demand Coupling
authors: Qingyun Gao, Shijun Lu, Yuxian Zhang, Zongyuan Li
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: Public transport accessibility is a key indicator of urban transportation performance, and advances in spatial analysis and multi-source data enable POI-based quantification of travel demand. Existing accessibility analysis techniques are often constrained by manual data preprocessing, coarse spatial discretization, and limited capability in handling high-dimensional supply-demand relationships, which restrict their scalability and analytical depth in complex urban environments. To address these limitations, this study develops an automated spatial computing approach that integrates multi-source data fusion, grid-based density modeling, and exhaustive supply-demand coupling computation, enabling refined accessibility measurement and efficient pattern extraction from large-scale urban transport data.

title: A Multi-Modal AI Framework for Real-Time Stress and Sleep Monitoring with Intelligent Interventions
authors: Sharon D, Iyyappan V, Prabhu Shankar B, Alex David S, Garima Jain, Rithik A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Growing instances of stress and sleep disorders in both students and remote workers demand smart and user-friendly mental health tools. This article suggests creating an AI-driven solution that will implement speech analysis, recognition of emotional facial expressions, and behavior patterns to determine the level of stress and propose proper interventions. The assistant uses Whisper in speech-to-text transcription, Deep Face in facial expression recognition, as well as TextBlob in determining the polarity of sentiments. When the stress is high, the system provides real-time warnings based on the Twilio SMS and plays some soothing music or message based on text-to-speech and Pygame. It also shows the long-term stress trend visualization at matplotlib on a Streamlit application. Neither cloud-based heavy APIs nor extensive hardware is required to use the tool that runs in real-time and uses only a microphone and webcam. The simulations of using the system experimentally reveal that it can respond to indicators of stress timely manner and without compromising the privacy of the user. The given solution is inexpensive, scalable, and efficient regarding the promotion of sleep hygiene and psychological well-being.

title: Compressed Sensing Techniques for High-Speed Biomedical Signal Acquisition
authors: Omprakash Gurrapu, J. Vijayasree, P. Sudhakar, Jami Venkata Suman, Egambergan Xudaynazarov, Mukhammad Khabibullaev
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Compressed sensing (CS) is a game-changing way to acquire and reconstruct biomedical signals at sampling rates significantly below the Nyquist rate to gain powerful reduction in data size, power, and hardware. In this work, we discuss CS methods for fast biomedical signals, including ECG, EEG, and MRI. We review the theory concerning CS as it relates to sparsity, incoherent sampling, and convex optimization methods. We also propose a new adaptive reconstruction method which combines structured sparsity with iterative thresholding, providing better reconstruction under noise and real-time requirements. Simulations and experimental results show that the proposed method achieves higher (up to 70%) diminution of the sample requirements with signal fidelity above 95% compared to other methods. Overall, we build a case for CS allowing for new portable, low-power biomedical devices that are capable of non-invasive real-time monitoring and diagnosis. We also identify and discuss challenges and future work including hardware implementation, and deep learning frameworks.

title: IoT-Enabled Solar Microgrid Monitoring and Management System
authors: Ambati Pravallika, Vipul Atluri, Nithish Chowdhary Cherukri, Arnesh Chauhan, Vishal Madireddy
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: Effective network management plays a key role in optimizing energy consumption through effective monitoring and planning. By integrating the Internet of Things (IoT), energy networks can be monitored and controlled more intelligently and precisely. The proposed IoT-enabled solar microgrid monitoring management system uses a network of sensors to collect real-time data from solar panels and batteries. The system evaluates the effectiveness of Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT), which was measured at 90%. It continuously monitors key parameters, including battery health, state of charge, and other system metrics. A machine learning algorithm is used to predict the SoC, enabling data-driven power management. Realtime Data and predictive information are displayed via local and remote interfaces, providing users with an intuitive way to manage available energy resources. This approach offers a practical, costeffective, reliable, and scalable solution for rural and energydeficient areas, demonstrating how the integration of IoT and machine learning can create smarter, more sustainable and reliable grid systems.

title: Fuel Economy Enhancement of Fuel Cell Electric Vehicles Using ANFIS-Based Energy Management
authors: Vinod Kumar Vishwakarma, Swapnil Srivastava, Venktesh Mishra
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: Improving the fuel economy of Fuel Cell Electric Vehicles (FCEVs) is essential not only for lowering hydrogen consumption but also for ensuring that the fuel cell stack operates reliably over its lifetime. In this study, an energy management strategy built on an Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS) is proposed to manage how power is shared between the fuel cell and the battery. The ANFIS controller combines learning-based behaviour with fuzzy logic, that allow it to handle the nonlinear and rapidly changing conditions which occur during real driving, more effectively than traditional control methods. Simulation results shows that the proposed approach gives higher fuel economy than a conventional Proportional Integral Derivative (PID) controller. This improvement arises from the better hydrogen utilization, smoother operation of the fuel cell, and fewer abrupt power fluctuations within the hybrid system. Overall, the findings show that intelligent control methods such as ANFIS can play a meaningful role in enhancing the efficiency and operational performance of future FCEV energy management systems.

title: A Lyapunov-Based Age of Information and Energy Consumption Optimization in D2D-Aided Social IoT Networks
authors: Saurabh Chandra, Rajeev Arya, Maheshwari Prasad Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: The Age of Information (AoI) has become a vital measure of real-time data transmission effectiveness in Device-toDevice (D2D)-supported Social Internet of Things (SIoT) networks, particularly in dynamic settings marked by interference, user movement, and limited energy resources. To address these limitations, this paper presents a novel two-stage optimization framework that combines subchannel allocation using a Generalized Knapsack Problem (GKP) with a Lyapunov-based dynamic scheduling policy for minimizing AoI and energy consumption. The proposed Lyapunov optimization-based AoI minimization (PLOA) algorithm jointly minimizes AoI and energy consumption under time-varying user positions and communication constraints. A Lyapunov drift-plus-penalty method is utilized to derive scheduling decisions that balance information freshness and energy consumption. Simulation results confirm that the proposed algorithm achieves superior performance over benchmark schemes, attaining up to 19.32% reduction in AoI, 46.86% improvement in throughput, and 40.93% reduction in energy consumption across various network scenarios, including different user densities, transmission power levels, and SINR thresholds. This work provides a scalable and adaptive solution for energy-aware information freshness in nextgeneration D2D-aided SIoT networks.

title: Location Prediction Empowered RIS Phase Optimization for ISAC System with High-mobility
authors: Yudong Li, Xueting Xu, Hongcheng Zhuang, Fan Jiang
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS) is a key technology for 6G communications. However, in high-mobility scenarios, traditional approaches based on statistical location information respond slowly, while those relying on instantaneous CSI are impractical due to excessive pilot overhead. To this end, we propose a location prediction empowerd RIS phase optimization (LP-RISPO) approach. Its core idea is to shift from passive “static optimization” to proactive “dynamic predictive optimization”. Specifically, it predicts the future motion state of user equipment (UE) based on real-time position, velocity, and uncertainty (covariance), and uses this prediction as prior knowledge to perform a one-time optimization of the RIS phase configuration. As a result, LP-RISPO significantly reduces pilot transmission and RIS phase reconfiguration frequency in high-mobility scenarios while improving the system’s average achievable rate, compared to benchmarks.

title: KDG-Rec: Enhanced Dual-GNN Programming Exercise Recommendation via LLM-Powered Knowledge Annotation and Preference-Decoupling
authors: Bangqi Li, Qing Sun, Tianyi Zhu, Ji Wu, Li Jing, Wenge Rong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: The rapid expansion of online programming exercise platforms has brought abundant learning resources for programming education, but also presents challenges for personalized exercise recommendation due to missing or imprecise knowledge annotations and the sparsity of learner-exercise interaction records, which together lead to reduced accuracy and a lack of explainability in the recommendation results. Existing natural language processing and large language models (LLM)-based annotation methods struggle to capture implicit knowledge and often generate redundant or inconsistent results. Moreover, mainstream recommendation systems are ineffective at handling the severe sparsity of interaction sequences in programming exercise datasets, and typically model student preferences in a single dimension—overlooking key educational factors such as knowledge gaps, difficulty tolerance, and preferred learning rhythm. To address these challenges, we propose KDG-Rec, a novel framework that first introduces AgentCo-KAS, a multiagent LLM-based collaborative annotation method with role-specific fine-tuning and cross-agent verification for high-precision, fine-grained knowledge annotation. Building on these enriched annotations, we develop a disentangled graph neural network model that constructs dual exercise-interaction graphs to effectively capture learning patterns at multiple granularities in interaction sequences and explicitly decouples student preferences into four interpretable dimensions for adaptive fusion. Extensive experiments on real-world datasets demonstrate that KDG-Rec outperforms nine state-of-the-art methods across multiple metrics, significantly advancing personalized programming exercise recommendation.

title: BMT_EEG: A Novel EEG Dataset for evaluating the effects of new protocols on biometric authentication systems
authors: A. Suri, M.A. Wani, S. Jabin, M. Khan, K.I.K. Sherwani, M. Sardar, M. Salman, A. Alomran
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Due to uniqueness, revocability, and resilience to theft, Electroencephalography (EEG) biometric solutions are becoming more popular for securing high stake systems. To propel research in EEG-based biometrics, we introduced a novel dataset: Biometric EEG Dataset (BMT_EEG), uniquely designed to address anti-spoof concerns of Biometric applications. The dataset captures neural responses of 23 users across three sessions, encompassing eleven protocols that include seven motor movement (MM) and motor imagery (MI) tasks, two visually evoked potentials (VEPs), and two resting-state conditions. Leveraging BMT_EEG, we employed EEGNet, a compact convolutional neural network designed for EEG analysis, and a baseline CNN-GRU model combining 1-D Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) for modeling spatio-temporal dependencies for user authentication. Both the models were evaluated under random and skilled forgery attacks in inter-session setting, using two sessions for training and the third for testing, enhancing robustness against session variability and its suitability for real-world deployment scenario. The CNN-GRU model achieved the best average Equal Error Rate (EER) of 1.29 ± 0.92% for random forgery using the MI2 task and 6.15 ± 3.78% for skilled forgery using the MI1 task. Notably, EEGNet, achieved better performance with an EER of 0.06±0.04% for random forgery using MI2, and 2.14±4.58% for skilled forgery using MI1. This work underscores the importance of realistic evaluation protocols, comprehensive EEG dataset, and suitability of deep learning architectures in advancing biometric research.

title: Direct vs. Score-based Selection: Understanding the Heisenberg Effect in Target Acquisition across Input Modalities in Virtual Reality
authors: Linjie Qiu, Duotun Wang, Boyu Li, Jiawei Li, Yulin Shen, Zeyu Wang, Mingming Fan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Target selection is a fundamental interaction in virtual reality (VR). But the act of confirming a selection, such as a button press or pinch, can disturb the tracked pose and shift the intended target, which is referred to as the Heisenberg Effect. Prior research has mainly investigated controller input. However, it remains unclear how the effect manifests in the bare-hand input and how score-based techniques may mitigate the effect in different spatial variations. To fill the gap, we conduct a within-subject study to examine the Heisenberg Effect across two input modalities (i.e., controller and hand) and two selection mechanisms (i.e., direct and score-based). Our results show that hand input is more susceptible to the Heisenberg Effect, with direct selection more influenced by target width and score-based selection more sensitive to target density. Based on previous vote-oriented technique and our temporal analysis, we introduce weighted VOTE, a history-based intention accuracy model for target voting, that reweights recent interaction intent to counteract input disturbances. Our evaluation shows the method improves selection accuracy compared to baseline techniques. Finally, we discuss future directions for adaptive selection methods.

title: Recall This Gesture? Investigating Gesture Memorability in Extended Reality
authors: Zahra Borhani, Ethan Holen, Adam Sinclair Williams, Mayra Donaji Barrera Machuca, Anil Ufuk Batmaz, Brendan Kelley, Xiaoyan Zhou, Matthew G. Rhodes, Francisco Raul Ortega
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Gesture interaction is standard in modern extended reality head-mounted displays, e.g., Microsoft Hololens 2 and Apple Vision Pro. These gestures come from multiple sources, such as elicitation studies, the application designer, or an expert. However, it is unclear if the source of the gesture affects the memorability of gestures (i.e., level of recall). In a user study, 35 participants used the HoloLens 2 to test three gestures set from different sources: 1) expert-defined, whereby we asked four experts to define gestures; 2) user-defined, whereby each participant defined their gestures; and 3) elicitation-defined, whereby we used gestures proposed by previous work. Our results showed that expert-defined gestures (72.73% recall) were more likely to be remembered than elicitation-defined (59.87% recall) gestures. Further, user-defined gestures (88.75% recall) were the most memorable. These data suggest that the source of gestures has a substantial impact on their memorability.

title: High Fidelity Digital Twin Channel Modeling for RIS-Assisted Wireless Communication Systems
authors: Yin Fang, Shu Xu, Chao Fang, Shiwen He, Chunguo Li, Yongming Huang, Luxi Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) plays an essential role in alleviating severe path loss in millimeter wave communication systems. Its performance hinges on the precise modeling of high-dimensional cascaded channels. However, traditional modeling approaches require extensive experience in radio propagation, resulting in complex and inefficient processes. To overcome these limitations, we transform the RIS channel modeling into a channel distribution transport mapping problem and introduce a generative model based on rectified flow. Our approach integrates distance information into a diffusion transformer (DiT) architecture through cross-attention mechanisms, resulting in a conditional DiT capable of synthesizing target channels from distance inputs. We further optimize the rectified flow into a single-step generator via reflow techniques. Building on this framework, we design a generative digital twin (DT) channel model that serves as a high fidelity data generator for downstream tasks. The proposed model acts as a virtual replica of the propagation environment, enabling efficient channel data synthesis for training communication algorithms such as channel state information feedback and channel estimation. Simulation results show that our approach generates channels with minimal distribution discrepancy compared to real channels (a maximum mean discrepancy < 0.01), outperforming existing generative methods. Furthermore, the reflow-driven DT channel model achieves the shortest generation time among all evaluated benchmarks.

title: Artificial Intelligence as a Catalyst for Sustainable Innovation in Oral and Maxillofacial Radiology-Review
authors: R. V. Deepikaa Shri, N. Aravindha Babu, R. Kishore Kanna, R. Anitha, S. Raju, K. Sundaramoorthy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Oral and maxillofacial radiology (OMR) is an important part of figuring out what’s wrong with teeth. It uses different kinds of pictures to give patients a lot of care. But the heavy use of advanced imaging technologies makes health services much worse for the environment by using more energy, making more waste, and exposing people to radiation. AI becomes an important tool for a more durable future in area by maximizing resource use, reducing environmental impact and promoting proper access to high quality care. In addition to discussing important issues such as privacy, algorithm bias and human inspection and strong regulations, analysis examines, especially in AI applications and ecological, economic and social domains, many of their benefits. The feeling of durable, fair and patient-focused oral health care finally depends on the integration of AI, which is run by responsible innovation and interdisciplinary collaboration.

title: Distance Graduate Education in Uruguay: Regulatory dilemmas
authors: Mabel Dávila, Adriana Chiancone, Enrique Martínez Larrecheah
publish: eLearn
abstract: None

title: Cyber-Physical System Design For IoT-Enabled Smart Walkers For Assisted Mobility
authors: B Arjunan, Kagitala Lakshmi Ramani Reddy, S.T. Thunhaal, Pranav A Kamath, Piyush Pratap Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Assistive mobility tools are gradually undergoing an evolutionary process to become Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) that integrate perception, computation, and action for the safety of the user. This paper proposes an Internet of Things (IoT)-enabled smart walker that uses LiDAR-based Simultaneous Location And Mapping (SLAM) and safety-aware motion arbitration based on multi-sensor fusion and obstacle detection. This IoT-enabled smart walker integrates indoor mapping and obstacle detection achieved by the 360-degree LiDAR sensor, as well as ultrasonic, infrared, and inertial sensors. These various modes are fused for state estimation and obstacle identification, which are carried out by the Extended Kalman Filter. The computation component of the smart walker uses the Raspberry Pi, which processes the control component running at 10 Hz. An IoT component supported by the cloud provides for the monitoring of the caregiver, alerting for fall detection, and location information. Results show an accuracy of 4–7 cm for the 3-D maps, an average obstacle detection of 94.3%, and latency performance of less than 110 ms.

title: Water Quality Monitoring and Purification using Banana Fiber Extract
authors: M. Saranya, R. Kiruba, P. Veeramani, M. Shresthi, K. Srinithi, S. Vaishnavi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The main goal of this paper is to develop natural filtration method for providing clean drinking water that is economical and cost effective. The developed model reduces the higher-end cost of available water purifiers by serving as an inexpensive natural water purifier for rural communities. The banana fiber extracted from the banana plant serves as a natural filtration medium with its intrinsic antimicrobial and adsorption properties. The project is developed using ESP32 microcontroller and turbidity sensor to test the purity of water at different levels. Turbidity tests are conducted before and after filtration for efficient particle removal. The ESP32 allows wireless transmission for real-time monitoring in addition to local data processing and storage. The LM2596 buck converter offers a stable and effective solution for all the voltage regulations. The design focuses on developing an integrated embedded system using various sensing methods and communication hardware, together with natural filtration medium for water purification.

title: An Actor-Critic Deep Reinforcement Learning Approach for Dynamic Resource Allocation in Containerized Environments
authors: Karthikeyan S, Manoj Kumar S, Abhilash S Nath
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Dynamic resource allocation is a critical challenge in modern cloud and edge computing environments, particularly for containerized applications demanding low latency and high throughput. Traditional heuristic schedulers struggle to adapt to volatile, unpredictable workload fluctuations. This paper proposes a solution utilizing Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL), specifically the Advantage Actor-Critic (A2C) algorithm, to learn an optimal scheduling policy. We formulate the resource allocation problem as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) in a custom-built simulator. Our DRL agent observes real-time node utilization and job requirements and executes actions to minimize average job latency while maintaining load balance across multiple compute nodes. Comparative simulation results demonstrate that the proposed A2C-DRL scheduler significantly outperforms conventional First-Come-First-Served (FCFS) and Least Utilized Node (LUN) heuristics in terms of job turnaround time and resource utilization standard deviation, achieving 54% latency reduction and 47% improvement in load balance metrics.

title: MetaGMS-ReID: A Camera-Invariant Meta-Learning Framework with Generalized Margin-Based Softmax Loss for Single-Camera-Training Person Re-Identification
authors: Harish Aggarwal, Justin Gopinath A, Apurv Anand
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Person re-identification (re-ID) is a key component in modern computer vision systems deployed for public safety and large-scale surveillance. The problem becomes substantially harder in the Single-Camera-Training (SCT) setting, where each identity is observed from only one camera during training. Under this constraint, learning features that can generalize across cameras with different viewpoints, illumination patterns, and imaging styles is extremely challenging. Many existing methods implicitly assume reliable correspondences of identities across cameras, an assumption that is often unrealistic in practical deployments. In this work, we present MetaGMS-ReID, a framework that addresses SCT re-ID from two mutually reinforcing directions. First, we employ a camera-invariant meta-learning paradigm that mimics cross-camera transfer at training time. Each batch is divided into meta-train and meta-test subsets according to camera IDs, encouraging the model to downweight camera-specific artifacts and instead focus on identity-related cues. Second, we integrate a Generalized Margin-based Softmax (GMS) loss derived from recent automated loss-design research. This loss introduces both angular and feature-level margins, yielding more compact within-class clusters and clearer separation between identities in the embedding space. Experiments on the Market1501 benchmark show that the joint use of camera-invariant meta-learning and the GMS loss significantly boosts performance over strong baselines, demonstrating the effectiveness of our unified MetaGMS-ReID framework.

title: Puma Optimization Based Polar Fuzzy Regulator for Restructured Agc System
authors: Ram Naresh Mishra, Narendra Kumar, Shiva Mishra, D. K. Chaturvedi
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: Efficient operation of a fuzzy-logic controller depends on the quality and compactness of its rule base. However, a large number of fuzzy rules often increases computational cost and memory usage. To overcome this limitation, this paper presents a Polar Fuzzy Regulator (PFR) optimized through the Puma Optimization (PO) algorithm for Automatic Generation Control (AGC) in a restructured power system comprising thermal and gas units. The proposed controller employs a generalized bell-shaped (gbell) membership function on the input side and explores multiple output membership functions, including gbell, psig, triangular, gauss 2 mf, and trapezoidal. The PO algorithm is used to determine optimal PFR parameters that minimize the Integral of Time multiplied Absolute Error (ITAE) performance index. Simulation results show that the PO-based PFR achieves faster frequency recovery and smoother tie-line power restoration than conventional genetic algorithm-based tuning methods. A sensitivity analysis with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{\pm 2 0 \%}$</tex> parameter variation validates the robustness and adaptability of the proposed scheme in diverse operating conditions.

title: Committees
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)

title: Deep Learning Techniques for User Authentication in Medical IoT Applications
authors: Lithick Sanjaiyan S, Mohammed Ansar Ali M A, Kanagaviswesh G, Sugansingh Kumaran K S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The rapid integration of the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) into healthcare systems has introduced new challenges in secure, contactless, and real-time user authentication. Conventional authentication mechanisms such as passwords and access cards are inefficient in medical environments where hygiene, speed, and hands-free interaction are critical. Face recognition has emerged as a viable alternative; however, recognition performance under unconstrained environments remains a challenge due to pose variation, illumination changes, occlusions, and background clutter. This work presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of five deep learning and hybrid face recognition models evaluated as an authentication framework for IoMT devices. Experiments conducted on the Labeled Faces in the Wild (LFW) dataset demonstrate that metric learning approaches significantly improve discriminative embedding quality. The ArcFace backbone improves authentication accuracy from 39.26% to 76.44%, while ArcFace embeddings combined with SVM achieve the highest accuracy of 78.06%. Transformer-based fusion underperforms due to limited dataset scale. The study highlights that embedding geometry and margin-based optimization are more critical than architectural complexity for reliable IoMT user authentication.

title: Software-in-the-Loop Validation of a PEC9-Based Multifunction Solar Active Filter
authors: Soufian Khettab, Amel Kasri, Youcef Belkhier, Aissa Kheldoun, Mohamed Benbouzid
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
