abstract: This paper presents a novel double-stage, singlephase photovoltaic (PV) system for grid connection and active power filtering, employing a Packed E-Cell nine-level (PEC9) inverter. The proposed architecture offers an integrated solution for harmonic mitigation, reactive power compensation, and maximum power extraction from the PV source, while ensuring efficient real power injection into the grid. A modified instantaneous power (P-Q) algorithm is developed to enhance dynamic performance by generating a reference current that combines both harmonic and solar power components. This reference current is accurately tracked using Model Predictive Control (MPC) strategy applied to the PEC9 inverter, ensuring precise and fast control. The system's performance is validated through software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation using RT-LAB, demonstrating its effectiveness under realistic operational conditions. The results confirm significant improvements in total harmonic distortion (THD) reduction, unity power factor operation, maximum power tracking, and overall dynamic response. These findings highlight the potential of the proposed PEC9-based multifunction solar active filter as an efficient and reliable solution for enhancing power quality in grid-connected PV systems.

title: Influence of AI-Based CME Platforms on Brand Engagement and Loyalty of Diabetologists: An Empirical Study
authors: Waseem Shaikh, Sajid Alvi, Zoya Shaikh
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)
abstract: Rapidly integrating Artificial Intelligence (AI) into Continuing Medical Education (CME) platforms have transformed the learning experience of healthcare professionals. This study empirically investigated the influence of AI-based CME platforms on brand management and loyalty of diabetologists. Emerging trends and recent developments in digital health technologies, specifically CME with embedded artificial intelligence (AI), provide a significant opportunity for educating specialists on achieving better efficiency in diabetes care, which may reduce the rising diabetes-related health-care expenditures. The CMEs that cover Artificial Intelligence (AI) integrated into mobile health applications (apps) present a promising avenue for enhancing diabetes management through personalized patient education and behaviour modification. Healthcare providers can use AI to develop a patient-centered strategy and improve diabetes care by working with researchers and government. The result highlights the strategic significance of leveraging Artificial Intelligence technologies in CME for building strong emotional and professional connections between medical professionals, patients and educational brands.

title: Comparative Study of Authentication Schemes in Vehicular Ad-Hoc Networks: Security, Privacy, and Future Research Directions
authors: Priya Kumari Gupta, Sonika Bhardwaj, Ramesh Saha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Vehicular Ad-hoc Network (VANET) is a recent emerging technology that creates the mobile network using cars in place of nodes. It improves the safety of vehicles and provides comfort to drivers and passengers in times of congestion. Due to the mobile nature and rapid change in network topology, it suffers from various types of attacks like denial of service, location tracking, impersonation, replay attacks, eavesdropping, block holes, session hijacking, and many more. To solve these security-related problems, various types of schemes have been proposed. This paper presents the comparative study of various accessibility schemes that are propose for VANETs in order to establish secure communication. These schemes are discussed in detail, and then a comparison based on their advantage, disadvantage, security, capabilities, and features has been carried out. The detailed analyses of these schemes reveal new research fields and opportunities to improve the quality of services for users.

title: Enhancing Outpatient Care Using an LLM-Based Model for Clinical Ward Disposition
authors: Nancy Jane Y, Kingslin Issac R, Vinishaa K R, Vidhya Bharathi S, Ramya D B
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Enhancing outpatient care in the Emergency Department (ED) requires optimized patient flow to reduce waiting times and improve hospital resource utilization. This work presents a Clinical Ward Disposition (CWD) system designed to optimize patient flow and support admission decisions following initial nursing assessments. The proposed system uses a hybrid CNN–BiLSTM model for optical character recognition (OCR) to digitize handwritten admission notes, achieving an accuracy of 86.57%. The extracted clinical information is analyzed using ClinicalBERT and XLNet models to determine the most appropriate ward for patient admission, achieving a disposition accuracy of 90.44%. The proposed system improves patient care by automating note digitization, extracting clinical data, and supporting efficient ward disposition decisions.

title: IPRights-Net: A Hybrid CNN-LSTM Model for Assessing Human Rights Violations in Intellectual Property Conflicts
authors: H.S.Adithya, E.Venugopal, S.K.Kamalakhannan
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: As the amount of historic data on legal cases within the legal domain grows, there is increasing motivation for domain experts to gather and structure this information for analysis and prediction in order to predict court rulings on IP (Intellectual Property) violations. However, predicting and explaining such decisions based on judicial facts is still a difficult task. Some unanticipated findings have been reported in previous attempts to predict court decisions using small experimental datasets through the use of machine learning (ML) models and traditional method of encoding categorical features. To enhance the accuracy of IP violation rate prediction with a historical legal data, this study presents a novel hybrid neural network model which combines an LSTM network structure with a convolutional neural network (CNN) network structure, named IPRights-NET. The model is designed to focus attention to only that few features that rank highest in the given dataset. It was followed by training the LSTM with CNN combined model to predict the violation outcomes. This composite model performed robustly compared to previous similar studies with the accuracy of 92.25%, precision of 93.45%, recall of 94.12%, F1-score of 92.98%.

title: Physics-Informed Spectral Modeling for Hyperspectral Imaging
authors: Zuzanna Gawrysiak, Krzysztof Krawiec
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: We present PhISM, a physics-informed deep learning architecture that learns without supervision to explicitly disentangle hyperspectral observations and model them with continuous basis functions. PhISM outperforms previous methods on several classification and regression benchmarks, requires limited labeled data, and provides additional insights thanks to its interpretable latent representation.

title: SemSteDiff: Generative Diffusion Model-based Coverless Semantic Steganography Communication
authors: Song Gao, Rui Meng, Xiaodong Xu, Haixiao Gao, Yiming Liu, Chenyuan Feng, Ping Zhang, Tony Q. S. Quek, Dusit Niyato
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Semantic communication (SemCom), as a novel paradigm for future communication systems, has recently attracted much attention due to its superiority in communication efficiency. However, similar to traditional communication, it also suffers from eavesdropping threats. Intelligent eavesdroppers could launch advanced semantic analysis techniques to infer secret semantic information. Therefore, some researchers have designed Semantic Steganography Communication (SemSteCom) schemes to confuse semantic eavesdroppers. However, the state-of-the-art SemSteCom schemes for image transmission rely on the pre-selected cover image, which limits the generalization. To address this issue, we propose a Generative Diffusion Model-based Coverless Semantic Steganography Communication (SemSteDiff) scheme to hide secret images into generated stego images. The semantic related private and public keys enable legitimate receiver to decode secret images correctly while the eavesdropper without the completely correct key-pairs fail to obtain them. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the plug-and-play design in different Joint Source-Channel Coding (JSCC) frameworks. Results under different eavesdropping settings show that, when Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) = 0 dB, the peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) of the legitimate receiver is 4.14 dB higher than that of the eavesdropper.

title: Decoupled Observer for Health State of Aircraft Power Generation System: A Physically Guided Differentiable Approach
authors: Haoran Du, Yongzhi Liu, Tianxing Li, Peirong Zhu, Guodong Niu, Zihan Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: State identification for complex systems under missing key information is a research challenge in industrial applications. Focusing on the aircraft power generation system, this work aims to decouple healthy state from incipient fault state under strong interference. Traditional analysis methods based on spectrum and energy fail in this scenario due to their inability to decouple the load factor from the state features. To bridge this gap, this paper proposes a physically-guided differentiable decoupling observer. The observer transforms system's physical laws into hard constraints and employs neural networks with fixed parameters to construct nonlinear relational constraints. This formulation is solved within a differentiable programming framework, effectively addressing the mixed-constraint optimization problem. Given the inaccessibility of key rotor-side signals, a system-level semi-physical cross-linking test platform for the system was established for experimental validation. The observer successfully achieves clear state decoupling and high-accuracy identification, as validated on samples. The decoupled state space exhibits a distinct identification safety margin, alongside excellent robustness to load factor and strong engineering interpretability. The core differentiable paradigm of “physical hard constraints + neural network nonlinear constraints” also presents a transferable theoretical framework for the state observation of similar complex systems.

title: LeLaR: The First In-Orbit Demonstration of an AI-Based Satellite Attitude Controller
authors: Kirill Djebko, Tom Baumann, Erik Dilger, Frank Puppe, Sergio Montenegro
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Attitude control is essential for many satellite missions. Classical controllers, however, are time-consuming to design and sensitive to model uncertainties and variations in operational boundary conditions. Deep reinforcement learning offers a promising alternative by learning adaptive control strategies through autonomous interaction with a simulation environment. Overcoming the simulation-to-reality gap, which involves deploying an agent trained in simulation onto the real physical satellite, remains a significant challenge. In this work, we present the first successful in-orbit demonstration of an artificial intelligence-based attitude controller for inertial pointing maneuvers overcoming the simulation-to-reality gap. The controller was trained entirely in simulation and deployed to the InnoCube three-unit nanosatellite, which was developed by the Julius-Maximilians-Universität Würzburg in cooperation with the Technische Universität Berlin, and launched in January 2025. We present the artificial intelligence agent design, the training methodology, and a characterization of the discrepancies between the simulation environment and the in-orbit behavior of the satellite. To provide a reference, we evaluate the in-orbit performance of the artificial intelligence-based controller against both simulation results and the in-orbit performance of InnoCube’s default proportional-derivative controller under identical mission conditions. Steady-state metrics confirm the robust performance of the artificial intelligence-based controller during repeated in-orbit maneuvers.

title: Robotic Surgery Systems for Neurosurgical Applications Using AI
authors: Debabrata Sahana, Monali Gulhane, Fazil Sheikh, Mario Antony, Garima Shukla, Aarthi Sivasankaran
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) into robotic surgery systems is changing neurosurgery in a big way, with new ways to be more precise, work faster, and help patients do better. In neurosurgery, which requires a lot of complicated and delicate processes, AI-powered robotic systems can be very helpful. These systems can help the surgeon make better decisions in real time, navigate more accurately, and make mistakes less likely. Robotic systems that are controlled by AI, like robotic arms and smart imaging technologies, make it possible to do high-resolution imaging, 3D mapping, and prediction analytics. This makes it possible to do extra correct remedies in the mind, backbone, and tumors. While AI is blended with robot structures, it makes it easier to address statistics all through surgery, examine on the fly, and make changes based on the affected person's state. Additionally, these gadgets can make minimally invasive remedies possible that may shorten the time it takes to heal and enhance consequences after surgical procedure. AI can help discover crucial structures, guide the robotic in real time, and locate the fine surgical operation paths via the use of deep gaining knowledge of strategies. While robots and AI paintings together, they make neurosurgery more specific, much less probable to head incorrect, and more assured within the surgeons. There are technical features, rewards, and difficulties to growing and using AI-incorporated robot surgical operation systems in neurosurgical settings. This study talks about them. It also shows future trends and possible changes in this area that is changing very quickly. Finally, robotic surgery systems with AI could completely change neurosurgical care. They could make treatments safer and more effective, which would eventually improve the results for patients in the field of neurosurgery.

title: Addressing Non-IID Challenges in Federated Learning with Transfer Learning
authors: Rakshavi Dessai, Pravati Swain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) trains a global model while keeping user data on local devices. This local data creates statistical heterogeneity. Statistical heterogeneity implies that the data distribution across clients is non-Independent and Identically Distributed (non-IID). The non-IID data hamper model training efficiency and model accuracy in federated learning. The factors that contribute to non-IID and their effect on federated learning are analyzed. To enhance the efficiency of federated learning in the non-IID nature, the VGG based Feature Extraction method (V-FEM) is proposed. The V-FEM uses Transfer Learning on VGG to extract features and modifies fully connected layers of the VGG model. Then, ensemble learning with probability of classification was used for prediction. In addition, the implementation techniques and results achieved are discussed in detail. The proposed method improves the model efficiency by using transfer learning and the ensemble method in federated learning on non-IID data. By modifying transfer learning and replacing fully connected layers, our model increases the performance of image classification.

title: Enhanced Gender Recognition in Telugu Speech Using Augmented Transgender Audio
authors: Gangabattula Durga Sai Pavan, Kalal Pavan Teja, Karella Udayram, Paluru Pavan Kumar, Madasamy Anand Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive approach to gender classification in Telugu audio using Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC) and pitch features. This work fills a critical gap in inclusivity within speech-based systems by expanding the problem to include transgender voices, building on earlier research that mainly concentrated on binary (male/female) classification. The proposed system has two primary objectives: robust preprocessing and feature extraction, and multi-model classification for gender prediction. Each audio sample was processed to extract MFCCs and pitch from a carefully selected and balanced dataset of male, female, and transgender voices that was standardized to 16 kHz. Five popular machine learning models—Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), and XGBoost—were trained and evaluated. Data augmentation techniques, including minor pitch shifting and noise addition, were applied to improve model generalization, only for limited transgender samples to reduce data imbalance. The results show that the integration of the MFCC and pitch features with augmentation significantly improved performance, achieving a total accuracy of over 97% in both both SVM and MLP models. This study underscores the effectiveness of feature fusion and data balancing in multi-class gender classification and promotes inclusivity in modern voice recognition systems.

title: VaaniVerse: Low-Latency Speech Translation Using Universal Earphones
authors: Kalpitha S Naik, Megha Prasad, Ajina A, Rajatha Rajatha, Khushi D K
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: In multilingual societies like India, language barriers are a major obstacle to effective communication. Due to their reliance on internet connectivity, high cost, poor performance in noisy environments and high latency, existing solutions frequently fail. This paper introduces VaaniVerse, a real-time, multilingual speech translation system with low latency that can be operated with smartphones and standard earphones. The system also integrates an efficient pipeline for speech-to-text (STT), text-to-speech (TTS) synthesis noise cancellation and context-free translation. VaaniVerse provides accurate and adaptable translations between Hindi, English and Kannada even in noisy environment using lightweight edge AI models like Whisper, Coqui TTS and IndicTrans2. Given that the architecture is optimized for offline use, accessibility and affordability are ensured. The system also futher incorporates several GUI-based novelties that enhances usability and distinguishes it from existing translation applications.

title: Automatic Detection of Cyberbullying Behaviour on Social Media Using Hybrid Transformers and Deep Learning Models
authors: Namrata Namrata, Javed Wasim, Varun Malik
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The exponential growth of social media has transformed communication, but it has also facilitated the spread of cyberbullying—an aggressive and harmful behavior that causes severe psychological and emotional distress to individuals, often leading to anxiety, depression, and even suicidal tendencies. Despite numerous attempts using traditional machine learning approaches, accurate and real-time detection of cyberbullying remains a major challenge due to informal language, contextual ambiguity, multilingual content, and rapidly evolving online discourse. To address these challenges, the proposed framework introduces an automatic detection system for cyberbullying behavior on social media by integrating hybrid transformer architectures and deep learning models. The framework employs advanced transformer-based encoders, including m-BERT, BanglaBERT, and XLM-RoBERTa, for contextual feature extraction across multilingual text representations, capturing nuanced semantics and linguistic patterns associated with cyberbullying. These extracted features are processed using a stochastic gradient dynamic recurrent neural network (SGD-RNN), which enhances sequential dependency learning and improves classification robustness. Evaluation on the Kaggle Twitter dataset (39,870 tweets, five categories) shows the proposed BanglaBERT + SGD-RNN framework achieving 98.4% accuracy, 97.9% precision, 98.2% recall, and 98.0% F-measure. It outperforms traditional models by 3–5% and advanced baselines by 0.52–1.15%, offering a scalable, high-accuracy solution for multilingual cyberbullying detection and safer online communities.

title: Mitigating Bias in Brain Stroke Prediction Using Explainable Artificial Intelligence
authors: Swarnima, Mala Saraswat, Divya Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: A stroke features a problem that needs to be diagnosed and treated right away since it can have major repercussions for the patient if it is not addressed. Carers must enhance patient management by systematically mining and conserving medical data about the patient because technologies and medical diagnosis are growing more and more synergistic. As a result, it is crucial to investigate the relationships between these risk factors in patient medical records and comprehend how each one influences the likelihood of stroke We employed XAI techniques to look at the choice more thoroughly and exaggerate a feature for better performance. We also demonstrated how the oversampling increases the model’s accuracy. Furthermore, we have been able to demonstrate the imbalance in the dataset and increase the accuracy of our predictions. We employed four assessment metrics—recall, precision, accuracy, and f1 score—to demonstrate and XAI as a way in order to enhance model capabilities and give practitioners a degree of dependability. This f1 score for the second model was 81.78%. In order to examine the model’s decision-making procedure, we employed explainable techniques like Shapely Additive exPlanation (SHAP) and LIME. As a result, we investigated and oversampled a particular feature to obtain an innovative f1 score for 83.34%. The stroke information was significant smaller than the non-stroke dataset, and the initially collected data carried a f1 score of 0% because it was unbalanced. In order to better investigate a model’s decision-making process, we suggest explainable AI as an approach.

title: Duck Curve Characterization, Challenges and Mitigation Techniques: An Extensive Analysis
authors: Dheerendra Kumar Singh, Sanjay Kumar Maurya, Sachin Goyal
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: The growing application of solar energy is quite important. Changing the duck curve or the power system net load profile. Duck curve is used to illustrate the operating difficulties caused by low demand before daylight and an increase in demand in the evening, emphasizing the importance of flexibility, resource allocation, and dependability. The present post explains in great detail the duck curve, along with its interpretation and the difficulties it presents. presents the ways to map its severity and measures to counteract its impacts. We have examined the technologies of the flexible closely. generation, power storage, demand response, probabilistic modeling, and assessment of flexibility. This analysis provides recommendations for future directions in terms of reliability and sustainable grid integration, while also highlighting research gaps.

authors: N/A

title: A 5.8-GHz GaN High-Power Rectifier with Harmonic-Compression Network for Space-Based Wireless Power Transmission
authors: Xiaochen Yu, Haoran Wang, Ta-Jen Yen, Xiantao Yang, Shawn S. H. Hsu, Furong Yang, Jinyao Zhang, Yejun He, Chaoyun Song, Yi Huang, Jiafeng Zhou
publish: IEEE Journal on Wireless Power Technologies
abstract: This paper presents the design, fabrication and xperimental validation of a high-power and high-efficiency 5.8 GHz voltage-doubling rectifier for wireless power transfer (WPT) in space-based solar power (SBSP) systems. The proposed rectifier leverages a custom-developed GaN-on-SiC Schottky barrier diode (SBD) and a harmonic compression circuit topology to simultaneously achieve watt-level power handling and superior efficiency. The proposed GaN SBD features a Y-shaped anode geometry, yielding a superior breakdown voltage exceeding 200 V, an ultra-low zero-bias junction capacitance of 0.34 pF, a threshold voltage of 1.25 V and a series resistance of 11 Ω (specific on-resistance R<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</sub>,<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sp</sub> = 3.87 Ω·mm) and maintains stable performance at elevated temperatures up to 400 K. These characteristics facilitate efficient rectification under high input power conditions. At the circuit level, two one-eighth-wavelength microstrip transmission lines form a harmonic compression network that provides a DC path, in-band reactive compensation and second-harmonic suppression, thereby eliminating the requirement for conventional LC filters. Experimental results demonstrated a peak RF-to-DC conversion efficiency of 73.3% at an input power level of 40 dBm. These results verify that co-optimizing the GaN device and the circuit topology enables scalable and efficient microwave power conversion for high-power SBSP WPT systems.

title: Design Methods and Characteristics of Active EMI Choke Using a Customized IC for Real 11-kW On-Board Charger
authors: Jeongbin Kwon, Sangyeong Jeong, Heejun Jung, Dongil Shin, Hyunwoo Seo, Jingook Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electromagnetic Compatibility
abstract: This article presents a design methodology for an active electromagnetic interference (EMI) choke that enhances common-mode (CM) impedance through complex impedance modeling and loop-gain stability analysis. The feedback impedance is designed based on the measured impedance of the original CM choke and the EMI filter and is optimized to achieve both impedance boosting and stability across the target frequency range. A customized integrated circuit was developed to realize this function, enabling compact implementation within an 18 mm × 20 mm printed circuit board and supporting cost-effective mass production. Experimental validation using an 11-kW on-board charger demonstrated that the proposed active EMI choke increases CM impedance by more than five times near 300 kHz. Conducted emission tests confirmed that a two-stage EMI filter incorporating the active choke achieves comparable or improved suppression performance relative to a three-stage passive filter while reducing the overall weight by 38%. The proposed method was also applied to various magnetic core materials, with nanocrystalline cores providing the most effective impedance enhancement for broadband EMI suppression.

title: Large Language Model-Driven Framework for Dynamic Stakeholder-Associated issues Analysis in Mega Infrastructure Projects_supp1-3670398.docx
authors: Qiping SHEN
publish: N/A
abstract: The complexity and dynamic nature of megaprojects necessitate effective stakeholder engagement strategies to ensure the successful achievement of project objectives. However, the lack of an effective framework to uncover interactions and trends between stakeholders and associated issues within the vast, encapsulated project data is a key factor hindering dynamic stakeholder engagement. Traditional methods are constrained by time-consuming, limited semantic interpretability, accuracy issues, and weak reasoning capabilities, making them insufficient for addressing dynamic stakeholder engagement challenges. To address this gap, this research introduced an integrated LLM-TOT (Large Language Model-Topic over Time) dynamic analysis method that enables the automated identification of stakeholders and the analysis of their interactions with relevant issues over time, prioritizing stakeholders directly linked to relevant issues at each project phase. Dynamic Stakeholder Engagement Strategies Framework was proposed to provide efficient and adaptive stakeholder engagement strategies for mega infrastructure projects (MIPs). Using a 14-year case study of the Hong Kong International Airport Third Runway System (HKIA 3RS) project, this study validated the proposed framework by analyzing 1,323 official project documents. The analysis identified 16 critical stakeholder-associated issues, 43 key stakeholders, and their relevance, which were systematically scored. Two validation experiments were conducted to rigorously evaluate the accuracy and reliability of the results derived from the LLM-TOT dynamic analysis method. Based on these findings, a stakeholder engagement framework tailored to the HKIA 3RS was developed and verified against real-world practices, offering strategies for phase-specific, issue-level, and dynamic engagement. This study introduced an integrated LLM-TOT dynamic analysis method as an innovative application that connects a standard TOT model with a commercially available LLM to analyze stakeholder-issue dynamics in MIPs. In addition, it presented a Dynamic Stakeholder Engagement Framework that equips decision-makers with targeted, systematic, and actionable strategies to address the “how-to” challenges of dynamic stakeholder engagement throughout the lifecycle of MIPs.

title: Active Gate Drivers for SiC MOSFETs: Circuitry, Control, and Future Outlook
authors: Nart Gashi, Chengmin Li, Yoganandam Vivekanandham Pushpalatha, Georgios Papafotiou, Dimosthenis Peftitsis
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: The development of silicon carbide (SiC) MOSFETs has enabled the design of power electronic converters with significantly higher efficiency, power density, and maximum allowed operating temperatures. Gate driver circuits are essential for fully exploiting the superior electrothermal capabilities of SiC MOSFETs. Conventional gate drivers rely on fixed designs that limit performance improvements under varying load conditions—conditions commonly encountered in emerging applications, such as renewable energy systems and electric vehicles (EVs). In contrast, active gate drivers (AGDs) allow s dynamic adjustment of switching and conduction behavior to meet specific performance objectives. However, effective AGD control requires computationally demanding algorithms operating in real time. This article presents an overview of key AGDs designs and compares three prominent real-time control approaches for AGDs: model-based, partial-model-based, and model-free. All three approaches have been experimentally validated, and key results are presented and analyzed. Future perspectives of AGDs design and operation are also discussed.

title: Micro-collimated EFPI Hybrid Sensor for Long-travel Displacement and Axial Vibration Measurements
authors: Yizheng Chen, Yan Tang, Biyao Shi, Cheng Zhong, Zewei Wu, Yang Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: High-precision fiber-optic interferometry faces a persistent trade-off between measurement range, alignment stability, and sensor compactness. This paper reports a micro-collimated extrinsic Fabry-Perot interferometer (EFPI) designed to bridge the gap between macro-scale displacement and nanometer-level vibration metrology. The sensor head utilizes a monolithic quarter-pitch GRIN fiber collimator coupled with a coaxial spring-loaded guided probe. This mechano-optical architecture overcomes the beam divergence and lateral misalignment constraints of conventional long-stroke sensors, preserving high fringe visibility across the full 20 mm travel. To address signal fading caused by large cavity-length variations, a reference-assisted phase-generated carrier demodulation scheme is implemented to normalize residual amplitude modulation and stabilize phase tracking. Rigorous metrological characterization demonstrates a static displacement linearity of R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> > 0.9999 with a maximum residual error of 2.5 μm. The sensor achieves a displacement repeatability better than 510 nm (1σ), a noise spectral density of 4.85 pm/√Hz, and a limit of detection of 1.5 nm. Furthermore, the probe simultaneously functions as an accelerometer, exhibiting a flat frequency response from 5–200 Hz with high cross-axis rejection. By integrating robust guided mechanics with adaptive interrogation, this work realizes a compact, dual-modality instrument suitable for in-situ structural monitoring.

title: A Weighted Hybrid Strategy for Fast and Accurate 2D Computation of Transformer Short-Circuit Electromagnetic Forces
authors: Dengfeng Zhu, She Chen, Jun Liu, Hang Guo, Yudi Wu, Qianwang Wang, Lipeng Zhong, Qiuqin Sun, Feng Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Under short-circuit impulses, power-transformer windings can undergo deformation, instability, and insulation failure, threatening grid reliability. Accurate and efficient computation of short-circuit electromagnetic forces is essential to elucidate winding-deformation mechanisms and optimize insulation designs. This work investigates a three-phase core-type transformer by comparing axisymmetric two-dimensional open-core (2D-open) and closed-core (2D-close) models with a full threedimensional (3D) model under equivalent boundary conditions. We systematically assess the effects of mesh resolution and equivalent boundary conditions on the computed fields and forces. The error analysis highlights two key modeling requirements: end insulation must be excluded from the excitation domain, and sufficient local mesh refinement is required in the 3D model, especially in end and corner regions. We further show that the 2D open-core and closed-core models exhibit consistent but opposite systematic deviations from the 3D reference, corresponding to two limiting magnetic return path state. Finally, a weighted hybrid strategy (WH-2D) is developed to combine two complementary 2D models for accurate reconstruction of the 3D reference forces. The method achieves an average error below 3.0% with substantially lower computational cost, making it efficient for practical transformer design and short-circuit force evaluation.

title: Measurement of Interface Pressure Distortion and Flashover Behavior in Cable Terminal Layers Under Thermomechanical Stress
authors: Pengzhen Wu, Qingyi Kong, Shuaibing Li, Yi Cui, Min Pang, Minghan Ma, Yongqiang Kang, Haiying Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Cable composite insulation interfaces are susceptible to pressure distribution distortion under combined electric, thermal, and mechanical stresses, potentially triggering insulation failure. To investigate this phenomenon, this research develops a comprehensive three-stage approach. Initially, a bilateral shaft-sleeve contact model is established to theoretically analyze the mechanism of uneven interfacial pressure distribution under bending stress and temperature at cable terminal interfaces. Subsequently, a specialized bending-temperature coupling experimental platform validates the theoretical findings, confirming that extreme temperatures and bending stresses significantly distort interfacial pressure. Finally, surface flashover discharge experiments on XLPE-SiR interfaces are conducted, examining discharge characteristics through multiple parameters including starting discharge voltage, grey scale histograms, and infrared spectra. The results reveal that mechanical-thermal stress coupling alters the interference fit of cable composite interfaces, leading to non-uniform pressure distribution. Furthermore, the study demonstrates that pressure distribution transition modes directly influence discharge path characteristics. The extent of discharge damage is found to correlate with the combined effects of discharge channel broadening at initiation sites and dielectric polarization. These findings provide crucial insights for improving cable terminal design and reliability in high-voltage power systems.

title: TraceNet: Iterative Gradient Amplification for Backdoor Discovery
authors: Jatin Prajapati, Madhav Yadav, Girish Mishra
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: As deep learning models are increasingly deployed in critical applications, the risk of backdoor attacks has increased. These attacks introduce small triggers that can significantly alter model predictions, posing serious security threats. Existing detection methods are often complex and computationally expensive, limiting their practical applicability. In this paper, we introduce a novel and efficient strategy for detecting backdoor attacks using gradient ascent-based analysis. Our approach computes gradients for targeted classes, reduces them using Principal Component Analysis - based dimensionality reduction, and calculates anomaly scores per class. This method allows for the detection of multiclass backdoors. We demonstrate that our technique is computationally efficient and highly interpretable, making it a practical solution for real-time model auditing.

title: Network Latency Prediction Using Structured Data Completion: A Review
authors: Ved Prakash Tiwari, Vinay Kumar, Princy Matlani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Recent years have experienced the explosive growth of network traffic, driven by increasing users and portable devices. As we approach the era of $\mathbf{6 G}$ communication, achieving ultra-low latency becomes paramount to meet the stringent requirements of emerging applications such as immersive XR (Extended Reality), real-time holographic communication, autonomous systems, and industrial automation. This makes maintaining Quality of Service (QoS) crucial for network service providers. There are many metrices that are utilized for this monitoring, such as Round Trip Time (RTT), Time to live (TTL), etc. Network latency is a key QoS parameter, but directly measuring end-to-end latency across all $N^{2}$ paths in an N -node network is impractical due to cost implications and non-scalability.

title: High-Efficiency Non-Isolated Dual-Port DC-DC Converter for Solar-Powered EV Charging and Auxiliary Systems
authors: Banothu Somanna, Sushma Gupta, Banothu Linganna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: The growing shift toward sustainable energy has increased the use of renewable sources such as photovoltaic (PV) systems, particularly in electric vehicle (EV) applications. This study introduces a non-isolated dual-port DC-DC converter (DPC) capable of delivering both boost and buck voltage outputs simultaneously from a single DC source. The design addresses the requirements of EV battery charging and auxiliary power supply, emphasising high efficiency, compact construction, and compatibility with fluctuating solar input and varying load conditions. A control approach integrating constant voltage and constant current regulation ensures stable operation and reliable performance. The proposed converter is a practical solution for incorporating solar energy into EV charging systems, supporting the transition toward cleaner transportation. Simulation results demonstrate its effectiveness in maintaining output stability and efficiency, indicating its potential in advanced smart energy networks where flexibility, reliability, and performance are essential.

title: Optimized Analysis of Image-Based Detecting Leaf Diseases in Crops Using Deep Learning
authors: Khushboo Pachori, Megha Vashishtha, Nikita Shivhare, Vandana Patel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Global food supply depends heavily on agriculture, yet a number of leaf diseases (LD) frequently jeopardize crop health, drastically lowering yield quality and quantity. Plant LD must be identified early and accurately in order to reduce losses and guarantee sustainable agricultural methods. Traditional methods rely on manual examination, which is laborious, prone to mistakes, and heavily reliant on specialized knowledge. Deep Learning based image processing methods have become effective instruments for automatically diagnosing agricultural diseases in recent years. An enhanced examination of deep learning models for image-based leaf disease diagnosis is presented in this paper. In order to increase classification accuracy, the study investigates CNNs, hybrid architectures, and transfer learning frameworks like VGG-16, ResNet50, and DenseNet-121. To improve image quality and lower computational cost, preprocessing techniques such as data augmentation, contrast enhancement, and noise removal are used. Moreover, optimization techniques like feature selection and hyperparameter tweaking are combined to increase robustness and efficiency. The suggested framework lowers over-fitting and training costs while showing a notable increase in disease classification accuracy. The results demonstrate how well-suited DenseNet-121 learning models can be to deliver automated, scalable, and real-time precision agriculture solutions.

authors: N/A
