title: Machine Learning-Based Multi-Class Classification of Chronic Kidney Disease Stages
authors: Aisiri A P, Naveen Kumar H N, Ramya B, Rishika M
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Early detection of the phases of chronic kidney disease (CKD), a progressive illness characterized by the increasing loss of kidney function, is essential for prompt clinical intervention and better patient outcomes. The course of chronic kidney disease (CKD) can be divided into five phases based on the Glomerular Filtration Rate (GFR): Stage 1 (normal kidney activity), Stage 2 (mildly decreased), Stage 3A and 3B (moderately decreased), Stage 4 (severely decreased), and Stage 5 (end-stage renal failure). This paper describes a machine learning-based method for automated CKD stage categorization utilizing the University of California, Irvine (UCI) Machine Learning Repository's publicly accessible dataset. The preprocessing pipeline comprised univariate feature selection to find reliable and discriminative predictors, one-hot encoding for categorical variables, and the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) to resolve class imbalance. The classification performance of five machine learning methods was assessed: Random Forest, Decision Tree, XGBoost, K Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and Multilayer Perceptron (MLP).

title: SmartWaveConnect: A Contextual Framework for Optimized Energy Utilization in IoT Smart Systems
authors: Reshma Soman N, Aswathy Prakash G, Hanan Azza
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Recently, we have been experiencing a revolution in the technology with Smart IoT Computing and Internet of Things (IoT). A new technology also referred to as Smart IoT also known as Smart Cloud of Things, S-IoT, is combining cloud computing with Internet of Things. Dynamic and heterogeneous environments, energy efficiency, delay sensitivity are some of the challenges that S-IoT systems have to face. S-IoT faces an important challenge associated with the energy efficiency of IoT applications and of cloud resources. In this paper we introduce a SmartWaveConnect Framework (SWC-F) with modified Genetic Algorithm (M-GA) to optimized energy schemes for the S-IoT. A set of numerical simulations were run, the effectiveness of this method was verified by comparing the results against the CSLCM algorithm. The results show the improvement of using proposed technique making energy consumption optimize better. Additionally, the results of the proposed method compared to CSLCM are that they win over jobs requests in terms of diminishing energy. In sectors such as healthcare, where real time data processing and energy efficiency are necessary for remote monitoring, the SWC-F’s adaptability is extremely appropriate. The ability of the framework to optimize energy consumption for job requests can substantially reduce operational costs of time spent from collecting and analyzing data from wearable sensors and other IoT devices.

title: Consortium Blockchain Model for Donation Fund in Corporate Social Responsibility(CSR)
authors: Inayatulloh, Dwi Nova Wijaya, Prasetya Cahya Saputra, Ahmad Fathurrozi, Yasri, Aida Fitriyani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) is a strategy used by companies to contribute socially to the community, in line with government regulations. However, managing CSR funds often encounters issues such as limited transparency in their distribution and use. This study aims to develop a prototype of a blockchain-based CSR fund management system to enhance transparency in the distribution and utilization of funds. The blockchain type used is a consortium blockchain, managed jointly by several institutions that are members of the consortium. In this research, the system will be collaboratively managed by the government, the company providing CSR funds, and the CSR fund distributor. The research method employed is Research and Development (RD), which involves stages such as needs assessment, system design, prototype creation, and limited testing. Simulation results using Hyperledger Fabric demonstrate that every transaction related to the distribution and use of CSR funds must be validated by both the fund distributor and the recipient, ensuring that the information recorded on the blockchain remains transparent and accurate.

title: Fine-Tuning Meta’s NLLB-200 Model for Telugu–English Neural Machine Translation and Real-Time WikiHow Deployment via Android App
authors: Akshat Govil, C Ravindra Reddy, Haren A, Manju Venugopalan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: This paper showcases the fine-tuning and deployment of the Telugu-English Neural Machine Translation system developed using Meta’s NLLB-200 model. To tackle noisily parallel corpora and make it more amenable for low-resource languages, semantic filtering is adopted via a multilingual Sentence-BERT model on the Samanantar corpus, filtering out only high-quality sentence pairs. These filtered corpora are then further fine-tuned using the nllb-200-distilled-600M model bidirectionally for both Telugu and English translation tasks. Quantitative assessments using the filtered test data indicate that the fine-tuned translation system is capable of reporting BLEU scores of 0.705 for Telugu-to-English translation, 0.796 for English-to-Telugu translation, with corresponding ROUGE-L metrics of 0.380, 0.878, METEOR of 0.788, 0.871, and finally, BERTScore of 0.989 and 0.981 respectively, indicating a significant improvement over the pretrained models. This translation system is deployed via a FastAPI server and is accessed through a Telugu-first Android application that is capable of real-time extraction and translation of WikiHow data, with the objective of more Telugus gaining access to procedural English knowledge.

title: A Closed-Loop Information System Architecture for Smart-City Public Service Monitoring and SLA Breach Warning
authors: Boyi Li
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: Smart-city public services generate large volumes of requests, while operational teams can only intervene in a small fraction of cases each day. This capacity constraint means that monitoring must do more than visualize indicators: it must produce an actionable ranked alert queue that can be dispatched and traced. Many existing solutions either remain at the dashboard level without decision support, or report model accuracy without specifying how scores translate into dispatching and feedback-driven improvement. This paper presents a closedloop information system architecture that connects data ingestion and governance, KPI computation, request-level risk scoring, alert queue generation, dispatching, and feedback write-back into a unified workflow for operational monitoring and early warning. Using NYC 311 service requests from 2025-10-01 to 2026-01-01, we formulate an SLA-breach early-warning task with SLA <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=72$</tex> hours and evaluate an interpretable logistic-regression baseline scored at request creation using only fields available at that time. The model achieves ROC-AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.9011$</tex> and Average Precision <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{AP})=0.6386$</tex>. Under a Top 1%-5% intervention budget, Precision @ K <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0.80$</tex> with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Lift} {@} \mathrm{K} \approx 4.7$</tex>, indicating that the proposed pipeline can generate high-precision priority queues suitable for targeted intervention in capacity-constrained settings.

title: Unified Residual Attention U-Net Framework for Accurate Brain Tumour Segmentation
authors: Aryan Jain, Piyanshu Saini, Naman Jain, Vipul Dixit
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Brain tumours, characterized by abnormal cell development in or around the brain, pose significant health risks due to their impact on vital brain functions. Accurate segmentation and classification remain challenging due to variations in tumour size, shape, and location. This paper presents a deep learning approach for brain tumour detection using magnetic resonance imaging (MRI). Our methodology involves preprocessing MRI images through Gaussian blur and augmentation, followed by binary thresholding for segmentation and morphological feature extraction. A U-shaped Convolutional Neural Network (U-Net) is employed for classification, achieving 97% accuracy on a dataset of 255 MRI images containing both normal and malignant samples. The study incorporates statistical techniques and computational intelligence to enhance detection precision. Additionally, we review relevant literature on brain tumour identification, discuss available datasets and augmentation techniques, and explore future possibilities in deep learning applications. This comprehensive approach aims to provide researchers with valuable insights and methodologies for advancing brain tumour detection capabilities.

title: Synergizing the study of deep learning in medical imaging: A comprehensive review
authors: Kartik Sharma, Sumit Kumar, Surbhi Vijh, Ubaid Mubashir Wani, Waqqas Manzoor
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Deep learning has become a revolutionary new paradigm in the field of medical imaging, providing automated, precise and scalable solutions to diagnostic problems in a wide range of modalities including MRI, CT, X-ray, ultrasound and histopathology. This paper provides a synthesis and benchmark of twenty latest models of deep learning used in medical image classification, image segmentation, and image enhancement. ResNet50, DenseNet121 and InceptionV3 are convolutional neural networks that show good performance in hierarchy feature extraction and transfer learning to limited size annotated datasets. ViTs bring into the frame self-attention mechanisms which capture global contextual relationships and they outperform CNNs in high-resolution diagnostic tasks. GANs are employed to complement the training data that decreases the imbalance of classes and improves generalization, that is, detection of rare diseases. Ensemble learning techniques such as soft voting, stacking, and hybrid fusion are techniques that are used to increase diagnostic reliability with multiple architectures. The domain-specific preprocessing, attention mechanisms and interpretability frameworks contained in the reviewed models also vary with the clinical needs. The performance of each of the models in the metrics of accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and inference time is assessed in this paper, which gives a strict comparative basis to the subsequent studies. These findings reveal that it is important to include architectural variety, synthetic data creation, and multi-modal integration in constructions of smart diagnostic systems. Explainability, computational performance, and translational opportunities are put into focus in order to solve the gap between algorithmic innovation and clinical translation.

title: Semantic Interoperability in Smart Grids: Integrating Electrical Engineering, Machine Learning, and Software Architecture
authors: Azmat Ullah, Rasika Tharanga, Damindu Thathsara
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: Smart-grid operations hinge on data that is both timely and electrically meaningful. We present a contribution that blends electrical engineering, machine learning (ML), and software engineering to deliver semantic interoperability across PLC/SCADA domains. Field data from OPC UA and Modbus devices (e.g., Siemens S7-1500 PLCs, Modbus/TCP meters) is mapped to IEC 61850 logical nodes and bound to CIM (IEC 61970/61968) topology. A streaming broker emits RDF/OWL triples with QUDT units, phase labeling, and time-synchronization quality (SOSA/SSN). Two distribution-level use cases- Volt/VAR optimization (VVO) and FLISR-are realized with constraint-aware ML, enforcing voltage limits, tap ranges, inverter P-Q capability, and interlocks. On a SCADA testbed, sub-second latency is shown with improvements in voltage compliance and restoration time while preserving legacy SCADA. The work provides a deployable path for utilities to combine ML with auditable, standards-based electrical semantics.

title: Personalized Learning through Adaptive Testing in Higher Education
authors: Sharvil Barhanpure, Siddhesh Singh, Aaditya Bhure, Jay Vasani, Akhil Gupta, Monali Gulhane
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: In the fast changing situation of higher education, traditional teaching methods have often proved inadequate to cater for and to adjust to the diverse needs and different learning styles of the students. The platform connects a front-end dedicated website and back-end adaptation algorithms. Our research indicates that the development of technologies has been a major force in the transformation of adaptive learning. The study was conducted using qualitative descriptive research design, and the data were collected by means of questionnaires, interviews, observation, and documentation. The evidence from the questionnaire about the importance of AI in learning shows that 88% students very strongly agree, 9% agree, 2% disagree, and 1% very strongly agree that AI is important in learning.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Psoriasis Diagnosis Using Fuzzy-Aware Preprocessing and Gradient-Enhanced CNN
authors: Soumya C S, Jayanna H S
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Although skin disorders rarely threaten life, they often cause continuous irritation, visible lesions, and psycho-logical stress, especially in chronic conditions such as psoriasis. In routine clinical practice, diagnosis still depends heavily on visual inspection, which makes consistency difficult when patient volume is high or image quality is poor. Recent work has shown that deep learning can assist in this process, but many reported systems remain sensitive to noise, dataset bias, and weak feature separation. Instead of only modifying the classifier, this work places strong emphasis on the reliability of the input images themselves. A fuzzy-guided multi-stage denoising and contrast enhancement strategy is first applied, after which a gradient-enhanced convolutional network with an attention-based decision module performs the classification. When tested on large dermatology datasets, the proposed approach reached an accuracy of 99.14% and consistently outperformed standard architectures. This indicates that the method is not only accurate in controlled experiments but also more suitable for real diagnostic environments.

title: Enhancing Vehicle Tracking Using AI Powered Surveillance
authors: Aditya Gautam, Durvish Mehta, Aditya Chaudhary, Lakshay Bhardwaj, Jaishree Jain
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The Enhanced Surveillance Vehicle Tracking System leverages AI-powered Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) cameras to improve real-time vehicle monitoring and law enforcement investigations. At the moment, these cameras are set up in cities to carry out E-Challan and find traffic violations by using YOLO V8-based deep learning models to read vehicle number plates. In criminal investigations, though, police still have to look at CCTV footage by hand, which takes a long time, is easy to make mistakes, and doesn't work well for tracking large numbers of people. To overcome these constraints, this paper suggests an automated AI-driven vehicle tracking system that leverages the current ANPR infrastructure at road intersections. The system finds, identifies, and records the movement of vehicles in a central database, where it stores time, location, and image data for later use. Investigators can quickly look up a suspect vehicle's travel history, which cuts down on the time it takes to investigate because they don't have to watch video footage. A comparison of manual tracking and AI-based tracking shows that automation is better in terms of speed, accuracy, and efficiency. Also, YOLO V8 is better than other object detection models like Faster R-CNN and SSD because it has a higher detection accuracy (~90%), a faster processing speed (~70 FPS), and a lightweight architecture that makes it perfect for large-scale use. The suggested system makes law enforcement more efficient, lightens the load on investigators, and makes the most of the current ANPR infrastructure. This method is a big step forward in intelligent surveillance and urban security because it can be scaled up, processes data in real time, and is automated.

title: An Ephemeral Investigation on Gastrointestinal Cancer Detection Techniques
authors: Vanishree Abhay, Achyutha Prasad N, Sathish Babu N
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Gastrointestinal cancer (GIC) includes Esophageal, stomach, colon, and rectal tumors. Medical imaging techniques such as CT scans, magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), endoscopy, and histopathology slides are frequently used in the diagnosis of gastrointestinal cancer. This gives specific information on the features, location, and size of tumors. Despite the existence of several extrapolative and predictive biomarkers, the high death rate for patients with GIC indicates that additional analysis may be done for a more individualized treatment program having improved prognosis with few adverse effects. Classifying GIC is a difficult yet crucial field of study and application in ML and medical imaging. A review considering 25 articles based on GIC detection is focused in this survey. The survey includes scrutiny on diverse aspects. The diverse methodologies including ML-based and DL-based methods for GIC detection are analyzed in this survey. In addition, the diverse datasets utilized in each considered work is analyzed. Analysis on diverse performances and accordingly the best achievements are also reviewed. Lastly, the research gaps on GIC detection are discussed.

title: Automatic Surveillance System
authors: Harshalatha Y, Prajwal B C, Sujan D, Supreeth G L, Dilip N, Shristhi N Akkalkot
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Surveillance plays an important role in managing security in various situations and it has significant advantages in managing security. The areas which require high security like military, commercial buildings, banks need continuous monitoring and automatic surveillance is the best solution. In this paper, a real-time automatic surveillance system is proposed with an advantage of reduced storage requirement. The implementation uses background subtraction for motion detection, histogram of oriented gradients (HOG) Algorithm for human detection and local binary pattern histogram algorithm for face recognition. The system utilizes Raspberry-Pi along with camera modules which operate only motion detection. The system also recognizes the face in the captured frames and notifies the user when an unknown face is detected via Telegram.

title: Model Then Revise: A Scalable AI Supported Workflow for Knowledge Integration in Engineering Writing
authors: Binyi Huang, Cong Wang, Khalegh Barati
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: Knowledge Integration (KI) is essential for postgraduate engineering writing, yet automated feedback in online learning often triggers superficial edits instead of integrated revisions. This limitation is well-documented in research on modeling revision and on how learners construe feedback as coaching or as verification, and in reviews of automated feedback systems that emphasize actionability gap. This study addresses that gap in a Building Information Modeling (BIM) postgraduate course <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(n=26)$</tex> by comparing two guidance sequences: model-then-revise, a brief Annotator activity that models integrated revision followed by one adaptive automated round; versus automated-only, two automated rounds. A mixed-methods design combined KI rubric scoring on pre/post and embedded tasks, log-coded revision action, and short interviews; a light human-in-the-loop calibration sampled outputs to probe perceived fairness. Results indicate that modeling-first led to more coherent, evidence-linked revisions, a higher share of 'Connected New Idea' edits, and disproportionate benefits for initially low KI learners; brief Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) checkpoints improved perceived transparency without undermining scalability. The contribution is a practical design rule, Annotate-then-Revise, and a measurement template that transfers modeling-supported guidance from science explanations to engineering genres, aligning with calls in higher education to pair AI in Education with educator oversight and with evidence that well-designed digital tools can yield meaningful learning gains.

title: Transparent AI for Financial Security: Explainable Deep Learning for Fraudulent Transaction Detection
authors: K. Praveena, K. Pavithra, S. Padmavathy, Ismankhan Y M, A. Monisha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Financial fraud detection represents a critical challenge for banking institutions and digital payment platforms in protecting customers from monetary losses. We developed an explainable deep learning model to identify fraudulent transactions in financial data. The deep learning model was trained on a highly imbalanced dataset and achieved exceptional accuracy. To enhance trust and transparency in model decisions, we applied SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) and Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME) explainability techniques. SHAP identified the most influential features which are affecting the fraud detection, while LIME provided the explanations for individual transaction predictions. The findings demonstrate that our proposed model outperforms conventional machine learning algorithms such as logistic regression, XGBoost and random forest mainly in precision, recall and Area Under the Curve (AUC) performance metrics. This work emphasizes the importance of combining accurate fraud detection models with explainability tools to assist financial experts and managers in making reliable decisions.

title: Enhancing Software Project Management with Machine Learning: A Performance Analysis of Effort Estimation Models
authors: Vikrant Malik, Renuka Arora, Hitesh Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Accurate software effort estimation is crucial for project planning, cost control, and resource allocation throughout the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC). Traditional estimation techniques, such as expert judgment and algorithmic models like COCOMO, often rely on subjective assumptions and show limited adaptability to modern software environments. This study applies machine learning (ML) regression models to enhance the accuracy and reliability of effort estimation using historical project data. A dataset of over one hundred software projects was analyzed, incorporating attributes such as team and manager experience, project duration, transactions, entities, adjusted and unadjusted function points, and programming language. After data cleaning, normalization, and categorical encoding, three ML algorithms—Decision Tree Regressor, Random Forest Regressor, and Gradient Boosting Regressor—were implemented and compared using Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and the Coefficient of Determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>). Among the models, the Gradient Boosting Regressor achieved the best performance (MAE = 1391.16, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.91), demonstrating strong predictive accuracy and generalization capability. Feature importance analysis identified adjusted function points, transactions, and entities as key predictors of development effort. The results indicate that ML-based estimation models can significantly improve project planning precision and promote data-driven decision-making in software engineering.

title: The Protection of The Right To Privacy In Criminal Proceedings Under The EU AI Regulation
authors: Suhail Masoud Albaloushi, Ibrahim Suleiman Al Qatawneh, Moustafa Elmetwaly Kandeel, Mansour Ibrahim Alnuaimi, Ali Saif Aldarmaki, Saeed Abdullah Alkalbani
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)
abstract: With the advancement of technology, Artificial Intelligence has become a significant tool in all fields, including in criminal procedures, being utilized in facial recognition, video analysis, crime prediction, and tracking individuals’ movements. However, this extensive use poses risks of privacy violations, necessitating stringent legal safeguards to ensure protection. This study aims to explore the impact of using AI in criminal proceedings on the protection of privacy and individual rights. It addresses the legal and ethical challenges arising from the use of such technologies in the collection and analysis of personal data during criminal investigations. The research concludes that AI represents a powerful instrument for enhancing the effectiveness of criminal investigations due to its capacity to process large volumes of data with speed and accuracy. Nevertheless, the use of these technologies raises concerns regarding privacy protection and the safeguarding of individuals’ rights, particularly when personal data is collected without explicit consent. The study further highlights the need to establish a legal framework governing the use of AI in this context, ensuring the protection of individual rights and shielding them from potential violations resulting from the use of such technologies. The study recommends the development of comprehensive legislation in the United Arab Emirates to regulate the use of AI technologies in criminal investigations, ensuring transparency and prior consent from individuals before collecting their data. It also stresses the importance of maintaining human oversight over AI systems to prevent automated analysis from infringing upon the right to a fair trial.

title: Enhanced Clustering of Large Language Model-Generated Tasks: A Hybrid Approach Using UMAP, DBSCAN, and KMeans and XAI
authors: T Satyanarayana Murty Poppoppula, Venkatesan M Sundaram, Kiran Sree P, Prabhavathy P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Categorization of tasks produced by Large Language Models (LLMs) is necessary to interpret their practical uses. Conventional clusterization with BERTopic and BERT embeddings is restricted due to the inferior separation of clusters and interpretability of topics. This paper presents a better approach to clustering utilizing hybrid embeddings (BAAI/bge-large-en), dimensional reduction (UMAP), and clustering algorithms (DBSCAN and KMeans). Our approach sorts 5,316 tasks into 300 substantive clusters, bettering interpretability and performance indicators. In contrast to existing models, our method obtains higher topic coherence, improved separation, and better clustering quality.

title: Miniaturized Meandered Slot Patch Antenna with Defected Ground for Implantable 2.45 GHz ISM Applications
authors: Saisriusha Magapu, Prakhar Pratyush, Tushar Kumar Singh, Christina Josephine Malathi A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: This work successfully presents a compact implantable microstrip patch antenna designed for biomedical telemetry applications in the 2.45 GHz ISM band. The proposed design follows a structured four-stage miniaturization approach, strategically incorporating a low-loss RO3003 substrate, meandered slots, and a rectangular Defected Ground Structure (DGS) to enhance the electrical length and improve impedance characteristics while maintaining a small dimension. The final optimized antenna demonstrates excellent performance, achieving a deep reflection coefficient of -30 dB at 2.45 GHz and a peak gain of 4.89 dB. This superior radiation performance, even in lossy tissue environments, is attributed to the reduced dielectric loss of the RO3003 substrate and the enhanced matching provided by the combined slot-and-DGS configuration. The antenna offers a simple, single-layer, and fabrication-friendly structure suitable for robust short-range wireless biomedical monitoring.

title: Feature-Preserving Filtering for Pipeline Safety Construction Threat Signals
authors: Qiang Liu, Yitong Zhao, Dongyu Liu, Yong Wang, Shuai Yao, Qisong Wu
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: Third-party construction activities are critical sources of risk that lead to pipeline leakage and rupture. The accurate extraction of third-party construction-induced threat signals, acquired by distributed acoustic sensors (DAS), is the core of real-time identification of such threat events. However, these signals are severely corrupted by continuous wideband traffic roar in urban pipeline corridors. The noise partially overlaps with the time-frequency characteristics of construction signals. To address this challenge, a feature-preserving filtering method is proposed. First, non-threat interference bands are adaptively separated via a dual-criterion frequency-domain screening mechanism. Then, multi-dimensional signal presence probability (SPP) fusion including dual-window energy ratio and time-domain energy mutation rate are designed for robust SPP estimation, followed by accurate noise power spectrum estimation based on the derived SPP. Finally, SPP-modulated minimum mean square error (MMSE) wiener filtering dynamically adjusts gains to preserve impulsive features and suppress residual noise. Experimental verification shows that compared with traditional algorithms, the proposed method exhibits superior feature retention and identification accuracy in low SNR scenarios, providing a reliable solution for real-time monitoring of pipeline construction threats using DAS.

title: A Hybrid Multi-Algorithmic Strategy for Real-Time Forecasting and Economic Optimization in Renewable Microgrids
authors: Nimai Charan Patel, Bhabasis Mohapatra, Adithya Ballaji, Ritesh Dash, Swarnika Gupta, Binod Kumar Sahu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Micro grids powered by renewable energy face challenges in balancing variable generation with fluctuating demand, often resulting in operational inefficiencies and higher energy costs. Accurate forecasting of load, solar generation, and tariff trends is essential to enhance reliability and minimize cost. This paper presents a comprehensive hybrid forecasting and optimization framework that integrates statistical models-ARIMA, SARIMA, and Holt-Winters with a Multi-Algorithmic Strategy (MAS) hybrid approach. The proposed methodology was validated on a real micro grid dataset from April 2025, with the objective of reducing the average purchase cost of electricity while ensuring operational feasibility. The hybrid MAS model demonstrated superior forecasting performance across all variables, achieving lower error measures compared to individual models. In addition, economic analysis showed that the hybrid framework reduced the average unit price of energy procurement from 4.22/kWh (baseline) to 3.71/kWh, corresponding to a 12% cost reduction. This improvement was achieved under practical battery storage and power exchange constraints, highlighting the framework’s adaptability to real-world micro grid settings. By jointly addressing forecasting accuracy and economic optimization, the proposed framework provides a scalable solution for enhancing the sustainability and cost-effectiveness of renewable energy micro grids. The outcomes offer valuable insights for policymakers, utilities, and researchers seeking to advance micro grid planning and operation in the context of increasing renewable penetration.

title: Real-Time State-Of-Charge Estimation for Electric Vehicle Batteries Using Driving Pattern Synchronized Neural Networks
authors: Gunjan Haldar, Siddharth Mishra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Digital transformation has opened new opportunities and innovations in several industries, especially in the transportation sector with the help of information and communication technology assisted with artificial intelligence and industrial IoT (IIoT). Hence our research focuses primarily on the development of the digital twin of the Battery Management Systems (BMS) for lead acid batteries and enhance its functionality and intelligence. We propose PyBMS, a novel and enhanced BMS digital twin framework that leverages a driving-pattern-synchronised long short-term memory network combined with parallel rectified filtration to enable real-time estimation of the State-of-Charge (SoC) and Performance Index. The system dynamically identifies current driving patterns and detects battery pack anomalies, further improving accuracy through the integration of the Voltage-independent Extended Kalman Filter (V-EKF) algorithm - a novel advancement over the conventional Extended Kalman Filter (EKF).

title: Quantum Communication Protocols to Improve the Security and Reliability of Next-Generation Smart Energy Systems
authors: Sandip Gurudas Lanjewar, Aditya Parate, Gousia Ahmed, Hrushikesh Madhukar Panchabudhe, Ravindra Ramesh Rasekar, Praveen Kumar Dhankar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Smart energy systems are expediting the development of IoT-based devices, distributed energy resources and smart grid operations. This has eased more efficiency and environmental benefits. The digital systems lack privacy, accuracy, and reliability of data, thus making it susceptible to hacking and other evil acts. Even though it is resistant to most of the attacks, traditional cryptography may not work anymore as computers accelerate and quantum computers are put into practice. One can also utilize quantum communication technologies which are based on quantum physics which is more secure. BB84 and E91 algorithm of quantum key distribution in next-generation smart energy systems are the ones used in the current paper. The suggested solutions contribute to the improved security and stability of the operation as they bring the laws of quantum entanglement and superposition in line with the needs of the energy sector. The paper also outlines the major ways through which smart grids can be hacked like manipulating data, using false information and denying-ofservice and how they can be minimized by employing quantumenhanced communication. The success rate and level of data integrity of 98.7 % and 97.6 respectively are better than the classical cryptography and blockchain-only systems by 7.3 and 5.3 % respectively, as indicated by simulation analysis. The hybrid framework demonstrates the resilience against the false data injection and denial-of-service attacks up to 17 %.

title: Agri Pulse: A Comprehensive Tool for Smart Agriculture Management
authors: Sandhya N, Naveena S, O Sachin, Shamshad Banu S, Ubedulla Khan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Agriculture is the pillar of most economies, but farmers are not getting the latest and accurate information, which leads them to face challenges such as climate change, market access issues, pests and diseases. AgriPulse is an AI-based modular web application that aims to enable the farmers by provide all kind of information related to agriculture. AgriPulse consists of six modules and each module serves differently by utilizing ML models and with integration of APIs. The Crop Recommendation System predicts the suitable crop to grow in their land based on the environmental factors and soil parameters using Machine Learning algorithm. The Plant Disease Detection Module, built on Convolutional Neural Network (ResNet34), detects the diseases from the infected leaf and allows farmers to protect their crops. The Commodity Price Report Extractor is based on web scraping technique and it retrieves and displays current market prices as well as percentage of change in price compared to previous month and year using Selenium to enable farmers to make effective selling decisions. Real-time Weather Fetcher retrieves current weather data for a particular location using WeatherAPI.com and this helps farmers to assist in the optimal scheduling of agricultural activities. The News Fetcher also collects the latest news in agriculture from Google News RSS feeds to provide users with updates on trends and developments happening in the Agri Industry. Finally, a consolidated Chatbot provides the user with proper guidance and assistance which is developed using Retrieval based QA. Utilizing real-time data and smart automation, AgriPulse intends to enhance farm productivity, mitigate risk, and foster sustainable farming techniques.

title: Analysis of SynRM Drive Through Optimized PI Controller Design
authors: Anjali Gupta, Sushma Gupta, Sanjeev Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: This drive performance of a Synchronous Reluctance Motor (SynRM) is investigated on a multi modal speed track. The PI gains of the drive for speed and current loops i.e <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$q$</tex> axis currents, are obtained under a coordinated optimization framework developed in this paper by the selection of suitable bandwidth (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\omega$</tex> ni) and damping ratio (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\zeta$</tex> i). Initially, the gains are determined using a pole placement method, after which they are further refined through bandwidth-based optimization process. Simulation results by the obtained optimized PI gains on multi track reference speed shows zero steady state error, i.e no speed ripples, fast response i.e settling time of approximately 0.28 s, with a small overshoot of 1.8% without adjusting these six PI gains as often practiced in hit and trial approaches. On increased loads of 10 Nm, 30 Nm, and 50 Nm, the corresponding speed drops were <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.38 \%, 6.88 \%$</tex>, and 12.71% respectively, with settling times of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.08 ~\mathrm{s}, 0.10 ~\mathrm{s}$</tex>, and 0.13 s, respectively are under acceptable limits.

title: Reviewers: CECCC 2025
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 7th International Communication Engineering and Cloud Computing Conference (CECCC)

title: Real-Time Monitoring and Fault Detection in Intelligent BMS Architecture
authors: Yogendra Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: A Battery Management System (BMS) is the most advanced technology in diagnosing and maintaining battery pack health. The weakest cell's capacity and the individual cells' respective internal resistance are the key factors that determine the process. Using these metrics, a health index is produce with percentage values ranging from 0% to 100%, which clearly shows the cell's health. If the health index drops below the limit set beforehand, then fault codes will be, generate and freeze frame data will be stored for the purpose of diagnosis. Battery management system (BMS) employs a variety of techniques to protect the battery including controlling cycle charge/discharge, voltage balancing, and real-time monitoring for the best method to maximize battery life, energy delivery, and performance. The effects of all these protective measures are that the system is always operating under safe conditions and can supply high power if necessary. Studies have shown that the BMS plays a crucial role in the lifetime and efficiency of battery power systems.

title: Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)

title: Assessment of Integration of PV System with Grid
authors: Ashpin Pabi D J, Kasturi Sai Sandeep, Sai Rama Krishna Indarapu, Surendar Kumar S, Deeksha Verma, Takveer Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: Interactive photovoltaic (PV) systems connected to the electric grid offer a powerful approach to harnessing solar energy while utilizing existing grid infrastructure. This paper summarizes the literature surrounding interactive PV grid systems, reviewing system components and architecture, performance loss evaluation, economic consideration, environmental impacts, the necessary grid integration, and the future of interactive PV grid systems. The system components and architecture present an overview of the relevant system components accounting for PV grid interaction such as solar panels, inverters, grid connection, energy storage elements, and control architecture. Performance loss evaluation of interactive PV grid systems have been examined in terms of PV efficiency, energy yield, power quality, and reliability. The economic consideration of interactive PV grid systems looks at the capital costs, operational costs, revenue generation, and returns on investments. The environmental impact of PV grid system operation includes possible carbon footprint reduction and land occupation. Finally, grid integration consideration of the current literature encompasses different aspects such as voltage stability, frequency stability, and grid codes. The future of interactive PV grid systems includes a discussion on energy storage, advance control algorithms, cyber security, and modernized grid system opportunities. The aforementioned literature review will assist the reader in completing their understanding of interactive PV system connected to the electric grid and its possible role within a sustainable energy future.

title: A Hybrid 3DMM-Guided GAN Framework For Identity-Preserving Face Frontalization
authors: Gangalakshmi T, P Velvizhy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Face frontalization can be defined as synthesizing a canonical frontal face from arbitrary pose images while retaining identity information. The task serves as a critical means for enhancing the robustness of face recognition systems that work under unconstrained pose variations. Most existing Generative Adversarial Networks(GAN)-based face frontalization approaches produce realistic images but usually lack explicit geometric constraints, leading to identity drift and structural distortions when dealing with extreme poses and self-occlusion. By contrast,Three Dimensional Morphable Models(3DMM)-based techniques guarantee strong geometric consistency but tend to suffer from over-smoothing and limited texture realism. In this paper, we present a hybrid face frontalization approach by incorporating 3DMM-based geometric priors with a supervised Generative Adversarial Network. The integrated 3DMM-based encoder extracts pose-normalized geometric representations as latent embeddings, which serve as implicit identity-preserving priors for the generative network during frontal face synthesis.Extensive experiments on the Multi-PIE dataset show that the proposed hybrid framework improves the similarity of identity and recognition accuracy over GAN-only baselines.

title: WhatsAPP-Integrated AI for Multilingual, Multimodal Education in Low-Resource Areas
authors: M Meyyappan, Sweta Singh, Suresh Anand M
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: This research presents the development a realtime conversational chatbot using WhatsAPP as an interface so that it can be accessed by people around the world. The chatbot has been integrated with multilingual and multimodal capabilities by leveraging the use of State of Art Performing Models such as Llama 3.2 11b vision to provide the responses and performing the analysis of the documents and with the use of Sarvam API to mainly focus on providing support to the indic languages. The chatbot supports 10 different indic languages along with multimodal inputs which include text, audio, document, and images. The chatbot has been integrated with FastAPI and using AWS S3 storage makes it globally accessible to each and every corner.

title: Real Time Suspicious Object Detection in Surveillance Footage Using AI
authors: Shikha Srivastava, Deepak Kumar Pathak, Aadya Nagar, Ashish Pandey, Aishwarya Mishra, Alok Kumar Shukla
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Now a days security is the major concern as the crime rate increases; there is a growing demand for intelligent surveillance systems that can detect potential dangers in actual times. Established CCTV footage setups often fall short due to their reliance on continuous human supervising, delayed alert generation, and limited capacity to oversee multiple locations simultaneously. This study presents an AI-driven surveillance framework utilizing the YOLOv5s model for computerized detection of suspicious objects. The model was trained on a dataset comprising 5,000 pictures representing diverse categories of suspicious objects. It accomplishes a precision of 89.1% and a recall of 95.7%. The proposed system is designed for deployment in highsecurity environments such as airports, hospitals etc. It continuously analyzes video streams to identify abnormal objects and generates instant alerts. The objective is to minimize false alarms, enhance adaptability and reliability, and reduce human intervention.

title: Artificial Intelligence Enabled Animal Detection Using IoT for Sustainable Smart Precision Agriculture Automation
authors: Dhiraj Thote, Yugant Bambal, Sandesh Nahate, Avantika Belkhode, Madhura Gabhane, Sapna Choudhary
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The combination of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) and the Internet of Things (IoT) can entirely transform the manner in which smart precision gardening is approached in the future. The proposed system uses a web of Internet of things (IoT) sensors including motion sensors, temperature sensors, and global positioning sensors, and high-resolution cameras to monitor the farming environments in real-time. These devices can transmit data to a central processing unit via such low power communication protocols as LoRa, ZigBee, or Wi-Fi. Convolved neural networks (CNN) and You Only Look Once (YOLO) algorithm make up the AI model. It is trained and tested with regard to a pre-selected set of animal pictures in order to precisely and promptly locate farm animals or breaking in animals. In this study benchmark dataset as Animals Detection Images Dataset was utilized in animal detection and training and test of AI models. The AI approach reduces the necessity of human assistance, reduces the amount of wasted resources, and streamlines the management of farms. The proposed solution is beneficial to the environment as well, it conserves energy and contributes to the growth of farming in the long term since it does not harm crops and prevents animals from going too far. This paper demonstrates how IoT smart systems and powerful AI models could collaborate to develop the future of scalable, environmentally friendly, and affordable precision farm solutions.

title: An adaptive RELAXation method for spectrum identification in active vibration control
authors: Donglong He, Zengkun Wang, Minyue Lu, Yu Ruan, Chenxi Wang, Zhibo Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Active vibration control (AVC) in marine and rotating machinery applications requires reliable identification of both the number and frequencies of discrete spectral lines embedded in noise and time-varying operating conditions. This paper proposes an adaptive RELAXation framework, termed ARELAX, for multi-sinusoidal line-spectrum identification. The method integrates (i) an iterative RELAX estimator that decouples closely spaced components by alternating single-component nonlinear least-squares updates, and (ii) a Penalizing Adaptive Likelihood (PAL) criterion for model-order selection. PAL is constructed from generalized likelihood-ratio measures that quantify, respectively, the gain of increasing the order and the remaining gap to the maximum-order model, thereby enabling an adaptive penalty that is mild before the true order and becomes stringent beyond it. Simulation studies under abrupt frequency jumps, linear and nonlinear frequency sweeps, and amplitude variations, together with experimental validation on a cylindrical-shell vibration isolation platform, demonstrate that ARELAX provides improved robustness to nonstationary and low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). Quantitative comparisons against short time fast Fourier transform (STFT), Multiple Signal Classification (MUSIC), and Estimation of Signal Parameters via Rotational Invariance Techniques (ESPRIT) are reported using global mean squared error (GMSE), steady-state mean squared error (SMSE) and runtime, showing that ARELAX achieves a favorable accuracy-complexity tradeoff for practical AVC deployment.

title: Reliability Modeling of Cyber-Physical System With Network Topology Based on Survival Signature
authors: Xinyu Wang, Jianfeng Yang, Jing Chen, Wenqian Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Reliability
abstract: With the widespread application of Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) in fields such as intelligent manufacturing and autonomous driving, their reliability has emerged as a critical challenge affecting overall system performance and safety. In response to the limitations of existing CPS reliability modeling approaches—such as insufficient structural expressiveness, difficulty in modeling heterogeneous software and hardware components, and the lack of a general modeling framework suitable for various system structures and component types—this paper proposes a universal CPS reliability modeling and fitting method based on survival signature theory. This method enables the separation of structural characteristics and component lifetime distributions without losing CPS structural information, thus providing a unified modeling framework for reliability evaluation under different distributional assumptions. Specifically, a generalized CPS reliability model is first developed using survival signature theory, incorporating structural assumptions, parameter configurations, and detailed mathematical derivations. The model's applicability is validated through systematic reliability calculations within a standard CPS framework. To better reflect real-world engineering scenarios, a CPS architecture with complex topological features is designed. Reliability variations in cyber and physical networks are then simulated using two distinct approaches: (1) an event-driven Monte Carlo method utilizing component reliability profiles for cyber network failure simulation, and (2) a time-censored Monte Carlo approach incorporating order statistics principles for physical network failure modeling. Building upon the simulation data, the paper innovatively introduces a reliability fitting method that combines survival signature and maximum likelihood estimation (MLE). In this approach, MLE is first applied to estimate the parameters of component lifetime distributions, after which the fitted CPS reliability is computed using the survival signature model. Finally, the proposed method is compared with five traditional regression models to assess its adaptability and performance across various network structures and simulation environments.

title: Federated Deep Learning for Emotion Recognition in Clinical Narratives
authors: Ramesh Wadawadagi, Tej Hiremath, Shrikant Tiwari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Emotion Recognition in clinical narratives aims to identify the emotional states expressed by patients regarding their health conditions, diagnoses, drug responses, side effects, etc., from conversation transcripts, feedback, medical blogs, and social media posts. However, sharing sensitive health data with a centralized system for training DL models presents significant privacy, authorization, and regulatory concerns. Federated Learning (FL) provides an efficient framework for local devices (edge devices) to train a shared global model cooperatively and collaboratively while enabling users to control their data locally. In this research, we present a Federated Deep Learning (FDL) framework for estimating users’ emotions in clinical environments that leverages distributed narrative data stored across multiple sources. More specifically, we employ two DL models, including LSTM and Transformers, to train the edge devices using the proposed FDL framework. Nevertheless, the non-IID trait of the subjective text content when used with the FL framework poses many challenges. To simulate this aspect, we employed an efficient Quantity-Level Dirichlet Allocation strategy for distributing data across various clients. For model aggregation, a robust aggregation strategy known as FedMedian is used, which efficiently handles non-IID data. The efficiency and stability of the proposed FDL framework is assessed using two distinct clinical narrative datasets: the Mental Health Status dataset and the Medical Drugs Sentiment Analysis dataset. The empirical study illustrate that the proposed architecture outperforms centralized learning across both the datasets for Emotion Recognition task. Further, we integrated the proposed FDL framework with a Secure Aggregation environment for enhanced security. The study reports that the model’s performance is also consistent with secured aggregation, ensuring data privacy.

title: Toward Multimodal Privacy in XR: Design and Evaluation of Composite Privatization Methods for Gaze and Body Tracking Data
authors: Azim Ibragimov, Ethan Wilson, Kevin R. B. Butler, Eakta Jain
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: As extended reality (XR) systems become increasingly immersive and sensor-rich, they enable the collection of behavioral signals such as eye and body telemetry. These signals support personalized and responsive experiences and may also contain unique patterns that can be linked back to individuals. However, privacy mechanisms that naively pair unimodal mechanisms (e.g., independently apply privacy mechanisms for eye and body privatization) are often ineffective at preventing re-identification in practice. In this work, we systematically evaluate real-time privacy mechanisms for XR, both individually and in pair, across eye and body modalities. We assess privacy through re-identification rates and evaluate utility using numerical performance thresholds derived from existing literature to ensure real-time interaction requirements are met. We evaluated four eye and ten body mechanisms across multiple datasets, comprising up to 407 participants. Our results show that when carefully paired, multimodal mechanisms reduce re-identification rate from 80.3% to 26.3% in casual XR applications (e.g., VRChat and Job Simulator) and from 84.8% to 26.1% in competitive XR applications (e.g., Beat Saber and Synth Riders), all while maintaining acceptable performance based on established thresholds. To facilitate adoption, we additionally release XR Privacy SDK, an open-source toolkit enabling developers to integrate the privacy mechanisms into XR applications for real-time use. These findings underscore the potential of modality-specific and context-aware privacy strategies for protecting behavioral data in XR environments.

title: LocoScooter: Designing a Stationary Scooter-Based Locomotion System for Navigation in Virtual Reality
authors: Wei He, Xiang Li, Per Ola Kristensson, Ge Lin Kan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Virtual locomotion remains a challenge in VR, especially in space-limited environments where room-scale walking is impractical. We present LocoScooter, a low-cost, deployable locomotion interface combining foot-sliding on a compact treadmill with handlebar steering inspired by scooter riding. Built from commodity hardware, it supports embodied navigation through familiar, physically engaging movement. In a within-subject study (N = 14), LocoScooter significantly improved immersion, enjoyment, and bodily involvement over joystick navigation, while maintaining comparable efficiency and usability. Despite higher physical demand, users did not report increased fatigue, suggesting familiar movements can enrich VR navigation.

title: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)

title: Hybrid ANN PV Power Forecasting with Multi-Decomposition: Tindouf Case Study
authors: Soundous Sekayar, Khaled Ferkous, Mawloud Guermoui, Fatima Zohra Oulad Laid, Abderahmane Bellaour
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This work proposes a hybrid framework for short and medium-term forecasting up to the one-day-ahead horizon of photovoltaic (PV) power generation. Unlike the common approach in hybrid models which often relies on combining only one or two decomposition techniques, the proposed hybrid model ANN integrates three advanced decomposition techniques Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD), Swarm Decomposition (SWD), and Iterative Filtering (IF). with artificial neural networks (ANN) to enhancing signal clarity and capturing various underlying dynamic patterns. The performance of the proposed model was benchmarked against its counterparts with individual decompositions, to clearly demonstrate its superiority and ability to capture nonlinear patterns. Half-hourly data recorded in the Tindouf region of Algeria over a three-year period (2022-2024) were used. The models were evaluated across multiple forecasting horizons <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{H}+1, \mathrm{H}+6, \mathrm{H}+24, \mathrm{H}+48)$</tex> using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex>, RMSE, and nRMSE as performance metrics. The results showed very high predictive accuracy, with the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{\mathrm{2}}$</tex> value exceeding 98.8% across all horizons, while the nRMSE value remained below 1 % even at the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{H}+2 4$</tex> horizon (12-hour forecast). It did not exceed 6.7091 % at the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{H}+\text{4 8}$</tex> horizon (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 4}$</tex>-hour forecast)

title: Fast and Scalable Three-Phase Voltage Sensitivity Method for Unbalanced Distribution Networks
authors: Cristhian G. Da R. De Oliveira, Wandry R. Faria, Benvindo R. Pereira, João B. A. London
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Voltage control in modern distribution systems (DSs) has been increasingly challenged by the high penetration of distributed energy resources (DERs), unbalanced three-phase topologies, and voltage-sensitive loads. Traditional voltage estimation approaches, based on repeated power flow simulations or simplified single-phase models, are often computationally intensive or insufficiently accurate for practical applications. This paper presents a novel three-phase voltage sensitivity analysis (VSA) methodology that preserves the full Newton-Raphson power flowformulation while explicitly incorporating voltage-dependent ZIP load models. The framework handles arbitrary load configurations, unbalanced multi-phase feeders, and DERs operating at any power factor, enabling fast and accurate voltage estimation across a wide range of operating conditions. Additionally, a new Maximum Admissible Injection (MAI) index is introduced to quantify the DER hosting capacity in multi-phase unbalanced networks. The method naturally extends to single-, two-, and three-phase injections and accounts for load-model variability, mitigating systematic errors observed in simplified sensitivity formulations. Comparative analyses showed that the proposed method achieves voltage estimation errors below 0.02% and 0.2% for a 200% power excursion on IEEE 37- and 123-node feeders, respectively, while providing a 1.77×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sup> computational speedup compared to full power flow simulations on the 123-node system. Additional validation using a real Brazilian distribution feeder further confirmed the scalability and robustness of the approach, with very low voltage estimation errors and computational speed-up factors on the order of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7</sup> when compared to repeated power-flow simulations. The results demonstrate the suitability of the approach for real-time operational assessments, large-scale planning studies, and hosting capacity assessments in modern smart grids.

title: Lightweight Local Transformer PointPillars: A 3-D Object Detection Framework for Autonomous Driving
authors: Xiting Peng, Tingkai Zhang, Xiaoyu Zhang, Mianxiong Dong, Kaoru Ota
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: 3-D object detection from Light Detection and Ranging point clouds is a critical task for autonomous driving systems. While recent voxel-based methods (e.g., VoxelNet and PointPillars) have achieved promising performance, they face two critical limitations in practical deployment: 1) the unstructured nature of point clouds hinders contextual feature extraction in complex driving scenarios; and 2) the high computational demands of 3-D convolutions prevent real-time inference on resource-constrained edge devices. To address these challenges, we propose lightweight local transformer PointPillars (LLTP), a lightweight 3-D detection framework that integrates local transformer encoding with a MobileNet-inspired architecture. Our approach introduces two key innovations: first, a columnar self-attention encoder models intra-pillar contextual relationships through multihead attention, enhancing spatial semantics preservation while improving feature representation. Second, a depthwise separable convolutional backbone with adaptive channel attention reduces parameters by decoupling spatial and channelwise operations. Experimental results demonstrate LLTP’s superiority over existing methods—achieving a 75.7% parameter reduction (from 4.9M to 1.2M + 4.2k) compared to PointPillars while improving mAP by 3.48% on Karlsruhe Institute of Technology and Toyota Technological Institute moderate difficulty tasks. The framework also achieves 3.7% improvement on nuScenes benchmarks and real-time inference at 23 FPS. Notably, LLTP requires only 11% of the computational cost of conventional 3-D convolutions, outperforming state-of-the-art methods [e.g., sparsely embedded convolutional detection (Second) and structure-aware single-stage 3-D detection (SA-SSD)] in parameter efficiency while maintaining competitive detection accuracy. These advancements enable robust deployment on embedded systems without compromising performance in complex driving environments.

title: DiffTile: High-Resolution Periodic Pattern Generation Via Splice, Focus and Enhance_supp1-3676838.pdf
authors: Shuai Yang
publish: N/A
abstract: High-resolution periodic patterns are widely used in the printing and dyeing industry. However, the manual design of such periodic patterns has considerable time and manpower costs. Recent advances in powerful image generation models, which are represented by latent diffusion models (LDMs), provide an automated solution. However, owing to the inherent randomness of the generation process and the computational complexity of LDMs, generating strictly periodic and highresolution patterns is still challenging. In this paper, a novel text-driven framework for high-resolution periodic pattern generation is proposed. For periodic pattern generation, we propose a training-free Splice-and-Focus mechanism, which constrains latent features and modifies the attention mechanism to generate natural and strictly periodic patterns. On this basis, we provide two types of generation modes: direct periodic pattern generation and periodization of arbitrary existing patterns. For high-resolution pattern generation, we propose a ReLife pipeline that integrates superresolution and image detail enhancement to improve pattern resolution while removing generative artifacts and distortions. Furthermore, for highly nonstationary patterns, we propose a compositional generation framework to design complicated patterns interactively. The experimental results show that our framework can generate high-quality patterns that meet the strict standards of the printing and dyeing industry.

title: General Algebraic Modulation Scheme for Single-Stage Multi-Port Inverters
authors: Yanjia Yu, Dehong Zhou, Xin Liu, Yu Zeng, Zewei Shen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: The single-stage multi-port inverter (SSMPI) provides a cost-effective solution for multi-source systems thanks to its compact design that eliminates the need for additional dc/dc converters. However, the modulation scheme for SSMPI is challenging to design, because the degrees of freedom (DoFs) provided by conventional carrier-based or space-vector frameworks are insufficient for synthesizing ac voltage and distributing power among all dc ports simultaneously, especially when the number of ports is large. To bridge this research gap, this paper proposes a general algebraic modulation scheme that provides sufficient DoFs for simultaneously achieving ac voltage synthesis and power distribution, regardless of the number of ports. In the proposed scheme, the modulation design is formulated as a straightforward solution process for algebraic equations from a switching-duty-cycle perspective, allowing the available DoFs to expand autonomously as the variable space increases. Based on this concept, the modulation implementation is further carried out using a two-step method, where the duty cycle differences are first solved to guarantee power distribution performance, and the volt-second balance constraint is then calculated for ac voltage synthesis, providing the advantages of easy implementation and low computational burden. Finally, the effectiveness and advantages of the proposed scheme are verified through simulation and experimental results.

title: Data-Driven Modeling of High-Resolution Residential Load Profiles Using Low-Resolution Smart Meter Measurements
authors: Jiyu Wang, Xiangqi Zhu, Fei Ding, Keyang Xuan, Barry Mather
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: Accurate and high-resolution residential load profiles are essential for power system modeling, demand response planning, and effective grid operation. As the energy sector moves towards a more actively managed distribution system, the ability to understand residential energy consumption at a minute-by-minute scale becomes increasingly critical. High-resolution load profiles provide key insights into demand patterns and user behavior, enabling grid operators to design more effective energy solutions; however, residential load measurements in the field are typically recorded at low resolutions, such as 15-60 minutes, which makes it hard to study the characteristics of different residential customers. This paper addresses these challenges by introducing a data-driven approach to generate realistic, high-resolution residential load profiles based on lowre-solution measurements and weather information. The proposed method retains the key features of the actual residential load measurements while offering appliance-level energy consumption details for each residential building. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed load profile generator, proving its capability to support utilities in optimizing residential energy management and ensuring a more reliable and resilient grid.

title: From Fuzzy Global to Clear Local: A Focus and Super-Resolution Guided Tiny Target Detection Method for Full-Scene Images
authors: Yucong He, Gui Gao, Zhenghuan Xia, Dunyun He, Gang Yang, Xi Zhang, Gaosheng Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Detecting tiny targets, a fundamental satellite observation task, always suffers from information loss, additional computation costs, and imbalance samples in full-scene imagery. However, generic methods rely on complex deep neural networks to traverse every region of the image, irrespective of the non-uniform target distribution, severely hindering the detecting accuracy and leading to a heavy computation burden. This paper proposes a feature focusing super-resolution small target detection network (FFSSTD-Net), alleviating the above three problems. First, the proposed convolution focus detection module (CFD) uses a lightweight full-convolutional network query mechanism to adaptively search the regions with targets, eliminating unnecessary computation costs and noise from the background. Then, the proposed feature super-resolution (FSR) module guides the backbone network enhancing the representation of small target objects in high-dimensional features through an auxiliary branch to achieve a balanced optimization, alleviating the information loss caused by sub-sampling operations. Experimental results demonstrate that the FFSSTD-Net outperforms state-of-the-art generic methods, improving mAP and Fps by at least 3.14% and 10%, respectively. Meanwhile, ablation experiments indicate that the CFD and FSR modules enhance detection accuracy while accelerating full-scene image processing.

title: 0.5V BD-MIMO DDTA based Universal Multimode Biquad Filter
authors: Chandra Shankar, Surekha Khened, B Karunakara Rai, F. Khateb
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: A novel low-voltage universal mixed-mode filter that makes use of a multiple input/output differential difference transconductance amplifier (MIMO-DDTA) is presented in this study. A multiple-input bulk-driven PMOS transistor (MI-BD-PMOST) technique is used to actualise the DDTA's multiple inputs. This technique preserves a single differential pair, resulting in a simple structure with low power usage. All grounded passive components—two capacitors and three resistors—as well as 3-DDTA as active elements are used in the proposed filter. The proposed filter can offer five common filtering functions, i.e., low-pass (LP), high-pass (HP), band-pass (BP), band-reject (BR), and all-pass (AP) in four modes: voltage (VM), current (CM), transadmittance (TAM), and transimpedance (TIM) in a single topology. This mixed-mode filter offer total 71 responses at different ports. Additionally, the proposed filter does not require any buffer circuit, matching conditions, and any scaled or inverted input signals. A setting current can be used to electronically control the pole frequency of all filtering functions. The filter consumed 19.12 μW of power, with a voltage supply of ±0.25 V, and is suitable for biomedical and sensor applications. Using 90 nm CMOS technology, the proposed filter was simulated in the Cadence Virtuoso Tool.

title: Towards Compact Representations: A Region-based Logit Distillation Framework for Edge-Friendly Semantic Segmentation
authors: Xuyi Yu, Yuqi Li, Yinghai Zhao, Huipeng Zhang, Kuizhi Mei
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Semantic segmentation enables fine-grained scene understanding for Internet of Things (IoT) applications such as intelligent transportation and UAV-based monitoring. However, high-resolution segmentation models impose substantial memory and computational demands, making them impractical for resource-constrained IoT edge devices. Knowledge distillation (KD) transfers knowledge from a large teacher model to a compact student model. Yet, conventional logit distillation in segmentation suffers from two limitations: (1) dense pixel-wise alignment introduces spatial redundancy, and (2) erroneous teacher predictions propagate to the student, degrading reliability. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Region-based Logit Distillation framework (RLD). Instead of distilling all logits, RLD summarizes logit distributions within categories to reduce redundancy. To avoid interference from incorrect logits, we isolate their influence using label guidance, then summarize logits for a batch of samples via intraclass similarity. Teacher-guided singular value decomposition (SVD) facilitates logit dimensionality reduction. RLD retains only a small set of principal logits, for example 19 for Cityscapes, achieving performance comparable to dense distillation. Combined with classic distillation baselines, RLD further achieves state-of-the-art results: 77.80% mIoU on Cityscapes and 69.90% on CamVid. Deployed on an NVIDIA Jetson Xavier NX, our distilled model achieves 46.78 FPS at 14.05 W in FP16 and 85.12 FPS at 7.73 W with INT8 quantization, demonstrating real-time capability and high energy efficiency for edge IoT applications.

title: Adaptive Collision Detection via Impulse-Momentum Theorem for Safe Sensorless Physical Human-Robot Interaction
authors: Hongzhe Shi, Chao Ye, Chenlu Liu, Weiyang Lin
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Collision detection is critical for safe physical human-robot interaction (pHRI). The generalized momentum observer (GMO) is a prevalent sensorless method, estimating momentum deviations to detect collisions. However, its reliance on iterative solutions and static thresholds severely limits operational efficiency and dynamic adaptability, leading to reduced sensitivity and accuracy in complex collision scenarios. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes an impulse-based dynamic threshold generalized momentum observer (IDT-GMO)for adaptive sensorless collision detection. Key innovations include: (i) An impulse-based dynamic threshold mechanism, leveraging the impulse-momentum theorem, enabling adaptive collision detection with enhanced sensitivity and accuracy; (ii) A closed-form analytical solution, formulated through Lagrangian mechanics and Lie group theory, eliminating iterative computation and achieving 45% faster processing than conventional GMO. Experimental validation across soft contact, rigid impact, and multi-contact scenarios confirms that IDT-GMO achieves superior detection sensitivity and accuracy compared to existing methods. Thus, IDT-GMO exhibits significant potential for applications ranging from delicate contact tasks to collision-prone environments.

title: Extending the field of view in modulation-based X-ray phase microtomography_supp1-3676631.mp4

title: Predicting Cement Strength as Probability Density: Resolving Partial Observability and Sample Scarcity for Industrial Quality Control_supp1-3673478.pdf
authors: Lin Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Compressive strength is the critical quality metric in industrial cement production, yet conventional assessment relies on destructive 28-day tests or inaccurate accelerated methods, hindering timely quality control. These methods inherently cause sample scarcity (only 52–195 batches/year/plant) due to the 28-day curing requirement, labor-intensive specimen preparation, and destructive testing protocols—rendering them insufficient for data-driven models requiring large samples. Concurrently, plants face partial observability with arbitrary critical features (e.g., chemical composition) missing due to sensor limitations and prohibitive assay costs. While machine learning models accelerate prediction, their deterministic single-value outputs fail to infer missing features under partial observability and require extensive samples unattainable under scarcity, causing significant compliance risks and overdesign costs. We resolve these by reframing strength prediction as a probability density estimation task, replacing single-value estimates with full probability densities. Our proposed cement strength density estimator (CSDE): first, outputs strength as Gaussian mixture densities, second, resolves partial observability via latent variable inference, and third, overcomes sample scarcity through likelihood optimization. Validated on industrial data under partial observability and scarcity, CSDE exhibits only 2.78% MAE degradation under 82% feature masking (versus 3.73% for MLPs) and achieves 97% of strengths within 95% confidence intervals. By converting densities into compliance metrics [P(strength $\geq$ critical value)], CSDE flags high-risk batches, reduces overdesign costs, and supports timely quality interventions. The framework is extensible to flexural/tensile strength prediction via input redefinition.

title: A 9b 3-7.5GHz 2.53LSB-INL High Linearity Phase Interpolator with CMOS-Signal-Targeted Calibration in 65nm for High-Speed Data Links
authors: Zexin Su, Junyan Zhu, Chang Liu, Tao Hu, Yao Liu, Jixin Chen, Franco Maloberti, Bo Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems II: Express Briefs
