abstract: In this work, a high-linearity 8-phase CMOS-targeted-calibration phase interpolator (PI) is proposed. In classical current-mode logic (CML) PIs, improvement efforts primarily focus on correcting the CML signal phase error, while neglecting a significant transfer phase error that occurs when converting the CML signal to the CMOS signal, resulting from the amplitude differences of CML phases and the threshold variation of the transfer circuit. To address this problem, a CMOS-signal-targeted calibration technique is proposed, which considers both error sources: the CML inherent phase error and the transfer error from CML to CMOS. The phase error will be extracted directly from the final CMOS signal, and the calibration will be implemented according to this total phase error. In the proposed calibration scheme, the specific CMOS phases (22.5° phase group) are adopted as phase references, which have identical CML amplitudes before CMOS to avoid the transfer error; and other CMOS phases will be calibrated based on the references. Fabricated in a 65nm CMOS process, the chip achieves an integral non-linearity (INL) of less than 2.53 LSB and a differential non-linearity (DNL) of less than 0.77 LSB with 9 bits resolution across a frequency range of 3 to 7.5 GHz.

title: It’s about Time
authors: Vinton G. Cerf
publish: Communications of the ACM
abstract: None

title: A Novel Maize Classification Dataset for Starch Industry in Uncontrolled Environment: Using Data-Centric Approach
authors: Narinder Pal Singh, Rattan Pal Singh Rana
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Maize production is of utmost importance for the starch industry, food processing, and the consumption of staple food. Manual classification techniques that are commonly employed by untrained personnel are prone to yielding poor classification results, which may have an adverse impact on revenue realization, production turnover, and quality. Most classification systems based on artificial intelligence (AI) require a large dataset to produce effective results. Most datasets undergo preparation in controlled lab conditions, avoiding real-world scenarios that could lead to biased classification results. Therefore, a new, smaller, balanced, clean dataset is a fundamental requirement to build robust AI models for real-time conditions. This article presents a novel dataset prepared using a data-centric approach, which is a concise, smaller, feature-oriented, structured, and diverse dataset obtained in real-time conditions. The proposed maize dataset is captured under real-time uncontrolled conditions without constant lab conditions and contains 5460 unique images structured into three classification classes. Using a data-centric approach, the dataset is extended with augmented image counts to 174720. The dataset is focused on providing a more accurate real-time representation of maize classification and facilitating the development of robust artificial intelligence models. The proposed dataset can be extended to multiple variants for various algorithms to compare and analyze prediction performance.

title: Detection of Papaya Ringspot Disease Using Segmentation Neural Networks in Precision Agriculture
authors: M. Sangeetha, A Bhagyalakshmi, A. Murugalakshmi, G. Sheeba, S. Rukmani Devi, Nirmal M. D
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Papaya production is an important part of Indian agriculture, but it is often threatened by diseases such as papaya ringspot virus (PRSV), which can seriously affect yields and crop sustainability. This study proposes a method to detect papaya ringspot disease symptoms using aerial images acquired by unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) and deep learning (DL) segmentation models. High-resolution RGB images were provided by a commercial farm in Tamil Nadu, India, were annotated by agronomists to create ground truth data. This study combined multiple segmentation networks (U Net, PSPNet, LinkNet, and FCN) with VGG16 based on spine transfer learning. The dataset contains 40 images, divided into training and test sets. The results show that the combination of U-Net with VGG16 (UNETVGG) achieved the highest accuracy (98%) and F1 score (0.99) by effectively segmenting the diseased area. However, some issues were also found, such as false positives, overfitting of the model on some features, and environmental variables. This study shows that deep learning combined with segmentation techniques can help early detection of PRSV in papaya plants, thereby reducing the need for manual inspection and supporting precision agriculture initiatives.

title: Advancing Weed Detection in Cotton with a Self-Attention Vision Transformer
authors: Ritika, Savita Sheoran
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Weeds pose a persistent challenge to cotton cultivation by reducing crop yields and increasing input costs through competition for water, nutrients, and sunlight. Traditional weed management practices such as manual removal and herbicide spraying, are not only labor-intensive and costly but also contribute to long-term environmental concerns. Recent advances in Deep Learning (DL), particularly with Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) have enabled automated and highly accurate weed detection in agricultural systems. However, CNN often exhibit limitations in handling field variability, overlapping cropweed patterns, and complex backgrounds. The objective of this study is to evaluate the effectiveness of Vision Transformers (ViT) compared to conventional CNN architectures for automated weed detection in cotton fields. To achieve this, three CNN modelsResNet50, VGG16, and VGG19were benchmarked against a fine-tuned pretrained ViT using a cottonweed image dataset due to well-established, widely used CNN architectures representing both classical and advanced designs. Experimental results demonstrate that the ViT achieved superior performance with an overall accuracy of 97.24%, precision of 86.9 %, recall of 97.3%, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score of 91.9%, while significantly reducing false negatives for cotton plants. The study reports comprehensive evaluation metrics, including overall accuracy, F1-score, recall, confusion matrix for robust performance assessment. In comparison, CNN-based models exhibited lower robustness under challenging field conditions. The novelty of this work lies in applying transformer-based architectures for weed detection in cotton agriculture, a domain previously dominated by CNN. Unlike CNN, ViT leverages self-attention to capture long-range spatial dependencies, enabling enhanced discrimination between cotton and weeds in scenarios involving overlapping vegetation, variable lighting, and cluttered field imagery. These findings advance state-of-the-art weed detection, providing a scalable, field-ready, and environmentally sustainable solution that minimizes herbicide dependency and supports precision cotton farming.

title: Federated Learning for Cyber-Physical System Security: A Survey on AI-Based Threat Detection Techniques
authors: Swetha C B, Chetan J Shelke, Mano Paul P
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Modern infrastructure relies heavily on CyberPhysical Systems (CPS), which merge computing capabilities with real-world physical operations in domains such as healthcare, energy, industry, and transportation. The increasing interconnection among these systems exposes them to diverse and advanced forms of cyber threats. Ensuring the security of CPS is critical to maintaining system reliability, data privacy and real-time operational safety. This study compiles and analyzes recent developments in cyber-attack detection strategies for CyberPhysical Systems (CPS), with particular emphasis on approaches based on Machine Learning (ML), Deep Learning (DL), and Federated Learning (FL). The review covers research contributions published between 2021 and 2024, sourced from prominent academic repositories. The findings indicate that while ML and DL models offer effective detection mechanisms, Federated Learning stands out by achieving high detection accuracy while preserving data privacy across distributed environments. This review outlines prominent algorithms, benchmark datasets, and evaluation metrics, concluding with unresolved issues and future research pathways in securing cyber-physical systems.

title: BillBot: A Smart RFID-Based Automated Billing System for Retail and Smart Carts
authors: Ankit Kumar, Vishnu Kumar, Sanjay Kumar Soni
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: In modern retail environments, enhancing customer convenience and reducing checkout delays are key priorities. This paper introduces BillBot, an intelligent and affordable billing system that leverages RFID technology and Bluetooth communication to enable self-service billing. Designed around the Arduino Uno microcontroller, the system incorporates an MFRC522 RFID module, HC-05 Bluetooth, LEDs, Keypad, a $16 \times 2$ I2C LCD display, and a set of user input switches for adding or removing items from the virtual cart. Each item in the store is tagged with a unique RFID tag. When a customer places a product near the RFID reader, the system automatically recognizes it, displays its details along with the price on the LCD screen, and updates the total bill accordingly. What makes BillBot distinctive is its interactive and user-friendly interface. If a customer wishes to remove an item, they can simply scan it again using the RFID reader, and the item is subtracted from the cart total. The buzzer module provides real-time audio feedback during each transaction. This approach eliminates the need for humanoperated billing counters, significantly reducing queue times and human error in retail transactions. With Bluetooth integration, the system is adaptable for mobile-based future expansions such as remote billing or e-receipt generation. The prototype was tested in a simulated retail setup and demonstrated high accuracy, reliability, and ease of use. BillBot serves as a step toward the future of smart retail automation, offering a cashless, queue-free, and contactless billing experience that empowers customers to manage their shopping independently.

title: Integrating UAV Imaging and Panoptic Segmentation for Accurate Horticultural Crop Detection and Classification
authors: Nagashree Mohan Kumar, Spoorti P Huded, Jagadish S, Muskhan Sindagi, Hebbar R
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The ability to distinguish and classify horticultural crops correctly is vital for yield prediction and sustainable farm management. This paper utilizes panoptic segmentation employing very high-resolution unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) RGB imagery to enhance tree-crop classification for assessment in heterogeneous horticultural crops. The Horticultural Research and Extension Centre (HREC) in Karnataka state, Kolar District, is home to multiple cashew and mango plantations, as well as other vegetation. UAV data collected was processed using a deep learning segmentation framework, MaXDeepLab, which automates segmentation of background objects (e.g., soil, vegetation, and built-up classes) as well as individual tree crowns. The proposed method produced a Panoptic Quality (PQ) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 2. 3 \%}$</tex>, Semantic Accuracy (SA) of 82.5% and 80.1% Recognition Quality (RQ) values, which outperformed traditional segmentation methods. Ground-truth validation tests with independent observers indicated a high level of reliability and accuracy for defining closely spaced favourable tree crowns and non-crown (or background) classes, with a Kappa coefficient of 0.89 and an overall accuracy of 91.12%. This framework and supporting research indicate a high level of reliability and accuracy to address crop classification and orchard monitoring, resulting in improved yield estimation, which has significant potential for application to broader agricultural context.

title: AI-Powered Multi-Cloud Optimization: Anomaly Detection and Resource Efficiency with SVM and PPO″
authors: Seema Bhatt, Ranu Pandey
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: In this paper, also, we analyze a recent work which combines Anomaly Detection (SVM) and Efficiency Optimization (PPO) in multi-cloud environments. By employing SVM for anomaly detection based on cloud resource usage, with an accuracy of 95.45% and 72.73% in precision and 80.00% in recall. The model shows good performance for identifying outliers even in the presence of class imbalance. We applied PPO to guarantee that resource scheduling, load balancing and task scheduling are updated at real time, and then showed a huge improvement in the decision making about the cost and performance with adaptation. The average and cumulative rewards received during evaluation are a signal to whether the model is effectively handling the dynamic load. SVM offers security by recognizing irregularities, and FFQ optimizes resource allocation for higher operational performance. It is flexible, and a viable solution to aggregate these models to get some intelligent and automatic management for multi cloud.

title: Enhancing Security in IoT Networks by Applying Deep Learning Approaches for Accurate and Efficient Botnet Detection
authors: Jahanvi Dubey, Deepshikha Patel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: IoT (Internet of Things) is spreading so quickly and allows devices to share data in real time, smart surroundings have become extremely intelligent. This advancement not only enhances convenience for users but also enables more efficient resource management across various sectors. As a result, we can expect to see significant improvements in areas such as energy consumption, healthcare, and urban planning. However, this advancement has been accompanied by serious cybersecurity problems, such as botnets, which may be readily compromised to be used in malicious operations like DDoS (Distributed Denial of Service) attacks. Making use of a considered mechanism integrated into CNN-LSTM Hybrid for accurate detection of botnets in IoT environments is proposed here. These are convolutional neural networks to extract spatial features and LSTM networks intended to capture temporal dependencies. With the account of the CICIDS 2017 dataset, the training has resulted in excellent performances: 94.6 % accuracy, 92.1 % precision, 91.8 % recall, 91.9% F1-score, and 96.3% ROC-AUC. Though the architecture attempted to capture anomalies in both space and time, the model does extremely well in detecting botnet traffic with minimal cases of false positives and negatives. However, there are issues, like a certain level of class imbalance and a follow-up on the false negative rates, which would suggest that improvement was still possible through feature engineering and hyperparameter tuning. This study proved that hybrid dimension deep learning models could improve IoT networks and, thus, develop stronger frameworks for cybersecurity.

title: Hierarchical Mapping-Partitioning-Search with Attention-Weighted Communication for UAV Swarms in Search and Rescue Operations
authors: Ashish Ashok Khetal, Helen K. Joy, Rupali Sunil Wagh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: UAV swarm Search and Rescue (SAR) operations demand intelligent coordination to function efficiently in unfamiliar terrains while maintaining communication under bandwidth limitations. To address this, we propose a Hierarchical Mapping-Partitioning-Search (HMPS) framework that combines quadtree-based adaptive partitioning of the search area with deep reinforcement learning for region selection, together with an Attention-Weighted Flooding (AWF) communication protocol to enhance swarm coordination. The HMPS framework adapts search granularity to uncertainty and obstacle density, uses a Deep Q-Network (DQN) to learn a region-selection policy, and employs a lightweight local coverage planner to improve exploration efficiency. The AWF protocol prioritizes message relays based on content and link quality, reducing bandwidth while preserving essential information flow. This paper presents HMPS as a practical option for autonomous swarm SAR operations, and reports encouraging preliminary results in GPS-denied terrains.

title: Statistical Analysis of Energy-Efficient Optimization AI, ML, DL Based Models for IoT Networks-The Review
authors: Pallavi Charde Lonkar, Uma Patel Thakur
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The rapid expansion of the Internet of Things (IoT) networks has created an importance and urgent need for energy-efficient mechanisms that can function effectively in dynamic, heterogeneous, and resource-constrained environments. Various computational and intelligent approaches have been applied to optimize energy consumption across different IoT layers. However, a structured and comparative synthesis of these energy optimization methods is still lacking in the current research landscape. This paper shows a detailed analytical review of 25 recent studies that focus on techniques for enhancing energy efficiency in IoT networks. The reviewed works emphasize performance metrics such as Packet Delivery Ratio (PDR), reliability, computational complexity, energy efficiency, and system overheads. The analysis in the paper combines both qualitative and quantitative assessments to derive statistical trends and establish benchmarks among methods, including federated learning, biologically inspired optimization, reinforcement learning, and clustering-based techniques. The comparative and empirical review results highlight high-impact models and also research challenges are identified related to mobility, convergence, and infrastructure limitations. Furthermore, the study provides insights into the scalability, adaptability, and computational balance of various models. Overall, this review aims to support researchers and system designers in selecting suitable algorithmic frameworks for developing energy-aware and performance-efficient IoT systems.

title: Machine Learning-Based Resource Management for Internet of Vehicles in 6G Networks Using the Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) Model
authors: Pramod Kumar P, V Thirupathi, K Sagar, Srinivas Madira, J Srinivas, Nampally Vijaay Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: The sixth-generation (6G) communication technologies are transforming the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) by enabling ultra-low latency, high reliability, and massive connectivity for autonomous and intelligent transportation systems. However, efficient resource management remains a critical challenge due to dynamic vehicular mobility, varying network conditions, and the need for energy-efficient communication. This study proposes a Soft Actor-Critic (SAC)-based deep reinforcement learning framework for intelligent, continuous, and adaptive resource allocation in 6G-enabled IoV environments. The model leverages entropy-regularized policy optimization and twin-critic networks to achieve stable convergence and robust decision-making under highly dynamic vehicular conditions. Experimental evaluation in a 6G vehicular network scenario demonstrates that the proposed SAC model significantly reduces latency, enhances throughput, and improves energy consumption compared with existing DRL models such as DDPG, PPO, and DQN. Achieving nearly 90% overall system performance, the model provides real-time adaptability, scalability, and sustainability for next-generation autonomous 6G-IoV communication networks.

title: Hybrid Multi-Model Stock Price Forecasting Using Technical, Fundamental, and Statistical Learning
authors: Napolean, Prasad Prakash Olekar, Ramesh Chigarer, Puneeth Kumar B R, Rithish Kumar Chandrahas Gouda
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Forecasting stock prices is difficult due to the non-linear, volatile, and sensitive nature of financial markets that react to investor sentiment. In addition, most single model forecasts cannot maintain stable accuracy across varying market conditions. To tackle this issue, this paper proposes a Hybrid Multi-Model Stock Forecasting Framework. This Framework uses a combination of three complementary prediction models: Linear Regression, Technical Analysis Forecasting, and Fundamental Analysis Forecasting. The Framework uses a combination of historical OHLCV (open, high, low, closed, volume) data, computed trend indicators, momentum indicators, and financial attributes for a company to create a unified feature vector. For each stock, each Model develops an independent forecast; these forecasts are merged using a weighted fusion approach to enhance the Models’ stability, interpretability, and robustness. Test results from applying the Hybrid Model to multiple stock tickers (Tesla, Microsoft, LIC, Reliance, and BNY Mellon) indicate that the Hybrid Model consistently outperforms each of the Models separately with respect to MAE, RMSE, and MAPE metrics. The key innovation of this study is the ability to integrate three prediction methodologies (statistical, technical, and fundamental) into a single Decision Support System (DSS), providing a more robust and reliable solution for Investors, Analysts, and Financial Institutions.

title: From Syntax to Semantics: AI Assisted Computational Linguistics in the Era of Large Computational Language Models
authors: Kiran Kumar Pappula, Gowtham Reddy Enjam, Sunil Anasuri
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The large language computational methods have brought about significant changes in the area of linguistics. The previous systems statically followed the rules of grammar to comprehend the connotation of definition and context in words. The proposed method focuses a novel method that intentions to enhance the depth, accuracy and sense of words through understanding of natural language. The available methods follow patterns of words in sentences and patterns. Although, it is helpful, the traditional methods struggle to find the meanings deeply, such as structure, language style, and shifts of word context. To overcome these challenges, the proposed method joins tasks-based definition alterations and pre-trained largescale language methods. The novel method follows two phases: initial phase placed efforts to organize the structure of sentence, describing words and in the following phase it focused to present definition fine-tunes such as improving sense of word, inference of the word context, and sentiment explanation. The method combines contrastive learning to find relationships of subtle definitions in large and multifaceted sentences. Using datasets, experimentations were directed. The datasets of GLUE and SamEval were used along with similarity of sentence, question and answering based on context and entailment of text. The outcomes of proposed method are F1-score, accuracy and scores of BLEU as well. Results showed a 6-13% than traditional works specifically in handling of reasoning of multi-sentence, clarification of context. The enhanced method works perfectly across tasks and languages suits in platforms of multilingual and several language applications, language assistants and customer care service centres.

title: A Spatio-Temporal Gated Graph Attention Network with Structural Constraints
authors: Ze Zhao, Mingyan Jiang, Feng Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: To address the challenges of high computational complexity and insufficient spatiotemporal feature fusion in traditional spatiotemporal models, this paper proposes an optimized approach for efficiently capturing spatiotemporal dependencies. Firstly, A hierarchical processing architecture is designed, incorporating a node-level folding strategy to reduce the temporal modeling complexity of LSTM. Additionally, a gated feature alignment mechanism is introduced to enable smooth transitions between spatial and temporal features. Then a structurally constrained graph attention mechanism is proposed. By applying an adjacency matrix mask and a negative infinity padding strategy, the model maintains the flexibility of attention while suppressing interference from invalid connections. Next, a dynamic dimension adaptation module is developed, leveraging a learnable linear transformation layer to map between the latent spaces of the LSTM and the Graph Attention Network (GAT), thereby resolving the dimensional mismatch between spatial and temporal features. This architecture offers a novel perspective for spatiotemporal modeling, particularly suited for real-time traffic forecasting in edge computing environments. Experiments on the PeMS dataset demonstrate that the proposed model significantly outperforms baseline methods in terms of MAE and RMSE. Furthermore, validation experiments on the SDU-QIT end milling tool wear dataset highlight its potential for predictive maintenance and precision measurement applications.

title: A Unified NLP Framework for Scalable Phishing Detection Across AI Systems
authors: B. Jaya Prakash Narayana, P. Shoyub Khan, R.V. Krishna Sai, Thirunavukkarasan M
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Natural Language Processing (NLP) is sort of the MVP of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in that once machines are made to the correct above-board understand, interpret, and write human language properly, it provides limitless opportunities to ensure machines are intelligently doing the right thing. Recent advances such as deep learning systems, attention devices, and transformer systems have taken the situational knowledge and real-time decision making in NLP with a grave concern. Nonetheless, it still has a number of unresolved challenges including the large computational cost, limited language coverage, portability across multiple languages, ethical considerations, and scalability. The solution to these issues needs enhanced algorithms, multilingual solutions, good data privacy practices and fairness-conscious AI models. This paper presents the introduction of a natural language processing (NLP) platform in a modular and scalable fashion, and the intended functions of the platform are to handle the whole pipeline of data ingestion, preprocessing, feature extraction, model training, inference, and deployment professionally. However, unlike their works in which single models are assessed individually, our framework goes further to unify traditional, sequential, and transformer-based methods into a single unified architecture, which, in turn, allows making systematic performance comparisons under the same conditions. A case study of the necessity of availability of the framework in phishing email detection demonstrates running experiments of TF-IDF with Logistic Regression, LSTM, and RoBERTa models on an annually updated, multi-source phishing dataset. This finding of the experiment has been consistent with the transformer-based models that have great capabilities of detection. Far beyond this we mention the flexibility of the framework to the variations in the phishing scenario, so a preliminary phase of practical implementation and future research extensions can be defined.

title: Predictive Modeling for Image Classification of Indian Medicinal Flowers
authors: Rishit Mahapatra, Sapna R
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The recognition and categorization of Indian Medicinal flowers are mandatory for various applications of herbal medicine, biodiversity control measures, and automated botanical systems. The corresponding image classification framework is developed in the presented work. An in-house prepared dataset of 28 classes of Indian medicinal flowers, with 100-120 images per flower class, was used. The CNN models like VGG16, ResNet50, InceptionV3, and a simple SVM classifier have been used. OpenCV methods, which were based on openCV, were used in the process of preprocessing and image reduction in OpenCV. The models have been evaluated on the basis of the accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, training/testing time, and resource efficiency in making the computation. In deep learning models, InceptionV3 and ResNet50 demonstrated good accuracy and feature extraction, while VGG16 was accurate and computationally manageable. The basic SVM model, despite being lightweight, performed worse than deep learning models. It has been demonstrated that deep CNN-based architectures, particularly InceptionV3, are effective in accurately classifying Indian medicinal flowers, which will enable the development of large-scale plant recognition tools for real-life applications.

title: Intelligent Disease Detection for Crops and Nutrient Optimizer
authors: Athivelly Vrishank, Darla Nehal, Gundagoni Deepika, M. Naresh Kumar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Early detection and management of crop diseases has a significant impact on agricultural productivity. Significant crop losses and ineffective fertilizer use result from farmers' manual monitoring, which is frequently erratic, slow, or impractical over wide areas. In order to provide small and medium sized farms with a comprehensive precision farming solution, this paper introduces a real-time, rule-based system that automates fertilizer dispensing and crop disease detection. Our system uses a fixed camera module to continuously monitor crop leaves. The project employs a lightweight, rule-based image processing algorithm instead of computationally demanding AI models to recognize outward signs like red, yellow, or orange spots, which are commonly linked to bacterial infections, rust, and leaf blight. A reaction time less than 3 seconds, and a 5% error margin for fertilizer dispensing accuracy. Detection accuracy somewhat declined in lowlight conditions the system was also found to be resilient to common environmental fluctuations.

title: Optimizing the Energy Output of a Piezoelectric Plate Wave-Energy Device in Regions of Variable Nearshore Bathymetry
authors: Kailash Chand Swami, Maheswaran S, Santanu Kumar Dash, Santanu Koley
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: This research examines a piezoelectric wave energy converter (PWEC) device integrated with an impermeable vertical wall placed over variable bathymetry and the optimization of power generation. The boundary element method (BEM) coupled with the finite difference method (FDM) handles the present problem. The study investigates how factors such as plate submergence depth <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d$</tex>, plate length <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$B$</tex>, seabed profiles (types I and II), ripples' amplitude <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$a_{1}$</tex>, and the number of ripples <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m$</tex> affect power generation. Results show that a plate submergence depth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d / h=0.1$</tex> and a plate length of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$B / h=2.0$</tex> yield the maximum power output, and this has been further verified through a genetic-algorithm-based optimization, which identifies these values as the optimal design parameters. Furthermore, the seabed profile significantly impacts the resonant behavior, producing higher power output across a wide range of incident wave frequencies.

title: Analysis of Energy Recovery and Switching Stress in Regenerative and Active Clamp Snubbers for Flyback Converters
authors: R. Ninitha Ashwini, Ila Rai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This study provides a detailed comparison of regenerative snubber and active clamp snubber techniques in flyback converter circuits, focusing on their effects on efficiency, switching stress, and overall reliability. Supported by comprehensive circuit simulations, the paper examines key performance metrics such as energy recovery, thermal performance, and cost implications. Results indicate that the active clamp snubber offers superior energy recycling and lower voltage transients and thus enhances converter efficiency and lifespan. Conversely, the regenerative snubber presents a more cost-effective, less complex option with acceptable performance trade-offs. The findings significantly influence power electronic design optimization, especially for high-power and high-frequency applications, contributing to the development of more efficient and sustainable power conversion systems. This research deepens the understanding of snubber selection, with a notable impact on the advancement of power electronics technology.

title: Navigating the Ethical Frontier: A Framework for Designing Equitable and Effective Generative AI Tutoring Systems
authors: Bui Thanh Khoa
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: This study looks at ethical issues appear as applying artificial intelligence (AI)-generated learning algorithmic tutoring systems. The research investigates educational fairness and equality because AI-based personalized learning systems have become mandatory for all educational institutions at every level. The technology system creates multiple ethical problems which need to be solved. Previous research studies formed the basis for this investigation which employed ethical analysis to develop a framework for AI tutoring systems. The framework operates through fair treatment because it uses transparent open procedures for its operations. Student well-being functions as a vital assessment criterion which exists within the defined assessment framework. The findings pointed out ethical principles should be included in both the design and the use of AI in education. AI systems can use this information to create learning environments which support both effective and equitable education for every student.

title: Bridging Speech and Text Using Multimodal Artificial Intelligence for Next-Gen Language Understanding
authors: Vasanta Kumar Tarra, Arun Kumar Mittapelly, Guru Pramod Rusum
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Speech, body language, and design features are challenging due to lack of learning practices. Conventional multimodal systems process speech, text, and visual cues separately using rigid encoders, which limits contextual understanding and reduces performance in sentiment analysis, transcription, and emotion recognition (ER). Recent approaches use attention-based transformers to model cross-modal relationships, but they often struggle with long-range dependencies and high computational costs. This paper presents a hybrid transformer-based framework that fuses audio, text, icons, body language, and design features through adaptive cross-modal attention to enhance semantic understanding and reduce word error rate (WER). Audio is encoded using Wav2Vec 2.0, text is processed with a transformer, and visual cues such as lip movements and hand shapes are embedded for fusion. A joint loss models cross-modal relationships for stable training. An attention-aware mechanism selects key tokens, and a lightweight gated projection controls feature flow, lowering computation without reducing accuracy. Experiments on CMU-MOSEI, LibriSpeech, and MuST-C demonstrate gains in semantic accuracy (SA), F1-score, ER, and reductions in WER, achieving up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 {\%}}$</tex> improvement in SA and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 5 {\%}}$</tex> drop in WER.

title: Impact of High-Brightness Entangled Photon Pairs on CHSH Inequality Experiment
authors: Jin-Woo Kim, Heonoh Kim, June Koo Kevin Rhee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Quantum Engineering
abstract: Verifying the violation of Bell's inequality is one of the most representative methods to demonstrate that entangled photon pairs prepared in a quantum optics-based system exhibit quantum properties. While experiments on Bell inequality violations have been theoretically well-established and extensively conducted to implement various quantum information technologies in laboratory settings, mathematical modeling for systematically describing the distribution of high-brightness entangled photon pairs in high-loss environments remains an issue that requires further research. As the brightness of the entangled photon pairs increases, the influence of multi-photon effects becomes more significant, leading to a decrease in the CHSH value <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$S$</tex-math></inline-formula> and also a reduction in the standard deviation of the CHSH value <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Delta S$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Therefore, a new analysis of the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$(S-2)/\Delta S$</tex-math></inline-formula> value is required to more precisely confirm the degree of CHSH inequality violation including the reliability of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$S$</tex-math></inline-formula>. In this paper, we propose a mathematical model to predict the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$(S-2)/\Delta S$</tex-math></inline-formula> value as a function of the brightness of the entangled photon pair source, and we also suggest the need to optimize the brightness of this source. Additionally, we provide experimental evidence supporting this model. The experiment confirms that when the mean photon number is <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu =0.026$</tex-math></inline-formula> in an entanglement distribution setup with a total loss of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$-19.03$</tex-math></inline-formula> dB, the CHSH value drops to 2.69, while the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$(S-2)/\Delta S$</tex-math></inline-formula> value increases to 60.95.

title: Copiloting the Copilots for Automated Program Repair
authors: Yuxiang Wei, Chunqiu Steven Xia, Lingming Zhang
publish: Communications of the ACM
abstract: None

title: A Smart Charging Control Considering Charger Efficiency and Battery Temperature Using an Attention-Augmented Multi-Output Neural Network
authors: Mohamed Yasko, Fanghao Tian, Wilmar Martinez, Johan Driesen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Smart charging control of electric vehicles (EVs), considering charger and battery characteristics, is essential for optimizing energy use and power profiles of different EV models. This paper proposes an attention-augmented multi-output neural network (AMONN), trained and validated on a high-resolution experimental dataset from two EV models with 11 kW on-board chargers. Compared with the baseline models, AMONN achieved significantly higher accuracy, reducing MAPE from 28.65% to 0.49% and improving R2 from 0.9712 to 0.9995. Simulation results showed that AMONN, coupled with a greedy optimization (GO) algorithm, outperformed fixed charging profiles (4, 8, and 11 kW), reaching overall efficiencies of 94–95% and reducing energy losses by up to 60%, while considering battery temperature evolution. To validate AMONN GO based charging control in practice, we built an experimental setup with a commercial charging point (CP), the test EV, a CAN data logger, and two laptops for control and acquisition. Tests of AMONN in open-loop (OL), closed-loop (CL), and GO CL modes confirmed its practicality, achieving efficiencies of 91.5–94%. These results demonstrate the potential of AMONN-based smart charging to enhance efficiency, reduce energy losses, and improve battery temperature optimization in real-world EV charging systems.

title: Impacts of Interactions between Grid-forming and Grid-following VSCs on Stability of Hybrid System Connected to Weak Grid
authors: Yunhui Huang, Zheng Fang, Zhenyu He, Dong Wang, Jinrui Tang, Changjun Xie, Keliang Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: This paper investigates the effects of interactions between grid-forming and grid-following Voltage Source Converters (VSCs) on the stability of a hybrid system connected to weak grid. Initially, the stability analysis model and the interaction analysis model for the hybrid VSCs connected to weak grid are developed. The self-impact and interaction-impact components are delineated to quantify the degree of interaction between grid-forming and grid-following VSCs. Subsequently, the self-impact and interaction-impact components of VSCs are integrated with participating factors analysis. By exploring the magnitude-frequency characteristics of the interaction components under various operating points and control parameters, the analysis not only tracks the migration of system eigenvalues but also elucidates the underlying physical mechanisms driving these changes. Specifically, the impact of the interaction between grid-forming and grid-following VSCs on the hybrid system's stability is traced to the energy exchange and phase coupling between their core synchronization control loops through the weak grid impedance. Finally, the aforementioned analytical results are corroborated through both simulation and experimental validation.

title: Experimental and theoretical analysis of the influence of beam irradiance conversion factor on PV module mounting systems
authors: Arsenio Barbón, Covadonga Bayón-Cueli, Luis Bayón, Jaime Martínez-Suárez
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: The beam irradiance conversion factor is essential for estimating the beam irradiance on photovoltaic arrays. Given its importance for photovoltaic siting and performance assessment, this study analyzes its behavior in two common mounting configurations: horizontal single-axis trackers and fixed-tilt systems. The effect of the beam irradiance conversion factor was evaluated using the incident energy ratio, examined across annual, monthly, and daily timescales to capture temporal variability. An experimental and theoretical study has been carried out at the Department of Electrical Engineering, University of Oviedo, in Gijón (Spain). In addition, an analysis of ten Northern Hemisphere sites showed that mounting-system selection in photovoltaic plants is fundamentally shaped by geographic location. Based on the results presented, the following conclusions are drawn: (i) The theoretical and experimental results obtained at the same location show a certain trend; (ii) The annual incident-energy ratio exhibits a strong latitudinal dependence, increasing toward lower latitudes; (iii) Monthly incident-energy ratios are consistently positive and vary little (max <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10.73\%$</tex-math></inline-formula>) at low latitudes, whereas high-latitude ratios are mostly negative, highly variable, and reach up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$114.73\%$</tex-math></inline-formula>; (iv) All sites reach maximum positive values in June, coinciding with peak solar declination (highest in Morelia, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$55.57\%$</tex-math></inline-formula>), and minimum values in December, aligning with minimum declination (lowest in Helsinki, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$-71.32\%$</tex-math></inline-formula>); and (v) At latitudes between 6 (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${{}^{o}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) and 19 (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${{}^{o}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>), the horizontal single-axis tracker outperforms the fixed tilt angle system year-round. Above 24 (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${{}^{o}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>), the number of days when the fixed tilt angle system surpasses the tracker increases with latitude, reaching 203 days in Helsinki. However, the tracker indicates a benefit with regard to annual performance.

title: Transceiver Optimization of FDA-MIMO Radar-Communication Coexistence Systems
authors: Qihang Xu, Lan Lan, Tong-Xing Zheng, Fan Liu, Guisheng Liao, Derrick Wing Kwan Ng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: This paper investigates transceiver design optimization strategies for frequency diverse array (FDA)-multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) radar-communication coexistence (RCC) systems, focusing on both radar-centric and communication-centric modes. Specifically, the former formulates the design problem to maximize the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) in mainlobe deceptive jammer scenarios, whereas the latter aims to maximize the communication rate while simultaneously satisfying a predefined radar SINR constraint. In this framework, practical constraints pertaining to the radar’s transmitted waveform, communication codebook, frequency increment, and receive filter are taken into account. To address the resultant non-convex and NP-hard optimization problems, a maximum block improvement (MBI) approach is employed, where the variables are alternately examined, which are achieved either by leveraging closed-form expressions and hidden convexities or by resorting to the minorization-maximization (MM) approach, while keeping the remaining parameters fixed. The convergence performance of the devised algorithm is thoroughly examined, alongside their computational complexity analyses. Numerical results are provided to validate the efficacy of our approach against mainlobe deceptive jammers, demonstrating superior SINR and communication rate performance compared to existing optimization strategies and benchmark system frameworks.

title: Double Phase Shifter Based Hybrid Beamforming and User Scheduling for Coexistence of Near-Field and Far-Field mmWave NOMA Systems
authors: Thuan Van Le, Nam Van Dinh, Ngoc-Thanh Nguyen, Nguyen Cong Luong, Xingwang Li, Nguyen Tien Hoa, Dusit Niyato
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: This paper proposes a double phase shifter–based hybrid beamforming (DPS-HBF) framework for millimeter-wave NOMA systems, enabling the simultaneous realization of beam steering and beam focusing within a unified analog architecture. By superposing two independent phase-shifter vectors per beam, DPS-HBF flexibly supports heterogeneous near-field and far-field users without requiring full channel state information. To exploit this capability, a hierarchical scheduling framework combining Bitmask Dynamic Programming, Maximum Weight Matching, and k-best Semi-Greedy User Scheduling is developed to balance optimality, scalability, and computational complexity. The proposed design relies solely on low-overhead SINR feedback, making it suitable for practical large-scale deployments. Simulation results show that DPS-HBF consistently outperforms existing hybrid beamforming and orthogonal multiple access baselines in terms of sum-rate and fairness, achieving up to 30–35% throughput gains over the strongest benchmark under moderate-to-high SNR conditions.

title: Insights from particle simulations for diffusion MRI microstructure studies
authors: Viktor Vegh, Qianqian Yang, Megan Farquhar, Thomas R. Barrick
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
abstract: Diffusion MRI mostly involves quantification of how water diffuses in tissue, and its relationship with tissue microstructure. The technique promises great impact for soft tissue studies, since it provides a method of studying tissue microstructure based on millimetre-scale measurements. While multiple analytical models have been proposed to describe how water diffuses in tissue, specifically by simplifying cell structure and organisation, questions remain around the understanding and relevance of existing models. We performed simulations to provide insight into hindered and restricted tissue compartments, and how the diffusion process changes when inter-compartment exchange occurs. We consider distinct cases for modelled (spherical) cell size and diffusivity and describe the observations using the theory of diffusional retardation. Our observations may lead to new approaches for tissue microstructure imaging, and in parallel improve our understanding of the factors influencing the temporal dependence of diffusion and kurtosis.

title: Adaptive Shortest Path Tracking for Robust Leaf–Wood Separation in Individual Trees from TLS Point Clouds
authors: Yupeng Shen, Weifeng Ma, Chong Wang, Xiaodong Wu, Yuncheng Deng, Wanjing Yan
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Leaf-wood separation is crucial for single-tree aboveground biomass estimation and 3D reconstruction. Although the non-destructive and efficient acquisition of fine-grained, high-density point cloud data can be performed using terrestrial laser scanning (TLS) technology, existing methods suffer from various drawbacks, including insufficient detection of fine branches, limited robustness to point cloud subsampling, and weak adaptability across different tree species and crown structures. A core issue lies in the over-reliance on prior values for key algorithm parameters. This study proposes an adaptive shortest path tracking for robust leaf–wood separation (ASPTS) in individual trees. First, a graph is constructed, and the shortest path backtracking is employed to extract skeleton points. Second, an improved k-nearest neighbor (KNN) algorithm is proposed to adaptively optimize the number of neighboring points based on the shortest path, thereby obtaining initial wood points. Third, the feature descriptor construction for characterizing trunk and branch structures is optimized using principal component analysis (PCA) by implementing an enhanced adaptive neighborhood radius selection strategy. Finally, final wood points are extracted using a region-growing approach guided by a stepwise feature thresholding scheme. Twenty-two individual trees, which represent different species, heights, and crown structures, are selected as test subjects. The results demonstrate the capability of ASPTS to make a good balance between type I and type II errors. ASPTS consistently exhibits strong fine-branch detection capability and robust performance under varying conditions, including different tree species, crown structures, and point cloud densities. ASPTS demonstrates superior performance compared to four state-of-the-art methods.

title: Spatiotemporal Thermal Deformation Error Calibration for Agile Optical Remote Sensing Satellite
authors: Yanli Wang, Pinxiu Liu, Zihang Yang, Yingdong Pi, Mi Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: With high maneuverability, the imaging range of agile optical remote sensing satellites can reach 45<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula>-60<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula> from the nadir point, which significantly improves the temporal resolution and observation efficiency and is widely used in resource surveys, urban development and disaster monitoring. However, due to the changes of the solar radiation energy and heated regions, there is the severe thermal elastic deformation in the payloads of agile satellites, which introduces the spatiotemporal thermal deformation errors. This paper proposes a novel spatiotemporal error calibration method for agile optical remote sensing satellites based on thermal deformation factors. To express the variations in short-period and long-period thermal environments, and the heated regions, five categories of thermal deformation factors are constructed. Then, the spatiotemporal error calibration model with three hidden layers is established with two training optimization strategies. The complex and nonlinear relationship between thermal deformation factors and spatiotemporal error is established automatically without the prior analysis. The sample dataset for spatiotemporal error is established using the measurement data of star cameras and star sensors of LuoJia3-02 satellite. The dataset comprises 588 training samples and 146 test samples, with the data acquisition from August 8 to November 26, 2024 and the attitude angles from -35<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula> to 40<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula>. The improvement rates of thermal deformation error along the optical axis angle, pitch angle, roll angle, and yaw angle are 53%, 69%, 56%, and 92%, respectively. The experiment results demonstrate the proposed method effectively corrects the spatiotemporal thermal deformation error.

title: Cosplay in Higher Education Marketing: Evaluating the Effectiveness of Anime-Inspired Recruitment Videos
authors: Ting-Sheng Weng, Chien-Kuo Li
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: Responding to enrollment challenges posed by declining birth rates, this study examines the feasibility and effectiveness of incorporating anime cosplay elements into university recruitment videos. Taking a continuing education program at a university as a case study, a promotional short film was designed and produced featuring popular anime characters in a cosplay theme. The aim was to enhance prospective students' emotional resonance and enrollment intentions. Following the video screening, 140 valid questionnaires were collected. A five-point Likert scale was used for the survey. SPSS was employed to conduct reliability, validity, and principal component analyses. The results indicate that the questionnaire exhibits high internal consistency (Cronbach's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha=0.889$</tex>), with a KMO value of 0.841, confirming the data's suitability for factor analysis. Most items demonstrated communalities above 0.60. This finding suggests the video effectively evoked positive emotions, increased school identification, and encouraged sharing intent among viewers. However, lower scores were observed for the items related to “enhancing visibility” and “sense of character honor,” indicating room for improvement in the completeness of information and character immersion. The study confirms that cosplay-themed recruitment videos can serve as an innovative approach to higher education marketing. Future efforts should strengthen informational content, broaden the sample base, and incorporate interactive media to improve actual conversion outcomes.

title: Real-Time Radio Environment Mapping for Multi-Class Occupancy Detection
authors: Toufiq Aziz, Insoo Koo
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: Accurate indoor occupancy detection is essential for smart environments, yet existing methods often overlook the combined benefits of fine-grained spatial mapping and robust deep learning classification. This work presents a hybrid framework that integrates Radio Environment Mapping (REM) with convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for multistate occupancy detection using WiFi received signal strength indicator (RSSI) data. A TurtleBot3 mobile robot is employed to collect synchronized RSSI and odometry measurements in an indoor environment. Three occupancy scenarios are considered: no-occupancy, singleoccupancy, and double-occupancy. For each scenario, 500 REMs are generated to capture spatial signal variations. Extra Trees Regression constructs high-resolution REMs, which are subsequently classified by a CNN model. Experimental evaluation shows consistently high classification accuracy across all scenarios, effectively distinguishing between occupancy states while preserving detailed spatial features. The method demonstrates robustness to environmental changes and variability in human presence. The suggested method combines deep learning classification with regression-based mapping to achieve both classification reliability and spatial accuracy.

title: Design of Smart Community Education Model Based on Personalized Recommendation
authors: Gao Lin
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: Against the background of the in-depth integration of the lifelong learning concept and smart community construction, community education, as the core carrier of the lifelong education system, faces problems such as unbalanced resource supply and demand, homogenized services, and low demand matching. This paper proposes an intelligent community education model integrating personalized recommendation technology, which combines the improved collaborative filtering algorithm (ES-CF) with educational scenario characteristics to construct a three-dimensional hybrid recommendation mechanism of “user portrait-resource attribute-scenario factor”. Through multi-dimensional data collection to depict residents' learning needs, the model realizes precise push based on the improved weighted collaborative filtering algorithm with entropy weight scenario weighting and cosine similarity attribute fusion, and builds a closed loop of “recommendation-feedback-optimization”. To enhance readability, technical components of the model are visualized via flowcharts, and real-world implementation cases are added to verify effectiveness. Experiments show that the model is superior to traditional models in accuracy, recall rate and user satisfaction, providing technical support for the intelligent development of smart community education.

title: A Multi-Pipeline Deep Learning Based Framework for Fraud Detection in Financial Transactions
authors: Arpit Mittal, Anchal Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Globalization and rapid advances in digital innovation have greatly increased the complexity of financial networks, increasing their susceptibility to fraud. The changing nature of fraudulent tactics makes it difficult for traditional fraud detection technologies to detect them at a high rate. Thus, this paper proposes a novel hybrid framework based on Machine learning (ML) and Deep learning (DL) techniques to detect fraud with high accuracy in the imbalanced dataset. The proposed framework consists of three pipelines. Initially, the dataset consisting of Principal Component Analysis (PCA) components of transactions is reduced using the feature reduction technique. This reduced data is combined with the completed data and is then used for training the three proposed pipelines. Pipeline 1 uses Deep Neural Networks (DNN) as a classifier. The second pipeline uses autoencoders to optimize the data before classification, and then DNN and XGBoost are used for classification. The third pipeline uses different boosting and tree-based ML algorithms for classification. Among the three pipelines, which is able to handle the imbalanced data is pipeline 3, i.e., boosting algorithms as a classifier on combined data. XGBoost achieves the highest performance among all the models by attaining the accuracy of 98.84%, F1 score of 98.77%, and Area under the Curve (AUC) of 0.9674. The proposed method, implemented in real time, can detect fraud accurately.

title: Optimized Deep CNN for Prediction and Classification of Exudates in Retinal Images
authors: H S Vijaya Kumar, Mohammed Nauman
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: The eyes of people who are suffering from diabetic retinopathy often have small yellowish spots which are made up of fats and proteins that can appear on the retina which is called as Exudates. They are an important sign that the disease the person is suffering with is getting worse and if it is further ignored it can also lead to vision loss. This work built an automated system using optimized deep CNN to detect the presence of Exudates in retinal images and along with that it also checks how severe they are. The system majorly has two parts. The first part is the convolutional neural network (CNN) which is based on EfficientNet model which actually decides if the image uploaded is healthy or has exudates. This first step gave an accuracy of around 92.15% on the dataset that are used. Then in the second part if exudates is detected, a U-net model which is used for segmentation which also has a pre trained encoder is used to mark the exact affected areas. By the help of these markings Exudates Area Ratio (EAR) is calculated which is nothing but the percentage of the retina covered with exudates. By the help of these EAR values the system grades the severity from none to very severe. This model is trained and also tested on IDRiD dataset, which consists of both retinal fundus images as well as hand marked exudate masks. Altogether, this pipeline which covers detection, segmentation, and also the severity grading, gave an accuracy of 90-94%. This means that this system has the potential to become a useful tool for faster and efficient screening of DR.

title: Eco-AI Intelligent System for Aquatic Waste Identification and Classification
authors: Balaji A, Nivitha K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Long-term effects on aquatic ecosystems as well as biodiversity are caused by underwater waste, which has grown to be a significant source of pollution in the sea. Underwater waste is difficult to identify then surface debris because of background, low visibility, light absorption, and colour confusion. complexity. Underwater waste has been automatically detected using creative image-based models due to recent developments in deep learning and computer vision. The accuracy, speed and use of several object identification and instance segmentation models for real-time development on underwater systems are examined in this paper’s thorough analysis of contemporary deep learning architectures. XAI Integration method is used to obtain the results by GRAD-CAM(primary XAI) and SHAP(secondary XAI). Small deep learning models are highlighted in the study as possible options for current underwater waste monitoring and remediation applications.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Traditional AI and Explainable AI (XAI) for Diabetic Retinopathy Detection: A Review
authors: Sumit Sharma, Kanta Prasad Sharma, Kavita Saini
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is the leading cause of avoidable blindness in the world, especially among those with long-standing diabetes. Rapid detection of DR is essential for appropriate treatment and prevention of blindness. Artificial Intelligence (AI), specifically deep learning approaches demonstrated substantial promise in AU-detector workflow for automated detection of DR based on the fundus retinal images. The results obtained from conventional AI based models (CNNs in particular – Convolutional Neural Networks) are highly accurate but work as black box model and provide almost none transparency for their data. This lack of transparency hinders their acceptance in the clinic and leveraging them on a regular medical work-flow. On the contrary, the Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) makes a new paradigm whereby the AI model is made transparent, interpretable, and trustworthy. Visual and Feature-based Explanations: XAI method as Grad-CAM, LIME, and SHAP these methods tie model outputs to literature evidence for clinical reasoning. In this review paper, we evaluate and compare the performance of traditional AI as well as XAI models in each case statistic(s), metrics with respect to accuracy, interpretability, clinical relevance, and deployment. Although traditional AI does a bit better than XAI in its raw predictive accuracy, XAI has important long-term benefits for transparency and trust which are necessary for regulatory approval and real clinical practice. This paper compares the performance of Traditional AI and Explainable AI for detection of Diabetic Retinopathy (DR)

title: A Techno-Economic and Environmental Analysis of Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure Powered by Hybrid Renewable Energy Systems
authors: Abdul Khadar Asundi, Shabana S S, Reshma Sulthana S S, Naseeruddin, Farzana Begum K
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The potential for decarbonization through electric vehicles (EVs) is fundamentally dependent on the sustainability of the electricity used for charging. This study conducted a comprehensive techno-economic and environmental analysis, comparing conventional grid-powered EV charging with systems powered by solar photovoltaics (PV), wind, and an integrated solar-wind hybrid configuration. By employing high-fidelity simulation models in MATLAB/Simulink and HOMER Pro, we assessed key performance indicators, including charging efficiency, levelized cost of energy (LCOE), greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions, and grid independence, over a 25-year lifecycle. Our findings indicate that while grid charging achieves the highest operational efficiency (92%), hybrid renewable systems offer the most balanced and sustainable solution, achieving a 35% reduction in CO₂ emissions and the lowest LCOE (USD 0.14/kWh). The hybrid model also produced the highest net present value (NPV) of USD 31,000, highlighting its economic viability. This study addresses critical operational challenges, such as intermittency, through an optimized system design and provides a robust framework for policymakers and investors to expedite the deployment of sustainable EV charging infrastructure, thereby directly contributing to global climate mitigation goals.

title: ICECIT 2025 Breaker Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)

title: Deep Learning-Based Residual and Recovery Framework for Channel Estimation in 5G/B5G Networks
authors: Srivardan. B, Sachin Naachimuthu, Sree Sailesh K. S, Indrasen Singh, Jagan Bihari Padhy, Mohd Javed Khan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Channel estimation in wireless communication has gotten progressively more complex with the growth of modern methods. This paper uses a deep learning framework using a multilayer perceptron (MLP) to estimate channels in fifth-generation (5G), beyond 5G (B5G), and sixth-generation (6G) millimeter-wave (mmWave) environments to improve the reliability and accuracy of signal transmission. As the process deals with communication in 6G environment, a synthetic data set generation module has been designed using a sparse multi-path propagation model for simulating 60 GHz mmWave channels in different signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions. This paper investigates fast learning-based channel estimation for 6G mmWave systems. The framework introduced is a switchable two-stage MLP-based estimator, where a lightweight primary network performs residual correction over Least Square (LS) estimates and a secondary refinement network is selectively activated under challenging channel conditions. In order to avoid redundancy in learning across stages, a controlled corruption-recovery mechanism is used, which forces the secondary network to learn robust channel recovery rather than identity mapping. Simulation results show improved robustness of estimation in noise-limited regimes while preserving high-SNR performance with reduced unnecessary inference complexity.

title: Machine Learning for Web Vulnerability Detection
authors: Ashish Dibouliya, Varsha Jotwani, Anand Kumar Gupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The increasing growth of online applications has heightened the importance of proactive vulnerability assessment in protecting sensitive data and ensuring system stability. This study describes the creation of an AI-assisted vulnerability scanner that combines standard security testing with machine learning-based risk prediction. The primary goal of this effort is to create a user-friendly framework that can detect common web vulnerabilities—specifically SQL Injection, Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), and unsafe HTTP configurations—while also offering an intelligent risk assessment to assist security decisions. Unlike traditional scanners, which just enumerate vulnerabilities, the proposed solution uses a lightweight neural network to forecast overall threat severity based on identified patterns, improving interpretability and priority. The scanner further improves usability by featuring a graphical interface, automated report generation, and data visualization through multiple charts. This novel integration of real-time vulnerability detection, risk prediction, and visualization offers a practical and extensible solution for researchers and practitioners seeking to evaluate and strengthen web application security.

title: STACS: Spatio-Temporal Attention Charging Strategy for Dynamic Multi-Objective Optimization in Wireless Rechargeable Sensor Networks
authors: Haoting Du, Minghua Wang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: Large-scale dynamic wireless charging networks (WRSNs) face challenges due to the decision complexity caused by the large number of nodes and the resource waste caused by tracking charging patterns. To address these issues, a spatiotemporal attention charging strategy is proposed. This strategy reduces computational cost and improves efficiency through clustering methods and predicts node state information using reinforcement learning methods. Experiments show that the model improves charging efficiency by 31.7% and the survival period by 37.8% in a network of 1700 m scale.

title: Generative AI for Self-Healing Selenium Tests: Comparative Evaluation of Multiple Open-Source LLMs and Rule-Based Methods
authors: Chaithra B C, Chandan B Ram, Swathi Papanna Gari
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Selenium-based automated web testing becomes brittle because even small modifications to web page elements and their corresponding locators result in repeated test breakdowns which need prolonged upkeep. Web applications face limitations in their self-healing capabilities because traditional rule-based strategies fail to adapt to changes in application structure. The research assesses five open-source Large Language Models (LLMs) which include Zephyr, Mistral, Falcon, LLaMA and Gemma to perform self-healing operations on Selenium tests through generative AI while evaluating their performance against rule-based heuristics. The method works for all web operations beyond login verification and produces better results in repair operations than other methods. It produced better results than rule-based heuristics and smaller LLMs because Gemma achieved 53.3% and LLaMA achieved 46.7% success rates. The research evaluates system performance through its assessment of execution time and system resource consumption to demonstrate its suitability for CI/CD operations. It demonstrates how LLMs can decrease maintenance expenses while enhancing testing precision through their upcoming development stages which will concentrate on prompt optimization and domain-specific fine-tuning and industrial-scale deployment.

title: Design and Implementation of a Data Encapsulation Method for Heterogeneous Communication
authors: Hong Lian, Tianzhi Cao
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: With the deep integration of industrial internet and Internet of Things (IoT) technologies, modern communication systems exhibit diversified interface characteristics. Due to significant differences in physical layer properties, transmission rates, reliability, and other aspects among various interfaces, designing a unified communication architecture across platforms and media poses severe challenges. This paper proposes an efficient and reliable data encapsulation method for heterogeneous communication. This method employs a unified frame format, frame header recognition, data verification, transmission/response mechanisms, and frame-splitting transmission to achieve stable and reliable data transmission above the physical layer. Practical applications show that this method effectively reduces protocol overhead, simplifies application-layer design, and performs well across various heterogeneous communication interfaces such as serial ports and Ethernet, exhibiting strong generality and practicality.

title: Graph Reinforcement Learning for Scalable Zero-Day Behavioral Intrusion Detection in Social Networks
authors: Eswari Petchiammal G, Hariprasath E, Arun Vignesh V, Aashif Ahmed S, Harish S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Due to the large-scale, dynamic nature of social graphs, the lack of prior signs, and the quickly changing user interactions, zero-day behavioural intrusions in social networks are challenging to identify. Conventional signature-based as well as supervised intrusion detection systems cannot be applied to unknown attacks, while the current anomaly detection methods commonly fail in terms of scalability and adaptability factors. The issues mentioned above are tackled by the Graph Reinforcement Learning (GraphRL) framework that highlights the behavior aspect and is suitable for the zero-day intrusion detection in social networks which is scalable. The proposed approach combines Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) for learning structural and behavioral embeddings with Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) for adaptive decision-making. The GNN module records user relationships, interaction patterns, and community behaviors to produce high-dimensional representations that highlight deviations from regular activity. The DRL agent then develops an optimal detection policy through reward-driven exploration, enabling the system to identify novel and unknown threats without the use of labeled datasets or preset attack rules. A scalable graph sampling and training method ensures computational efficiency on large social platforms. When compared to conventional GNN-, clustering-, and autoencoder-based techniques, experimental evaluation on benchmark social network datasets shows that the suggested GraphRL approach increases zero-day detection accuracy, decreases false positives, and successfully adapts to changing threat behaviours. These findings demonstrate GraphRL’s scalability and intelligence as a next-generation social network intrusion detection system.

title: SilentBridge: A Multimodal ISL Translator Full Stack System Using NLP and Vision-Based Learning
authors: Dhruvin Mehta, Tanishka Patil, Aaryan Goswami, Ezhil Arasi V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: India has a significant disconnect in the area of communication between the hearing-impaired section and the rest of the population. The primary cause for this disconnection is the lack of Indian Sign Language (ISL) interpreters in India. We have developed SilentBridge, a complete, full stack, web based system which will allow for both simultaneous, two-way communication. In addition to communication capabilities the proposed system includes the ability to learn how to perform various gestures through a gamification application, allowing the user to learn ISL. The proposed system architecture includes three modules - 1) A sign to voice module, which utilizes MediaPipe to extract 3d landmarks from hands and also utilize a deep learning model to classify hand gestures; 2) A voice to sign module utilizing the browser’s native WebSpeechAPI to determine the audio input to generate sequential video outputs; 3) A gamified learning environment to create increased user engagement and retention. The proposed work utilizes advanced coordinate normalization techniques incorporating enhanced feature extraction algorithms, that spans beyond the standard normalization techniques.
