Moreover, the proposed Long Short-Term Memory network (LSTM) based model has an accuracy of 97.2% in identifying ISL, surpassing our Support Vector Machine (SVM) baseline model (89.5%) that was tested on our personalized dataset with thirty ISL signs. In conclusion, the proposed work is an attempt towards developing innovative, two-way systems for identifying ISL, as well as offering a gamified solution for learning sign language.

title: Resilient Power Management for Unmanned Small-Scale Hybrid Diesel–Electric Shipboard Microgrids
authors: Youcef Belkhier, Florent Becker, Adel Oubelaid, Jean-Frédéric Charpentier, Abdeslam Mamoune, Mohamed Benbouzid
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: The push for cleaner and more efficient marine propulsion has led to increasing interest in hybrid diesel-electric systems. This study explores a hybrid propulsion architecture combining diesel generators, batteries, and supercapacitors, specifically designed to handle the highly variable loads typical of Unmanned shipboard operations. Traditional diesel-electric systems often suffer from poor fuel efficiency and limited transient performance, issues that can be mitigated by strategically integrating energy storage devices. We propose a rule-based power management strategy that adaptively allocates energy between the diesel generator, battery, and supercapacitor while maintaining low computational overhead. Its performance is evaluated through detailed MATLAB/Simulink simulations, modeling the dynamics of all system components under realistic load scenarios. Simulation results indicate significant improvements in fuel consumption, transient response, and energy storage lifespan.

title: Secure Data Transmission Over Networks Using Symmetric Encryption and TLS
authors: Mohammed Uveise S A, Ramgopal Pasupuleti, Nagapudi Ramesh Lohith, Ramamoorthi S, Mohamed Sithik M, Priya S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Secure transmission of data over networks is a critical requirement in the digital era due to persistent threats like eavesdropping, data manipulation and man in the middle attacks. In the current paper, the authors explore the application of symmetric encryption algorithms and their application in the context of Transport Layer Security (TLS) protocol with the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) algorithm to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and authentication of data in a network with low security levels. In the paper, the basic concepts of symmetric encryption are presented where a common secret key facilitates effective encryption and decryption and this approach is very suitable in transmitting data over a high speed. Transport Layer Security (TLS) is analyzed then as a stratified security protocol that uses the symmetric encryption of bulk data transfer once the secure session is created with the help of asymmetrical key exchange and certificate-based authentication. It proposes an experimental model to examine the operational feature of such a hybrid approach and show that the adoption of symmetric encryption in Transport Layer Security (TLS) may decrease the communication delay and computing cost by a significant margin compared to solutions that only use asymmetric cryptography, and that it can still offer high-level security features. The findings indicate the empirical effectiveness of symmetric encryption in the framework of Transport Layer Security (TLS), and it is applicable to the real-world contexts, e.g., web communications and Internet of Things (IoT) systems.

title: Intelligent Predictive Maintenance Approach for Grid-Connected PV Systems
authors: Parashiva Murthy B M, V Sumalatha, Gayathri R, Minu Balakrishnan, Nidhi, Nittin Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: Solar photovoltaic systems requires effective maintenance strategies which ensure their operational reliability in response to increasing system complexity and geographical distribution of solar installations. The existing maintenance techniques which focus on corrective and preventive work do not function effectively because they fail to detect problems during their initial development. The paper presents a predictive maintenance model for solar PV plants which uses data analytics to address the existing limitations. The system combines data gained through supervisory control and data acquisition systems, distributed electrical sensors, and environmental monitoring units to facilitate continuous evaluation of the conditions. The complete data pre-processing process together with feature engineering and dimensionality reduction techniques produce essential health indicators which demonstrate system deterioration and fault progression. The combination of statistical analysis with machine learning models enables the detection of anomalies and the prediction of faults and the evaluation of critical PV components through their prognostic assessment. The proposed solution has been implemented through a MATLAB Simulink model which enables modular development and works with existing PV plant monitoring systems. The framework will enable solar PV plants to move from traditional maintenance methods to conditionbased maintenance by providing early fault detection together with tools for making maintenance decisions.

title: Multiscan Joint Target Detection in High-Resolution Fast-Scan Maritime Radars via Coherent Radial Velocity Spectrum
authors: Xiao-Jun Zhang, Xiang Liang, Hang Wen, Sheng Sun, Peng-Lang Shui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: High-resolution fast-scan maritime radars for small target search encounter two intractable problems. In intrascan detection, a low false alarm rate necessarily incurs the missing of small targets due to heavy-tailed sea clutter. Moreover, spatial-temporal structural textures induced by long waves result in false alarms that are difficult to eliminate. To address the two problems, this paper proposes a multiscan joint detection (MSJD) method based on the coherent radial velocity spectrum (CRVS) and the retrospective filter bank (RFB). As a new concept, the CRVS of multiscan radar data is a reintegration of intrascan coherent integration along an interscan path specified by the radial velocity in the range-scan time plane. It realizes the intrascan coherent integration and interscan non-coherent integration of multiscan target returns, while impeding the integration of sea clutter along structural textures. In homogeneous generalized Pareto (GP) distributed sea clutter, the probability density function (pdf) of the CRVS for multiscan sea clutter is derived in a closed form, and the framework of the CRVS-based MSJD of constant false alarm rate (CFAR) is given. Considering the target maneuvering during interscan processing, the RFB based on the target radial constant velocity (RCV) motion model is built. The CRVS and the RFB are combined to construct an adaptive RFB-based CRVS and an MSJD based on it. Experimental results using measured radar datasets with simulated and test small targets show that the MSJD using adaptive RFB-based CRVS attains much better performance than recent state-of-the-art MSJDs.

title: Dual-Stage Adaptive Fuzzy MPC for Autonomous Vehicle Obstacle Avoidance
authors: Wenpeng Dong, Noraishikin Zulkarnain, Mohd Faisal Ibrahim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Robust trajectory tracking of autonomous vehicles under varying speeds and uncertain road adhesion remains challenging. This paper proposes a dual-stage adaptive fuzzy model predictive control (MPC) framework that tunes the MPC cost weights online based on vehicle states and road friction conditions. A fuzzy inference system (FIS) is optimized offline using particle swarm optimization (PSO) followed by Bayesian optimization (BO), and then deployed for real-time weight adaptation. Extensive simulations in obstacle-rich scenarios with diverse speeds and friction coefficients evaluate the proposed controller against baseline MPC, proportional–integral–derivative (PID) control, linear quadratic regulator (LQR), and nonlinear MPC (NMPC). Compared with baseline MPC, the proposed method reduces lateral tracking error by 6%–38% (scenario-dependent), improves heading accuracy by up to 49%, and decreases steering effort by 12%–39%. It further achieves lower tracking errors and smoother steering than PID, while providing a competitive accuracy–effort trade-off relative to LQR and NMPC in obstacle-rich conditions. The results demonstrate improved tracking accuracy, control smoothness, and robustness for autonomous vehicles operating in complex environments.

title: Policy Deviation Integral Guided Meta-Reinforcement Learning: Applications to High-Speed Train Trajectory Optimization
authors: Haotong Zhang, Wanyuan Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) has emerged as a promising approach for the train trajectory optimization (TTO) problem in real-world high-speed rail (HSR) systems. However, there remain two issues with current DRL-based HSR operations: 1) the driver-centric markov decision process (MDP) with sparse rewards, and 2) optimizing a single rail trajectory (i.e., single task), but is less adaptable to practical HSR scenarios that require rapid responses to changing conditions (i.e., multiple tasks). In terms of sparse rewards, this paper first proposes a looped segment-wise gradient optimization (LSGO)-centric MDP that discards human-driving-pattern imitation, where the complete trajectory and terminal reward can be obtained at every agent’s action directly from the trajectory state. In terms of multiple tasks learning, meta-RL is promising to learn a policy that is capable of adapting to any new task with as little data as possible. Existing meta-RL algorithms directly use the meta parameters to train new tasks, overlooking task similarity and leading to a “lazy” agent issue with high training costs. In addressing the TTO problem, this paper finds that there exists a linear relationship between task-specific optimal policies. By fully exploiting the similarity policy between tasks, this paper proposes a policy deviation integral guided meta-reinforcement learning (PDIMRL) algorithm. It linearly adjusts and initializes new-task policies by integrating deviations between known task optima. Finally, experiments show that 1) LSGO achieves a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$16.8\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> speedup in single-task training compared to driver-centric MDPs, and 2) based on LSGO-centric and driver-centric MDPs, PDIMRL only requires 41.29% and 14.74% meta-training times than benchmark meta-RL algorithms (e.g., Reptile), respectively.

title: Securing Networked Discrete Event Systems for Diagnosability under Attacks
authors: Ruotian Liu, Tengbo Li, Shaopeng Hu, Agostino Marcello Mangini, Maria Pia Fanti
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper addresses the diagnosability analysis problem under malicious attacks of a networked discrete event system modeled by a labeled Petri net. A stealthy replacement attack is considered to alter or corrupt the observation of the system, in which the transition labels are replaced by others or the empty string, and its attack stealthiness requires that the corrupted observations should be contained in the behavior of system. The objective of this work is, from an attacker’s viewpoint, to design such an attack for compromising the attack-induced diagnosability of a system. Specifically, a new structure, called an attack verifier, is constructed by integrating the attack behavior to enumerate all the attack paths to be transformed into fundamental ones that violate the attack-induced diagnosability. Then an optimal attack synthesis problem in terms of minimum energy cost is formulated by integer linear programming problems. An example of an automated manufacturing system is provided to show the efficiency of the proposed attack strategy compared with the existing approaches.

title: Green DRL-Based Embedding of 5G User Plane Network Functions Over Heterogeneous Platforms
authors: Khadijeh Shahsavand, Amina Hentati, Mouhamad Dieye, Roch H. Glitho
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Network function virtualization is a key enabler for 5G and beyond, allowing network functions to run on general-purpose hardware for flexibility and resource efficiency. User plane network functions (UP-NFs) in the Radio Access Network (Distributed and Centralized Units) and the core (User Plane Function) are deployed across heterogeneous platforms, including virtual machines, containers, and bare metal nodes. These functions are typically implemented as virtual network functions (VNFs) and interconnected as VNF forwarding graphs (VNF-FGs), capturing interdependencies and traffic flows. As 5G infrastructures scale, energy efficiency becomes a critical challenge, especially when embedding VNF-FGs onto platforms with varying overhead, performance, and power consumption. Inefficient embedding leads to excessive energy use and degraded service quality. This paper addresses the green embedding of UP-NF-based VNF-FGs to minimize power consumption while meeting strict latency and bandwidth requirements. We propose a novel graph attention network-enhanced deep reinforcement learning (GAT-DRL) approach. The GAT component assigns dynamic attention weights, effectively modeling the relationships within the VNF-FG and the substrate network, enabling the DRL agent to make context-aware, energy-efficient embedding decisions. Simulation results show that our approach reduces power consumption by up to 38% and improves acceptance rates by up to 49%, demonstrating strong potential for scalable, energy-efficient 5G deployments.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Target State Estimation Under Communication Constraints
authors: Yiming Chen, Zheng Zhang, Zheng Guo, Kanghao Zhang, Qingdong Li
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: To address the cooperative target tracking problem in sensor networked distributed state estimation with constrained communication resources, a method, reinforcement learning-based event-triggered cubature Kalman filter (RL-ETCKF), is proposed. First, the method derives closed-form solutions for optimal estimation and event-triggered gains under event-triggered conditions, forming the foundation of a distributed cooperative estimation algorithm that supports multi-node information fusion. Second, by integrating reinforcement learning with an adaptive event-triggered mechanism, RL-ETCKF enables intelligent adjustment of communication frequency based on multidimensional state perception and dynamic thresholding, which effectively balances communication load and estimation accuracy. In the final step, the algorithm fuses local information across the network in a cooperative manner, maintaining robustness under limited bandwidth conditions. Extensive simulations based on typical maneuvering target scenarios demonstrate that the proposed RL-ETCKF method significantly reduces communication overhead while enhancing estimation accuracy and tracking performance compared to conventional distributed estimation approaches.

title: ANN-Based Dynamic AP Selection for Seamless LiFi Handover in Hospitals
authors: Ajay Sharma, Lalit Garg, Peter Albert Xuereb
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Wireless Light Fidelity (LiFi) provides high data rates, robust physical-layer security, and electromagnetic interference (EMI) immunity, qualifying it as a valuable technology in hospital environments where connectivity and patient privacy must not be compromised. The practical limitation is that LiFi access points (APs) have limited coverage, which makes mobility and seamless handover difficult. This paper presents an artificial neural network (ANN)-based approach for adaptive AP selection and handover based on readily measurable properties correlated with receiver location (X, Y, Z), distance to the APs, received signal strength (RSS), and signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). A Monte Carlo dataset representing a simulated 15 m × 15 m hospital hall with four APs mounted on the ceiling was used to train and test the model. The ANN reached 96% Top-1 accuracy, which is affirmed by the training/validation performance curves, row-normalised confusion matrix, and a regression plot of true versus predicted class indices. Online tests in a simulated environment demonstrated reliable connections and handover performance without interruptions during movement. These findings show that ANN-based AP selection can be an efficient and scalable solution for LiFi-enabled hospital networks, and can also be integrated as a decision module in hybrid LiFi/WiFi networks to provide EMI-free, secure, and high-capacity connectivity.

title: Novel Hybrid Compression Techniques for Full-Stack AI Deployment
authors: Ibrar Ahmed, Mohd. Nazim, Vaishali Mishra, Sana Anjum, Pooja Kumari, Neeti Taneja
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: There is a necessity to establish some balance between the model size, inference speed, and quality when the AI models are integrated into full-stack applications. Though the conventional compression methods such as pruning, quantization and knowledge distillation all have their own individual contribution to the efficiency increase, these methods present enormous possibilities to hybridization approaches. The paper discusses the hybrid compression methods which can decrease computation and maximize the use of AI through Pruning to drop the irrelevant parameters, quantization to decrease numerical precision, and knowledge distillation to adapt the knowledge of large models on small ones. Hybrid compression becomes particularly useful in limited environments like mobile and edge devices, therefore, they gain the most out of hybridization, efficiency being paramount in this case. The effects of such approaches are evaluated by the experiments performed on a benchmark dataset to evaluate such measurements as performance, model size, and speed of inference, accuracy, and energy consumption that was used. We make extensive experimental comparisons of hybrid compression strategies between techniques used in isolation and identify the best performing configurations in different deployment environments. Our results suggest a trade-off exists between compression efficiency and model correctness, where one would have insights on the best options to deploy optimized AI models within full-stack applications. This research addresses the challenge of scalable and efficient AI solutions toward real-time and high-performance-based AI integrated into web and mobile platforms.

title: Exploring Communication Authenticity Anxiety: A Data-DrivenPsychological Analysis of Al-Generated Content on StudentSelf-Perception and Expression
authors: Simran Kaur, Rajesh R., Radhalakshmi K N
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) tools such as ChatGPT and Gemini are becoming more common in student communication, owing to the improvement that they offer in fluency and efficiency, but at the same time raise concerns about authenticity. Students struggle to put their authentic voice forward in the quest to enhance their work using these writing assistants. Many surveys have been conducted, which indicate widespread use of AI tools for education-related chores, yet these studies ignore the emotional effects related to this. The psychological discomfort related to authenticity in text-based communication is still not well examined and to address this gap, this study introduces a term called Communication Authenticity Anxiety and successfully examines its relationship with self-perception, academic stress, resilience, and AI dependence. Data were collected via a structured student survey and analyzed using exploratory factor analysis, regression modeling and machine learning techniques. Results show that self-perception and academic stress are the strongest predictors of authenticity anxiety, while resilience and AI dependence have weaker effects. These findings were further validated by Machine Learning models, with Random Forest achieving 75 % accuracy and XGBoost achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 2 \%}$</tex>. This study, thus, successfully contributes to understanding the various psychological consequences of AI-generated content on student identity and expression, thereby providing valuable insights for crafting responsible educational policies.

title: Validation of Design Rule Checks using SKILL Automation
authors: Aman Kumar, Bhanu Pratap
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: In the field of VLSI design, verifying layout compliance with foundry-specific design rules is essential to ensure manufacturability and performance. Manual validation of Design Rule Checks (DRC), however, can be tedious and error-prone, especially as technology nodes evolve. This paper presents a SKILL-based automation approach for validating DRC rules within a commercial EDA environment. Using the SKILL programming language, the proposed method enables efficient generation of layout testcases that verify both valid and invalid design scenarios for each rule. This automated flow significantly reduces manual effort, ensures consistency, and enhances the accuracy of rule implementation. The methodology is particularly effective in handling frequent rule updates by enabling quick regeneration and validation of testcases. Experimental results demonstrate substantial improvements in validation time and coverage, making the approach suitable for scalable and repeatable DRC verification workflows in modern semiconductor processes.

title: AI-Driven Adaptive Encryption Framework for Real-Time Surveillance Video Security Using VIRAT and UCSD Datasets
authors: Lalita Nayak, K P Yadav, Shailesh Deshmukh
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Surveillance systems are now used in both public and private places, which has created a bigger need to keep video content safe from unwanted access and security threats. This paper offers an AI-based encryption system that adjusts the amount of energy it spends on encryption in real time, based on what it finds strange or unusual activity. It makes the encryption more intelligent and efficient. The system employs machine learning to look for potential threats and react accordingly by adjusting encryption based on the context, ensuring that the security is robust and resources are conserved. When tried on the VIRAT Aerial and UCSD Anomaly datasets, the system was very efficient with a detection rate of 98 %, F1-score of 0.981, very low encryption delay of 21.4 milliseconds, and high security levels with an entropy of 7.9992. It also performs better than other approaches by achieving the best trade-off between protecting the data well, performing fast, and being able to handle additional use as necessary. This approach is effective, responsive, and operates in real time, thus ideal for future surveillance systems to remain responsive to evolving security requirements as they remain quick and efficient.

title: Improving Accident Prediction Accuracy Using Weather Data Analysis with an Enhanced Random Forest and Naïve Bayes Algorithm
authors: Guna G, Anusharani V, Sarathkumar D, Raymon Antony Raj, Leo John Baptist Andrews, Sampath Kumar Venkatachary, Sheik Sidthik A, Balamadesh A, D. Ezhilarasan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The investigation aims towards improve accident prediction by analyzing weather data using a Novel Random Forest algorithm (NRF), comparing its performance to the Naive Bayes (NB). The analysis entailed comparing two groups utilizing different techniques, namely the NRF and NB. Each group consisted of 20 samples. The number of iterations was determined using G-power calculations, with an effect size of 0.8 and a 95 % confidence interval. NRF achieves an accuracy of 83.20 % in predicting accidents with weather data, whereas Naive Bayes achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 7. 5 0 \%}$</tex>. Independent tests discovered a notable distinction (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}=\mathbf{0. 0 3 2}$</tex>) between the two algorithms. The NRF outperforms the NB algorithm in predicting accidents with weather data, achieving a precision rate of 83.20 % compared to the NB algorithm's accuracy rate of 67.50 %.

title: Overcoming Environmental Challenges in Solar Energy: An Optimized MPPT Solution for Shading and Thermal Effects
authors: Mohd Ahfaz Khan, Amol Barve, Anurag S. D. Rai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Environmental variations such as partial shading and thermal effects significantly impact the performance of photovoltaic (PV) systems. This study presents a comparative analysis of two metaheuristic algorithms-Cuckoo Search Algorithm (CSA) and Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO)-for Maximum Power Point Tracking (c) under dynamic conditions. A PV array comprising three series-connected modules was simulated in MATLAB/Simulink and tested across three scenarios: (i) partial shading at constant temperature, (ii) temperature variation at constant irradiation, and (iii) simultaneous shading and thermal effects. Real irradiance and temperature data for Jabalpur, India, from 2005 to 2020 were used for validation. The results show that CSA achieves faster convergence and more accurate tracking of the global maximum power point, while GWO provides smoother output characteristics. The study concludes that CSA is highly effective for real-time MPPT under fluctuating environmental conditions, thereby enhancing the energy yield and reliability of PV systems.

title: A Next Gen Motorcycle Helmet with Integrated Al, LoT and Cyber Physical Safety Systems
authors: Sivasankari R, Dinesh Y, Moheshwar S, Basheerah Batool M, Magdalina Pearlin D, Rathna C K
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Motorcycle riders are at high risk of accidents due to lack of helmets, slow response, and drunkenness. Conventional helmets only protect the head from injury. They do not feature accident detection, health monitoring, deterrent to theft or insurance covers. This research work is on Smart Helmet System based on AI and IoT. In this we will present the functionalities of our smart system which include real time health monitoring, fall and accident detection, alcohol detection and reporting, helmet wear checks, GPS tracking and engine lock with anti-theft features. Blockchain technology secures health record storage and claim initiation. The use of solar, kinetic and piezoelectric energy helps power up the console. Advanced security uses voice, facial recognition, plus geofencing. Tested in laboratories and in the field, the system boasts reliable detection, good user acceptance and long life and transforms helmets into active safety systems that enhance motorcycle safety, facilitate emergency response and improve rider security.

title: A 12-bit 1-GS/s Single-Channel Pipelined ADC With Open-Loop Floating Inverter Amplifier and Residue-Dependent Integration Time Compensation
authors: Hongjiang Chen, Yuhang Peng, Liang Qi, Biao Wang, Sai-Weng Sin, Rui P. Martins, Mingqiang Guo
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits
abstract: A 12-bit 1-GS/s three-stage pipelined analog-to-digital converter (ADC) employing an analog compensation technique to enhance residue amplifier (RA) gain linearity is presented. The proposed residue-dependent integration time compensation (RITC) technique mitigates the compressing nonlinearity in an RA by scaling the integration time for large RA input signals. This technique leverages the time residue generated by the time-domain (TD) sub-ADC to track the variation of the voltage residue. Corresponding mathematical analyses are incorporated to elaborate on its effectiveness. Benefitting from the RITC technique, the prototype ADC achieves high linearity while consuming 5.1 mW from a 1-V power supply and operating at 1 GS/s. The signal-to-noise-and-distortion ratio (SNDR) and spurious-free dynamic range (SFDR) are 61.3 and 75.7 dB with a Nyquist input, respectively, leading to a 171.2-dB Schreier figure-of-merit (FoM) and 5.4-fJ/conversion-step Walden FoM.

title: DeepFWI: Identifying Bug-Sensitive Warnings with Multi-Modal Code-Warning Semantics
authors: Han Liu, Jian Zhang, Cen Zhang, Xiaohan Zhang, Kaixuan Li, Sen Chen, Shang-Wei Lin, Yixiang Chen, Xinghua Li, Yang Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering
abstract: Static analysis tools have evolved over time to assist in detecting bugs. However, the excessive false warnings can impede developers’ productivity and confidence in the tools. Previous research efforts have explored learning-based approaches to identify bug warnings. Nevertheless, their coarse granularity, focusing on either long-term warnings or function-level alerts, are insensitive to individual bugs. Also, they rely on manually crafted features or solely on source code semantics, which is inadequate for effective learning. In this paper, we propose DeepFWI, a learning-based approach that identifies bug-sensitive warnings at a fine-grained granularity. Specifically, we design a novel LSTM-based model that captures multi-modal semantics of source code and warnings from automated static analysis tools (ASATs) and highlights their correlations with cross-attention. To tackle the data scarcity of training and evaluation, we collected a large-scale dataset of 280,273 warnings. We conducted extensive experiments on the dataset to evaluate DeepFWI. The experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach, with an F1-score 67.06% for confirming true warnings in a finer-grained manner, significantly outperforming all baselines. Additionally, to validate the practicality of DeepFWI from the perspective of developers, we applied DeepFWI to four popular open-source projects. Our approach filtered out the vast majority of warnings, while still successfully surfacing 25 true bug-related warnings that were confirmed through manual analysis.

title: Novel Deep Learning-Based Approach for Detection, Classification, and Mitigation of Cyber Attacks in Power Distribution Systems
authors: Aparna Acharya, Bhavesh R. Bhalja
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Distribution automation (DA) systems face critical cybersecurity challenges due to legacy communication protocols and the expanded attack surface introduced by Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) integration. To address these issues, this paper proposes a two-stage cyber-resilient framework for detecting and mitigating cyber-attacks. The first stage uses an advanced deep neural network (ADNN) to detect and classify multiple attack types, including unauthorized command injection, network latency anomalies, and Denial-of-Service (DoS) attacks. The model is trained using a hybrid feature set that combines Wireshark footprints with distribution system data. The second stage improves system resilience by applying a transformer-based deep learning model to recover data packets lost due to cyber-attacks. The framework is validated on a cyber-physical testbed based on an IEEE 13-bus distribution network implemented in a real-time digital simulator (RTDS) with industrial-grade hardware and a communication network emulator. Experimental results demonstrate strong generalization to dynamic attacks, achieving a 45-61% reduction in retraining effort while retaining 99% performance on unseen data. The algorithm achieves over 96% per-class accuracy, a per-class false alarm rate below 1%, and an overall detection accuracy of 98%, effectively distinguishing cyber-attacks from normal power system events. High-fidelity data recovery is achieved with an <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\mathrm{R}^2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> value of 0.9898, confirming accurate reconstruction of lost measurements. The end-to-end detection, classification, and mitigation process completes within approximately 95 μs, meeting latency requirements for time-critical DA operations. This demonstrates computational efficiency and real-time applicability. Further, it outperforms existing studies across multiple metrics.

title: Robust Spherical Wavefront Beamforming for Near-Field ISAC with MMSE Optimization: A Distance-Angle Perspective
authors: Mengjin Sun, Yongkang Gong, Ronghui Zhang, Yang Zhao, Xiaojun Jing, Chau Yuen, Derrick Wing Kwan Ng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) emerges as a transformative paradigm for enabling future wireless networks by jointly supporting high-rate communication and precise environmental perception. In this paper, we investigate a robust beamforming framework tailored for monostatic ISAC systems in near-field channels, where the impact of spherical wavefront effects is significant and cannot be neglected. In particular, we formulate a minimum mean squared error (MMSE)-driven beamforming optimization problem that strikes an effective balance between sensing accuracy and communication quality, while considering both power and outage constraints and explicitly accounting for channel estimation errors. To address the inherent non-convexity arising from coupled sensing-communication constraints and uncertainties due to channel errors, a semidefinite relaxation (SDR)-based algorithm leveraging sphere bounding techniques is developed to acquire a favorable suboptimal solution, with polynomial-time complexity. Furthermore, extensive simulations are conducted to rigorously validate the proposed methodology under a variety of practical conditions. Our results demonstrate that the proposed design reduces sensing mean squared error (MSE) by up to 3.8 dB and improves achievable communication rate by 33.4% over non-robust baselines under imperfect channel state information (CSI). Furthermore, compared to conventional far-field schemes, our design achieves up to 1.6 dB lower MSE and 28.0% higher rate in millimeter-wave (mmWave) scenarios, and 2.5 dB lower MSE and 31.7% rate gain in terahertz (THz) scenarios at 10 dB communication signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR). To further underscore the practical significance of our approach, the experimental results reveal critical insights into the delicate balance between communication and sensing performance in ISAC systems under real-world conditions. Specifically, our findings emphasize the importance of robust beamforming techniques that optimize resource allocation to mitigate the adverse effects of channel estimation errors, particularly in high-frequency regimes such as mmWave and THz. These insights are vital for the design of adaptive ISAC systems, where trade-offs between sensing precision and communication reliability must be dynamically managed to meet stringent performance requirements in next-generation wireless networks.

title: Reorienting with the Bare Hand: Gesture-Based Techniques for Orientation-Enabled Teleportation in Virtual Reality
authors: Yushi Wei, Xinru Cheng, Rongkai Shi, Hai-Ning Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Teleportation is a widely adopted locomotion technique in virtual reality (VR), with reorientation commonly integrated into commercial systems to reduce physical turning and improve navigational control. However, research on reorientation-enabled teleportation has received limited attention and still lacks clear design guidance, resulting in longer interaction times, greater complexity, and higher mental workload. To bridge this gap, we revisit reorientation-enabled teleportation through bare-hand interaction, a natural and device-free modality that is now increasingly supported and widely adopted in mainstream VR systems. Accordingly, four gesture-based techniques, Concurrent Wrist Rotation (C-WR), Decoupled Wrist Rotation (D-WR), Joystick Wrist Rotation (J-WR), and Direction Drawing (DD), were introduced and evaluated in a controlled user study with 24 participants. Results show that C-WR was fastest but error-prone, D-WR improved accuracy at the cost of time, J-WR supported precise orientation but was demanding and slow, while DD offered balanced performance and was most preferred overall. From these findings, four design implications were distilled, emphasizing efficiency, intuitive mappings, and support for diverse user preferences. Finally, two example extensions illustrate how these techniques may extend to broader VR applications.

title: Demagnetization Induced Torque Ripple Minimization in an Open-Winding Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machine
authors: Abdur Rahman, Rukmi Dutta, Guoyu Chu, Dan Xiao, Minghao Gao, Muhammed Fazlur Rahman
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: In an open-winding permanent magnet synchronous machine (OW-PMSM) with a common DC bus configuration, the zero-sequence current (ZSC) flows through the stator winding due to the inverter-induced zero-sequence voltage and the third harmonic back-EMF. Under healthy conditions, the ZSC primarily consists of the third electrical harmonic. However, under partial demagnetization, additional low-order harmonics appear in the ZSC due to subharmonics and inter-harmonics induced in the phase back-EMF, resulting from asymmetry in the air gap flux density. These additional harmonics contribute to the increased torque ripples and higher losses. This paper proposes a torque ripple suppression strategy based on a proportional-resonant (PR) controller. Moreover, a second PR controller was employed to suppress the additional harmonics in the ZSC. The proposed method effectively reduced the torque ripple from 88% to 16% under the magnet fault conditions. Furthermore, it significantly decreased the total harmonic distortion (THD) of the phase current, lowering it from 32% to 8%. The effectiveness of the proposed control scheme was verified using a 1-kW fractional-slot concentrated-winding (FSCW) OW-PMSM.

title: Stair Sketch: Towards Clearer Memory of Recent Events for Network Security Audits
authors: Ce Zheng, Wei Zhou, Yikai Zhao, Tong Yang, Zhenyu Guan, Bin Cui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: In large-scale and complex network environments, frequent security events make security audits increasingly important. Effective audits rely on maintaining a clearer memory of recent security events to support reliable identification and analysis. However, in high-volume data streams, existing methods struggle to ensure more accurate recording of recent time periods under a fixed memory budget while keeping the overall error controllable. To address this, we propose a novel data stream processing structure, the Stair Sketch. The key idea is to organize limited memory into a staircase of atomic sketches, where newer time periods hold more sketches. When memory is exhausted, one sketch is reclaimed from each existing period for the new one, efficiently allocating more memory to recent periods without updating all sketches. To record longer time periods and handle load imbalance, we introduce vertical sampling and horizontal sharing techniques to optimize our method. We theoretically derive the error bounds of Stair Sketch. Extensive experiments show that Stair Sketch improves accuracy by at least one order of magnitude compared with state-of-the-art methods, while reducing memory accesses by up to 50%. All related codes are open-sourced at GitHub.

title: Detection of Read-Disturb Effects in RRAM-Based Computation-in-Memory Architectures for Neural Networks
authors: Mohammad Amin Yaldagard, Ankit Bende, Sumit Diware, Vikas Rana, Said Hamdioui, Rajendra Bishnoi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: Resistive random-access memory (RRAM)-based computation-in-memory (CIM) architectures offer a promising solution to meet the stringent energy efficiency demands of executing artificial intelligence (AI) algorithms directly on edge devices. However, these architectures suffer from the read-disturb problem, which can lead to accumulated computational errors over time. To maintain the required level of computational accuracy, conventional approaches rely on a static reprogramming process after a predefined number of read cycles, necessitating large counters and resulting in inefficiencies. This paper presents experimental results using real RRAM devices to analyze the read-disturb effect and builds on these insights to propose a circuit-level detection methodology for real-time monitoring of conductance drifts. The proposed method initiates reprogramming only when the device drift exceeds a defined threshold and reprogramming is actually needed. Additionally, an analytical method is developed to determine the minimum conductance state ratio needed to meet reliable detection criteria. Based on this foundation, the proposed detection technique is further optimized for dynamic identification of read-disturb effects. Experiment-augmented SPICE simulation results, using a calibrated model implemented in TSMC 40 nm CMOS technology, validate the functionality and effectiveness of the proposed detection approach. These results demonstrate its potential to improve both the reliability and efficiency of RRAM-based CIM architectures that provide up to a 4x improvement in energy-efficiency compared to traditional periodic reprogramming methods.

title: Marginalized Particle Filter Degeneration
authors: Jakob Åslund, Fredrik Gustafsson, Gustaf Hendeby
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract:
The marginalized particle filter (MPF) is known to often outperform the standard particle filter (PF) in terms of estimation accuracy for the same number of particles. This is due to the fact that the MPF uses a Kalman filter (KF) to handle a linear sub-part of the system in a more efficient way than a particle approximation. However, for certain systems, marginalization implies no gain (MING), resulting in identical output distribution, including estimation accuracy.

This article explains when and why this happens in two steps. The first step is to show that the MPF and PF algorithms are algebraically identical in the case the KF covariance is zero. The second step is to show that for MING systems the KF in the MPF degenerates, in that the KF covariance approaches zero. We provide a practical guide for easily checking if a system is a MING system or not, and show by examples that MING systems exist, and in fact are quite common in, e.g., tracking applications.

title: Sequential Detection of an Unknown Transient Change with Nuisance Parameters
authors: Igor Nikiforov
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: The paper addresses the sequential transient change detection (TCD) in a Gaussian linear model with nuisance parameters by using the finite moving average (FMA) test. Unlike the conventional quickest change detection, which assumes that the post-change period is infinitely long, in safety/security critical systems it is necessary to detect a change (target) with an a priori upper-bounded (usually short) detection delay. All detections that exceed the required time to alert are assumed missed. We consider the Gaussian linear model with informative and nuisance parameters. The assumption about the known transient change profile is relaxed. The FMA tests are designed by using the generalized likelihood ratio (GLR) and the theory of invariant tests. These new quadratic FMA tests are compared to each other and with the FMA test based on the a priori known transient change profile by their operating characteristics. The integrity monitoring of the GPS channels for civil aviation is considered to illustrate the theoretical findings.

title: Quantum Computing for Predictive Transport Modeling: A Systematic Literature Review and Future Research Needs
authors: Daniel Guerrero-Domínguez, Carlos Lima Azevedo, Sven Karlsson, Otto Anker Nielsen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Modeling in transportation is essential for planning and operating current and future mobility and logistics systems, with significant implications for modern economies. However, many of these modeling tasks involve computationally complex problems that challenge traditional models and forecasting techniques. Quantum computing offers a promising way to address these difficulties. This systematic literature review examines the state-of-the-art in applying quantum computing to transport modeling, including and classifying 40 studies, selected through a systematic search of three databases using predefined inclusion and exclusion criteria. We identify three main types of quantum computing techniques used: quantum algorithmic primitives, quantum optimisation methods, and quantum machine learning approaches. With respect to the transport modeling problems addressed, the reviewed works cover forecasting demand, systems’ operation economic variables, predicting network states, estimating vehicle dynamics, and queueing systems. A notable trend is the application of quantum walks and quantum neural networks for the prediction of network state variables. Our review also underscores key challenges and research gaps, including the lack of standardized benchmarks and theoretical guarantees, particularly for quantum machine learning, the underexploited use of quantum walks for vehicle dynamics estimation, and quantum annealing for network state forecasting. We conclude with future research directions aimed at closing these gaps and building stronger theoretical foundations for sustained progress.

title: Adaptive Perceptual Graph Learning for Robust Wind Power Forecasting Under Incomplete Observations
authors: Dazhong Ma, Qing Xu, Zinan Wang, Yaobo Liu, Qingchen Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Sustainable Energy
abstract: Reliable wind power forecasting plays a crucial role in enabling efficient scheduling and effective energy management in virtual power plants. However, incomplete data observations caused by sensor malfunctions and volatile environmental conditions significantly compromise the reliability of wind power forecasting. To address these challenges, this paper presents a robust adaptive perceptual graph learning framework designed to enhance forecasting accuracy and robustness. The proposed framework uses an attention-gated GRU encoder to restore spatiotemporal dependencies under incomplete observations and integrates an adaptive graph convolution module to dynamically capture complex, non-stationary spatial correlations between wind turbines. To further promote physical consistency, a physics-informed discriminator is introduced as a plug-in component, guiding the model to align its outputs with the nonlinear aerodynamic relationship between wind speed and power generation. In addition, a sensitivity-aware adjustment strategy is developed to dynamically emphasize low-speed operating regimes. Comprehensive experiments conducted on real-world wind farm datasets show that the proposed method consistently surpasses baseline approaches, achieving a reduction of up to 35% in mean absolute percentage error, even under severe missing data scenarios.

title: Comprehensive Thermodynamic, Electrical and Magnetic Characterization of Superconducting Nb–47Ti Foil
authors: Harshil Goyal
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: This work presents a comprehensive characterization of a 66.04 μm thick, commercially annealed Nb–47Ti (47 wt% Ti) foil. The material was measured in the as-received condition, with no heat treatment or other processing applied prior to characterization. Magnetization, heat capacity, resistivity, and thermal conductivity were measured over the low-temperature range under magnetic fields up to 9 T using a Quantum Design DynaCool PPMS. All measurements were performed on the specimens cut from the same NbTi foil sheet and measured using standardized procedures within each PPMS option. A superconducting transition near 9.25(5) K with BCS-like characteristics is observed. Magnetization provides both the upper and lower critical fields and flux-pinning behavior, while transport measurements are used to assess the scattering contributions and quasiparticle heat transport, allowing for the extraction of key superconducting parameters. The measurements reported here provide a set of reference data for commercially annealed NbTi foil and establish representative parameters and a baseline for comparison with other Nb-Ti compositions relevant to superconducting applications.

title: Integrative Deep Diagnostic Architecture: A Convolutional Variational Attention Transformer for Personalized and Multi-Strategy Lung Nodule Detection
authors: Zia ur Rehman, Juanjuan Zhao, Muhammad Zaman, Qiang Yan, Aoyu Li, Adnan Akhunzada
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Lung cancer persists as a primary cause of cancer mortality worldwide, largely due to delayed diagnosis and the morphological similarity between benign and malignant pulmonary nodules in early imaging. To enhance early diagnostic precision, this study introduces a Three-Dimensional Convolutional Variational Attention Network (3D-ConVAT), a generative deep learning framework that unifies learning of convolutional representation, variational encoding, and contextual reasoning driven by attention. The proposed architecture integrates a Faster VGG16 detection head with a SegNet-inspired encoder-decoder backbone, enabling precise volumetric nodule localisation and robust classification through latent-space feature embedding. A Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier refines decision boundaries, distinguishing malignancy with increased reliability. To promote personalized clinical interpretation, physiological biomarkers and patient-specific indicators are fused within the decision process, bridging computational inference with the biomedical context. Moreover, integration with IoT-enabled Wireless Body Area Networks (WBANs) supports continuous physiological monitoring, positioning the framework within the broader domain of AI-driven personalized healthcare electronics. Experimental validation in the LIDC-IDRI dataset demonstrates a sensitivity of 97.2%, a specificity of 95.4% and superior MCC and AUC scores compared to contemporary methods. The results highlight the diagnostic precision, interpretability, and computational efficiency of the system, underscoring its potential for next-generation intelligent, personalised, and wearable-assisted lung cancer detection and monitoring.

title: Enabling Immersive Experiences With Edge AI and 5G Cellular Network: The Case of Virtual Try-On
authors: Hamza Kchok, Ilhem Fajjari, Nadjib Aitsaadi, Mourad Gueroui, Richard Li, Faten Chaieb
publish: IEEE Network
abstract: Immersive technologies such as Augmented, Virtual, and Mixed Reality are transforming digital interaction, but applications across training, healthcare, entertainment, and retail demand ultra-low latency, high bandwidth, and real-time intelligence that exceed cloud-only capabilities. The convergence of Edge Artificial Intelligence (Edge AI) and 5G connectivity addresses these challenges by processing data closer to users while ensuring reliable, high-speed communication. Together, they enable responsive, context-aware, and energy-efficient immersive systems. This article examines how Edge AI and 5G empower the next generation of immersive applications, using Virtual Try-On (VTO) as a representative example. It outlines key architectural principles, performance needs, and deployment challenges shaping the evolution of intelligent, edge-enabled immersive experiences.

title: Radio Frequency Identification Sensing Techniques and Systems for Structural Health Monitoring - A Review of the State-of-the-Art
authors: Chenxi Li, Zhaozong Meng, Zhen Li, Haichao Liu, Nan Gao, Zonghua Zhang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The structural damages of metallic structure components in many critical facilities and equipment may result in disasters that endanger human life. Existing Structural Health Monitoring (SHM) solutions commonly suffer from the limitations of bulky equipment, poor environmental adaptability, and high costs, which raise challenges for detection efficiency and large-scale multi-target monitoring. Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) sensing technology, featuring Non-Line-of-Sight (NLoS), flexible and pasteable, and easy deployment, show great promise for SHM. Recent studies have demonstrated the potential of RFID sensors for structural damage sensing including cracks, strain, and corrosion of metal structures, along with the analysis of parameters like crack width, structural deformation, and corrosion depth. This study provides a survey and in-depth analysis of recent technical progress in RFID sensor-based SHM. The main contributions include: (1) Classification of the novel sensing techniques and systems based on the functional model of RFID backscatter sensing; (2) Summarization of the common structural damage types and the feature extraction techniques of RFID sensing for SHM; (3) Survey of the recent progresses of the techniques, methods, and applications for RFID-based SHM; (4) Analysis of the challenges facing the state-of-the-art, including characterization and quantification of structure damage parameters and the impact of environmental factors, followed with an outlook of the future work. This study provides a timely reference for the innovation and practice of RFID sensing techniques in the field of SHM.

title: Split-Federated BERT with Adversarial Training for Edge Intrusion Detection
authors: Hang Shen, Qi Liu, Fang Li, Tianjing Wang, Yuanfei Dai, Guangwei Bai
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Pre-trained language models, represented by Bidi-rectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT), show great potential for deep learning-based intrusion detection systems (IDS) due to their strong semantic modeling capability. However, the high cost of training and fine-tuning limits their applicability in large-scale and resource-constrained environments. To address this challenge, we propose a Split-Federated BERT framework with adversarial training for edge intrusion detection. The framework partitions BERT into an Embedding layer deployed at the edge and Transformer and Head layers hosted in the cloud, enabling collaborative training between edge devices and the cloud. At the edge, a conditional generative adversarial network (CGAN) integrated with BERT enhances traffic feature extraction. Guided by BERT, the generator adapts to local traffic distributions, improving sample coverage and feature representation. Edge devices perform local updates to the Embedding layer, while the cloud conducts high-dimensional semantic learning using BERT’s Transformer and Head layers. During federated aggregation, a multi-head attention mechanism is employed in the cloud to differentially weight model updates, ensuring distributional alignment and stable convergence. This design decouples edge-side adversarial enhancement from federated aggregation, reducing both computational and communication overhead. Experimental results on multiple authoritative datasets demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms local deep learning, BERT, federated learning, and split learning baselines in precision, recall, and F1-score, while improving edge computational efficiency and communication cost.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Bidding Strategies for Virtual Power Plants in Electricity Spot Markets: A Review
authors: Pingyan Mo, Kai Li, Yanqian Lu, You Wen, Kangqian Huang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Electricity spot markets increasingly require flexible, fast, and risk-aware decisions as renewable penetration grows and price volatility intensifies. Virtual power plants (VPPs) aggregate distributed energy resources, storage, and flexible loads to participate in day-ahead and real-time markets, but their bidding decisions must balance profit, operational constraints, and uncertainty. Recent VPP reviews emphasize coordination and market integration challenges, while surveys on reinforcement learning in energy markets highlight the rapid adoption of DRL for decision-making under uncertainty. This review focuses on DRL-based bidding strategies for VPPs in electricity spot markets and related competitive market settings. We synthesize market structures and bidding rules, VPP modeling assumptions, uncertainty and risk treatments, and DRL algorithm design for single-agent and multi-agent settings. We also examine common simulators, datasets, and evaluation metrics, and we discuss limitations such as simplified market rules, weak adversarial modeling, and limited generalization. Finally, we outline future research directions, including safe and distributionally robust RL, multi-timescale coordination across energy and ancillary services, and cross-market transfer using digital twins and data-driven validation.

title: RNN Based Predictive Modulation Control for Dynamic Load Adaptation in Three-Level Dual-Buck Inverters
authors: N Prasad, G B Mohankumar, V. Murali Karthik, R. Ranjith, G. Renuga Sri, S. Sridhar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: In high-performance power conversion systems, maintaining stable output voltage and low harmonic distortion under varying load conditions poses a critical challenge — especially in three-phase inverter designs. This paper proposes an intelligent modulation control strategy for a Three-Level Dual-Buck Inverter utilizing a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) to predict and adaptively tune the Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) parameters in real time. The system architecture incorporates a dual-buck inverter topology known for its shoot-through immunity, reduced switching losses, and improved power density. Traditional controllers often struggle to respond quickly to sudden load variations. To overcome this, the proposed approach leverages an RNN to model temporal patterns in load demand and inverter behaviour. The current approach utilizes historical trajectories of phase voltage, load current and inverter states to form predictive relationships in the form of recurrent neural networks (RNNs). The resultant predictions are used to directly adjust the modulation index or duty cycle to guarantee a steady transient response and strong steady state operation. By sheet, MATLAB/Simulink were used to formulate the control loop and simulate it. A pulse-width modulation scheme (PWM) that allows space (dispersed) essence of sinusoidal PWM (SPWM), space-vector PWM (SVPWM) or space-vector discontinuous current PWM (DSVPWM) is formulated in bringing in the RNN predictions. This broader objective is to minimize the level of the total harmonic distortion (THD) and to enhance the power regulation.

title: Farm to Market Price Optimizer and Crop Sale Predictor Using an AI-Driven Decision Support System for Agricultural Profit Maximization
authors: Ibid Merrill MN, Harish KP, Niresh Kumar S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: This paper presents "Farm-to-Market Price Optimizer and Crop Sale Predictor," an innovative decision-support system designed to revolutionize agricultural selling strategies through artificial intelligence and data-driven market intelligence. The system addresses the critical gap in existing agricultural technologies by shifting focus from crop cultivation to post-harvest profit optimization. Farmers provide essential crop details through an intuitive interface, which the system processes alongside real-time market data from local mandis, wholesale price repositories, weather conditions, and demand indicators [1]. A comprehensive algorithmic framework encompassing data preprocessing, hybrid price prediction, demand analysis, optimal selling time detection, market selection, profit optimization, alternative strategy recommendation, and buyer-farmer matching generates actionable recommendations. Experimental evaluation demonstrates 94.7% prediction accuracy and 22.8% average profit improvement, validating the system’s efficacy in transforming agricultural decision-making and enhancing farmer livelihoods.

title: Bayesian-Optimized Advantage Distillation for Enhanced Six-State MDI-QKD Performance
authors: Madhubala A, Sumitha Murthi K G, Vijayarajan V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: This paper proposes a robust Six-State MDI-QKD system that integrates Advantage Distillation with Bayesian Optimization to enhance transmission robustness. By optimizing post-processing parameters through Bayesian optimization, the proposed hybrid model extends the secure distance from 120 km to 180 km and achieves average Secret Key Rate improvements of 51.83% to 65.03% over baseline Six-State MDI-QKD. The intrinsic MDI architecture eliminates the detector side-channel attacks, while the optimized post-processing improves resilience against environmental noise, resulting in a scalable, high-performance framework for secure long-distance quantum networks.

title: L-PRISMA: An Extension of PRISMA in the Era of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI)
authors: Samar Shailendra, Rajan Kadel, Aakanksha Sharma, Islam Mohammad Tahidul, Urvashi Rahul Saxena
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: The Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses (PRISMA) framework provides a rigorous foundation for evidence synthesis, yet the manual processes of data extraction and literature screening remain time-consuming and restrictive. Recent advances in Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI), particularly large language models (LLMs), offer opportunities to automate and scale these tasks, thereby improving time and efficiency. However, reproducibility, transparency, and auditability, the core PRISMA principles, are being challenged by the inherent non-determinism of LLMs and the risks of hallucination and bias amplification. To address these limitations, this study integrates human-led synthesis with a GenAI-assisted statistical pre-screening step. Human oversight ensures scientific validity and transparency, while the deterministic nature of the statistical layer enhances reproducibility. The proposed approach systematically enhances PRISMA guidelines, providing a responsible pathway for incorporating GenAI into systematic review workflows.

title: A Comprehensive Study and Implementation of Agentic AI via MCP Servers
authors: Nitin Kumar, Vikas Sagar, Garima Jain
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Agentic and Multi-Agent Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems are revolutionizing business operations and collaborative decision-making. The convergence of Agentic Artificial Intelligence (Agentic AI) and Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) into enterprise ecosystems marks a paradigm shift to autonomous, adaptive, and context-aware decision-making. A structured approach is taken, classifying the literature into five themes: agentic architectures, multi-agent coordination, ethical governance, tool ecosystems, and enterprise case studies. Comparative analysis picks up improvements like layered agentic frameworks and real-time anomaly detection, as well as revealing substantial deficits in scalability, explainability, persistent learning, and multi-agent orchestration. Standardized evaluation benchmarks, interoperable toolchains, ethical governance models, and hybrid human-agent collaboration frameworks need to be developed through future research. The research concludes that the shift from conceptual innovation to enterprise-level deployment is a multidisciplinary exercise that synergizes AI research, systems engineering, and organizational change management. This review has been designed to steer researchers and practitioners alike to convert Agentic AI into an actionable force for sustainable digital transformation in enterprise settings. Additionally, we introduce a multi-location MCP(Model Context Protocol) Server implementation comprising three servers: (1) a Web/API server, (2) a data source server hosting Text-to-SQL and RAG tools, and (3) a secondary data source server with identical tools, all operating in distributed locations. The MCP servers are critical for aggregating and managing data from heterogeneous sources, enabling seamless integration with Large Language Models (LLMs) for real-time query resolution and reasoning.

title: Q-ALERT: Quantum Methodologies Risk to Classical Encryption and Learning for Resilient Transition
authors: Nandini Tonger, Ravikant Kumar Nirala
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Quantum computing poses a significant threat to the security of modern cryptographic systems, particularly those relied upon in today's digital infrastructure. This paper explores the practical implications of quantum algorithms on classical encryption standards and assesses the current level of awareness regarding post-quantum cryptography (PQC). We begin by presenting a comparative study of RSA, ECC, and emerging post-quantum algorithms such as lattice-based cryptography, focusing on clarity for undergraduate learners. Next, we analyze the impacts of Grover's algorithm on symmetric encryption methods such as AES and hash functions like SHA, highlighting how quantum search can effectively reduce their security strength. Finally, we examine the awareness and preparedness of Indian industries to adopt post-quantum security measures, based on available surveys and secondary data. Our findings indicate a pressing need for broader educational efforts and policy-level initiatives to accelerate the transition toward quantum-resilient cryptographic systems. This paper aims to serve as both an educational resource and a call to action for proactive adoption of PQC across academia and industry.

title: Saving Critical Time: An AI-Driven Approach to Optimizing Pre-Transplant Screening
authors: Aman Aswal, Anubhav Choudhary, Deepansh Raj, Dharmendra Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Pre-transplant donor and recipient screening is a crucial step in organ allocation, yet it often relies on manual review. This paper introduces Life-Link, a proof-of-concept web-based decision support system that speeds up screening through a clear two-stage process. First, deterministic clinical rules, including blood group compatibility and distance thresholds, quickly eliminate ineligible donors. Then, an interpretable decision-tree model ranks the remaining candidates based on features like human leukocyte antigen mismatch count, age difference, donor viability, and recipient urgency.To protect privacy while allowing thorough assessment, we create a clinician-informed synthetic dataset based on published population distributions of blood groups and human leukocyte antigen alleles. We also add realistic missing data and noise to mimic electronic health records. We present a detailed evaluation that covers classification metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and area under the receiver operating characteristic curve. Ranking metrics include Spearman correlation and normalized discounted cumulative gain at five, along with the runtime per recipient. A usability study with proxy users evaluates the clarity of explanations and how well the workflow fits.Simulation results show that Life-Link can shorten the time to create shortlists while providing understandable, audit-ready recommendations. The system serves as decision support rather than making decisions on its own. Future work will validate it with larger, multi-center historical cohorts under institutional review, expand the feature set, and integrate it with hospital information systems.

title: Quantum-Inspired Probability Features with Random Forests for SAR Image Change Detection
authors: Elaveni P, Neelaveni R, Priya R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Change detection in Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery is challenging due to speckle noise and complex terrain structures. This paper proposes a Quantum-Inspired Random Forest (QRF) framework for pixel-level SAR image change detection. The method computes a quantum-inspired probability feature from the normalized intensity difference between bi-temporal SAR images and integrates it with spatial and statistical descriptors, including local mean, local standard deviation, gradient magnitude, and normalized spatial coordinates. A balanced sampling strategy is employed to address class imbalance between changed and unchanged regions, and a Random Forest classifier is used to generate the initial change map. Median filtering and small-object removal are applied as post-processing steps to enhance spatial consistency. Experiments are conducted on bi-temporal Sentinel-1 SAR images acquired over the Haiti earthquake-affected region in August 2021. Quantitative evaluation using Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score, and Intersection over Union (IoU) shows that the proposed method achieves an accuracy of 0.9965 and a recall of 1.0000. The results demonstrate that the proposed QRF framework effectively suppresses speckle noise and produces accurate and coherent change maps, making it suitable for landslide monitoring and other Earth observation applications.

title: Beyond Traditional Metrics: Graph Convolution Network for Meme Virality Prediction
authors: Roshan Kumar R, Krithigesh J, Mirthul KV, Suraj Prakash Sahoo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The explosion of meme content on social media creates real challenges in understanding what makes the content in the meme go viral. Virality Prediction is a complex task in the field of Social Media Analytics, and Computational Social Science. Traditional Machine Learning (ML) approaches focusing on simple metrics like "upvotes" and "comments" have been used in the literature, but they treat each meme as isolated entities. We propose a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) framework that uses memes as nodes, by shared metadata like subreddit membership and template similarity. The proposed methodology uses Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (CLIP) and Residual Networks (ResNet), for multimodal feature extraction. For the Reddit dataset of 1856 memes, our proposed GCN+CLIP architecture achieved a peak accuracy of 82.5% and Macro-F1 score of 0.735, outperforming traditional baseline models. The model predicts virality based on not only content, but also as a function of the social graph and provides pathway for future research into information diffusion.

title: AI-Driven Vision-Based Fitness Coaching Using Adaptive Skeleton Matching and Real Time Feedback System
authors: Charan M, A. Mary Mekala, Manoj D, Mohammad Alsamad Dandu Shaik, Shaik Mohammed Suhail
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The growing adoption of unmonitored home workouts has increased the demand for reliable, real-time posture correction systems that improve form and reduce potential injury risks through real-time guidance. Current vision-based solutions mainly use two types of methods: angle-threshold techniques and deep learning-based approaches. While threshold-driven methods are simple and easy to use, they struggle with different camera angles, body types, and joint occlusion. These methods also need manual adjustments for various joint limits. On the other hand, optimized neural network-based pose estimators can function on regular devices. Nevertheless, deep-learning posture correction systems require labeled datasets, exercise-specific adjustments, and added costs for analyzing sequential motion over time, making them less practical than lighter heuristic options for quick multi-exercise use. This paper presents a real-time fitness coaching system that melds canonical skeleton alignment with a single joint-angle tolerance. Instead of adjusting several thresholds for different joints, this system saves reference positions for key exercise positions. It aligns these to user body types using rotation, scaling, and translation transforms. The system checks posture correctness by comparing joint angles to a chosen baseline tolerance of 30 degrees. Temporal consistency mechanisms provide stable recognition of movement progression, continuous corrective cues with highlighted joint deviations, and fully automated repetition counting with audio feedback. Experimental evaluation on multiple participants performing bicep curl exercises demonstrates 85% posture-matching accuracy and 88% feedback relevance, measured through stage-level correctness and manual verification of feedback relevance.

title: Performance Assessment of Integrated Smart Lighting Controls for Energy Efficient Buildings
authors: Gopal Krishna Kushawaha, Anandakumar Haldorai, Dune Satyanarayana, R Sujitha, Rini Saxena, Nittin Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: Smart lighting systems are a new game changer in improving the energy efficiency of buildings and the comfort of people in the present-day building environment. Though most of the control strategies like occupancy detection, daylight harvesting and dimming are already in use, a comparison analysis comparing their respective results under dynamic operational conditions has not been done comprehensively. The paper is a systematical analytical work, and it is used to assess the energy efficiency and operation effectiveness of occupancybased, daylight-linked and combined strategies of smart lighting control. The modeling considers mathematical similes of the natural light changes, stochastic occupancy performance and room arrangements to form various operational cases. The study utilizes statistical analysis in order to evaluate the performance measures such as energy usage, system reaction period, and luminous efficiency. The methodology offers a firm, evidence-based ground in the determination of the best control measures or strategies to offer optimal advice on the choice of the best lighting solutions to optimize the energy saved and provide comfort in the eyes of the user in both commercial and residential settings.

title: Designing of a 5.1 GHz Microstrip Patch Antenna for UNII Band
authors: A.Sagaya Selvaraj, K.Shobana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The planning and imitation of a Microstrip patch antenna working at two different operating frequencies such as 4.9 GHz and 5.1 GHz have been productively carried out in this paper. The frequencies 4.9, 5.1 and 5.9 GHz achieved S-Parameters of about –16.8 dB, -31 dB and -13 dB which contribute efficient signal transmission. Only 2.09%, 0.079% and 5.01% power were reflected back. The gain and directivity acquired in the antenna are permissible only for 4.9 and 5.1 GHz. It also supplies VSWR values of 1.1, 1.0 and 1.4 which effectively remit power from the transmission line to the microstrip patch antenna. These operating frequencies offer an extensive range of Wi-Fi applications such as IEEE 802.11a(c/x), IEEE 802.11a, IEEE 802.11n, (Wi-Fi 5 and 6), high definition video transmission and also for drone applications such as public safety. This proposed antenna uses an FR4 substrate and is applicable for smart home appliances. It can also be used as a dual-band integrated antenna structure for future applications where public safety and HD video transmission are supported.

title: Comparative Analysis of Transient Stability and Fault-Ride-Through Performance of PV Inverters
authors: Arti Badhoutiya
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: The growing integration of photovoltaic (PV) systems into contemporary power systems has transformed the issue of the transient stability and fault-ride-through (FRT) capability of PV inverters into a problem of grid stability. With increased grid code requirements, the inverter performance needs to be tested under realistic distortion conditions. In this paper, the comparative evaluation of temporary stability and FRT behavior of grid-connected PV inverters through two complementary testing techniques has been provided: an ideal voltage source (IVS) testing and a Power-Hardware-in-theLoop (PHIL) testing method. Four commercial 10 kW PV inverters of varying topologies and control strategies are tested in standardized voltage dip and recovery conditions models of low-voltage ride-through conditions. The research concentrates on active, reactive power response, phase synchronization behavior, harmonic distortion and post fault recovery dynamics as key performance indicators. Through a comparison between simplified lab testing and closed-loop PHIL testing, this paper intends to demonstrate the significance of more elaborate testing procedures in the precise characterization of inverter behaviour under real grid conditions and in aiding the creation of solid, grid-conformant PV inverter designs.

title: BEE-OCHS: Bee-Inspired Energy-Efficient Optimal Cluster Head Selection for Industrial IoT
authors: S Rajkumar, R Myvizhi, V Dharani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The advent of the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) has necessitated novel approaches for optimizing energy consumption and cluster management in sensor networks. In this research, we propose a Bio-Inspired Energy-Efficient Optimal Cluster Head Selection (BEE-OCHS) algorithm tailored for IIoT environments.
