Drawing inspiration from the foraging behavior of bees, BEE-OCHS dynamically selects optimal cluster heads, adapting to changing network conditions. The algorithm integrates a multi-objective fitness function, considering energy efficiency, network lifetime, and data quality, providing a balanced optimization approach. Extensive simulations were conducted, comparing BEE-OCHS with established cluster head selection algorithms and heuristic-based methods. Results demonstrate BEE-OCHS’s superiority in terms of energy consumption showcasing its potential for addressing the unique challenges of IIoT applications. Additionally, the algorithm exhibits competitive data quality and cluster formation efficiency. The percentage improvement analysis further substantiates the effectiveness of BEE-OCHS, positioning it as a promising solution for energy-efficient and optimal cluster management in IIoT networks.

title: Multi-Model Feature Fusion-based Ensemble of Pre-trained CNNs for Chest X-ray Classification
authors: Soumyarashmi Panigrahi, Dibya Ranjan Das Adhikary, Binod Kumar Pattanayak, Bibhuti Bhusan Dash, Utpal Chandra De, Sudhansu Shekhar Patra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: A correct diagnosis is necessary to confirm prompt and effective therapy for the proliferation of aberrant cells in the thoracic area and pulmonary nodules. Accurate classification of chest X-rays (CXR) is utmost necessary to generate effective treatment and improve patient condition. CXR are widely and fundamentally available diagnostic tool for initial findings. However, assessment of CXR is very challenging and leads to human error, which evolves the need for automatic and accurate pathology classification. The latest developments in deep learning (DL), specially convolutional neural networks (CNNs), have transformed medical image analysis. In this study, framework ensembling multiple pre-trained CNN models such as EfficientNetB7, InceptionV3, ResNet50V2, and VGG19 are proposed for image classification. The feature-fused model, including individual models are trained and evaluated on a particular dataset with variety of metrics such as accuracy, AUC, Precision, Specificity, and F1-Score. Our results demonstrate that the ensemble of these pre-trained models achieves an accuracy 98.88%. This shows the efficacy of advanced CNN architectures in giving reliable decision support for CXR classification.

title: Exploring the Role of Artificial Intelligence in Closed-Loop Supply Chain
authors: Shahbaz Khan, Zafar Husain
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) has the potential to improve decision-making, efficiency, and sustainability in closed-loop supply chains (CLSCs). However, the implementation of AI in CLSCs is facilitated by several critical success factors (CSFs) that must be systematically assessed. Therefore, this study applied the Decision-Making Trial and Evaluation Laboratory (DEMATEL) approach to determine the causal relationship between CSFs. After an extensive literature review, ten CSFs were identified and confirmed by experts in the field. Further, these CSFs are categorised into cause-and-effect groups. The findings showed that the cause group is made up of digital infrastructure, AI experts, AI knowledge, organisational capacity, organisational readiness, and government support. These factors are the main factors that have a big impact on other factors. On the other hand, information sharing, ethical considerations, stakeholder collaboration, and data quality and integrity form the effect group, representing dependent outcomes shaped by the cause factors. These findings highlight the socio-technical aspect of AI adoption in CLSCs and give practitioners and policymakers a roadmap for prioritisation, which contributes both theoretically and practically. CLSCs can become more effective, robust, and sustainable by indirectly improving dependent outcomes through strengthening the cause group factors.)

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Global and Local Graph Association
authors: Qi Wang, Dongan Wang, Weisheng Wang
publish: 2025 7th International Communication Engineering and Cloud Computing Conference (CECCC)
abstract: Retrieval Enhancement Generation (RAG) effectively alleviates the knowledge limitations and “illusion” problems of large language models (LLMs) by retrieving information from external knowledge bases. However, the current performance bottleneck of the RAG system mainly lies in its retrieval module. Traditional retrieval such as DPR rely on dense vector similarity calculations, which often leads them to retrieve semantically relevant but inaccurate or incomplete contexts when dealing with queries that require multi-step reasoning or understanding complex relationships between entities. To solve this core problem, this paper proposes a novel graph association retrieval device and constructs an enhanced framework named GL-GraphRAG. This retrieval tool first conducts a preliminary candidate entity recall using the global knowledge graph, then dynamically constructs a local association graph for specific queries, and precisely reorders the knowledge paths by using the query-aware graph attention network. In this way, our search engine can go beyond simple semantic matching and precisely capture and provide structured factual evidence. Our experiments on mainstream open-domain question-answering benchmark datasets show that, compared with the RAG model using vector retrieval, our GL-GraphRAG has achieved significant improvements in both factual accuracy and answer completeness, demonstrating the effectiveness of this method.

title: Robust Framework for Secure Partial Shading Detection in Photovoltaic Systems Under Adversarial Attacks
authors: Ali Teta, Abdellah Kouzou, Okba Fergani, El Ouanas Belabbaci, Mohamed Elbar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Partial shading in photovoltaic (PV) systems leads to power loss and inefficiencies, while deep learning-based detection models remain vulnerable to adversarial attacks such as FGSM. To address this, we propose a robust framework combining a lightweight 1D-CNN, trained on DC-link voltage time-series from the GPVS-Faults dataset, with an unsupervised OC-SVM adversarial detector. The 1D-CNN achieves 96.67 % accuracy under normal conditions but suffers performance degradation under FGSM attacks. By filtering adversarial inputs using OC-SVM detector, the integrated system restores reliability, ensuring secure and accurate fault diagnosis. This hybrid approach ensures secure, accurate, and reliable fault diagnosis in smart PV systems, making it suitable for real-time deployment in resource-constrained and safety-critical energy infrastructures.

title: Leveraging Social Network Analysis to Enhance Customer Relationship Management in Retail Commerce
authors: Snehlata Barde, Satya Kishan, Dinesh Swami, Sneha Soni, Himanshu Tiwari, Rahul Kumar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: In the present-day retail industry, which is very much cut-throat, it remains crucial to have the customer engagement and retention activities highly active and visible. The traditional Customer Relationship Management (CRM) systems primarily deal with transaction data; they often overlook the influence of social interactions on customers' behavior. The introduction of this research is the hybrid framework that integrates Social Network Analysis (SNA) with Machine Learning (ML) to enhance the effectiveness of CRM. The research was conducted on anonymized e-commerce data that was gathered over one year. The authors constructed customer networks, and from these, they extracted key network metrics, which were degree centrality, betweenness, PageRank, and modularity. These metrics were then handed over to ML models such as Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, and Neural Networks for churn prediction. It is indicated by the findings that network-informed features inclusion raises predictive performance significantly, with XGBoost being the one attaining an ROC-AUC of 0.93. The SHAP feature importance analysis has identified the major churn drivers. The hybrid approach is compared to conventional RFM segmentation and it proves to be 18% (p < 0.05) more accurate in retaining customer prediction. The network visualizations are making the influencing customers and community structures visible. Besides, limitations and future research directions, such as real-time SNA-CRM integration, have been mentioned in the discussion.

title: Automatic Traffic Controller Using Deep RNN (AI)
authors: R. Hariharan, K. Valarmathi, L Mohanapriya, I Merlin Esther, M Meena, D Sarathkumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: In urban contexts, efficient traffic management plays a crucial role in reducing congestion and guaranteeing smooth transit. this study elaborates on a work based entirely on a deep recurrent neural network (rnn) system that is focused on traffic intensity classification using image processing to optimize timing of traffic signal system. this system presents real time traffic photos, which are then subjected to the latest advanced computer vision techniques of traffic intensity classification. the system is designed to get the intensity of traffic classes so as to dynamically adjust traffic light signals in real time. this system was fully designed with python since arduino controlled the execution of this system in hardware where setup of the traffic signal was done with the setup of the led drivers. through advanced traffic management technologies, the system has been geared to boost the release of vehicles and reduce delays in intersections, which bestows its potential to fit into automated intelligent cities. experiments result show that the rnn model is an excellent performer in traffic classification due to traffic intensity displayed and as it can be used for adaptive signal control.

title: Optimised Deep Learning Architecture for Pneumonia Detection Using Chest X-Rays
authors: Biju Angalees, Anupama D Sakhare
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Pneumonia is a widespread and potentially fatal illness, with a marked impact on the elderly population. Evaluating chest radiographs reveals the severity of pneumonia, underscoring the urgent need for efficient screening methods. The growing demand for pneumonia diagnosis has coincided with increased pressure and burnout among radiologists. In recent years, state-of-the-art deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have established new standards in medical image analysis, demonstrating remarkable effectiveness and reliability. These deep learning approaches have demonstrated strong accuracy in detecting pneumonia within thoracic X-ray images. This work uses a dataset from the Kaggle repository assembled by Paul Mooney, which includes 2,720 images for training and 624 for testing, each labelled as Pneumonia or Normal. Before input to the convolutional neural network (CNN), all images were standardised to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$128 \times 128$</tex> pixels. The network extracts image features using four convolutional layers, each containing 64 filters. To expand the diversity of the training data, augmentation approaches such as random rescaling, shearing, and zooming were implemented. A ReLU activation is applied to every layer of the network except the last classification layer, which uses a different activation function. Model parameters, including a learning rate of 0.001, were tuned for best results. The final model attained over 97% accuracy during training and a binary cross-entropy loss of 0.25.

title: Proactive Defense-in-Depth Against Deepfakes: Integrating Watermarking, Provenance, and ISAC for Digital Trust
authors: Tharaniya R, Agshaya D, Lavanya M, Harini B, Rajeshkumar J, Ananthi G
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The proliferation of hyper-realistic synthetic media (“deepfakes”) has created an urgent need for robust, proactive content authentication mechanisms that remain effective against evolving adversarial threats. Current solutions, such as digital watermarking (e.g., SynthID) and provenance metadata (e.g., C2PA), possess critical individual vulnerabilities to removal attacks and metadata stripping, respectively. This paper proposes a novel, multi-layered architecture that synergistically integrates Synth-ID, C2PA, high-throughput Wi-Gig communication, and Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) to create a resilient defense-in-depth system. The main innovation is the combination of a watermark inside the media with a digitally signed record, linked to real-world data using ISAC technology. Conceptual simulations demonstrate that the integrated system maintains a high (80%) detection accuracy under intense attacks, significantly outperforming standalone components. Furthermore, Wi-Gig enables real-time provenance for high-fidelity media with transmission latencies under 3 seconds for a 2 GB file. The results confirm that the proposed architecture raises the adversarial cost by requiring the simultaneous defeat of multiple, dissimilar protection mechanisms, thereby offering a more durable strategy for restoring trust in the digital ecosystem. This integrated approach also opens new opportunities for ISAC applications in next-generation wireless networks.

title: Honey Adulteration Detection Using Explainable XGBoost Classifier: A Physicochemical Feature-Based Approach
authors: V. Lakshmi Priya, Senthilselvi A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Honey adulteration is a growing concern in the food industry, with direct implications for consumer health and market authenticity. This study proposes a robust machine learning framework enhanced with Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) to accurately detect adulterated honey samples and interpret model decisions. A curated dataset comprising 1,000 laboratory-verified honey samples—equally split between pure and adulterated variants—was utilized. Each sample was characterized by 12 physicochemical attributes, including pH, electrical conductivity, sugar profile (glucose, fructose, sucrose), moisture content, ash percentage, and hydroxymethylfurfural (HMF) levels. The data preparation involved imputing missing data, min-max scaling, and handling of abnormal data. In order to encourage generalization, feature selection was based on mutual information criteria and Recursive feature elimination. Three classifiers (Random Forest, Support Vector machine (SVM), and XGBoost) were trained and evaluated by use of 10-fold cross-validation. XGBoost performed the best with the highest accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC of 97.4, 96.9, 97.2, 97.0, and 98.1 respectively. To enhance model transparency, SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) was integrated post-classification, allowing interpretation of feature influence on individual predictions. SHAP results revealed that HMF, sucrose content, and moisture level were the most critical indicators influencing classification outcomes. The integration of XAI not only bolstered user trust but also enabled domain experts to validate model logic against known chemical patterns. Overall, the proposed framework offers a high-performing and interpretable solution for honey adulteration detection, suitable for deployment in food quality labs and regulatory environments.

title: Sign Language to Text and Speech Conversion Using Hybrid Spatial-Temporal Graph Attention Fusion Network
authors: Nareddy Koushik Reddy, D. Shiva Mani, Mohammed Zaid, Karthik Reddy Padhira, N S S S Girish Kumar, M. A. Jabbar
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: For people with speech or hearing impairments, sign language is an essential communication tool. However, intra-class variability, signer variances, and intricate temporal dynamics make real-time interpretation difficult. In this paper, a novel Hybrid Spatial-Temporal Graph Attention Fusion (HST-GAF) framework is presented that efficiently captures temporal dependencies in sign sequences as well as spatial configurations of hand landmarks. The suggested pipeline uses MediaPipe-based hand landmark extraction, models spatial relationships by processing these landmarks with a Graph Neural Network (GNN), and uses temporal attention to recognize sequential patterns. By combining visual and graph-based clues, multimodal feature fusion allows for reliable classification. With a macro-average AUC of 0.96, extensive studies show better performance than traditional CNN and RNN-based methods. Additionally, the suggested system makes it easier to generate text and synthesize voice from recognized signs. For practical accessibility, future research will investigate continuous sentence-level recognition and mobile device deployment optimization.

title: Q-Learning-Based Load Balancing for JSON-RPC Requests in Blockchain Networks
authors: Ba Ngoc Nguyen, Tuan-Dat Trinh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: Blockchain JSON-RPC servers play a critical role in enabling client interactions with decentralized applications. However, they face substantial load balancing challenges due to unpredictable workloads, such as transaction spikes during hightraffic events. These fluctuations result in uneven load distribution, increased latency, and reduced throughput. Traditional approaches like round-robin and least-connections fail to adapt effectively in heterogeneous blockchain environments. This paper introduces a Q-learning-based reinforcement learning (RL) framework to dynamically balance loads across JSON-RPC servers in Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) networks. By formulating the problem as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), the algorithm learns optimal routing policies based on real-time server states and request types, improving both latency and load distribution. Experiments on a locally deployed network demonstrate that our method outperforms traditional baselines. It achieves 19.61 ms average latency with zero failures under average load, boosts throughput fivefold to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1, 2 9 3. 7 2}$</tex> requests per second under high load, and maintains 0.03% failure rate with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1,600 \text{ms} \text{P99}$</tex> latency under variable conditions. These results highlight the approach's effectiveness in enhancing scalability, reliability, and responsiveness for blockchain-based applications.

title: Bridging the Equity Gap in Global Higher Education: A Conceptual Framework for AI-Powered Student Advisory Systems
authors: Jiangye Zhu, Yiwei Gao, Haitao Wu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: This conceptual paper presents an equity-first framework for AI-enabled student advising intended to expand fair access to international higher education. Addressing three persistent barriers—information asymmetry, prohibitive advising costs, and the absence of individualized guidance—the model integrates a dynamic recommendation engine, automated application support, and multilingual information services within a cost-sensitive, scalable architecture. Distinctively, the framework operationalizes ethics through three interlocking safeguards: algorithmic fairness audits and impact assessments; transparent data governance with consent and accountability mechanisms; and inclusive, low-bandwidth user experience aligned with universal design for learning. We outline a phased deployment strategy for under-resourced contexts (pilot → evaluate → scale) and specify evaluation indicators that foreground equity outcomes rather than throughput alone. The paper contributes (1) an explicit equity objective for AI-driven recommendations, (2) a public-interest access model, and (3) an actionable policy and governance playbook for institutions and governments. We argue that ethically grounded implementation and cross-sector collaboration are prerequisites for ensuring AI reduces, rather than reproduces, structural inequities in global higher education.

title: Initial Coin Offerings and Decentralised Finance: Roadmap Towards New Age Venture Capital Financing with Cryptocurrency
authors: Radhika Singh Niranjan, Shyam Sunder Agrawal, Divya Mahajan, Nidhi Sharma, Suraj Kumar Mukti
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: This study examines Initial Coin Offerings (ICOs) and Decentralized Finance (DeFi) as emerging tools in entrepreneurial finance, leveraging blockchain technology and smart contracts to enhance transparency and accessibility for investors, and also examines stablecoin trends that reveal investor preferences and risk dynamics in the crypto market. The research analyzes global ICO market trends, highlighting leading countries in ICO quantity, success rates, and funds raised, with a special focus on Singapore, the USA, Estonia, and India. The study identifies regulatory frameworks, technological readiness, and investor education as critical factors influencing ICO success. In the Indian context, while ICO adoption is growing, regulatory ambiguity and limited awareness present challenges. The research also explores DeFi's transformative role in disrupting traditional funding mechanisms by enabling decentralized, transparent financing without intermediaries. The study underscores the need for comprehensive legal frameworks and technological advancements to foster ICO growth and investor protection. Future directions emphasize integrating cross-disciplinary approaches to address scalability, regulation, and behavioral aspects, ensuring ICOs and DeFi sustainably support entrepreneurial ventures globally.

title: Secure and Privacy-Preserving Machine Learning for Cloud Intrusion Detection with Federated Learning and Generated Attack Data
authors: Premanand Ghadekar, Nandini Gulhane, Harsh Patil, Ishita Ramdasi, Gaurav Hote, Siddhant Ghodke
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The growing popularity of cloud services, which provides a large and appealing area for attacks, places immense pressure on the defense mechanisms to protect the privacy of the user. We present an inclusive security architecture that incorporates multiple cutting-edge solutions to traverse this challenging landscape. We train an Isolation Forest anomaly detector across the network of disperse clients by using Federated Learning, an approach that fundamentally inhibits the centralization of confidential data. This collaborative operation is secured by an effective privacy architecture: the CKKS strategy permits computation over encrypted data, whereas the Secure Multi-Party Computation ensures the secure and discreet combination of the model updates. This technique overcomes the typical problem of scarce attack data by employing the use of Generative Adversarial Networks for the construction of naturalistic, artificial attack vectors, enhancing the forecast quality of the model. The Ethereum blockchain, which forms the basis of this architecture, records all significant operations, including model modifications and security alerts, creating an auditable and immutable record. The framework demonstrates its value by producing high detection rates and an exceptional F1-Score when tested against practical benchmarks. Finally, our results show that privacy and performance may be successfully combined, providing a workable model for safe cloud operations.

title: YOLO-Based Segmentation and RF+CatBoost Classification Model for Nodular Goiter Diagnosis
authors: Desidi Narasimha Reddy, Swapna Siddamsetti, B. Srilakshmi, K. Kishore Kumar, K S Ranjith, Krishna Priya Burra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Thyroid disorders have become a worldwide health problem in recent years which has been experienced worldwide among various ages. Based on the broad importance of the thyroid gland to the body in the control of important metabolic processes, it is important to make the diagnosis accurately and in time. Nevertheless, the current diagnostic frameworks are narrow and generally restrict as they can only categorise one or two kinds of dysfunction, and in many circumstances fail to provide the inherent imbalance that is commonly found in the clinical datasets. To address these weaknesses, the study is suggested to be a powerful and scalable diagnostic model with the capacity to categorise ten different thyroid illnesses. The method builds upon YOLO (You Only Look Once) to extract features quickly and accurately followed by a hybrid classifier which is a combination of the Random Forest (RF) and CatBoost. The experimental findings will indicate that the YOLO+RF+CatBoost system has an impressive 96 percent accuracy with significant improvement over the accuracy of traditional classifiers like the Support Vector Machines (SVM) and Naive Bayes (NB) in terms of accuracy and generalization. This solution will open the way to more extensive and effective diagnosis of thyroid disease because it tackles the main issues of medical data modeling.

title: Hybrid Acoustic-RF Underwater Comms
authors: Samiksha Chansoria, Namrata Sahayam
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: In the present paper a new combination in the field of underwater wireless communication is proposed which combines both acoustic and radio frequency (RF) technologies. If one compares it with acoustic, then it is farther more advantageous as acoustic is far more superior in range underwater but lacks in having wide bandwidth and is also very high in latency. The RF signals give high data rates; however, have a very high degree of attenuation in water. In integrating the two technologies, the weaknesses of one are compensated by the strengths of the other as we propose in the following section. Therein, we propose an adaptive protocol which always uses acoustic and RF to transmit data, but the selected mode depends on the given environment and needful amount of information. The numerical outcome and experimental intensity tend to show enhanced data rates, plus latency and reliability over single-mode systems. The findings of this research are significant in several underwater uses, and could significantly alter approaches to subsea data transfer.

title: A Modified Constant Voltage MPPT Method Using Database Lookup for Enhanced PV Performance
authors: Pappala Vamsi Krishna, Karpagam N, Y. Alexander Jeevanantham, S. Nooray Sashmi, R. Venkatasubramanian, Lingala Syam Sundar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This study presents the implementation and validation of a generation control strategy for a photovoltaic (PV) self-generation installation operating under the Indian distribution grid standards. The system consists of ten 100 kW inverters connected to a PV array of 360 modules with a total capacity of 117 kW. A simplified single-inverter simulation model is used to design and evaluate the control approach. To meet the constraints of the distribution utility (DISCOM), in particular to prevent power fluctuations under varying solar irradiance, an improved disturbance observer algorithm is implemented. The current and voltage control loops are designed using symmetric optimization techniques and tested in a 2-second simulation window. The results show that the proposed control strategy limits the power input, keeping the trip power below 4.9 kW for a duration of only 0.001 seconds. In comparison, the maximum voltage of the uncontrolled system is 33.3 kW, which is beyond the limit set by the DISCOM. The developed approach is efficient, responsive and meets the comprehensive grid requirements set by the Central Electricity Authority (CEA) and government regulators, making it ideal for use in power distribution scenarios in India.

title: Redefining Security: A Deep Dive into the Zero Trust Networking Model
authors: J Aakash Kumar, Robin Britto V, Sumathi S
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Cyberthreats today have shown that traditional security models that rely on a perimeter are becoming less and less useful. When businesses allow employees to bring their own devices (BYOD), move to the cloud, and work from home, the attack surface grows a lot. The Zero Trust Networking Model (ZTNM) is a new way to keep networks safe because it says that no user or system, even one that is inside the network perimeter, should be trusted by default. This study looks at how Zero Trust solves certain problems in modern network security, how it can be used in real life, and how well it works through case studies and critical analysis.

title: Improving Intrusion Detection Systems Using Machine Learning Algorithms for Real-Time Network Security Monitoring
authors: Prashant Khobragade, Bhagwat Thakran, Praveen Kumar Dhankar, Priya Dasarwar, Chandu Vaidya, Kanchan Dhote
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Companies all over the world are getting more and more worried about keeping their networks safe as the digital world changes quickly. Standard IDS can't find complex attacks because they only look for set patterns or rules of bad behaviour. It seems like a good idea to add Machine Learning (ML) techniques to intrusion detection systems (IDS) to make them safer and more accurate at tracking. This study represent how different machine learning techniques could make IDS better at keeping an eye on network security in real time. The study looks at controlled learning models, such Decision Trees, Random Forests, and Support Vector Machines, and unstructured models, like k-Means clustering. Those ML model can help IDS learn about new threats and adapt to them, which means that humans do not need to keep upgrading them. The suggested ML-based IDS technique analyses network traffic pattern to discover patterns and anomalies linked to each recognized and developing threats. Examined to find how well the system runs in real network environments are various performance measures including the number of false positives, the accuracy of the detections, and the speed at which the processing may occur. The results show that although lowering the number of false alarms, ML-enhanced IDS could greatly raise the number of detections. This improves the dependability and effectiveness of network security tracking.

title: Intelligent System Identification Using a Hammerstein Filter Enhanced by Moth-Flame Optimisation: Application to a Real-Time System
authors: Lakshminarayana Janjanam, Chandan Nayak, Suman Kumar Saha, Budati Sarada
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: In this study, the Moth-flame optimization (MFO), a popular bio-inspired evolutionary approach, is utilised for accurate tracking the non-linear characteristics of dynamic systems. MFO is inspired by the navigational patterns of moths, which utilize a transverse orientation mechanism to maintain a fixed angle relative to moonlight for guidance, allowing it to efficiently balance exploration and exploitation in the search space. The algorithm employs a dynamic control parameter that enhances its capability to locate globally optimal solutions with faster convergence and minimal steady-state error. The identification framework utilizes a Hammerstein filter formed by a Volterra nonlinear operator in cascade with a finite impulse response (FIR) filter, optimized according to the normalized mean squared error metric. The proposed approach is tested on a real-time cascaded water tank system. Comparative evaluations against the gravitational search algorithm (GSA) and differential evolution (DE) show that MFO achieves higher identification accuracy, supported by reduced output error metrics such as normalized mean square error (NMSE) and global prediction error (GPE) and, improved fitness quality.

title: Plant Leaf Disease Diagnosis Using Deep Learning Architectures: A Study
authors: Santosh Onker, Alpana Pandey
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Agriculture contributes major role in economy development of any country. Plant disease adversely affects production of any crop or any plant's fruit. For a good amount of production in agriculture field, there is an urgent need to tackle the issue of plant disease. The main reasons of plant disease are various pathogens such as virus, fungi, and bacteria. The plant disease is detectable in different parts of plant. Thus, plant leaves can be used to effectively identify the plant diseases. Nowadays, precision agriculture or smart farming has come into picture. Precision agriculture is an advancement of farming which changes from the old farming concept to smart agriculture, with implementation of ICT technologies that help beneficiaries to monitor, manage, and optimize their crops/plants. Artificial intelligent enabled machines are widely used to detect the disease in plants through leaf images. This study gives a detailed insight of deep learning architectures, which are used for plant disease diagnosis. It provides an information of commonly used freely available datasets for plant disease detection. In addition to this, current challenges and issues have also been discussed in the article.

title: Performance Analysis of Full-duplex650nm Laser Nodes in Optical Wireless Communication System for File and Data Transfer
authors: Dhruv Gupta, Praranya Giri, Abhigya Gupta, Adi Garg, Km Priyanka, Ritu Raj Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This manuscript investigates the capabilities of Free Space Optical (FSO) communication using visible light (wavelength of 650nm), a subset of Optical Wireless Communication (OWC) systems. These systems, operating within the visible light spectrum, provide a secure, high-speed alternative to traditional radio frequency (RF) communication. The manuscript focuses on designing and implementing an optical link using a laser diode for file transmission and a photodetector for reception, interfaced through a USB-to-UART converter. The system’s performance was analyzed in controlled indoor environments under varying lighting conditions. Results demonstrate successful error-free data transmission up to 4.5m in ambient light and up to 6m in darkness. Observed limitations include reduced efficiency over longer distances, influenced by environmental factors. Python-based encoding and decoding ensured smooth operation and accurate data transfer. The findings emphasize the potential of FSO communication for applications requiring secure and interference-free file exchange.

title: FloodGuard: An Embedded System for Real-Time Monitoring and Flood Management System
authors: Pavan Kumar Shukla, Sumit Tiwari, Dweep Kumar Singh, Ashutosh Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Flooding is still a serious issue in many places, especially where the drainage isn’t working well or was never properly set up. For this project, our goal was to create a simple and affordable tool that could detect water rising to dangerous levels and take early action. We built the system around an Arduino Uno and used an ultrasonic sensor to measure how much water collected in a $\mathbf{1 5 ~ c m}$-high test container. Whenever the water level went above a certain point, the Arduino turned on two solenoid valves through a relay connection. These valves directed the excess water into separate containers, which mimicked redirecting floodwater away from danger zones. We also added a GSM module to send an SMS alert when flooding starts. That way, people don’t have to be physically near the setup to know there’s a problem. We included a $16 \times 2$ LCD to show real-time water levels on-site. In all our tests, the system worked fast and gave consistent results. We changed the water height multiple times to test how it responds. The whole setup didn’t use much power and was made from low-cost components. It’s simple to operate and can be installed in many places — especially in flood-prone rural or urban areas that don’t have advanced infrastructure.

title: IoT-Enabled Real-Time Indian Sign Language Interpreter Using Raspberry Pi and MediaPipe
authors: Jahnvi Rai, Shivam Kumar Yadav, Sanjay Kumar Soni
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This paper presents a low-cost, real-time Indian Sign Language (ISL) recognition system designed for deployment on a Raspberry Pi edge device to aid communication for hearing and speech-impaired individuals. The system captures live video input using a camera connected to the Raspberry Pi and applies Google’s MediaPipe framework to detect and extract 21 hand landmarks representing finger and palm positions. These landmarks serve as input features for a lightweight feedforward neural network (FNN), trained on a publicly available ISL dataset, to classify static hand gestures corresponding to ISL alphabets or words. The classified output is then converted into speech using a text-to-speech (TTS) engine, and played through a connected speaker, enabling real-time gesture-to-audio communication. The system is developed entirely in Python and optimized for the limited computational resources of the Raspberry Pi, making it suitable for offline and portable use in classrooms, public services, or assistive wearable devices. Experimental results demonstrate that the system achieves reliable recognition accuracy with F1-score of 0.9896 and accuracy of 99.95% on test data, confirming its potential as an effective AI-powered IoT solution for inclusive communication.

title: Fluid Antennas for Non-Monotonic THz-WPT With Channel Estimation Errors
authors: Triantafyllos Mavrovoltsos, Eleni Demarchou, Constantinos Psomas, Ioannis Krikidis
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing
abstract: In this study, we explore a terahertz (THz) wireless power transfer (WPT) system. Since traditional Schottky diodes may be inefficient for THz rectification, we focus on a resonant tunneling diode (RTD)-based rectifier. Such rectifiers are generally characterized by a non- monotonic energy harvesting (EH) behavior, where an increase in input power may not necessarily correspond to higher levels of harvested power. To this end, we propose the exploitation of fluid antennas (FAs) to align the non- monotonic rectification of RTDs with favorable channel realizations. More specifically, a generalized piecewise linear function is adopted in order to approximate the instantaneous input-output power relationship. Based on this, we derive an analytical framework in terms of the energy outage probability and the average harvested power for three FA port selection policies, namely (i) the input-based selection, (ii) the harvesting-based selection, and (iii) the random selection, each corresponding to different performance. The impact of channel estimation errors on the port selection strategies is then examined, revealing a trade-off between the number of estimated ports and harvested power. To further improve EH performance, we introduce a new receiver architecture featuring a power splitter, multiple RTD-based EH circuits and a DC combiner. Based on this, we provide two dynamic power splitting schemes tailored to avoid inefficient rectification regions. Numerical results which validate our analysis, reveal a novel utilization of FAs, stemming from the alignment of the port selection process with the non- monotonic harvesting characteristics. Finally, our findings demonstrate that the proposed receiver architecture can yield significant gains in harvested power.

title: Temperature-Optimized Self-Supervised Contrastive Learning Enables Data-Efficient and Explainable Fabric Defect Detection
authors: Raihan Ul Islam, Israt Jahan, Md. Samiul Islam, Tomal Ahmed Pantho, Md. Atik Ahammed, Mohammad Rifat Ahmmad Rashid, Ahmed Wasif Reza, Shamim H Ripon
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Fabric defect detection plays a pivotal role in the textile industry, as it ensures product quality and minimizes waste. Traditional machine learning models often struggle with the scarcity of labeled data, which is a major challenge in automated defect detection systems. This paper explores the potential of Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) techniques, such as SimCLR and BYOL, to address this issue by leveraging large volumes of unlabeled fabric data. By pretraining models using SSL, we propose a framework for fabric defect detection that minimizes the need for extensive labeled datasets while maintaining high classification accuracy. Furthermore, our approach integrates anomaly detection for localizing fabric defects, providing a robust solution for real-time monitoring in production environments. Experimental results demonstrate that fine-tuning SSL-based models on smaller labeled datasets significantly enhances defect detection capabilities across various fabric types, including plain, regularly printed, and irregularly printed fabrics. The SimCLR model with the ResNet50 backbone at a temperature of T = 0.1 achieved the best performance, yielding a fine-tuned accuracy of 91.4% and top-1 accuracy of 91.97%, and top-5 accuracy of 98.5%. This model also showed high precision and recall, particularly for the detection of contamination, stains, and cuts. Statistical validation with paired t-tests confirmed highly significant improvements (p < 0.05) in comparison to several baseline models, confirming the robustness of the proposed approach. Additionally, a quantitative explainability assessment revealed that LayerCAM achieved the highest intersection over union (IoU) overlap of 0.92 between attention maps and annotated regions, indicating superior interpretability and model transparency. This research showcases the promise of SSL in automating fabric defect detection while reducing reliance on labor-intensive manual inspections and enabling scalable deployment in industrial settings.

title: Bandwidth-aware average-event-triggered security path following control of networked AGVs with dynamic observer and NN-based attack estimator
authors: Shen Yan, Xinyu Hu, Yu Sun, Ju H. Park
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: This paper investigates an event-triggered security control strategy for networked autonomous ground vehicles against actuator attacks. Firstly, a novel bandwidth-aware average-event-triggered protocol is proposed to improve the usage efficiency of limited network resources. There are two main merits of this protocol, the one is that the average of historical data is used to mitigate the over-triggering resulted from random data jitter, the other is that a bandwidth-aware dynamic triggering threshold is constructed to increase the adaption ability of protocol to the time-varying bandwidth status. Secondly, in order to implement state feedback control, a dynamic observer based on a dynamic variable related to the observation error is presented to enhance the state estimation accuracy. Thirdly, a new attack estimator relying on hyper basis function neural network is devised to estimate the unknown malicious attacks injected into the actuator. By combining the observed state and estimated attack, an attack-compensation-based security control strategy is developed to mitigate the negative influence caused by actuator attacks and further increase control performance. Then, by using Lyapunov theory and linear matrix inequality techniques, several sufficient criteria to calculate the controller and observer matrices are derived. Finally, the validity of the addressed strategy is verified via some simulation outcomes.

title: Structure-Enhanced RT-DETR for Real-Time Ship Detection in Remote Sensing Imagery
authors: Zhong Shao, Xihang Su, Jing Ma, Jiale Wang, Han Sheng
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Ship targets in remote sensing imagery typically exhibit elongated structural characteristics, significant scale variations, and are often embedded in complex sea-surface backgrounds, resulting in low separability between targets and background. Consequently, achieving high detection accuracy while satisfying real-time deployment requirements remains a challenging task in practical ship detection applications. To address the insufficient modeling capability of existing end-to-end detection methods for elongated structural targets under complex backgrounds, this paper proposes a structure-enhanced end-to-end ship detection method based on RT-DETR. Specifically, three dedicated modules—CSP-PPEA, PSMD, and MANSTARNet—are introduced into the stages of feature extraction, structural modeling, and feature fusion, respectively. These modules strengthen the representation of ship edges and structural features while effectively suppressing background interference such as sea-surface textures, thereby improving detection stability and robustness in complex scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate that, on the HRSC2016 dataset, the proposed method improves mAP50 by 6.03% and mAP50–95 by 4.26% compared with the RT-DETR-r18 baseline, while reducing the number of parameters by approximately 0.91M. Experiments on the MASATI-V2 dataset also achieve competitive overall detection performance, further verifying the adaptability of the proposed model in different marine remote sensing scenarios. In addition, cross-dataset experiments conducted on a ship subset constructed from the DOTA dataset further demonstrate the strong generalization capability of the proposed method across different remote sensing environments.

title: Automatic Segmentation of Placenta from MR images Using a Novel BiGC U-Net
authors: Dengjiang Wu, Suzie Coldman, Guanglong Zhou, Xinshan Li, Elspeth Whitby, Lingzhong Guo
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Accurate segmentation of the placenta in Magnetic Resonance (MR) images is required for quantitative techniques such as texture and shape analysis, which have been proposed to improve placenta accreta spectrum (PAS) diagnostic rates. However, it is challenging due to the low contrast, image noise, blurred boundaries, and manual annotation variability. Hence, we proposed an enhanced U-Net architecture named BiGC U-Net, to automatically segment placental MR images. This deep learning (DL) structure incorporates a bidirectional gated convolutional module (BiGC) to capture complementary spatial dependencies, a hierarchical regularization mechanism (HRM) to enhance crosslayer semantic consistency, and an innovative data augmentation strategy to synthesize new images. The performance of BiGC U-Net was evaluated on three placental MR datasets: (1) public, (2) Sheffield Teaching Hospitals (STH) and (3) combined (public + STH + augmented), and compared against existing DL models including U-Net, Attention U-Net, ResNet, UNet++, TransUNet, nnUNet, and SSM-Mamba. The BiGC U-Net exhibited the best Dice similarity coefficient (90.74 ± 0.44), 95th percentile Hausdorff distance (3.84 mm ± 0.53 mm) and relative volume difference (9.06 ± 0.41) in comparison with other DL models in the combined dataset. These findings indicate the effectiveness and robustness of the BiGC U-Net in accurately and automatically segmenting the placenta.

title: An Optimized Hybrid Deep Feature Fusion Framework for Hardware-Efficient Music Retrieval
authors: Xing Li, YuanYuan Xu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper introduces an optimized hybrid deep feature fusion framework designed for hardware-effi;cient music retrieval, specifically architected for heterogeneous AMD Xilinx FPGA platforms. To overcome the computational bottlenecks of probabilistic indexing in large-scale retrieval, the framework integrates three specialized hardware-accelerated engines, orchestrated via asynchronous ping-pong buffering and cycle-accurate AXI4-Stream scheduling to seamlessly synchronize dataflow across the PL and AIE domains: (1) a pipelined hybrid feature fusion module synthesized via Vivado HLS, which dynamically merges deep convolutional embeddings with statistical spectral descriptors to generate robust acoustic signatures, achieving a 93.8% retrieval precision with a processing latency of only 2.1 ms for 30-second audio segments; (2) an AI Engine-accelerated Variational Inference Processor that executes complex logistic normal approximations required by Correlated Topic Models (CTM) over an AXI4-Stream interconnect, optimizing covariance matrix operations to reduceBRAMutilization by 38% compared to standard architectures while sustaining a throughput of 480 audio segments per second; and (3) a DSP-optimized Topic Likelihood Estimator utilizing DSP48E2 slices to parallelize posterior probability calculations for rapid similarity matching, reducing inference latency by 5:6×relative to software-based Gibbs sampling and capping total power consumption at 14W. Validated on three benchmark music datasets (GTZAN, FMA-Small, and MagnaTagATune), the framework demonstrates a 91.5% Top-1 retrieval accuracy, outperforming baseline CNN-only approaches by 12.7% and achieving a 45% reduction in prediction variance across diverse musical textures. The system delivers 431× and 20× higher energy efficiency (29 mJ per inference) than equivalent high-performance CPU- and GPU-based solutions, respectively, along with a 48× smaller memory footprint (8.1 MB), facilitating deployment in edge-based music discovery systems. The complete hardware-software co-design has been verified on Xilinx Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC devices using the Vitis 2022.2 toolchain.

title: Numerical Study on the Effects of Cavitation Bubble Evolution on Dielectric Recovery Characteristics of Water
authors: Zhibo Yang, Haibing Li, Yuanhang Yu, Jianyi Wang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Water as a common medium is extensively used in pulsed power systems, high-voltage engineering, and insulation applications. This paper developed a numerical simulation model to study cavitation bubble evolution in water, realizing the first quantitative analysis of the coupling effect of bubble internal pressure variation on second breakdown voltage and recovery time. The bubble evolution dominated by pressure inside bubble was developed using the modified Rayleigh-Plesset equation, and the second breakdown voltage was calculated. The calculated results showed the precise values for second breakdown voltage and recovery time. A variation of internal pressure in the bubble’s first pulsation, which was dependent on the second breakdown voltage, was quantified by numerical calculation. The calculated voltages were in good agreement with the experimental results. The calculated results showed that the cavitation bubble could be compressed by surrounding medium, lower initial pressure of bubble results in a considerably shorter recovery time.

title: A Kernel Correlation-Based Enhanced Resolution Fault-Location Method for Lossy Transmission Lines
authors: Shao-Yin He, Chang-Jian Sun, Tong Zheng, Andrea Cozza, Zhao-Yang Wang, Yan-Zhao Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electromagnetic Compatibility
abstract: This article presents a nonlinear fault-location method that leverages a kernel correlation coefficient to map transient fault waveforms into a high-dimensional Hilbert space using a Gaussian radial basis function (RBF) kernel. This approach enhances spatial resolution by nonlinearly amplifying subtle differences between signals that appear similar in the original space. Unlike linear methods, the resolution is not limited by signal wavelength components but is controlled by a tunable kernel scale parameter, enabling a flexible tradeoff between accuracy and robustness, even under strong attenuation. Field validation using real fault data from a 10-kV distribution system shows that the proposed method achieves a location error of only 13 m (0.85%), whereas the error of the conventional natural frequency-based method ranges from 67 to 198 m (6.6% –9.6%). Moreover, a spatial resolution of 60 m based on low frequency components (below 120 kHz) was found, a fivefold improvement over the linear correlation metric. The proposed kernel-based framework provides an effective and scalable solution for high-resolution fault location in distribution networks and long HVDC power lines with complex topologies and strong attenuation characteristics.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices

title: High-Performance Photovoltaic-Fed DOEWIM Drive: A Backstepping and Reduced-Order Observer Approach with Matrix Converter Integration
authors: Sellah Mourad, Dourari Ahmed Lamine, Kouzou Abdellah, Naas Bachir, Souissi Amar, Boukhalfa Mohamed
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This article presents an advanced drive system for a Dual Open-End Winding Induction Motor (DOEWIM) powered exclusively by photovoltaic (PV) solar energy. The motor is fed by two three-level dual-output matrix converters, each modulated via Space Vector Modulation (SVM) to ensure high-quality power delivery. To enhance system autonomy and performance, a sensorless nonlinear backstepping control strategy is employed, enabling precise regulation of stator currents, rotor flux, and rotational speed without relying on mechanical sensors. Motor speed is accurately reconstructed in real time using a reduced-order state observer. Concurrently, a Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) algorithm ensures optimal harvesting of solar energy under varying irradiance conditions. The core contribution of this work lies in the seamless integration of renewable energy into high-performance induction motor drives featuring open-end winding topologies. This integration not only aligns with sustainable energy objectives but also delivers significant technical advantages: superior robustness to both internal parameter variations and external disturbances, markedly reduced torque and flux ripples, minimal harmonic distortion in stator currents, and complete elimination of common-mode voltage (CMV). Simulation results corroborate the efficacy of the proposed architecture, demonstrating its viability as a high-efficiency, reliable solution for next-generation solar-powered motor drives.

title: Autours' index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)

title: Innovative Sustainable Technology-Based Education: A Bibliometric Exploration of the Frontiers of Knowledge
authors: Najah Al Mohammedi, Hanan Shaher Almarashdi, Jihan Yousef, Baderaddin Yassin, Wafaa El Zaatari, Maha Alhabbash
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)
abstract: This bibliometric study investigates the edge of technological education of emerging sustainable technology based on a comprehensive review of academic papers for 20152025 using VOSviewer network visualisation. The study finds that there was a sharp surge in 2020, whereby annual publications increased threefold and peaked at 51 publications in 2024, a surge of 280%. There are three separate communities driving research: a highly connected European community that includes Shulla, Katerina, and Nikolaou, I.E., a bridge community led by Leal Filho, Walter D.S., and a new Latin American community. China, the United States, Spain, and Germany remain the geographical leaders, but India, Taiwan, Turkey, and Belgium are becoming frontier nations. Themed, the topic is a paradigm shift from technology-driven policies to equity-driven, community-driven innovation. The flagship study (2024-2025) focuses on access to justice, community, and green law, as well as the nexus of corporate social responsibility, digital empowerment, and Industry 4.0. This innovation introduces sustainable, technology-based education as a transformative approach to environmental sustainability and social justice, promoting the welfare of the world and humanity.

title: An Electronic Information Innovative Training Mode Base on Competition-Curriculum Collaboration
authors: Zhang Yan, Sui Jinxue, Liu Wei
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: Driven by New Engineering and the innovative nation strategy, engineering education is facing an urgent need to transition from knowledge teaching to innovation ability training. There are some problems in cultivation of electronic information specialty, such as weak engineering practice ability, lack of innovative thinking, and the disconnection between knowledge and engineering practice. This paper constructs a diversified innovation ability training mode, which is based on the guidance of high-level discipline competition, the in-depth reform of core courses, and the integration of online and offline learning. This mode integrates project driven and problem oriented into the whole teaching process of specialized courses through the “course-competition-practice” linkage, reconstructs the progressive curriculum system of “theory-design-practice-innovation” and the multi-level practice platform of “foundation-synthesisinnovation”, and implements the academic evaluation of multi-dimensional interactive teaching and diversified process assessment aiming at the cultivation of innovative ability. Practice shows that the training mode effectively stimulates students' innovative potential, and significantly improves their comprehensive abilities of development and design, team cooperation and solving complex engineering problems.

title: Correlation Analysis of Conversational Focus with Learning Experience in Python Courses
authors: Chen-Chieh Yen, Yen-Yu Chang, Ying-Zhu Chai, Pei-Tsen Hsieh, Kuei-Hao Li, Chia-Kai Chang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: This study aims to investigate how the depth and divergence of students' questions, when interacting with a coursespecific language model configured with a dedicated system prompt (UeduGPTs), relate to their learning outcomes. We collected dialogue data from a Python programming course and focused on analyzing students' questions to the model. Each dialogue was vectorized using the OpenAI embedding model, and a core topic vector was derived through an averaging algorithm with potential weighting to filter out semantically insignificant content. To evaluate the divergence of questions, we applied Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to the distribution of question embeddings. The proportion of variance explained by the first principal component was used as an indicator: a high proportion suggested concentrated, thematically coherent questioning, while a low proportion indicated more divergent questioning patterns. We then conducted correlation analyses, including Pearson correlation coefficients and regression models, to examine the relationship between this divergence indicator and student performance measures derived from questionnaires. This approach provides a quantitative framework for linking students' questioning strategies with learning outcomes and offers a novel perspective on evaluating dialogue-based learning processes.

title: A Dual-stage Spectral–spatial Deep Learning Framework for Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Manoj Sagar Kuppusamy, S Babu, Sujit Das
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Hyperspectral images(HSI), especially in remote sensing applications, are known for high-dimensional and spatial variability. This makes the classification of HSI a challenging task. The proposed methodology contains a two-stage deep learning framework that is devised to take advantage of both spectral and spatial information which improves HSI classification further. In the first phase, spectral features are extracted using a ResNet50 deep network, followed by an optimal band selection using KMeans clustering. The optimal band choice reduce redundancy effectively for the classification process by keeping discriminatory class information intact. In the second phase, a U-Net deep network is used to selected optimal bands. It provides a final classification map that captures both spatial structure and contextual relationships. A detail analysis in the spectral domain has been incorporated with spatial domain information using the proposed method, which provides excellent results with over all accuracy 99.09% in benchmark dataset such as Indian Pines. The simulation results represents the effectiveness of the proposed method which has outperformed the existing methods overwhelmingly based on comparative parameters.

title: Ai-Enabled Fire Fighting Robot Review Paper
authors: Naveen Kumar Singh, Aryan Vashistha, Kritika Upadhyay, Anil Kumar Sagar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Fire outbreaks continue to pose serious risks to human life, property, and the environment. Traditional fire detection and suppression systems often suffer from delayed response times, high false alarm rates, and limited adaptability in dynamic or hazardous environments. Recent advances in Artificial Intelligence (AI), Internet of Things (IoT), and robotics have paved the way for intelligent, autonomous fire-fighting systems. This paper presents a systematic review of seventeen peer-reviewed studies focusing on AI-enabled fire-fighting robots, categorizing them under fire detection, navigation, communication, and suppression modules. A structured methodology was used to select and analyze relevant studies from 2018 to 2024. The paper highlights the integration of vision-based AI models such as CNNs and YOLO, multi-sensor fusion, and edge computing for real-time fire detection and response. Additionally, it discusses communication protocols (5G, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth), modular robot designs, and swarm intelligence for coordinated action. A conceptual framework for a future AI-enabled fire-fighting robot is proposed, emphasizing real-time detection, autonomous navigation, and cloud-based alerts. This review identifies key research gaps, such as the need for standardized datasets, robust communication under failure scenarios, and real-world testing, and provides directions for future work to develop cost-effective, scalable, and reliable fire-fighting solutions.

title: AI-Powered Period Tracker: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models for Menstrual Health Prediction
authors: H Harshavardhan, Nausha Tendulkar, Prathi U Shetty, Rithika P Shetty, Sakshi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Menstrual health is a critical factor in the overall welfare of women, yet most people still depend on inaccurate and non-personalized manual or calendar-based tracking systems. This paper introduces a period tracker that utilizes artificial intelligence to forecast menstrual cycles, fertile windows, and premenstrual symptoms with greater reliability. The frame-work utilizes user-inputted cycle history and symptom information to train and compare various machine learning and deep learning algorithms such as Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. Comparative analysis of the algorithms reveals that although older methods like Logistic Regression and SVM yield baseline accuracy, Random Forest demonstrates improved performance for classification tasks. But LSTM, the most appropriate for time-series data, produced the best accuracy of 90% providing better prediction of abnormal cycles and symptom patterns. The findings demonstrate the potential for AI to provide reliable, personalized menstrual health predictions, leading to better reproductive health knowledge and active self-care. Future areas of development are wearable integration, symptom-driven health advice, and chatbot-based interaction for greater user engagement.

title: EmergIN 2025 TOC
authors: N/A
