title: Automated Depression Diagnosis using EEG Signal Analysis
authors: Meena L C, Chidambarathanu K, Sankara Narayanan S, Karthikram Anbalagan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Depression, which is an endemic mental disorder, remains an enormous burden to the people and the society in general. Addressing this urgent issue, the given research attempts to offer the progressive solution to the problem of detecting depressive symptoms by examining the electroencephalogram (EEG) biomarkers. Using the potentials of the MNE library in Python, this paper will investigate a new approach of detecting signs of depression using EEG recordings. The data set used is EEG records of 181 patients in a hospital. Bandpass filter is employed to improve signal quality and minimize noise. The key characteristics such as Alpha, Beta and Theta band powers and such indices as Alpha Power Variation, Relative Gamma Wave and Spectral Asymmetry Index are then derived out of the preprocessed EEG data. These characteristics act as input variables to the training of four different models, namely, Support Vector Machine (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Decision Tree and Logistic Regression. These models are thoroughly tested using a supervised learning paradigm to determine the accuracy of the model in detecting depression when offered a similar dataset of EEG signals. The broader scope of this method would be to not only find EEG biomarkers in depression but also develop predictive models that can be reliable enough to differentiate individuals with depressive symptoms at an early stage. By combining data analysis of EEGs and supervised learning techniques, the study can contribute to the rapidly growing area of computational psychiatry, as it provides a solid model to detect depression. The results are highly promising in improving the personalized management and early detection of depression thus contributing to the improvement in mental health care practices.

title: Learning Urban Heat Signatures via Coordinated Multi-Stream Convolution and Attention-Based Fusion
authors: Sachin Jadhav, Aaditya Patil, Parth Kalani, Omkar Lolage, Nishant Lanjewar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Urban Heat Islands (UHIs) exacerbate public health and environmental challenges in rapidly urbanizing regions. This study presents a coordinated multi-stream convolutional encoder and attention-based fusion network to learn urban heat signatures from three geospatial inputs: PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf> (particulate matter), land surface temperature (LST), and high-resolution land cover maps. Using Google Earth Engine (GEE) datasets MOD11A2 LST, GLC-FCS30D land cover, global satellite-derived PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf>—we train our attention-U-Net fusion model to predict Urban Heat Island Intensity (UHII). Comprehensive experiments over six major Indian cities demonstrate that the proposed architecture achieves superior performance with mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.814 C, root mean square error (RMSE) of 1.253 C, and structural similarity index (SSIM) of 0.047 through leave-one-city-out cross-validation. The model yields interpretable feature and attention maps that align with known UHI patterns. This framework offers a scalable, data-driven tool for urban planners to assess heat risk drivers and guide mitigation strategies across diverse urban environments.

title: SimEV 1.1: A Computational Framework for Electric Vehicle Simulation, Performance Analytics and System-Level Optimization
authors: Swapnil Srivastava, Abdul Zeeshan, Vinod Kumar Vishwakarma, Manish Kumar Singh, Jay Singh, Rajeev Kumar Chauhan
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: The rapidly growing demand for acquiring electric vehicles (EV) have created need for reliable simulation tools which are capable of modeling energy/power flow, vehicle performance, and component sizing estimation. SimEV 1.1 developed by Dorleco Technologies, is a unique simulation tool which estimates system sizing and characteristics with complete vehicle dynamics. This paper presents a detailed study of the SimEV 1.1 tool, its architecture, its analytical capability and its utility for researchers and academicians. It is demonstrated that how the simulation tool helps the users in understanding energy flow/ consumption, torque speed characteristics, and validating system design matching real world scenario. The analysis confirms that SimEV 1.1 offer significant improvements as compared to other restricted EV simulation platforms available as on date.

title: A Novel Implementation Smart IoT Device GSM - Based LPG Leakage Detection & Prevention System
authors: Tanya Nayak, Utkarsh Kumar, Tanmay Nayak, Sarthak Sharma, Kamal Bhatia, Yogesh Kumar Verma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Reports and articles clearly demonstrate that workers in industrial settings and residents at home face continuous risk due to the increasing frequency of gas leaks. Whether CNG or LPG is used in industrial or residential environments, the underlying danger of potential mishaps remains significant. Preventive action is essential to ensure the safety and well-being of all individuals involved. While various gases serve as fuels in our daily lives, LPG stands as the most prevalent, with LPG gas leakage being responsible for most residential gas explosion cases in India. Therefore, implementing a system that can help mitigate and reduce risks in these environments is of paramount importance. The main objective of our study is to create and implement a low-cost, easily accessible LPG gas leakage detection and prevention module that can provide enhanced safety measures in both residential and industrial settings. To achieve this goal, we constructed a LPG gas leakage detection and prevention system based on ESP 32 and subsequently tested it against commercially available kits in the market. Through our testing, the module has proven to be highly dependable and reliable, accurately detecting high concentrations of LPG gases. Consequently, this system will prove instrumental in saving lives by preventing fire accidents and explosions caused by gas leakages. The market is very competitive, many systems are present that provide either detection or prevention functionality; but the novelty of our system provides the perfect blend of both detection and prevention functionalities combined with the advanced deployment of technologies like GSM and Cloud operations.

title: FaceEmo Recognition System Using Voting-Based Ensemble Transfer Learning
authors: Shreya Chandrakar, Gyanendra K. Verma
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Facial emotion recognition is a crucial component in human computer interaction, uplifting smart systems to interpret & responds to human emotions. This research addresses the challenges of emotions recognition from multiple facial expressions using Deep Learning (DL) methods. Firstly, to enhance the input image quality, preprocessing steps such as scaling, normalization, data augmentation and data balancing were applied. Next, the proposed study is applied in Lightweight Convolutional Neural Networks (LCNNs) models such as, MobileNetV2 and ResNet50 for emotion classification. Lastly, to improvise the performance of the model, a hard voting-based ensemble approach is applied. The proposed approach is evaluated on the AffectNet dataset and achieves an accuracy of 74.02% outperforming standalone models. These outcomes present the effectiveness of Ensemble Learning (EL) for facial emotion recognition and also boosting its potential applications in Affective computing, cognitive monitoring and adaptive human computer interactions.

title: Predictive Analysis of Malignant Risk Scoring in Non-Cancerous Skin Diseases Using Deep Learning
authors: Sandhya B, Malathi G
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The neoplastic development of clinically benign skin lesions into carcinomas like melanoma, basal cell carcinoma (BCC), or squamous cell carcinoma (SCC) is a crucial diagnostic problem. Conventional diagnostic techniques like dermoscopic examination and biopsy are usually invasive, time-consuming, and prone to observer variation. To overcome this, the current study proposes a regression-oriented deep learning architecture that forecasts the malignancy risk scores in non-cancerous skin lesions based on dermoscopic images and corresponding patient metadata. Based on the PAD-UFES-20 dataset, five state-of-the-art models were utilized: EfficientNetV2L with LightGBM, Inception-ResNet-v2 with a Vision Transformer (ViT), MABSCNet, TurkerNet (lightweight CNN), and Xception coupled with a Support Vector Regressor (SVR). Each model was optimized to produce a continuous malignancy risk score optimized through Mean Squared Error (MSE) loss. The results of the evaluations prove that the presented models can differentiate between benign lesions and those with early malignant-like appearance under simulated progression non-invasively.

title: A Deep Learning Framework with Attention Mechanisms for Voltage Stability Classification in EV Charging Systems
authors: Kanaka Raju Kalla, R. Ramya Swetha, Saswati Kumari Behera, Ranjan Keshari Pati, G. Sanyasi Naidu, Pramod Kumar Gouda, Vijaychandra Joddumahanthi, Jayalaxmi Anem, B. Srinivasarao
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The rapid growth of electric vehicles (EVs) introduces significant challenges in maintaining voltage stability within smart grid environments. Traditional control methods often fail to anticipate the variability and scale of EV charging behaviors. This paper presents a deep learning-based framework for classifying voltage stability categories in EV integrated distribution systems. A dataset of contextual and real-time grid parameters is used to train and evaluate three models: a simple neural network, a multilayer perceptron (MLP), and TabNet, a modern attention-based architecture optimized for structured tabular data. Models are assessed using accuracy, precision, recall, and the F1 score to gauge classification performance. Among the three, TabNet achieved the highest accuracy of 90.50 percent, with all evaluation metrics exceeding 90 percent, demonstrating its superior capability to capture nonlinear feature interaction. This study contributes a robust and scalable approach to predictive voltage stability classification, with direct implications for improving grid resilience under rising EV load conditions. The integration of interpretability and high accuracy makes the framework well suited for real-time applications in smart grid monitoring and control.

title: AI-Powered Learning Ecosystems: Shaping the Future of Professional Education
authors: Jaishree Jain, Inderjeet Kaur, Santosh Kumar Upadhyay, Sumit Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: A major development in contemporary education, AI-powered interactive learning systems seek to improve the learning process through individualized, captivating, and adaptable approaches. These platforms change conventional learning paradigms by utilizing artificial intelligence to produce dynamic learning environments that meet the various demands of students. With the advent of interactive 2D and 3D simulations, students may become fully immersed in their studies, which makes difficult ideas more approachable and entertaining. By transforming studying into an entertaining game where they can win prizes and reach milestones, gamification components further inspire pupils and encourage dedication to their academic path. Additionally, these systems use real- time data analytics to customize content based on each learner's unique learning preferences and speed, guaranteeing that every student gets the help they require to effectively grasp concepts. When compared to traditional static content, this tailored approach not only improves understanding but also dramatically increases information retention rates. Bilingual resources encourage diversity by allowing students from different language backgrounds to fully interact with the content. In addition to tackling the issues of packed classrooms and diverse student demands, educational institutions are preparing students for a future in which critical thinking, creativity, and problem-solving abilities will be crucial by embracing these AI-driven solutions more and more. Essentially, interactive learning systems driven by AI are revolutionizing education by fusing cutting-edge technology with educational techniques. They provide a comprehensive strategy that goes beyond academic performance, developing critical abilities that will prepare pupils for succession a world that is constantly changing. We examine user success stories, quantitative data, and subjective comments to determine the impact of our used solution.

title: Evaluating the Impact of Solar PV Integration on the Performance and Stability of Practical Distribution Systems
authors: M Vamsikrishna, K Kiran, Subramanya Sarma Saraswathula, Minu Balakrishnan, Priyanka Rani, Deekshant Varshney
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: The integration of Solar Photovoltaic (PV) systems into modern distribution networks has become a critical factor of whether energy supply can be guaranteed and at the same time curtail carbon emissions. Unlike the purely theoretical work, which has been done in this area, this paper gives a relatively comprehensive analysis of the issue. It looks into what happens when Solar PV panels are integrated and becomes increasingly involved in lateral distribution systems. Alongside penetration level we evaluated these metrics-the rate at which power is lost or dissipated, network reliability and variations of Voltage induced by changing loads. The study also looks at how the distribution system as a whole respond to the unpredictable volatility of solar generation. Concludes that further problems, including Reverse Power Flow issues and instability in grid interconnections, will emerge due to this new phenomenon. This paper presents simulation results based on actual distribution network data. These results show both the advantages and the operational risks of Solar PV integration. They provide us with valuable insights on how to handle PV resources beneficially and also make future distribution networks more resilient.

title: DeepSCS+C: A Physics-Regularized Network for Adaptive Topographic Correction in Mountainous Forests
authors: Yang Song, Enping Yan, Yubin Tang, Hua Sun, Dengkui Mo
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Topographic radiation distortion, induced by terrain relief, is a significant challenge limiting the accuracy of quantitative analysis in mountainous remote sensing applications. Although semi-empirical topographic correction models, such as the widely used SCS+C, are prevalent, they often exhibit significant overcorrection tendencies and spectral distortions in steep terrain, presenting unresolved challenges. This study proposes DeepSCS+C, a physics-regularized network for adaptive topographic correction in mountainous forests. By characterizing a spatially varying C-field from multiscale terrain features, this framework utilizes SCS+C outputs as weakly supervised proxy labels to guide optimization while effectively suppressing intrinsic over-correction noise. Subsequently, using Sentinel-2 imagery of mountainous forested areas, the performance of the DSC model was systematically validated, and multidimensional evaluation criteria confirmed its robust performance. Experiments demonstrated that the DSC framework mitigates the terrain shadow effect with an efficacy comparable to traditional models and leverages the nonlinear mapping capabilities of deep learning to restore the intrinsic spectral characteristics of surface features accurately. The model’s effectiveness is evidenced by several key metrics: the correlation between reflectance and illumination conditions was reduced to near zero (R2 < 0.1); the spectral variance in forested areas decreased by 47.99% in the near-infrared band compared to the original imagery; and the radiance difference between sunlit and shaded slopes converged to a near zero level (<1.0W m−2 sr−1 μm−1) across all bands. The findings of this research present a promising and advanced solution for high-precision topographic correction.

title: Focus on Pinning Properties of RE -Ba-Cu-O Bulks and Tapes via Lift Factor Analysis
authors: Ya Shi, Jiale Liu, Haoran Jiang, Dabin Wei, Kai Zhang, Yibing Zhang, Difan Zhou, Chuanbing Cai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: RE-Ba-Cu-O bulk superconductors can be magnetized to generate high flux density, making them promising for applications such as desktop NMR&MRI, HTS undulators and hybrid magnets assembled with magnets wound by HTS tapes. For these novel concepts, the pinning properties of RE-Ba-Cu-O bulk materials are critical for generating high trapped field and resisting demagnetization especially in cryogenic environments. In the presentation, we are going to report the trapped field of various RE-Ba-Cu-O samples (samples consisting different RE elements and chemical doping) under different temperatures and their lift factor as a macroscopic parameter. The lift factor of trapped field will be compared with that of the HTS tapes to reveal the difference in pinning characteristics between thin film and bulk HTS materials. Best on the achieved understanding, optimization from the material point of view will be proposed.

title: Integrated Design of Data-Driven Fault Detection and Fault-Tolerant Control for Industrial Systems Based on Nuclear Norm Subspace Identification Under Limited Samples
authors: Biao Li, Jinzhu Peng, Lina Yao, Hui Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Considering situations such as sensor failures and communication losses, limited data samples are a common challenge in actual industrial processes, making the traditional integrated architecture of fault detection (FD) and fault-tolerant control (FTC) based on subspace identification difficult to be applicable. Regarding this problem, this article proposes a nuclear norm-based subspace identification method for FD and FTC. This method leverages key structural matrix properties in the input and output data model, alleviating reliance on data samples. The parameter matrices required to construct the fault detector and fault-tolerant controller can be directly identified within the nuclear norm optimization framework, enabling the design of an integrated FD and FTC architecture. Two case studies demonstrate that the developed method enhances detection and control performance compared with traditional subspace identification methods, particularly in the case of limited data samples.

title: Investigating Virtual Reality in Industrial Design Education: Reducing Cognitive Load During Factory Equipment Training
authors: Yo-Wen Liang, Yu-Teng Lin
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: This study investigates the effectiveness of Virtual Reality (VR) in reducing cognitive workload during factory equipment training for industrial design students. Traditional hands-on instruction, though authentic, often results in high cognitive demands due to safety concerns, equipment limitations, and unequal learning access. A controlled experiment involving 30 undergraduate students (aged 19-21, with no prior machine experience) compared conventional training with a VR-based teaching module simulating a bandsaw machine. The VR environment was constructed using Autodesk 3ds Max and Unreal Engine and structured into three interactive stages: demonstration, guided operation, and self-directed practice. Workload was evaluated using the NASA Task Load Index (NASA-TLX) across six dimensions. Paired-sample t-tests revealed significant workload reductions in Mental Demands, Temporal Demands, Performance, Effort, and Frustration (p < 0.05), with no significant difference in Physical Demands. The total NASA-TLX score decreased from 52.82 (traditional) to 37.51 (VR), confirming the cognitive benefits of VR-based training. These findings highlight VR's potential to enhance learning efficiency, reduce student anxiety, and provide safe, scalable instructional alternatives in industrial design education.

title: AI-Powered Early Warning Systems for Resource-Constrained Schools: A Lightweight Machine Learning Framework for Predicting Student Performance
authors: Alidor M. Mbayandjambe, Fiston O. Oshasha, Jean Didier M. Batubenga, Grevi B. Nkwimi, Vladislav Slavov, Kyandoghere Kyamakya, Tasho Tashev, Selain K. Kasereka
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)
abstract: Identifying at-risk students early is essential for improving educational outcomes, especially in resource-limited secondary schools that lack access to advanced AI systems due to financial constraints, limited technical infrastructure, insufficient human expertise, and fragmented or low-quality student data. Although accuracy values above 90% are often reported in the literature, such performances typically rely on intermediate-term grades (G1 and G2), large datasets, and highend computational resources. In realistic early-warning conditions where such intermediate grades are unavailable as in the present study accuracy levels between $\mathbf{6 0 - 7 2 \%}$ are commonly observed. Our framework therefore aligns with the upper bounds of early-stage prediction accuracy while remaining fully compatible with low-resource environments. This paper presents a lightweight machine learning framework tailored for such environments to predict student performance and support early intervention. Five efficient machine learning algorithms, including Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and Naive Bayes, were evaluated using readily available student data. Logistic Regression achieved the best overall performance with 68.35% accuracy, 72.58% precision, 84.91% recall, and 0.7826 F1-score on the test set. The notably high recall ensures that most at-risk students are correctly identified. The system categorizes students into risk levels (High, Medium, Low), enabling strategic resource allocation. Key predictive factors include past failures, age, higher education aspiration, and social activity patterns. The framework operates offline on CPU-only hardware, requires less than 2 seconds of training time on standard laptops, and functions effectively with datasets of $\mathbf{3 0 0} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{4 0 0}$ students and basic teacher training ($2-4$ hours). This approach demonstrates that accessible AI tools can empower underserved schools to implement data-driven interventions with minimal infrastructure.

title: ResNet50 and VGG16 in ICH Diagnosis: Quantifying the Performance- Stability Trade-Off
authors: M.Swathika, R.I.Minu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Intracranial Hemorrhage (ICH), is a severe health problem. The timely detection along with accurate identification of not just the presence of bleeding, but its subtypes and location is crucial for guiding clinical intervention. The proposed model addresses the need for the refined diagnostic tools by performing an implementation and comparative evaluation of two widely adopted Convolutional Neural Network architectures, VGG16 and ResNet50, for the six class classification of ICH. The target classes will be the five major hemorrhage subtypes like Epidural, Subdural, Subarachnoid, Intra parenchymal, Intraventricular and a No Hemorrhage class. The models are trained using a publicly available CT dataset in Kaggle database and applied all the necessary steps like normalization, rotation, etc., The results show the superior performance of ResNet50 which accurately distinguished between the subtypes, achieving the accuracy of 95.41% whereas the VGG16 model’s accuracy is 94.81%. Though VGG16 exhibited better training stability, the overall discriminative performance of ResNet50 makes it the preferred architecture for this diagnostic task.

title: Hybrid EffiNet-GRU: Integrating XAI with Deep Learning Models for Breast Cancer Prediction through Histological Image Analysis
authors: Kalangi Praveen Kumar, P Sammulal
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Breast cancer is one of the leading causes of death worldwide, emphasizing the need for accurate and early diagnosis of the disease. This study created a hybrid deep learning model based on EfficientNetV2 and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) networks, supported by Explainable AI via Grad-CAM++, to improve the identification and classification of breast cancer from histopathology images using the BreakHis dataset. For the efficient construction of strong multi-scale spatial features from complicated tissue images and GRU to find sequential dependencies between these powerful features to enhance context awareness. With Grad-CAM++, the model becomes more interpretable as we create heatmaps that demonstrate which areas are crucial for predictions. This increases the likelihood that doctors will trust and use the model. The experimental results demonstrated significant improvements in the performance. Given a batch size of 16 and 50 epochs, model achieved an accuracy of 98.72%, precision was at 98.15%, recall had provided it with rate of 95.68% and F1-score was maintained at the level of approximately 96.82%. Using a batch size of 32 and epoch of 30, it achieved an accuracy of 94.50%, a precision of 97.00%, recall 94.10% and F1-score 93.30 of %. It achieved the 94.7% accuracy, 96.5% precision, 94.1% recall and the F1-score of 94.30%, respectively, with a batch size of 64 and epochs of 20. The proposed approach addresses the problem of tissue heterogeneity and shape variations for automated breast cancer histopathology image analysis in a robust, interpretable, and scalable manner. This study highlights the potential of combining spatiotemporal architectures with explainable AI (XAI) for improved cancer diagnosis and clinical decision support.

title: A Multimodal Deep Learning Framework for Automated Scalp Psoriasis Detection and Severity Assessment
authors: Shaik Sumiya Sultana, V. Sathyendra Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Scalp psoriasis is a persistent inflammatory skin condition that is often confused with dandruff or seborrheic dermatitis because of their similar visual appearance. This makes early diagnosis difficult, especially in settings where dermatologists are not easily accessible. Most existing automated systems focus only on image classification and do not consider the patient’s clinical symptoms, which reduces their diagnostic reliability. To address this limitation, this study proposes a multimodal deep learning framework that jointly analyzes scalp images and structured clinical features for automated psoriasis detection and severity prediction. The model integrates a CNN-based image encoder, a clinical-feature encoder, and a Grad-CAM module to highlight affected scalp regions. During evaluation, the proposed system achieved an overall accuracy of 96.42%, with strong regression performance for severity scoring, obtaining a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.11, a Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 0.18, and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.94. These results show that combining image features with patient-specific clinical information significantly improves diagnostic stability and severity estimation. The proposed framework provides a practical and interpretable solution for computer aided scalp psoriasis screening and may support real-world dermatology decision-making.

title: Predictive Finance Maintenance of Textile Machinery using Machine Learning Models
authors: R.Priyadharshini, Dharaneshwaran V, Dhibesh S, Hemalatha G, Ravikumar S, Vinitha K
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: The textile industry is supported by the long-term stability of the machinery, the breakdown of which leads to considerable uncontrolled loss of time, the reorganization of production processes, and the cost of maintenance. To overcome this problem, machine learning models have been employed together with predictive maintenance to predict failures and optimize maintenance schedules. The proposed model will be based on the works of the trainers of Random Forest, Support Vector Machines, and Gradient Boosting using sensor and financial cost data, which will be collected throughout the textile machine operation. Based on the experimental results, it can be seen that the performance of the Random Forest model has been demonstrated to be better than that of the other models tested, and with six times more data, the accuracy of the model was 96.8%, the precision was 95.7%, and the recall was 96.4%. A comparative analysis with other methods reveals that reliability increases by approximately 4-6% and the maintenance cost is reduced by 12%. The proposed method will enable improved decision-making in maintenance planning, lead to a reduction in unplanned breakdowns, and facilitate the allocation of improved resources. A scalable solution to the problem of intelligent maintenance in the textile industries with significant financial and operational advantages.

title: Smart Gesture Interpretation and Speech Synthesis System using AI
authors: Veeresh M Patri, Revathi V, George Fernandez
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Deaf people and individuals who are Deaf or hard of hearing, unable to speak, rely on sign language to convey messages to others and their communities. Sign languages are complex, previously agreed upon visual manual languages that convey messages using hand actions. Smart Gesture Interpretation and Speech Synthesis system using advanced Artificial Intelligence and Computer Vision technologies to fill communication gaps between the deaf and hard of hearing communities. The system integrates Media Pipe and OpenCV to produce a real time interpretative platform that detects hand movements using a webcam. MediaPipe’s sophisticated hand tracking tracks 21 hand landmarks, while OpenCV operates on images with incredible speed and precision. The system includes audio output by Google Text to Speech technology, translating detected sign language or gestures into words and audio directly. This forms an end to end communication bridge between individuals who are hard of hearing and unable to speak. This revolutionary solution empowers individuals.

title: Echo-Sight: AI-Driven Autonomous UAV Surveillance System for Real-Time Object Detection and Monitoring
authors: A Ramesh Kumar, P S Giriprasath, S Karthikeyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Echo-Sight presents an AI-driven autonomous unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) surveillance system designed for real-time object detection and monitoring with minimal human intervention. The platform integrates a YOLO-based deep learning model with embedded edge computing, high-resolution imaging, and GNSS-assisted navigation to enable intelligent aerial perception during autonomous missions. Field experiments were conducted in open and semi-urban environments to evaluate detection accuracy, flight stability, and operational reliability. The system achieved a mean detection precision greater than 85% in humans, vehicles, animals, and obstacles, with a real-time inference speed of approximately 0.25 s per frame. Autonomous flight tests demonstrated an average mission duration of 21.2 minutes, a GNSS navigation error below 2 m, and a mission success rate of 95%, confirming stable operation under practical conditions. Echo-Sight effectively supports applications such as security surveillance, wildlife monitoring, and industrial inspection. Although limitations remain in communication range and onboard processing capacity, the results validate the feasibility of integrating embedded AI with UAV platforms for scalable intelligent surveillance. Future enhancements will focus on advanced sensors, extended communication, improved onboard computation, and coordinated multi-drone deployment to further improve autonomy and coverage.

title: EdgeIDS: Distributed Machine Learning Framework for Scalable IoT Network Protection
authors: Gaurav Sarraf
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Detection and prevention of network attacks have become important in the industrial sector in the fast-changing cybersecurity environment. The research offers a robust intrusion detection architecture for IoT environments using a CNN-GRU deep learning framework, which stands for hybrid Convolutional Neural Network and Gated Recurrent Unit. Using the Edge-IIoT-2022 dataset, the research applies class imbalance (Random Under-Sampling) and feature importance (Random Forest-based selection). The proposed model shows better performance with accuracy of 99.99%, precision of 99.87%, recall of 99.99% and F1score of 99.32% as compared to the baseline models like DT (Decision Tree), LDA (Linear Discriminant Analysis), RNN (Recurrent Neural Network) and AdaBoost (Adaptive Boosting). The model achieves high convergence rates and low loss rates, and AUC scores are perfect across all classes, which validates its generalization ability. These results support the validity of the merging of spatial and temporal learning based on CNN and GRU layers supported by robust preprocessing and feature selection. The findings demonstrate that the hybrid CNN-GRU is the most reliable and accurate choice for intrusion detection in IIoT scenarios.

title: IoT Enhanced Farming and the Revolution in Crop and Soil Monitoring
authors: Ankur Ranjan Dass, Tushar, Anshika Verma, Nandita Pal, Utkrisht Chaurasia, Nandita Pradhan, Pooja Jaishwal, Venktesh Mishra
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: In greenhouse growing, it's necessary to constantly analyze the microclimate and soil conditions, because these are directly responsible for the growth of plants or disease/pestic occurrence. Traditional manual means are low, laborious and not compatible with real-time analysis. This article describes a low-cost, Raspberry Pi-based intelligent multi-sensor system pertaining to smart greenhouse applications. The sensing platform incorporates soil moisture, temperature-humidity compound (DHT11), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{p H}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{C O}_{\mathrm{2}}$</tex> (Sensirion SCD30) sensors, UV sensor, harmful-gases (MQ-135) sensor and leaf phi-color transducer TCS3200 for an accurate real-time estimate of agronomic parameters. Ventilation and watering is automatic by relay controlled actuators, which react instantly in case of shifting over threshold. The alarms are activated in case one of the parameters is not within user-specified values and emailed to your mailbox, but all the data is remotely controlled in the cloud. One of the most interesting functions is leaf color analysis this option will enable you to detect the initial signs of nutrient deficiencies, wilting and plant stress. The results of the experiments demonstrate that the system is better to overcome changes in the environment and faster to detect issues with plant health. The system is scalable and modular which fits the size of any type of greenhouse (small to commercial) and provides a convenient yet smart solution to the automated data-driven automation.

title: Spectral Unmixing for Identification of Colorants in Candies Using Hyperspectral Imaging
authors: John Shiny J, Sathya Bama B, Ananth N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Identifying food colorants is an important step in food quality and safety, as colors play an important role in influencing consumer preferences. This work focuses on the identification of synthetic food colorants in gems candies in a non-destructive manner. Hyperspectral imaging system (HSI) operating in the Visible-Near Infrared (VIS-NIR) range from 400 to 1000 nm is employed to acquire the spectrum data. Conventional techniques like High Performance Liquid Chromatography, Thin Layer Chromatography require a complex sample preparation and are time consuming. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a spectral unmixing method based on Nonnegative Matrix Factorization (NMF) to extract pure spectral signatures along with their corresponding abundance fractions and end-members. Additionally, Sparse NMF and Multilayer NMF are utilized to enhance spectral separation. The proposed method offers a reliable and non-destructive alternative to food colorant analysis.

title: Optoelectronic Tuning of Sputtered ZnO–Au–Ag (NPs) Films Through Gold Interlayer Design
authors: K. Kacha, F. Djeffal, R. Nezzar, A. Bendjrad, A. Benhaya
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This study investigates the tunability of the optoelectronic properties of ZnO-Au-Ag nanoparticle (NP) heterostructures through accurate control of the intermediate gold layer thickness. Three different configurations were fabricated via RF magnetron sputtering, followed by post-deposition annealing at 300 μC to induce the formation of Ag nanoparticles while preserving the Au interlayer. The resulting ZnO-Au-Ag (NP) structures exhibited Au ultrathin layers with different thicknesses. Optical characterization revealed a bandgap modulation from 3.35 to 3.66 eV, demonstrating the strong influence of plasmonic coupling between the Au interlayer and Ag nanoparticles. The incorporation of the gold layer enhanced visible-light absorption while maintaining UV transparency, with absorption at 550 nm increasing from 48.2% to 51.8%, despite the higher metallic content. Electrical transport measurements indicated a significant increase in carrier concentration, from 4.04 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">19</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup> (reference) to 3.37 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">22</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup> for the 4 nm Au layer, confirming efficient carrier activation through localized surface plasmon resonance (LSPR). These findings highlight the potential of ZnO-Au-Ag (NP) heterostructures for the development of nextgeneration transparent conducting electrodes and plasmon-enhanced electronic devices. In addition, the electrical and optical properties can be tuned by increasing the thickness of different layers for power optoelectronic applications.

title: EmergIN 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)

title: Investigation on Deformation Response and Excavation Method Optimization in Large-Span Tunnel Engineering
authors: Yazhen Sun, Xiaopeng Yang, Wei Cheng, Lifan Yuan, Jinchang Wang, Weize Zhao
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: Large-span tunnels present significant construction risks and stability challenges due to their wide cross sections, low flatness ratios, and complex multi-step excavation sequences. To support the rational selection and optimization of construction methods, this study conducts a comprehensive investigation on a representative large-span highway tunnel in Zhejiang Province by integrating refined three-dimensional numerical simulations with systematic field monitoring. The deformation characteristics of the ground surface, tunnel crown, and surrounding rock are analyzed under different excavation schemes. The findings indicate that both the three-bench seven-step method and the CRD technique induce the most pronounced crown settlement and ground surface subsidence, mainly because of their greater excavation disturbance and delayed support installation. By comparison, the double-side guide pit approach effectively restrains deformation and exhibits markedly better construction stability. Differences in excavation sequences result in varying magnitudes and rates of stress relief and disturbance propagation, which are identified as the dominant factors controlling deformation behavior. Among them, step 3 causes the least disturbance to the surrounding rock. The findings clarify the underlying deformation mechanisms associated with different excavation strategies and provide a scientific basis for optimizing construction methods and support parameters for large-span tunnels.

title: Review And Analyze Brain Tumor Segmentation Techniques Using Deep Learning Techniques : Detection and classification of Brain Tumors using Machine Learnig Algorithm
authors: Shivani Sharma, Supriya Raheja, Vivek Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Brain Tumor Segmentation is a Technique for identifying the brain tissues based on the tumor The detection and treatment planning of brain tumors necessitate advanced medical imaging techniques for accurate assessment and planning for treatment based on MRI. There is no accurate segmentation process for segmenting brain tumor. Brain Tumors characterized by uncontrolled cell growth, pose a severe threat to health. Researchers are actively interested in the segmentation and processing of medical images. With the development of CT and MRI, it has advanced to a fantastic point in tumor diagnosis. Brain tumors can be detected with MRI, and segmentation is used to extract the relevant information from an image. This work aims to give a general review of several image segmentation techniques employing MR images, such as the watershed algorithm, morphological operations, thresholding, clustering, C-means, neutrophilic sets, etc.

title: From Prediction to Prognosis: Explainable Machine Learning for Obesity Risk Classification
authors: Vedanshu Singh, Pushkar Kumar Jha, Amit Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Obesity is a growing global health challenge with severe consequences for morbidity and mortality. Machine learning provides opportunities for early obesity risk stratification, yet many predictive models suffer from poor probability calibration and limited interpretability. In this work, We suggest a comprehensive paradigm for predicting the risk of obesity that unifies high-performing classification with calibrated prognosis and explainability. Using a public dataset of 2,111 individuals with demographic, anthropometric, and lifestyle attributes, we benchmarked five classifiers (Decision Tree, Random Forest, XGBoost, SVM, and KNN). Ensemble models outperformed baselines, with XGBoost achieving 96.2% accuracy and Random Forest 93.9%. To extend beyond diagnosis, we trained calibrated Random Forest models to generate individual-level risk scores (0–100) for (i) obesity-only and (ii) overweight-or-obesity categories, achieving excellent discrimination (AUC > 0.99) and reliable calibration (low Brier scores). By allowing for classification into low, moderate, and high-risk groups, these risk scores offer a predictive viewpoint on the development of obesity. Weight, height, age, and lifestyle factors like water intake, snacking, and physical activity were found to be the most significant predictors using SHAP-based explainability. To our knowledge, no existing work on the Kaggle Obesity dataset has combined classification, probability calibration, prognostic risk scoring, and explainability, and thus this framework represents a new contribution.

title: An Enhanced Classification Model for Identifying Real vs Fake Image Using ResNet
authors: Rakesh Kumar, Vishal Bhatnagar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: AI-generated images are growing at a phenomenal pace, and nowhere is that more apparent than in digital art. It's hard enough to know anything, what's real and not real. In this article we take a look at how deep learning can be used to identify the difference between real and computer-generated art. Enhancements We proposed a CNN model with the structure of CIFAR-10. One such type are the GANs or generative adversarial networks that produce AI-generated art that visually and perceptually mimics the aesthetics of such diverse forms of art. The training set were augmented and pre-processed to be more generalization. There's a good ratio of real and fake art to keep you guessing. While we investigate our model, we use common fixed measures such as accuracy and F1. We can observed that the ground-truth based architecture in CIFAR-10 has stronger convergence ability and performs much more classification work than the CNNs used in many papers. This method is specifically flexible in that it can be applied to various digital-content evaluation systems. This shows the extent to which transfer learning convolutional networks are able to tell AI-drawn art from human-drawn art. This research work may benefit from assisting the construction of AI and digital forensics multidisciplinary effort, supporting the further research in multimedia websites copyright protection, content verification, and fake content identification.

title: Accelerating the Connectivity Revolution in India: An Insight of the 4G-6G Transition
authors: Shekhar, Vikash Bhardwaj, Bindu Kashyap, Saroj Nayyar, Hemant Kumar Khatkar, Gyanendra Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: A survey of mobile communication generations from 4G to 6G in upcoming days, which provides systematic summary on the KPIs such as data rate, latency, spectral efficiency, network capacity and device connections for these individual generations. This paper consists of the essential improvements achieved by 5G over 4G regarding data delivery latency, mobile broadband capabilities and new machine-type communications. It also looks at 6G's anticipated capabilities, which include even faster data rates, sub-millisecond latency, and ubiquitous intelligence via hybrid artificial intelligence and cutting-edge networking designs. The transformative implications of these advancements for applications like immersive communications, smart cities, and the Internet of Things are also examined in this paper. It also covers the difficulties and future research paths related to the 6G transition, such as the demands on infrastructure, energy efficiency, security, and spectrum availability. This paper provides a conceptual view towards the technological revolution in mobile communications and highlights the technological trends that will shape the future of wireless connectivity.

title: Quadratic Boost Converter Based Dual-Module Solar Microinverter for Off-Grid Application
authors: Ajay Kumar Sahu, Ramnarayan Patel, Lalit Kumar Sahu, Saket Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: This work investigates the performance of a dualmodule photovoltaic microinverter employing a Quadratic Boost Converter (QBC) topology. The proposed architecture operates in two stages, where a QBC performs Direct current (DC)-DC voltage boosting, followed by an H-bridge inverter for DC-Alternating Current (AC) conversion. The system combines the advantages of a microinverter while utilizing a QBC to handle the power output of two solar panels simultaneously. An Artificial Neural Network (ANN)-based MPPT control strategy is implemented at the QBC stage to ensure effective operation under variable environmental conditions. Experiments are conducted to assess the performance of microinverter. Experimental results validate the feasibility of simultaneous dual-panel operation and confirm stable system performance. This research contributes to the practical implementation and potential applications of QBCbased dual-module microinverters, providing an efficient solution for solar energy conversion.

title: IoT and Machine Learning Algorithm for Intrusion Detection and Crime Prediction
authors: Anirudh Kumar Tiwari, Bakhtawer Shameem, Sharmila Sharad More, Poonam Singh, Bhavana Narain
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The two most important conditions in the current digital period are crime disquisition and soothsaying. Any action or inactivity that qualifies as an offense and carries legal impacts is considered a crime. One existent or a group can perform it. Crime can beget both direct and circular detriment to the victim; it can be directed at the public or private sectors and can hurt someone's character, cause physical detriment, or beget emotional discomfort. Our design's thing is to develop a prototype that helps law enforcement detect areas with high crime rates. We've set up a system that will incontinently shoot the print of an accident to the closest police station if someone is traveling and substantiations it. We must design an operation that works for both the sender and the philanthropist in order to do this. The police will have direct access to the crime scene thanks to this entire issue, which will grease their entry. GPS will be used to help with position. As part of our study, we used an IoT-enabled digital camera to collect a dataset. We outlined the base of our exploration on crime, digital image processing, GPS, and the Internet of effects in the first portion of our report. The ways we used, similar as the detector board, GPS setup, and dataset, were bandied in the alternate part of our study. The Internet of effects has a rich data set by using data collection methods. In order to efficiently control the communication bumps, the wireless detector network (WSN), the most extensively used technology, uses tone-association and multihopping.

title: Integrating Digital Twin and Machine Learning for Cybersecurity in Power Electronics-Dominated Grids
authors: Kanchan Yadav
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: The industrial city of Manchester is one of the most popular in England which attracts migrants of numerous different countries. In this paper, the Digital Twin based Cyber Security and Machine Learning (ML) System in Google Enterprise Design Grids (EDES) is explain to be one of the key points in that it provides the virtual replicas that exist behind the apparent picture of a physical grid to be in operation. It allows constant surveillance, gathering of data and identification of anomalies. The proposed system has ML algorithms become members of DTs that will learn how a grid should be functioning properly to be able to report trouble points or simply plain attacks made to impair information systems. The work consists of: generation of strong ML models, including Random Forest and Long Short-Term Memory networks of real-time computerassisted threat detection Development of advanced data preprocessing methods, including class imbalance and feature selection, are introduced in the system to achieve substantial improvement in detection performance with data combined DT enhancement. The RF model has been found to have an approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}-\mathbf{1 5 {\%}}$</tex> increase in accuracy and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score compared with its standalone implementation when DT-aided. To conclude, integrating DT into the same system as ML can bring transformative benefits for safeguarding critical energy infrastructure. Future work will be critical for scaling up to higher-dimensional grids and adding adaptive learning models that handle growing PEDGs as their position evolves.

title: Secrecy Performance Optimization of a Two-User MAR-Aided Cooperative NOMA System over a Nakagami-m Fading Channel
authors: M Srinivasa Rao, Suseela Vappangi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: This paper investigates the secrecy performance of a cooperative NOMA system that facilitates two-user transmission utilizing a multi-antenna relay (MAR) over channels characterized by Nakagami-m fading. New analytical expressions are developed to characterize the secrecy ergodic rate (SER) and the total secrecy ergodic sum rate (SESR), considering both perfect and imperfect successive interference cancellation (SIC) conditions. An optimal power allocation (PA) method is proposed to meet user quality-of-service (QoS) requirements while improving secrecy efficiency. Simulation results support the analytical model and demonstrate that the suggested framework outperforms traditional fixed power allocation schemes in terms of secrecy sum-rate. The findings also indicate that optimal PA and multi-antenna relaying can effectively reduce interference and channel fading effects, providing a reliable way to strengthen physical layer security (PLS) in future 5G and 6G cooperative NOMA networks.

title: Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)

title: Design and experimentation of nonlinear model predictive control for gantry robotic manipulator
authors: Ayushman Jena, Ayan Fareed Siddiqui, Ezhilarasi Deenadayalan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: This paper presents the real-time implementation of a Nonlinear Model Predictive Control (NMPC) framework for a gantry robotic manipulator consisting of a planar gantry system and an inverted robotic arm. The NMPC algorithm is executed for the inverted 3-DOF (Degree of Freedom) where actuation and sensing are handled by a standalone microcontroller board. Angular position feedback is obtained by accessing the internal potentiometers of the servos. Experimental results demonstrate standard trajectory tracking and stable real-time performance, validating the feasibility of advanced nonlinear control strategies on low-cost robotic platforms.

title: Optimizing Haptic-Enabled Gloves and Socks for Virtual Reality Driving Simulation Using Genetic Algorithm
authors: Geetha B G, Shagana T, Sasmitha J
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Virtual reality (VR) driving simulators are extensively utilized for training, rehabilitation, and research on human-machine interaction; nonetheless, the lack of tactile realism persists as a significant barrier. This research introduces a genetic algorithm (GA)–driven optimization methodology for haptic gloves and socks, aimed at improving immersion and perceptual precision in VR driving simulations. The technology utilizes multi-point vibrotactile actuators to replicate steering, pedal engagement, road texture, and vehicle dynamics. A multi-objective fitness function that integrates driving performance measurements and user feedback is employed to optimize actuator location and vibration settings, concurrently decreasing delay and energy consumption. Experimental findings indicate that the suggested GA decreases average reaction time from 530 ms to 340 ms, enhances steering and braking precision to 92% and 94%, respectively, and minimizes lane deviation to 0.3 m. The system attains a user-perceived realism score of 4.8/5, accompanied by low latency of 25 ms and energy usage of 65 mWh. These findings validate the efficacy of evolutionary optimization in improving wearable haptic systems for next-generation VR driving simulations.

title: Economically Stabilizing Heliostat Costs in Solar Tower Technology Through Design Structure and Materials Selection
authors: Maaz Ahmed, Syed Faiq Musharraf, Mehran Abolghasem, Fatemeh Faramarzi Zadeh, Aymen Nadeem, Mohamed Boudjenah
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: This Study investigates the economic stabilization of heliostats costs in Solar Tower (ST) technology through systematic optimization of design, structure, and materials. Heliostat contributes significantly to optical losses (40%) and they account for 3 0 - 5 0% of plant CAPEX, their cost volatility directly impacts project bankability. The methodology employs a multi-faceted approach: (1) analysis of optimal heliostat size (48-64 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) using field efficiency models; (2) structural topology optimization to reduce mass by 25-35% using GFRP or recycled aluminum; and (3) cost modeling based on SAM, calibrated with real-world data from Gemasolar and Hami plants. Results show that co-optimizing of medium sized heliostats (48-64 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), topology optimization from GFRP torque tubes along with thin-glass reflectors can reduce unit cost by l2 9% (from 8 0/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> to 5 7/ m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) without compromising optical error (<1.5 mrad). Furthermore adopting modular, preassembled designs and anti-soiling coatings reduces O&M costs by 0.80- 1.20/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/year and economies of scale and predictive O&M strategies can lower LCOE by 8-1 4%. This integrated approach offers a trail to cost-stabilized, scalable CSP utilization, making it competitive with fossil utilization in highDNI regions (≥ 2400 kWh/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/yr).

title: Smart Attendance System Using PCA-Based Face Recognition and Hybrid Machine Learning Classifiers
authors: Bharath U, Anant Ganapati Bhat, Darshan R, Chandan Gowda K S, Shobha T, Rashmi R
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Conventional forms of checking attendance in class-rooms like manual roll call or paper based log books are time consuming and prone to proxy marking. In order to address these constraints, this paper introduces a Smart Attendance System, which entails classical machine learning and live face recognition. This system uses Haar Cascade classifiers to detect face and uses Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to extract high dimensional expression of faces. Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Random Forest (RF) were compared in terms of recognition performance, with SVM having an 92% accuracy and being better than Random Forest (RF) with 88% performance. To eliminate spoof attacks made with the help of printed photos, a blink-based liveness detection module is included. The results of the experiment prove that the suggested solution is highly accurate and can be effectively used with the help of standard hardware powered by laptops, which makes it a feasible and comparatively affordable option when it comes to face-recognition-based methods of attendance.

title: Distributed Data Clustering using Chaotic Red-Billed Blue Magpie Optimization in IoT Networks
authors: Anita Panwar, Dinesh Kumar Kotary
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Efficient data processing in IoT networks is critical for managing energy consumption and ensuring reliable communication in complex and remote environmental locations. Traditional optimization techniques often struggle to handle the nonlinearities and constraints associated with IoT devices. To overcome these challenges, metaheuristic approaches particularly based on swarm behaviour have gained more attention. This paper introduces a distributed version of the Red-Billed Blue Magpie Optimization (DRBMO) mimicked by the cooperative and adaptive behaviour of the blue magpie. A cooperative diffusion approach is employed by sharing the best food position and its asociated objective value with neighbouring nodes. By incorporating chaotic initialization, DRBMO effectively addresses the limitations of conventional optimization techniques, resulting in better exploration and faster convergence in IoT environments. The proposed DRBMO algorithm is evaluated on two real-life datasets: (a) Forest Fire and (b) Thames River Water. Comparative evaluations with established algorithms such as Distributed K-Means and Distributed PSO reveal that DRBMO consistently achieves superior performance in terms of Average Euclidean Distance, Silhouette Index and the Davies–Bouldin Index.

title: The Empirical Study of Biometric Systems and Drones
authors: Neha Singh, Ajita Pathak, Suman Mishra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Biometric techniques have become a highly reliable and commonly used approach to verify a person’s identity through their different physical or behavioral symptoms including facial characteristics, fingerprints and iris scans. Unlike traditional methods such as passwords or identity cards, biometrics provide a more secure and convenient means of authentication, allowing individuals to confirm their identity by only looking at the camera or scanning their fingerprints. With the increasing requirement of rapid and strong authentication solutions, biometric systems are looking to adopt widely in both government and private sectors. In recent years, integrating biometric systems with unmanned air-vehicles (UAVs), commonly known as drones, has created new possibilities to increase security, monitoring, and real-time identification verification. This review provides an overview of biometric technology combined with UAVs, examining various biometric modalities, and their integratability with drone platforms. It also examines principle performance standards like reliability, security, and usability on a practical basis. Several types of biometric techniques are used to identify users, and we are reviewing the literature on biometric user authentication along with their compatibility with drones in detail, addressing important performance standards, such as security and other commonly used biometric functions.

title: Design and analysis of low-power, high-output Schmitt trigger employing Domino Logic
authors: Shailendra Mishra, Abhay Pratap Singh, Shivangi Dwivedi, Ravi Rastogi, Anurag Govind Rao, D.K Mishra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This work briefs a non-inverting, domino-logic Schmitt trigger circuit having improved dynamic power and delay. The work contributes to the study of the proposed Schmitt trigger for the upshot of low switching power consumption, decreased propagation delay, and reduced leakage power. in the result of the proposed Schmitt trigger gives clear hysteresis behavior and better dynamic power and less delay. It is because of the usage of one PMOS and one NMOS for the 2-layered feedback approach, which may be used in low-noise receivers along with a waveform remodeling circuit. The Domino Schmitt trigger has less delay and lower dynamic power in contrast to the conventional Schmitt trigger. We have used 45 nm technology and obtained results that are significantly better compared to the conventional Schmitt Trigger. We have also included graphs and comparative analysis with respect to circuits such as Xiaojin, Bn1, and Bn2. Our study focuses on key performance metrics, including delay, leakage power, and dynamic power of the proposed Domino Schmitt Trigger (DST) circuit. These results are compared with those of the Xiaojin, Bn1, and Bn2 circuits, and the comparisons are presented as graphs plotted using MATLAB.

title: Applications of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Business Management
authors: Someet Singh, Navjot Kaur, Arshi Naim, Mostafa Mohamad, Anurag Sharma, Praveen Kumar Malik
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Subjects of business management (BM) like Inflation, Unemployment Ratio, Phillips Curve, Customer Relationship Management (CRM) Analytics, Performance Management, Sales, Strategic Planning, Purchasing Power Parity, Mass Production, Money, Taxes have been in the discussion globally for its assessments and analysis. Artificial Intelligence (AI) is an accepted technology for solving the inverse impact of these subjects and in its measurement, but this study extends the applications of AI to generative AI (AGI) because AI is suitable to give solutions to definite challenges, but AGI provides broader perspective to any problem and analyzes more like humans do for any decision-making processes. The research is based on secondary data collected from the firms and open access to data from consulting groups using AGI for BM subjects and what are the opportunities and challenges they experienced during the process of application.

title: AI Powered Fraud Detection in SAP S/4HANA Finance
authors: Karunakar Grandhe
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The issue of financial fraud has long been a significant concern in Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems, and one of the more complex systems vulnerable to fraud is SAP S/4HANA Finance. Old-fashioned, rule-based controls have not been as effective in detecting complex and dynamic fraud schemes. This paper introduces an AI-based solution for detecting fraud that utilizes Machine Learning and anomaly detection to improve accuracy and responsiveness. The proposed modular architecture incorporates SAP HANA for data ingestion, advanced feature extraction capabilities, and real-time notifications delivered through SAP Fiori dashboards. The proposed solution enables proactive anomaly detection in financial workflows, helping to minimize fraud while maintaining transparency. The experimental results show enhanced precision and recall compared to legacy controls. The current study presents a scalable and explainable model that aligns with modern ERP environments.

title: NASA Asteroid Classification Using Heterogeneous Ensemble Learning with Machine Learning Models
authors: Allu Pranav Srihas, Venkata Naga Ganesh J, Subramaniyaswamy Vairavasundaram, Pichaimuthu Mohankumar
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: The early detection of hazardous asteroids is crucial because asteroid collisions with Earth are harmful to human life and other living organisms, to address this problem, in this research, machine learning and deep learning models are used to classify asteroids into hazardous and non-hazardous types based on the 19 features with a row size of 4,688 taken from the NASA dataset. We have implemented and compared advanced techniques such as Heterogeneous Ensemble Learning (HEL), Category Boosting (CatBoost), XGBoost (Extreme Gra- dient Boosting) with SHAP (XGBoost-SHAP), Bootstrap Your Own Latent (BYOL), Self-Distillation with No Labels (SDNL), Graph Neural Network (GNN), Graph Attention Network (GAT). Finally, evaluating and comparing performance metrics for these models helps astronomers gain a better understanding of the classification of hazardous and non-hazardous asteroids. Overall, among these models, HEL achieved the best performance, and it is the most suitable model to classify asteroids. This technological improvement will contribute greatly to the planetary defense systems and the protection of Earth against future asteroid threats.

title: Hybrid Model for Air Quality Prediction
authors: Atul Srivastava, Ankit Verma, Vijay Shankar Sharma, Kamlesh Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: The prediction of air quality will play a key role in public health, urban planning, and environmental monitoring. Traditional machine learning models are not very accurate for predicting levels of air pollution because multiple environmental factors interact in complex ways. This paper presents a hybrid predictive model which combines Random Forest (RF), Naive Bayes (NB), and Gradient Boosting (GB) for better prediction accuracy of air quality. It considers both individual algorithms to eliminate their individual drawbacks and ensure high generalization of the learned hybrid model. Thus, with a view towards verifying the proposal method, testing is performed over an available open-source air quality dataset containing some meteorological parameters as well as other types of pollutants. The experimental results showed that the hybrid model outperforms individual models about accuracy and robustness of prediction and provides an effective solution in air quality forecasting. This paper contributes to advancing methodologies for the prediction of air quality and points out potential directions for further work in this domain.

title: An Accessible Real-Time Document Question Answering Extension for People with Visual Impairment
authors: Anagha V, K S Kuppusamy
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Visually impaired users face challenges in retrieving specific information from digital documents due to the linear design of traditional screen readers and limited contextual support. This paper introduces AccessQuery, an AI-powered Chrome extension that employs large language models (LLMs) for real-time, intelligent question-answering (QA) to enhance document accessibility. The system integrates speech recognition, natural language processing (NLP), and text-to-speech (TTS) to support multimodal and multilingual interaction with PDF content, leveraging a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipeline with LangChain, ChromaDB, and the DeepSeek R1 LLM for context-aware responses. Evaluation on 270 questions from 50+ academic, technical, and multilingual documents achieved strong accuracy across key metrics (BERTScore F1>0.90, precision 87.5%, recall 83.2%), while a user study with visually impaired participants confirmed high satisfaction with usability, clarity, and multilingual support. These results demonstrate the system's effectiveness in enabling non-linear, LLM-driven document navigation for visually impaired users.

title: VeriVoice: Enhancing Audio Deepfake Detection with Depthwise Frequency Convolutions
authors: Vinayak Pandey, Amiradha Satsangi, Bhavya Saini, Shivam Kapoor, Preetam Suman
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Audio deepfakes, or speech synthesized from voice cloning or text-to-speech (TTS) models, are security and privacy threat. The ability to create realistic-sounding audio forgeries of someone's voice can be used for malicious ends, such as impersonation or spreading disinformation. This paper introduces a novel solution to audio deepfake detection with Graph-Enhanced Audio Transformer (GEAT) model. The contributions are (1) developing a novel dataset of 32 speakers (1,479 real samples) and corresponding clones produced with OpenVoice Model (1,479 fake samples), and (2) developing a Transformer-based audio classifier combining spectral features with graph-based relational reasoning. Compared with LSTM (89.70 % test accuracy), SVM with MFCC (96.80 %), and Random Forest with MFCC (98.14 %), GEAT achieves a test accuracy of 99.66 % on the same dataset with the same formatting, demonstrating the strength of combining spectral graph-like convolution with Transformer encoding for audio deepfake detection.

title: Enhancement of Fuel Efficiency in Hybrid- Electric Vehicle Using an Adaptive Power- Split Technique
authors: Ghanshyam Kumar, Biswaranjan Mishra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Hybrid Electric Vehicle (HEV) requires efficient power management strategies to achieve lower fuel consumption and maintaining better battery health. Traditional Rule-based controllers are easy to set up but lack adaptability, whereas ECMS delivers near-optimal performance at a higher computational cost. This paper presents an adaptive power split algorithm that combines SOC-aware feedback with logistic-map functions to dynamically allocate torque between the engine and motor. The proposed method ensures smooth transitions, efficient use of stored energy, and robustness under varying load conditions. A complete HEV model was developed in MATLAB/Simulink and tested on the standard FTP-75 drive cycle. Results show that the adaptive controller achieves a 28% reduction in fuel consumption compared to rule-based control, with effective utilization of the SOC window (0.60 to 0.40). The method delivers a favorable trade-off between efficiency, simplicity, and real-time feasibility, making it a strong solution for next-generation HEV energy management systems.

title: Enhanced Parkinson Disease Prediction with Hybrid Machine Learning and Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Jayashree R, Vanitha S
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Parkinson Disease (PD) is a neurological disorder that affects the movement of human beings, which leads to difficulties in daily activities. Recently, the number of cases is increasing due to factors such as aging and environmental exposures such as air pollution, pesticides, and industrial chemicals. However, there are other factors that are not a diredirectly related to the disease. Moreover, Parkinson is not a curable disease, but early diagnosis helps the patients live longer by taking proper medication and exercising. Nowadays, artificial intelligence is widely involved in health care for analyzing complex data and in the decision-making process. The existing system uses gait analysis, handwriting patterns, and voice recordings to identify the motor symptoms. This research work proposed a hybrid model that combines Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) techniques using the voice dataset to predict Parkinson disease. The goal of this research work is to develop an intelligent tool having the capability to explore patient details to uncover the hidden pattern. The proposed model bridges the variance between the disease symptoms and professional analysis. The model is trained on a relevant medical dataset to provide precise accuracy. Furthermore, the incorporated MVC architecture enables the modularity, scalability, and performance of the model. Furthermore, the web-based UI is developed to collect the patient details for instant decision. The combination of logistic regression (ML) and deep neural network (DL) encourages finding the complex medical samples and supports the medical community with the correctness of 98% of the F1 score. Finally, this research work enhances the healthcare technology by developing a robust decision-making tool that helps early intervention.

title: Multi-Sensor Information Fusion Fault Diagnosis Method Based on Bidirectional Time–Frequency Domain Information Interaction
authors: Bin Chen, Yang Zhao, Yuteng Zhang, Yuyan Jiang, Hongliang Zhang, Haiyang Pan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Under strong noise disturbance, local signals collected by a single sensor often fail to fully capture fault characteristics. In addition, diagnostic models commonly suffer from limited robustness and generalization under complex operating conditions. To address these problems, a multi-sensor information fusion fault diagnosis method is proposed in this paper. First, a time–frequency dual-branch feature extraction module is designed. It performs one-dimensional convolution on both the original time-domain signal and its frequency-domain amplitude. Then, a selectable convolution module is added to each branch. It dynamically fuses multi-scale information from different receptive fields. A bidirectional cross-attention mechanism enables deep interaction and complementary enhancement between time–frequency features. Finally, experiments are conducted based on the two datasets obtained from the metro bogie transmission system fault simulation test rig and the radial load bearing fault simulation test rig. Results show that the proposed method achieves high recognition accuracy and strong robustness under variable operating conditions and intense noise, which achieves promising performance over the comparison methods.

title: Multitasking across Physical and Virtual Displays: the Effect of Spatial Discontinuity and Task Load
authors: Peiyu Zhang, Mohamad El Iskandarani, Sara Riggs
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Optical see-through head-mounted displays (OST-HMDs) allow users to interact with both physical and virtual displays overlaid in the real world within augmented reality (AR) environments. When arranging these displays in space, spatial discontinuity—such as differences in viewing distance or lateral separation in the visual field—may occur. This study examined the effects of displays' spatial discontinuities on multitasking performance and perceived workload in AR, as well as how these effects were influenced by task load.
