This research is a huge step towards ensuring a collaborative approach to healthcare analytics, without compromising patient confidentiality.

title: Attention-Enhanced Transformer Architecture for Automated Question Generation Based on Student Knowledge Level Estimation
authors: Tushar Dhar Shukla, Sakshi Manhas, Kalyanasundaram V, Vuda Sreenivasa Rao, M. Nanthini, M. Karthik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Personalized question generation is critical for adaptive learning, enabling tailored assessments that align with individual student mastery. Existing approaches often rely solely on textual context without incorporating learner knowledge, resulting in generic questions that lack appropriate difficulty levels. To address these limitations, a novel framework integrates the Self-Attentive Knowledge Tracing (SAKT) model to estimate student knowledge states dynamically with an attention-enhanced T5 transformer for question generation. This dual-attention mechanism simultaneously attends to passage context and student mastery, allowing generation of contextually relevant, difficulty-adaptive questions. Fine-tuning is performed on the SQuAD dataset using Python and PyTorch tools. Experimental results show improved performance over baseline models, with BLEU-4 at 36.15, ROUGE-L at 44.78, and METEOR at 30.31, alongside a 96.3 accuracy in knowledge estimation by SAKT. The approach effectively personalizes learning, enhancing engagement and educational outcomes through adaptive question complexity.

title: Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference for Smart Hybrid Power Control in Electric Vehicles
authors: Vivek Kumar Sharma, Lakshmi MLSNS, K Kiran, Anandakumar Haldorai, Aman, Rahul Thakur
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: This study introduces smart hybrid power management system that uses adaptive neuro-fuzzy inference method to improve energy distribution from multiple sources. The system relies on Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cell (PEMFC) as main power source, with ultracapacitors, and a battery pack to boost efficiency in power supply and storage. By using artificial intelligence, the system adjusts energy use in real time to enhance performance and reliability. The created approach guarantees intelligent control and adaptive optimization for increased efficiency because fuel consumption and power management of Hybrid Electric Vehicles (HEVs) are closely related. Important advancement in energy management is use of AI, which makes it possible to precisely regulate and analyze power sources in advance. This work advances smart energy systems by utilizing AI-driven strategies, providing scalable method for sustainable hybrid energy management. Comprehensive evaluations confirm the suggested framework's efficacy and show how it can improve system reliability and fuel efficiency.

title: A Distributed System Framework for UAV-Based Inspection of Renewable Energy Infrastructure
authors: K.R.N. Kiran Kumar, Naga Venkata Sarathbabu Goriparti, V Sumalatha, Anandakumar Haldorai, CH. Pradeep Reddy, Subhav Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: The fast growth of renewable energy sources especially wind power requires immediate maintenance of infrastructure systems. The paper describes a distributed system platform which uses UAVs to inspect renewable energy facilities with special focus on wind turbine inspection. The system operates to acquire data which it processes for real-time problem identification through modular UAVs that combine high-resolution sensors with thermal and optical imaging capabilities. The system uses distributed computers with advanced algorithms to enhance inspection precision while minimizing the time-consuming aspects of human-based inspection methods. The wind power facility served as the testing site to validate the inspection method. The inspection process achieved 94% fault detection while cutting down turbine inspection time to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 5}$</tex> full hours. The proposed system enables safe and cost-effective monitoring of extensive renewable energy assets through reduced human involvement. The existing energy infrastructure is improved in terms of its dependability and sustainability as a result.

title: Automated Brain Tumor Classification in MRI Scans Using an EfficientNet-B5 Deep Learning Framework
authors: Deepak Kumar K, Selvam R, Rajalingam R, Shaji DS, Noor Mohamed I, Senthil Pandi S
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Brain tumors are one of the most perilous medical conditions today, and getting the diagnosis right, as quickly as possible, could mean the difference between life and death for the patient. This work intends to explore how EfficientNet-B5, a next generation artificial intelligence system, can be used to automatically distinguish between categories of brain tumors based on MRIs of the patient's brain. What is unique to EfficientNet-B5 is that it is designed in a way that provides accuracy relative to speed. In other words, it does not require an enormous amount of computer processing/image processing power, but provides satisfactory performance. We tested the system with thousands of brain MRIs, with images that represented gliomas, meningiomas, pituitary tumors, and an image of a healthy brain. The artificial intelligence learned to detect different conditions by analyzing differences in image patterns. Our results were impressive. EfficientNet-B5 accurately identified brain tumors 96.8% of the time, better than other artificial intelligence-based computer programs we have accessibility of testing. What is particularly exciting is that it is fast enough to diagnose the conditions in real-time settings, such as in the hospital. The doctor could potentially use it as a second opinion on their diagnosis in seconds, and find conditions that could have been missed, or confirm their thoughts were right. The technology is also efficient enough to be implemented on a standard ho spital desktop computer. Allin-all, this work shows that modern artificial intelligence can be a tool for the medical community in the fight against brain cancer, as it can save lives through improved speed and accuracy with diagnosis.

title: Thermo-Electrical Performance Analysis of Grid Connected PV/T Systems Employing SiC Nanofluid Cooling
authors: K. Kavita, Ranga Jarabala, Nagendar Yamsani, Anandakumar Haldorai, Chaitanya Singla, Roshan Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: The study evaluates grid-connected photovoltaic thermal systems which use SiC nanofluid technology to achieve their maximum performance through extensive MATLAB simulations. The paper contrasts the efficiency and the energy output between PV/T systems cooled using water and the conventional water and SiC nanofluids under typical environmental conditions. The system behavior is studied through mathematical models which incorporate temperaturedependent PV efficiency and heat transfer mechanisms. The simulation models the thermal and electrical interactions within the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{PV} / \mathrm{T}$</tex> module by using SiC nanofluid thermophysical properties to demonstrate enhanced cooling performance. The study conducts sensitivity analyses to evaluate how different nanoparticle concentrations and fluid flow rates affect optimal operating conditions. To further develop more efficient and sustainable technologies of solar energy, this study will seek to offer information about the possible advantages and operational issues of the implementation of SiC nanofluid cooling in gridconnected PV/T systems. Unlike existing studies that primarily focus on standalone or experimentally driven PV/T systems, this work presents a coupled thermo-electrical MATLAB-based modeling framework for grid-connected PV/T systems employing SiC nanofluid cooling, including parametric and sensitivity analyses.

title: Deep Learning-Based Doppler Compensation for Reliable MmWave V2X Communication
authors: Zhuocheng Li, Lixiao Pang
publish: 2025 7th International Communication Engineering and Cloud Computing Conference (CECCC)
abstract: In the Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) scenarios, millimetre-wave communications are susceptible to Doppler shift, which can lead to inter-carrier interference and significantly degrade the bit error rate. This paper proposes a Doppler compensation method based on deep learning. Doppler shift is introduced as an auxiliary feature at the neural network input, and a model combining a residual structure and an attention mechanism is further designed. Experimental results show that this method significantly reduces Bit Error Rate (BER) under varying channel complexity and noise conditions. Compared to traditional methods and baseline models such as CNN, DNN, and LSTM, it demonstrates greater robustness and generalization, providing an effective compensation solution for mmWave V2X communications in high-speed mobile environments.

title: Performance Evaluation of Quantum Encoding Techniques in QCNN for Bone Marrow Cell Classification
authors: Harshitha Prasanthi Kanna, Prathibha Goriparthi, K.S Rajasekhar, Aishwarya Kolusu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: This paper investigates the effectiveness of classical and quantum-enhanced deep learning models for bone marrow cell classification, with particular emphasis on the role of quantum data encoding strategies. A classical EfficientNet combined with a convolutional neural network (CNN) is evaluated alongside multiple Quantum Convolutional Neural Network (QCNN) architectures employing single, dual, and multi-encoding schemes, including angle, basis, and amplitude encoding. The experimental results indicate that single encoding QCNN models achieve performance comparable to the classical baseline, with amplitude encoding yielding improved validation accuracy. Dual-encoding approaches demonstrate improved generalization, with the angle-amplitude encoding QCNN achieving the highest macro F1-score of 0.895 and a test accuracy of 89.25%. The multi-encoding hybrid QCNN further improves overall accuracy, attaining the highest training accuracy of 95.90% and test accuracy of 89.40%, though at the cost of increased model complexity. These findings highlight that carefully designed hybrid quantum encoding strategies can effectively balance representation capacity and generalization, offering competitive performance over classical models and underscoring the potential of QCNNs in Bone marrow cell classification.

title: LightEdge-IDS: A Lightweight Real-Time Intrusion Detection System for Cyber-Physical Attacks in Water Distribution Systems
authors: Pradeep Chandran, K.S. Sunil, Amritha Remesh, Veena Roy, Vidya Roy, Vivek D V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: With the growing digitization of critical infrastructure, Water Distribution Systems have become vulnerable to complex cyber–physical attacks. Machine learning-based Intrusion Detection Systems, such as the Rectilinear Hybrid Belief Classifier, achieves strong offline accuracy. However, they are not suitable for real-time use because of their limited computing power and latency at the edge. This paper presents LightEdge-IDS, a real-time, lightweight, zone-adaptive IDS designed to secure WDS in embedded environments. The system models the WDS as a graph and uses a hybrid Graph Neural Network and Gated Recurrent Unit architecture on sensor-focused subgraphs to capture spatial and temporal dependencies. Lightweightzone-specific adapters perform localized inference and generate feature embeddings for global analysis, enabling scalable and efficient edge deployment. This design achieves high adaptability and resource efficiency, providing a practical basis for real-time anomaly detection in distributed water networks. This paper presents the architectural design of LightEdge-IDS, along with key implementation details and experimental results. A complete description of the full implementation and an extended evaluation will be presented in a future, extended version of the work.

title: Viability of Procedurally Generated Normal Maps as Steganographic Carriers
authors: Porchezhian R, Devasena G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: We investigate whether tangent-space normal maps—procedurally generated using Perlin/FBM noise—are viable carriers for image steganography, and whether domain-aware embedding can overcome the statistical limitations of naive least-significant-bit (LSB) substitution on this carrier class. Normal maps are semantically meaningful graphics assets whose channel statistics differ substantially from photographs and unconstrained noise, making them a novel and practically motivated carrier class. Our reproducible prototype (a) encrypts payloads with AES-256-GCM using PBKDF2 key derivation, (b) generates deterministic normal maps from seeded Perlin/FBM parameters, and (c) embeds via two strategies: naive 2-LSB at 6bits/pixel, and a domain-aware strategy (NM-Aware) that preserves the blue-channel bias by embedding exclusively in R and G channels at 4bits/pixel. Across 100 unique carriers (20 seeds × 5 configurations), all methods achieved 100% lossless recovery with PSNR≈46dB and SSIM≈0.9998, matching noise textures and photo-like carriers on every perceptual metric. Domain-aware embedding reduces Shannon entropy change from +20.2% (naive) to +2.5%—an 8× improvement. Chi-squared analysis establishes that the B-channel χ<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> ≈ 0 in NM-Aware reflects the carrier’s natural blue-channel bias (clean-vs-stego comparative test: p ≈1.0), not an embedding artifact—whereas in NM-Naive the identical value is a genuine, detectable artifact. Baseline ML detectors achieve AUC=1.0 for all methods under spatial 2-LSB embedding, establishing a common ceiling and motivating adaptive embedding as future work.

title: AI Individualism: What Are Social Relationships in the Age of Artificial Intelligence?
authors: Petter Bae Brandtzaeg, Marita Skjuve, Asbjørn Følstad
publish: Communications of the ACM
abstract: None

title: Boost DC–DC Converter Topologies for ViPV Systems: A Technical Review
authors: Sebastián Rodríguez-Romero, Jorge Rabanal-Arabach, Christian A. Rojas, Mauricio Trigo-Gonzalez, Diego Arías, Fernando Castro-Gallardo, Edward Fuentealba-Vidal
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The integration of photovoltaics into electric vehicles ViPV imposes constraints, including partial shading, curvature induced irradiance gradients, and low profile packaging that challenge conventional DC DC converter designs. This paper reviews 115 boost derived topologies and evaluates their suitability considering electrical, geometric, thermal, and mechanical conditions in automotive environments. A multi criteria evaluation framework is introduced, combining voltage gain, light load efficiency, input current ripple, magnetic volume, and mismatch tolerance, assessed using weighted charts. The analysis shows a divergence between domains. Isolated topologies such as DAB and LLC suit kilowatt scale traction and charging, but their magnetic footprint and light load losses make them unsuited to module level ViPV. In contrast, distributed power processing architectures, especially active clamp flyback converters at submodule level and multi input MISO boost aggregation, emerge as structurally coherent options. The results highlight the potential of hybrid ACF MISO converters with GaN devices to reconcile high gain, low ripple, and minimal vertical profile in solar electric vehicles.

title: Collaborative Lane Change of Heterogeneous Truck Platoon Based on MEC Control Architecture With Constant Communication Delay
authors: Gengsheng Yang, Wenbo Chu, Xiaolin Tang, Dongpu Cao, Keqiang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Truck platoons are expected to play a key role in the future of cargo transportation, while high-speed cooperative lane changes pose significant risks. This paper introduces a collaborative lane change control (CLCC) framework for truck platoons that utilizes a multi-access edge computing (MEC) control architecture. A parallel signal superposition method is presented, which has doubled the control signal refresh frequency. Additionally, an improved nonlinear articulated model predictive control (NA-MPC) algorithm, which accounts for the nonlinear articulated characteristics of trucks, is proposed and deployed on the roadside and on-board equipment to form a key component of the CLCC framework. A cooperative adaptive cruise control (CACC) algorithm based on proportional-integral (PI) and proportional-derivative (PD) controllers is also adopted for longitudinal following. Simulation results demonstrate that the signal superposition approach reduces lateral deviations, and the improved NA-MPC algorithm enhances the robustness of the platoon during emergency maneuvers. The CLCC framework even effectively minimizes maximum lateral deviations under the shortest path <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$ S\_{53}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Furthermore, the CACC algorithm ensures the string stability. These results validate the effectiveness of the proposed methods, particularly in the presence of constant communication delays, highlighting their potential to improve the stability of cooperative lane changes within truck platoons.

title: Wheat Stripe Rust regional Monitoring with Dynamic Multi-Indicator Weight Updating method
authors: Jiayi Zhang, Mingxian Zhao, Yingying Dong, Linyi Liu, Zhenhai Li, Kun Wang, Wenjiang Huang, Giovanni Laneve, Bin Du, Yunfeng Yu, Meilin Tan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Wheat stripe rust is one of the most severe diseases responsible for significant wheat yield losses. The rise in its incidence and severity, driven by global warming and increasingly frequent extreme weather events, underscores the urgent need for the development of efficient monitoring technologies to ensure wheat production security and advance sustainable control strategies. Although advancements in remote sensing technology and monitoring algorithms have supported large-scale, high-precision monitoring, challenges persist in the adequate extraction of spatial associations, as well as improving model accuracy and applicability in regional monitoring of stripe rust. This paper integrates phenological information to construct remote sensing and meteorological monitoring features, optimizing them based on the spatial distribution disparities of stripe rust. Subsequently, the Geographically Neural Network Weighted Regression (GNNWR) model is introduced, dynamically updating the weights of various monitoring features to capture the nonlinear relationships between the disease and its influencing factors. The study demonstrates that the GNNWR model outperforms traditional Geographically Weighted Regression (GWR) and Random Forest (RF) models in capturing the spatial distribution of the disease. In particular, the dynamic weight update mechanism enhances its ability to adapt to spatial heterogeneity across different regions. Despite a decline in the monitoring accuracy of all three models when applied to the limited and unevenly distributed samples in Ningqiang County, the GNNWR model still achieved the highest accuracy. In conclusion, this research highlights that the multi-indicator dynamic weight updating model for wheat stripe rust monitoring significantly improves the accuracy of regional disease monitoring.

title: Structured Ytterbium and Erbium -doped Silica Fiber for Dual Wavelength Laser Operation
authors: Ivo Barton, Pavel Peterka, Martin Grabner, Jan Aubrecht, Michal Kamradek, Ondrej Podrazky, Petr Varak, Dariusz Pysz, Marcin Franczyk, Rafal Kasztelanic, Ryszard Buczynski, Ivan Kasik
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: We report on a novel type of dual-wavelength fiber laser with a structured-core design inside silica glass, forming a spatial separation of the several core areas doped with ytterbium and erbium ions. We have optimised the key parameters of the fiber core, such as the concentration of rare earth elements, and the optimal length of active fiber to operate simultaneously at two different wavelengths. Using the Modified Chemical Vapor Deposition method to obtain initial optical fiber preforms, and using the stack and draw technique, we have fabricated two types of active fibers, one with 7 and one with 19 rare-earth-doped rods (elements) forming the fiber core. We characterized the drawn fibers by investigating their structure by scanning electron microscopy, confirming the spatial separation of the elements within the core. Measuring absorption shows that concentration ratios NtYb: NtEr were approximately 52: 48 for 7 core fibers and 56:44 for 19 core fibers. Lifetimes for both active fibers were 0.84 ms for Yb3+ ion and 10.30 ms for Er3+ ion. The performance of fiber lasers was determined, proving that fibers are capable of laser emission simultaneously at 1042 nm and 1550 nm. We have shown experimentally that the output power ratio between both lasing wavelengths can be controlled by the length of the fiber.

title: MPAExpo-LM: Fine-Tuned Large Language Model for Mycophenolic Acid Exposure Estimation After Renal Transplantation
authors: Dehua Chen, Huilin Liu, Qingyuan Ge, Ming Zuo, Huimin An, Quan Zhou, Shangxi Fu, Peijun Zhou, Xianghui Wang, Bing Chen, Kun Shao
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: An accurate estimation of mycophenolic acid (MPA) exposure after renal transplantation is important for reducing acute rejection in patients treated with mycophenolate mofetil (MMF) or enteric-coated mycophenolate sodium (MPS). We propose MPAExpo-LM, a fine-tuned large language model with a differential attention denoising module for this task. Experiments on real-world datasets demonstrate that MPAExpo-LM achieves superior results compared to existing methods in the task, offering a new pathway for precise immunosuppressive therapy. It attains an RMSE of 8.78 mg·h/L using three sampling points (C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>, C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>, C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">8</sub>) and 7.43 mg·h/L with four points (C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</sub>, C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>, C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sub>, C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">8</sub>). Furthermore, we conducted additional fine-tuning by incorporating a limited amount of data from external data source from another hospital. In the subsequent multi-center validation, the model demonstrated robust generalization, achieving RMSEs of 7.342 mg·h/L (three points) and 7.281 mg·h/L (four points). Via reinforcement fine-tuning, the model demonstrates strong generalization capability in cross-hospital tests.

title: A Dual Switched Inductor-Switched Capacitor Based High Gain Converter for Green Energy Micro Source Applications
authors: M F Baba, P Raviteja, S. V. K. Naresh, K Ashok Kumar, A V Giridhar, B L Narasimharaju, Atif Iqbal
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: A dual switched inductor-switched capacitor-based dc-dc boost converter evolution and analysis are presented in this article. The front-end converter with single inductor and switched capacitor provides a voltage gain similar to Quasi-Z-Source converter while the succeeding modified SEPIC based structure is embedded to enhance the voltage gain further. The output capacitor elevated peak current is abbreviated by reconfiguring the aforementioned topology. On this premise, two converters with common ground and continuous source currents are proposed in this article. A 400 V, 300 W, 50 kHz prototypes are developed to verify the performance indices in steady state analysis.

title: Localization of a Magnetic Capsule Endoscope Using Bilinear Interpolation and Coordinate Transformation of an External Magnetic Field and the SNR-based Weighting Factor in Optimization
authors: Junhyoung Kwon, Jinhyung Lee, Suhong Bae, Gunhee Jang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: A magnetic capsule endoscope (MCE) embedded with a permanent magnet (PM) can be actuated and controlled by an external magnetic field generated by a magnetic navigation system (MNS), enabling wireless examination of lesions in the gastrointestinal (GI) tract. For precise control of the MCE, accurate localization of the MCE is required, and prior researchers localized an MCE by estimating its generated magnetic field. However, it is difficult to separate the magnetic field of the MCE from that of the MNS due to modeling errors, sensor magnetization, and electronic noise. In this study, we propose a robust localization algorithm that operates even in the presence of a strong external magnetic field generated by a cylindrical PM of an MNS to actuate an MCE. We constructed a bilinear interpolation model of the external magnetic flux density measured at the central magnetic sensor when the cylindrical PM of the MNS moves along the axisymmetric plane. Then we applied coordinate transformation to predict the magnetic flux density at other sensors. Using this model, the magnetic flux density generated by the MNS can be subtracted from the measured magnetic flux density to determine that of the MCE. In addition, we incorporate a weighting factor considering the signal-to-noise ratio of the magnetic sensor into the proposed algorithm to increase the estimation accuracy of the position and orientation of the MCE. The proposed algorithm is validated through experiments with a 3×3 magnetic sensor array and a commercial MCE. We experimentally demonstrate that the proposed algorithm can estimate the position and orientation of the MCE within a clinically applicable range even in the presence of an external magnetic field to actuate an MCE.

title: Development of a Multiaxial Loading Device for Mechanical Property Testing
authors: Guohua Zhao, Yang Yang, Baiquan Su, Hanjun Gao, Ping Zou, Tianqiang He, Yuxuan Tian, Shixu Ji, Dinghua Liu, Ruixi Luo, Xuefang Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Mechanical mechanisms or components typically subjected to multiaxial loads in engineering practice, and materials in such working conditions usually exhibited new mechanical properties or behaviors that were different from those under uniaxial loads. However, maximum errors of the widely used strength criteria, such as Von Mises and Tresca criteria, could reach up to 15.00% since they were derived equivalently from the results of uniaxial mechanical experiments due to the lack of multiaxial loading devices (MLD). To utilize materials more safely and effectively, a new MLD based on the hybrid mechanism was designed and fabricated in this paper. Meanwhile, its workspace was optimized based on the kinematic analysis, while values of the driving force decreased 66.67% compared to that before. Following mechanical calibration and compensation, the expanded relative uncertainty of the MLD was only 0.42% (k=2). Notably, the maximum error in uniaxial and multiaxial loading experiments on the MLD was merely 2.40%, which demonstrated that it was feasible to perform multiaxial force loading experiments on the MLD. Finally, the coupling and strengthening effects, under different types of loads on a carbon fiber reinforced polymer (CFRP) composite material, were investigated based on the multiaxial force loading experiments.

title: TEMPEST: A Machine Learning Framework for Battery Thermal Prediction and Chemistry Selection
authors: Aadya Arora, Siya Patil, Pallavi Bhardwaj
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Lithium-ion batteries are the cornerstone of modern portable electronics and electric vehicles. However, cell temperature profoundly influences their operational efficiency, safety, and lifetime. We present TEMPEST: Thermal Estimation and Chemistry Selection Toolkit, a data-driven framework that couples machine learning-based temperature prediction with chemistry recommendation. Our method trains long-short-term-memory (LSTM) models on three lithium-ion battery chemistries: nickel-cobalt-aluminum (NCA), nickel-manganese-cobalt (NMC), and lithium-iron-phosphate (LFP). These models are used to estimate surface temperature from voltage and current time series under controlled ambient conditions. TEMPEST consistently achieves high fidelity under varying operational load and ambient conditions, with mean absolute error below <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.5^{\circ }\mathrm{C}$</tex-math></inline-formula> for the same chemistry. The proposed approach is also extended to cross-chemistry transfer, providing stable generalization at room temperatures, with typical errors up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.0^{\circ }\mathrm{C}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, enabling accurate thermal monitoring without intrusive sensors. At extreme temperature conditions, the mean absolute error can rise up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$5^{\circ }\mathrm{C}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, giving higher errors for larger electrochemical mismatch between chemistries. Further, to experimentally validate the proposed method, in-lab data is collected using a standard electro-thermal battery cycler, where thermal prediction errors as low as <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.4^{\circ }\mathrm{C}$</tex-math></inline-formula> are achieved, demonstrating strong adaptation capability under limited data. When benchmarked against popular methods, our model achieves a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> accuracy improvement while requiring approximately <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$20\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> less compute. We also introduce a battery selection algorithm that, given ambient temperature, recommends the battery chemistry expected to operate at the lowest temperature. Our results reveal that NMC and NCA cells are preferable at sub-ambient conditions, whereas LFP chemistries offer thermal stability at elevated temperatures.

title: User-Centered and Task-Oriented Software System Design and Implementation for Smart Cities
authors: Jing Zhang, Xinyi Sun
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: To address the practical limitations of existing smart city information systems—such as complex information organization, fragmented business processes, and low user operation efficiency—this paper proposes a user-centered, task-oriented software system design and implementation approach for smart city applications. The method begins with systematic user needs analysis, followed by modeling and optimization of typical smart city tasks. Based on task workflows, a task-driven system architecture and corresponding functional modules are constructed to achieve tight coupling between system functions and user tasks. At the implementation level, a smart city software prototype supporting multi-role collaboration and concurrent task execution is developed. Representative urban management scenarios are designed, and comparative experiments are conducted to evaluate system performance in terms of task completion time, number of operational steps, and system usability. Experimental results show that, compared with traditional smart city information management systems, the proposed system significantly reduces task execution time and operational complexity across multiple typical tasks, while markedly improving overall usability. The results confirm the effectiveness of user-centered, task-oriented design methods in smart city software system development and provide a practical technical pathway for the engineering design and optimization of smart city information systems.

title: AI assisted IOT framework for real-time detection and selective tripping to prevent unauthorized electric fence tapping
authors: Ambikha C, Badi Alekhya, Kondapaturi Srichandana, Mahadiya Maheen K F, Mohammed Burhan K, Gandhavalli Kalyani, Kamalika M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: In rural and agricultural communities, the impacts of electric fence tapping and power theft is a serious issue affecting public safety, energy loss, and grid reliability. Farmers and landowners frequently tap fences directly into live distribution lines for the purpose of keeping animals out, often without awareness of the high-voltage risk these practices pose not only to themselves, animals, and wildlife, but also the electrical equipment itself. These practices not only jeopardize the safety of the electrical equipment but can also result in significant financial loss as well, with estimations throughout some areas serviced by the Kerala State Electricity Board (KSEBL) ranging from 8 to 9 percent of power loss annually due to widespread nature of these activities. Current methods, such as manual patrolling of distribution networks, feeder load comparison, Voltage/ Current/ watt hours audits, are not effective. To overcome these challenges, we proposed a real-time detection, selective tripping, and AI-assisted IoT framework to prevent unauthorized access of electric fencing. This framework consists of a Residual Current Monitoring (RCM) system to continually detect leakage, Digital Signal Processing (DSP) analysis to validate anomalies, Time-Domain Reflectometer (TDR) for real-time fault location, and a Smart Relay Control Unit to provide selective tripping to isolate damaged line segments. Our predictive AI model, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), continues to analyze historical and current patterns to distinguish genuine faults from transient disturbances and locate locations at risk. Events detected are provided through a Live Network Intelligence Dashboard providing real-time diagnostic information including grid health, fault location, and automated service alerts. The framework provides uninterrupted service to authorized users and enables a scalable and sustainable proposition of supply and demand for the future smart power distribution network.

title: Low-Cost Wireless Sensor Networks for Continuous Air and Noise Pollution Monitoring in Urban Areas
authors: Jharna Agrawal, Purnima Sharma, Durga Puja
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: The health hazards of air and noise pollution in the city are significant, and they should be managed by the modern solutions. Conventional systems prove to be very expensive and inefficient in urban environment that is dynamic. The present paper suggests a low-cost wireless sensor network (WSN) system to real time pollution monitoring which uses virtual particulate matter and gas sensors, MEMS microphones, and a hybridized communication model (LoRaWAN/Wi-Fi). It utilizes sustainable operation that uses solar-assisted battery simulation and duty-cycling, whereas the use of machine learning models (Linear Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost) enhances the accuracy of the data by resolving sensor drift. The simulation produces 438 thousand fake samples on 104 days to emulate seasonal changes. The processing and visualization of the data processed on the cloud present interactive dashboards, spatial heatmaps, and automatic warning messages related to the surpassing of the pollution thresholds. The proposed simulation framework can be used as a vital instrument in smart city planning, which allows making evidence-based policies, traffic management strategies, as well as sustainable urban development due to the ability to conduct cost-effective testing on a virtual level and analyze scenarios prior to physical implementation.

title: Application of Knowledge-Augmented Generative Agents in Dynamic Scheduling for Discrete Manufacturing Workshop
authors: Yanpeng Wang, Yuanjian Zhong, Xu Sun
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: Discrete manufacturing workshops face dynamic events such as order disruptions and energy price fluctuations, necessitating rapid adjustments to production scheduling. Traditional approaches rely on predefined rules or optimization model reconstruction, limiting responsiveness and adaptability. This paper proposes a knowledge-enhanced generative agent that constructs a domain knowledge base containing process rules, equipment parameters, and historical strategies. This base provides contextual information to large language models, enabling automatic generation of scheduling solutions from disturbance commands. In an energy disturbance response scenario at an automotive component assembly workshop, the agent generated a scheduling solution within seconds. Peak-hour loads were significantly reduced, production tasks were completed on schedule, and overall profitability increased.

title: Zero-Knowledge Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning for Cross-Institutional Medical Imaging Diagnostics
authors: Bharath M B, Ashwni S S, Mamatha M, Sowjanya S, Kumar Dilip, Mala B A
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: With increasing dependence on AI for medical imaging diagnostics, privacy concerns and strict regulations continue to restrict data sharing across healthcare institutions. To address this, we propose a novel framework that enables cross-institutional collaboration without compromising sensitive patient information. Our system integrates federated learning with advanced privacy-preserving techniques, including homomorphic encryption, secure aggregation, differential privacy, and zero-knowledge proofs. Hospitals retain their data locally and contribute encrypted, noise-added model updates, ensuring that raw data never leaves the premises. Secure aggregation and encryption prevent any entity, including the central server, from accessing individual contributions. Differential privacy introduces mathematically bounded noise to mitigate risks from inversion and membership attacks. Meanwhile, zero-knowledge proofs allow clients to verify the legitimacy of their training process and updates without revealing internal computations or data. This layered privacy defense effectively counters gradient inversion, model poisoning, and membership inference attacks, all while maintaining strong diagnostic performance. Evaluated on real-world medical imaging datasets, our method balances accuracy with compliance to privacy laws like HIPAA and GDPR. The proposed architecture offers a scalable and trustworthy approach to enable AI-driven diagnostics across hospitals, ensuring patient confidentiality is never compromised.

title: Transfer Learning Driven Flower Classification With Optimized MobileNetV2 Architecture
authors: Nagaraj M. Lutimath, Swathi A, Tripti Kulkarni, Vinay T R, Usha C R, Jyothi M V
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Transfer learning is a widely utilized and crucial technology that facilitates the transfer of knowledge from one domain to solve new problems. It is extensively applied in various classification tasks, including flower classification, which presents unique challenges due to the extensive variety of flower species, various of those have similar shapes, appearances, or share environmental features namely, the leaves and grass of the different flowers. In this work, we present AdMobileNet, a refined MobileNetV2 model specifically designed for floral classification. The AdaGrad (Adaptive Gradient Algorithm) optimizer, which is popular in deep learning because it can modify the learning rate of each parameter separately, is used in the model. AdaGrad makes it possible for the neural network to be trained more efficiently and adaptably by utilizing the collected historical gradients. AdMobileNet utilizes SparseCategoricalCrossEntropy loss function to achieve superior performance on the Kaggle flower dataset. Sparse Categorical Cross Entropy is also an efficient loss for any multiclass classification problem. We have demonstrated classifying performances of the AdMobileNet and compared with classical CNN design. The experiment outcome showcase that the proposed AdMobileNet has superior performance than CNN in this task, which illustrates the efficiency in recognizing diverse flower patterns. This is a promising alternative in areas such as botany, agriculture, and other situations where flower identification is essential.

title: An Efficient Technique for Classifying Multiple Skin Diseases Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Vandana, Aashdeep Singh, Manpreet Kaur, Surbhi Dhiman, Dinesh Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: Monkeypox (Mpox) virus infection along with other viral and malign skin diseases is still posing formidable challenges in terms of their visually similar characteristics and class imbalance in clinical data sets. Proper classification of such diseases is considered equally importantin order to take early actions towards rapid interventions to achieve good results in clinical settings. The proposed work utilizes the framework of Dense Convolutional Neural Network (DCNN) to implement multi-class classification of clinical dermoscopy images of human skin to properly classify such diseases. The proposed methodology uses image normalization, image resizing, along with Random Over-sampling to cure class imbalance problems in clinical data sets. A proposed deep CNN model is trained to compete against other renowned transfer learning methodologies such as VGG16, VGG19, DenseNet121, DenseNet201, along with MobileNetV2 to measure their proficiency using accuracy, recall, precision, F1 measure, specificity, along with area under Curve (AUC). The proposed model delivers out-and-out results in terms of attaining accuracy of 98.5% along with AUC of 99.92%, outshining all other contemporaries in this field utilizing similar data sets.

title: Energy Yield Enhancement Using Bifacial Photovoltaic Modules Under Indian Climatic Conditions
authors: Revatthy Krishnamurthy, Bura Vijay Kumar, Babitha Lincy R, Nagendra Kumar Yakkala, Sumanpreet Kaur, Nimesh Raj
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: Bifacial photovoltaic (PV) technology has been proposed as an effective way of increasing the solar energy production by taking advantage of the irradiance on the front and back of the module. The Indian weather offers the desirable conditions to locate bifacial PVs due to the high levels of solar insolation, high ambient temperature, and a wide range of surface features. The paper contains a systematic performance study of bifacial photovoltaic modules under the representative conditions of Indian climatic conditions. The paper has used an analytic modeling platform that involves consideration of effective irradiance estimation, rear-side irradiance input guided by ground albedo, and temperature-sensitive electrical performance analysis. An alternative evaluation of bifacial and mono-facial PV systems of the same rated power is performed with standard performance measurements, such as energy yield, performance ratio, capacity utilization factor and bifacial gain. The methodology incorporates climatic parameters, installation geometry and system losses that will make the operating conditions appear realistic. It is done by sensitivity analysis to interest in how ground albedo and installation parameters affect bifacial system performance. The findings of this paper give useful information on the design options and performance capabilities of bifacial photovoltaic system in the Indian environment and form an informed decision-making regarding the large scale and rooftop solar systems.

title: Decentralized Certificate Authentication and Fraud Prevention Using Blockchain for Educational Institutions and Employers
authors: Raghul D, Hari Prasath U, Muthunagai S U
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The increasing issue of fake educational and professional certificates are creating serious difficulties in the verification of certificates. The proposed project will focus on implementing a decentralized certificate verification and fraud protection scheme based on concept of blockchain technology, with the intention of achieving secure, transparent, and tamperproof digital certificate verification. The proposed project will apply cryptographic hash functions to produce digital signatures of certificates, unchangeable on the blockchain. The proposed system has three primary modules: a certificate issuance and blockchain registration module for the administrator, a verification module for organizations, and a scanning module that scans certifications using quick response technology to make the real time verification possible. By using the quick response code from the certificate and associating it with blockchain-based information, there is greater usefulness in terms of transferability. The system identifies alterations made to certificate using a hash function.

title: Electromagnetic Design of a 10 GHz Conformal Band-Pass Frequency Selective Surface for Airborne Radome Applications
authors: Rajesh Mooda, Manimegalai B, Ravi Kumar Arya, Maxon Okramcha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: This paper presents a 10 GHz band-pass frequency selective surface (FSS) for airborne radome applications. The proposed design is developed on Rogers RT5880 substrate (thickness = 0.508 mm) works well for both planar and conformal forms, like hemispherical radome structures. The FSS has a bandwidth from 8.89 GHz to 11.2 GHz (23.1% fractional band-width) and an insertion loss of less than -0.1 dB in the passband. Stable transmission characteristics are maintained up to 50° oblique incidence for both TE and TM polarizations, confirming excellent angular stability. The cylindrically and spherically conformal radome design of the proposed structure makes sure that there is very little transmission loss and electromagnetic transparency across the operational band and good angular stability of 40° and 35°, respectively. The simulation results confirm the FSS-based radome’s potential for X-band airborne applications.

title: Hierarchical Federated Edge Intelligence Architecture for Transaction-Level Fraud Detection in Digital Banking Systems
authors: Syed Hassan Imam Gardezi, Timur Kholmukhamedov, Sudha Arogya Mary Chinthamani, Botlaguduru Sai Naga Sravani, Feras Almarashdi, I Infant Raj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Due to the rapid growth of digital banking, the number of fraud cases has risen, and it requires intelligent, safe, and real-time detection systems. The current centralized and fixed models have scale, privacy and cross-institutional limitations. The proposed study presents a new architecture called H-FEFTNet (Hierarchical Federated Edge Fraud Transaction Network), a framework that combines federated edge intelligence, explainable AI and adaptive aggregation systems to detect transaction-level fraud. The proposed model is implemented with the help of the Bank Account Fraud Dataset Suite (NeurIPS 2022) to make collaboration privacy-preserving, low-latency, and better model generalization. Experimental analysis is shown to have better results with an accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score of 98%, 97%, 96%, and 96% respectively, and is better than related works in performance and interpretability. The system is very relevant to real time digital banking ecosystems, which fosters transparency, compliance and resiliency in financial fraud prevention.

title: Selection of Motor Drive for Electric Vehicle Applications Using AHP-SAW Based MCDM Approach
authors: Kalkin Loganathan, M K Loganathan, C M Tan, Rohith Krishna C, Josheph Fernando L, Ajit Kumar Verma
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: Choosing a suitable motor drive is a critical decision making process in electric vehicle (EV) design, as it substantially affects overall performance, efficiency, and cost. Various motor types such as Brushed DC motors, Brushless DC (BLDC) motors, Switched Reluctance Motors (SRM), Induction Motors (IM), and Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSM) are widely considered for EV systems. Each type offers various benefits and limitations with respect to torque, efficiency, power density, control complexity, reliability, and life cycle cost. This paper evaluates and compares these motor drive options based on multiple operational parameters to chose the best alternative for EV applications, using a well- known Multi-Criteria Decision Making (MCDM) approach; AHP-SAW. This research provides a framework for selecting optimal motor drives, supporting informed decision-making in developing efficient and reliable EVs.
