title: Scalable Multilingual Fake News Detection using Parameter-Efficient Transformers
authors: Shiwali Shiwali, Poornima Tyagi, Pradeep Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: In multilingual societies like India where fake news spreads fast through social and regional boundaries, internet misinformation has become a major menace. The majority of the existing fake news detection systems are very effective in English but fail to generalize to multilingual environment because it is very computationally inefficient and as more languages introduced, model performance often degrade due to multilingual scalability challenges.This paper introduced a lightweight multilingual fake news detector,based on parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) for a transformer-based models. The framework incorporates LoRA adapters into existing multilingual transformers to allow adapting effectively across languages and with lower computational needs. Multimodal inputs comprising of written and pictorial information are taken into account with the help of datasets like MMIFND and FakeNewsNet.The suggested strategy will incorporate the development of competitive detection capacity and enhance the parameter efficiency, training cost, and inference efficiency. On balance, this paper would offer a scalable and practical approach to detecting multilingual misinformation, and specifically to low-resource language environments.

title: BicephNet: A Hybrid CNN–ViT–SE Architecture with FFT-Head and Bidirectional Cross-Attention for Hierarchical Multi-Disease and Multi-Stage Brain MRI Classification with Web Application
authors: B Arthi Priya, Mohammad Farukh Hashmi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Accurate diagnosis of neurological abnormalities such as Alzheimer’s disease (AD), Parkinson’s disease (PD), and brain tumors remains a major obstacle because of significant inter-patient variability and overlapping structural patterns. The majority of current architectures take a limited, single-disease approach and fall short of fully utilizing the complementary nature of spatial, contextual, and frequency-domain information, despite the fact that deep learning techniques have shown great promise in medical image analysis. In order to overcome these constraints, this paper presents BicephNet, a novel hierarchical MRI diagnostic architecture that combines spectral FFT-head for frequency-aware learning, Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) channel re-weighting, Vision Transformer (ViT)-based global attention modeling, convolutional spatial encoding, and a bidirectional cross-attention mechanism for multi-branch feature fusion.The suggested framework first determines the disease category at Level-1, then performs a more detailed Level-2 subclassification in order to mimic clinical diagnostic workflows including Alzheimer’s disease stages and tumor subtype identification.

title: Performance Optimization of a Cylindrical DBD Ozone Generator Using RMS and Metaheuristic Algorithms
authors: E. Khelifi, M.N. Brahami, I.S. Bousmaha, F.Z. Boudjella, M. Brahami, R.D. Mohammedi, S.E. Bechekir, M. Jbilou
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This study focuses on optimizing a cylindrical dielectric barrier discharge (DBD) ozone generator used in air and water treatment systems. The impact of key parameters applied voltage, electrode length and discharge gap on ozone concentration and energy efficiency was experimentally examined, resulting in values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$51.5 \text{mg} / \mathrm{L}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.45 ~\mathrm{g} / \text{Wh}$</tex>. Response Surface Methodology (RSM) was employed to develop predictive models and investigate parameter interactions. To identify optimal operating conditions, two advanced metaheuristic algorithms, the Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA) and the Black Hole Optimizer (BHO), were implemented. The WOA achieved superior results with an ozone concentration of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$53.33 \text{mg} / \mathrm{L}$</tex> and an energy efficiency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.88 ~\mathrm{g} / \text{Wh}$</tex>, while BHO obtained <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$52.09 \text{mg} / \mathrm{L}$</tex> and 5.8 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{g} / \text{Wh}$</tex>. The hybrid use of RSM with metaheuristic optimization provides an effective strategy for enhancing DBD ozone generator performance, offering valuable guidance for future design and development.

title: Machine learning-Based Energy Prediction Model for Residential Buildings
authors: B.Vinodhkumar, S.Manikandan, S.Udhaya, M.S.Vijay Manikandan, K.Mohan, R.Krishnasamy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Residential buildings represent a major share of worldwide energy consumption, particularly due to heating, ventilation, and air-conditioning operations. Reliable forecasting of energy demand is essential for improving operational efficiency, lowering costs, and reducing environmental impact. This study presents a machine learning framework for predicting heating and cooling requirements in residential structures. A feed-forward artificial neural network (ANN) was developed using architectural and geometric building parameters such as compactness ratio, surface and wall areas, roof configuration, orientation, and glazing characteristics. The trained ANN demonstrated strong predictive capability with low mean squared error and a high regression coefficient, confirming its effectiveness in modeling nonlinear relationships between structural parameters and energy demand. The findings indicate that data-driven predictive systems can support intelligent energy management and sustainable residential design.

title: Finite-time Formation for Two-Wheeled Mobile Robots with A Triggered and Saturated Control
authors: Chao Wang, Peng Shi, Chee Peng Lim, Mehrdad Saif
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: In practical applications, input saturation is an unavoidable phenomenon that can degrade system performance and even lead to instability. This work, therefore, addresses the practical finite-time formation control problem of two-wheeled mobile robots under input saturation, a consequence of the physical limitations of their drive motors. To mitigate the adverse effects of input saturation while achieving finite-time convergence, a saturated controller is developed to realize leader-follower formation control. Furthermore, event-triggered strategies—both with and without continuous monitoring—are incorporated into the proposed controller to accommodate the limited computational resources of two-wheeled mobile robots. The Zeno phenomenon is explicitly excluded by ensuring that all inter-event intervals are bounded below by a strictly positive constant. The experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the developed controller in achieving finite-time formation for two-wheeled mobile robots in real-world applications.

title: DPSH-YOLO: An Efficient Detector for Capturing Local Details and Global Semantics of Tiny Objects
authors: Changzhi Liu, Yibin He, Xiuhua Zhang, Yanwei Wang, Zhenyu Dong, Hanyu Hong
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: With the rapid development of high-resolution remote sensing technology, detecting tiny objects in complex scenes still faces challenges such as weak feature representation and strong background interference. Current mainstream detectors have limitations in capturing fine-grained details of tiny objects while balancing efficiency and accuracy. To address these issues, we propose a new detector called DPSH-YOLO, which efficiently captures both local details and global semantics for tiny objects. First, we design a Dynamic Perception Aggregation (DPA) module that dynamically captures local detailed information along both horizontal and vertical directions, and effectively aggregates it with global information, thereby enhancing edge detail and semantic feature representation. Then, we introduce a dual-branch Snake Hybrid Attention (SHA) module, which fuses the local details from the high-resolution branch with the global context from the low-resolution branch to mitigate background interference. Experimental results on the VEDAI and AI-TOD remote sensing tiny object detection datasets show that DPSH-YOLO achieves accuracies of 78.18% and 65.43%, respectively (using mAP50 as the evaluation metric), significantly outperforming current state-of-the-art methods.

title: Multi-Domain Features Guided Supervised Contrastive Learning for Maritime Target Detection
authors: Junjie Wang, Yuze Gao, Zhenyu Xu, Dongying Li, Wenxian Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper presents a maritime target detection framework termed Multi-Domain Features Guided Supervised Contrastive Learning (MDFG_SCL), which integrates physically interpretable statistical features with deep feature representations within a supervised contrastive learning framework. To further enhance the discriminative capability of the shallow features, the Gini Index is calculated for different candidate features to evaluate their separability between clutter and target echoes, and these features are then adaptively combined through a weighted strategy. Specifically, in the wavelet domain, two effective features are designed, where radar echoes are processed through wavelet decomposition to extract the Wavelet Approximation Energy Ratio and Wavelet Entropy, which characterize signal energy distribution and complexity. These features, along with additional domain-specific components, are incorporated as structured priors to guide the representation learning of a convolutional neural network. This fusion enhances the model's ability to distinguish between sea clutter and target echoes. Experimental results on both simulated and real radar datasets demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves detection probability and enhances generalization performance across diverse maritime environments.

title: Deep Learning for Real Time Antenna Array Failure Recovery
authors: Jacob T. Young, Sawyer D. Campbell, Douglas H. Werner
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: We present an exceptional AI-assisted framework for fast and robust recovery of phase failures in planar antenna arrays. Using a U-Net-based neural architecture trained to replicate the results of gradient descent optimization. Effectively, removing the reliance on time consuming optimization methods for in situ recovery. Our approach is able to be adapted to any array geometry and provides failure recovery for up to 50% failure rate. Our method recovers on average over 90% of the performance achieved by an equivalent optimization—often matching or exceeding key directivity metrics. The model’s adaptability allows the user to dictate in the training process what array characteristics need recovered, such as null placement and sidelobe suppression, making it highly effective even in high-failure scenarios. Leveraging GPU-accelerated neural network predictions we reduce computation times dramatically, achieving a remarkable 67,127x in situ speedup over conventional optimization methods.

title: Ultra-Sensitive F-P Humidity Sensor Based on an Open-Cavity Note-Type Structure for GIS Low-Humidity Monitoring
authors: Hanrui Yang, Shuang Xie, Feng Xin, Shengxi Jiao
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This paper presents a novel Fabry-Pérot humidity sensor based on a polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) film with an open cavity, note-type structure, fabricated via a simple and repeatable cold splicing process. By humidity-sensitive characteristics of PVA, the sensor monitors the variation in cavity length to determine relative humidity (RH) precisely. Experimental results demonstrate that the sensor achieves a high cavity-length sensitivity of 13.42 μm/% RH and a corresponding wavelength sensitivity of 60.25 nm/% RH within a low-humidity range (6.12–16.69% RH), surpassing most reported fiber-optic counterparts. Crucially, it exhibits low cross-sensitivity to temperature, with a sensitivity of only 0.1856 nm/°C in the range of 14.12-46.27°C. The open cavity design and the note type structure, synergistically integrated with the humidity-responsive characteristics of the PVA film, endow the sensor with excellent high-sensitivity performance in low-humidity environments. This combination makes it a highly promising candidate for applications such as in-situ monitoring in gas-insulated switchgear.

title: Continual Reinforcement Learning Framework for Scalable Collision Avoidance and Mitigation System With Packing Strategy_supp1-3678453.mp4
authors: Dongsuk Kum
publish: N/A
abstract: Collision Avoidance and Mitigation System (CAMS) in autonomous driving systems is crucial for ensuring safety by formulating strategies to address various collisions and planning the trajectory accordingly. Although recent learning-based motion planning methods for CAMS have shown promising results for specific collision scenarios, the question of how to continually scale up their knowledge across different driving environments has not yet been thoroughly investigated. In this paper, we propose a scalable learning framework for CAMS that combines deep reinforcement learning and continual learning. By sequentially packing knowledge of new scenarios into a model trained on specific scenarios, the proposed method continually scales up the range of scenarios the model can handle while maintaining performance on previously trained scenarios. We apply this learning framework to a motion planner for CAMS and evaluate its scalability across new driving environments. This approach demonstrates a significant ability to pack knowledge by outperforming a model that jointly trains on all scenarios—typically considered an upper bound for continual learning—in about 2.4× fewer training steps, with a 4.19% higher collision avoidance rate and a 12.02% lower collision impact. Moreover, our approach achieves performance close to models trained on each scenario individually, with a model about 5.8× smaller.

title: MBD-Planner: A Real-Time Obstacle Avoidance Framework for UAVs via Feature-Domain Motion Blur Decoupling
authors: Sitian Peng, Rui Wang, Yu Liang, Longwei Wang
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: To address UAV obstacle avoidance under motion blur, we propose Feature-Domain Motion Blur Decoupling Planner (MBD-Planner), a real-time feature-domain motion blur decoupling framework that jointly disentangles blurred visual features and optimizes trajectories in an end-to-end manner. Unlike methods relying on explicit 3D reconstruction or ignoring blur in planning, MBD-Planner introduces a lightweight dewarping module that transforms blurred features into virtual sharp ones via depth back-projection and pose compensation, achieving sub-millisecond latency. A blur-aware optimization objective further couples camera motion and trajectory smoothing, while privileged training guided by ESDF gradients enables robust learning without expert demonstrations. Experimental results demonstrate that MBD-Planner enables reliable, smooth, and real-time navigation in visually degraded environments, effectively bridging the gap between theoretical planning and robust navigation.

title: AutoTrialGen: Automated Data Generation from Few Human Demonstrations via Trajectory Annotation and Simulation Trials
authors: Huailiang Ma, Aiguo Song, Mutian He, Mingyu Li, Yibing Yan, Linhu Wei
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: While imitation learning is a powerful paradigm for teaching robots complex manipulation skills, its effectiveness is often bottlenecked by the need for large-scale, human-collected datasets. This paper presents AutoTrialGen, an automated framework designed to generate large and diverse datasets of successful simulated demonstrations through trial-and-error, from only a few human demonstrations. Initially, AutoTrialGen employs a vision-language model to automatically decompose unprocessed human demonstrations into a library of object-centric and reusable skill primitives. Within a simulated environment, it then intelligently composes these primitives to solve new task instances, employing a novel weighted manipulability selection mechanism to ensure the quality and efficiency of the generated trajectories. Policies trained using the proposed sim-augmented data demonstrate improved performance and enhanced data efficiency across six diverse and challenging real-world manipulation tasks, compared with the baseline method.

title: A Traceability Method for Impulse Current Based on Parameter Identification of Its Magnitude-Frequency Characteristics
authors: Ning Yang, Yi Liu, Shijun Xie, Mingxing Lin, Jiyu Wang, Liuxia Li, Fuchang Lin, Zhaozhi Long, Yinglong Diao, Wenting Li
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Quantity traceability serves as the fundamental prerequisite for ensuring the accuracy of impulse current measurements. Existing methods primarily focus on the impulse scale factor, achieving its traceability through AC calibration at multiple frequency points and a synthetic model; however, the model accuracy is limited by preset static parameters. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a dynamic and self-adaptive traceability method based on parameter identification of the impulse current's magnitude-frequency characteristics. A universal analytical expression for the output impulse voltage of the measuring system is established to accurately fit the output waveform and extract its magnitude-frequency characteristics. Combined with the AC scale factors obtained via frequency sweep, the magnitude-frequency response of the input impulse current is reconstructed. Subsequently, parameter identification is performed based on the magnitude-frequency characteristic model of the input impulse current, enabling the inversion of its time-domain waveform. Throughout the uncertainty propagation process, uncertainty evaluation is conducted synchronously, thereby enabling a systematic assessment of the traceability accuracy for both the impulse current peak and time parameters. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed method, experimental investigations were carried out using a current transformer and a shunt. The results demonstrate that the relative uncertainties of the reconstructed input impulse current's peak, front time (T<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sub>), and time to half-value (T<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>), derived from the output impulse voltages of the two measuring devices, do not exceed 0.926%, 0.719%, and 0.664% (at 95% confidence level), respectively, for the 8/20 μs impulse waveform. Furthermore, the obtained quantities exhibit good consistency, validating the accuracy and efficacy of the proposed theoretical approach.

title: A Multi-Level Collaborative Voltage Optimization Method for Flexible Interconnected AC/DC Distribution Networks with High Proportion of Localized Renewable Energy Integration
authors: Fei Yang, Haitao Liu, Zhipeng Lv, Xiong Xiong, Yue Zhu, Zhihui Zhang, Shuai Liang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Flexible interconnected AC/DC distribution networks are increasingly deployed to accommodate high penetrations of renewable energy sources (RESs) and emerging DC loads. Coordinated voltage regulation and network reconfiguration across AC and DC subgrids, however, is challenging because day-ahead optimization decisions may be inconsistent with the real-time droop response of voltage source converters (VSCs), leading to voltage violations or reactive power shortages after implementation. This paper proposes a multi-level collaborative voltage optimization framework with iterative feedback and consistency constraints. The system layer solves a scenario-based stochastic mixed-integer convex optimization to determine reconfiguration states and VSC reference commands. The device layer evaluates the closed-loop response under real-time RES realizations and, whenever violations occur, generates sensitivity-based linear consistency cuts that are iteratively appended to the system-layer optimization. Case studies on a 51-bus hybrid AC/DC test system with four reconfigurable switches and three VSC links demonstrate that the proposed method eliminates voltage violations with limited operating cost and maintains practical computational performance. Sensitivity studies with respect to RES penetration, VSC capacity, and scenario number further validate robustness and scalability.

title: Digital Narratives as Academic Projects for Resilient and Culturally Sustainable Online Teaching
authors: António Maneira, Selma Pereira, Adérito Fernandes-Marcos
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: Digital narratives are gaining prominence in academic settings, demonstrating clear advantages for enriching learning environments and fostering deeper engagement, particularly in online education. This paper presents a comprehensive framework for strategically employing digital narratives to cultivate vibrant learning communities through meaningful cultural exchange and to integrate individual perspectives into a broader, interconnected global context. Investigating the nuanced role of personal digital narratives in elearning, we underscore their significant potential to foster inclusive pedagogical approaches that champion cultural sustainability, promote intercultural understanding, and bolster resilience in the face of globalization's challenges. Drawing upon a comprehensive and critical review of existing literature, an insightful analysis of student-created projects, and direct feedback from student participants, this study robustly highlights digital storytelling's capacity to facilitate deeper and more empathetic cultural understanding, safeguard intangible cultural heritage by providing platforms for its expression, and shape both individual and collective identities within a globalized world. We advocate for the thoughtful and ethical integration of digital narratives to transform online higher education, fostering more impactful, culturally sensitive, and ultimately transformative learning experiences in globally connected and multicultural learning environments. Additionally, the study examines the growing potential of accessible technologies and AI-assisted pedagogical tools to enhance creativity, inclusion, and reflective practice in online higher education. We advocate for the thoughtful and ethical integration of digital narratives to transform online higher education, fostering more impactful, culturally sensitive, and ultimately transformative learning experiences in globally connected and multicultural learning environments.

title: Public Acceptance of Artificial Intelligence in China: Evidence From a National Survey*
authors: Delong Tang, Junping Hu, Yuele Huang, Lei Ren
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: As artificial intelligence (AI) becomes embedded in economic and social systems, public acceptance has emerged as a critical factor shaping development trajectories and governance effectiveness. This study examines public attitudes toward AI in China through a “Cognition-Perception-Attitude” framework. We draw on scientific literacy theory, the Technology Acceptance Model, Diffusion of Innovations theory, and technophobia research. Using data from the 14th National Survey on Scientific Literacy in China (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=15,149$</tex>), we analyze how different population segments understand, perceive, and respond to AI technologies. Results show widespread public confidence: 83.2% express optimism about China's AI development, and 69 % recognize its transformative potential. However, concerns persist: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 1. 5 \%}$</tex> worry about job displacement. Public understanding varies considerably by education, occupation, and region. Perceptions of usefulness, usability, and ethical implications emerge as strong predictors of trust and acceptance. Broadening AI adoption requires addressing three interconnected challenges: raising scientific literacy, improving algorithmic transparency, and reducing structural inequalities. These findings offer practical guidance for designing literacy programs, communication strategies, and governance mechanisms suited to China's developmental context.

title: Real-Time Sudden Cardiac Arrest Prediction from ECG Using a Temporal Deep Ensemble Framework
authors: M. Sri Rangalakshmi, Nareddy Koushik Reddy
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Due to the lack of reliable techniques that can provide early warnings before clinical collapse, sudden cardiac arrest (SCA) continues to be a significant cause of unanticipated mortality. Traditional ECG monitoring techniques are typically reactive, identifying issues only after an arrhythmia has begun. A Temporal Deep Ensemble architecture-based real-time early SCA prediction system is presented in this paper. Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN), CNN-BiLSTM, and HRV-based Transformer models simultaneously gather and evaluate multi-scale ECG data, such as time-frequency representations, HRV indices, and raw waveform morphology. To create reliable risk scores, the outputs of these networks are combined using weighted probabilistic fusion with uncertainty estimates. Experimental evaluation using annotated ECG datasets results in an average accuracy of 78–82%, sensitivity of 84–88%, specificity of 70–75%, and an AUC of around 0.88 for predicting SCA up to 60 seconds ahead of time. Supporting proactive cardiac intervention through prompt risk assessment is the main goal.

title: Beyond the Written Test: A Comprehensive Study of New-Generation Examinations
authors: Vani Hiremani, Yash Mulay, Prakash Sonwalkar
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: The traditional examination systems in India depend on rote learning, academic dishonesty, and have scalability issues. They do not meet the changing needs of 21st-century education. This paper examines closely the landscape of assessments in India today. The paper brings to the forefront the influence of NEP 2020 and the rapid evolution of technology. The paper suggests new modes of assessment. These are AI-adaptive assessments, competency-based tests, gamification-based learning spaces, integrated project-based formats, peer evaluation, and secure open-book and open-web exams. These are tailor-made for the Indian context. The advantages of these methods are to foster higher-order thinking, enhance participation, and enhance employability. The article also discusses the problem of applying these methods, the technology facilitating them, and crucial ethical concerns. These are concerns about academic integrity, fairness, access, and the protection of student data. The paper concludes with a consideration of the policy implications and those for educators. It outlines prospects for future research that are necessary for India's transition toward a more balanced and effective assessment system.

title: A Patient-Centric Approach to Electronic Healthcare Data Management Leveraging Hyperledger Fabric
authors: Yaduvir Singh, Shalini Jaiswal, Vivek Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The fast digitising of healthcare systems has made a strong and the safe approaches for handling electronic healthcare data necessary. Using Hyperledger Fabric (v2.0), a permissioned blockchain architecture, we propose a patient- centric method of electronic healthcare data management in this paper. Our deployment creates a distributed network intended to safely save patient medical records, therefore ensuring that patients remain at the centre of the system under complete data management. The need of specific patient permission for any access to medical records is a main characteristic of this system, hence improving privacy and security. Authorised doctors can effectively execute Create, Read, Update, and Delete (CRUD) actions on individual patient records using this system, therefore enabling flawless and safe data management. This paper shows a scalable and efficient framework for handling sensitive healthcare data while giving patient autonomy and operational efficiency top priority by including Hyperledger Fabric's advanced features—smart contracts and private data channels.

title: Real-Time Stress Mood Detection Using Deep Learning And Webcam-Based Facial Analysis
authors: Chandrakala G Raju, Divyashree S, Samarth Prasad, Shrijani Vaikunth Bhat, Siri R G, YashRaj
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: People are often subjected to increased psychological stress and emotional instability in today’s academic and professional settings, which has led to a significant need for easily accessible mental health monitoring services. In order to identify emotional states and gauge stress levels, this work presents an automated framework that analyzes live video captured by webcams. In order to differentiate between seven different affective categories—anger, disgust, fear, happiness, sadness, surprise, and neutral expressions—the implementation makes use of convolutional neural networks trained on greyscale facial imagery (48×48 resolution). Random oversampling techniques were used to correct dataset imbalances, and Haar cascade algorithms were used for real-time processing in facial localization. Comprehensive visualization elements such as probabilistic emotion distributions, dynamic stress gauges, and aggregate stress metrics calculated over monitoring sessions are fed by detected emotions. One unique feature is an emotionally responsive conversational agent that provides individualized wellness adviced by adjusting interaction strategies based on detected affective states. Performance evaluation shows effective real-time operation and satisfactory classification accuracy, indicating viability for realistic affective computing deployments in mental wellness applications.

title: Emerging Trends in Nanorobotics for Environmental Remediation: A Bibliometric Review
authors: Dwarikanath Choudhury, Sasmita Nayak, Sushil Kumar Sahoo
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Nanorobotics is a breakthrough approach to cleaning up the environment since it can find and get rid of pollutants in a precise, flexible, and multipurpose way. By analyzing global publication, citation, and collaboration trends, this bibliometric research finds developing trends, and major players. Based on the data, there was a dramatic increase in research activity in the 2015-2025 time period. China, India, and the United States play major roles in this research landscape with many leaders in research being top-ranked universities, top ranked journals, and top rated researchers. While applications include wastewater treatment, heavy metal removal, and microplastic degradation, the focus of research is primarily in engineering and the chemical sciences. While research has been advancing in recent years, there still exists gaps in appropriate mitigation at large scales, energy efficiency, and contaminant specific optimization. In addition, a number of emerging technologies such as magnetic actuation, nanomaterials, and propulsion are driving innovation through convergence of technologies. Overall, the review provides an excellent basis for researchers to anchor their research on in the future and encourages collaborative, interdisciplinary approaches to develop and deploy scalable and sustainable nanorobotic solutions applied to environmental problems.

title: Design and FPGA Implementation of CNN Accelerator with RISC-V for System-on-Chip
authors: Nirmal Lunagariya, Gunjan Thakur, Dev Desai, Deepak Sahu
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: This paper presents a CNN accelerator designed as a coprocessor to a RISC-V core for efficient execution of machine learning tasks on System-on-Chip (SoC) platforms. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are widely used in applications such as image processing and speech recognition, but their high computational demand poses challenges related to power consumption and execution time. General-purpose processors often fall short in handling these workloads efficiently. To address this, the proposed architecture offloads core CNN operations to a dedicated hardware accelerator while leveraging the flexibility and low-power benefits of the open-source RISC-V platform. The design is implemented to perform the digit recognition task in Verilog HDL and tested on an Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA). Results show a significant reduction in number of clock cycles to execute the task compared to using the RISC-V core alone. This indicates improved efficiency and faster execution with the proposed CNN architecture and hence suitable for embedded machine learning applications.

title: Identification and Analysis of Burst Missing Temperature Data in Weather Stations Using Comparative Statistical Methods
authors: Pankaj Agrawal, Pratik Gite
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Accurate and uninterrupted temperature data from weather stations are significant for climate studies, weather forecasting, smart farming resource optimization and environmental simulation. However, missing data, particularly in bursts where more than five consecutive values are absent, impose a significant challenge to data quality. This research work focuses onidentifying and diagnosing burst missing temperature data across seven automatic weather stationsin Delhi, viz. PRAGATI_MAIDAN, PUSA_AMFU, DPSRU_SAKET_PUSHPVIHAR, KV_JANAKPURI, KV_JHARODA_KALAN, KV_NARAYNA, and SALWAN_SCHOOL_MAYUR_VIHAR. The previous time series interpolation methods are suitable for handling occasionally missing values but they generally fail to accurately estimate burst missing data due to the lack of persistent timeframe. To solve this problem, we advised a robust framework that uses daily correlation between weather stations to impute burst missing values. By analyzing the strong spatial relationships and daily temperature patterns across stations, our method provides reliable estimates even in scenarios with extended data gaps. Experimental results demonstrate that this hybrid approach achieves a root mean squared error (RMSE) of less than 0.5°C across various weather conditions in Delhi and other places, outshining conventional interpolation techniques of random missing data. This study highlights the importance of geographical interconnection in handling burst missing data and offers a efficient solution for improving the quality of meteorological datasets. The results shows significant implications for climate research, weather prediction, and environmental monitoring, enabling more accurate and trustworthy analysis.

title: Automated Rule-Based EV Charging Station Allocation
authors: Sam Priyesh. V, Dhanush. S, Piyush Pratap Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The rapid growth of electric vehicles (EVs) has created a new set of challenges in the refueling infrastructure and energy distribution. This research paper presents an "Automated EV Charging Allocation" integrated with a "Path Navigation System" solution that utilizes a rule-based system to reserve the nearest charging station based on distance, battery information, and availability of chargers. This system employs a two-way communication to transfer real-time data such as battery level, estimated arrival time, slot availability, hence assuring safe navigation and arrival of the rider. Upon continuous simulation and testing, it has shown that utilizing automated communication reduces manual errors, congestion and improves the reliability of EV charging networks.

title: Digital Readiness and STEM Integration in Primary Mathematics: Evidence From Indonesian Teacher Education
authors: Ade Gusti Ningsih, Firmansyah, Amran, Nadhira Fasya Salsabila
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: This study explores the intersection between STEM pedagogy and technology-enhanced learning (TEL) within the context of digital transformation in Indonesian primary mathematics education. Employing a quantitative descriptive survey design, the research integrates a survey of 340 in-service teachers enrolled in the Professional Teacher Education Program with an analytical framework grounded in digital transformation theory and the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM). Descriptive results indicate that teachers demonstrate high theoretical perceptions of STEM but moderate levels of classroom implementation, reflecting a gap between awareness and practice. Conceptually, the study argues that digital readiness and institutional support are key drivers of sustainable TEL-based STEM implementation. The proposed framework aligns STEM integration with digital pedagogical competencies and technological infrastructure, contributing to the broader discourse on transforming mathematics education in developing contexts.

title: Green Technology Innovation and Firm-Level Technical Efficiency: A Two-Stage Analysis in the Philippines
authors: Ibrahim Niankara
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)
abstract: This study examines how green technology innovation (GI) influences firm-level technical efficiency in the Philippines, applying the Resource-Based View (RBV) and institutional theory Using data from 748 firms in the 2024 World Bank Enterprise Survey Green Economy module, a two-stage method is employed First, Data Envelopment Analysis measures technical efficiency across firms of different sizes, sectors, and regions Second, a control function Tobit model assesses the impact of GI (via the GreenInov2 index), adjusting for internal resources (e g, financial access, energy and internet costs, labor, and managerial experience) and external pressures (e g, environmental inspections, climate strategies). Results show GI improves efficiency by 7.3-8.7%, with greater gains for large firms and urban manufacturers, especially in Calabarzon. Robustness is confirmed using alternative metrics and instrumental variable models. The findings position GI as a VRIN resource under RBV and highlight the role of regulation under institutional theory.

title: Automatic Diagram Generation with LLMs for Visual Reasoning in Education
authors: Shicheng Liu, Tong Lin
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown strong capabilities in text-based educational tasks, they struggle with diagram generation due to the absence of visual perception. Vision-language models offer partial solutions but are limited by cost and imprecision. In light of these challenges, we introduce a model-agnostic framework that enables LLMs to reason about and generate diagrams using structured, text-based object representations and callable tools. A custom library defines manipulable diagram elements, while a self-reflective prompting strategy guides the model through iterative planning and revision. Applied to bar modelling in the Singapore math curriculum, our approach produces accurate, interpretable visuals without relying on image understanding—offering a scalable and explainable alternative for visual reasoning in education.

title: Dijkstra based routing in Vehicular Network using IoT emphasis on QoS parameters
authors: Raghavendra M, Manjunath K G
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: In this paper Quality of Service (QoS)-aware vehicular routing is presented. The study employs Dijkstra’s algorithm to determine the best route between source and destination nodes. As a shorted path technique, Dijkstra’s algorithm ensures efficient route computation. The simulation is conducted on a 1 km × 1 km urban vehicular network. Initially route with out accident is calculated. In the subsequent run, accident scenario is modeled. Using Dijkstra re-routing strategy, the route is calculated, which avoids the accident area and vehicle will move in alternative route. This increases the efficiency of the system. The vehicle need not have to wait in the traffic jam. The proposed simulation framework evaluates routing performance based on critical QoS metrics such as Energy Consumed by Vehicle, Energy Consumed by the Data, Time Taken by the Data To Travel to base station and Time Taken by the ACK To Travel back to vehicle.

title: Evaluating Optimization Algorithms for Enhanced Heart Disease Prediction Using Neural Networks
authors: Beera Raju, Rajesh Kumar Kanji, Bollapalli Althaph, Nagendra Panini Challa
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Heart illness is a significant global cause of mortality, underscoring the imperious for the expansion of accurate and dependable forecast techniques. This study estimates the efficiency of eight optimizations techniques Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD), RMSProp, Adam, AMSGrad, Nadam, AdaBelief, LAMB, and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), working exclusive a reliable neural network context for forcasting heart illness. The dataset comprises important clinical attributes like Blood Pressure, Cholesterol levels, and age. The learning looks at how well different optimizers can make models converge, minimize loss, and generalize. AdaBelief and LAMB are good at generalizing, while Particle Swarm Optimization is good at finding its way through complicated loss landscapes. The comparative estimation of accuracy (94.8 %), precision, recall, and F1-score specifies significant differences among the optimization approaches. The results offer critical control for selecting suitable optimizers to enhance the reliability and resilience of medical diagnostic systems. Future study will observe hybrid optimization methods and their practical execution in clinical situations.

title: Proceedings
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)

title: Human-Centered Explainability in AI-Enhanced UI Security Interfaces: Designing Trustworthy Copilots for Cybersecurity Analysts
authors: Mona Rajhans
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) copilots are increasingly integrated into enterprise cybersecurity platforms to assist analysts in threat detection, triage, and remediation. However, the effectiveness of these systems depends not only on the accuracy of underlying models but also on the degree to which users can understand and trust their outputs. Existing research on algorithmic explainability has largely focused on model internals, while little attention has been given to how explanations should be surfaced in user interfaces for high-stakes decision-making contexts [8], [5], [6]. We present a mixed-methods study of explanation design strategies in AI-driven security dashboards. Through a taxonomy of explanation styles and a controlled user study with security practitioners, we compare natural language rationales, confidence visualizations, counterfactual explanations, and hybrid approaches. Our findings show that explanation style significantly affects user trust calibration, decision accuracy, and cognitive load. We contribute (1) empirical evidence on the usability of explanation interfaces for security copilots, (2) design guidelines for integrating explainability into enterprise UIs, and (3) a framework for aligning explanation strategies with analyst needs in security operations centers (SOCs). This work advances the design of human-centered AI tools in cybersecurity and provides broader implications for explainability in other high-stakes domains.

title: Heart illness forecast using a hybrid transfer machine learning classifier integrated with a deep feature extractor
authors: Nirmala M, Bhargavi Krishna, Chengareddy P, Naveen Karamala
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: CVDs (cardiovascular diseases) continue to be a major global cause of death, primarily due to the late detection of cardiac abnormalities. Advance with accurate forecast of heart illness is therefore essential for preventing cardiac arrest and enhance patient outcomes. Although machine learning (ML) methods is revealed ability in automated analysis, most existing approaches depend on single-source data and rely on isolated techniques, limiting their predictive capability. In order to improve the precision and reliability of heart attack forecasts, this investigation introduces a novel hybrid diagnostic framework that combines several machine learning models with an advanced feature generator. Statistical representations are extracted from the dataset using deep learning models (DL_Simple and DL_Complex). These representations are then assessed using a number of machine learning methods, such as Random Forest, XGBoost, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Logistic Regression (LR), and Gradient Boosting. To choose the best classifier, a thorough performance evaluation is carried out. Results from experiments show that the suggested multidisciplinary architecture greatly increases prediction accuracy. Logistic regression outperformed other cutting-edge methods among the assessed models, showing an overall precision of 92.11% and an extent beneath the ROC curve (AUC) of 96.59%.

title: Smart Agriculture used for Insect Detection Framework Edge Computing Technology
authors: Raju Kolluri, K. Kishore Kumar, Sankara Rao Allada, R. Jawwharlal, Maddala Janakidevi, Amit Gupta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Efficiency and environmental friendliness are two of precision agriculture's many advantages brought forth by data-driven technologies. Distributed transparent automation is required due to the fragmentation of farmlands. Along with fuzzy optimizers and auction procedures, we built an edge computing framework to link different parts of the supply chain.It recognizes insects on agricultural properties and provides accurate classification using CNN designs such as Visual Geometry Group 16 (VGG16), Xception Version 2 (XceptionV2), Residual Network 50 Version 2 (ResNet50V2), and Mobile Network Version 2 (MobileNetV2). Farmers may lower insect-related risks and make educated decisions by implementing a coordinated alarm system, which offers timely warnings and advice.

title: Cardiac Arrest Prediction Using Machine Learning Methods
authors: Mohit M Rao, Virendra Kumar Shrivastava
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: This study aims to analyze electrocardiogram data related to sudden cardiac death in subjects who have undergone COVID-19. The objective is to collect ECG data from both categories (subjects who were affected with COVID-19 and not) and analyze the results to identify any patterns which could be used to predict the probability of sudden cardiac arrest among covid-19 subjects. Multiple studies have concluded a relationship between sudden cardiac arrest and COVID-19, and this research aims to validate that hypothesis using ECG records. The scope of the research includes collection and identification of relevant datasets, conducting data preprocessing, transforming or extracting relevant information from ECG records, and determining any patterns by leveraging neural networks. SVM, Random Forest, XGBoost and CNN were leveraged to analyze the ECG datasets. The data was pre-processed using various techniques and analyzed with Orange and Scikit-learn packages. All the four algorithms demonstrated accuracy, precision, and recall above 80%. VGG19 was utilized to analyze the problem, while SMOTE was applied to handle the class imbalance. VGG19 achieved an accuracy of 0.91. XGBoost had an accuracy of 87%, and SVM had 85%.

title: AI and XAI in Stock Market Prediction: Exploring Limitations, Gaps, and Collaborative Solutions
authors: Chaithra Sathyampeth, Nagaraj B Patil, Arun Kumar R
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence significantly making changes in stock market trading by using deep learning approaches like LSTM and CNN, though they are powerful in predication but limited in transparency. XAI addresses this transparency issue by structure interpretability there by enhancing the trust and confidence in traders. This paper studies the multilingual emotion analysis, human center evaluation and latency. A pilot study on the data sets of NIFTY50 by combining the XAI methods of LSTM and SHAP achieving the highest accuracy in trade-off in Indian context. Comparative analysis of both AI and XAI techniques in stock market outlines the performance accuracy, interpretability and also highlights challenges involved in a regional data set. AI acts as structured road map in India by increasing the trust and confidence of the traders in stock market trading.

title: Financial and Trading Forecasting with Advanced Artificial Intelligence Techniques
authors: Neha Tripathi, Jamshid Pardaev, Juginis Dosekeev, Izzatbek Nafasov, Sindor Sapaev, Sharmila
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is now widely used in financial markets. This has changed how financial transactions are managed and made financial services more efficient, secure, and tailored to individual needs. Technical evaluation, basic questions, and investor views form the base for AI trading systems. This paper looks at the latest research on trading algorithms, showing which combinations worked well in improving trading results and which helped improve outcomes by using the mentioned data and steps together. Compilation of significant market news, historical stock prices, and fundamental financial indicators obtained from reputable sources is used as a dataset. Several AI architectures are applied, among them, Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, Transformer-based models (Temporal Fusion Transformer, Informer), Graph Neural Networks to capture cross-asset relations, and probabilistic models to estimate uncertainty. To solve this trading problem, decision-aware layers are trained, and reinforcement learning agents are developed in direct optimization of trading performance, besides transaction costs. The study found that developing a method to determine the optimal duration for training programs is essential. Future research will involve creating an automated financial trading system that integrates both fundamental and technical analysis.

title: Vision Transformer-Enabled Fruit Profiling: A Multi-Stage Ripeness Classification System
authors: K. Sarita, T. Jayananda Kumar, R. Tamilkodi, R. Ganesh, Sk. Maksood, P. Sai Venkatesh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Accurately determining fruit ripeness is vital in contemporary agriculture, as it ensures quality control, reduces post-harvest waste, and aids in harvesting fruits at their peak condition. This research proposes a better system utilizing a Vision Transformer (ViT) architecture for fruit profiling and classification across multiple ripeness stages, specifically targeting apples, oranges, and bananas. By harnessing the capabilities of transformer-based architectures, the system captures high level visual semantics and contextual cues from fruit images to classify them into three distinct ripeness stages which are fresh, unripe, and rotten. Traditional Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) rely on localized filters to process image data, which constrains their capacity to recognize dispersed or fine-grained visual cues essential for detailed classification tasks. Unlike CNNs, Vision Transformers apply a self-attention mechanism that links information from all parts of an image at once, allowing the model to understand spatial relations holistically. The ability to detect ripeness will improve due to the self-attention mechanism of Vision Transformers.

title: Improving EEG Emotion Recognition with Music using ST-GAT Model
authors: Saavi Mittal, Atishya Pradhan, Vipul Dixit
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Emotion estimation from EEG (electroencephalogram) signals has attracted a lot of attention in affective computing and has found applications in brain-computer interfaces, mental health tracking, and adaptability. Most of the conventional methods tend to neglect the intricate spatiotemporal features found in EEG signals. This paper introduces a new Spatial-Temporal Graph Attention Network (STGAT) specifically designed for EEG-based emotion estimation. Our model represents dynamic inter-channel relationships through graph structures and utilizes attention mechanisms to highlight discriminative spatial and temporal features. Evaluation on the MEEG dataset demonstrates that our proposed model achieves superior performance metrics relative to established baseline approaches, including TSception and AT-DGNN. The model exhibits enhanced accuracy and F1-score performance while maintaining greater robustness, reaching 88.2% accuracy and an F1-score of 0.86 on the MEEG dataset.

title: Ternary Variable-to-Fixed Length Balancing Scheme with Simple Encoding/Decoding
authors: Thabang C. Rapudu, Theo G. Swart, Elie N. Mambou
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The application of balanced (DC-free) codes is widely spread in the field of telecommunication, especially in magnetic and optical recording systems. However, most balancing schemes rely on lookup tables to decode information bits and therefore, they become memory inefficient, especially for long codewords. Additionally, most of such codes convert fixed-length information sequences to fixed-length balanced sequences which adds to the memory requirement. Thus, in this paper, a ternary variable-to-fixed (VF) length balancing scheme is proposed which does not require a look-up table. The proposed scheme requires significantly lower memory and has lower encoding/decoding complexity than prior-art balancing schemes. Specifically, the proposed VF length scheme requires O(n) digital operations for both the encoder and decoder. However, it trades off higher redundancy for these benefits.

title: Design of a Super-Elliptical Waveguide Bend Mode Converter and Multistage Tapered Waveguide for Dual-Polarized Omnidirectional Antennas
authors: Renshi Funabiki, Jiro Hirokawa, Takashi Tomura
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A dual-polarized configuration radiating from a circular aperture is proposed, in which circular TE01 (horizontal polarization) and TM01 (vertical polarization) modes are efficiently generated at 158 GHz using a super-elliptical waveguide bend mode converter and a multistage tapered waveguide. Undesired higher-order modes are effectively suppressed, and desired-mode transmissions of −0.02 dB (H) / −0.09 dB (V) in the mode converter and −0.07 dB (H) / −0.12 dB (V) in the tapered waveguide are achieved. Furthermore, in radiation from a circular aperture without a reflector, maximum azimuthal ripples of 1.9 dB for horizontal polarization and 1.7 dB for vertical polarization were obtained. The proposed feeding and mode-conversion system is suitable as a building block for reducing circumferential ripple in horizontally omnidirectional antennas.

title: Dynamically Segmented IRS-Assisted UAV Computing Power Networks: Towards System Delay and Energy Consumption Optimization
authors: Hao Hu, Yan Zhang, Zhaolong Ning, Chau Yuen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: In this paper, we propose a dynamically segmented Intelligent Reflecting Surface (IRS)-assisted Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) Computing Power Networks (CPNs) with tightly integrated communication and computing power resources. The IRS can be dynamically segmented and allocated to users, with computing resources allocated accordingly to satisfy their delay constraints. Considering the energy limitations of UAVs, we formulate a multi-objective optimization problem to minimize user delay and UAV energy consumption. To solve the problem, we propose a new scheme jointly considering UAV <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">t</u>rajectory, <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</u>omputing power allocation, reflecting element allocation, <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">p</u>hase shift, and UAV-user <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</u>ssociation (TCPA) scheme. The phase alignment theory is utilized to determine the IRS phase shift control and decompose the problem into three subproblems based on the coupling of variables. Specifically, we use channel optimal matching to solve the first subproblem to obtain user association decisions. Then, we formulate a computing power communication matching subproblem, and propose a successive convex approximation scheme to solve it. The trajectory subproblem is optimized by a multi-agent deep reinforcement learning-based method. The evaluation results demonstrate that our proposed TCPA achieves high performance in terms of reward and system delay. Additionally, it demonstrates that integrating IRS and CPNs can effectively reduce the total system delay with only a marginal increase in energy consumption.

title: Screen-Space Vortex-Aware Sound Synthesis for Ocean Waves via Particle-Based and Learning-Based Mapping
authors: Jong-Hyun Kim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents an audio–visual mapping framework for synthesizing ocean wave sounds that are temporally aligned with scene dynamics, derived from the motion of foam particles in particle-based fluid simulations. To efficiently handle large-scale particle data, the proposed method employs dynamic K-means clustering that adapts to variations in particle count, and performs example-based sound matching and volume control using cluster-level velocity- and density-based features. To overcome the limitations of scale-oriented mappings that fail to capture viewpoint changes and structural differences in flow patterns, we introduce a screen-space vortex representation. Specifically, the physical quantities of visible foam particles are projected onto a screen-space grid from the camera viewpoint, and a vortex (2D vorticity) field is computed from the projected 2D velocity field. This vortex feature is combined with velocity–density descriptors to improve the discriminability and temporal stability of data-driven sound matching. Furthermore, to preserve the interpretability of rule-based mappings while compensating for nonlinear and viewpoint-dependent relationships, we introduce a learning-based embedding as a solver extension that maps cluster- and vortex-level features into a latent space. Experimental results across various ocean wave scenarios (wave initiation, reflection, moving objects, spinning emitters, and propellers) demonstrate that the proposed approach produces differentiated auditory responses depending on the viewpoint, even under identical simulations, and significantly improves the stability of sound synthesis and audio–visual coherence compared to fixed clustering and scale-based mappings.

title: Improvements of the Single Angle Overlap Josephson Junction Technology for Qubit Application
authors: Muhammad Shoaib, Valentina Di Meo, Carlo Barone, Davide Buono, Alessandro Mauro, Sergio Pagano, Berardo Ruggiero, Mikhail Lisitskiy, Paolo Silvestrini
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: We develop a fully complementary metal-oxide semiconductor (CMOS) fabrication technique for the realization of a superconducting qubit network (SQN). In our current research work, we focus on a single-angle overlap Josephson junction fabrication technique, based on the lift-off process for defining both base and top electrodes, avoiding the wiring insulator layer and requiring an RF Ar etching process before AlO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> barrier growth. The fabricated Al/AlOx/Al microscale Josephson junctions have been tested at low temperature (T = 300 mK) in the superconducting state using current–voltage characteristic measurements.

title: Extraction of Critical Raw Materials from Waste Printed Circuit Boards Using Machine Learning and Computer Vision
authors: Muhammad Mohsin, Stefano Rovetta, Francesco Masulli, Alberto Cabri
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The extraction of critical raw materials (CRMs) from waste printed circuit boards (WPCBs) represents a crucial challenge for the economy, particularly in the electronics sector. To improve the concentration of individual CRMs and facilitate their extraction from WPCBs, we propose an innovative approach based on machine learning and computer vision to guide mechatronics system in selectively disassembling various types of electronic components from WPCBs. Our approach addresses the need for a robust and automated solution to identify and localize valuable components that contain CRMs. Specifically, we use YOLOv5 and YOLOv8 models, which are the state of the art convolutional neural networks (CNN) for detection and localization tasks. Our pipeline, called EC-Det, starts from collecting a custom image set called V-PCB, and obtains a large collection of 10,829 high-quality images of WPCBs. These images are used to train the YOLO family variants which show excellent accuracy. Furthermore, we consider not only accuracy but also memory usage, training time, and inference time for our CNN models, evaluating their suitability for resource-constrained on the edge systems. Our results show that integration of machine vision system provides a highly effective and reproducible solution for guiding automated mechatronics system, thereby enhancing the efficiency of the CRMs extraction process.

title: State Trajectory Control for Four-Switch Buck-Boost Converter With the Finite State-Machine Controller
authors: Shilin He, Rui Ling, Qilin Yi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: In this paper, a state trajectory control scheme is proposed to regulate the output voltage of a four-switch buck-boost converter. The proposed control scheme achieves fast dynamic responses without overshoot of the output voltage, which brings a stable output voltage in a wide range with the input voltage variety and rejection of load disturbance. The analysis in the state plane provides an accurate framework in the geometrical domain and insight into the behavior of switching modes of the four-switch buck-boost converters. The optimal switching conditions of the state trajectories are derived to obtain fast dynamic response and low inductor current spikes in transients. As a result, a finite state-machine controller is designed, the operation principle and the characteristics in steady-state are analyzed. The proposed control scheme is verified with experimental results on a 15V, 1.5A four-switch buck-boost converter prototype.

title: Multi-grained Gesture Modeling for Emotion Recognition
authors: Rong Wei, Xuepeng Huang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In recent years, emotion recognition from body gesture movements has become an important research field with the continuous development and improvement of depth sensors and computer vision algorithms. Most of the public datasets are records of continuous emotional actions, and frame by frame analysis or as a single gesture recognition will ignore the context relationship between actions. Thus, the method based on manual feature extraction performs poorly on large-scale datasets. To solve the above problems, this paper proposes an effective gesture emotion recognition model. The model is composed of pseudo energy model and multi-grained model. The pseudo energy model quantifies the energy of gesture and provides an important basis for key frame extraction. The multi-grained model is proposed on the basis of both fine grained which provides specific information and coarse grained which provides logical information. By integrating multiple levels of granularity into a unified architecture, we demonstrate enhanced classification performance. Our method surpasses the prior state-of-the-art approaches in two datasets and achieves the highest 82.7% classification accuracy in the KDAE dataset and 80.6% classification accuracy in the EMBG dataset. The excellent experimental results on two public datasets show that it is feasible to divide the emotion of action from the perspective of energy and use multi-grained model to provide the low-level and high-level features required by the network. In the task of gesture emotion recognition, our method is obviously superior to the state-of-the-art methods.

title: Together in Sync: Assessing Prosocial Behavior with a Full-Body Interactive Mixed Reality Experience
authors: Sena Beste Ercan, Belen Gonzalez, Olga Gali, Gray Atherton, Liam Cross, Narcis Pares
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: This study investigates how interpersonal synchrony (IPS) in full-body interactive Mixed Reality (MR) environments can shape children's prosocial behavior. We introduce DragonIce 2.0, a two-part MR game designed to induce synchronous or asynchronous group activity and assess their impact on prosocial behavior. To examine changes in affiliation (team belonging and peer liking) and collaboration (overt joint actions), we conducted a comparative study with children aged 9–10, using both system logs and self-report measures. The results highlight the importance of context, task structure, and perceived coordination in shaping social outcomes, offering insights into how interaction dynamics shape social engagement in immersive settings. This work contributes a novel approach for studying prosociality in ecologically valid, technology-mediated settings by combining playful, immersive interaction with controlled manipulation of group synchrony. Our findings inform the design of inclusive MR experiences that support social connection, collaboration, and group engagement among children.

title: Rethinking Frequency Modeling: Tail-Aware Dynamic Adversarial Training for Long-Tailed Robustness
authors: Chengze Jiang, Minjing Dong, Zhuangzhuang Wang, Jie Gui, Ju Jia, Yuan Yan Tang, James Tin-Yau Kwok
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Adversarial training (AT) is among the most effective defenses against adversarial attacks on deep neural networks. However, in real-world scenarios where data often follow long-tailed distributions, conventional AT methods struggle to handle such imbalance, resulting in severe robustness disparities across classes and limited overall robustness. Although recent efforts attempt to improve robustness through class frequency-aware weighting or distribution adjustments, our empirical analysis reveals that class frequency alone is an insufficient indicator of adversarial vulnerability, as robust accuracy does not correlate with the number of examples per class. Furthermore, AT under long-tailed distributions exhibits optimization instability, particularly for tail classes with limited data. To address these challenges, we present Tail-Aware Dynamic Adversarial Training (TAD-AT), which integrates three complementary components targeting the training loss, attack strategy, and weight average. TAD-AT captures data imbalance and performance disparity, improving adversarial robustness under long-tailed distributions. First, our training loss incorporates frequency- and accuracy-aware regularization to emphasize learning for vulnerable classes. Second, our attack adjusts perturbations based on class-wise vulnerability, encouraging robust feature learning around vulnerable regions, thereby mitigating robustness overfitting and improving clean accuracy. Third, our weight average improves robust generalization and training stability by adaptively controlling the decay rate across classes. Experiments on long-tailed benchmarks demonstrate that our TAD-AT significantly improves adversarial robustness, offering a systematic and practical solution to robustness challenges under long-tail distributions. Our code is publicly available on https://github.com/bookman233/TADAT.

authors: N/A
