title: GF-1-Assisted Pseudo-Label Augmentation and Leakage-Free Stacking for Sentinel-2 Chlorophyll-a Retrieval in Narrow Urban Rivers
authors: Fanle Meng, Che Wang, Xuejun Wang, Luanbin Yin, Ning Lu, Jun Qin
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Narrow urban rivers remain challenging for Sentinel-2-based chlorophyll-a (Chl-a) retrieval because mixed pixels, adjacency effects, and bank shadows can contaminate spectral signals, while in situ observations are often limited. This study develops an integrated workflow that integrates a high-confidence urban-river water mask with explicit shadow suppression to constrain feature extraction and sample selection, cross-resolution pseudo-label transfer by mapping a GF-1 Chl-a field to the Sentinel-2 10 m grid and stratifying it to form pseudo-labeled samples for augmentation, and a leakage-controlled out-of-fold stacking regressor with an XGBoost meta-learner to improve stability under data scarcity. On the in situ test set (n = 6), the GF-1 retrieval achieves R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.86 and RMSE = 3.32 μg/L. Using mapped pseudo-labels for Sentinel-2 training, the model attains R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.84 and RMSE = 3.88 μg/L on a held-out pseudo-label set (n = 40), while the in situ-only baseline yields R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.70 and RMSE = 4.47 μg/L on the separate in situ test set. These results indicate that cross-resolution pseudo-label augmentation and leakage-controlled ensembling can be effective under limited field supervision; the pseudo-label evaluation reflects internal consistency rather than independent ground truth.

title: Dual-Scale Graph Transformer with Spectral-Spatial Convolution for Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Yuanjiang Chen, Bin Yang, Taonian Bian, Xuan Zhou, Li Yue, Shunshi Hu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Hyperspectral image (HSI) classification has emerged as a critical task in remote sensing. However, the inherent high spectral dimensionality, limited labeled data, and complex spatial structures in HSIs present substantial challenges to traditional and even deep learning-based approaches. Traditional convolutional neural networks (CNNs) often struggle to capture the irregular, non-Euclidean spatial relationships among pixels, whereas standard Transformer architectures are limited in modeling localized spectral-spatial dependencies and are prone to overfitting in scenarios with limited training data. In this paper, we propose a novel Dual-Scale Graph Transformer with Spectral Spatial Convolution (DGTSS) framework that integrates multi scale structural modeling and deep spectral feature extraction in a unified architecture. Specifically, we construct two complementary graph representations: a pixel-level graph that captures fine grained spatial relationships and a super-pixel level graph that encodes broader semantic structures. These dual-scale graphs are processed by a Graph Transformer module that incorporates node features and enables effective modeling of long-range dependencies and context-aware feature aggregation. Furthermore, we introduce a dedicated Spectral-Spatial Convolution (SSC) module to enhance spectral feature discrimination by maintaining local spatial continuity. Extensive experiments on four benchmark hyperspectral datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art classification performance. Moreover, compared with existing Transformer-based models, our framework significantly reduces model complexity and computational cost, while maintaining superior performance.

title: HyIMC: A 16 TOPS/W Hybrid Analog-Digital In-Memory Computing SoC with Model Compression and Recurrent Optimization for Deep Learning-Based Speech Enhancement
authors: Wanru Mao, Guangyao Wang, Hanjie Liu, Tianshuo Bai, Jingcheng Gu, Han Zhang, Saiya Wang, Guilin Zhao, Xitong Yang, Aifei Zhang, Xiaohang Wei, Meng Wang, Biao Pan, Wang Kang
publish: IEEE Journal on Emerging and Selected Topics in Circuits and Systems
abstract: In-memory computing (IMC) presents a promising avenue for deep learning-based speech enhancement (DL-SE). However, the current state-of-the-art IMC architectures are hindered by their inability to simultaneously achieve high precision and energy efficiency, coupled with a lack of parallelism necessary for DL-SE’s distinctive temporal dependency. This paper introduces the HyIMC, which features: 1) an analog-digital hybrid IMC architecture that facilitates DL-SE algorithms; 2) a schedule controller designed to optimize recurrent dataflow in skip connections; and 3) non-key dimension shrinkage (NKDS), a model compression technique that retains superior accuracy while compressing DL-SE models. Our approach achieves 16 TOPS/W energy efficiency at system-level, implemented on a 40nm eFlash-based IMC SoC prototype. The HyIMC with NKDS compresses DL-SE model size by ∼83.3%, enhances the figure of merit by ∼1200%, and improves perceptual evaluation of speech quality score by ∼120%.

title: NIINet: Towards Real-world Nighttime Deraining via Infrared Image Guidance
authors: Yubo Wang, Kui Jiang, Jinghua Wang, Xiang Chen, Laizhong Cui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Image deraining has achieved considerable progress in recent years. However, few efforts have been focused on nighttime rain scenarios, which present several challenges as follows. 1) Nighttime scenes suffer from insufficient and uneven lighting, leading to a decline in both image quality and visibility. 2) The absence of high-quality paired datasets makes data collection time-consuming and labor-intensive. 3) RGB sensors provide limited perception to the observed nighttime scene, where crucial information may be obscured or lost. Good datasets facilitate the deraining performance. Instead of using simulated rain perturbations to create rainy samples, we propose to characterize the realistic rain distributions from nighttime rainy videos. These distributions are then projected onto rain-free nighttime scenes to generate a substantial number of rainy samples. Consequently, we constructed the novel NIRrain13k dataset, which contains 16,000 triples of images (rainy, rain-free, and infrared images) with resolutions of 1280 × 1024 and 1024 × 768, covering various scenes. Based on NIRrain13k, we devise an elaborate nighttime deraining approach guided by infrared images, termed NIINet. Specifically, NIINet explores the mutual representation of visible and infrared modalities and employs the bidirectional vision Mamba module for better feature learning. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed NIINet on nighttime rainy datasets, surpassing other representative deraining technologies. In terms of Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), NIINet outperforms NeRD-Rain, DRSformer, and FMRNet by 0.33 dB, 1.53 dB, and 2.38 dB, respectively.

title: A Deep Spatio-Temporal Graph Learning Framework for Real-Time Urban Accessibility Prediction and Facility Optimization in Smart Cities
authors: Feiyang Xu
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: Smart cities increasingly rely on data-driven intelligence to support public-service allocation, transport planning, and urban management. Traditional accessibility evaluation methods based on static GIS buffers or manual surveys fail to meet the real-time, predictive, and large-scale requirements of modern smart-city systems. This paper proposes a novel real-time urban accessibility prediction and facility optimization framework that integrates multi-source sensing data, spatio-temporal graph neural networks, and deep reinforcement learning. First, we construct a dynamic urban mobility graph by fusing IoT pedestrian sensors, mobile trajectory data, road-topology data, and transit network feeds. Second, an ST-GNN model is developed to predict future 5-30 minute walking accessibility distributions with high temporal resolution. Third, a DRL-based facility optimization module is introduced to recommend optimal locations for parks, commercial facilities, and transit stops, maximizing coverage within a ten-minute accessibility threshold. Experiments on real-world data from Songdo International City demonstrate that the proposed ST-GNN achieves 18.6 % lower MAE and 21.3 % lower RMSE than state-of-the-art baselines, while the DRL optimizer improves ten-minute life-circle coverage by up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{4 2. 5 \%}$</tex>. This work provides a generalizable algorithmic foundation for intelligent urban accessibility management and smart-city service planning.

title: Synthetic Resurrection – A Multimodal Pipeline for Expressive Voice and Avatar Generation
authors: Akshat Thakur, Mayon Rajpoot, Harminder Kaur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: This paper introduces Synthetic Resurrection, a new multimodal pipeline that generates high-fidelity synthetic voices and their associated animated avatars from sparse input data. The system meets the need for personalized digital content generation through the intersection of state-of-the-art speech synthesis with real-time avatar animation. Our pipeline incorporates cutting-edge neural vocoding, clarity- and naturalness-oriented text-to-speech synthesis, and facial landmark-based animation to produce unified audiovisual representations. The approach takes a three-stage methodology: (1) voice cloning based on few-shot learning methods to extract speaker identity from short samples, (2) natural and intelligible speech generation with low emotional variation, and (3) synchronized avatar animation through facial motion synthesis in coordination with synthesized speech patterns. Experimental results show that our system has high performance with regard to voice quality, speaker similarity, and lip-sync accuracy compared with other methods. The pipeline has low-latency real-time capabilities. Possible uses are virtual assistants, content creation, digital preservation, and accessibility technologies. This research adds a unified framework for creating expressive multimodal digital personas from small training data, achieving computational efficiency as well as output quality, and currently offering limited emotional expressiveness.

title: Decoding Histopathology: Deep Learning Advancements in Early Breast Cancer Detection
authors: Zarina Begum M, Gowthami V, Monish R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Breast cancer remains a major health challenge for women, yet recent technological advancements have significantly improved the prospects of early detection. Deep Learning approaches have emerged as highly effective in medical image analysis, providing accurate and dependable solutions for cancer classification and detection. The BreakHis dataset is used to test the latest Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architectures, including ResNet-50, MobileNet, DenseNet201, InceptionV3, NASNetLarge, InceptionResNetV2, and NASNetMobile, for the classification of histological pictures. These mod- els show remarkable success in deriving meaningful features to increase the diagnostic power. The findings indicate that the use of DL-based approaches has a lot of potential to assist healthcare professionals and help them make faster diagnoses, more accurate classifications, and achieve better outcomes when taking care of a patient.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Second Language Acquisition: An Inquiry of Ethical Implications and Creativity Decline Among Technical Students in Gujarat
authors: Barnali Chetia, Dharti Sharma, Dharna Bhatt
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: Introduced in 2022, ChatGPT became one of the world's most popular AI chatbots. Students from the Indian subcontinent have adopted this technology in educational contexts. This research examines the educational ethics of ChatGPT use in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{L 2}$</tex> classrooms of Gujarat, India. Given the developments in AI and natural language processing, ChatGPT's ability to generate human-like text makes it an asset in language teaching. Still, this study aims to address the challenges and risks of the technology, including an overdependence, “techno-ethical” concerns like ChatGPT-assisted plagiarism, and others. It also studies the impact of using ChatGPT on engineering students' learning experiences and education outcomes in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{L 2}$</tex> classrooms. This study employed an extensive literature review, and an empirical survey of 500 B. Tech students (aged 17-22) from several engineering colleges in Gandhinagar and Vadodara (Gujarat, India) to develop its conclusions. This mixed-methods approach reveals a great deal of practical insight regarding the use of ChatGPT in second language learning, particularly its challenges, advantages, and integration issues.

title: Strategic Approaches to Quality Management in 21st-Century Education Through the Integration of Digital Technologies
authors: Taufik Hidayat
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: The rapid digital transformation in education has created both opportunities and challenges, as many institutions still struggle to align technology adoption with effective quality management systems. This study investigates strategic approaches to quality management in 21st-century education through the integration of digital technologies. Using a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) guided by the PRISMA framework, a search in Scopus was conducted in August 2025. The process generated 3,038 articles, which were screened through inclusion and exclusion criteria, resulting in 296 eligible studies. Five representative articles were purposively selected for in-depth thematic analysis. The results identify five key strategies, namely developing standardized frameworks for e-learning quality assurance, revising open and distance learning agendas, implementing AI-based personalized learning, institutionalizing online quality assurance, and designing the Smart Education Index to measure digital readiness. These findings emphasize that successful quality management requires the alignment of digital infrastructure, governance, pedagogical innovation, and continuous evaluation. By synthesizing best practices and highlighting critical gaps, this study contributes to bridging the divide between quality management and digital technology research. The outcomes provide valuable insights for policymakers, institutional leaders, and practitioners to foster inclusive, ethical, and sustainable digital transformation, thereby enhancing global educational competitiveness in the 21st-century.

title: Interactive AI Training Agent for Tomato Farmers: A GenAI and LLM driven Approach to Agriculture 4.0
authors: Gangadhar S, Pramod T. C.
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Agriculture in developing regions continues to face challenges of limited access to timely, localized, and expert guidance. Traditional advisory systems often lack scalability, personalization, and contextual relevance. Advances in Artificial Intelligence (AI), particularly Generative AI (GenAI), Large Language Models (LLMs), and autonomous agents, provide opportunities for more adaptive solutions. This paper proposes an Interactive Training Agent for Tomato Farmers that combines multimodal interfaces, retrieval-augmented generation, and AI-driven modules for disease detection, soil analytics, and predictive insights. It is multimodal voice, text and image and edge deployed, which makes it reliable in rural areas and achieved 98% detection accuracy on different tomato leaf diseases. The framework belongs to Agriculture 4.0 that is supposed to improve the decision-making process of farmers, reduce crop losses, and produce tomatoes sustainably.

title: GCET 2025 Cover Page

title: AI-Based Blood Pressure Estimation Using Biosignals: A Review of Machine Learning and Deep Learning Approaches
authors: Rashmi Bhardwaj, Simran Chugwani, Nandini Sharma, Ankur Sisodia, Swati Vishnoi, Medha Khenwar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Managing the treatment and diagnosis of cardiovascular disease is derived from monitoring blood pressure over time. While cuff-type methods for patient monitoring are utilized often, they do not allow for continual monitoring in a classroom setting. Therefore, using cutting-edge technologies, including artificial intelligence utilizing AI's powerful capabilities (e.g., machine learning, deep learning), has led to exciting new methodologies for obtaining non-invasive continual BP estimation from multiple types of bio-signals, including oscillometric waveforms, photoplethysmograms, etc. This review consolidates the findings from 15 recent major papers detailing a variety of AI-driven methodologies developed to infer BP. Our summary organizes the papers into two classifications: those that involve multi-stage or multi-level Deep Learning architecture and those that rely on Classical ML-based Classifications/Regression Techniques. Additionally, the manuscript will present experimental performance innovation and comparison of the various performed results as well as indicate the obstacles that investigators presently face, while proposing future research challenges to maximize UC's practical utility in providing reliable and accurate results.

title: Golden Jackal Optimizer-Based PI Controller for Efficient MPPT in Photovoltaic Systems
authors: Said Benkaihoul, Derradji Bakria, Amar Regaz, Yıldırım Özüpak, Abdesselam Belkheir, Ahmed Memdouh Younsi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This paper proposes an enhanced Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) method for standalone photovoltaic (PV) systems by integrating a classical Perturb and Observe (P&O) algorithm with a Proportional-Integral (PI) controller whose parameters are optimized using the Golden Jackal Optimizer (GJO). The PV system includes a Boost DCDC converter regulated by the GJO-tuned PI controller to improve dynamic response and maximize power extraction under varying irradiance conditions. The performance of the proposed control strategy is evaluated through MATLAB/Simulink simulations and compared against a competing metaheuristic, the Spotted Hyena Optimizer (SHO). Results demonstrate that the GJO-based approach significantly outperforms SHO in terms of power stability, voltage regulation, and convergence speed. Particularly under fluctuating solar irradiance, the GJO exhibits superior tracking efficiency and reduced steady-state oscillations. The findings confirm that the integration of nature-inspired optimization algorithms into MPPT control frameworks offers a robust and adaptive solution for improving the efficiency and reliability of PV systems.

title: Privacy-Preserving Synthetic Data Generation for Fair and Collaborative Microfinance Analytics
authors: Uttoorwar Meher Mani, Bodapati Lakshmi Kanth Vamsi, Saraswathi S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The use of credit analytics, based on data, has become an essential part of microfinance institutions, but regulatory issues (privacy) and data fragmentation, coupled with demographic biases in the model, severely undermine the possibility of sharing the data and collaborative model building. This research paper attempts to deal with these issues by making suggestions on the application of a domain-specific privacy-sensitive synthetic data generation model in microfinance ecosystems. The methodology builds upon CopulaGAN to add reinforcements with Differentially Private Stochastic Gradient Descent (DP-SGD) and therefore has formal privacy guarantees and non-deconstructs the complex borrower-loan relationships. Conditional sampling with fairness is included to reduce bias in data generation based on gender and locality. Operationalization It is operationalized as an entirely reproducible pipeline with Apache Airflow, DVC, MLflow, and containerized deployment to establish safe collaboration without revealing raw financial records. Statistical fidelity, low disclosure risk, and higher downstream machine learning utility are shown by experimental assessments of the models compared to state-of-the-art synthetic data models. The suggested system adds a pragmatic, scaled-down solution integrating privacy safety, impartiality execution, and functional reproducibility to aiding responsible analytics and the policy creation based on evidence in microfinance settings.

title: AI Powered Smart Surveillance System For Wildlife Monitoring And Anti-Poaching Using Yolo On Raspberry Pi
authors: G. Sheeba, C. Esther Hephzibah, M. Nisha, V. Priyadharshini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Conservation of wildlife is becoming more challenging because of habitat loss, human-wild life conflict and poaching that are affecting the biodiversity and survival of the endangered species. Conventional surveillance techniques tend to be manual, time consuming and not convenient in distant locations. The research is a plan of an AI-enhanced smart surveillance system that will be used to monitor wildlife in real-time and prevent poachers. The system combines a USB camera with a Raspberry Pi with the YOLO12 (You Only Look Once) object detection model to automatically recognize animals and illegal human presence on the periphery. When it detects possible risks, it delivers an instant email notification to the forest administrators, and they can promptly take appropriate action. The effectiveness of the system was confirmed by the successful response to both wild organisms and the suspicious human presence by means of experimental deployment in simulated forest conditions. This low cost, scalable solution improves situational awareness, lessens the manual monitoring activities, and boosts anti-poaching operations by incorporating edge computing, artificial intelligence and automated alerting, to support the sustainable conservation measures of endangered species.

title: BiLSTM-Enhanced CNN for Robust Multi-Label Classification of Dental Prosthetic Devices
authors: Shruthi Vijayakumar, P. Pabitha, Menaka Vasudevan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Accurate and automatic detection of dental pathologies from radiographic images has become a critical activity in modern dentistry diagnosis and treatment planning. In this study, the proposed hybrid deep learning model Convolutional Neural Network(CNN) combined with Recurrent Neural Network(RNN) to perform multi-label classification of dental prosthesis and diseases. In this model, the pretrained MobileNetV2 backbone was used as a spatial feature extractor, coupled with a Bidirectional long short-term memory(BiLSTM) that captures contextual dependencies across sequentially augmented image sequences. The BiLSTM utilized the generated multiple augmented glimpses for each dental radiograph to aggregate feature representations in both forward and backward directions, enhancing robustness against image noise, variation, and occlusion. Experimental results of the proposed model based on Orthopantomogram(OPG) image datasets demonstrated improved accuracy and generalization compared to the traditionally used models that rely only on CNN. The proposed CNN–BiLSTM architecture provides an efficient, scalable and noise-resilient approach for automated dental prosthetics and disease detection, offering potential applications in clinical diagnostic support and digital dentistry workflows.

title: IR-PFC–HBO: An Intelligent Optimization-Driven Energy Management Framework for Renewable-Integrated DC/AC Microgrids Supporting EV Charging
authors: Jessy Pradeep Surendranath, Susitra D, Pradeep Surendranath Thomas
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The growing reliance of renewable energy sources (RES) within contemporary microgrids has presented a major issue of operational uncertainty and intermittence because of the unpredictability of wind and solar power generation. Such variations have a direct impact on the stability of power systems, energy production, and charging infrastructure of electric vehicles (EVs). In a bid to solve these problems, the proposed study suggests the best energy management and control system that is designed to be done by an Intelligent Renewable Power Flow Controller (IR-PFC) that is optimized with the help of Humming Bird Optimization (HBO) algorithm. The developed DC/AC hybrid microgrid has photovoltaic (PV)-units, wind turbines (WT), hybrid energy storage system (HESS), DC loads and integrated Energy Management System (EMS). The IR-PFC will be able to smartly balance the two-way power flow between the RES, storage appliances, loads in the system, and the EV charging stations and keep the voltage stable and avoid power imbalance caused by renewables. HBO is used to refine the control parameters of IR-PFC, improve the scheduling process and optimize the size of PV, WT and battery storage units in the dynamic environment. Such a way of integration greatly enhances the use of energy sources, eliminates fluctuations in the output of renewable sources, and guarantees the stability of EV charging behavior. The simulation studies conducted in the MATLAB/Simulink environment reveal that the IR-PFC-based framework provides more efficient load management, enhanced renewable penetration, and dynamic response and high system robustness in comparison to traditional controllers.

title: AI-Driven Pedestrian and Accident Detection with Real-Time Emergency Response and Safety System Using IoT
authors: Anayra Shankar Mahajan, P.K. Deshmukh
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Road accidents still remain among the leading causes of death all over the world particularly in places where emergency assistance is delayed or not available. In the present paper, we discuss a compact real time embedded safety framework, which is capable of early detection of pedestrians, classification of accident events and instant revelation of search using cost-effective hardware. The system provides the YOLOv3 model for real time detection of pedestrians and a custom made manually trained Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) which classifies events of accident from annotated and videos. Physical impact detection is done with the help of ADXL335 accelerometer and MQ2 gas sensor giving real time feedback. The alertess are directly sent by Fast2SMS services and SMTP services. The location of the accident is obtained through geo-location APIs using Wi-Fi rather than GPS modules. The CNN produced a classification accuracy of 99.2% and gave alerts within a period of 1.5 seconds using the Raspberry Pi 4B hardware. The practical experiments carried out in daylight achieved stable performance without the integration of the GSM or GPS circuits. The low cost and scalability of this proposed system thus is applicable to semi urban areas, in those places where this communication based on Internet is an useful option.

title: Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)

title: Voltage Profile Enhancement and Loss Mitigation via Optimal D-STATCOM Allocation in Distribution Networks
authors: Abdulhadi Haj Ahmad, Evgeniy B. Sheskin
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: This paper develops a systematic methodology for optimizing distribution network performance through strategic D-STATCOM deployment. The study formulates a multi-objective optimization framework that simultaneously addresses voltage profile enhancement and active power loss reduction in radial distribution systems. A computational intelligence technique combining Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) with Backward-Forward Sweep power flow analysis is implemented to determine optimal device locations and ratings. The IEEE 33-bus and IEEE 69-bus distribution networks are utilized as benchmark systems to evaluate the proposed strategy. Load flow analysis is conducted for both the uncompensated and compensated network conditions to assess the impact of optimal D-STATCOM allocation. The simulation results, obtained through MATLAB implementation, verify that the PSO-based approach achieves substantial improvements in the voltage profile and notable reductions in system losses. The outcomes demonstrate that the optimal integration of D-STATCOM devices can serve as a robust means for enhancing the overall efficiency of distribution networks.

title: Evaluating Generative AI Policies in Higher Education: Insights from Japan's Top Universities
authors: Mohammed Galib Hasan Abir, Katsusuke Shigeta
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: The rapid integration of Generative Artificial Intelligence tools into higher education underscores the urgent need for transparent, responsible, and adaptive institutional governance. This study examines the scope and quality of Generative AI-related policies at Japan's top 50 universities, as ranked in the 2025 QS World University Rankings. Using a 24-item analytical framework derived from established literature, we systematically analyzed publicly available institutional guidelines to assess their comprehensiveness and focus. The results reveal that 37 universities (74%) have issued Generative AI guidelines; however, deficiencies were found in the following areas: specifications of AI model types, documentation protocols for prompts and outputs, detection mechanisms, and procedures for reporting misuse. These deficiencies highlight significant gaps in institutional preparedness to manage emerging AI technologies. Overall, the findings indicate that national universities are leading the development of formal Generative AI governance frameworks, whereas public and private institutions show more fragmented approaches. The study emphasizes the need for comprehensive, transparent, and regularly updated Generative AI policies to ensure the ethical, practical, and future-ready integration of AI in higher education.

title: A CNN-RNN System for Real Time Credit Card Fraud Detection Using Three-Way Classification to Reduce False Positives /Negatives and Detect Fraud Across All Transaction Values
authors: David Maina, Merlin Kasirajan, Victor Obarafor
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: Financial institutions and consumers whose accounts are compromised suffer financial loss from credit card fraud caused by traditional fraud detection systems which rely on static, rule-based mechanisms which are limited in adaptability to evolving fraud techniques, generation of high false positives, false negative and Inadequate capacity to detect low-value fraud-transactions under £50 that are increasingly exploited for “card testing.” These limitations point out a critical need for a system that can accurately detect fraudulent transactions of any value in real time, minimise false positives and negatives, and adapt to emerging patterns. This research aims to design a real time CNN-RNN hybrid fraud detection system with three way classification to reduce false positives and negatives and detect low and high value credit card fraud.

title: Leak Localization in Pressurized Pipelines Using Distributed Acoustic Sensing
authors: Yitong Zhao, Qiang Liu, Dongyu Liu, Yong Wang, Shuai Yao, Qisong Wu
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Smart City and Information System (ICSCIS)
abstract: Distributed acoustic sensing (DAS) has emerged as a prominent technology for real-time pipeline monitoring due to its high spatial resolution and distributed sensing capability. However, leakage signals acquired by DAS are often characterized by low-frequency, non-stationary, and susceptibility to environmental noise, which complicates accurate time delay estimation (TDE) and precise leakage localization. To address these challenges, this article proposes a robust pipeline leakage localization method. First, raw DAS data are preprocessed to enhance the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) in the target frequency band. To mitigate phase randomness caused by unstable fiberpipe coupling and environmental interference, a cross-correlation of Hilbert envelopes method is employed for robust TDE. Furthermore, to improve localization stability, a multi-channel fusion strategy integrating K-means clustering for outlier removal and Least Squares fitting is introduced. Field experiments on a pressurized pipeline testbed demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a localization error of approximately 1.0 meter. This performance significantly outperforms the basic crosscorrelation (BCC) and generalized cross-correlation with phase transform (GCC-PHAT). The experimental results demonstrate its accuracy in pipeline leak location applications.

title: Renewable Energy Based Smart-Agriculture System
authors: Seepana Gowthami, Janapati Sivavara Prasad, Botta Naidu, Eti Sri Latha, Nomula Malla Redddy, Srinivasarao Boddepalli
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Effective water management is essential to sustainable farming and efficient crop production in agriculture. A solar-powered smart irrigation monitoring system that maximises water utilisation, minimises manual labour, and increases crop productivity is presented in this research. The system uses an ESP-8266 microcontroller to integrate several sensors and automated components for real-time monitoring and control. The system has soil moisture sensors to measure the amount of water in the soil, temperature and humidity sensors to monitor the climate, and a rain sensor to prevent unnecessary watering when it rains. A motion sensor (PIR) increases security because it may detect undesired movement in the field. To ensure precise water distribution, a relay-controlled water pump is activated in response to sensor readings. By combining smart sensing technology with renewable energy, this project offers farmers a scalable and cost-effective solution that promotes sustainable agriculture practices.

title: Intelligent Power Flow Management for a Photovoltaic El Abiodh Sidi Cheikh Grid: A PSO-Based Approach
authors: Ali Abderrazak Tadjeddine, Hichem Hamiani, Ridha Ilyas Bendjillali, Sofiane Mohammed Bendelhoum, Mourad Kholkhal, Salih Lachache
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: The growing integration of renewable energy sources poses critical challenges to modern power-system stability, particularly in regions with high photovoltaic (PV) penetration. This paper investigates voltage instability and excessive power losses in the El Abiodh Sidi Cheikh (ESC) grid, a 30 kV distribution network in Algeria, during peak PV generation conditions. Conventional load-flow analyses reveal a significant voltage depression to 0.83 p.u. at high injection levels (13.18 MW), highlighting the grid's vulnerability. To address this, an enhanced Particle Swarm Optimization algorithm with integrated reactive-power compensation (PSO_CMP) is proposed. The optimization simultaneously minimizes activepower losses and improves system-voltage profiles by dynamically adjusting generator outputs and compensator settings. Simulations performed in MATLAB R2024a show that the PSO_CMP method restores the minimum voltage to 0.964 p.u. and reduces active-power losses to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 0 4 ~ M W}$</tex>, achieving a 27.79 % efficiency improvement over conventional approaches. These results confirm that intelligent reactive-power management through metaheuristic optimization can substantially enhance both the reliability and efficiency of PVintegrated power systems.

title: Stuttering Detection and Classification using Self-Supervised Models for Natural and Simulated Speech Data
authors: Aahir Basu, Sameer Bisht, Nersisson Ruban
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Stuttering is a speech disorder that consists of disturbances in speech that occur involuntarily. These disturbances may be repetitions, prolongations, blocks, etc., in speech. People who stutter in their speech face social barriers and are often excluded from many aspects of everyday life. They are also excluded from using modern applications like voice assistants. Accurate automatic detection of stuttered speech is crucial for developing tools to diagnose and assist in therapy. Most of the traditional methods depend on generated acoustic features (MFCC, LPCC). This limits the potential to generalise them across a wide range of speakers. This work explores the use of Wav2Vec2, a self-supervised speech representation model, to detect stuttered speech automatically. It also explicitly compares end-to-end fine-tuning with embedding-based classifiers such as SVMs, incorporating class imbalance handling. Previous studies have focused on binary detection or overall stuttering accuracy. This work aims to classify individual stuttering types using Wav2Vec2 on benchmark datasets. The model distinguishes between fluent and stuttered segments effectively. It achieved an F1 score of 0.85 on LibriStutter and 0.75 on SEP-28k. Integration of SVM classifiers and SMOTE optimisation improved the SEP-28k F1 score to 0.84. The results show that this approach demonstrates strong performance across precision, recall, and F1-score, with particularly high recall for minority stutter classes. The results indicate the potential of self-supervised models in developing reliable and generalised stutter detection systems for real-world use.

title: Quantum Bio-Mutation Foresight Simulator (Q-BMFS): A Hybrid Quantum–Classical Framework for Proactive Mutation Analysis
authors: Mahesh Babu Chittem, Venkata Arjun Cherukuri, Avinash Reddy Cheerapareddy, Sai Manvitha Pamulapati, Pavithra Borra, Swetha Pappula, Sikhinam Nagamani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Rapid viral evolution and mutation pose continuous challenges to genomic surveillance and public health preparedness, while most existing computational approaches focus on analysing mutations only after they appear in real-world samples, limiting their ability to support early intervention. This paper presents the Quantum Bio-Mutation Foresight Simulator (Q-BMFS), a hybrid quantum–classical framework designed to explore and evaluate potential future mutation pathways before they naturally emerge. The proposed system combines quantum generative models with classical machine learning to simulate a large bio-mutation search space and assign probabilistic and risk-based interpretations to generated variants. Quantum Generative Adversarial Networks are employed to generate candidate mutation sequences, Quantum Circuit Born Machines are used to model mutation likelihood distributions, and Quantum Reservoir Computing captures temporal evolutionary behaviour, while a transformer-based classical model interprets long-range protein sequence dependencies. Experiments conducted using publicly available viral genome datasets and synthetically generated mutation samples demonstrate that Q-BMFS achieves improved predictive accuracy and more stable mutation likelihood modelling compared with classical baselines, and structural validation using RMSD analysis further indicates that the generated mutations preserve biologically plausible folding behaviour. These results suggest that quantum-enhanced foresight modelling can support proactive mutation analysis and may serve as a useful tool for future research in predictive virology and computational biology.

title: Deepfake Detection on Image and Video Using Generative Adversarial Network
authors: Vivek Kumar, Vinay Kumar Mishra, Alok Singh Sengar, Vipin Khattri, Rohit Sharma, Vineet Kumar Chauhan
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: The authenticity of digital content is facing new challenges with the widespread use of highly realistic synthetic media. This gave birth to one of the most important problems in digital forensic investigations known as deepfake. The detection of deepfakes includes images, videos, and audio that are created entirely by machine learning. This work proposes an image-based deepfake detection framework. The work integrates discriminative feature selection with contrastive representation in a GAN-based approach. Video inputs are processed through frame level decomposition. The work uses a public dataset of more than 140,000 annotated images. Data imbalance is addressed through loss reweighting. The comparative analysis shows better performance across accuracy, F1-score, and AUC metrics. The work is compared to state-of-the-art (SOTA) models like XceptionNet and MesoNet and found better even with skewed class distribution.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 7th International Communication Engineering and Cloud Computing Conference (CECCC)

title: Deep Learning Based Robust Deblurring Model for FOD Detection
authors: Amarjeet Kumar, Abhishek Dixit, Pooja Agrawal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: This work focuses on robust deep learning based foreign object debris (FOD) detection model to improve image quality in vision-based system for successful FOD detection. Here, Gaussian blur is considered to be degrading the image quality. Gaussian blur, commonly introduced through defocus or camera motion and therefore reduces important visual details. In this study four deep learning models based on Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Autoencoder (AE), MIMO-UNet, and MIMO-UNetPlus is studied and compared for their effectiveness on restoring blurred images. A synthetically large, blurred dataset of FOD was used with varying Gaussian kernel sizes to simulate real-world scenarios. The models were assessed on both single and mixed blur conditions to measure restoration accuracy and computational efficiency. The results show that while CNN achieved the highest inference speed, it struggled with moderate to high blur levels. The Autoencoder achieved moderate restoration quality but showed limitations under severe blur, whereas MIMO-UNet achieved a balance between accuracy and speed, effectively bridging toward the superior performance of MIMO-UNetPlus. MIMO-UNetPlus outperformed all other models in terms of Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), showing strong robustness and consistency. It is evident in the study that, MIMO-UNetPlus multi-scale architecture and feature refinement make it highly suitable for practical deblurring tasks in real-time FOD detection and identification.

title: Performance Benchmarking of YOLOv8s–YOLOv12s for Real-Time Vehicle Detection on WEDGE Data for Self Driving Car Application
authors: Manjit Jaiswal, Kapil Kumar Nagwanshi, Amit Kumar Chandanan, Upendra Verma
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Autonomous driving, traffic monitoring, and intelligent transportation all rely on real-time vehicle detection; however, inclement weather and complex driving situations present significant obstacles. The Weather-Enhanced Driving Environment (WEDGE) dataset, which encompasses a variety of conditions such as rain, fog, snow, and low light, is used in this study to benchmark five YOLO variants, ranging from YOLOv8s to YOLOv12s. An NVIDIA A100 GPU is employed for the experiments, which evaluate models in terms of detection accuracy (mAP@0.5), inference speed (frames per second, FPS), and computational efficiency (parameters and GFLOPS). The results indicate that YOLOv11s achieves the best overall performance by combining the fastest inference speed with the highest accuracy. While YOLOv12s provides the most balanced trade-off between accuracy and efficiency, YOLOv8s demonstrates strong accuracy at the cost of increased complexity.

title: Real-Time Facial Region Identification Via a Custom Six-Layer Convolutional Network
authors: Pan Thiri Tun, Keniya Roy, Oakkar Min, Manish Deshwal, Gaurav Aggarwal, Rishabh Dev Shukla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This paper recommends a customized sixlayer Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for detecting facial region in real-time using Vasuki Patel dataset (2,562 face and 90 non-face images). It consists of a compact model of convolution, batch normalization, average pooling, dropout, and dense layers that balance efficiency and accuracy with tradeoff. Trained for 50 iterations, it accomplished a 98% train and an 88% validation set accuracy with strong learning capability and low level of overfitting. Compared with traditional methods, it is superior under background and illuminance variation and under pose variation and is therefore suitable for applications with security, identity authentication, and automatic attendance tracking.

title: Intelligent control of Autonomous Underwater Vehicle for 3DPath Planning using Enhanced Subpopulation-Based Differential Evolution Algorithm
authors: Shubham Gupta, Vinay Kumar, Deepak Sahu, Sadanand Yadav
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: Ensuring the safe navigation of autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) in underwater relies on path planning. It is also a crucial indicator of the intelligence of AUV. In this paper, an enhanced subpopulation based differential evolution (DE) algorithm, denoted by ESDE, is designed to effectively handle 3D path planning for AUVs. In the ESDE, sub-population and multi-mutation rules are employed to enhance the performance of the classical DE. These advancements are executed to generate efficient mutant vectors at different generations of the algorithm to provide an effective trial vector in the evolutionary process of the proposed algorithm. The historical success of its parameters is used to define a setting for ESDE parameters. The results obtained by proposed ESDE algorithm is compared with six metaheuristic algorithms (MAs) on nine different test case scenarios. Results comparison of ESDE with other MAs and their analysis have verified the better convergence and accuracy of the solution of the ESDE for effective path planning.

title: Garbage Classification with Hardswish and Efficient Channel Attention: A GhostNetV2-Based Approach
authors: Devaprakash Umapathy, Baby Shamini P, Sterlin Rani Devakadacham, Jency A, Hemala R
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Classification of garbage data is essential for effective waste management and to make the environment sustainable. A lightweight yet highly accurate deep learning model is used for garbage image classification with the enhanced GhostNetV2 architecture. The Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) module is integrated into the selected blocks of GhostNetV2 which improves feature recalibration without significant computational overhead and the network focus on the most discriminative patterns. Additionally, the traditional ReLU activations are replaced with the Hardswish functions to boost non-linearity and to maintain computational efficiency. The garbage classification dataset contains ten classes, including 19,762 images, which allow for the comparing process across various types of garbage. The proposed model achieves a superior performance and experimental results show that the GhostNetV2-ECA model outperforms the baseline GhostNetV2 in terms of accuracy by achieving 96 % on the test dataset. The proposed model is lightweight in nature which makes it highly suitable for the real-world deployment on mobile and edge devices for intelligent waste management systems.

title: Face Spoofing Detection
authors: Parneeta Dhaliwal, Roshi Saxena, Faiz Ali, Shiva Kumar Reddy, Yashwanth Reddy
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: With the increasing speed of digital identity verification technology, face recognition technology has primarily been applied to biometric authentication in e-learning systems, smart attendance systems, and secure access control. Despite this, such systems are still susceptible to presentation attacks through photographs, videos, or 3D masks, enabling unauthorized individuals to evade security controls. To overcome this challenge, we introduce a real-time face spoofing detection system using YOLOv8, a cutting-edge deep learning object detection model. Our system works by way of a live webcam stream, identifying faces and labeling them real or spoofed in real time. We have trained our model on our own custom dataset of 14,600 labeled images, balanced between real and spoofed classes. The dataset was extended to improve generalization over various facial conditions and environments. Experimental results show that our model attains a validation accuracy of around 98%, successfully discriminating between real and fake face inputs. This method greatly improves the robustness of face authentication systems and provides a scalable solution for realworld applications.

title: Smart Home: Technology Enabling Autonomy for People with Physical Disabilities
authors: C Innocent Pious, R. Sivasankari, Ilakiyaa Saravanan, A. Lubna Fathima, M. Kushika, Infant Andreas
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Smart homes are evolving with IoT and AI, but many remain challenging for elderly or differently-abled users. We present a user-friendly smart home system that enables control of appliances via voice and hand gestures, managed by a Raspberry Pi with offline processing for faster, networkindependent operation. Appliance status is synchronized in real time using Firebase, with secure communication and custom Android dashboard for remote monitoring and control. Voice commands are processed with Python-based speech-to-text algorithms, while gestures are detected using OpenCV, tested with multiple users to ensure reliable recognition in diverse lighting conditions. Real-home testing with 5 0 0 voice commands and 300 gestures achieved 94% voice recognition in quiet environments, 84% with noise, and 86% gesture accuracy, with system response averaging 180 ms. Using standard components, the system integrates easily with existing smart homes, providing a secure, accessible, and interactive experience, demonstrating how AI-driven IoT can enhance convenience, accessibility, and everyday living.

title: Handwriting based Personality Assessment using Image Processing
authors: Aarush Kinhikar, Varada Khadake, Aditya Khuman, Swapnali Kurhade
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Personality is a distinct combination of an individual's experiences, values, and life goals. The proposed methodology identifies eight personality traits from seven key handwriting features such as baseline, slant angle, letter size, pen pressure, top margin, line spacing and word spacing. A dataset comprising 100 handwritten samples labeled by a professional graphologist was expanded using a semi-supervised learning technique to label 12,825 samples from the IAM Handwriting Database. A multioutput random forest classifier achieved prediction accuracies between 95% and 98%. The proposed system demonstrates an automated approach for personality prediction using handwriting analysis, offering a scalable and interpretable solution for behavioral analysis.

title: Real-Time Fabric Defect Detection Using a Lightweight YOLOv8 Model on Edge Devices
authors: Abhiram S, Heena Gupta, James C.K.
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The detection of defects in fabric is a critical process for maintaining quality standards and reducing economic losses in the textile industry. Traditional inspection methods, which rely on human operators, are often slow, inconsistent, and susceptible to error. This research introduces an innovative solution that harnesses Edge AI and deep learning to facilitate real-time, on-site defect detection. We developed a highly efficient and lightweight model based on the YOLOv8 architecture, specifically tailored for deployment on resource-constrained edge devices like NVIDIA Jetson Nano or Raspberry Pi. Through a process of comprehensive literature analysis and domain expertise, a compact, high-precision model was trained on diverse fabric defect datasets. To ensure optimal performance on edge hardware, we employed advanced optimization techniques like quantization and pruning. The primary offering of the work are threefold: the making of a streamlined YOLOv8-based model for fabric defect detection, a comparative analysis of various edge inference strategies, and a proposed system architecture for real-time embedded deployment. This study effectively demonstrates the practical application of advanced AI to solve longstanding challenges in textile quality control. Future efforts will be directed towards extensive real-world operational testing and exploring localized Model Training with Federated Learning enhancement.

title: Unified Multimodal Knowledge Integration for A Secure Framework for Cross-Modal and Scalable Retrieval
authors: Premanand Ghadekar, Shraddha Randhave, Srushti Veer, Pranav Gole, Samarth Suntnure, Shubham Pawar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The proposed model presents a unified multimodal knowledge integration framework that exploits contrastive learning to naturally project text, image, audio and video data to the common embedding space and align–sense across modalities. The embedded mentions are indexed in a single vector store and organized as a knowledge graph using node2vec, thus allowing to learn contextual semantic relationships between the entity embeddings, and performing graph–based retrieval. Low–latency cross–modal querying is realised, supporting retrieval–augmented generation with complementary multimodal context. In empirical studies, the proposed model outperformed state–of–the–art methods (LLaVA/, ImageBind, CLIP–BART) by approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{15 \%}$</tex> relative gain in key retrieval metrics like Mean Average Precision (MAP), and Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (NDCG). It also enables secure, scalable deployment in both distributed and edge environments with the consideration of data privacy and computational efficiency.

title: Developing of Predictive Analytic System to Improve the Efficiency of Hydroelectric Power Plants
authors: Marina K. Altukhova, Maria A. Lyulina, Daniil M. Dorokhin, Vladimir S. Chudny, Elena A. Ivanova, Irina E. Ryndina
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: Problem of synthesizing a predictive analytic system for non-stationary processes in complex dynamic systems using hydroelectric power plants (HPP) as an example is being solved in the paper. Comparative analysis of predictive forecasting methods is performed, and the specific application features of the singular value decomposition method that influence the quality of the analysis of non-stationary processes in HPPs have been identified. Singular value analysis method is proposed that allows complex dynamic processes in hydroelectric power plants to be decomposed into elementary components that have both predictable characteristic and simple mathematical description. A methodology for predictive analysis and forecasting of non-stationary processes based on a modified singular value decomposition framework has been developed to improve the efficiency of HPPs and optimize their operation.

title: Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)

title: Turning Data into Action: Predictive Insights for Employee Satisfaction and Retention
authors: Veeramani R, V. Arunkumar
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Organizational effectiveness and retention of employees are impacted by employee satisfaction and turnover, which continue to be two of the most difficult challenges faced by organizations. The current study was designed to better understand employees’ satisfaction and turnover predictors through the use of machine learning. Data from 674 employees was used, analyzing predictors using a Random Forest Regressor (to predict satisfaction with an MSE of 0.0077) and a Random Forest Classifier (to predict turnover with 0.63 accuracy). Through feature importance and SHAP analysis, it was found that work-related factors (number of projects worked on and number of hours worked per month), combined with performance evaluations, were the most predictive of engagement outcome variables. Traditional factors (salary, department, promotions, etc.) were shown to have minimal impact on predictive accuracy. A subgroup analysis found a subgroup of mid-tenured employees earning a mid-range salary to be the group with the highest risk for attrition. The study’s results provide a data-driven framework for HR analytics that will provide actionable insight into employee retention strategies and optimizing workloads to incorporate the human element in the decision-making processes necessary to meet the needs of Industry 5.0.

title: Generative AI Models for Simulating Brain Lesion Impacts
authors: Nitin Rakesh, Monali Gulhane, Sana Raj, Marion Antony, Garima Shukla, Aarthi Sivasankaran
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Brain damage, which can happen from a number of things like an accident, a stroke, or a neurodegenerative disease, can significantly change how neurones work. To improve the accuracy of diagnosis, treatment planning, and therapy approaches, it is important to be able to accurately simulate and predict the effects of brain injuries. Statistical models and simple computer methods are often used in traditional ways to try to figure out how brain injuries affect people. These models don't fully show, though, the complicated, nonlinear links between brain damage and how it affects function. This article looks into how generative artificial intelligence (AI) models, especially deep learning and generative adversarial networks (GANs), might be able to simulate the effects of brain injuries. We look into how well these models can create accurate brain lesion simulations and guess how those simulations will work based on factors like the size, location, and type of the lesion. By using a lot of brain scans and clinical data to train generative AI models, these models learn to find complex patterns in the structure and functional connections of the brain. The training results from AI models are compared with real-life patient data to see how well they can predict cognition, movement, and sensory problems. We also talk about how these models could be combined with scanning tools to give real-time simulations of how lesions affect patients so that they can get better care. Using generative AI, this study shows a new way to understand brain tumours. This could lead to big improvements in diagnosing, treating, and recovering from brain injuries.

title: AI-Powered Automated Evaluation of Handwritten Student Answers Using OCR and BERT-Based Semantic Similarity
authors: Aswanalal, Amallesh B, Aldrin Roger S
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Marking of handwritten answer sheet is also time consuming, inconsistent and subject to human bias. The AI Answer Sheet Evaluator solves these issues by presenting a new model of automated grading with Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Natural Language Processing (NLP). The system uses OCR programs like Tesseract and TrOCR to turn the handwritten student answers into a digital text. Semantic similarity models such as Sentence-BERT and GPT are then used to evaluate extracted text by comparing answers of students with answers given by the instructor using the models. Judging is done according to accuracy, relevancy, completeness and coverage of important points. This model is unlike traditional systems that only provide binary scoring since it supports partial marking whereby fair distribution of mark can be made to partly correct answers. With OCR and NLP, the system is effective in handling big amounts of scripts in addition to being transparent and objective. This smart and scalable solution will help bring about a transformation in the field of academic assessment in schools, universities, and extensive test-taking facilities.

title: Virtual Assistant for Blind Using Gen AI
authors: Sandhya N, K S Md Sayeed, G R Durga Prasad, K Deekshith Reddy, Gadi Sameer Ahmed
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: People who are visually impaired encounter a variety of obstacles in their daily lives. Most of the difficulties arrive from the lack of visual information and the challenges of the visual world. The present work is a Virtual Assistant for the Blind using Generative AI. This method analyzes the photo and speaks the explanation out loud and instantly. The user can get instant commentary on the pictures he has taken. The framework uses Google Text-to-Speech (gTTS) [14] to generate clear audio, Google's Gemini API [5] to generate accurate scene descriptions, and speech recognition for user inquiries. The study looks at the combination of multimodal AI tools to create a useful assistive system that increases accessibility for people who have lost their vision.

title: Finite Time Convergence Analysis of Policy Gradient Learning in Stochastic Stackelberg Games
authors: Pranoy Das, Muhammad A. Zaman, Vijay Gupta
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: Designing and analyzing learning algorithms for general sum stochastic Stackelberg games remains challenging. We propose an inner-outer loop policy gradient-based learning algorithm for this problem and analyze its finite time convergence. Our analysis does not assume a time scale separation between the leader and the follower or a special structure on the game such as being Markov potential or zero-sum.

title: Real-Time Driver Drowsiness and Distraction Detection Using Google ML Kit on Android Platforms
authors: Shilpy Sharma, Mridula Singh, Rashmi Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Driver fatigue and distraction-related traffic accidents have become a major issue, resulting in a high percentage of road fatalities globally. Currently available in-car systems developed to monitor driver attention typically depend on costly hardware, advanced sensors, or integrated vehicle systems and hence are not available to the masses. In the present research, we present a low-cost and scalable smartphone-based approach that uses computer vision methods for detecting driver drowsiness, distraction, and inattention in real time. The system we introduce here uses the camera of the smartphone to quantify facial landmarks and body posture and classify driver states based on eye closure analysis, head position, and facial orientation analysis. The application utilizes OpenCV for facial and posture analysis without employing any other hardware. Our approach differs from traditional methods based on costly sensors or inboard vehicle modules in that it makes driver monitoring democratic using a widely available device Initial tests conducted in test environments revealed encouraging results, with an estimated 91% detection accuracy for closed eyes, 87% for head-turning activity detection, and a general detection accuracy of nearly 89% for drowsiness or distraction conditions. In addition to using the pre-trained dataset from Google ML Kit, which is composed of millions of different facial images, the system was tested in 30 real-world trials with 15 individuals in age groups ranging from 20 to 50 years old in various driving and lighting scenarios. Cross-device adaptability was validated by testing on several devices.

title: Strategic of Wind Farms Re-Powering the Market-Driven Assessment Across Integrated Energy Systems
authors: Ravi Kumar Mugadhanam, R Sujitha, Sumitra Sureliya, Ananda Kumar Haldorai, Puneet, Takveer Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: With the expanding uptake of combined energy power systems, there is mounting attention to renewable energy sources and storage technologies. This research centers on addressing the issues posed by the variability and instability inherent in wind energy, which hamper its market competitiveness and operability reliability. In an effort to resolve these challenges, a new analytical approach was established to examine maturity of wind markets and determine viability of repowering old wind farms at local, national, and international levels. On the basis of systematic criteria, the research detects unevenness in the progression of markets within various U.S. states, indicating premature aging in a number of wind farms, particularly in Texas, where lifespans in operation have been below the estimated twenty-year threshold. These results underscore the pressing necessity of strategic repowering measures and offer insight into the wider development of wind energy infrastructure within consolidated energy systems.

title: Seamless Transition Between Grid-Following and Grid-Forming Modes for Grid-Tied Virtual Synchronous Generator with LCL Filter
authors: Ahmed Lakhdar Kouzou, Ali Sharida, Sertac Bayhan, Haitham Abu-Rub
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: The ongoing transition from conventional grids to power-electronic-dominated grids (PEDGs) has significantly increased the complexity of the grid's dynamic characteristics, particularly in terms of the short-circuit ratio (SCR) and inertia requirements. In order to adapt to the dynamic grid conditions, seamless transition of distributed energy resources (DERs) between grid-forming (GFM) and grid-following (GFL) modes becomes essential. However, traditional mode-transition approaches typically rely on multi-stage procedures using cascaded PI-based control loops designed for single-variable regulation, such as controlling either current or voltage at one point of the LCL filter. Such methods are unable to coordinate multiple control objectives, leading to power instability, transient fluctuations, and current surges during mode transitions. This paper proposes a fast and direct seamless transition technique between GFM and GFL modes for DERs equipped with LCL filters without the need for intermediate stages. The GFM operation is governed by a virtual synchronous generator (VSG) control scheme. The proposed method employs a model predictive controller (MPC) that acts as the lowest-level control layer with two selective cost functions. One function regulates active and reactive power during GFL operation, while the other one ensures output voltage regulation during GFM operation. The effectiveness of the proposed approach has been demonstrated through real-time simulation under both high and low SCR conditions.

title: AI-Powered Predictive Maintenance in Industrial Robotics using Vibration Signal Analysis
authors: Usha Bala Varanasi, P. Janaki Ramal, Deepigha.V, B.Sundara Pandiyan, Helina Rajini Suresh, Maheswari.S
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: This study introduces an intelligent predictive maintenance system based on AI and robotic industrial vision on vibration signal processing that provides a more reliable and reliable operating system and reduces downtime. The suggested system combines the IoT-based data collection, feature extraction with Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) and fault classification with the help of the Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model. Significant improvement is shown by experimental evaluation with the accuracy of 97.4 and fault detection rate of 98.1 and also reduction of maintenance costs by 44.2 percent as compared to that of traditional systems. The data on vibration was evaluated to determine the trends in terms of bearing faults, shaft imbalance, and wear in gears, which are efficient in the prediction of failures prior to the catastrophic breakdowns. The findings substantiate the hypothesis that incorporating the deep learning approach with the vibration-based sensors increase diagnostic accuracy and the speed of decisions in industries. This outline will create a predictive maintenance scale and efficient model that will facilitate the industry 4.0 and smart factory development.

title: Classification of Diabetic Retinopathy Using Inception V3 Model
authors: Shilpashree P S, Rajath N, Yuvaraj M Pujari, Rahul S P, Shreyas S, H Parimala
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Damage to retinal blood vessels leads to diabetic retinopathy (DR), a serious complication of diabetes that can impair vision. Early detection is crucial to prevent irreversible damage to the retina, as timely intervention can slow down the progression of the disease. Traditional diagnostic methods often fall short and are prone to errors. This study uses inception v3 model, to classify DR into five stages: No DR, Mild, Moderate, Severe, and Proliferative. The system can analyze medical images efficiently, ensuring accurate and swift disease classification. This approach aims to enhance early detection, improve diagnostic precision, and support ophthalmologists in treatment planning, ultimately leading to better patient outcomes. Experimental results demonstrate the proposed model’s high efficacy, achieving an accuracy of 97.7%, precision of 96.2%, recall of 94.7%, and an F1-score of 95.7% in DR detection. These findings highlight the potential of CNN-based DL models in medical diagnostics, facilitating early identification and assisting in treatment planning for individuals at risk of vision impairment. While this approach enhances existing screening techniques, it also holds promise for improving healthcare outcomes for diabetic patients.

title: The Legislative Combating of Deepfake using Artificial Intelligence Applications: A Comparative Study
authors: Tariq A. Kameel, Nadine M. Soboh, Abdulla A. Alnuaimi, Humaid N. Alblooshi, Mohammed A. Alblooshi, Sultan I. Alblooshi
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)
abstract: This research examines of Deep fake crime, which has become one of the most dangerous and threatening crimes emerging in the digital world as a result of employing advanced Artificial Intelligence algorithms. This study elucidates the serious damages and destruction that have led to the loss of trust in everything that can be heard or seen due to the treacherous manipulation of clips, audio recordings, videos, and images. Therefore, this took a negative toll on societal, economic, political, security, and international levels, given that it infringes upon fundamental human rights that are protected by constitutional and international laws. Furthermore, this study reviews the position of comparative legislation on these newly emerging crimes and examines the adequacy of such legislation in confronting this type of crime. It was found that the existing legislation is insufficient to address this type of crime due to its novelty. As a result, it is necessary to enact strict legislation or introduce amendments to existing legislation with specific provisions criminalizing the use of deep fakes for illegal activities, while imposing deterrent penalties on violators

title: GCET 2025 Preface
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)

title: Design and Evaluating a Real-Time Legal Verdict Prediction System Using Transformer Models with Explainable AI
authors: Aditi Singh, Km Meenakshi, Kirti Dilip Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The rising amount of legal records is a major challenge facing legal practitioners, hence there is need to design smart tools to enable quick analysis of cases and sound judgments. The paper outlines the architecture, deployment and critical testing of a new real-time Application Programming Interface designed to forecast real-life legal decision-making verdicts (Accepted, Rejected) studying only brief case descriptions. To accomplish this goal of classification, the system is based on the use of a Legal-BERT model which is a powerful transformer architecture carefully pre-trained on a large set of domain-specific corpora. The second crucial aspect of this study will be the inclusion of an explainability module that will allow seeing how the model makes its choices using LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations) in a novel way of explaining the working mechanism of the model by explicitly pointing to the important terms in the source material with the help of its inner workings. Fast API framework in the Python language was prudently implemented in the provision of the API hence its performance is robust and operational latency is minimal. Testing was defined with manually curated 29 English legal cases summarization, obtaining an overall accuracy of 85.31 percent, and both precision and recall is at 85.71 percent on class Accepted that means the F1-score is 85.71 percent. These findings verify the practicability of the methodology, though this performance on a small dataset is noted as a proof-of-concept rather than a generalizable benchmark.

title: Network-Aware Multi-Objective Dynamic Allocation of Virtual Machines in Cloud Data Centers
authors: Sara Shar Faiz Alshehri, Khaoula Hamdi
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)
abstract: In order to improve cloud processes and services while fulfilling both consumer and service provider requirements, many recent studies have focused on Virtual Machine (VM) placement in cloud data centers one of the key processes in provisioning Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) in cloud environments. This research proposes solutions to improve cloud data center productivity by minimizing the traffic generated by the placement process of VMs in Physical Machines (PMs). This process is critical in provisioning services from cloud providers. We introduce Multi-objective Networkaware Dynamic Virtual Machine Placement algorithms that assign VMs to PMs in a network-aware manner. In addition to traffic minimization, other objectives are also considered in the proposed algorithms, such as maximizing resource utilization, achieving load balancing between PMs, and minimizing energy consumption. Optimization algorithms are used to support the cloud administrator in making placement decisions. The proposed algorithms are evaluated through simulated instances of a cloud data center environment to demonstrate their performance concerning the considered objectives. The solution domain covers single cloud data centers only.

title: Robust Multi-Face Tracking and Recognition in Unconstrained Videos Using Deepsort and Arcface Embeddings
authors: Evany Anne M, Brindha M, Sivakumaran N, A. KalaiSelvan
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Real-time face tracking in real-world videos is still a difficult problem in computer vision, especially for surveillance and identity verification. Traditional approaches will fail under varying lighting, partial face occlusion, or typical head poses. The below is a joint system that integrates deep facial embeddings and appearance-based multi-object tracking in an attempt to provide identity persistence between frames. The method uses ArcFace for producing discriminative facial embeddings and DeepSORT for temporal detection association based on motion and appearance features. Every frame is initially processed with RetinaFace for localizing accurately, and embeddings are compared against a reference database based on cosine similarity. Intersection over Union (IoU) is also employed for spatial association for enhancing robustness in overlapping detection. Experiments on actual video sequences show excellent identity retention and high accuracy tracking. The modular architecture, together with GPU acceleration, is guaranteed to support real-time processing and render the framework appropriate for deployment in intelligent surveillance systems.

title: Blockchain - Enabled Human Resource Management Systems for Transparent Recruitment and Payroll Optimization
authors: Vijayakumar M, S. Vijayakanthan, Jeyaprabha Jeyaprabha, Sahithi Arangi, Ramchandra D. Patil, Krishnamoorthi M B
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: This study presents a blockchain-based human resources management system that enhances productivity, safety, and transparency through the use of smart contracts decoupled ledger technology and permanent verification of credentials. With the help of smart contracts, wage calculation and payment are automatic, and candidates’ credentials are verified and stored on the blockchain using cryptography. To create the system simulation, 100 employee records were created on Ethereum as a test database and compared to standard HRM methods.
