With the integration of blockchain, verification of credential, calculation of wages, and storing legal documents is mistake-proof. It also automates wage payments in 1.5 seconds for each transaction and shortens verification from 48-72 hours to 3.2 seconds. Scalability testing shows it works well with larger employee records as well. The framework developed shows how a Blockchain can aid human resource management by making operations more transparent, reducing mistakes, enhancing trust, and creating a safer and auditable HR environment.

title: ESDNet: Epilepsy Seizure Detection Using a Novel Hybrid Deep Sequential Network from EEG Signals
authors: Albania Suting, Ansuman Kumar, Anindya Halder
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Epilepsy is a neurological disorder typically characterized by recurrent, sudden seizures. Seizures in patients with epilepsy often result in involuntary movements and fainting, which can lead to severe injuries or even death in certain situations. Manual seizure detection depends on long-duration electroencephalogram (EEG) signal inspection, which is computationally and labor intensive and necessitates expert involvement. Therefore, early and automated epilepsy detection from EEG signals is an important task for system biologists and neurologists to ensure the safety of epileptic patients throughout a seizure and prevent possible dangers as earliest. A novel Hybrid Deep Sequential Network for Epilepsy Seizure Detection from EEG Signals (ESDNet) is proposed in this article. It utilizes the benefits of One-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (1D CNN) and Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit (BiGRU) networks as it combines the 1D CNN with BiGRU deep sequential architecture. Here, three layers of 1D CNN and two layers of BiGRU are used for extracting features from EEG sequences and a dense layer is used as a classifier. The performance of the proposed ESDNet model is validated on two EEG datasets viz., UCI Epileptic Seizure Dataset and CHBMIT Scalp EEG Dataset with respect to precision, recall, F1-score measure, accuracy, confidence interval and its result is compared with existing state-of-the-art methods. In the UCI Epileptic Seizure Dataset and CHB-MIT Scalp EEG Dataset, the proposed ESDNet model achieved the accuracy rate of 99.74% and 93.20% respectively compare to existing methods.

title: Dual-Attention Optimized EfficientNet-B0 for the Diabetic Retinopathy Classification
authors: Km Meenakshi, Manav Mittal, Rakhi Chauhan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: This research presents an optimized Diabetic retinopathy classification deep learning solution using the EfficientNet-B0 model. The consideration for vision loss in persons affected by diabetes is diabetic retinopathy. The proposed method gives excellent metrics on APTOS 2019 datasets with respectively 95.27% accuracy, 93.12% precision, 91.05% recall and 92.07% F1 score. The validation set was strongly generalized with Accuracy 93.04%, precision 91.15%, recall 89.71% F1 score 90.4. These significant transformations were achieved through the renovation of architecture and augmentations strategies, and optimization strategies. The stability of the model is established through vast performance, estimation through confusion matrices, ROC curves, AUC and a range of 0.85-0.95 on respective classes, both on training and validation data. The provided EfficientNet-B0 implementation suggests an effective, highly precise framework for the Diabetic Retinopathy classification.

title: A Tree-Based Routing Protocol with Collaborative Optimization of Multi-Hop Neighborhood and Global Position Information
authors: Jiagang Dai, Guanli Yi, Shangjun Liu, Gege Ju
publish: 2025 7th International Communication Engineering and Cloud Computing Conference (CECCC)
abstract: To address the challenges of suboptimal paths and inadequate adaptability to dynamic topologies in tree-based routing for wireless ad hoc networks, this paper proposes a tree routing optimization method that integrates multi-hop neighborhood and global positional information. The method, based on a tree topology, maintains node connectivity states and geographical location information through periodic HELLO message broadcasts. A multi-level priority-based routing decision mechanism is designed to ensure accurate packet forwarding within one-hop or two-hop neighborhoods. If the destination node is beyond the local scope, the packet is directed to the corresponding branch according to the tree-based successor node list. Conversely, global location information is utilized to select the optimal next-hop node based on Euclidean distance. NS3 simulation experiments demonstrate that, compared to basic tree routing, shortcut tree routing, and two-hop neighbor table tree routing, the proposed algorithm reduces the average hop count by 7.2% to 31.4%, decreases end-to-end delay by over 27.5%, and improves the packet delivery ratio by more than 14.4%. It exhibits excellent adaptability to dynamic network topologies.

title: Program Schedule
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)

title: Tweets to Traits: An Ensemble Approach
authors: Gayatri Gamini, Achyuth Kasyap P, Janviba Dharmendrasinh Gohil, Utkarsh Mishra, Avinash Chandra Pandey
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Artificial intelligence has transformed social media sites like Twitter, making them useful tools for smart personality prediction. While transformer models like BERT and RoBERTa provide solid results, they tend to be computationally expensive, slower to train and suffer from imbalanced data. To mitigate these limitations, this paper proposes a hybrid ensemble strategy that leverages powerful contextual representations from transformers alongside the efficiency and speed of machine learning methods. Not only does this approach reduce computation time and memory complexity, but it also enhances robustness and scalability. Experimental results on Twitter data prove that the suggested model performs better than previous methods, with 90% accuracy and 91% precision, illustrating its efficiency in predicting personality characteristics based on social media content.

title: On the Potential for Low Frequency Resonance in Power Networks with Long Cable Lines
authors: Anton A. Kuznetsov, Elena A. Ivanova, Maria A. Lyulina, Ekaterina N. Kartamysheva, Ekaterina V. Pechenina
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: Conditions involving hazardous resonant phenomena developing at frequencies near the second harmonic (100 Hz) in power systems incorporating medium-and high-voltage cable lines of considerable length are being analyzed in this paper. Analytical estimates are obtained for the lowest resonant frequencies and network input impedances at these points, depending on the short-circuit power at the cable line connection node, as well as the lengths and parameters of the cable lines. Results of numerical modeling of transients during transformer switching in such power systems are presented, illustrating the risks associated with the resulting levels of harmonic disturbances.

title: Analyzing Construction Investment Dynamics via Multi-Criteria Decision-Making and Gradient Boosting Methods
authors: Charles Ubagaram, Venkat Garikipati, Rohith Reddy Mandala, Narsing Rao Dyavani, Bhagath Singh Jayaprakasam, R. Pushpakumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Construction investments involve intricacies that need advanced frameworks in project evaluation and prioritization against economic spillovers, limits on resources, and strategic objectives for regional development. Multi-Criteria Decision-Making (MCDM) with Gradient Boosting for Optimal Project Selection, with improved accuracy of prediction, avoiding spillover effects, and risk factors. This is the suggested method, which combines gradient boosting in predictive analysis and MCDM in dynamic project appraisal. The complete decision-making can be carried out through risk sensitivity and economic spillover analysis. Efficiency increased to 96.9%, prediction accuracy increased to 95.7%, and quick response ratio increased to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 6 \%}$</tex>, showing better performance compared to earlier approaches. This methodology improves strategic resource allocation, reduces risk, and promotes long-term growth by overcoming gaps in previous methodologies.

title: An Emerging Wireless Technology With Progressive Approach Visible Light Communication
authors: Nidhi Bhukar, Surender K. Grewal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Visible light communication technology is an emerging futuristic approach wireless technology in which transmission of data is based on the utilization of visible part of (380 nm-750 nm) electromagnetic spectrum. VLC is considered as an optimal alternative to RF communication in order to satisfy the users need of lighting and communication. This paper represents the introduction of VLC with its applications, evolution from origin to till now and basic components of VLC including transmitters and their types followed by different types of channels and receiver. Lastly different filtering stages and some of research concerns in VLC has been discussed. This review of VLC technology provides users a very clear comprehensible, sorted understanding of existing research issues and emphasises potential concerns and prospects to guide further research in this area.

title: Fuzzy Decision-Making Framework of Glaucoma Diagnosis and Interpretation
authors: Jaydeep Kishore, Shweta Goyal, B.P. Joshi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Glaucoma is a deteriorating and chronic disease of the optic nerve and has been one of the major causes of blindness to the world. The intricacy of interactions between structural, functional, and clinical risk factors, borderline and undecided cases still makes the task of early and accurate diagnosis hard. This paper illustrates a fuzzy based decision-making system to detect and analyse glaucoma by the use of multimodal input variables through image-based variables (e.g., cup-to-disc ratio, neuroretina rim variables), visual arena variables Diagnostic evaluation is typically based on visual field measurements, of mean deviation (MD), pattern standard deviation (PSD), and visual field index (VFI) and clinical evidence in the form of intraocular pressure (IOP), central corneal thickness, patient age and family history. It operates on the basis of preprocessing and segmentation modules to obtain credible features and then fuzzy inference system based on linguistic variables, membership functions and rule-based reasoning to obtain interpretable risk ratings. This model was put to test using 200 cases of patients (70 with glaucoma, 50 suspected, and 80 normal) and sensitivity of 92: 89: 90, specificity of 88: 85: 86.5 and accuracy of 92: 89: 86.5 respectively. The reactions of clinicians indicated that they were more confident in the explanations of the system, which are governed by rules, especially with borderline cases. The results demonstrate that the uncertainty may be successfully addressed in fuzzy decision making, fuzzy decision making may provide interpretative results and may aid in the early diagnosis of glaucoma. The approach shows opportunities both with deep learning models in combination with it, and at scale in primary care or resource limited settings.

title: Achieving Optimal Sample Complexity for a Broader Class of Signals in Sparse Phase Retrieval
authors: Mengchu Xu, Yuxuan Zhang, Jian Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: Sparse phase retrieval aims to recover a k-sparse signal from m phaseless measurements. While the theoretically optimal sample complexity for successful recovery is Ω(k log n), existing algorithms can only achieve this bound for signals with specific structural assumptions, leading to a notable gap between theory and practice. To bridge this gap, we introduce an efficient initialization algorithm, termed generalized Exponential Spectral Pursuit (gESP). We prove that gESP can significantly expand the family of signals that are guaranteed to be recovered with the optimal sample complexity, thereby extending the scope of theoretical optimality to a much broader class of signals. Extensive simulations validate our theoretical findings and demonstrate that gESP consistently outperforms the state-of-the-art methods across diverse signal types.

title: Activation Space Clustering for Transparent AI-Driven Cybersecurity Against Deferred Backdoor Attacks
authors: Yashwant Aditya, Dr. Priyank Jain
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Adversarial robustness remains a critical challenge in AI-driven cybersecurity systems, particularly against sophisticated threats like Deferred Backdoor Activation (DBA) that exhibit long-term stealth behavior. This paper presents a novel transparent AI framework that integrates activation space clustering with built-in interpretability to enhance cybersecurity defense mechanisms. The proposed methodology introduces the K-Goodall Overlap Prism Medoid (K-GOPM) clustering algorithm to identify and mitigate poisoned samples in neural network activation spaces, effectively preventing DBA before trigger activation. A Built-in Interpretable Deep Few-Shot-Adaptive Lame Recurrent Neural Network (BIDFS-ALRNN) is developed, incorporating Stable and Explainable Attention (SEAT) mechanisms to provide immediate explanations without post-hoc interpretability tools. The framework employs Concentrated Vieta’s Differential Privacy (CVDP) for model privacy protection, Cubic Edwards-377 Curve Cryptography (CEdCC) for data protection, and Leaky Half Angle Bucket Algorithm (LHABA) for query rate limiting to prevent adversarial probing. Experimental validation on the NSL-KDD dataset demonstrates superior performance with 99.32% accuracy and 0.89 adversarial robustness. The proposed approach significantly advances transparent AI methodologies by combining theoretical foundations in activation space analysis with practical cybersecurity applications, contributing to both AI interpretability research and robust defense strategies against evolving adversarial threats in networked systems.

title: A Low-Complexity and Efficient Spatial Resampling Technique for Range–Angle Decoupling With 4-D High-Resolution MIMO FMCW Radar System
authors: Jiayu Zhang, Yuchen Li, Zhiwei Zhang, Changzhan Gu, Junfa Mao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: Multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) frequency-modulated continuous wave (FMCW) radar systems have been widely adopted in advanced sensing applications due to affording superior resolution and accuracy. However, when both a wide modulation bandwidth and a large aperture are incorporated, which are recognized as essential features of the high-resolution radar systems, performance degradation is often introduced due to the range-angle coupling (RAC) effect. This phenomenon impairs resolution in both range and angular domains, with especially severe degradation occurring at off-boresight angles. Although various compensation techniques have been proposed, most of them involve substantial complexity and have limited practical applicability. To address these challenges, a low-complexity and efficient spatial resampling technique (SRT) is proposed, in which the discrete frequency components of the intermediate frequency (IF) signal are utilized to perform resampling in the virtual elements. For each frequency bin, the resampling process is conducted based on the corresponding wavelength, instead of the physical wavelength. By aligning the spatial sampling grid with the wavelength of each frequency across the entire modulation bandwidth, the inconsistency in phase differences across virtual array elements is effectively eliminated. Subsequent 2-D angle-range fast Fourier transforms (FFTs) are applied, ensuring each target to be concentrated within its correct cell and effectively mitigating resolution degradation. In contrast to the conventional techniques that typically introduce higher orders of complexity, the proposed technique computational burden is constituted almost entirely by highly efficient FFT operations, ensuring that an exceptionally low complexity is maintained, with an improvement of 100 times over existing methods. Experiments have been conducted to validate the proposed technique. The technique provides a practical solution for high-resolution, low-cost MIMO FMCW radar by addressing a fundamental wave-propagation issue inherent to wideband large-aperture radar systems.

title: Quantitative Analysis of Complex Fault Trees Using Importance Sampling with Monte Carlo Tree Search
authors: Mohamed Y. Nassar, Yunfei Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Reliability
abstract: Fault tree analysis is widely used in reliability engineering. However, it is a challenging task to be performed accurately and fast with limited computational resources when the fault tree becomes large and the top event probability becomes small. A novel algorithm is introduced that uses importance sampling with Monte Carlo tree search to tackle this challenge, with focus on static fault trees. Importance sampling uses proposal probabilities to bias the sampling of the basic events' states towards those the combination of which would cause the top event to happen, and Monte Carlo tree search is used to iteratively improve the proposal probabilities of the basic events' states. A case study is performed to analyze the accuracy, and the computational efficiency measured by the runtime and the memory usage of the new algorithm. Well established deterministic and stochastic algorithms are used as benchmarks, and a wide range of fault trees are used: ranging from simple to complex fault trees, and fault trees with both high and low top event probabilities. The new algorithm is shown to be significantly faster (on average one order of magnitude) than the other algorithms for fault trees that are complex (with more than one million minimal cut sets) and with low top event probabilities (less than 0.01), as well as having similar performance for other classes of fault trees while maintaining low error.

title: Near-Field and Far-Field Sub-Terahertz Beam Steering Using Electrically Controlled Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface for 6G Applications
authors: Abhishek Chauhan, Amit K. Singh, Akhilesh Kumar, Mohd Farman Ali
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: This work presents the design and numerical validation of a sub-terahertz (sub-THz) reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) capable of beam steering in both the near-field and far-field regions. The RIS is specifically designed to enable efficient operation across both regimes without requiring significant modifications to its physical structure. The proposed RIS employs an H-shaped unit cell with a centrally placed graphene strip, operating at 0.27 terahertz (THz) and a periodicity of approximately <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\lambda $</tex-math> </inline-formula>/2 (free-space wavelength), enabling maximum reflection with a phase difference of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$180^{\circ } \pm 10^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula> between two graphene chemical potential (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu _{c}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) states: <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</small> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu _{c} =1.0$</tex-math> </inline-formula> eV and <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</small> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu _{c} =0.01$</tex-math> </inline-formula> eV. The RIS is excited in near-field by a center-fed pyramidal horn antenna (HA) to achieve a broadside gain of 23.1 dBi and beam steering up to ±60°, with a scan loss of 4.8 dB. Moreover, in the far-field regime, plane-wave excitation demonstrates beam steering capability up to ±40°, with a scan loss of 4.6 dB, as verified numerically. An equivalent circuit model (ECM) based on transmission-line techniques is developed to further validate the full-wave simulation results. The simulated outcomes show close agreement with the numerical calculations. These results indicate that the proposed graphene-based RIS is a promising candidate for sixth-generation (6G) systems, beam steering, imaging, and sub-THz wireless communication applications.

title: Event Triggered-Based $H_{\infty }$ Optimal Tracking Control and Application to Fused Magnesia Furnace System
authors: Qiuxia Qu, Shaodong Zhang, Yinlei Wen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Considering complex uncertain nonlinear systems with input constraints, unknown disturbances, this study proposes an <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula> optimal tracking control scheme employing adaptive dynamic programming integrated with an event-triggered (ET) mechanism. Firstly, a Neural Network (NN)-based identifier is constructed to approximate the unknown parameters. Secondly, by constructing an augmented system, the robust optimal tracking problem is reformulated as a zero-sum differential game between the controller and disturbance inputs. Subsequently, utilizing a novel critic-actor-disturbance (CAD) NN architecture and concurrent learning algorithm, a novel ET-based <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula> optimal control law is derived through approximately solving of the Hamilton-Jacobi-Isaacs equation. This control law and the CAD NNs weights are updated only when predefined ET conditions are satisfied. Theoretical analysis, using the Lyapunov method, demonstrates the uniformly ultimately boundedness of the closed-loop system while guaranteeing the avoidance of Zeno behavior. Finally, the effectiveness in relieving the communication load and improving convergence speed of the proposed approach is validated through simulation analysis. Furthermore, this ET-based <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula> optimal tracking control strategy is successfully applied to the electrode current control system in a Fused Magnesia Furnace, showcasing its significant potential for industrial applications.

title: Enhancing the Stability of STATCOM Topology Under Three-Phase Short Circuit Fault Using Fuzzy Logic Controller
authors: Ahmed Mokhtari, Ameur Fethi Aimer, Cherif Mokhtar, Mohamed Amara
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Electronics and their Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Flexible AC Transmission Systems also called FACTS systems are used to enhance efficiency and stability of electric power network. In this aim, Static Synchronous Compensator (STATCOM) is used in this paper in both open loop and closed loop topology. Indeed, a fuzzy logic controller is associated with STATCOM in order to enhance the stability of the whole network and stabilize the performances under threephase short-circuit fault. The robustness of this system is also investigated under line parameter variation through simulation results.

title: I5CPS 2026 Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)

title: Application of Deep Learning Models for Prediction of Physical Layer Security of NOMA-Enabled Overlay Cognitive Hybrid Satellite-Terrestrial Networks With Adaptive Relaying
authors: Abdul Hadi K K, A V Babu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: In this paper, we examine the secrecy outage probabilities (SOPs) experienced by both primary user (PU) as well as secondary user (SU) in Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA)-enabled overlay cognitive hybrid satellite terrestrial networks (NOMA- OCHSTNs) with adaptive relaying in the presence of multiple non-colluding eavesdroppers. Extensive simulation results are described to verify that adaptive relaying enhances the SOP performance compared to fixed relaying methodology. We also present two frameworks, i.e., deep neural network and generative adversarial networks (GAN), for predicting the SOPs with high level of accuracy and reduced inference time, compared to Monte-Carlo simulations-based approach.

title: Indian Sign Language Interpretation for the Banking Sector Using CBAM-Enhanced YOLOv8
authors: Karthikeyan G, Naveen Solomon J J, Iyshwarya Rathi K, Yogameena B
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Particularly in service settings like banks, Indian Sign Language (ISL) is essential for facilitating communication between individuals with speech and hearing impairments. However, background clutter, uneven lighting, multiple signers, and motion variations continue to make real-time ISL recognition difficult. In order to improve spatial focus and feature extraction in complex scenes, this work presents a real-time ISL-to-text and speech translation system based on You Only Look Once Version 8 (YOLOv8) and enhanced with the Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM). To guarantee practical training, a custom dataset of 679 annotated photos covering seven banking gestures was gathered in actual banking environments. According to experimental results, the CBAM-integrated YOLOv8 outperforms the baseline YOLOv8 and current systems like MudraNet, achieving a mean average precision of 86.54% and a precision of 87.35%. The suggested model shows great promise for real-time deployment in banking environments to support accessible customer service due to its improved accuracy and low latency.

title: Temporal Dynamics Analysis for Cognitive vs Physical Stress Differentiation Using Multi-Modal Wearable Sensors
authors: Sulav Dahal, Bipul Bhattarai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Wearable sensors integrated into Internet of Things (IoT) healthcare systems offer unprecedented opportunities for continuous stress monitoring in smart life applications. However, distinguishing between cognitive stress and physical exertion remains a significant challenge in wearable-based health monitoring. This study presents a comprehensive temporal dynamics analysis framework for differentiating cognitive stress from physical exercise using multi-modal physiological signals from wearable devices. We analyzed data from 41 participants across three distinct protocols: cognitive stress induction, aerobic exercise, and anaerobic exercise. Our approach extracts temporal features capturing onset, peak, recovery patterns, and trend dynamics from heart rate (HR) and electrodermal activity (EDA) signals. Results demonstrate that temporal features account for 36.74% of the top 30 most important features, with trend slope being the most discriminative feature. Statistical analysis reveals highly significant differences (p < 1e-08) in temporal patterns between cognitive stress and physical exercise. The proposed multi-modal fusion approach achieves 86.67% classification accuracy using ensemble methods, with perfect discrimination of cognitive stress (100% recall). Leave-One-Participant-Out cross-validation demonstrates 68.24% accuracy, indicating robust generalization to new individuals. Key findings indicate that cognitive stress exhibits faster onset (3.6% vs 18.6% session duration), earlier peak times (34.9% vs 66.0%), and better recovery patterns compared to physical exercise. These temporal dynamics insights provide a foundation for developing intelligent stress monitoring systems in smart healthcare and IoT-enabled wellness applications, enabling real-time differentiation between psychological and physiological stress responses.

title: Assessing the Readiness of Augmented Reality for Industrial Assembly: A Deployment Study Comparing Immersive and Non-Immersive Solutions
authors: Xinyi Tu, Benedikt Hartmann, Per Ola Kristensson, John Liu, Thomas Bohné, Sławomir K. Tadeja
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Integrating augmented reality (AR) into industrial assembly processes has great potential, yet most studies use simplified lab tasks and rarely benchmark AR against commercial systems, limiting their industrial relevance. To address this gap, we present a comparative study of immersive AR and non-immersive touchscreen-based assembly support systems on a manufacturing shop floor, with 16 participants assembling two industry-grade assets of different complexity. The results reveal that while AR's immersive capabilities excelled in spatial guidance for complex tasks, the simplicity and reliability of the touchscreen interface proved more effective for simple assemblies. We derive three design implications for deployment practice.

title: Debiased Driver State Recognition via Irrelevant Context and Social Bias Removal for Vision-Language Model in Reproducing Kernel Hilbert Spaces
authors: Siyuan Li, Xinde Li, Chuanfei Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: A vision-based driver monitoring system (DMS) is essential for ensuring driving safety. Recent research emphasizes the development of vision-language model (VLM)-based approaches to build DMS, leveraging the powerful alignment capabilities of visual and language embeddings. While existing methods for monitoring driver states have shown promising results, there remains a discrepancy between the advanced techniques and real-world applications. First, the existing VLM-based methods might ignore the impact of the irrelevant visual contexts surrounding the driver, resulting in the irrelevant context bias. The human-centric cues of driver would be captured insufficiently. Second, the social bias of VLM-based driver state recognition methods should be further explored, which leads to biased model outputs against specific social groups. In this paper, we propose a novel debiased driver state recognition (DDSR) via irrelevant context and social bias removal for VLM in reproducing kernel Hilbert spaces (RKHSs). The visual and textual embeddings are first mapped in RKHSs, which has the universal approximation properties to reduce the complexity of debiasing procedure. Then, the debiasing procedure can be cast as an optimization problem. It obtains a solution to map the embeddings in the debiased spaces, which reduces the irrelevant context and social bias of embeddings. Extensive experiments are conducted on two public benchmarks, where the superiority of DDSR is demonstrated compared with the state-of-the-art methods. The superior performance of DDSR to recognize dangerous states are 85.93% and 83.45% in terms of accuracy and F1 score, which outperforms the state-of-the-art methods by 5.20% and 4.32%.

title: A Blockchain-Assisted Holographic Counterparts for Secure Consumer Electronics in Healthcare 4.0
authors: Ashish Kumar, Kakali Chatterjee, Ashish Singh, Abhinav Kumar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Consumer Healthcare Devices (CHD) in Healthcare 4.0 (HC 4.0) increasingly generate continuous physiological data that are transformed into 3-dimensional holographic visualizations for remote monitoring, diagnosis, and clinical decision support. However, existing IoMT and blockchain (BC)-based healthcare systems protect data storage and access but do not verify the integrity, freshness, or provenance of holographic patient representations, leaving such visualizations vulnerable to spoofing, replay, and slice-level tampering. This paper proposes a Blockchain-Assisted Holographic Counterpart (BAHC) framework that cryptographically binds wearable devices to holographic updates using PUF-derived Holographic Authentication Tokens (HAT), enforces slice-level integrity through a Merkle-Hologram-Commitment Tree (Merkle-HC Tree), and anchors updates on a permissioned Proof-of-Authority (PoA) BC. Privacy-preserving access control and verification are achieved using Ciphertext-Policy Attribute-Based Encryption (CP-ABE) and Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs). The framework is evaluated on a controlled experimental testbed emulating 500 concurrent patient streams using independent public physiological datasets and public MRI volumes for synthetic hologram generation, measuring end-to-end latency, anomaly detection performance, rendering efficiency, and blockchain throughput under up to 100 validators. Experimental results show a 68.6% reduction in holographic rendering latency, a 34% reduction in diagnostic latency, a relative 27% improvement in anomaly detection performance, and sustained throughput close to 500 transactions per second, demonstrating that BAHC provides a scalable and trustworthy foundation for secure holographic monitoring in HC 4.0 systems.

title: HydraGuard-ID: A Hybrid Ensemble Framework with Stratified Feature Selection for High-Accuracy DDoS Attack Detection
authors: L Sankar Ganesh, P Mohan Kumar
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Distributed Denial-of-Service attacks continue to threaten heterogeneous IoT network infrastructure over the world. In response to these attacks’ complexity, traditional detection methods fail to adapt evolving threats. In this paper, we present HydraGuard-ID, a hybrid ensemble framework that identifies DDoS attacks with high precision by utilizing three core components. First, STRATA-FS, a stratified feature selection algorithm that reduces the feature space by 65.5% while preserving discriminative power across cross-validation folds. Second, HydraBoost constructs an out-of-fold ensemble of six diverse machine learning models with adaptive SMOTE balancing to handle class imbalance without information leakage. Third, Aegis-Elastic, an ElasticNet-based meta-learner that combines base predictions with original features and provides interpretable coefficient analysis. Experimental evaluation on the CIC-DDoS2019 dataset shows HydraGuard-ID achieves 96.87% accuracy, 96.81% weighted F1-score, and a 0.9945 micro-averaged AUC-ROC, outperforming the strongest single model baseline by 2.75%. The framework demonstrates excellent probability calibration (ECE = 0.0082) and efficient inference (2 – 5 ms per sample) suitable for real-time deployment, demonstrating strong and practical performance for explainable DDoS detection in real-world scenarios. The evaluation focuses on BENIGN and three DDoS attack variants (DNS, LDAP, MSSQL) from CIC-DDoS2019, representing realistic experimental setting.

title: Combining Particle Swarm Optimization with the Modified StOMP Algorithm for Sparse Channel Estimation in GFB-OFDM Waveform Systems
authors: Bohan Xia, Yu Xin, Jian Hua, Rui Xue
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Generalized filter bank orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (GFB-OFDM) waveform is a promising 6G waveform for integrated satellite–terrestrial communications owing to its flexible subcarrier configuration and low out-of-band emissions, but its multi-subband structure introduces inter-subband interference that degrades channel estimation performance. This paper proposes a sparse channel estimation algorithm for GFB-OFDM that combines stagewise orthogonal matching pursuit (StOMP) with a particle swarm optimization based group search (PSO-StOMP). The method exploits StOMP for fast support detection while using PSO with a dynamically updated soft threshold to refine the dominant tap set in the multi-subband structure. Simulation results demonstrate that PSO-StOMP has better performance than some conventional and mainstream algorithms in the GFB-OFDM system.

title: The Impact of the Spatial Variability of the Seafloor on Mid-Frequency Reverberation During the Target and Reverberation Experiment 2013
authors: Brian T. Hefner, Dajun Tang, Jie Yang
publish: IEEE Journal of Oceanic Engineering
abstract: The goal of the Target and Reverberation Experiment in 2013 was to make contemporaneous mid-frequency (2–10 kHz) transmission loss (TL) and reverberation measurements with extensive environmental characterization such that any data/model comparisons would be highly constrained. In a previous article, it was shown that the spatial variability of the sediment had a significant impact on TL, with narrow bands of soft sediments leading to an increase in overall bottom loss. The goal of the present manuscript is to examine the impact on reverberation by this spatial variability and by the changing oceanographic conditions at the site. Reverberation at the site is modeled using a coupled mode model which accounts for the range-dependent variations in the sediment types. Using scattering parameters derived from measurements, the mud sediments are shown to be the source of the range-dependent fluctuations observed in broadband reverberation measurements. Volume heterogeneity is found to be the dominant scattering mechanism for mid-frequency reverberation at this site. The range-dependent reverberation fluctuations were found in a previous article to decrease in amplitude after the passage of a storm. Prior to the storm, a cold, bottom layer was present while after the storm, the water column was well-mixed and the layer was largely absent. The cold bottom layer is shown to lead to both trapping of sound near the seafloor and an increase in the angles of the seafloor-incident sound. This leads to an increase in the amplitudes of the reverberation fluctuations.

title: Integration of CSI and Camera Image for Indoor Wireless Localization
authors: Yoshihiko Tsuchiya, Norisato Suga, Kazunori Uruma, Masaya Fujisawa
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Research in integrated sensing and communications is advancing toward next-generation systems beyond 5G and 6G, with significant progress in highly accurate indoor localization methods, particularly those leveraging channel state information (CSI) and deep learning (DL) techniques. However, most existing approaches assume access points equipped with a large number of antennas, which limits their applicability in current communication systems that typically use fewer antennas. Furthermore, DL-based localization methods often require extensive training datasets, posing a significant challenge in terms of the cost of generating training samples. This study addresses these challenges by proposing a camera-assisted localization framework designed to reduce both the number of required antennas and the cost associated with generating training samples. The proposed method integrates object detection from camera images to identify users among multiple detected objects and combines the estimated user coordinates from images with CSI data to achieve precise localization. This approach achieves high localization accuracy with a limited number of antennas and training samples. Furthermore, to minimize the effort required to acquire labeled data, the framework incorporates a semi-supervised learning strategy that uses a small set of labeled data together with a larger set of unlabeled data. This method enhances localization accuracy while reducing the cost of training data acquisition. Numerical simulations demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior localization accuracy with fewer antennas and labeled data compared with conventional methods that rely solely on CSI.

title: Modeling and Simulation of Hysteron Dynamics in HfO₂-Based Memristive Devices
authors: E. Miranda, H. García, G. Vinuesa, J. Suñé, F. Campabadal, M.B. González, H. Castán, S. Dueñas
publish: IEEE Electron Device Letters
abstract: Published studies on memristive devices primarily focus on the conventional current-voltage major hysteresis loop, often overlooking the switching dynamics that emerge under controlled amplitude excitation. The resulting major and minor loops, often referred to as hysterons, provide a more stringent benchmark for model validation, while also offering deeper insight into the key physical mechanisms governing resistive switching. In this work, hysteron behavior in HfO₂-based memristive devices is analyzed within the framework of the Dynamic Memdiode Model (DMM). Hysterons are particularly relevant for neuromorphic computing, as they encode synaptic weights. Both experimental and modeling results indicate that the switching dynamics are predominantly governed by the applied voltage, whereas thermal effects associated with dissipation in the conductive filament appear to play a secondary role or remain implicit within the proposed description. Overall, these findings demonstrate that the DMM provides a robust and effective framework for the analysis and compact modeling of memristive devices. An updated LTspice implementation of the DMM is provided.

title: Recycling Flux-Leakage: A Novel Design Strategy for High Torque Density Permanent Magnet Motors
authors: Zexin Jia, Qian Chen, Gaohong Xu, Wei Tian, Zhengmeng Liu, Guohai Liu, Nicola Bianchi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Conventionally considered as detrimental to torque density, flux-leakage contains components that can improve output capability. Due to the nonlinear permeability of the core material, flux-leakage flowing into the bridge is more beneficial than that flowing into the air. The useful component is defined as utilized flux-leakage (UFL), with the remainder termed nonutilized flux-leakage (NUFL). Building on this distinction, a novel motor design strategy named recycling flux-leakage is proposed. It is divided into three key steps: 1) inlet control; 2) path selection; and 3) outlet guidance. The inlet control step ensures that all flux-leakage enters the bridge. The path selection step minimizes the flux-leakage losses when passing through the bridge and maximizes the proportion of UFL. The outlet guidance step aims to guide partial UFL into the air-gap, converting it into a unique effective flux defined as the continuous pole flux (CPF). The proposed design strategy is applied to a conventional spoke-type motor. The resulting improved rotor features elements such as square barriers and L-shaped bridges. Through simulation and experimental verification, the proposed rotor can simultaneously improve PM utilization and torque density.

title: A Semantic-Aware Integrated A$^*$ and Artificial Potential Field Path Planning Framework
authors: Wei Zhou, Zhouyingmiao Chen
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: This letter addresses the challenge of reliable path planning for mobile robots navigating complex, semantically constrained environments. We propose a systematically integrated path planning framework that combines the A<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^*$</tex-math></inline-formula> algorithm with the artificial potential field (APF) method. Planning efficiency is improved through an adaptive cost function and an Environment-Aware Adaptive Robot Motion Block (EA-RMB) strategy, while navigation safety is reinforced by incorporating high-level semantic constraints, such as prohibited zones, via cost penalization. Furthermore, the repulsive component of the APF is integrated into the heuristic method of the proposed algorithm, enabling the planner to proactively avoid zones with dense obstacles during the global search process. Extensive simulations and experiments demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly improves planning efficiency while maintaining highly competitive path quality. Furthermore, it rigorously enforces semantic constraints, ensuring safe and reliable navigation in complex environments.

title: Real- Time Monitoring and Visualization of Mine IoT Sensor Data Using LabView
authors: Swapnil Kumar, Prashant Pandey, Ravi Prakash Tripathi, Himanshu Katiyar, Adhinav Pal, Padmaja Tripathi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This paper presents an IoT-based safety monitoring system for miners, designed to measure critical environmental and physiological parameters such as temperature, humidity, pressure, $\mathrm{CH}_{4}, \mathrm{CO}, \mathrm{CO}_{2}$, NH<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>, smoke, and miner’s heart rate using the ESP32 platform. AQI is measured based on different sensor data and alert is generated based on threshold value. The system also incorporates video surveillance to enhance situational awareness in case of alert. Sensor data and video feeds are transmitted to the ThingSpeak cloud platform for real-time monitoring and further data processing. Subsequently, the data is integrated into LabVIEW, which provides live dashboards and triggers alerts when predefined safety thresholds are exceeded. Combining sensor inputs with video data, the system significantly improves the accuracy, efficiency, and speed of detecting hazardous conditions. Data collected at ThingSpeak is further utilized to develop machine learning model. Different machine learning models such as KNN, CNN and LMBP-ANN are analyzed and compared for performance metric. The proposed setup can be further integrated with machine learning based model makes it proactive in terms of situational alertness in hazardous mining operations.

title: ML-Powered Handwriting Analysis for Early Alzheimer's Identification
authors: R. Raja Kumar, Kuppala MadhuSudhan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The neurodegenerative condition known as Alzheimer's disease is progressive and affects behavior, memory, and cognitive abilities. This disease is global health challenge led to various socio-economic challenges in world. People who receive an early diagnosis have better results and the disease's course is slowed down. Using Machine Learning Algorithms, we can detect early signs of cognitive decline through handwriting patterns related to Alzheimer's Disease. For early Alzheimer's disease (AD) identification, the system gathers samples from raw Hand writing data from persons, extracts features such as stroke pressure, spacing, and curvature. To enhance prediction accuracy at an early stage, Machine Learning (ML) models like Random Forest, Decision Tree, Linear Regression, and XGBoost are used to early disease prediction from extracted Hand writing samples. This advanced approach for early Alzheimer's disease (AD) detection is discreet, simple, potentially helping medical professionals in identifying early intervention strategies of disease diagnosis, suitable for remote areas and cost-effective. Finally, experimental results prove that Random Forest and XGBoost are gives highest accuracy than other algorithms like Decision Tree, Gradient Boosting, KNN and SVR.

title: A Framework for Analyzing Financial Market Risks Using Multi Model Machine Learning Approach and Efficient Financial Indicators
authors: Tanisha Salil Mishra, Vijay Shankar Sharma, Atul Srivastava, Sandeep Kumar Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This invention focuses on a system that integrates machine learning models with financial indicators to analyze volatility in market prices and trends and help user and businesses take better financial decisions based on the results. This model makes use of historical as well as real time financial dataset to find the most accurate insights and predictions. The proposed invention, collects yahoo finance dataset from Kaggle, preprocesses and analyzes it to extract features like opening price, closing price, stock returns and number of stocks sold and bought on that particular day. It uses financial indicators like RSI, SMA, Momentum indicator to analyze trends and price volatility to extract insights. These insights are used to make predictions whether a stock should be sold or bought and when the market is reaching in a risky position. This is done by integrating supervised machine learning models to detect market patterns. The system also gives pictorial visualizations on the calculated results for better understanding.

title: An Ensemble Learning Approach for Medical Insurance Fraud Detection
authors: Rasanarayan Chaurasiya, Kirti Jain
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: This paper aims to detect medical insurance fraud and analyze the impact of internal factors on healthcare fraud, which has become a significant challenge causing billions of dollars in annual financial losses for medical insurance providers. Traditional rule-based fraud detection methods often struggle to adapt to the dynamic and complex nature of fraudulent claims. To overcome this limitation, this research proposes a Medical Insurance Fraud Detection framework based on Ensemble Learning, which combines Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, and Logistic Regression within a stacking architecture. The model includes preprocessing steps such as class balancing with SMOTE, categorical encoding, and normalization to enhance its robustness. Experimental results on healthcare fraud detection datasets show that the ensemble approach significantly outperforms individual base learners in terms of accuracy, recall, F1-score, and PR-AUC, leading to more effective detection of minority fraud cases. The proposed framework not only provides fraud risk scores but also supports feature importance analysis, making it suitable for practical implementation in insurance companies. This work can also be expanded for future research into fraud categories involving demographic features.

title: Efficient Detection of Tomato Leaf Diseases Using Lightweight CNN and Transfer Learning
authors: Anshika Rani, Bam Bahadur Sinha
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Numerous leaf diseases frequently afflict tomato plants, which can lower their output and quality. A hybrid deep learning system was created to facilitate the early and precise detection of various illnesses. This system classifies tomato leaf illnesses into many categories by combining a proprietary Sequential Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) with a pretrained DenseNet121 model. Images of both healthy and sick tomato leaves were taken from a dataset. The pictures were preprocessed using augmentation methods, including flipping and rotation, as well as scaling and normalization. TensorFlow's validation tools were used to eliminate photos that were corrupted or unintelligible. The DenseNet121 model was modified for the classification problem in order to implement transfer learning. A lightweight Sequential CNN was added to improve the accuracy and reduce false predictions. The proposed model achieved a classification accuracy of 98.74% on the test dataset. Diseases such as Early Blight, Bacterial Spot, Leaf Mold, and healthy conditions were successfully identified.

title: Mitigating Power Loss in a Power Array Using Progressive Module Shift Approach Under Partial Shading Condition
authors: B Karthick, A Kavitha, M Senthil Raja, C KalaiVani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) system's operational efficiency is significantly reduced by partial shading (PS), which results in significant energy losses. This study presents a modified Progressive Module Shift (PMS) algorithm aimed at mitigating the impact of partial shading by optimizing photovoltaic array configuration while maintaining electrical connectivity. The researchers tested the algorithm by performing single-diode simulations in MATLAB with actual irradiance patterns which they used to study a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(9 \times 9)$</tex> array under four different shading conditions. Key performance indices were analyzed to evaluate its effectiveness. The PMS algorithm demonstrates superior performance compared to both Total-Cross-Tied (TCT) and Sudoku (SDK) configurations by achieving a GMPP value of 23650 Watts together with a power loss of 0.33 % and a fill factor of 56.23 and an execution ratio of 0.90.

title: Grayscale Image-based Deep Learning Model for Efficient and Scalable Malware Detection
authors: Asimkiran Dandapat, Bhaskar Mondal
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Malware generally implies a significant threat of information security regarding to jeopardize user data, privacy, and assets. Therefore, early detection is vital to prevent seri-ous consequences. Moreover, the diverse array of carriers and technologies that have created a disconnect between training scenarios and real-world detection methods. This illustration actually emphasizes this gap. Because the identification of new malware variants mostly hinders threat responses. Therefore, researchers are prone to re-think to solve such effects. Image-based malware classification could be an authentic option to detect new and evolving malware variants. In this work an attempt has been attributed introducing an innovative deep learning-based detection method that classifies malware image in the form of grayscale. In this study, the MalImg dataset has been taken upon and evaluated a model using key performing metrics to achieve remarkable accuracy compared to existing benchmarks. Further, it also establishes a robust system for real-time malware classification paving the way for effective visual detection. Additionally, it suggests that computers can autonomously identify malware threats that mark a potential shift enhancing security measures for complex digital landscape.

title: A Survey on Inference Engines for Large Language Models: Perspectives on Optimization and Efficiency
authors: Sihyeong Park, Sungryeol Jeon, Chaelyn Lee, Seokhun Jeon, Byung-Soo Kim, Jemin Lee
publish: ACM Transactions on Intelligent Systems and Technology
abstract: None

title: My Journey in AI: Inspired by a Nobel Laureate and an Unusual Fish
authors: Qiang Yang
publish: Communications of the ACM
abstract: None

title: GraphSAGE-based Detection of Wormhole and Sinkhole Attacks in IoT Networks with Smarter Simulation
authors: Rinki Kaur, Surendra Kumar Patel
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) networks are increasingly targeted by advanced security threats such as wormhole and sinkhole attacks. This paper presents an enhanced graph-based machine learning approach, utilizing GraphSAGE (Graph Sample and Aggregate), to detect these attacks in large-scale IoT environments. We simulate smarter, more dynamic attack scenarios and incorporate realistic node features, including battery temperature, energy levels, and packet delays. A deeper GraphSAGE architecture with hyperparameter optimization is trained on large graphs (500+ nodes), achieving high detection accuracy. The model is saved and deployable for real-time security monitoring.

title: Unified Food Tracking and Smart Assistance: Enhancing Personalized Nutrition with LLM Integration
authors: Abhijeet Mohapatra, Aditya Kumar Jha, Parthsarthi Dutt, Kavita Jaiswal
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: A centralized system that along with monitoring your food consumption, also provides you with intelligent consultations and advice is a crucial need in the times of digital medicine. This paper reflects the idea and execution of a complete end-to-end and seamless food diary and chatbot system where users can track their multiple nutrient consumption by inculcating to it their meals, and interact with a smart chatbot which can use these information and with the help of AI, provide appropriate advice. This application is developed with a modern web development stack and is very dynamic, begineer-friendly and scalable for self nutritional tracking. Including Biomistral's large language model (LLM) also optimizes user experience and engage context-full conversations. The paper addresses the system design, implementation, evaluation and servey of relevant works and technologies in this field.

title: DVM-Net: A Deep Learning Integrated ViT and MLP Framework for High-Precision Renal Carcinoma Classification
authors: Prasanna P, J. Visumathi
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Data Science and Intelligent Network Computing (ICDSINC)
abstract: Recognized as one of the prevalent kidney cancers, renal carcinoma detection requires precise staging and classification for effective treatment planning and diagnosis. This paper presents an AI-driven classification model called “DVM-Net” – Deep Learning framework leveraged with MLP and transformer models. The proposed architecture accurately classifies renal carcinoma from multi-modal data like clinical records or medical imaging. For improving the subtle patterns in cancer progression, MLP is used for feature extraction along with Vision Transformer (ViT) for global attention-based learning. Further, transformer-based cross-attention mechanism is used for merging the imaging and tabular features. Finally, classification is performed using the fully connected SoftMax layer. Upon evaluation on dataset TCGA-KIRC, the proposed approach outperformed with superior classification accuracy (90 %), sensitivity (85 %), and specificity (85 %). This novel hybrid AI approach outperformed baseline approaches like ResNet50, MUF-Net making it an interpretable, and adaptable renal carcinoma classification model that assists radiologists and oncologists in making effective decision making and treatment planning.

title: Analysis of the Quid Pro Quo mechanism with Correlated Players
authors: J. Doncel, A. Cerezo, A. Santos, A. Fernández Anta
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: We address the problem of allocating a finite set of heterogeneous resources among multiple players with individual preferences. Each player assigns a valuation to every resource, and its utility is defined as the sum of the valuations of the resources it receives. The goal is to design a mechanism that simultaneously (i) maximizes the total social welfare, and (ii) guarantees fairness in terms of both the proportion of resources and the utilities obtained by the players. Existing work on fair division and Quid Pro Quo (QPQ) mechanisms either focuses on welfare maximization without fairness guarantees or ensures fairness without providing structural or uniqueness results for the optimal allocation rule; our work closes this gap. We study the two-player QPQ setting. Using an analytical characterization of the optimal allocation rule, we prove that, for any prescribed sharing of utilities and resources and any joint density of player preferences, the boundary separating the allocations of the two players must be linear. Under independence of the players’ preferences, we further establish existence and uniqueness of the optimal linear rule. We then show that the identity rule (a linear rule with slope one and zero intercept) is always a critical point of the welfare-maximization problem and identify conditions under which it satisfies both fairness constraints. Numerical experiments suggest that the theoretical findings extend to more complex situations. The main results of this work provide theoretical foundations for designing fair and efficient resource-allocation rules in practical systems where users compete for limited shared resources.

title: HumanoidMamba: Generalized Mamba-Based Policy Learning with Next-Action Prediction for Humanoid Locomotion
authors: Yi Zhou, Jianbin Qiu, Wei Zhang, Fenglei Ni
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive and Developmental Systems
abstract: In various human-centered unstructured environments, humanoid robots tend to exhibit superior adaptability and operational performance relative to other types of robots. Existing approaches to whole-body control in humanoid robots can be primarily composed of model-based and learning-based methods. While model-based methods rely heavily on manual design, current state-of-the-art (SOTA) learning-based methods employ Transformers as backbone networks, often leading to suboptimal computational efficiency. Inspired by the demonstrated superiority of Mamba over Transformers in large language models (LLMs) and computer vision domains, HumanoidMamba is proposed as a framework that leverages Mamba as the core backbone of the policy network to generate humanoid locomotion. Mamba, serving as the backbone, improves computational efficiency compared to Transformers while maintaining the capacity of the model for content-based reasoning. In addition, analogous to next-token prediction in LLMs, a modality-aligned prediction approach is introduced for generating the next action in the policy network, referred to as next-action prediction. The modality-aligned next-action prediction approach establishes a paradigm that not only enhances the understanding of temporal information by the policy but also facilitates extension to handle trajectories with missing modalities. Experimental results substantiate that the proposed HumanoidMamba consistently generates humanoid locomotion characterized by exceptional stability and robustness. Beyond surpassing existing SOTA methods across multiple performance metrics, HumanoidMamba further unveils remarkable generalization capability by adapting to velocity commands that are absent from the expert demonstration trajectories.

title: A Lightweight Curriculum and Contrastive Learning Framework for Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction
authors: Hu Yuan, Yan Kang, Yingmei Tang, Ke Zhao, Yansheng Xu
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Protein—protein interaction (PPI) prediction is essential for understanding cellular functions and enabling applications in drug development and disease research. PPI networks exhibit multi-scale structural and semantic heterogeneity and introduce representational bias under topological imbalances, leading to insufficient exploitation of subgraph-level semantic complexity. Moreover, many existing approaches rely heavily on external data during modeling, resulting in high computational costs for large-scale inferences. In this paper, we propose JCCLPPI, a joint curriculum- and contrastive-learning framework for lightweight PPI prediction. First, we implement a PPI network-structure encoding module designed to mitigate topological bias and learn biologically interpretable representations without relying on handcrafted features or external annotations. Next, we propose a motif-based curriculum learning module that incrementally introduces training samples according to their structural-semantic complexity, thereby enhancing model robustness to long-tail distributions and structural heterogeneity. Finally, our approach incorporates two graph neural network-based modules during PPI inference to perform local structural modeling and global context encoding, facilitating multi-scale feature extraction. Experiments conducted on two widely used human PPI benchmark datasets, SHS27k and SHS148k, demonstrate that JCCLPPI improves model generalization. It achieves an approximate 4% increase in micro-F1 score compared to state-of-the-art methods, while also improving computational efficiency by reducing memory consumption by 76% and inference time by 35%. Furthermore, JCCLPPI provides a scalable basis for therapeutic target prioritization and early-stage drug discovery.

title: 5G-AKA-HPQC: Hybrid Post-Quantum Cryptography Protocol for Quantum-Resilient 5G Primary Authentication with Forward Secrecy
authors: Yongho Ko, I Wayan Adi Juliawan Pawana, Hoseok Kwon, Seonghan Shin, Jongkil Kim, Joonsang Baek, Ilsun You
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: 5G serves as a catalyst for transformative digital innovation by enabling convergence with various services in our daily lives. The success of this paradigm shift undeniably hinges on robust security measures, with primary authentication— securing access to the 5G network—being paramount. Two protocols, 5G Authentication and Key Agreement (5G-AKA) and the Extensible Authentication Protocol for Authentication and Key Agreement Prime (EAP-AKA’), have been standardized for this purpose, with the former designed for 3rd Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) devices and the latter for non-3GPP devices. However, recent studies have exposed vulnerabilities in the 5G-AKA protocol, rendering it susceptible to security breaches, including linkability attacks. Furthermore, the advent of quantum computing poses significant quantum threats, underscoring the urgent need for the adoption of quantum-resistant cryptographic mechanisms. Although post-quantum cryptography (PQC) is being standardized, the lack of real-world deployment limits its proven robustness. In contrast, conventional cryptographic schemes have demonstrated reliability over decades of practical application. To address this gap, the Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) has initiated the standardization of hybrid PQC algorithms (HPQC), combining classical and quantum-resistant techniques. Consequently, ensuring forward secrecy and resilience to quantum threats in the 5G-AKA protocol is critical. To address these security challenges, we propose the 5G-AKA-HPQC protocol. Our protocol is designed to maintain compatibility with existing standards while enhancing security by combining keys negotiated via the Elliptic Curve Integrated Encryption Scheme (ECIES) with those derived from a PQC-Key Encapsulation Mechanism (KEM). To rigorously and comprehensively validate the security of 5G-AKA-HPQC, we employ formal verification tools such as SVO Logic and ProVerif. The results confirm the protocol’s security and correctness. Furthermore, performance evaluations highlight the computational and communication overheads inherent to 5G-AKA-HPQC. With only average of 56.5 millisecond(+112.32%) on a total authentication time, proposed protocol provides its advantages. Also, on a environment of multiple UE registration, compared to single UE registration, the difference of increased rate of average authentication time is only 0.01%. The negligible difference 0.01% indicates that the addition of PQC does not lead to increased overhead in multi-UE registration environments, demonstrating its scalability and practical feasibility. In conclusion, our research provides significant insights into the design of secure, quantum-safe authentication protocols and lays the groundwork for the future standardization of secure authentication and key agreement protocols for mobile telecommunications.

title: Bounds of Fractional Domination in Space-Filling Curves
authors: S. Amutha, G. Uma, N. Anbazhagan, Woong Cho, Gyanendra Prasad Joshi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Among space-filling geometric constructions, the Peano and Hilbert curves generate recursively defined graph structures with rapidly increasing order and self-similar connectivity. However, the behavior of the fractional domination number in recursively constructed space-filling graphs has not been systematically examined. In this paper, we provide a precise graph-theoretic formulation of the n-th iterations of the Peano and Hilbert graph and analyze their fractional domination parameters. The fractional domination number of a graph is defined as the minimum total weight assigned to vertices, where each weight lies between 0 and 1 inclusive, such that every vertex is covered by weights from itself and its neighbors summing to at least one. We further investigate the behavior of the fractional domination number under structural perturbations, including vertex additions, vertex deletions, and edge deletions. In addition, we present results concerning Cartesian products of space-filling graphs and certain related graph classes. The accompanying Java and Python programs implement the derived closed-form expressions for the fractional domination numbers of the Peano and Hilbert graph and serve as formula-based computational tools for further experimentation. The results clarify the structural behavior of domination parameters in recursively defined space-filling graphs and contribute to the broader study of domination in self-similar network structures.

title: Deep Learning for Skin Diseases Classification from Dermoscopic Images: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Chahinez Guermat, Larbi Boubchir, Vicente González-Ruiz
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)
abstract: Skin diseases encompass a wide range of conditions, from benign skin problems to serious skin cancers. In such cases early and accurate diagnosis is essential. In recent years, deep learning-based approaches have significantly advanced automated skin disease classification, particularly through the analysis of dermoscopic images. This paper gives a full and organized review of recent deep learning methods used for this task. We review convolutional neural network (CNN) based models, hybrid architectures, and transformer-based approaches, highlighting their methodological principles and comparative performance. In addition, we analyze the main publicly available dermatological datasets commonly used for model development and evaluation, including ISIC Archive, HAM10000, PH ${ }^{\mathbf{2}}$, and DermNet, with particular attention to their strengths and limitations. Beyond summarizing existing methods, this survey discusses persistent challenges such as data quality, class imbalance, reproducibility, and clinical relevance. By summarizing recent work and highlighting open issues, this paper aims to support the development of more reliable models for skin disease diagnosis.

title: AgroConnect: An AI-Driven Digital Marketplace for Direct Agricultural Trade with Quality Assurance and Dynamic Pricing
authors: Priyanshu Mishra, Saksham Kumar, Laxman Singh, Rekha Kashyap, Nagendra Nath Dubey
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: AgroConnect is an AI-backed digital marketplace that aims to connect farmers directly with consumers in order to decrease reliance on middlemen. The marketplace combines computer vision-based quality control, dynamic pricing algorithms, and secure ESCROW transactions delivered through a Progressive Web App designed in the MERN stack. AgroConnect combines real-time market data from e-NAM, Random Forest models for fraud detection, and Mapbox API for optimal logistics and delivery routing. Early adopters that have tested the pilot phase with over 500 users reported a 25–30% income boost to farmers, quicker order fulfillment, and an 85% decrease in payment disputes. AgroConnect has garnered significant interest from users and has potential to expand significantly, and can help improve market access and reduce post-harvest loss, while also serving UN Sustainable Development Goal 2 — Zero Hunger.

title: Big Data and IoT Technologies: Prospects and Difficulties for Higher Education
authors: Firas Tayseer Ayasrah, P. William, Thipendra P. Singh, D. B. Pardeshi
publish: 2025 Global Congress on Emerging Technologies (GCET)
abstract: In this essay, the incorporation of Big Data and Internet of Things (IoT) technology into higher education is investigated. The research gives a dual image of revolutionary opportunities and challenging problems in this regard. We take a look at the ways in which Internet of Things (IoT) devices and Big Data analytics are being used to enhance the efficiency of administrative processes, tailor learning, and develop intelligent campus environments. Within the scope of this study, a critical analysis is also performed on the implications of this technological integration with regard to the requirements of infrastructure, data privacy, and data security. Through a combination of case studies, survey data, and research into the relevant literature, we provide strategies for tackling the inherent obstacles that these technologies present, as well as insights into the revolutionary potential that they potentially possess.

title: Multi-Spectral Vision Transformers for Early Crop Disease Detection Under Natural Field Variability
authors: Nisha J C, K.R.Ram Victoria, G Linda Rose, S.Navaneethan, Maheswari.S, K. Ashini
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: The given paper describes an integrated approach to the early detection of crop diseases in real-time through a new Multi-Spectral Vision Transformer (MSViT). The method proposed incorporates spatial, spectral and contextual information by simultaneously processing the RGB as well as the near-infrared (NIR) channels with a dual-stream transformer encoder, which trains on cross-spectral attention. Extrinsic spectral enhancement block is added to enhance the faint physical stress signals that are not easily seen in normal imagery. In order to combat field-level ailments like illumination shifts, soil background noise, leaf occlusion, and phenological variation, add an adaptive domain-aware normalization layer which dynamically adjusts the scaling of features depending on environmental metadata. This model is trained in a self-supervised spectral consistency objective to minimize the use of large annotated datasets. The experimental findings of multi-spectral field datasets show that MSViT has a higher accuracy of 96.4% in the early stage of detecting diseases and is stable under real-world scenarios. The approach provides a scalable approach that can be used in precision agriculture and automated crop health-sensing.

title: The Application of Computerized Listentrain Games for Students with Phonological and Chinese Reading Difficulties
authors: Hui-Shan Cheng, Ting-Yu Chang, Yu Tsao, Hsiao-Lan Wang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Modern Educational Technology (ICMET)
abstract: Developmental Dyslexia is a neurodevelopmental disorder that negatively affects word recognition and spelling abilities, typically resulting from poor phonological representations. This study investigates whether the ListenTrain Games, a self-designed computerized training program, can have a positive effect upon students (Grade 7 to Grade 9) with Chinese phonological difficulties and reading impairments. In this study, students were recruited and assigned to an experimental training group (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N}=8$</tex>) or a control group (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N}=10$</tex>). The experimental group received 12 weeks of intervention (three sessions per week, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30-40$</tex> minutes per session), utilizing the ListenTrain Games. The results showed some positive average developmental changes on listening comprehension, phonetic decoding, and Chinese character recognition. However, the limitation of training intensity and participant attendance may diminish the statistical significance.

title: Performance Analysis of IoT Based System for Efficient Waste Collection and Recycling
authors: G Prabhakar Raju, Siva Krishna Teki, R Archana Reddy, Babitha Lincy R, Neha Dhiman, Sujeet Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Power Electronics &amp; IoT Applications in Renewable Energy and its Control (PARC)
abstract: The world is increasingly producing trash and this is a serious threat to the sustainability of the environment. This work examines how the Internet of Things (IoT) technology can be used to transform waste management practices. Sensors, data analytics and automation can be used to optimise waste collection routes, enhance recycling efficiency and reduce the total environmental impact through IoT-based solutions. In this paper, the fundamental aspects of the IoT-based trash management software are discussed, such as immediate waste retrieval trucks, intelligent analytics of collected data to predict maintenance and optimal routes, and intelligent bins equipped with sensors to monitor waste materials and fill rates. The paper has made a comparative evaluation of various sensors. The benefits of such systems are discussed such as reduced costs of operation, improved allocation of resources and greater environmental sustainability. Also, the paper discusses the challenges and potential limitations to the use of IoT in trash management such as infrastructure requirements, social acceptability, and data privacy. To prove the potential of the IoT-based systems to help make the future of waste management more sustainable and efficient, case examples and the areas of research are analyzed.

title: Early Detection of Alzheimer’s Disease Using Curvelet Transform and Deep Learning with Voice-Enabled NeuroScan+ Interface
authors: S. Ijaz Ahmed, M. Dharanibalan, V. S. Dhanussh Raj, M. Hemalatha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: Early diagnosis of Alzheimer’s Disease (AD) is crucial for timely clinical intervention but remains challenging due to subtle structural changes in early stages. This work proposes a multimodal diagnostic framework integrating Curvelet-based MRI preprocessing, a 3D Convolutional Neural Network (3DCNN), and a voice-accessible interface to support hands-free clinical interaction. Curvelet Transform enhances fine-scale edges and textures in structural MRI scans, improving feature extraction, while the 3D-CNN leverages volumetric spatial information for accurate classification of AD, Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI), and Normal Controls (NC). A voice-enabled NeuroScan+ module facilitates clinician interaction and accelerates workflow with high recognition accuracy and low latency.
