Experimental evaluation on ADNI and OASIS datasets demonstrates an average accuracy of 94.1%, supported by Grad-CAM interpretability and statistically significant improvements over baseline CNN models. The results highlight the potential of combining advanced image preprocessing, deep learning, and voice accessibility for practical, early-stage Alzheimer’s diagnosis in clinical environments.

title: Optimizing Animal Birth Control and Anti-Rabies Vaccination Deployment Using Artificial Intelligence: A Case of Delhi–NCR
authors: Samarth Sharma, Narendra Singh, Pallavi Sharda Garg, Nitendra Kumar, Nisha Sharma, Mayank Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: The burgeoning population of free-ranging dogs in urban India remains a serious public health threat primarily because of the risks of dog bites and rabies transmission. This paper presents and validates a structured survey instrument to describe the drivers of community perceptions of bite prevention and risk of rabies. The model estimates the six independent variables--Stray Dog Population Dynamics, Environmental and Waste Management, Community Awareness and Safe Practices, Access to Health Care and Bite Response, Program Implementation Efficiency, Policy and Governance Support-against the dependent variable, Perceived Reduction in Bites and Rabies Risk. Through a Likert-scale measurement, the model provides count of behavioural and structural issues of stray dogs’ management. This framework paves the way for empirical investigation using PLS-SEM, and delivering practical advice for the municipal officials, NGOs and decision makers in their attempts to improve the stray dog control and rabies prevention efforts.

title: Forecasting Solar Power: Enhancing Energy Predictions with LSTM Networks
authors: PM Yakupitiyage, ML Saputantri, HMRV Herath
publish: 2025 International Conference on Data, Energy and Communication Networks (DECoN)
abstract: The renewable energy sector is experiencing rapid growth, and within this thriving industry, accurate solar power forecasting serves an important function in maintaining efficient energy utilization and reliable grid integration. Moreover, it offers significant economic benefits, contributing to cost savings and enhancing energy distribution efficiency. This paper demonstrates the use of LSTM models to estimate solar plant energy output using sensor-based irradiance data collected through a pyranometer. The LSTM models were trained based on historical data, including time, irradiance, and power output, to forecast future power generation for a solar plant in Vavuniya, Sri Lanka. The dataset covered a one-year period from March 2023 to March 2024. The study shows that the developed models are capable of identifying both long- and short-term dynamics of solar energy generation, making them valuable for forecasting and supporting industries to renewable power systems.

title: Numerical Modeling of an Enhanced Twin-Core Hexagonal PCF-Based High Sensitivity SPR Sensor
authors: Shreya Gupta, Sudhanshu Verma, Dharmendra Kumar, Anupam Sahu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Electrical and Electronics Engineering (ICE3)
abstract: This study numerically analyzes guidance properties of light and sensing efficiency of a twin-core PCF-based sensor structure constructed with air holes in a hexagonal configuration, designed using surface plasmon resonance (SPR). To simplify fabrication, a gold layer is positioned externally and serves as an excellent plasmonic material to enhance the resonance sensitivity. Finite element method (FEM) simulations, conducted using both amplitude and wavelength interrogation strategies, reveal significant sensitivity metrics. The sensor demonstrates a level of amplitude sensitivity 1276 RIU$^{-1}$ and reaches its highest wavelength sensitivity, such as 8500 nm/RIU, the achieved resolution reaches $1.18 \times 10^{-5}$ RIU. This SPR-based sensor efficiently detects refractive index shifts in the analyte throughout 1.33 to 1.38. Its structurally simplified layout supports high-precision biochemical recognition and offers practical advantages for real-world sensing applications.

title: A Phase-Based Ethical Alignment Framework for Mitigating In-Context Scheming Behaviour in Superintelligent Systems
authors: Chandrashekhar Nagaraju, Prateek J H
publish: 2025 International Conference On Emerging Computation and Information Technologies (ICECIT)
abstract: Recent work has demonstrated that frontier language models are capable of in-context scheming: the deliberate and strategic pursuit of misaligned goals through deceptive behavior while appearing aligned under human oversight. Such behaviors pose a serious risk to the safe deployment of increasingly autonomous AI systems on a trajectory toward superintelligence. This paper proposes a culturally grounded, phase-based ethical alignment framework inspired by the Padvidhi Sutra, an ancient Indian account of moral development in teacher–disciple relationships. A six-stage progression is adapted into a machine learning alignment protocol intended for application during fine-tuning of frontier-capability and superintelligent systems; the framework is evaluated via an in-context proxy implementation, due to restricted access to model parameters. Using an established scheming evaluation taxonomy and standardized testing protocol, the effectiveness of the proposed intervention is assessed across six scheming criteria. Empirical results show no observed incidents under the evaluation protocol for Oversight Subversion, Goal Guarding, Instrumental Alignment Faking, and Sandbagging, along with a substantial reduction (78%) in Self-Exfiltration. These findings indicate that structured, phase-based ethical scaffolding can reduce observed deceptive and strategic misalignment under a standardized evaluation protocol, without introducing evidence of pathological underperformance.

title: Modeling, Control, and Performance Evaluation of MMC-Based STATCOM Using SSBC Topology
authors: Khiat Walid, Djehaf Mohammed Abdeldjalil
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Power, Energy &amp; Industry Applications (ElCon-PEIA)
abstract: Compared to classical two-level voltage source converters, modular multilevel converter (MMC) based STATCOMs offer superior scalability, enhanced fault tolerance, and significant total harmonic distortion reduction due to multilevel waveforms with lower filtering requirements. This paper presents the modeling, control, and performance evaluation of an MMC-based STATCOM using single-star bridge cell topology for dynamic reactive power compensation in medium voltage networks. A detailed dqframe control strategy is developed, comprising an outer loop for DC link voltage and reactive power regulation, and an inner loop for decoupled current control with feedforward compensation. Phase balancing control and individual submodule capacitor voltage balancing methods ensure stable cascaded operation. The system is evaluated under capacitive and inductive operation scenarios. Simulation results demonstrate uniform capacitor voltages, superior current tracking, faster disturbance recovery, reduced THD, and improved dynamic response, confirming effectiveness for modern converter-dominated grids.

title: AI-Driven Cybersecurity Threat Detection: A Hybrid CNN-LSTM Deep Learning Approach
authors: Haijia Zhong, Fucai Xie, Lanke Yu, Xianpeng Wang, Qi Xie, Mey Sok
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence Security and Governance (ICAISG)
abstract: Network intrusion detection is critical for cybersecurity and safeguarding digital infrastructure. However, it faces significant challenges from evolving attack patterns, sophisticated evasion techniques, and severe class imbalance inherent in real-world network traffic data. This paper proposes a CNN-LSTM hybrid deep learning model with adaptive balancing strategies for effective and real-time threat detection. The hybrid architecture leverages CNN layers for spatial feature extraction and LSTM layers for temporal pattern modeling in network traffic sequences. We conduct comprehensive experiments on three benchmark datasets: KDD Cup 99, CICIDS2017, and UNSW-NB15. Our method achieves 91.69% accuracy on KDD Cup 99, 95.80% accuracy on CICIDS2017, and 89.50% accuracy on UNSW-NB15. On CICIDS2017, the model attains exceptional performance with 93.2% attack recall and 96.8% normal recall, achieving balanced detection with only 3.24% false positive rate. On UNSW-NB15, the model achieves 89.20% F1-score with 97.33% attack recall and 94.11% normal precision. Our ablation study reveals the importance of adaptive balancing strategies tailored to each dataset' s characteristics. These results demonstrate strong generalization capability across diverse modern network environments, validating the practical applicability for deployment in real-world network security systems. This work contributes to UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) 9, 11, and 16 by enhancing resilient infrastructure, supporting sustainable development through improved cybersecurity, and strengthening institutions against digital threats.

title: A Secure Role-Based Access Control Framework for Employee Plantilla Management System in the Local Government Unit of Magallanes, Agusan del Norte
authors: Federico M. Grino, Erika M. Abellar, Jessel M. Cagmat, Cristemi F. Lasco
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: This study addresses the security weaknesses in the Excel-based employee plantilla management system of the Local Government Unit of Magallanes, Agusan del Norte. The existing system lacked proper access control, exposing sensitive employee data to unauthorized access and non-compliance with the article III, section 11 and section 12 of the Data Privacy Act of 2012. The study aims to developed a secure role-based access control framework to regulate user privileges, enhance data security, and ensure compliance with the Data Privacy Act. Additionally, it includes user activity logging and a lockout mechanism after multiple failed logins attempts to enhance accountability, traceability, and monitoring of actions performed by the users. A waterfall model covers planning, analysis, design, development, implementation, and testing phases. The system was developed using PHP, the Laravel framework, and MySQL. The ISO/IEC 25010 standard evaluated software quality attributes such as performance, security, usability, confidentiality, reliability, and user satisfaction. The system achieved high acceptance, with overall user satisfaction score of 4.65. It significantly improved the security, operational efficiency, and compliance. Feature like role-based access control, activity logging, and the lockout mechanism were well-received. The system enhances the LGU’s ability to manage employee plantilla records securely and serves as a model for future digital transformation and compliance in the local government human resource management.

title: A Real Time IoT Machine Learning Pipeline for Tomato Crop Disease Detection Irrigation Optimization and Yield Prediction
authors: R. Swathika, N. Radha, Yogeshram R, Nithish Kumar S, Kezia Stephano
publish: 2026 International Conference on Computing, Communication, Control and Cyber-Physical Systems (I5CPS)
abstract: The requirement for food on a global scale and the unstable climatic conditions have shifted agriculture towards a more capable, data-driven method of doing things. A real-time IoT-Machine Learning pipeline for tomato cultivation is outlined in this paper, combining intelligent irrigation control, early disease detection, and analytical yield prediction into a single precision-farming framework. The Internet of Things (IoT) layer consists of soil moisture, temperature, and pressure sensors that help in monitoring the field conditions around the clock, and this is the basis for the automated irrigation that uses solenoid valves powered by a solar-based energy module. In addition to the sensor data, weather forecasts are used to optimize water delivery and reduce wastage. Additionally, the use of Machine Learning model that has been trained with tomato leaf disease datasets is now able to classify pests and pathogens in real-time, which facilitates early intervention and hence, less crop loss. A mathematical yield estimation module takes into account the germination rate, plant density, pest severity index, and water-stress deviation to provide figures for ideal yield, adjusted yield, loss percentages, and total crop productivity. These metrics along with water requirements and market value predictions are visualized through a mobile app and web dashboard, enabling farmers to make informed, evidence-based decisions. The proposed system demonstrates how IoT sensing, automated actuation, and ML-driven analytics can collectively enhance crop health, optimize water usage, minimize economic loss, and move traditional farming towards a more sustainable, efficient, and resilient agricultural model.

title: Precision Agriculture Through Spectral Signatures: An Integrated Evalaution of NDVI, EVI, GCI AND NDWI for Stress Detection
authors: Ayush Tripathi, Vanshika Yadav, Tanishq Chauhan, Ali Imam Abidi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Innovation for Sustainability (EmergIN)
abstract: Crop health monitoring is an essential element of sustainable food security and agriculture. Conventional field-based methods of monitoring tend to be slow and spatially limited, therefore scalable, efficient alternatives are necessary. The article discusses a spectral analysis technique with the central vegetation indices - NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index), GCI (Green Chlorophyll Index), EVI (Enhanced Vegetation Index), MSI (Moisture Stress Index), and NDWI (Normalized Difference Water Index) to quantify vegetation health, chlorophyll density, water stress, and water condition. Using multispectral reflectance measurements, each index was calculated, plotted, and analyzed to deduce suitable patterns under different vegetation conditions. The method allows for vegetation classification into very healthy, moderately healthy, stressed, and barren groups with normalized thresholds and color maps. Comparative examination indicates how indices react differently to certain plant attributes. The study illustrates a flexible, data-driven approach to preventive crop health diagnostics and lays the groundwork for more sophisticated decision-support systems in precision agriculture. The findings show the viability of extensive vegetation monitoring by spectral index analysis and confirm the application of composite indices in multi-dimensional aspects of plant health detection.
